PT P
PN CN112626555-A
TI Preparing copper sulfide/graphitic carbon nitride electro-catalytic material for performing electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves e.g. fusing cupric selenide nano-sheet and graphitic carbon nitride nano-layer.
AU YIN X
   ZHANG H
   MU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 2021404948
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper sulfide/graphitic carbon nitride electro-catalytic material involves: fusing cupric selenide nano-sheet and graphitic carbon nitride nano-layer to form a heterogeneous structure between two-dimensional material, where the two-dimensional material helps to increase active surface area, and improve rate of electron transfer, so as to improve performance of carbon dioxide electro-catalytic reduction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing copper sulfide/graphitic carbon nitride electro-catalytic material. The electro-catalytic material is useful for performing electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to generate carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electro-catalytic material: is easy to obtain; has excellent electro-catalysis performance, high selectivity, single and stable product, low material price and obvious application prospect; and meets the idea of green development. The Faraday efficiency of carbon monoxide can reach 85.28% under 1.2Vvs.RHE. The current density is 10.125 mA/cm2.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN CN112375621-A
TI Preparation and application of Atractylodes lanceolata volatile oil involves crushing Atractylodes lanceolata root into powder, adding water to powder, extracting, concentrating and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU SU L
AE NANJING ZELANG PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NJZL-C)
GA 2021218275
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation and application of Atractylodes lanceolata volatile oil involves crushing A.lanceolata root into powder, adding water to 1 kg of the powder, extracting with steam distillation to obtain distillate and residue, re-extracting the residue twice, concentrating the filtrate, adding the filtrate to the supercritical extraction kettle to carry out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction with 5 vol.% ethanol (as entrainer) with respect to the total extraction solvent, collecting the extract, adding ether to extract the obtained extract twice, drying the extract with anhydrous sodium sulfate and concentrating under reduced pressure. The extraction kettle is maintained at 20-40 MPa and 40-50 degrees C. The flow rate of carbon dioxide in the kettle is 20-40 L/hour. The extraction time is 2-3 hours.
   USE - Preparation and application of A.lanceolata volatile oil used in preparation of antibacterial and bactericidal daily chemicals and medicines (claimed).
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-B01C1; B12-M07; B14-A01; D09-A01; D10-A01

PT P
PN CN112374470-A
TI Method for processing molybdenum concentrate, comprises roasting or decomposing molybdenum concentrate to generate sulfur dioxide and molybdenum oxide, and reducing sulfur dioxide by carbon reducing agent to obtain sulfur and carbon dioxide.
AU SUN Y
   CHE Y
   ZHANG S
   SONG J
   HAO Z
   ZHU J
   SHU Y
   HE J
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2021215906
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing molybdenum concentrate, comprises roasting or decomposing molybdenum concentrate to generate sulfur dioxide and molybdenum oxide, then reducing sulfur dioxide based on carbon reducing agent to generate sulfur and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for processing molybdenum concentrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon reducing agent to reduce sulfur dioxide to generate sulfur and carbon dioxide, which avoids pollution of sulfur dioxide to the environment, and reduces storage and transportation costs of sulfuric acid; generates sulfur, which saves relatively large expenses compared with the storage and transportation of sulfur dioxide; has increased domestic production of sulfur that is in short supply, reduced cost of sulfur imports, and promoted development of molybdenum and sulfur-related industries compared with the processing method of making sulfuric acid from sulfur dioxide; and ensures effective treatment and value-added utilization of sulfur element in the molybdenum concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The roasting or decomposition treatment of the molybdenum concentrate to generate sulfur dioxide and molybdenum oxide includes decomposing the molybdenum concentrate to generate sulfur, low-valence molybdenum sulfide and metallic molybdenum, and roasting the low-valence molybdenum sulfide to generate sulfur dioxide and molybdenum oxide. The roasting or decomposition treatment of the molybdenum concentrate to generate sulfur dioxide and molybdenum oxide includes pulverizing the molybdenum concentrate, then placing in a first-stage oxidation furnace, introducing air, and heating at 550-600 degrees C for 5-8 hours, constantly turning the molybdenum concentrate to completely oxidize the molybdenum concentrate during heating time, and obtaining sulfur-containing flue gas after the roasting is completed. The carbon-based reducing agent reduces the sulfur dioxide to generate sulfur and carbon dioxide includes cooling the sulfur-containing flue gas to less than or equal to 400 degrees C, then removing low melting point metal oxides in the flue gas to obtain sulfur dioxide flue gas, where the low melting point metal oxides include molybdenum trioxide and rhenium heptaoxide, passing the sulfur dioxide flue gas into a second-stage reduction furnace, and adding carbon reducing agent, controlling temperature of the second-stage reduction furnace to 850-1100 degrees C, and obtaining first gas after reduction is completed, where the first gas is a mixture including sulfur vapor, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen sulfide, maintaining the temperature of the first gas to greater than or equal to 600 degrees C, then passing the first gas into the condenser of the recovery system, and controlling the temperature of the condenser to 160 degrees C to obtain residual second gas, and recovering the liquid sulfur, where the second gas is gas after sulfur is removed including carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen sulfide, combusting the residual second gas, and cooling liquid sulfur to prepare solid sulfur. The decomposition treatment includes microwave decomposition treatment and/or vacuum decomposition treatment. Preferred Components: The carbon reducing agent includes activated carbon, coal, coke, carbon black and/or graphite.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-C; M25-G18
IP C01B-017/06; C01B-017/52; C01G-039/02; C22B-001/02

PT P
PN CN112126072-A; CN112126072-B
TI New super tetrahedral metal organic frame material useful for separating light hydrocarbon gas including acetylene and carbon dioxide gas.
AU ZHANG L
   LI F
   YOU J
   HUA N
   WANG Q
   CHEN W
AE UNIV FUJIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYFI-C)
GA 2021013518
AB    NOVELTY - Super tetrahedral metal organic frame material (I) is new.
   USE - The supertetrahedron metal organic framework material is useful for separating light hydrocarbon gas including acetylene and carbon dioxide gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supertetrahedron metal organic framework material (ST-sod-cobalt/titanium): has good thermal stability and high acid and alkali resistance, and exhibits excellent performance in acetylene/carbon dioxide separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Super tetrahedral metal organic frame material of formula (M3(OH/O)(H2O/F)(btc)2(tpt)2/3) (I) is new.
   M3 = Mg3, Mn3, Fe3, Co3, Ni3, Zn3, Al3, Sc3, In3, Ga3, TixMg (3-x), TixFe (3-x), TixCo (3-x), TixNi(3-x), VxMg (3-x), VxFe (3-x), VxCo (3-x), V xNi (3-x), ScxMg(3-x), ScxFe(3-x), ScxCo(3-x), ScxNi(3-x), GaxMg(3-x), GaxFe(3-x), GaxCo3- x, GaxNi(3-x), InxMg(3-x), InxFe(3-x), InxCo(3-x), InxNi3- x; and
   x = integer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises either dispersing cobalt sulfate heptahydrate, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylate in a mixed solvent, adding etrafluoroboric acid for acidification, reacting the mixed suspension by solvent heat, collecting, washing, and vacuum drying to obtain supertetrahedral (ST)-sod-cobalt; or dispersing nickel nitrate hexahydrate, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridinyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid salt in a mixed solvent, and adding fluorosilicic acid for acidification, reacting the mixed suspension by solvothermal, collecting, washing with dimethylacetamide, and drying under vacuum to obtain product ST-sod-nickel; or dispersing cobalt chloride hexahydrate, dichloro titanocene, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylate in a mixed solvent, and adding fluorosilicic acid for acidification, reacting then the mixed suspension by solvothermal, collecting, washing with dimethylacetamide, and vacuum drying to obtain final product ST-sod-cobalt/titanium. Preferred Components: The supertetrahedral metal-organic framework material is metal-organic framework material with supermicroporous quadrilateral window and mesoporous sodalite cages constructed by supertetrahedral building units, and is arranged as ST-sod-metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), the ST-sod-MOFs is a sodalite network topology structure formed by a supertetrahedral structural unit composed of metal trimer nodes in a way of corner sharing, with beta cavity of mesoporous size, and each beta -cavity in the skeleton is connected through six ultra-microporous quadrilateral window, while the other eight hexagonal window are sealed. In preparation of supertetrahedral (ST)-sod-cobalt, the molar ratio between cobalt sulfate heptahydrate, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylate is 9:6:2, the mixed solvent is formed by mixing N,N-dimethylformamide and methanol in a volume ratio of 4:1, the tetrafluoroboric acid is 50 wt.% aqueous solution of tetrafluoroboric acid, the heating temperature of the suspension is 110-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-10 days. In preparation of (ST)-sod-nickel, the molar ratio between cobalt sulfate heptahydrate, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridyl)- 1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylate is 9:6:2, the mixed solvent is formed by mixing N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide and methanol in a volume ratio of 3:3:1; the fluorosilicic acid is 25 wt.% or 35 wt.% aqueous solution of fluorosilicic acid; the heating temperature of the suspension is 110-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-10 days. In preparation of ST-sod-cobalt/titanium, the molar ratio between cobalt chloride hexahydrate, titanocene dichloride, 2,4,6-tris(4-pyridyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylate is 6: 3:6:2, the mixed solvent is formed by mixing N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide and methanol in a volume ratio of 3:3:1; the fluorosilicic acid is 25 wt.% or 35 wt.% aqueous solution of fluorosilicic acid; the heating temperature of the suspension is 110-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-10 days.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E05-D; E05-L01; E05-L02; E05-L03A; E05-L03D; E10-J01A; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; J01-E02B; J01-E03C

PT P
PN CN111841547-A
TI Composite material used in catalyst for            electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises            bimetallic metal organic frameworks comprising            bimetallic ions comprising copper and nickel, and            organic ligands and polyacrylate carrier.
AU MI J
   JIN J
   WU H
   WANG Y
   ZHEN C
AE QINGDAO CHUANGQI HUINENG NEW MATERIAL (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2655H
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material comprises bimetallic metal                organic frameworks and polyacrylate carrier, where                the bimetallic metal organic frameworks comprises                bimetallic ions and organic ligands, the                polyacrylate carrier is polyacrylate pellets, and                the bimetal comprise copper and nickel, iron, and                cobalt, preferably copper and nickel.
   USE - The composite material is used in a catalyst                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has conductive network                inside the porous material that provides an                effective way for electron transmission, and the                spatial distribution of the pore structure also                reduces the spatial accumulation effect of metal                organic frameworks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide comprising the composite material                which is obtained after high temperature pyrolysis                and carbonization; and
   (2) a method for preparing the composite                material comprising (a) preparing porous                polyacrylate beads, and (b) loading the porous                polyacrylate pellet material prepared in step (a)                with bimetallic metal organic frameworks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material of                  the conductive material is                  3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene, where the                  3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene is polymerized to                  poly3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene in the matrix                  polymerization. The amount of                  3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene added to the emulsion                  in step (a) is 15-25% of the solid content of the                  polyacrylate. Preferred Method: The step of                  loading bimetal metal organic frame works in the                  step (b) comprises preparing the metal                  A-porphyrin complex, dissolving the metal                  A-porphyrin complex and copper nitrate trihydrate                  in N,N-dimethylformamide, and immersing the                  polyacrylate porous material in the above                  solution, where the method for preparing the                  metal A-porphyrin complex comprises dissolving                  the soluble salt of metal A and the porphyrin in                  N,N-dimethylformamide, and heating the                  reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation of porous                  polyacrylate pellets in step (a) involves first                  preparing the monomer of polyacrylate,                  emulsifier, porogen, initiator (A), reducing                  agent (A), and water into an emulsion, mixing                  initiator (B), reducing agent (B), dispersant and                  water to prepare an aqueous system, adding the                  emulsion to the aqueous system to disperse the                  emulsion into droplets to complete the monomer                  copolymerization to obtain spherical porous                  polyacrylate beads, the monomers are glycidyl                  methacrylate and divinylbenzene, the emulsifier                  is Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan monooleate), the                  porogen is toluene, the initiator (A) is benzoyl                  peroxide, and the reduction agent (A) is                  N,N-dimethylaniline, the initiator (B) is                  ammonium persulfate, the reducing agent (B) is                  N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine, and the                  dispersant is polyvinyl alcohol, where the                  emulsion comprises 50-55 wt.% acrylate monomer,                  3-5 wt.% emulsifier, 40-45 wt.% toluene, 1-1.5                  wt.% initiator (A), and 1-1.5 wt.% reducing agent                  (A), the sum of the above components is 100%, and                  the amount of water added is 75-80 vol.% of the                  total volume of the emulsion, and the aqueous                  system comprises 98.0-98.5 wt.% water, 0.3-0.5                  wt.% initiator (B), 0.3-0.5 wt.% reducing agent                  (B) and 0.5-1 wt.% dispersing agent. The porous                  polyacrylate pellets are modified by amine                  grafting before loading metal organic frameworks,                  that is, the porous polyacrylate pellets obtained                  in step (a) are immersed in a solution containing                  organic amines, where the organic amine is                  tetraethylenepentamine. The method involves                  uniformly distributing a conductive material in                  the polyacrylate carrier. Preferred Components:                  The polyacrylate carrier is grafted with organic                  amine.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); 
MC E05-L02; E05-L03B; E07-A03B; E10-B01E; E10-J02B4; E11-N; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; J03-B01; J04-E04A; J04-E04D; J04-E11; A02-A01; A04-B10; A04-F06E; A08-B01; A08-S01; A08-S02; A10-E05B; A10-E09A; A11-C04B1; A12-W11; A12-W11K

PN KR2141818-B1
TI Producing high-strength plastic composite, comprises e.g. adding power generation ash to water by injecting carbon dioxide, filtering, drying carbonated power generation ash, and molding plastic composite into desired shape.
AU SHIM S E
   AHN W
   YU G S
   HWANG S
   YONGHAKIM
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202075304G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high-strength plastic composite, comprises (a) adding power generation ash to water to prepare a water/generation ash mixture, (b) carbonating the power generation ash by injecting carbon dioxide into the water/power generation ash mixture, (c) filtering, drying the carbonated power generation ash from an aqueous solution to obtain dried carbonation power generation ash, (d) adding the obtained dried carbonation power generation ash into molten plastic to obtain the plastic composite, and (e) molding the plastic composite into a desired shape.
   USE - The method is useful for producing high-strength plastic composite.

   EXAMPLE - 45 g Polypropylene was added in a kneader (twin screw internal mixer) which is preheated to 220 degrees C, melted at 50-200 revolution/minutes for 3 minutes, and 5-15 g carbonated power generation ash was mixed for 9-12 minutes. TI Preparation of hollow mesoporous defect-type            tungsten trioxide nanosphere used as photothermal            catalyst, includes dissolving tungsten precursor in            glacial acetic acid, performing hydrothermal reaction,            cooling, and ultrasonicating.
AU GUO Q
   XU Y
   FENG H
   SHEN D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 202033968X
AB    NOVELTY - A hollow mesoporous defect-type tungsten                trioxide nanosphere is prepared by dissolving                tungsten precursor in glacial acetic acid to obtain                precursor solution, performing hydrothermal                reaction at 160-180 degrees C for 16-18 hours,                cooling, ultrasonicating for 12-16 hours,                centrifuging, washing, drying, calcining at 500-550                degrees C, cooling, and performing hydrogen                reduction at 400-450 degrees C.
   USE - The method is for preparation of hollow                mesoporous defect-type tungsten trioxide nanosphere                used as photothermal catalyst in photothermal                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce                methane using full-spectrum light source                (claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hollow mesoporous                  defect-type tungsten trioxide nanosphere of                  formula I (WO3-x) is prepared by dissolving                  tungsten precursor comprising tungsten                  hexachloride, phosphotungstic acid and/or sodium                  tungstate in glacial acetic acid according to                  mass-volume ratio of 0.15-0.25 g:40-60 mL,                  stirring for 1-2 hours to obtain precursor                  solution , performing hydrothermal reaction at                  160-180 degrees C for 16-18 hours, cooling,                  ultrasonicating for 12-16 hours, centrifuging and                  washing not less than 3 times at rate of                  10000-13000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes,                  freeze drying for 18-24 hours, calcining at                  500-550 degrees C at heating rate of 4-5 degrees                  C/minute for 1-2 hours, cooling, and performing                  hydrogen reduction at 400-450 degrees C for 2-3                  hours under hydrogen/argon mixed gas containing                  5-10 vol.% hydrogen at heating rate of 4-5                  degrees C/minute to obtain product with particle                  diameter of 200-500 nm.x=2.5-3.

The beer has alcohol content of not less than 0.05 vol.%, bitterness of 5EBC-10EBC, raw wort concentration of 4.5-7.5 degrees P, carbon dioxide content of 0.50-0.60 wt.%, total alcohol is greater than or equal to 30 mg/l, total esters is greater than or equal to 5 mg/l, and 4-VG is greater than or equal to 3 mg/l.
   USE - Used as 0.0 vol.% non-alcoholic white beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The 0.0 vol.% non-alcoholic white beer: has good taste and prominent acidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing 0.0 vol.% non-alcoholic white beer comprising subjecting the white beer to preheating, degassing, dealcoholization, cooling, carbon dioxide filling, post-modification, stabilization, and filling; to prepare 0.0 vol.% non-alcoholic white beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the method, degassing comprises vacuum degassing the white beer preheated to 40-43 degrees C under vacuum degree of 80-90 mbar, until the carbon dioxide in white beer is completely discharged. In the method, the dealcoholization comprises performing the vacuum distillation on the white beer after degassing at 40-43 degrees C under vacuum degree of 80-90 mbar, until the ethanol content falls below 0.05 vol.%. In the method, the post-modification comprises adding natural beer flavors and natural sweeteners to white beer after the dealcoholization. In the method, the natural beer flavor and natural sweetener ingredients are are added in manner of online addition. The preparation of white beer comprises using Pilsen malt and wheat malt as main raw materials, matching special malt; crushing, saccharifying, filtering, boiling, cyclic precipitation, cooling and oxygenation, adding the above yeast, fermenting, and centrifuging to obtain white beer. The preparation of white beer comprises using Pearson malt and wheat malt as main raw materials, matching special malt, crushing, mixing with water according to material-water ratio of 1: 3-1:4, saccharifying, filtering the saccharified mash at 73 plus minus 1 degrees C to obtain clear malt juice for boiling, adding hop or hop products were added during the boiling process; carrying cyclic precipitation after boiling, adding or not adding hops or hop products during the cyclic precipitation process; cooling to 16-20 degrees C, aerating to the above yeast for fermentation to obtain beer fermentation liquid, and centrifuging beer fermentation liquid to obtain white beer. Preferred Components: The added amount of natural beer flavor is 0.05-0.1 wt.%, preferably 0.05 wt.%; the added amount of natural sweetener is 0.5g/100 ml to 3.5g/100 ml, preferably 1/100 ml, based on the amount of sugar. The natural sweetener comprises fructose syrup, maltose syrup, sucrose syrup, glucose syrup, and/or caster sugar.

PN CN110015662-A; CN110015662-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon material            useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide comprises e.g.            breaking the waste polyurethane foam into particles,            immersing in aqueous solution of quaternary ammonium            and drying under vacuum.
AU GE C
   YANG S
   LU J
   LIU M
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201963777R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon                material comprises (i) breaking the waste                polyurethane foam into particles of 2 mmx 2 mmx 2                mm-5 mmx 5 mmx 5 mm, immersing in a 25% by mass                aqueous solution of quaternary ammonium for 1-10                hours and drying at 50-80 degrees C for 10-30 hours                under vacuum, (ii) subjecting a polyurethane foam                impregnated with a quaternary amine base to a low                temperature chemical activation treatment under                nitrogen atmosphere and (iii) directly subjecting                activated product to carbon dioxide physical                activation without the need of cooling and pickling                to obtain a nitrogen-doped porous carbon material                with a large specific surface area and a large pore                volume and a rich nitrogen content.
   USE - The preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon                material is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide                (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) the                  quaternary ammonium base is 25% by mass of                  tetramethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium                  hydroxide, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and/or                  tetrabutylammonium hydroxide aqueous solution;                  the mass ratio of polyurethane foam to aqueous                  quaternary ammonium base solution is 1:0.8-5;                  carrying out the immersion treatment under vacuum                  and magnetic stirring. In the step (ii) carrying                  out the activation treatment at a rate of 3-10                  degrees C/minutes in a tube furnace at 400-600                  degrees C for 1-3 hours, and continuously                  introducing the nitrogen atmosphere at a rate of                  60-120 ml/minute. In the step (iii) continuously                  carrying out the physical activation of carbon                  dioxide at a rate of 15-50 ml/minutes for 1-3                  hours at 800-1000 degrees C. In the method,                  heating carbon dioxide physical activation                  process at 800-1000 degrees C at a rate of 5                  degrees C/minutes under nitrogen atmosphere,                  passing into a carbon dioxide gas, maintaining at                  a constant temperature for 1-2 hours, switching                  the process to nitrogen and the nitroegn                  protection will drop to room temperature.                  Preferred Components: The adsorbed carbon dioxide                  is doped with a porous carbon material with a                  specific surface area of 847-1560 m2/g, a                  microporous pore having a pore diameter of less                  than 1 nm, a volume of 0.15-0.25 cm3/g, and a                  nitrogen content of 6.84-10.46 wt.%.

PN CN109592702-A
TI Preparation of nanoscale carbonate, involves adding soluble base and solvent, preparing carbonate gas-liquid mixture, sending to gas-liquid separation device, obtaining carbonate slurry, and subjecting to solid-liquid separation.
AU LIAO A
   DAI S
   LIU J
   LI T
AE FUJIAN BASIC ELECTRONIC MATERIALS CO LTD (XMTT-C)
GA 201935012H
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of nanoscale carbonate, involves (1) adding soluble base and solvent to dissolution vessel, stirring at high speed, and storing, (2) dissolving the solution into jet gas-liquid synthesis reaction device, charging gas-liquid synthesis reaction device with carbon dioxide gas for synthesis, where synthesis process temperature is 0-80 degrees C, preparing carbonate gas-liquid mixture and reacting soluble base with carbon dioxide gas to form carbonate, (3) sending carbonate gas-liquid mixture to gas-liquid separation device, performing gas-liquid separation and obtaining the carbonate slurry after extracting the remaining carbon dioxide gas from the reaction, and (4) subjecting carbonate slurry to solid-liquid separation, and drying solid in oven at 80-200 degrees C for 2-10 hours and sieving.
   USE - Preparation of nanoscale carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-scale carbonate has simple preparation process, uniform product particle size, low impurity content and low cost, and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (1) involves adding soluble alkali, particle growth inhibitor and solvent to dissolution vessel, and dissolving solution by high-speed stirring. The Step (3) involves pressurizing carbon dioxide gas, lyophilizing, and refilling into the injection gas-liquid synthesis reaction device to participate in the reaction, subjecting carbonate gas-liquid mixture to pH detection before being sent to the gas-liquid separation device, and sending carbonate gas-liquid mixture having pH of 4-12 to the gas-liquid separation device. Preferred Components: The particle growth inhibitor is carboxylic acid and/or carboxylate. The soluble base is hydroxide and alkali salt. The solvent is solvent of soluble base salt. The hydroxide is lithium hydroxide, barium hydroxide, barium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide. The alkali salt is lithium salt, phosphonium salt, phosphonium salt or calcium salt. Preferred Compositions: The content of particle growth inhibitor is 0-15% of the soluble base molar content. The ratio of the solution flow rate to carbon dioxide gas flow rate is controlled at 1:1-1:500.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN US2019085235-A1
TI Enhancing recovery from oil-containing subterranean formation by injecting treatment solution containing water-soluble carbon-dioxide-generating compound into formation in which compound dissociates and forms carbon dioxide in the formation.
AU HARWELL J
   SHIAU B
   KADHUM M
   WANG S
AE HARWELL J (HARW-Individual)
   SHIAU B (SHIA-Individual)
   KADHUM M (KADH-Individual)
   WANG S (WANG-Individual)
GA 201925877F
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery from oil-containing subterranean formation is enhanced by injecting treatment solution containing greater than or equal to 1 water-soluble carbon-dioxide (CO2)-generating compound into oil-containing subterranean formation, where greater than or equal to 1 water-soluble CO2-generating compound dissociates within oil-containing subterranean formation to form CO2, and where (1) oil-containing subterranean formation has pressure of greater than or equal to 1000 psi and is selected from oil shales, oil-bearing sandstones, and oil-bearing carbonate rocks, (2) weight percentage (wt.%) of water-soluble CO2-generating compound in the treatment solution does not exceed 25 wt.%, and (3) treatment solution has temperature not exceeding 50 degrees C when injected into the oil-containing subterranean formation.
   USE - Enhancement of recovery from oil-containing subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - Oil recovery is enhanced by greater than or equal to 5%, preferably 5-25% greater than oil recovery obtained by conventional water flooding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Treatment solution comprises a catalyst able to catalyze the dissociation of greater than or equal to 1 water soluble CO2-generating compound. Catalyst is selected from vanadium pentoxide and/or carbon nanotube-silica nanohybrid nanoparticle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Water-soluble CO2-generating compound is selected from ammonium carbamate and/or urea. Treatment solution comprises 1-20 wt.%, preferably 4-8 wt.% water-soluble CO2-generating compound. Preferred Processes: Injection of treatment solution into the subterranean formation causes oil phase swelling, reduction of oil viscosity, and/or reduction of oil-water interfacial tension in the subterranean formation. Injection of treatment solution into the subterranean formation causes a reduction of residual saturation (Sor) of subterranean formation of 1-20%, preferably 5-15% more than a reduction resulting from conventional water flooding treatment. Treatment solution is introduced into subterranean formation without a surfactant or chelating agent. Preferred Condition: Treatment solution has a temperature of about ambient temperature, preferably not greater than 45 degrees C when injected in the subterranean formation.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-D06C; H01-D10; J04-E; Q49-A; Q49-V35

PN CN109440465-A; CN109440465-B
TI Method for improving mechanical properties of carbon nanotube fiber involves placing carbon nanotube fiber impregnated with organic solution in closed container, filling container with carbon dioxide, and performing swelling reaction.
AU YU M
   LIU B
   ZHANG H
   WANG K
   CHENG C
   TENG C
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2019248380
AB    NOVELTY - The method for improving mechanical properties of carbon nanotube fiber involves placing carbon nanotube fiber impregnated with the organic solution in a closed container, filling the container with carbon dioxide, so that the internal space of the container is in a supercritical carbon dioxide state, carrying out swelling reaction, slowly releasing the pressure to obtain modified carbon nanotube fiber, selectively subjecting to high temperature treatment, organic matter entering the inside of the fiber is pre-oxidized and carbonized. Carbon nanotube fibers forming a complete carbon structure.
   USE - Method for improving mechanical properties of carbon nanotube fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improvement of mechanical properties of carbon nanotube fiber with high tensile modulus and tensile strength under short reaction time by simple, economical, environmentally-friendly and industrial process, and effectively carries the organic solution into the surface and inside of the fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The stretching process is carried out by subjecting the carbon nanotube fibers such as nascent fibers to stretching treatment with tension of 1-20 cN, filling container with carbon dioxide at 100-200 degrees C, and removing air in the container before charging with carbon dioxide. The swelling reaction process is carried out by performing swelling reaction in which fiber is infiltrated in a gas form within 5-60 minutes under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide, slowly releasing the pressure for slowly opening the air outlet valve and reducing the pressure inside the vessel to atmospheric pressure within 5-10 minutes. Preferred Conditions: The number of times the carbon nanotube fibers are wetted in the organic solution is 1-4 times. The high temperature treatment temperature is 180-280 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic matter of carbon substance is prepared. The concentration of the organic solution in carbon nanotube fiber is 0.25-1 times the critical overlap concentration of the organic solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic substance is polyacrylonitrile.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN CN108634236-A; CN108634236-B
TI Preparing ultra-low fat active squid-soybean mixed peptide oral liquid comprises e.g. extracting supercritical carbon dioxide, subcritical water and ethanol and carrying out operation of enzymatically hydrolyzing the squid and soy.
AU CAI L
   GUO J
   SONG W
AE ZHEJIANG HAIRUNDA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018829352
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-low fat active squid-soybean mixed peptide oral liquid comprises carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of a squid plate, carrying out supercritical water extraction and ethanol extraction to obtain squid solubilized protein solution, subjecting soybean to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, carrying out supercritical water extraction and ethanol extraction to obtain a soy alcohol solution and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis of squid protein solution and soy protein solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-low fat active squid-soybean mixed peptide oral liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can efficiently remove lipids and astringent substances from squid and soybeans, solves the problem of inconvenience caused by the astringency and bitterness of squid and soybeans, obtains functional peptides from squid and soybean, provides good health effect, thus easy to be digested and absorbed by the body and is suitable for patients with cardiovascular disease.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method comprises pre-treating the squid plate before subjecting the squid plate to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, by crushing the squid board, cutting into blocks, rinsing for 3-4 times with deionized water, pulping into a paste to obtain squid paste and pre-treating the soybean before subjecting the soybean to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, by pulverizing fresh soybeans and extruding them to obtain a puffed soybean material, mixing the puffed soybean material with water to obtain expanded soybean mixture, where the weight ratio of puffed soybean material to water is 1 g:(30-60) ml. The method further comprises performing adsorption purification after the enzymatic hydrolysis of the squid solution and the soy protein solution and/or concentrated operation. The squid plate is subjected twice to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the conditions of the two supercritical carbon dioxide extractions are the same or different, preferably the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the squid plate includes the pressure of 10-45MPa at 35-60 degrees C for0.5-3 hours with the carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-40 l/hour, the soybean is subjected twice to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the conditions of the two supercritical carbon dioxide extractions are the same or different, preferably, the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the soybean includes the pressure of 10-45 MPa at 35-60 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours with the carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-40 L/hour. The subcritical water extraction of the squid plate after the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 100-150 degrees C with the pressure of 0.5-40 MPa for 2-4 hours having the flow rate of 5-70L/hours, where the ratio of material to liquid is 1 g:(30-60) ml. The subcritical water extraction on the soybean extracted by the supercritical carbon dioxide is carried out at 100-150 degrees C with the pressure of 0.5-40 MPa for 2-4 hours having the water flow rate of 5-70 l/hours, where the ratio of material to liquid is 1 g:(30-60) ml. The ethanol extraction on the squid plate after the subcritical water extraction is carried out by using 60-90% ethanol concentration at 50-60 degrees C with the pH is 8-9, where the weight ratio of the material to ethanol is 1:(8-12), and conditions for carrying out ethanol extraction of the soybean after the subcritical water extraction include 60-90% ethanol concentration at 50-60 degrees C with the pH of 8-9,where the weight ratio of material to ethanol is 1:(8-12). The enzymatic hydrolysis of the squid solution and the soybean solution is carried out using a double enzyme, preferably alkaline protease and a flavor protease, where the weight ratio of the squid solution and the soybean protein solution is 1:(0.5-1.5), the weight ratio of the total amount of the squid protein solution and the solubilized protein solution to alkaline protease and flavor proteases is 100:(1-5):(1-5). The conditions of carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis using the double enzyme include adjusting the mixture of squid protein and soy protein solution to a pH of 8-10, adding alkaline protease and flavor protease, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 25-45 degrees C for 1-3 hours, inactivating alkaline protease and flavor protease at 90-100 degrees C for 4-10 minutes, reducing the temperature to 40-50 degrees C to obtain super-defatted squid-soybean mixed peptide liquid. The enzymatic hydrolysis using double enzyme is carried out by performing enzymatic hydrolysis uisng alkaline protease and flavor protease for 1-3 hours, performing pressurization operation to assist enzymatic hydrolysis by adjusting the temperature to 70-80 degrees C and pressurize to 2-4 kgf /cm2 for 10-20minutes, immedicately reducing the pressure to 0 kgf/cm2 within 3 seconds, where at least one pressurization operation is carried out during enzymatic hydrolysis. The method further comprises adsorption purification carried out at 20-35 degrees C under the pH 2.5-5 using activated carbon, where the weight ratio of activated carbon to liquid to be purified is 1:(5-15), and concentrating under vacuum of 82.7-90.6 KPa at 45-55 degrees C for 10-15 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-L05C; B04-N01; B04-N02; B11-B03A; B12-M07; B12-M12N; B14-F01; B14-F02; D02-A03A; D03-F06; D05-A02C; D05-C12; D05-H13

PN CN108619320-A
TI Extraction of tea polyphenol from tea leaves, involves pulverizing frozen tea leaves, mixing pulverized tea leaves with distilled water, freezing tea pulp, homogenizing, extracting, and treating extract with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU YAN G
   YAN Z
AE ANHUI LIUCHI CHUNCUI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201882327G
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of tea polyphenols from tea involves picking tea leaves, washing the tea leaves, placing the leaves in a freezer at less than -45 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, pulverizing the frozen tea leaves using an ultrafine pulverizer, mixing the pulverized tea leaves with distilled water to obtain tea pulp, freezing the tea pulp to less than -45 degrees C for 5-7 minutes in a freezer, removing the tea pulp, maintaining the tea pulp in a water bath for 30 minutes, homogenizing the tea pulp in a high-pressure homogenizer to obtain a tea homogenate, extracting the tea homogenate in a microwave extraction reactor to extract caffeine, and treating the extract with supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Extraction of tea polyphenol from tea leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and harmless, and enables efficient extraction of tea polyphenol with high purity and high yield by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology and microwave extraction technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The homogenization is performed at 4-6 degrees C and power of 50-80 kW under pressure of 50-70 MPa. The supercritical extraction process is performed at 50-80 degrees C under pressure of 16-30 MPa for 2-3 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the tea leaves to distilled water is 1:5-1:15.

TI Greenhouse smart weather management system, has sensor device provided with temperature sensor, soil humidity sensor and carbon dioxide concentration sensor, and controller for controlling spraying device, curtain and fan.
AU LIU Q
AE SHAANXI GAOHUA ZHIBEN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201830796A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sensor device provided with a DS18B20-type temperature sensor, a soil humidity sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor. The temperature sensor, the soil moisture sensor, a photo-resistor and the carbon dioxide concentration sensor are electrically connected with a controller. The controller is connected with a heating device and a water pump. The controller controls a spraying device, a curtain and a fan. A window shade is provided with a stepping motor. The stepping motor is connected with the controller.
   USE - Greenhouse smart weather management system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can control chip implementing data collection by using the sensors and the controller, automatically set environment parameter and satisfy different crops growing environment requirement and automatically manage greenhouse crop growing environment. The system is simple in structure, and has clear features and better application and popularization values.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The controller is provided with a multi-path A/D converter and a STC12C5A60S2 chip.

PN JP2022067702-A
TI Manufacture of mineral water involves reacting calcium carbonate and carbon dioxide in water to produce hydrogen carbonate calcium, and mixing mineral raw liquid and desalted water to obtain mineral water.
AU KONDO K
AE MITSUBISHI CHEM AQUA SOLUTIONS CO LTD (MITU-C)
GA 2022658435
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing mineral water involves (a) preparing a mineral stock solution in which calcium carbonate and carbon dioxide are reacted in water to produce calcium hydrogen carbonate, where the concentration of calcium hydrogen carbonate dissolved in water is greater than or equal to 0.5% with respect to the saturation solubility at 20 degrees C and (b) mixing the mineral stock solution and desalinated water.
   USE - The method is useful for producing mineral water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of mineral water, and prevents mixing of calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves reacting calcium carbonate and carbon dioxide with carbonated water having a carbonic acid concentration of greater than or equal to 500 mg/L. The method involves preparing the carbonated water by supplying carbon dioxide using an air supply membrane. The step (a) involves supplying carbon dioxide to the mixed solution so that the pH of the mixed solution containing water and calcium carbonate is 6.5-7. The step (a) further involves mixing at least one of a potassium salt and a magnesium salt to prepare the mineral stock solution. The method further involves performing a solid-liquid separation step of removing a solid substance from the mineral stock solution using a solid-liquid separation device in the subsequent stage of the step (a). Preferred Components: The air supply membrane is a hollow fiber membrane having a porous layer and a non-porous layer.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN US2021138401-A1; US11291950-B2
TI Treating acid gases using molten alkali metal            borates and associated methods of separation involves            exposing sorbent comprising salt in molten form, to            environment containing non-carbon dioxide acid            gas.
AU HALLIDAY C G
   HARADA T
   HATTON T A
AE MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 202169789A
AB    NOVELTY - Treating acid gases using molten alkali metal                borates and associated methods of separation                involves exposing sorbent comprising salt in molten                form, to environment containing non-carbon dioxide                (CO2) acid gas such that portion of non-CO2 acid                gas interacts with sorbent and portion of non-CO2                acid gas is removed from environment.
   USE - Method for treating acid gases using molten                alkali metal borates and associated methods of                separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to increase capacity, and                maintains ability to sequester and release                relatively large amounts of non-CO2 acid gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The salt in molten                  form comprises alkali metal borate, MxB1-xO1.5-x,                  where M is alkali metals, B is boron, O is oxygen                  and x is 0-1. The alkali metal comprises lithium                  (Li), sodium (Na), potassium (K) and mixture. The                  non-CO2 acid gas comprises sulfur monoxide (SO),                  sulfur dioxide (SO2), nitrogen dioxide (NO2),                  hydrogen sulfide (H2S), sulfur trioxide (SO3),                  nitric oxide (NO), nitrous oxide (N2O),                  dinitrogen trioxide (N2O3), dinitrogen tetroxide                  (N2O4), dinitrogen pentoxide (N2O5) and carbonyl                  sulfide (COS). The solution contains calcium                  ions.Preferred Conditions: The environment is at                  temperature of 200degreesC. The multiple non-CO2                  acid gases interacts with sorbent such that                  portion of multiple non-CO2 acid gases are                  removed from environment. The captured non-CO2                  acid gas forms solid suspended in liquid sorbent                  and is upconcentrated by physical separation. The                  physical separation uses cross-flow filter,                  centrifugation, crystallization and                  sedimentation. The cross-flow filter is operated                  at temperature of 200degreesC. The adding of                  solution results in precipitation of calcium                  sulfate.Preferred Process: The portion of sorbent                  containing portion of non-CO2 acid gas is removed                  from environment. The captured non-CO2 acid gas                  is released in gas phase in separate environment                  and upconcentrated. The release is driven by                  change in partial pressure of non-CO2 acid gas                  and change in temperature in second environment                  relative to first environment. The solution is                  added to environment to precipitate portion of                  sorbent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC J01-E03; E31-H02; E31-F01A; E31-F01B; E31-N05B2; E11-Q02C; E31-Q06

PN CN111871408-A
TI Direct type specific-scheme heterojunction            catalyst useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide.
AU ZHANG Z
   LV L
   LU H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2020B2278S
AB    NOVELTY - Direct type Z-scheme heterojunction catalyst,                is claimed. The catalyst is prepared by taking the                catalyst which forms heterojunction as starting                material, then placing in pressure vessel, sealing                after adding water, where aqueous phase and                catalyst are not in direct contact, then collecting                the sunlight and injecting into the pressure                vessel, and then performing hydrothermal treatment                for 1-3 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The direct type Z-scheme heterojunction                catalyst is useful in photocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has more uniform composition                distribution, better phase contact, and improved                performance. The method: achieves purpose of                driving by solar energy from catalyst preparation                to catalyst application; and is environmentally                friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrothermal treatment                  condition includes: sunlight intensity of 50-70                  mW.cm-2, concentration ratio of 400-1000,                  temperature of 300-700 degrees C and water vapor                  pressure of 0.4-2.4 MPa. Preferred Components:                  The catalyst which forms heterojunction is                  bismuth tungstate or iron oxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111056977-A
TI Preparing dicyandiamide comprises e.g. mixing lime nitrogen, water and carbon dioxide to hydrolyze and carbonize to produce monocyanamide aqueous solution, heating monocyanamide aqueous solution and polymerizing.
AU ZHOU Y
   LIU Y
   MA Z
AE SHIZUISHAN PENGSHENG CHEM CO LTD (SHIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202039325P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dicyandiamide comprises (1) mixing lime nitrogen, water and carbon dioxide to hydrolyze and carbonize to produce monocyanamide aqueous solution, (2) heating monocyanamide aqueous solution and polymerizing to produce the dicyandiamide aqueous solution, and (3) adding a small amount of polyacrylamide and polyethyleneimine composite flocculant to the dicyandiamide solution to flocculate and filter impurities, and (4) crystallizing dicyandiamide aqueous solution, dehydrating, drying and packaging as a product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dicyandiamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the quality of dicyandiamide; meets the quality requirements of electronic grade dicyandiamide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material comprises 15-20 pts. wt. lime nitrogen and 100-120 pts. wt. water, 0.010-0.015 pts. wt. polyacrylamide, and 0.015-0.022 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine. In the step (3), the raw material comprises 0.012 pts. wt. polyacrylamide , and 0.018 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine. Preferred Method: In the step (1), adding lime nitrogen and water to the slurry kettle, hydrolyzing the lime nitrogen to form a calcium cyanamide solution, adding carbon dioxide gas to the carbonization kettle, and carbonizing the calcium cyanamide to a monocyanamide solution.

TI Preparation of marked plant by preparing a box,            placing plant, preparing marking gas which is            isotope-labeled carbon dioxide gas, and standing so            that plant absorbs the marking gas to form marked            plant.
AU LIANG Y
   LI X
AE UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
   UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
   UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
GA 2020259206
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of marked plant comprises                preparing a box, placing plant that has the ability                of photosynthesis, preparing marking gas which is                an isotope-labeled carbon dioxide gas, and standing                for a preset time so that plant absorbs the marking                gas to form marked plant.
   USE - The method is for preparation of marked                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method marks the carbon element in the                plant during growth process, can control the                concentration of carbon-13C dioxide in the box, and                improves utilization rate of carbon-13C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The standing is done for                  6-12 hours. The preparation of marking gas is                  done by placing container in a box with acid                  solution, and adding isotope-labeled sodium                  carbonate solution. The marked plant is prepared                  by pretreating marked plant by killing, drying                  and crushing, adding to muffle furnace, adding                  sufficient nitrogen, and pyrolyzing to 350-500                  degrees C at rate of 15 degrees C/minute for 2-4                  hours.

Preferred Process: The barium sulfate is added to neutral amino acid-containing aqueous solution in precipitation process. The water is added to neutral amino acid-containing aqueous solution before introducing carbon dioxide gas in precipitation process.

AU YAMAKITA R
   MOROZUMI H
   NIIZUMA T
AE ISHIZUKA GLASS KK (ISHT-C)
GA 2022D1925S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of glass involves using                biologically-derived calcium source as a glass raw                material.
   USE - Production of glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of                glass by using calcium source derived from organism                such as eggshell, shell or shellfish eating                phytoplankton. The amount of limestone dug out from                limestone mine is reduced, and the amount of carbon                dioxide generated due to excavation, purification                and conveyance of limestone is reduced. Since                carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is fixed by                plants, the carbon dioxide emission amount from the                glass factory can be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view                illustrating the production of glass. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   10Glass melting furnace
   11Raw material hopper
   12Glass product
   13Heat storage chamber
   14Chimney

PN CN111233816-A
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves catalyzing epoxide compounds using carbon dioxide catalysts.
AU GUO K
   LI J
   LI Z
   YAN R
   TONG H
   ZHANG C
   HU Y
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202051887Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves catalyzing an epoxide compound (III) or (IV) using a carbon dioxide catalyst (I) and (II).
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables simple and economical preparation of cyclic carbonate using the catalyst having excellent activity, recycling property, and selectivity under mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves catalyzing an epoxide compound of formula (III) or (IV) using a carbon dioxide catalyst of formula (I) and (II).
   R1-R5 = H, or linear or branched 1-4C alkyl;
   R6,R7 = H, or linear or branched 1-5C alkyl optionally substituted by halo, hydroxy, hydroxyphenyl, vinyl, allyloxy, or 1-4C alkoxy, or optionally substituted phenyl;
   n = 1 or 2;and
   M = O or -CH2-.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compounds (I) and (II) are chosen from 12 compounds, e.g. chosen from 5 compounds of formulae (V)-(IX). The epoxide compounds (III) and (IV) are chosen from 16 epoxide compounds, e.g. compounds of formulae (X)-(XIV). Preferred Conditions: The method is performed at 20-140 degrees C, preferably 120-140 degrees C, and initial pressure of carbon dioxide is at 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 0.2 MPa. Preferred Composition: The method is performed using 0.1-10 mol%, preferably 2-5 mol% catalyst. Preferred Process: The method involves adding compounds (III) and (IV) to the reactor using the catalyst in which carbon dioxide replaces the air in the reactor, filling the reactor with carbon dioxide to an initial pressure and raising the temperature to 20-140 degrees C, and reacting for 1-24 hours, and cooling solutions.
   DEFINITION - Preferred Definitions:
   R4,R5 = H, or methyl.

   XIONG H
AE XIAN REJEE IND DEV OILFIELD SUPPLIES CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019092761
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur and carbon dioxide corrosion inhibitor comprises 20-40% propylene-allyl sulfonate, 10-20% alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, 5-15% ethanol, 5-10% hexamethylene tetramine, 10-20% ethylene glycol and 5-15% water.
   USE - The corrosion inhibitor useful in oil pipelines (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion inhibitor solves leakage and rupture of crude oil pipeline caused by sulfur, carbon dioxide corrosion resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing inhibitor, comprising adding propylene-allyl sulfonate and ethanol to reaction kettle and uniformly stirring, adding hexamethylene tetramine and alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, continuously stirring and reacting, adding ethylene glycol and water until completely mixed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inhibitor comprises 35% propylene-allyl sulfonate, 15% alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, 15% ethanol, 5% hexamethylene tetramine, 15% ethylene glycol and 15% water. Preferred Method: The propylene-allyl sulfonate is prepared by pressurizing and heating sodium allylsulfonate and 2-propylene-1-sodium sulfonate salt to 90 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen gas, dripping methacrylic chloride, adjusting pH of the solution to 7-10 using alkali, reacting for 10-12 hours, vacuum extracting and distilling unreacted methacrylic chloride, adding ethanol and filtering, evaporating ethanol. The synthesis of propylene-allyl sulfonate carried out in a reaction kettle provided with a thermometer, a stirrer, and a reflux condenser tube and nitrogen-protecting device. The preparation of propylene-allyl sulfonate specifically comprises protecting nitrogen gas and pressurizing to 2-4 atmospheres, dripping 1-5 mol methacrylic chloride each kg of sodium allylsulfonate and 2-propylene-1-sodium sulfonate salt, adjusting pH of the solution using sodium hydroxide solution and adding 150-300 kg ethanol in each ton of sodium allylsulfonate and 2-propylene-1-sodium sulfonate salt.

AE YANG M (YANG-Individual)
GA 201884639N

   USE - Tea beverage.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing tea beverage, which involves:
   (A) using final content (W/V) of the tea in the beverage to 0.5-2.0%;

   (C) filling tea liquid preparation with 2-4 g/L of carbon dioxide gas, standing, dispensing aseptically to obtain tea beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The tea is unfermented tea and/or semi-fermented tea and/or whole fermented tea. The filtrate obtained is mixed each time, a flavoring agent is added, diluted to a solution containing tea (W/V) 0.5-2.0%. The tea is placed in purified water at pH 3-4, 30-40 degrees C. The colloid mill circulation treatment, the homogenized tea liquid is added, and the flavoring agent allowed to be added to the beverage is added, diluted to 0.5-2.0% tea solution containing tea (W/V) according to the actual amount of tea used in the production process.

PT P
PN CN109651092-A
TI Preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises e.g. distilling high temperature coal tar or medium and low temperature coal tar and washing and dephenolizing by sodium hydroxide aqueous solution in a vertical washing tower.
AU LI Y
   SU G
AE SHANXI YONGDONG CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019391449
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises (a) distilling high temperature coal tar or medium and low temperature coal tar, (b) transporting phenol sodium salt solution having a temperature of 60-80 degrees C;(c) decomposing sodium phenolate by carbon dioxidein the circulating gas to form crude phenol and sodium carbonate and then passing into the decomposition tower; (d) transporting primary crude phenol by static separation to the upper part of the acidification tower with two layers of packing through the acidification tower raw material pump; and (e) passing carbon dioxidegas that has not participated in the reaction from the top of the decomposition tower and the acidification tower, passing the carbon dioxidebalance tank, and then passing to the bottom of the decomposition tower and the acidification tower by the circulating fan to be recycled by establishing a gas circulation system for carbon dioxidedecomposition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is clean, energy saving and environmentally-friendly; and reduces equipment corrosion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises (a) distilling high temperature coal tar or medium and low temperature coal tar to obtain a phenol oil fraction having a phenol content of 6-20%, washing and dephenolizing by sodium hydroxide aqueous solution in a vertical washing tower, where sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and the phenol oil fraction are in reverse flow contact with phenol and sodium hydroxide to form a phenol sodium salt solution, (b) transporting phenol sodium salt solution having a temperature of 60-80 degrees C and a concentration of 20- 40% to the upper part of the decomposition tower having two layers of packing and passing through the packing layer from top to bottom in reverse contact with the circulating gas with the active component of carbon dioxiderising in the tower, controlling carbon dioxideconcentration at 15-20%;(c) decomposing sodium phenolate by carbon dioxidein the circulating gas to form crude phenol and sodium carbonate and then passing into the decomposition tower at 60-80 degrees C according to the difference in specific gravity of the crude phenol and sodium carbonate solution static separation for 6-8 hours, completing the separation of crude phenol and sodium carbonate solution, where the separated sodium carbonate solution flows to the storage tank and crude phenol flows from the intermediate tank of the crude phenol; (d) transporting primary crude phenol by static separation to the upper part of the acidification tower with two layers of packing through the acidification tower raw material pump under the pressure of not less than 0.4 MPa, and flowing from top to bottom through the packing layer from the bottom of the tower, where active component is a reverse contact of a circulating gas of carbon dioxideand sodium phenolate is further decomposed by carbon dioxidein the circulating gas and flows into the bottom of the acidification tower; and (e) passing carbon dioxidegas that has not participated in the reaction from the top of the decomposition tower and the acidification tower, passing the carbon dioxidebalance tank, and then passing to the bottom of the decomposition tower and the acidification tower by the circulating fan to be recycled by establishing a gas circulation system for carbon dioxidedecomposition. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for clean production device of gas circulating coked crude phenol comprising a decomposition tower, an acidification tower, a primary crude phenol intermediate tank, an acidification tower raw material pump, a sodium carbonate solution intermediate tank, a sodium carbonate solution pump, carbon dioxide balance tank, acidification bubbler and circulation fan, decomposition tower and acidification tower adopt garland packing tower, where decomposition tower is installed on the ground, the top gas pipeline is connected with the gas pipeline at the top of the acidification tower, and the bottom gas pipeline is connected with the gas pipeline at the bottom of the acidification tower, bottom crude phenol pipeline is connected with the upper feed pipe of the primary phenol intermediate tank, the bottom sodium carbonate solution pipeline is connected with the upper tank of the intermediate tank of the sodium carbonate solution, the acidification tower is installed on the ground, and the bottom crude phenol pipeline and the finished product are thick, phenol pipeline is connected, the bottom sodium carbonate pipeline is connected with the upper feed pipe of the middle tank of the sodium carbonate solution, the primary crude phenol intermediate tank is installed on the ground, the upper feed pipeline is connected with the lower crude phenol pipeline at the lower part of the decomposition tower, and the lower outlet pipeline and the acidification tower are connected to raw material pump inlet pipe connection, acidification tower raw material pump installed on the ground, its inlet pipe and one lower crude phenol intermediate tank outlet pipe is connected, the outlet pipe is connected with the upper acid feed tower feed pipe, the sodium carbonate solution intermediate tank is installed on the ground, and the upper feed pipe is distributed with the decomposition tower and the acidification tower lower sodium carbonate solution pipeline, the lower part outlet pipe is connected to the inlet pipe of the sodium carbonate solution pump, the sodium carbonate solution pump is installed on the ground, the inlet pipe is connected with the lower outlet pipe of the middle tank of the sodium carbonate solution, the outlet pipe is connected with the sodium carbonate solution delivery pipe, the carbon dioxide balance tank is installed in one the high platform, the gas inlet is connected to the decomposition tower and the acidification overhead gas confluence pipeline, and the gas outlet is connected to the circulation fan gas inlet, the acidification bubbler is installed at the bottom of the acidification tower and connected with the 95% fresh carbon dioxide supply pipeline on the ground, the gas inlet is connected to a carbon dioxide balance tank and the gas outlet is connected to the decomposition tower and the gas inlet junction of the acidification tower bottom.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the clean production device of gas circulating coked crude phenol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The for preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises arranging an acidifying bubbler with a spray hole at the bottom of the acidification tower, and the liquid material of the acidified bottom liquid is further purified by using carbon dioxide gas having a concentration greater than 95%, bubbling acidification and controlling the carbon dioxide concentration in the acidification bottom gas in the range of 20-25%, separating acid-bubbled material for 8-10 hours according to the difference in specific gravity between crude phenol and sodium carbonate at a temperature of 90-95 degrees C to complete the separation of crude phenol and sodium carbonate solution, and the separated sodium carbonate, where solution flows to the storage tank, and the crude phenol reaches the quality requirement. The for preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises in order to maintain the balance of the system carbon dioxide, the acidification bubbler at the outlet of the circulation fan and the bottom of the acidification tower is supplemented with carbon dioxide gas having a concentration greater than 95%. The for preparing gas circulating coked crude phenol comprises in order to balance the pressure of the gas circulation system, a single-calling and single-suction valve is arranged on the upper part of the carbon dioxide balance tank body, subjecting system to positive pressing, the single-exhalation valve opens the exhaled gas to release the positive pressure, subjecting system to the negative pressure, opening the single-suction valve to release the negative pressure and ensuring that the system pressure safely operated within a certain range and reducing the gas discharge. Preferred Components: The diameter and the inspiratory volume of the single suction valve are determined according to the phenol sodium salt treatment amount and the supplemented fresh carbon dioxide concentration, and the designed suction pressure is -500 Pa and the system pressure is low, the air is sucked to balance the system pressure not lower than -500Pa. The diameter and expiratory volume of single expiratory cut according to the phenol sodium salt treatment amount and the supplemented fresh carbon dioxide concentration, single exhalation valve design exhalation pressure is +500Pa and when the system pressure is high, the gas is discharged to the tar distillation system, furnace is incinerated and the system pressure is not greater than +500Pa.

   LEEHEENAM
AE SANG WON IND CO LTD (SANG-Non-standard)
GA 202023750Y
AB    NOVELTY - The generator has a housing (100) which is directly coupled and attached. An oxygen discharge port (200) is formed on an upper surface of the housing to be combined with a gas mask or a mask to be coupled to an oxygen suction port. An exhalation intake (300) is formed on a lower portion of an oxygen outlet and connectable to an exhalation outlet. An oxygen generator (400) is attached to the housing and generates oxygen by reacting with carbon dioxide. An absorbent space (500) is formed in a lower end of the housing. An outer diameter of oxygen outlet and an outer diameter of the exhalation suction port are different from each other. The exhalation intake port and the oxygen exhaust port include a one-way air valve (210,310) that allows air flow only in one direction. A funnel having a spout of a certain length is included in upper portion of the absorption space. A duckbill valve is made of an elastic soft material, which is closed by elasticity after allowing fluid to pass through.
   USE - Portable oxygen generator attached to mask.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the portable oxygen generator supplies high-purity oxygen to the user, the human damage is reduced. Since the predetermined length of movement path is provided to the inhaled carbon dioxide, the constant reaction time is secured with the oxygen generator, and the oxygen generation efficiency is improved. Since the one-way air valve is provided at the exhalation intake and oxygen outlets, the inhaled carbon dioxide is forced to pass through a certain path. The constant reaction length and reaction time is secured, so that the constant amount of oxygen generation is ensured. Since the housing has an elliptical or rectangular shape with a long vertical distance, the oxygen mask wearer exhales through the lower exhalation intake when breathing, and the carbon dioxide flow path becomes longer in the process of discharging oxygen to the upper oxygen outlet. The reaction time with the oxygen generator increases, so that more oxygen is generated. The by-products are prevented from being sucked into the mask. Even if by-products are generated in the process of oxygen generation, the oxygen mask is safely turned over, so the oxygen mask is safely used. Due to the structure in which a large number of vent holes are formed to enter and exit, the reaction area that the oxygen generating agent comes into contact with the catalyst and carbon dioxide is widened, so that the reaction occurs quickly, and when the carbon dioxide enters the housing, the oxygen is rapidly generated. The portable oxygen generating device has a relatively simple structure, which is easy to manufacture and inexpensive according to manufacturing and maintenance.
