   CHEN T
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 201975275C
AB    NOVELTY - Distributed energy-based organic waste water resource recycling system comprises distributed energy station (1), organic wastewater tank (3), supercritical water oxidation reactor (8), high pressure gas-liquid separator (11), atmospheric pressure gas-liquid separator (13), gas separator, liquid oxygen storage tank and a carbon dioxide storage tank. The heating pipe of distributed energy station is connected to user end of load, and organic waste water outlet of user end of load is connected to the organic wastewater tank. The outlet of organic wastewater tank is connected to cold side inlet of first regenerator. The cold side outlet of first regenerator is connected to the cold side inlet of the second regenerator. The cold side outlet of second regenerator and heating duct of distributed energy station are connected with first heat source.
   USE - Distributed energy-based organic waste water resource recycling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The distributed energy-based organic waste water resource recycling system combines production of distributed energy station with treatment of organic wastewater, realizes recycling of organic wastewater resource, improves comprehensive energy utilization efficiency of distributed energy stations, realizes added value of distributed energy stations, and creates comprehensive energy service mode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating a distributed energy-based organic waste water resource recycling system, which involves:
   (A) operating whole system, using distributed energy station for sending out power supply to load user end or grid, and using distributed energy station for supplying steam or hot water to heat load user through heating pipeline;
   (B) using load user end for discharging organic wastewater through pipeline to organic wastewater tank, using water pump for extracting organic wastewater in organic wastewater tank and passing it through first regenerator and second regenerator, heating it to 400-500 degrees C by first heat source after combining it with steam or hot water from distributed heat station, and entering it into supercritical water oxidation reactor, where internal pressure of supercritical water oxidation reactor is stabilized at 22-25Mpa;
   (C) withdrawing liquid oxygen from liquid oxygen storage tank by liquid oxygen pump, pressurizing it to system pressure, separating it from high pressure gas-liquid separator by third regenerator, mixing high-pressure gas with tube, heating gas to a temperature of 300-350 degrees C through second heat source, introducing it into supercritical water oxidation reactor to react organic wastewater and oxygen in supercritical water to produce a gas product having carbon dioxide; and
   (D) exchanging gas product with third regenerator, entering it into high-pressure gas-liquid separator for gas-liquid separation, decompressing it through pressure reducing valve, entering atmospheric pressure, separating liquid by gas-liquid separator, sending liquid to load user end and distributed energy station to realize recycling of organic wastewater resources, where separated gas is carbon dioxide, and sending carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide storage tank, and when pressure in carbon dioxide storage tank reaches a preset value then liquefaction is taken out as an industrial product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of distributed energy-based organic waste water resource recycling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   Atmospheric pressure gas-liquid separator (13)

TI Electric vehicle battery thermal runaway self-extinguishing system, has pressure reducing unit that reduces injection pressure of carbon dioxide injected when thermal runaway of battery module occurs.
AU PARK J
AE HYUNDAI MOBIS CO LTD (HYMO-C)
GA 202287761X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a battery case with a storage space for accommodating a battery module (10) and a cooling channel (200) formed through the storage space of the battery case. A refrigerant injection unit (230) is allocated to the battery module and opens and closes according to the temperature of each battery module. A pressure reducing unit is provided for reducing the injection pressure of carbon dioxide when thermal runaway of battery module occurs. A battery cover is provided to close the space of battery case. A first valve and a second valve of the refrigerant injection unit are a bimetal, a mechanical valve, and an electromagnetic valve.
   USE - Electric vehicle battery thermal runaway self-extinguishing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system controls temperature by cooling the battery module through the carbon dioxide refrigerant flowing in the cooling channel, and the refrigerant injection unit of the corresponding cooling channel is opened during thermal runaway of the battery to flow the carbon dioxide refrigerant into the module or inside the battery pack, so that the battery cells can be cooled when thermal runaway occurs without need of a separate configuration for sensing heat of battery cells, and damage to the battery case can be prevented by using the pressure reducing unit when high-pressure of the carbon dioxide refrigerant is sprayed as a fire extinguishing agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrical vehicle battery thermal runaway self-extinguishing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a perspective view for explaining a battery cell cooling and thermal runaway self-extinguishing system.
   Battery module (10)
   Cooling channel (200)
   Refrigerant injection unit (230)

TI Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract of Isochrysis sp used to e.g. modify human skin and/or human hair, and to increase pigmentation of human skin and/or human hair comprising carotenoids, chlorophylls, free fatty acids.
AU HERRMANN M
   STUHLMANN D
   GAEBLER S
   TITZE N
   WESELOH A
   KRAELING R
AE SYMRISE AG (SYMR-C)
GA 202281587M
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract of Isochrysis sp and/or Tisochrysis sp comprising (a) not more than 1.5 wt.% carotenoids, (b) not more than 2 wt.% chlorophylls, and (c) at least 15 wt.% free fatty acids, where the extract has a gardner color value of less than or equal to 9 at an extract concentration of 0.018% in oil.
   USE - The extract is useful for: influencing or modifying human skin and/or human hair; increasing pigmentation of human skin and/or pigmentation of human hair; reducing oxidative stress induced changes when applied to the skin; for increasing skin hydration; and improving the cutaneous barrier properties of the skin, where the oxidative stress is UV-induced oxidative stress, or Maillard reaction induced oxidative stress (all claimed). Test details are described but no results for the extract of the claim is given.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: (1) obtaining the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract, comprising extracting cell material of Isochrysis sp, and/or Tisochrysis sp, preferably Tahitian Isochrysis with supercritical carbon dioxide, where the extraction comprises (a) exposition of the cell material to the supercritical carbondioxide for up to 24 hours, preferably for up to 8 hours at 30-70 degrees C and at 150-290 bar; and (2) a cosmetic composition comprising the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract and at least one further ingredient comprising tanner, skin moisturizing agents, stabilizer, and/or antioxidants, UV filters.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The extract comprises less than or equal to 1 wt.%, preferably less than or equal to 0.5 wt.% carotenoids, less than or equal to 1 wt.%, preferably less than or equal to 0.5 wt.% chlorophylls, at least 20 wt.% free fatty acids, 27 wt.% long chain alkenones. The extract further comprises less than or equal to 2 wt.%, preferably 0.5 wt.% phospholipids. Preferred Method: The cell material of Isochrysis sp. and/or Tisochrysis sp is obtained by a method comprising steps (a) cultivating Isochrysis sp. cells and/or preferably of Tahitian Isochrysis cells, (b) harvesting the cells to obtain completely or substantially intact cell material, (c) optionally washing the cell material of step (b) once or multiple times, to obtain washed, substantially or completely intact cell material, washed, substantially or completely intact cell material, (d) optionally freeze-drying the cell material of step (b) and/or step (c), or (e) optionally spray-drying the cell material of step b and/or (c), or (f) optionally thermal drying the cell material of step (b) and/or (c), or (g) optionally vacuum drying the cell material of step (b) and/or (c), and (h) optionally milling the cell material of step (b) and/or step (c) and/or step (d), (e), (f) and/or step (g).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tanner is dihydroxyacetone and/or erythulose.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Freeze-dried Isochrysis sp., was pre-treated using a mechanical three roller mill and then extracted with supercritical carbon dioxide in a supercritical fluid extraction unit equipped with a 1l extraction vessel at different conditions i.e. 223 g biomass, extraction for 1 hour at 150 bar and 60 degrees C followed by extraction for another 2 hours at 290 bar and 60 degrees C. The extracts were collected in a separator.

   HONG X
AE CHENGDU YITONG SEAL CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202272323T
AB    NOVELTY - The recoverable gas sealing device has a static ring and a moving ring, where the static ring is connected to a spring seat through a spring, so that the static ring moves relative to the spring seat and automatically resets in a dynamic cavity formed by assembling a static ring seat and the spring seat to limit the movement range of the static ring. A sealing boss (112) is provided on the end surface of the static ring in sealing contact with the moving ring, when the static ring moves towards the moving ring. The pressure between the moving rings is reduced, so that the mechanical seal between the static ring and the moving ring is realized.
   USE - Recoverable gas sealing device, particularly a supercritical carbon dioxide sealing device, for for sealing supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The sealing device can be automatically adjusted, when the system generates large leakage, it can effectively prevent the leakage of carbon dioxide into the bearing box for protecting the box to avoid the damage range is enlarged. When the pressure of the carbon dioxide of the leakage side disappears, the static ring moves away from one side of the movable ring to the original position by the automatic reset mechanism which avoids the damage of the mechanical sealing structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a recoverable gas sealing method.

A first catalyst (11) having a hydrogen oxidizing ability is arranged on the gas inlet side, and a second catalyst (12) having a hydrocarbon producing ability is arranged on the gas outlet side. TI Method for carbon sequestration using organic mineral layers after in-situ pyrolysis, involves providing pressure of high-pressure CO2 is higher than formation pressure, and sealing well pipes so that carbon dioxide is stored underground.
AU YANG D
   KANG Z
   ZHANG H
   ZHAO J
   WANG L
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021B43651
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing the room temperature water through the high temperature ore bed to exchange heat. The retained water is formed in the ore bed to dissolve calcium and magnesium ions, during the heat exchange process. The mining well and inject CO2 into the injection well close to make the CO2 and calcium magnesium ion solution fully react chemically. The mining well is opened , high-temperature fluid is injected into the injection well, the temperature in each wellbore is monitored , the mining well is closed and high-pressure CO2 injected into the injection well when the temperature of the ore bed reaches normal temperature, so that the inside of the porous and high-permeability ore layer is filled with liquid carbon dioxide. The temperature of the high-temperature fluid is 150-200degreesC.The pressure of the high-pressure CO2 is higher than the formation pressure. The well pipes are sealed so that carbon dioxide is stored underground.
   USE - Method for carbon sequecining of organic ore layer after in-situ pyrolysis used for combustion of fossil energy fuel such as coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively and stably sealing the underground of the organic ore layer after in-situ pyrolysis with high efficiency and high efficiency recycling energy, and provides good condition for reaction of carbon dioxide and calcium-magnesium ion thus reducing the carbon dioxide emission.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the method for carbon sequestration using organic mineral layers after in-situ pyrolysis.

   Outer well (2)
   Ore bed (3)

GA 202103862X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying (S100) the collected waste paper into the waste paper input unit. The waste paper carried in the waste paper input unit, is measured (S200) by a waste paper measuring unit. The waste paper is crushed (S300), which has is measured by the waste paper measuring unit, by a waste paper shredding unit. The amount of carbon dioxide generated during incineration of the waste paper crushed, is converted (S400) by the waste paper shredding unit into carbon money by a control unit. The carbon money converted by the control unit, is paid (S500) by the carbon money discharge unit.
   USE - Carbon conversion method of printing paper money.
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of carbon dioxide generated during incineration from the collected waste paper is collected without incineration, and the amount of carbon dioxide generated during incineration is converted into carbon money, thus generating an effect of reducing environmental pollution and energy use.

   Step for measuring the waste paper carried in the waste paper input unit by a waste paper measuring unit (S200)
   Step for crushing the waste paper (S300)
   Step for converting the amount of carbon dioxide generated during incineration of the waste paper crushed by the waste paper shredding unit into carbon money by a control unit (S400)
   Step for paying the carbon money converted by the control unit by the carbon money discharge unit (S500)

TI Use of carbonic acid, or aqueous carbon dioxide in            extraction of lithium used in e.g. battery,            electronics, lubricating grease, metallurgy,            pyrotechnic, air purification, optics, polymer            chemistry, military and medicine applications.
AU HAYNES B
   MANN J
   HAINES B
   MANN YA
AE UNIV SYDNEY (UNSY-C)
   UNIV SYDNEY (UNSY-C)
   UNIV SYDNEY (UNSY-C)
   UNIV SYDNEY (UNSY-C)
   NOVALITH TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (NOVA-Non-standard)
   NOVALITH TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (NOVA-Non-standard)
   NOVALITH TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (NOVA-Non-standard)
GA 201870329W
AB    NOVELTY - Use of carbonic acid, or aqueous carbon                dioxide in extraction of lithium from                lithium-containing ores, is claimed.
   USE - Use of carbonic acid, or aqueous carbon                dioxide in extraction of lithium from                lithium-containing ores (all claimed). Uses include                but are not limited to ceramic, glass, battery,                electronic, lubricating grease, metallurgy,                pyrotechnic, air purification, optics, polymer                chemistry, military and medicine                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonic acid or aqueous carbon dioxide                enables efficient extraction of lithium with high                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) extraction of lithium, which involves                milling the lithium-containing ore(s) to a                predetermined average particle size to provide a                milled crude ore, optionally calcining the milled                crude ore at a predetermined calcining temperature,                optionally performing milling upon the calcined                milled crude ore, providing an aqueous suspension                of the milled crude ore (calcined or non-calcined),                at a predetermined solid concentration, subjecting                the milled crude ore to an extraction medium by a                predetermined partial pressure of carbon dioxide, a                predetermined extraction temperature, for a                predetermined time, and obtaining technical grade                lithium carbonate/lithium bicarbonate in a                solution;
   (2) lithium, which is extracted from                lithium-containing ore(s); and
   (3) extraction apparatus, which consists of a                component for providing an aqueous suspension of                the lithium-containing ore(s), at the predetermined                solids concentration, a component for subjecting                the lithium-containing ore(s) to an extraction                medium, and a component for obtaining the technical                grade lithium carbonate/lithium bicarbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                micro-fluidized bed reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium-containing                  ores are chosen from spodumene, eucryptite,                  petalite, amblygonite, lepidolite, zinnwaldite,                  lithiophilite, tryphilite, hectorite and                  jadarite, preferably beta -spodumene. Preferred                  Composition: The yield ratio of the extracted                  lithium to the crude lithium is 1-99%. The                  impurities have concentration of 0.5-40 mol% in                  the lithium. The lithium is obtained as lithium                  carbonate or bicarbonate with purity of 85 mol%                  or more. Preferred Properties: The predetermined                  solid has concentration of 0.1-60 weight/weight%.                  The predetermined average particle size is                  0.1-1000 mu m. Preferred Process: The method                  involves calcining at 900 degrees C to convert                  alpha -spodumene to beta -spodumene, extracting                  impurities comprising sodium, potassium,                  magnesium, calcium, manganese, iron, aluminum,                  and silicon from the lithium-containing ore(s),                  concentrating the lithium carbonate,                  precipitating the lithium carbonate, filtering                  the precipitated lithium carbonate from a mother                  liquor and to separate an aqueous medium                  comprising water, mineral acid(s), organic                  acid(s), alkaline salt(s), and ionic liquid(s).                  The concentration step involves standard                  concentration method chosen from addition of                  concentrator, evaporation, reverse osmosis,                  electrodialysis, liquid-liquid extraction,                  selective adsorption and solid state extraction                  and/or membrane separation. The method is                  adaptable and/or scalable to a continuous flow or                  batch-type process. The mineral acids are                  optionally supplemented with a predetermined                  partial pressure of carbon dioxide 0.1-300 bar.                  Preferred Conditions: The predetermined                  extraction temperature is 20-350 degrees C. The                  predetermined time is 1-1000 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a cooler (21) for                cooling the second fluid, and provides the second                fluid circulation system (20) which is made to flow                through the second fluid cooled with the cooler by                second piping, and is returned to the cooler. The                second piping of a second fluid circulation system                is connected to the tank (12) and the pump (13)                while connecting to the heat exchanger (11). The                second fluid circulation system heat-exchanges the                second fluid and the first fluid in the tank and                the pump, and cools the first fluid while                heat-exchanges the second fluid and the first fluid                in the heat exchanger and cools the first fluid.                The first fluid is a carbon dioxide. The heater                (14) is comprised by heating the first fluid so                that the supercritical-carbon-dioxide fluid is                generated while a first fluid circulation system                (10) pressure-rises the first fluid with the                pump.
   USE - Fluid supply apparatus for                supercritical-carbon-dioxide fluid                generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature of fluid is maintained stably.                The presence or absence of cooling of a pump or the                refrigerating capacity with respect to a pump can                be adjusted by providing an on-off valve or a flow                control valve in a pump connection part and/or a                pump bypass part. The temperature rise of the                coolant supplied to the cooling jacket is                suppressed by the tank main body and the tank cover                being covered with the thermal insulation                case.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic of the fluid                supply.
   Fluid supply apparatus (1)
   Fluid circulation system (10,20)
   Heat exchanger (11)
   Tank (12)
   Pump (13)
   Heater (14)

PN CN114011221-A
TI New gaseous sugar is colorless, odorless and soluble in water, prepared by colliding protons and electrons with carbon dioxide and reacting.
AU ZHAO J
   GAO R
   XU M
AE XU M (XUMM-Individual)
GA 202232889L
AB    NOVELTY - Gaseous sugar (I), is new. (I) is prepared by colliding protons and electrons with carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain final product. (I) is gaseous at room temperature and pressure, colorless, odorless and soluble in water, and has molecular weight 30.002. (I) has ignition point at 797degrees Celsius, calorific value 3955 kcal/kg and pH 7.
   USE - The preparation method is useful for preparing (I) in carbon dioxide-containing waste gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): is novel, reliable, has no secondary pollution, turns waste into treasure, realizes environmentally friendlly and harmless disposal of carbon dioxide, and achieves outstanding substantive features and remarkable progress in resource utilization, when compared with the prior art; is gaseous at room temperature and pressure, colorless, odorless and soluble in water, has molecular weight 30.002, has ignition point at 797degrees Celsius, calorific value 3955 kcal/kg and pH 7.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gaseous sugar of formula (H-C-OH) (I), is new. (I) is prepared by colliding protons and electrons with carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain final product. (I) is gaseous at room temperature and pressure, colorless, odorless and soluble in water, and has molecular weight 30.002. (I) has ignition point at 797degrees Celsius, calorific value 3955 kcal/kg and pH 7.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) Preparation of (I);
   (2) Generated protons and electrons, which are derived from the proton conductor and electron conductor of the composite photosynthetic material respectively, and formed an electron gradient, and the reaction formula is as given in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises sequentially inputting carbon dioxide in a special device, splitting water to generate protons, electrons and oxygen, carbon dioxide colliding with protons and electrons, and obtaining final product.Preferred Method: The process of splitting water to generate protons, electrons and oxygen, comprises transparent body of the composite photosynthetic material absorbing the light quantum emitted by the lamp tube and focusing and converting the frequency to increase the quantum energy intensity; it can be emitted to the composite photosynthetic material when the energy intensity reaches the excited state; after a series of electron transfers, the water is finally split into protons, electrons and oxygen. The process step of the carbon dioxide colliding with protons and electrons is that carbon dioxide colliding with protons on the proton conductor and electrons on the electron conductor to produce carbon monoxide and water, where the reaction formula is as given in the specification. The processing steps of (I) are continuously colliding carbon monoxide with protons on the proton conductor and electrons on the electron conductor to obtain (I), where the reaction formula is as given in the specification.Preferred Components: The composite photosynthetic material is a cake-shaped transparent body with a diameter of 0.5-1 microm and a thickness of 0.1-0.2 microm. The flow velocity of the carbon dioxide gas is not less than 10 m/s, and the wind pressure is not less than 3000 Pa.

TI Separation of carbon dioxide gas from mixture of gases, e.g. nitrogen and oxygen gas, involves cyclic two stage adsorption process.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has two sets of two adsorber beds (A1,A2,B1,B2) along with a buffer vessel (D1), vacuum pump (VP1), compressor (C1), product valves (2,4,9,11), feed valves (1,3,8,10, vent valves (5,7,12,14,15,17), equalization valves (6,13), and check valve (16), which allows separation of carbon dioxide gas from a mixture of gases, such as flue gas to a concentration as high as 99 percent purity. The layers of adsorbent material are provided in each of the adsorbber beds. The feed gas is supplied under pressure. The feed gas is compressed by compressor to the desired pressure for set of the process. A process cooler is provided to cool the feed gas at the compressor discharge.
   USE - Apparatus for separating carbon dioxide from combustion flue gas mixture using cyclic two stage adsorption process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method integrates two sets of adsorption beds, used in a configuration that allows two stage separation and purification of gases. The process uses a buffer vessel to store product gas from one stage to before it enters a second stage as a feed. As such, the process can be used to separate any gas mixture and specifically but not limited to carbon dioxide from combustion flue gases.

PN WO2021140063-A1; TW202136183-A; IN202247035297-A; CN114929656-A; KR2022123230-A; BR112022012645-A2; EP4087833-A1
TI Preparing ethylene glycol by supplying gas            composition comprising ethylene oxide and carbon            dioxide to ethylene oxide absorber and contacting with            lean absorbent in presence of catalysts that promote            carboxylation and hydrolysis.
AU BLACK J R
   WARD G J
   EMBORSKY C P
   BASTINGS R G H
   BASTINGS R G H L
   EMBOSKEY C P
   BASTINGS R H H
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 202179738E
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene glycol is prepared from ethylene by                supplying 1st gas composition comprising 1-7 mol.%                ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide to ethylene oxide                absorber through gas inlet, where absorber                comprises absorption section of vertically stacked                trays, and allowing gas composition to pass upwards                through the absorption section; supplying lean                absorbent to the top of the absorption section and                allowing the lean absorbent to pass downwards                through the absorption section; intimately                contacting gas composition with lean absorbent on                trays in the absorption section in the presence of                greater than or equal to 1 catalysts that promote                carboxylation and hydrolysis to produce a fat                absorbent stream comprising ethylene glycol and                ethylene carbonate; withdrawing fat absorbent from                absorber; and withdrawing 2nd gas composition                comprising 1-10 mol.% carbon dioxide from the top                of the absorber. The temperature of the 1st gas                composition is 60-160 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of ethylene glycol from                ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - A higher ethylene oxide concentration in the                1st gas composition results in higher monoethylene                glycol production in the reactive absorber, and                less ethylene oxide leakage to the 2nd gas                composition and to the reactive absorber bottoms.                By controlling the amount of carbon dioxide that is                consumed or released in the ethylene oxide                absorber, the capacity and cost of a carbon dioxide                absorber column can be reduced. The optimal                operation of the reactive absorber provides reduced                concentration of ethylene oxide in the absorber off                gas, reduced production of acetaldehyde byproduct,                increased selectivity to monoethylene glycol, and                reduced production of ethylene glycol                oligomers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The 1st gas                  composition comprises ethylene oxide, ethylene,                  oxygen, carbon dioxide, and water vapor, and                  preferably is derived from the reactor product of                  an ethylene oxide reactor, in which ethylene is                  reacted with oxygen in the presence of a catalyst                  in a reactor to form ethylene oxide. The 1st gas                  composition comprises 1-5 mol.%, preferably 2-4                  mol.% ethylene oxide. The 2nd gas composition                  comprises 1-5 mol.%, preferably 1.5-2.4 mol.%                  carbon dioxide. Fat absorbent comprises less than                  or equal to 100 ppmw, preferably less than or                  equal to 10 ppmw ethylene oxide. The 2nd gas                  composition comprises less than or equal to 100                  ppmv, preferably less than or equal to 10 ppmv                  ethylene oxide. Catalyst comprises iodide, and                  amount of lean absorbent added to the absorber is                  0.16-0.46 kg of lean absorbent feed per total kg                  of gas feed. Amount of iodide catalyst in the                  absorbent feed stream is 7.5-15 wt.%. Preferred                  Conditions: Temperature of 1st gas composition is                  60-120 degrees C, preferably 70-100 degrees C.                  Lean absorbent feed temperature is 50-160 degrees                  C, preferably 85-120 degrees C. Preferred                  Process: The method further comprises feeding                  water to the absorber, where the ratio of water                  feed rate to gas feed rate is 0.01-0.15 kg water                  in liquid feed per kg of gas feed.

AU WANG B

GA 201859940Q
AB    NOVELTY - A flexible fibrous lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material comprises a metal/carbon composite material including a metal substrate and a carbon coating.
   USE - Flexible fibrous lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material used in flexible device.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high energy density, good cycle stability and fCN108321382Alexibility. Flexible device can work normally under different bending angle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the flexible fibrous lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material comprising:
   (A) placing the metal wire in acetone, absolute ethanol and deionized water, carrying out ultrasound for 30 minutes, drying at 30-60 revolutions/minute, and winding into a spring shape;
   (B) mixing iron source compound, nickel source compound and carbon nitrogen source compound, dissolving in an organic solvent to form a mixture solution, where the mass ratio of the iron source compound or nickel source compound to the carbon nitrogen source compound is 1:5-1:10, and the mass ratio of the carbon nitrogen source compound to organic solvent is 1:10-1:20; and
   (C) growing nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube by chemical vapor deposition on surface of the metal wire, setting the metal wire in the quartz tube, heating the quartz tube using the tubular furnace at 800-950 degrees C, passing the mixture solution into the quartz tube at 200-500 standard cubic centimeter per minute through the inert gas, reacting for 10-20 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain the flexible fibrous carbon dioxide lithium battery anode material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal substrate is a metal wire having a diameter of 120-300 mu m and a length of 10-50 cm. The carbon coating layer is nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube containing 1-3 wt.% nitrogen, and having a pipe diameter of 50-200 nm and a length of 2-100 mu m. The metal wire is titanium wire, nickel wire, iron wire, aluminum wire, copper wire, or zinc wire. The inert gas is nitrogen or argon gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The iron source compound is ferrocene or iron phthalocyanine. The nickel source compound is dicyclopentadienyl nickel, nickel phthalocyanine, and/or nickel oxide. The nitrogen source compound is imidazole, dimethyl imidazole, diethyl tetramethyl imidazole, and/or imidazole. The organic solvent is acetonitrile, ether, acetone, chloroform, pyridine, or benzene.

   USE - The additive is useful for enhancing mutual                solubility of carbon dioxide and crude oil and                reducing viscosity of crude oil in recovery of                carbon dioxide gas flooding process of deep heavy                oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The additive has high-viscosity and                low-permeability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Displacement oil additive comprises                solubilizing agent and oil-soluble viscosity                reducer, weight ratio between solubilizing agent                and oil-soluble viscosity reducer is 2-8:1;                solubilizing agent is p-methylbenzoate compound                i.e. ethyl 4-methoxybenzoate of formula (I); and                oil-soluble viscosity reducer is long chain                ester-vinyl acetate-acrylamide copolymer i.e.                methyl 4-acetoxy-5-carbamoyl-2-ethylheptanoate of                formula (II).
   n = 2-12;
   R = 12-18C saturated alkyl chain; and
   i, j and k = 2-6:1-5:2-8.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of displacement oil additive for enhancing mutual                solubility of carbon dioxide and crude oil and                reducing viscosity of crude oil in recovery of                carbon dioxide gas flooding process of deep heavy                oil comprising injecting or prepositioning                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio                  between solubilizing agent and oil-soluble                  viscosity reducer is 4:1. The solubilizing agent                  is propyl methyl benzoate. The viscosity reducer                  is octadecyl acrylate-vinyl acetate-acrylamide                  copolymer. The used amount of                  azobisisobutyronitrile is 5 wt.% of raw material.                  When concomitant injection is injected, then                  total amount of oil displacement additive is 3-8%                  of the total amount of carbon dioxide used for                  flooding; and then when the slug method is                  injected, total amount of oil displacement                  additive is 5-30% of the total amount of carbon                  dioxide in pre-slug layer. Preferred Method: The                  preparation method of long chain ester-vinyl                  acetate-acrylamide copolymer comprises taking                  alkyl acrylate, vinyl acetate and acrylamide as                  raw materials in a molar ratio of 2-6:1-5:2-8,                  taking toluene as solvent, adding initiator                  azobisisobutyronitrile, stirring reaction, and                  refluxing at 65-75 degrees C for 8-12 hours under                  nitrogen atmosphere to obtain terpolymer, then                  naturally cooling to room temperature, adding                  methanol to precipitate copolymer, filtering and                  vacuum drying at 50 plus minus 3 degrees C to                  obtain long chain ester-vinyl acetate-acrylamide                  copolymer. The preparation method of octadecyl                  acrylate-vinyl acetate-acrylamide copolymer                  comprises adding 80 ml toluene as reaction                  solvent, (total of 10 g octadecyl acrylate, vinyl                  acetate and acrylamide in a molar ratio of 2:3:5)                  and 5 wt.% initiator azobisisobutyronitrile in                  150 ml three-necked flask equipped with                  condenser, thermometer and mechanical stirrer,                  replacing air in reaction flask with nitrogen                  gas, stirring and heating at 70 degrees C,                  refluxing at constant temperature 10 hours,                  removing reaction flask, naturally cooling at                  room temperature, then adding 30 ml methanol to                  precipitate copolymer, filtering and vacuum                  drying at 50 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain                  product. The method comprises mixing solubilizing                  agent and viscosity reducer and stirring at room                  temperature for more than 1 hour.

AU KIM M H
AE KIM M H (KIMM-Individual)
   ELEPHANT CO LTD (ELEP-Non-standard)
   EL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ELTE-Non-standard)
GA 201823390A
AB    NOVELTY - The diffuser (100) has a handle connection portion (120) formed at a rear end of the case (110) and a welding tip connection portion (130) formed at a front end of the case. The gas ejection ports are formed on the outer surface of the welding tip connection portion at an oblique inclination angle toward the front. A gas introduction chamber is formed from a rear end of the welding tip connecting portion, and a gas diffusion electrode is mounted in the center of the gas introduction chamber.
   USE - Diffuser of welding torch using inert gas e.g. carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - As the diffuser is capable of forming a shield of a certain pattern without a pressure drop during injection by injecting carbon dioxide toward the tip of the nozzle, the welding characteristics is improved. The gas is uniformly injected using the gas introducing chamber while forming a high pressure. The shield is prevented from being easily broken. As the carbon dioxide is injected while forming a high pressure, the consumption of carbon dioxide is drastically reduced. The welding is allowed to be performed more smoothly by forming the stable shield so that even a non-skilled person does not have to carry out a large amount of welding work.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the diffuser for the welding torch.
   Diffuser (100)
   Case (110)
   Handle connection portion (120)
   Welding tip connection portion (130)
   Gas blower (140)

PN CN107629443-A; CN107629443-B
TI Producing microcellular foaming-conductive thermoplastic elastomer material comprises e.g. taking thermoplastic elastomer material, conductive filler, dispersant and hemp stalk core powder, blending and extruding to obtain sheet.
AU ZHEN W
   XIULEI J
   CHUNPING C
   WANG Z
   JIANG X
   CHEN C
AE ZHEJIANG XINHENGTAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201810153K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing microcellular foaming-conductive thermoplastic elastomer material comprises (i) taking thermoplastic elastomer material, conductive filler, dispersant and hemp stalk core powder; (ii) blending and extruding the thermoplastic elastomer material, conductive filler, dispersant and hemp stalk core powder to obtain sheet; and (iii) placing the sheet in the foaming die, increasing the temperature to the foaming temperature, passing the supercritical carbon dioxide gas in the sheet, releasing carbon dioxide gas in the foaming die and foaming the sheet to obtain microporous structure.
   USE - The method is useful for producing microcellular foaming-conductive thermoplastic elastomer material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low conductive filler content, is simple, economical and high production efficiency. The material has good mechanical properties and pressure-sensitive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing microcellular foaming-conductive thermoplastic elastomer material comprises (i) taking 100 pts. wt. thermoplastic elastomer material, 3- 9 pts. wt. conductive filler, 0.2-0.8 pts. wt. dispersant and 2-9 pts. wt. hemp stalk core powder, where the conductive filler is the mixture of carbon black and/or metal powder and carbon nano tube; (ii) blending and extruding the thermoplastic elastomer material, conductive filler, dispersant and hemp stalk core powder to obtain sheet; and (iii) placing the sheet in the foaming die, increasing the temperature to the foaming temperature, passing the supercritical carbon dioxide gas in the sheet until the supercritical carbon dioxide gas reaches saturated state, quickly releasing carbon dioxide gas in the foaming die and rapidly foaming the sheet to obtain microporous structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive filler is a mixture of carbon black and carbon nanotubes and the weight ratio of carbon black to carbon nanotubes is 1:1-30:1.The thermoplastic elastomer material is atleast one. The step (i) includes100 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane, 6 pts. wt. multi-wall carbon nano-tube, 0.4 pts. wt. carbon black, 0.5 pts. wt. hemp stalk core powder and 6 pts. wt. dispersant. Preferred Method: In step (iii) the foaming temperature is 110 degrees C, supercritical carbon dioxide gas pressure is 10 MPa and maintaining the inflated state for 30 minutes.

PN CN114681561-A
TI Purifying active ingredient in glossy privet            compound by weighing each component, drying, crushing,            sieving, adding powder to extracting kettle, taking            carbon dioxide as agent, adding neutral and alkaline            entrainer, extract mixture and filtering, recycling,            obtaining extracts and mixing.
AU HONG L
   GUO K
   WANG W
AE HONZ PHARM CO LTD (HONZ-Non-standard)
GA 202289959C
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying method of an active ingredient in a                glossy privet compound involves weighing each                component and drying, crushing and sieving to                obtain powder, adding the powder to the extracting                kettle, taking carbon dioxide as an agent, and                adding neutral entrainer for supercritical                extraction, extracting mixture is separating from                gas-liquid, and filtering to obtain solid matter                and solution, recycling neutral entrainer from the                solution to obtain an extract (A), adding the solid                to the extraction kettle again, taking carbon                dioxide as the agent, and adding alkaline entrainer                for supercritical extraction, extracting mixture is                separating from gas-liquid, filtering, taking the                liquid, recycling alkaline entrainer from liquid to                obtain an extract (B), and mixing the extract (A)                and the extract (B) to obtain a mixed                extract.
   USE - The method is used for purifying an active                ingredient in a glossy privet compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the beneficial effect that                is, two times of supercritical extraction under                different conditions extract active ingredients                with different solubility respectively, so that the                extracted product can retain various active                ingredients as much as possible.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In crushing step, drying                  method is freeze-drying, and the freeze-drying                  temperature is -50&#8451; to -60&#8451;,                  the degree of vacuum drying is 10-30 MPa, and the                  moisture content of each component is 10-20%,                  where after drying, the components are sieved;                  the mesh number of the sieve is &#8805;24. In                  first extracting step, neutral entrainer is the                  ethanol aqueous solution of 75-95 vol.%, carbon                  dioxide flow rate is 15-30 ml/minutes, the                  extraction temperature is 50-75&#8451;, the                  pressure is 45-60 MPa, the extraction time is                  90-120 minutes, and the addition amount of                  neutral entrainer is 6-12% the powder mass. In                  second extracting step, alkaline entrainer is the                  one containing 1-3% metal hydroxide, 30-50 vol.%                  ethanol aqueous solution, carbon dioxide flow                  rate is 15-30 ml/minutes, extraction temperature                  is 40-60&#8451;, pressure is 30-45 MPa,                  extraction time is 90-120 minutes, where the                  addition amount of alkaline entrainer is 6-12%                  powder quality.Preferred Components: The metal hydroxide                  comprises sodium hydroxide and potassium                  hydroxide.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN WO2022136046-A1
TI Producing thermally stable thermoplastic            polyoxazolidinone compound, comprises mixing            diisocyanate, organic compound having two epoxide            groups, catalyst and solvent, and contacting this            polyoxazolidinone with carbon dioxide.
AU ELING B
   AUFFARTH S
   QUELL A
   WEBER M
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202284895X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) a thermally stable                thermoplastic polyoxazolidinone compound,                comprises: mixing (a) at least one diisocyanate,                (b) at least one organic compound having two                epoxide groups, (c) at least one catalyst,                catalyzing the formation of oxazolidinone groups                and optionally (d) at least one solvent to form a                polyoxazolidinone, and contacting this                polyoxazolidinone with carbon dioxide, where the                amounts of diisocyanate (a) and organic compound                having two epoxide groups (b) are chosen that the                resulting polyoxazolidinone has epoxide end groups                and at least 1 wt.% carbon dioxide, based on the                total weight of diisocyanate (a) and organic                compound having two epoxide groups (b), is                contacted with the polyoxazolidinone.
   USE - The method is useful for the producing a                thermally stable thermoplastic polyoxazolidinone                compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides thermoplastic                polyoxazolidinone with improved thermal stability                which is processing easier and the recycling of the                products obtained is improved due to better                remeltability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) thermoplastic polyoxazolidinone,                obtainable according to the method (M1);
   (2) producing (M2) molded part, comprising                melting a polyoxazilodinone obtainable by method                (M1), introducing the melt into a mold, cooling the                polyoxazolidinone in the mold to temperatures below                its melting point and demolding the obtained molded                part; and
   (3) molded part obtainable by the method                (M2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A solvent (d) is used and                  the polyoxazolidinone formed is dissolved in the                  solvent (d) in form of a polyoxazolidinone                  solution and this polyoxazolidinone solution is                  contacted with carbon dioxide. The contacting of                  the polyoxazolidinone solution with carbon                  dioxide is performed at 130-280 degrees C. The                  polyisocyanates (a) comprises at least 70 wt.% at                  least one isomer of diphenylmethane diisocyanate,                  based on the total weight of the isocyanate (a).                  The carbon dioxide is contacted with the                  polyoxazolidinone solution by pursing carbon                  dioxide into the polyoxazolidinone solution or by                  mixing the carbon dioxide and the                  polyoxazolidinone solution. After contacting the                  polyoxazolidinone solution with carbon dioxide,                  the solvent is removed. Preferred Components: The                  molar ratio of epoxide groups in the compound                  having two epoxide groups (b) to isocyanate                  groups in the diisocyanate (a) is 0.9:1-1.1:1.                  The compound having two epoxide groups (b)                  comprises polyglycidyl ether of bisphenol A,                  bisphenol F and/or novolak. The solvent (d)                  comprises N-methylol-2-pyrrolidone,                  1,2-dichlorobenzene,                  1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone, sulfolane,                  N,N'-dimethylpropyleneurea,                  N,N'-dimethylethyleneurea, and/or                  dimethylacetamide.
   EXAMPLE - 4 l Vessel was equipped with a reflux                condenser, an electronic thermometer, an overhead                stirrer and a gas tube. The epoxide, catalyst and                1,2-dichlorobenzene were precharged into the vessel                and the isocyanate dissolved in                1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone (DMI) was added                using a metering unit via one of the necks. As                metering unit a membrane pump was used. The vessel                was blanketed under nitrogen and the content was                stirred continuously. The solvent DMI that was used                throughout the work was stabilized with 10 ppm of                diglycol bischloroformate. The 1-butyl-1                methylpiperidinium chloride was stored and handled                under nitrogen. A mixture was prepared containing                1256.6 g DMI and 301.12 g                diphenylmethane-4,4'-diisocyanate (MDI) (R2.1). The                vessel was charged with 2.31 g 1-butyl-1                methylpiperidinium chloride, 2.15 g Irganox 1076                (RTM: Highly efficient, non-discoloring, sterically                hindered phenolic, primary antioxidant stabilizer),                416.58 g bisphenol A diglycidyl ether and 416.58 g                1,2-dichlorobenzene and stirred at 155 degrees C.                After dissolution solution R2.1 was fed into the                vessel over a period of 90 minutes, where during                the first 60 minutes. The temperature was kept at                155 degrees C and for the remaining 30 minutes. The                temperature was increased to 190 degrees C. After                competition of the addition the temperature was                increased to 200 degrees C for 15 minutes followed                by a further increase in temperature to 210 degrees                C for another 15 minutes. Subsequently the content                was cooled to 170 degrees C and polyoxazolidinone                (without endcapping) was taken. Subsequently carbon                dioxide from a gas cylinder was bubbled underneath                the surface of the liquid at a rate of 30 g/hours                for 90 minutes. polyoxazolidinone (with endcapping)                was taken.

PN CN114426297-A
TI Continuously preparing pseudo-boehmite for            residual oil hydrogenation catalyst, comprises e.g.            preparing low-concentration and high-concentration            aluminum-containing alkaline solution, and mixing            high-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline            solution and carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas.
AU DUAN R
   GUAN Y
   ZHANG C
   HAN Y
   LIU T
   YUAN S
   WANG Y
AE SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202272947Y
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously preparing pseudo-boehmite                comprises (i) preparing low-concentration                aluminum-containing alkaline solution and                high-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline                solution respectively, (ii) adding                low-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline                solution in first reaction kettle, and passing into                mixed gas I containing carbon dioxide to carry out                shallow carbonization reaction, (iii) mixing                high-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline                solution and carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas II                into second reaction kettle for carbonization, and                controlling pH value of slurry in second reaction                kettle, (iv) entering slurry to third reaction                kettle when slurry in second reaction kettle                reaches or is higher than overflow port, adding                slurry in parallel to react, and controlling pH                value of slurry in third reaction kettle, and (v)                entering slurry to aging tank for aging when slurry                in third reaction kettle reaches or is higher than                overflow port, and washing and drying filter                cake.
   USE - The pseudo-boehmite is useful in preparing                residual oil hydrogenation catalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes continuous production.                The pseudo-boehmite: meets the requirements of                residue hydrogenation catalyst carrier after                roasting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                pseudo-boehmite prepared by the above-mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution is sodium                  metaaluminate solution and/or potassium                  metaaluminate solution, preferably sodium                  metaaluminate solution. The caustic ratio of                  low-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline                  solution is 1.15-1.35, preferably 1.25-1.35. The                  concentration of low-concentration                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution calculated                  as aluminum oxide is 40-100 g aluminum oxide/l,                  preferably 50-80 g aluminum oxide/l, and the                  caustic ratio of the high-concentration                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution is                  1.25-1.75, preferably 1.35-1.65. The                  concentration of the high-concentration                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution, calculated                  as aluminum oxide is 150-350 g aluminum oxide/l,                  preferably 160-250 g aluminum oxide/l. In the                  step (ii), the volume fraction of carbon dioxide                  in the mixed gas I containing carbon dioxide is                  30-60%. The shallow carbonization is pH value of                  the slurry after carbonization is lower than the                  pH value of the solution before carbonization by                  0.5-1.5. In the step (iii), the                  high-concentration aluminum-containing alkaline                  solution and the carbon dioxide-containing mixed                  gas II are mixed in a gas-liquid mixing pump. The                  gas-liquid mixing pump is dissolved air pump,                  gas-water mixing pump, air-mixing pump or                  gas-liquid pump. The volume fraction of carbon                  dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing mixed                  gas II is 50-90%. In the step (v), the content of                  gibbsite in the obtained pseudo-boehmite is less                  than 1.5%, preferably less than 0.5%.Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the                  carbonization reaction time is 1-5 minutes, and                  controlling pH value of slurry in the second                  reaction kettle to 4-6. In the step (iv), the                  temperature of the reaction is 40-70&#8451;,                  preferably 45-65&#8451;, and controlling the                  pH value of slurry in the reaction kettle to be                  8.5-9.5 by adjusting the flow rate of the slurry                  in step (ii). In the step (v), aging is carried                  out at 50-95&#8451; for 30-120 minutes, and                  drying is carried out at 100-150&#8451; for                  6-10 hours.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                process for adjusting the production of biomethane                according to the operation of a biomethane recovery                unit.

PN CN113967475-A
TI Preparing plasma-induced layered nickel-cobalt double metal hydroxide photocatalytic material useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. preparing nickle cobalt layered double hydroxides material, and carrying out plasma treatment using dielectric barrier discharge device.
AU CHU J
   ZHU X
   YANG J
   ZHOU A
   XU H
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202222228N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing plasma-induced layered nickel-cobalt double metal hydroxide photocatalytic material, comprises (i) adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate into deionized water, mixing, then adding glycerol and methanol, and stirring mixed solution at room temperature, (ii) pouring the mixed solution obtained in step (i) into the autoclave for heating reaction, collecting the precipitate, washing and drying to obtain nickle cobalt layered double hydroxides (NiCo-LDH) material, (iii) dissolving the NiCo-LDH material obtained in step (ii) with ethanol, uniformly fixing on quartz plate and placing in dielectric barrier discharge device for plasma treatment to obtain amino group-containing NH2-NiCo-LDH material.
   USE - The plasma-induced layered nickel-cobalt double metal hydroxide photocatalytic material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) to generate carbon monoxide (CO) (claimed)
   ADVANTAGE - The plasma-induced layered nickel-cobalt double metal hydroxide photocatalytic material: has good photocatalytic performance and stable structure; exhibits strong carbon dioxide adsorption capacity; has narrower band gap and larger specific surface area, and higher photocatalytic reduction efficiency. The method has simple process, readily available raw materials, mild reaction, quickness and high efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the ratio between nickel nitrate hexahydrate and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 4:1, adding deionized water to prepare nickle ion concentration of 0.017 M, and then adding deionized water, glycerol and methanol in volume ratio of 9:8:43. In step (i), the stirring is carried out at 1000-1200 revolutions/minute for 30-60 minutes. In step (ii), the reaction is carried out at 180degrees Celsius for 24 hours. In step (ii): the washing is carried out by centrifuge at rotating speed of 12000-13500 revolution/minute; the detergent is deionized and absolute ethanol; and the drying is carried out at 60degrees Celsius for 6-12 hours. In step (iii), in the dielectric barrier discharge device, the working atmosphere is ammonia gas, and the gas flow rate is maintained at 100-300 ml/minute. In step (iii), the input power of dielectric barrier discharge device is 50-100 W; the processing time is 5-30 minute.

   ZOLOTUKHIN N N

GA 2021128083
AB    NOVELTY - Invention refers to medicine, namely to physiotherapy, angiology and can be used for complex treatment of obliterating atherosclerosis of lower limbs arteries (OALLA). Sequential laser exposure (LE) is performed, followed by a rest break and a dry carbon dioxide bath (DCDB) procedure. LE is pulsed infrared low-intensity laser radiation (IR LLR) with pulse power (PP) of 5 W, pulse repetition rate (PRR) of 80 Hz. Region of projection of lumbar sympathetic ganglia - at level L2-L4 is paravertebral and on a projection area of the most superficially located neurovascular bundles of legs and foot - popliteal cavities and internal ankles. Exposure time for each zone is 4 minutes. During subsequent DCDB, carbon dioxide gas feed rate is 15 l/min, relative humidity of steam-air mixture is 100%. Therapeutic course consists of 10 daily consecutive procedures of the LE complex with DCDB. IR LLR from the first procedure act on said zones in turns. After the laser exposure with a rest period of 1 hour, a DCDB procedure is performed, with a bath temperature of 30C, by supplying carbon dioxide to the bath to concentration of 30-35 vol%. DCDB length is 20 minutes.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method provides correction of functional disorders, higher clinical effectiveness and quality of life of OALLA patients ensured by combined exposure of DCDB and IR LLR.

PN WO2020131958-A1; US2022081737-A1
TI Isolating metal involves contacting raw material comprises metal with lixiviant in first reactor to form soluble metal salt and extracted raw material, separating soluble metal salt, and contacting soluble metal salt.
AU WYRSTA M D
AE LIXIVIA INC (LIXI-Non-standard)
   LIXIVIA INC (LIXI-Non-standard)
GA 202057183S
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating metal involves contacting raw material comprises metal with lixiviant in first reactor to form soluble metal salt and extracted raw material, separating soluble metal salt from the extracted raw material, contacting soluble metal salt with source of carbon dioxide in second reactor to form insoluble metal carbonate and regenerated lixiviant, separating the insoluble metal carbonate from the regenerated lixiviant. The regenerated lixiviant is returned to the first reactor. The insoluble metal carbonate is collected. The unmodified air is the primary source of carbon dioxide utilized in formation of insoluble metal carbonate.
   USE - Method for isolating metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to isolate metal is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for reducing content of greenhouse gas in atmospheric air, which involves contacting raw material comprises metal in the form of insoluble metal salt or oxide with lixiviant in pond reactor to form soluble metal salt and extracted raw material, contacting soluble metal salt with atmospheric air to form purified metal salt and regenerated lixiviant, purified metal salt is essentially insoluble and comprises portion of greenhouse gas, collecting purified metal salt, greenhouse gas is carbon dioxide and purified metal salt is carbonate or bicarbonate of metal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material is selected from lime, dolomitic lime, steel slag, ash, fly ash, post-consumer waste, and mine tailings. The raw material comprises sub-optimal source of the metal. The source of carbon dioxide is unmodified ambient air. The source of carbon dioxide is selected from flue gas, fermentation byproduct, biomass digestion product, carbonate or carbonate solution, bicarbonate or bicarbonate solution, and pure carbon dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The portion of separation of the soluble metal salt from extracted raw material occurs to completion of formation of the soluble metal salt. The portion of separation of the insoluble metal carbonate from regenerated lixiviant occurs to completion of formation of the insoluble metal carbonate. The lixiviant is present in substoichiometric quantities relative to the metal of the raw material. The lixiviant is present in stoichiometric quantities relative to the metal of the raw material. The raw material is re-sized to contacting with the lixiviant. The lixiviant is amine-based lixiviant. The raw material is selected from low grade lime, dolomitic lime, steel slag, ash, fly ash, post-consumer waste, and mine tailings. Preferred Process: The method involves contacting the raw material with the lixiviant occurs over period of from 1 day-3 months, drying the insoluble metal carbonate, arrangement in and exposure to the insoluble metal carbonate to ambient environmental conditions, placing the insoluble metal carbonate in a kiln. The separation is performed by process selected from settling, filtration, centrifugal separation, and clarification. The source of carbon dioxide comprises unmodified air in amount sufficient to provide 80 wt.% of carbon dioxide. The source of carbon dioxide is introduced to the second reactor by one or more of surface exposure, stirring, mixing, sparging, and percolation. The method involves calcining insoluble metal carbonate to generate metal oxide. The method involves separating the soluble metal salt from the extracted raw material. The portion of separation of the soluble metal salt from the extracted raw material occurs to completion of formation of the soluble metal salt. The lixiviant is present in substoichiometric quantities relative to content of the metal in the raw material. The method involves treating the dry purified metal salt in kiln to form calcined purified metal salt, sequestering the purified metal salt, dry purified metal salt, or calcined purified metal salt. The atmospheric air is introduced contacted with the soluble metal salt by one or more of surface exposure, stirring, mixing, sparging, and percolation. The insoluble metal carbonate is calcined to generate metal oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The forceful photocatalytic effect completely decomposes and detoxifies bacteria, mold, virus, etc. to carbon dioxide gas, water, and nitrogen oxide.

The alkali metal carbonate aqueous solution is sodium hydrogen carbonate aqueous solution. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide with respect to titanium oxide is 1:1x 10-8-1:1x 10-5, preferably 1:1x 10-31-1:1x 10-1. Preferred Method: The titanium oxide slurry is obtained by adding a dispersant and/or a thickener to titanium oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The quantum catalyst comprises a dispersant and/or a thickener, which are chosen from carboxylic acid group copolymer, polyacrylic acid, sodium polyacrylate, ammonium polyacrylate, polycarboxylic acid, sodium carboxylate group copolymer, cycloamylose, polyvinylpyrrolidone compound, and cellulose nanofiber.

PN CN109420486-A
TI Zinc zirconium oxide solid solution catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation for methanol synthesis, has solid solution structure having zinc oxide as solute and zirconium dioxide as solvent.
AU LI C
   WANG J
   LI Z
   TANG C
   HAN Z
   LIU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201923391Y
AB    NOVELTY - Zinc zirconium oxide (ZnZrOx) solid solution catalyst has solid solution structure having zinc oxide (ZnO) as solute and zirconium dioxide (ZrO2) as solvent, where Zn/(Zn+Zr) is 5-38 mol.%.
   USE - ZnZrOx solid solution catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation for methanol synthesis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ZnZrOx solid solution catalyst has methanol selectivity of more than 88%, high temperature resistance and sulfur resistance, and maintains a single pass conversion rate of greater than or equal to 10%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the ZnZrOx solid solution catalyst comprising precipitation method, which comprises preparing Zn and Zr metal salt mixed aqueous solution, where total concentration of metal ions is 0.01-2 mol/L, preparing precipitating agent aqueous solution of the same concentration, precipitating in 0-90 degrees C water, controlling the pH at 6-10, dropping the precipitating agent aqueous solution into the metal salt solution or dropping metal salt solution into the precipitating agent aqueous solution or coprecipitating, drying the precipitate at 60-130 degrees C for 4-24 hours, and calcining at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours; and
   (2) use of ZnZrOx solid solution catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation for methanol synthesis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The Zn salt is nitrate, acetate, halide, and/or sulfate. The precipitating agent is ammonia water, ammonium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate,sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The Zr salt is zirconium nitrate, zirconium acetate, zirconium halide, zirconium sulfate, zirconyl nitrate, zirconium oxychloride, and/or zirconium isopropoxide. The calcination is static or flow atmosphere calcination. The calcination atmosphere is air, oxygen, and/or nitrogen. The application method comprises carbon dioxide hydrogenation in pressurized fixed bed continuous flow reactor at raw material gas pressure of 1-10 MPa, preferably 5 MPa, reaction temperature of 240-400 degrees C, preferably 320 degrees C, space velocity of 3000-40000/hour, preferably 24000/hour, and n hydrogen (H2):n carbon dioxide (CO2) molar ratio of 1-8, preferably 3. Before the application, the catalyst is activated using pure hydrogen, nitrogen and/or argon gas at 200-400 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours. The catalyst is tableted, crushed and sieved by 20-80-mesh sieve for evaluation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The Zn/(Zn+Zr) is 10-33 mol.%.

PT P
PN US10144878-B1
TI Producing liquid fuel from landfill gas by performing tri-reforming process on landfill gas by carbon dioxide reforming, steam reforming, water-gas shifting, and methane oxidation, and converting synthesis gas into liquid fuel.
AU KUHN J N
   JOSEPH B
   WALKER D
   GARDEZI S A
   ROBERGE T
AE UNIV SOUTH FLORIDA (UYSF-C)
   KUHN J N (KUHN-Individual)
   JOSEPH B (JOSE-Individual)
   WALKER D (WALK-Individual)
   GARDEZI S A (GARD-Individual)
   ROBERGE T (ROBE-Individual)
GA 201895224N
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid fuel is produced from landfill gas by providing landfill gas to tri-reformer; performing tri-reforming process on landfill gas within the tri-reformer to convert the landfill gas into synthesis gas, where tri-reforming process combines carbon dioxide reforming, steam reforming, water-gas shifting, and methane oxidation, and synthesis gas has hydrogen:carbon monoxide (H2:CO) ratio of approximately 2:1; providing synthesis gas to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis (FTS) reformer; and converting synthesis gas into liquid fuel within the FTS reformer.
   USE - Production of liquid fuel from landfill gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of only two reactors is unique because existing technologies typically require three separate reactors, including a water gas shift reactor, simplifying the process and making the system less costly to construct. The ability to use two reactors instead of three is the result of the conditions within the tri-reformer and the nature of the catalyst, which is specifically suited for a mixture of methane and carbon dioxide found in landfill gas. The unique combination of the conditions and catalyst used in the reactor enable the production of synthesis gas in the desired hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Tri-reformer contains 1st catalyst, and FTS reformer contains 2nd catalyst. The 1st catalyst comprises nickel, magnesium, cerium, and/or zirconium. The 1st catalyst comprises Ce(x)Zr(1-x)-yNizMg, where x, y, and z are integers. The 1st catalyst comprises Ce0.6Zr0.4-8Ni8Mg. The 2nd catalyst is a cobalt-silica catalyst. Preferred Processes: The method further includes providing heat to the tri-reformer generated using solar energy by providing heat generated by a solar collector that focuses the sun's energy on a heating medium within a central receiver. No reactors other than the tri-reformer and the FTS reformer are used to convert the landfill gas into liquid fuel. The method further includes cleaning the landfill gas with a cleaning unit prior to providing the landfill gas to the tri-reformer. The method further includes delivering a portion of the landfill gas from the cleaning unit to a combustion unit that provides heat to the tri-reformer. The method further includes cooling the synthesis gas from the tri-reformer in a heat recovery unit prior to providing the synthesis gas to the FTS reformer. The method further includes removing heat from the FTS reformer using a heat recovery unit. The method further includes refining the liquid fuel from the FTS reformer. The method further includes delivering a portion of the gas from the FTS reformer to a combustion unit that provides heat to the tri-reformer.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-F02E; H06-B; H06-P
IP C10G-002/00; B01J-023/00; B01J-023/78; C07C-001/12

PN CN108654578-A
TI Preparing silane-modified sepiolite absorbing            agent, comprises e.g. mixing sepiolite powder into            hydrochloric acid solution, washing, drying, processing            to obtain processed sepiolite powder, reacting with            anhydrous ethanol and drying.
AU LUO X
   YUAN M
   YUAN S
   LIANG Z
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2018837247
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silane-modified sepiolite absorbing                agent, comprises (i) adding sepiolite powder into                hydrochloric acid solution having concentration of                1-4 mol/l in a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:10-1:30,                then stirring for more than 10 hours, then                separating the sepiolite powder, washing to                neutral, drying and grinding, and then processing                at 200-300 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain                processed sepiolite powder, and (ii) adding                pre-treated sepiolite powder into anhydrous ethanol                in a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:20-1:60, stirring                uniformly, adding aminosilane coupling agent,                heating and refluxing the ethanol, reacting for                more than 10 hours, separating the sepiolite                powder, washing, drying and grinding to obtain                final product.
   USE - The absorbing agent is useful in absorbing                carbon dioxide (claimed).
