   USE - Rescue capsule is used in domestic emergency field and engineering emergency field and is also used for ordinary civil buildings or high-end residences where engineering includes mine, coal mine, or subsea operation or their combination (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to operate rescue capsule which provides oxygen and water for safe-keeping personnel and extends the lifespan of the safety personnel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for rescue capsule, which involves converting the carbon dioxide exhaled by the safety-relief personnel into oxygen recycling through oxygen recycle regeneration system, drinking water circulation recycling system converts urine discharged by safe haven into drinking water recycling and converting the carbon dioxide exhaled by safe haven to oxygen recycling by oxygen recycling system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Conditions: The main body of rescue cabin is equipped with power supply system and daily necessities; preferably where power supply system is lithium-ion battery power supply system and rated voltage of power supply system is 220 Volt. The necessities include satellite phone, inflatable bed, medical kit, tool box, convenient toilet, food or water, or or their combination. The oxygen regenerating device comprises first regenerating device and second regenerating device where first regenerating device comprises sequentially connected Sabatier reactor and electrolytic oxygen making device. The second regeneration device is chemical reaction device which is a reaction device of carbon dioxide and sodium peroxide. The adsorbent is solid amine and/or molecular sieve. The reaction temperature is 120-550 degrees C. The catalyst comprises an active component and a metal oxide matrix. The active component is a transition metal where transition metal is selected from niobium, nickel, cobalt, iron, molybdenum and niobium or their combination. The metal oxide is selected from aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, hafnium oxide and zirconium oxide or their combination. The reagent for deodorizing is ozone. The centrifugation is performed at high speed centrifugation. The water vapor is evaporated water vaporized by supernatant after being subjected to refrigeration processing. The recycled water ratio into the electrolysis oxygen plant 20-80%. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide exhaled by safe haven to oxygen recycling by oxygen recycling system involves placing adsorbent material in caorbo dioxide collection device to collect and concentrate incoming carbon dioxide, entering collected carbon dioxide in Sabatier reactor and reacting to reduce carbondioxide to water, water enters the electrolysis oxygen plant to generate oxygen and hydrogen, and resulting hydrogen returns as a reactant in the Sabatier reactor. preferably, the oxygen recycle regeneration system converts carbon dioxide exhaled by the safe-keeping personnel into oxygen recycling which involves placing the adsorbent material in a carbon dioxide collection device to collect and concentrate the incoming carbon dioxide and collected carbon dioxide enters chemical reaction device and reacts sodium peroxide to generate oxygen. The drinking water circulation regeneration system converts the urine discharged by the evacuation personnel into drinking water for recycling which involves deodorizing and sterilizing urine by deodorizing and disinfecting device. The urine treated enters a centrifugal device and subjected to centrifugal sedimentation. The supernatant is taken out and liquid enters into the freezing device and the vacuum dehumidifying device. The vapor extracted by vacuum is used as recovered water, and portion of the recovered water is used as reactant to enter the electrolysis oxygen generating device. The remaining recovery water passes through disinfection device and is sterilized to obtain drinking water.

PN CN107779345-A
TI Myrtus communis wine composition comprises Myrtus communis juice, glutinous rice or rice, water, citric acid, sodium citrate, edible essence, and carbon dioxide.
AU WANG P
   WANG H
AE SICHUAN FENGHE YELLOW WINE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018234319
AB    NOVELTY - A Myrtus communis wine composition comprises 15-50 wt.% Myrtus communis juice, 8-20 wt.% glutinous rice or rice, 42-65 wt.% water, 0.008-0.28 wt.% citric acid, 0.006-0.15 wt.% sodium citrate, 0.015-0.25 wt.% edible essence, and 0.03-0.35 wt.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - Myrtus communis wine composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine composition has unique flavor and rich nutrition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the wine composition comprising (A) washing glutinous rice or rice with water for 2-3 times, adding water, soaking at 20-25 degrees C for 10-14 hours, taking out, draining, high-heat steaming to 100 degrees C for 20-25 minutes, low-heat steaming for 5 minutes, cooling to 28-31 degrees C, and adding to fermentation tank; (B) adding saccharified yeast, fermenting at 28-32 degrees C, sealing, and heat-preserving for 28-36 hours; (C) washing fresh Myrtus communis, removing seeds, pulping, adding to fermented product, fermenting at 28-32 degrees C, lowering temperature to 2 degrees C each day until room temperature is reached, fermenting for 15-20 days, squeezing, sterilizing, and storing; and (D) adding remaining raw materials, filtering, adding carbon dioxide, and filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The Myrtus communis wine composition comprises 25 wt.% Myrtus communis juice, 14 wt.% glutinous rice or rice, 60.49 wt.% water, 0.15 wt.% citric acid, 0.07 wt.% sodium citrate, 0.16 wt.% edible essence, and 0.13 wt.% carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN109761248-A; CN109761248-B
TI Sodium bicarbonate preparation device comprises e.g. carbon dioxide production device, sodium carbonate solution preparation device, carbonization device, crystallizing barrel, first centrifuge, drying bed, and mother liquid tank.
AU XIE J
AE XIE J (XIEJ-Individual)
GA 2019464541
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium bicarbonate preparation device comprises a carbon dioxide production device, a sodium carbonate solution preparation device, a carbonization device, a crystallizing barrel, a first centrifuge, a drying bed, a mother liquid tank, a mother liquid recovery device and a heating device. The carbon dioxide production device comprises a calcium carbonate calcining kiln, a carbon dioxide dust removal and denitration device and a carbon dioxide desulfurization tower which are sequentially connected through a pipeline. The sodium carbonate solution preparation device comprises a sodium carbonate storage tank, a first water supply device and a dissolved alkali tank. The sodium carbonate storage tank is connected to the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank through the material conveying device. The first water supply device is connected to the inner cavity of the alkali solution tank through a pipeline. A stirrer is arranged in the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank.
   USE - Used as sodium bicarbonate preparation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has low wind pressure required for the carbonization equipment and more complete the internal reaction of the carbonization equipment; recycles raw materials; multiples the utilization of internal heat; reuses waste flue gas; and does not scar inside the carbonization equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sodium bicarbonate preparation device comprises a carbon dioxide production device, a sodium carbonate solution preparation device, a carbonization device, a crystallizing barrel, a first centrifuge, a drying bed, a mother liquid tank, a mother liquid recovery device and a heating device. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing a sodium bicarbonate using sodium bicarbonate preparation device, comprising (i) (a) calcining calcium carbonate through calcium carbonate calcining kiln to produce flue gas with a carbon dioxide concentration of 30%-35%, passing the flue gas through the carbon dioxide dust removal and denitration device, removing dust and nitrogen oxides, conveying to the desulfurization tower to remove sulfur oxides, and obtaining flue gas containing carbon dioxide, and (b) controlling the sodium carbonate storage tank and the first water supply device respectively output sodium carbonate and purified water to the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank, and restarting and stirring the agitator in the inner cavity of the sodium hydroxide tank to obtain a sodium carbonate solution, where the temperature of the sodium carbonate solution in the alkali storage tank is controlled to 35-36 degrees C and the concentration of the sodium carbonate solution in the alkali storage tank is controlled between 48-50%, (ii) (a) introducing the carbon dioxide gas prepared in the step (a) of the step (i) into the first carbonization tower through an induced draft fan, (b) introducing the sodium carbonate solution prepared in the step (b) of the step (b) into the second carbonization tower through a water pump, (c) conveying the sodium carbonate solution in the second carbonization tower to the first carbonization tower through a pipeline, reacting with the carbon dioxide gas in the first carbonization tower to obtain a sodium bicarbonate solution, conveying the carbon dioxide gas in the first carbonization tower to the second carbonization tower, and reacting with the sodium carbonate solution in the second carbonization tower to obtain a sodium bicarbonate solution, where the steps (a) and (b) are in no particular order, the wind pressure of the carbon dioxide gas introduced into the first carbonization tower is 0.003-0.0035 MPa, and the chemical reaction occurring in the first carbonization tower and the second carbonization tower is reacting carbon dioxide, water and sodium carbonate to obtain sodium bicarbonate, (iii) conveying the sodium bicarbonate solution prepared in the first carbonization tower to the crystallized barrel through a pipeline, and catalyzing by an ultrasonic generator in the crystallization tank to obtain a nucleation liquid, where in this step, the particle size of the crystal nucleus in the nucleation fluid can be changed by adjusting the frequency of the ultrasonic generator, and (iv) (a) conveying the crystal nucleus liquid outputted by the crystallized barrel to the first centrifuge through a pipeline for centrifugation, and separately outputting the mother liquid and the sodium bicarbonate crystal into the mother liquid tank and the drying bed by the first centrifuge, (b) removing the mother liquor in the mother liquor tank from the heavy metal ions by the mother liquor removing device, storing in the buffer tank, and conveying back to the caustic tank from the buffer tank to realize the recycling of the raw materials, and (c) the drying bed through the circulating heat exchange tube B and the hot air furnace for heat exchange, and drying the sodium bicarbonate crystals to obtain a dried finished sodium bicarbonate, where in this step, the steps (b) and (c) are in no particular order; and
   (2) utilizing thermal energy in the preparation sodium bicarbonate, comprising (i) conveying fresh air to the combustion chamber through a blower to promote carbon combustion in the combustion chamber more fully, and conveying the high-temperature flue gas produced by the combustion chamber to the hot blast stove and the heat exchanger through the pipeline respectively, (ii) realizing the heat exchange between the heat exchanger and the caustic soda tank the water circulating in the circulating heat exchange tube A, controlling the temperature of the sodium carbonate solution in the caustic soda tank at 35-36 degrees C, realizing the heat exchange between the hot air furnace and the drying bed by circulating water flowing in the heat exchange tube B, and drying the baking soda crystals in the drying bed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the sodium bicarbonate preparation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iii) (a) conveying a part of the flue gas discharged from the hot blast stove back to the combustion chamber through the pipeline to realize the recirculation of the flue gas, and conveying the other part to the flue gas comprehensive purification device through the pipeline to remove dust, sulfur oxides and nitrogen oxides from the flue gas, (b) conveying the flue gas discharged from the heat exchanger to the flue gas comprehensive purification device through the pipeline to remove dust, sulfur oxides and nitrogen oxides in the flue gas, where in this step, the steps (b) and (c) are in no particular order, conveying 10%-15% of the flue gas discharged from the hot blast stove back to the combustion chamber through the pipeline, and conveying the remaining flue gas to the flue gas comprehensive purification device through the pipeline, (iv) conveying the carbon dioxide-containing flue gas discharged from the flue gas comprehensive purification device to the third carbonization tower through the pipeline, and at the same time, transporting calcium oxide into the dissolution tank through the calcium oxide storage tank, stating the stirrer in the dissolution tank to be stirred to obtain a calcium hydroxide solution, conveying the calcium hydroxide solution in the dissolution tank to the calcium hydroxide solution decontamination device through the pipeline to remove heavy metal ions in the calcium hydroxide solution, conveying the calcium hydroxide solution in the calcium hydroxide solution decontamination device to the sedimentation tank through the pipeline and allowing to stand, so that the calcium oxide solid which is not reacted with water in calcium hydroxide solution is precipitated, conveying the clarified calcium hydroxide solution in the sedimentation tank into a third carbonization tower, and reacting the calcium hydroxide solution with the flue gas containing carbon dioxide to obtain a solution containing calcium carbonate precipitate, and (v) piping the solution containing the calcium carbonate precipitate outputted by the third carbonization tower into the second centrifuge for centrifuging, separately outputting the mother liquor and the calcium carbonate solid by the second centrifuge, conveying the mother liquor to the mother liquor pool through the pipeline, conveying back to the dissolution tank through the pipeline to realize the recycling of the mother liquor, and conveying the calcium carbonate solid to the drying kiln for drying. In the step (i), using a combined combustion method in which a suspension combustion and a layered combustion are combined in a combustion chamber, injecting the suspension combustion that the pulverized coal is mixed with air into the combustion chamber to be in a suspended state, which contributes to more complete combustion of the pulverized coal, placing the layered combustion means that the coal seam on the grate at the lower end of the combustion chamber, supplying the wind upward through the bottom of the combustion chamber, and making the coal seam burn more fully.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further comprises carbon dioxide production device comprises a calcium carbonate calcining kiln, a carbon dioxide dust removal and denitration device and a carbon dioxide desulfurization tower which are sequentially connected through a pipeline. The sodium carbonate solution preparation device comprises a sodium carbonate storage tank, a first water supply device and a dissolved alkali tank. The sodium carbonate storage tank is connected to the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank through the material conveying device. The first water supply device is connected to the inner cavity of the alkali solution tank through a pipeline. A stirrer is arranged in the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank. The carbonization device comprises a first carbonization tower and a second carbonization tower. The first carbonization tower includes a tower shell A and a tube sheet body A. The tower shell A is provided with a carbon dioxide inlet A, a carbon dioxide outlet A, an alkali inlet A, and a lye outlet A connected to the inner chamber thereof. The tube plate body A includes a plate A and a multiple of vent pipes A uniformly distributed and fixed on the plate A. The tube plate body A is installed in the inner cavity of the tower shell A, and the inner cavity of the tower shell A is divided into an upper air chamber A and a lower liquid chamber A. The upper air chamber A and the lower liquid chamber A are communicated only through the inner hole of the vent pipe A. The carbon dioxide inlet A on the tower shell A is in communication with the upper air chamber A. The carbon dioxide outlet A, the lye inlet A and the lye outlet A are both connected to the lower liquid chamber A. The second carbonization tower comprises a tower shell B and a tube sheet body B, The tower shell B is provided with a carbon dioxide inlet B, a carbon dioxide outlet B, an alkali liquid inlet B and an alkali liquid outlet B connected to the inner cavity. The tube plate body B includes a plate B and a multiple of vent pipes B uniformly distributed and fixed on the plate B. The tube plate body B is installed in the inner cavity of the tower shell B, and the inner cavity of the tower shell B is divided into an upper air chamber B and a lower liquid chamber B. The upper air chamber B and the lower liquid chamber B are communicated only through the inner hole of the vent pipe B. The carbon dioxide inlet B on the tower shell B is in communication with the upper air chamber B, and the carbon dioxide outlet B, the lye inlet B and the lye outlet B are both connected to the lower liquid chamber B. The carbon dioxide inlet A of the first carbonization tower is connected to the desulfurization tower through a pipeline. The carbon dioxide outlet A of the first carbonization tower is connected to the carbon dioxide inlet B of the second carbonization tower through a pipeline. The lye inlet A of the first carbonization tower is connected to the lye outlet B of the second carbonization tower through a pipeline. The lye inlet B of the second carbonization tower is connected to the inner cavity of the sodium hydroxide tank through a pipe. The carbon dioxide outlet B of the second carbonization tower is connected to the outside. The upper end of the crystal barrel is provided with a nucleation liquid inlet, the lower end is provided with a nucleation liquid outlet, and an ultrasonic generator is arranged inside, and the nucleation liquid inlet is connected with the lye outlet A of the first carbonization tower through a pipeline. The first centrifuge is provided with a solid-liquid inlet, a solid output port and a liquid output port, and the solid-liquid inlet is connected to the nucleation liquid outlet of the crystallized barrel through a pipe. The drying bed is connected to the solids output port of the first centrifuge through the material conveying device. The mother liquid tank is connected to the liquid output port of the first centrifuge through a pipe. The mother liquid recovery device comprises a mother liquid cleaning device and a buffer tank which are sequentially connected through a pipeline. The mother liquid removing device communicates with the mother liquid tank through the pipeline. The buffer tank is connected to the alkali storage tank through the pipeline. The heating device comprises a blower, a combustion chamber, a heat exchanger, a hot blast stove, a circulating heat exchange tube A, a circulating heat exchange tube B and a flue gas comprehensive purification device. The blower is in communication with the combustion chamber. The heat exchanger is provided with a flue gas inlet A and a flue gas outlet A connected to the inner cavity. The flue gas inlet A is connected to the combustion chamber through a pipe. The flue gas outlet A is connected to the flue gas comprehensive purification device through a pipeline. The hot blast stove is provided with a flue gas inlet B and a flue gas outlet B connected to the inner cavity. The flue gas inlet B is connected to the combustion chamber through a pipe. The flue gas outlet B is respectively connected to the combustion chamber and the flue gas comprehensive purification device through a pipeline. One part of the circulating heat exchange tube A is located in the heat exchanger, and the other part of the tube body is located in the alkali storage tank. A part of the circulating heat exchange tube B is located in the heat exchanger, and another part of the tube is located in the drying bed. The sodium carbonate preparation device further comprises calcium carbonate preparation device. The calcium carbonate preparation device comprises a calcium oxide storage bin, a second water supply device, a dissolution tank, a calcium hydroxide solution decontamination device, a sedimentation tank, a second centrifuge, a drying kiln, a mother liquid pool and a third carbonization tower. The calcium oxide storage tank is connected to the inner cavity of the dissolution tank through the material conveying device. The second water supply device is connected to the inner cavity of the dissolution tank through a pipe. A stirrer is arranged in the inner cavity of the dissolving tank. The calcium hydroxide solution removing device is connected to the inner cavity of the dissolving tank through a pipe. The sedimentation tank is connected to calcium hydroxide solution decontamination device through the pipeline. The second centrifuge is provided with a liquid solid inlet, a solid output port and a liquid outlet. The drying kiln is connected to the solid output port of the second centrifuge through the material conveying device. The mother liquid pool is respectively connected to the liquid outlet of the second centrifuge and the inner cavity of the dissolution tank through a pipeline. The third carbonization tower includes a tower shell C and a tube plate body C. The tower shell C is provided with a carbon dioxide inlet C, a carbon dioxide outlet C, an alkali liquid inlet C and an alkali liquid outlet C connected to the inner cavity. The tube plate body C includes a plate C and a multiple of vent pipes C uniformly distributed and fixed on the plate C. The tube plate body C is installed in the inner cavity of the tower shell C, and the inner cavity of the tower shell C is divided into an upper air chamber C and a lower liquid chamber C. The upper air chamber C and the lower liquid chamber C are communicated only through the inner hole of the vent pipe C. The carbon dioxide inlet C on the tower C is in communication with the upper air chamber C, and the carbon dioxide outlet C. The lye inlet C and the lye outlet C are both connected to the lower liquid chamber C. The carbon dioxide inlet C is connected to the flue gas comprehensive purification device through a pipeline. The lye inlet C is connected to the sedimentation tank through a pipe. The lye outlet C is in communication with the liquid-solid inlet of the second centrifuge through a conduit. A liquid level gauge is arranged on the outer wall of the sodium hydroxide tank, and an alkali liquid concentration detector and a temperature sensor are arranged in the inner cavity of the alkali storage tank. A liquid level gauge is arranged on the outer wall of the dissolving tank, and an alkali liquid concentration detector and a temperature sensor are arranged in the inner cavity of the dissolving tank. The first water supply device and the second water supply device are both reverse osmosis membrane water purification devices. The inner cavity of the first carbonization tower is cylindrical, and the number of lye inlets A are multiple, and all of the lye inlets A are annularly uniform on the outer wall of the first carbonization column and disposed in a direction tangential to the inner wall of the first carbonization column. The inner cavity of the second carbonization tower is cylindrical. There are a multiple of lye inlets B, and all the lye inlets B are uniformly distributed on the outer wall of the second carbonization tower, and are disposed along a direction tangential to the inner wall of the second carbonization tower. The inner cavity of the third carbonization tower is cylindrical, and the number of lye inlets C are multiple, and all of the lye inlet C is annularly uniform on the outer wall of the third carbonization column and disposed in a direction tangential to the inner wall of the third carbonization column.

PT P
PN WO2020160646-A1
TI Carbon dioxide capturing involves forcing air or carbon dioxide emission into carbon dioxide-capturing device, and then monitoring level of carbon dioxide captured by carbon dioxide-capturing device.
AU BOILY S
   ROSS R
AE AEQUIUM INC (AEQU-Non-standard)
GA 202076730K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capturing involves forcing air or a carbon dioxide (CO2) emission into carbon dioxide-capturing device, monitoring a level of carbon dioxide captured by the carbon dioxide-capturing device, and then monitoring or controlling temperature of the carbon dioxide captured and the carbon dioxide-capturing device. The captured carbon dioxide is released for stocking the captured carbon dioxide in a tank for converting the captured carbon dioxide into carbon-derived end-product, or for transporting the captured carbon dioxide at a different location than a location where the carbon dioxide is captured.
   USE - Method for capturing and transforming carbon dioxide, particularly for transporting captured carbon dioxide and converting captured carbon dioxide into carbon-derived end-products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method captures the carbon dioxide in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with great benefit in reducing the purported effects of carbon dioxide on global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide-capturing device, which comprises carbon dioxide-adsorbing substrate contained within column, carbon dioxide sensor in fluid flow communication with the column, and a temperature monitoring or controlling unit for monitoring or controlling temperature of the captured carbon dioxide;
   (2) a method for managing carbon dioxide capture or carbon dioxide emission, which involves providing carbon dioxide-capturing device, capturing carbon dioxide from air or from the carbon dioxide emission into the carbon dioxide-capturing device, transporting the carbon dioxide-capturing device at a different location than a location where the carbon dioxide captured, and the releasing the carbon dioxide for stocking the carbon dioxide in a tank or for converting the carbon dioxide into the carbon-derived end-product;
   (3) a method for controlling carbon dioxide concentration in a confined space, which involves sensing a CO2 concentration in the confined space, forcing air from the space into CO2-capturing device to decrease CO2 concentration in the confined space, and then releasing the captured CO2 for stocking the captured CO2 in a tank; and
   (4) a system for controlling carbon dioxide concentration in a confined space, which comprises an inlet for receiving air from the confined space, CO2-capturing device in fluid flow communication with the inlet, and an outlet in fluid flow communication with the CO2-capturing device for discharging into the confined space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the method for capturing and transforming carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The captured carbon dioxide is carried out by desorbing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-capturing device. The carbon dioxide-capturing device comprises a zeolite. The zeolite comprises copper, titanium, silicon or mixtures. The carbon dioxide-capturing device is a fluidized bed comprising carbon dioxide-adsorbing substrate. The heating is carried out by contacting a hot gas with the portion. The hot gas is air or carbon dioxide emission. The carbon dioxide emission is selected from plant emissions, plant smokes and plant fumes. The carbon-derived end-product is selected from synthetic fuel, methane, methanol, ethane, ethanol, propane, propanol, benzene, toluene, xylene and mixtures. The carbon-derived end-product is selected from aliphatic hydrocarbons, alcohols and aromatic hydrocarbons. The conversion of the captured CO2 into the carbon-derived end-product is carried out by using metal catalysts. The impurity is also captured. The impurity comprises a sulfur-based product or nitrogen-based product, preferably hydrogen sulfide. The hydrogen sulfide is converted into sulfuric acid.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J01-E02B; J04-C02; J04-C04A

PT P
PN WO2022147632-A1
TI Making lithium hydroxide monohydrate used in e.g.            batteries comprises converting lithium chloride to            lithium hydroxide, fractional crystallizing lithium            hydroxide to get slurry of crude lithium hydroxide            monohydrate and re-crystallizing.
AU MERUANE G
   MELIPILLAN P
AE SOC QUIMICA & MINERA CHILE SA (QUIM-Non-standard)
GA 2022906937
AB    NOVELTY - Making lithium hydroxide monohydrate                comprises: (a) converting lithium chloride to                lithium hydroxide from brine with 1-4 wt.% lithium                content, lithium/sodium ratio of 2-30 to get                lithium hydroxide, by reacting with caustic                solution (mother liquor 2 (LM2)); (b) fractional                crystallizing the obtained lithium hydroxide in                20-60 degrees C and 10-5-0.1 wt.% sodium hydroxide                to get slurry of crude lithium hydroxide                monohydrate; (c) re-crystallizing lithium hydroxide                monohydrate by dissolving in water and second                crystallizing to remove remaining impurities to get                high purity sodium hydroxide monohydrate; (d)                causticizing the mother liquor (LM1) by reacting                with sodium hydroxide solution; (e) fractional                crystallizing the mixture of LM1 and sodium                hydroxide in 80-120 degrees C to separate sodium                chloride from lithium hydroxide to get solid sodium                hydroxide without lithium co-precipitation; and (f)                separating the solid liquid mixture to get LM2 and                separating LM2 from the crystals of sodium                chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lithium                hydroxide monohydrate, which is mainly used in the                production of lubricating greases capable of                operating under extreme conditions of temperature                and load and in batteries and dyes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides lithium hydroxide                monohydrate without the need for an intermediate                production of lithium carbonate in the production                method; allows reducing the production costs of                lithium hydroxide monohydrate and reduces carbon                dioxide emissions in its production; provides                feasibility of lithium hydroxide production without                co-precipitation of sodium chloride; does not                require the production of lithium carbonate; has                lower production costs of lithium hydroxide;                eliminates the use of sodium carbonate, raw                material for the production of lithium carbonate, a                reagent with high carbon dioxide emissions in its                manufacture; and eliminates the use of lime, a                reagent with higher carbon dioxide emissions in its                manufacture than sodium hydroxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making lithium hydroxide monohydrate directly                from lithium chloride brine by reaction with sodium                hydroxide, comprises: (a) converting lithium                chloride to lithium hydroxide from brine with 1-4                wt.% lithium content, lithium/sodium ratio between                2 and 30 to produce lithium hydroxide, by reacting                with caustic solution (mother liquor 2 (LM2)); (b)                crystallizing the obtained lithium hydroxide, by                fractional crystallization in 20-60 degrees C and                10-5-0.1 wt.% sodium hydroxide to obtain slurry of                crude lithium hydroxide monohydrate, without sodium                co precipitation; (c) re-crystallizing lithium                hydroxide monohydrate by dissolving in water and                second crystallizing to remove remaining impurities                in the product, to obtain high purity sodium                hydroxide monohydrate; (d) causticizing the mother                liquor (LM1) by reacting LM1 with sodium hydroxide                solution; (e) fractional crystallizing the mixture                of LM1 and sodium hydroxide in 80-120 degrees C to                separate sodium chloride from lithium hydroxide to                obtain solid sodium hydroxide without lithium                coprecipitation; and (f) separating the solid                liquid mixture to obtain LM2, and separating LM2                from the crystals of sodium chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lithium hydroxide                  crystallization step comprises: sending the                  generated LM1 to step (d); and cooling is                  effected by the mother liquor from step (f) has a                  temperature of 80-120 degrees C. The                  re-crystallization step was carried out at 20-120                  degrees C. The high purity battery grade lithium                  hydroxide monohydrate was sent to subsequent                  drying and packaging steps, and leaving ready for                  commercialization. The step of crystallizing the                  lithium hydroxide at 20-60 degrees C. The                  fractional crystallization step comprises                  performing solid sodium chloride without lithium                  coprecipitation at 100 degrees C. The sodium                  hydroxide in step (e) of sodium chloride                  crystallization was 3-10%. In the step (e)                  crystallization of sodium chloride was effected                  by evaporation of part of the water contained in                  the mixture Of LM1 and sodium hydroxide. The                  liquid separated from the pulp in step (b) of                  crystallization of lithium hydroxide corresponds                  to LM1 having 0.5-7% sodium hydroxide, 3-6%                  lithium hydroxide and 14-20% sodium chloride. The                  method further comprises: the step of                  crystallization of lithium hydroxide (step b),                  effecting a purge of mother liquor in order to                  decrease the accumulation of impurities and by                  preventing impurities in the final product;                  conversion of lithium chloride to lithium                  hydroxide and crystallization of lithium                  hydroxide was performed simultaneously, i.e. in a                  single step where the chemical reaction of                  lithium chloride to lithium hydroxide conversion                  and the crystallization phenomenon of lithium                  hydroxide occur; and precipitation and                  crystallization of sodium chloride was performed                  simultaneously. The LM2 was generated in the                  sodium chloride crystallization having 3-10%                  sodium hydroxide, 4-7% lithium hydroxide and                  14-20% sodium chloride. The method comprises,                  during step (e) of crystallization of sodium                  chloride or during the step (f) of sodium                  chloride separation of LM2, effecting a purge to                  prevent the accumulation of impurities in the                  final product. Preferred Components: The caustic                  solution contains lithium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, lithium chloride and other ions from                  the step (f), where the caustic solution has a                  temperature of 80-120 degrees C. The                  concentration of sodium hydroxide in step (a) of                  crystallization of lithium hydroxide is                  3-10%.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); H07 (Lubricants and lubrication - this excludes self-lubricating surfaces e.g. PTFE coated surfaces and lubrication systems in general. The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN113573481-A
TI Preparation of all-organic transparent flexible            printed circuit used for flexible and transparent            device, involves coating polymer solution on flexible            printed circuit substrate, immersing etched flexible            printed circuit board in water-soluble conductive            polymer aqueous solution, and baking.
AU ZHANG N
   QI X
   YANG J
   YANG Z
   LI H
   JIN X
   WANG Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST JIAOTONG (UYSJ-C)
GA 2021C7660E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of all-organic transparent                flexible printed circuit involves coating 1-10 mL                polymer solution on the flexible printed circuit                substrate and drying at 50-70degrees Celsius for                2-8 hours or vacuum curing at 70-90degrees Celsius                for 1-3 hours, projecting to-be-printed flexible                circuit, removing the mask of the printed circuit                by carbon dioxide laser cutting or fiber laser                cutting, washing and drying to obtain a flexible                printed circuit board containing a printed circuit                pattern mask, clamping the cleaned flexible printed                circuit board between the mask plate and bottom                plate, irradiating with a vacuum UV light under a                wavelength of 185 nm for 1-3 hours, removing the                mask and bottom plate, etching the flexible printed                circuit board with printed circuits, immersing the                etched flexible printed circuit board in a                water-soluble conductive polymer aqueous solution,                and baking for 5-10 minutes under an infrared lamp                of 1300 nm.
   USE - Preparation of all-organic transparent                flexible printed circuit used for flexible and                transparent device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces all-organic transparent                flexible printed circuit using conductive polymer                having excellent light transmittance. The                all-organic transparent flexible printed circuit                produces flexible and transparent device with high                integration without generation of large amount of                environmental pollutants and high energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of all-organic transparent                flexible printed circuit involves projecting a                to-be-printed flexible circuit on a substrate,                cutting and hollowing the printed circuit by carbon                dioxide laser cutting or fiber laser cutting,                cleaning and drying to form a mask, or coating 1-10                mL polymer solution on the flexible printed circuit                substrate and drying at 50-70degrees Celsius for                2-8 hours or vacuum curing at 70-90degrees Celsius                for 1-3 hours, projecting to-be-printed flexible                circuit, removing the mask of the printed circuit                by carbon dioxide laser cutting or fiber laser                cutting, washing and drying to obtain a flexible                printed circuit board containing a printed circuit                pattern mask, clamping the cleaned flexible printed                circuit board between the mask plate and bottom                plate, irradiating with a vacuum UV light under a                wavelength of 185 nm for 1-3 hours, removing the                mask and bottom plate, etching the flexible printed                circuit board with printed circuits, or irradiating                the flexible printed circuit substrate containing                the printed circuit pattern mask under vacuum UV                light with a wavelength of 185 nm for 1-3 hours,                removing the mask and etching the flexible printed                circuit board with printed circuit, immersing the                etched flexible printed circuit board in a                water-soluble conductive polymer aqueous solution                or dripping the water-soluble conductive polymer                aqueous solution to a vacuum UV lithography area of                the etched flexible printed circuit substrate, and                baking for 5-10 minutes under an infrared lamp of                1300 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The substrate is a                  metal plate, a rigid plastic plate or a glass                  sheet.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer solution                  is a polyvinylidene fluoride solution, a                  polyurethane solution or a polydimethylsiloxane                  solution. The polyvinylidene fluoride solution is                  8-12 wt.% N-methylpyrrolidone solution of                  polyvinylidene fluoride. The polyurethane                  solution is 12-15 wt.% N-methylpyrrolidone                  solution of polyurethane. The                  polydimethylsiloxane solution is a mixture of                  polydimethylsiloxane and crosslinking agent                  SYLGARD 184 (RTM: silicone elastomer) in a mass                  ratio of 10:1. The flexible printed circuit                  substrate is polyimide, polymethylmethacrylate,                  polycarbonate, polydimethylsiloxane, or                  polyethylene terephthalate. The water-soluble                  conductive polymer aqueous solution comprises                  1-1.5 wt.% poly-3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene and                  poly(p-toluenesulfonic acid) aqueous solution,                  and 1-5 wt.% dimethyl sulfoxide, ethylene glycol,                  and sorbitol or 0.02-0.04 mol/L chloroplatinic                  acid.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Organic catalytic system used for preparing small organic molecules or polymers, comprises main catalyst comprising organoboron structure, and including multiple boron centers, and co-catalyst e.g. organic base, imidazole, imidazole salt, quaternary ammonium salt and/or quaternary phosphonium salt.
AU WU G
   ZHANG Y
   YANG G
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202181168B
AB    NOVELTY - An organic catalytic system comprises a main catalyst comprising an organoboron structure (I) or (II), and including multiple boron centers, and a co-catalyst, preferably organic base, imidazole, imidazole salt, quaternary ammonium salt, quaternary phosphonium salt, bis(triphenylphosphoranylidene)ammonium salt, aminocyclopropene salt and/or metal salt.
   USE - Organic catalytic system is used for preparing small organic molecules e.g. cyclic carbonate, cyclic lactone and/or cyclic thiocarbonate or polymers e.g. aliphatic polycarbonate, polyether, polythiocarbonate, polysulfide, polyester and/or polypeptide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic catalytic system has high activity, and excellent reaction activity when applied to preparation of organic small molecules and polymers, and can be prepared by easy method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An organic catalytic system comprises a main catalyst comprising an organoboron structure of formula (I) or (II), and including multiple boron centers, and a co-catalyst, preferably organic base, imidazole, imidazole salt, quaternary ammonium salt, quaternary phosphonium salt, bis(triphenylphosphoranylidene)ammonium salt, aminocyclopropene salt and/or metal salt.
   X = group of formula (i);
   Y' = R7-O-R8, R9-S-R10, R24, or group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(vi);
   R1-R24 = H, halo, or 1-30C alkyl optionally containing O, S, N, Si and/or P, 3-30C cycloalkyl, 2-30C alkenyl, 2-30C alkynyl, 6-30C aryl, 3-30C heterocyclyl, or 5-30C heteroaryl (all optionally substituted with group A);
   group A = halo, linear or branched 1-20C hydrocarbyl, linear or branched 1-20C alkoxy, linear or branched 3-20C cycloalkyl, 6-20C aryl and/or 5-20C heteroaryl;
   or two or more of R1-R24 = bond or ring;
   a = 2-100000;and
   b,c = 0-1000.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the organic catalytic system for preparing small organic molecules or polymers. The polymer is an aliphatic polycarbonate obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide, a polyether obtained by performing catalytic ring-opening polymerization of alkylene oxide, a polythiocarbonate obtained by performing catalytic ring-opening polymerization and copolymerization of carbon dioxide and alkane sulfide, a polysulfide obtained by performing catalytic ring-opening polymerization of cyclic sulfide, a polyester obtained by performing catalytic reaction of alkylene oxide and cyclic anhydride, a polythiocarbonate obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of carbon oxysulfide and alkylene oxide, a polyester by performing catalytic reaction of alkylene oxide and carbon monoxide, a polyester obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of lactone, a polyester obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of O-carboxyanhydride, and/or a polypeptide obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of N-carboxyanhydride. The organic small molecule is cyclic carbonate obtained by performing catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide or carbon disulfide and alkylene oxide or episulfide, cyclic lactone obtained by performing catalytic reaction of carbon monoxide and alkylene oxide, and/or cyclic thiocarbonate obtained by performing catalytic copolymerization of carbon oxysulfide and alkylene oxide or episulfide alkane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The main catalyst is prepared by reacting a reaction raw material (W1) comprising unsaturated bond(s) and a reaction raw material (W2) comprising borohydride bond(s). The main catalyst and co-catalyst are supported on an inorganic carrier or organic carrier for use, or are used as heterogeneous catalyst due to their own crosslinking structures. Preferred Composition: The organoboron structure is preferably chosen from 12 structures e.g. structures of formulae (III-1)-(III-5). The co-catalyst is preferably chosen from 24 compounds e.g. compound of formula (IV-1)-(IV-5). The compound (W1) is preferably chosen from 8 compounds e.g. compounds of formulae (V-1)-(V-5). The compound (W2) is preferably compound of formula (VI). The molar ratio of the main catalyst with respect to co-catalyst is 1:10000 to 10000:1. The alkylene oxide is preferably compound of formula (VII-1). The episulfide alkane is preferably compound of formula (VII-2). The lactone is preferably chosen from compounds of formulae (VII-3)-(VII-5). PN CN112111887-A
TI Supercritical mixed fluid flash explosion treatment of natural fiber e.g. cotton and its product e.g. fiber by filling natural fiber or natural fiber product in flash explosion processing unit according to predetermined form.
AU LONG J
   CAI C
   SHI M
   GAO C
AE SHANDONG CHUCAI ANHYDROUS FIBER DYEING (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   SHANDONG GAOJIN GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202102192H
AB    NOVELTY - The method includes filling natural fiber or natural fiber product in flash explosion processing unit according to a predetermined form, where the flash explosion processing unit is transferred into a flash explosion processing device connected with non-carbon dioxide (CO2) medium device, under standard atmospheric pressure, introducing non-CO2 medium into the flash explosion processing device, cutting off the connection with the non-CO2 medium device after finishing, connecting the flash explosion processing device with CO2 medium device, filling CO2 medium into the flash explosion processing device, simultaneously heating, when the modified processing temperature and the modified processing pressure of the mixed fluid in the flash explosion processing device reach the preset value, sequentially processing the predetermined modification processing time according to the fluid circulation and fluid static state, and making the fluid uniform.
   USE - The method is for supercritical mixed fluid flash explosion treatment of natural fiber and its product. The natural fiber is cotton, hemp, silk, and/or wool. The natural fiber product is fiber, yarn, and/or fabric (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize the modification treatment of natural fiber or product, thus improving the dyeing performance of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises filling natural fiber or natural fiber product in flash explosion processing unit according to a predetermined form, where the flash explosion processing unit is transferred into a flash explosion processing device connected with non-carbon dioxide (CO2) medium device, under standard atmospheric pressure, introducing non-CO2 medium into the flash explosion processing device, cutting off the connection with the non-CO2 medium device after finishing, connecting the flash explosion processing device with CO2 medium device, filling CO2 medium into the flash explosion processing device, simultaneously heating, when the modified processing temperature and the modified processing pressure of the mixed fluid in the flash explosion processing device reach the preset value, sequentially processing the predetermined modification processing time according to the fluid circulation and fluid static state, making the fluid uniform, fully entering and distributing in fiber phase, controlling the pressure and/or temperature of separating and recycling unit, performing flash explosion process of different temperature differences and/or different pressure differences, where the flash explosion processing device quickly releases pressure or instantaneously releases pressure in the flash explosion time, subjecting the natural fiber or natural fiber product to flash explosion treatment, opening the gas recovery pump of the separation recovery unit, separating and recycling the different media, reducing the pressure in the flash explosion system to atmospheric pressure, taking out the processing sample, and finishing the flash explosion treatment of the dry natural fiber or natural fiber product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram illustrating the supercritical mixed fluid flash explosion treatment of natural fiber and its product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The non-CO2 medium is saturated steam, superheated steam, methanol, ethanol, acetone, acid, and/or alkali.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The flash explosion processing unit is made of PTFE material.

PT P
PN US2020001225-A1; US11148092-B2
TI Separating carbon dioxide from a gaseous stream            comprising carbon dioxide and water vapor used in            regenerating a parallel bed for increased efficiency,            comprises providing a flow of a feed stream to a first            desiccant bed.
AU RITTER J A
   EBNER A D
   HOLLAND C E
AE UNIV SOUTH CAROLINA (USCL-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CAROLINA (USCL-C)
GA 202001580P
AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide (CO2) from a gaseous                stream comprising CO2 and water vapor comprises                providing a flow of a feed stream to a first                desiccant bed to form a dry gaseous stream,                providing the dry gaseous stream thus formed to a                first CO2 capture bed to form a dry, low CO2                content gas stream, and providing a first portion                of the dry, low CO2 content gas stream to a second                desiccant bed, the dry, low CO2 content gas thus                provided removing water from the second desiccant                bed and regenerating the second desiccant bed. Then                providing a second portion of the dry, low CO2                content gas stream to a second CO2 capture bed as a                purge gas, and regenerating the second CO2 capture                bed according to a regeneration process that                comprises depressurizing second CO2 capture bed,                heating second CO2 capture bed following the                depressurization, venting second CO2 capture bed                following heating, purging second CO2 capture bed                by use of purge gas, and cooling the second CO2                capture bed.
   USE - Method for separating CO2 from a gaseous                stream comprising CO2 and water vapor used in                regenerating a parallel bed for increased                efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing a heating system                during the heating process of a carbon-di-oxide                capture bed with a series of heaters that can be                located and controlled so as to obtain reduced                energy consumption by a system, thus improving                efficiency of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for capturing CO2 from a feed gas, which comprises                a first desiccant bed, a first CO2 capture bed,                where a light product end of the first desiccant                bed is in controllable communication with a heavy                product end of the first CO2 capture bed, a second                desiccant bed, where a light product end of the                first CO2 capture bed is in controllable fluid                communication with a light product end of the                second desiccant bed for counter current flow                through the second desiccant bed; a second CO2                capture bed, where the light product end of the                first CO2 capture bed is also in controllable fluid                communication with a light product end of the                second CO2 capture bed for counter current flow                through the second CO2 capture bed, and where the                light product end of the second desiccant bed is in                controllable fluid communication with a heavy                product end of the second CO2 capture bed, the                light product end of the second CO2 capture bed is                in controllable fluid communication with the light                product end of the first desiccant bed for counter                current flow through the first desiccant bed, and                the light product end of the second CO2 capture bed                is also in controllable fluid communication with                the light product end of the first CO2 capture bed                for counter current flow through the first CO2                capture bed.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110386861-A
TI Purifying cannabidiol, involves drying Cannabis leaves or roots, adding into carbon dioxide supercritical extractor, separating by ultrafiltration membrane, adsorbing and purifying cannabidiol, dissolving crude powder and adding silica gel.
AU YAO D
   WAN L
   LI Y
AE HEILONGJIANG DINGHENGSHENG PHARM CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201992479W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying cannabidiol, involves (a) drying Cannabis leaves or roots, crushing, adding into a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor, taking ethanol and extracting to obtain an extract, (b) adjusting the pH, carrying out suction filtration and concentrating to obtain a cannabidiol polyphenol crude extract, (c) separating by an ultrafiltration membrane, (d) adsorbing and purifying the cannabidiol by macroporous adsorption resin, (e) analyzing by 70-80 vol.% ethanol, tracking and detecting by a thin-layer chromatography method and collecting eluant, (f) recovering ethanol and concentrating the eluent to obtain a cannabidiol polyphenol semi-finished product, (g) dissolving the crude powder, adding a silica gel, stirring, carrying out silica gel column chromatography, carrying out re-purification, eluting and carrying out thin-layer chromatography tracking monitoring on eluent, and (h) recovering ethanol, collecting eluant, concentrating and freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures original color and taste are not damaged by heat, does not destroy the heat sensitive property and oxidizable substance, has excellent effective component purification effect, is conducive to industrial large-scale production, avoids structural changes, loss and physiological activity of the active ingredient due to thermal effects and improves yield of the active ingredient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for purifying cannabidiol, involves (a) drying Cannabis leaves or roots, crushing, adding into a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor, taking ethanol as an entrainer and extracting at 50-60 degrees C for 100-140 minutes at the pressure of 20-40 MPa to obtain an extract, (b) adjusting the pH of the treated extract to 3-4 by hydrochloric acid, leaving still the mixture, carrying out suction filtration and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain a cannabidiol polyphenol crude extract, (c) separating by an ultrafiltration membrane and carrying out membrane operating at the pressure of 0.2-0.3 MPa and the interception rate of 90-95%, (d) adsorbing and purifying the cannabidiol by macroporous adsorption resin with the volume flow rate of 1:6-1:8, the volume flow of 2-4 BV/hour, and the flow rate of 3-5 BV/hour for adsorption, where the model of the resin is AB-8, D4006 or NKA-II, (e) analyzing 70-80 vol.% ethanol with the volume flow of 2-3 BV/hour, tracking and detecting by a thin-layer chromatography method and collecting eluant at each stage, (f) recovering ethanol and concentrating the eluent at each stage of the cannabidiol to obtain a cannabidiol polyphenol semi-finished product, (g) dissolving the crude powder in ethyl acetate, adding a silica gel, stirring, carrying out dry loading, carrying out silica gel column chromatography on a 200-300 mesh silica gel column chromatography, carrying out re-purification on the extract, washing off impurities using water, eluting with 70-90% ethanol and carrying out thin-layer chromatography tracking monitoring on eluent, and (h) recovering ethanol, collecting eluant, concentrating and freeze-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The entrainer in step (a) is 1 vol.% of the total extraction solvent. Preferred Property: The ultrafiltration membrane has a molecular weight cutoff of 1000-5000. Preferred Method: The macroporous resin in step (d) is adsorbed, where the adsorption pH is adjusted to 6-7.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN109924064-A
TI Edible mushroom cultivation optimization system has spraying unit which is configured to spray water source on surface of mushroom and soil according to loose soil signal sent by tilling unit, and removes dust on surface of mushroom.
AU YANG H
   JIN J
   HAN T
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019591538
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a dust detecting component which is configured to detect the content of dust in the gas, and send the detection data to the judging unit. A judging unit is configured to judge according to the detection data sent by the soil collecting unit and the gas detecting unit, and to check whether the surface of the mushroom has more dust affecting the respiration effect, and if the surface dust of the mushroom is detected that too much, the respiration effect is affected. The signal is sent to a tilling unit. The tilling unit is configured to loosen the soil according to the signal sent by the determining unit, and send the loose soil signal to the spraying unit. The spraying unit is configured to spray the water source on the surface of the mushroom and the soil according to the loose soil signal sent by the tilling unit, and removes the dust on the surface of the mushroom to restore the breathing speed and accelerate the growth speed.
   USE - Edible mushroom cultivation optimization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The water condition in the soil is realized in real time by setting the soil collecting unit. The relaxation and nutrition of the soil, and the oxygen in the air in the mushroom cultivation base can be detected by setting the gas detection unit. The carbon dioxide and dust values can be analyzed by connecting the soil collection unit and the gas detection unit to analyze whether the surface of the mushroom is affected by too much dust. Therefore, the mushrooms can be sprinkled and cleaned to avoid the slow growth cycle of the mushrooms due to respiratory obstruction. The growth of various regions of the mushroom in the mushroom cultivation base is viewed by setting the growth potential acquisition unit, by which the growth quality of the shiitake mushroom in each region is judged, and at the same time, the shredded mushroom can be crushed by setting the crushing unit. The slag is used as a nutrient for the growth of other mushrooms, which greatly saves the consumption of nutrients, and the environmental detection unit and the summary unit can be used to analyze the growth environment of the better-growing mushrooms, and the growth environment data is carried out. Promotion can help to improve the overall growth quality of mushrooms in the mushroom cultivation base and expand the efficiency of enterprises.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has an oxygen detecting component which is configured to detect the oxygen content in the gas and send the detection data to a determining unit. A carbon dioxide detecting component is configured to detect a content of carbon dioxide in the gas, and send the detection data to the determining unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of edible mushroom cultivation optimization system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Incubating quail eggs at constant temperature, by spraying peracetic acid solution on ground and ceiling, transferring crack-free fertilized eggs with smooth surface to incubator, spraying acetic acid solution and monitoring incubation.
AU WAN X
AE ZHAOQING HI-TECH ZONE TIANMU NEW ENERGY (ZHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4576G

AU 
AE QUANZHOU TAIWANESE INVESTMENT ZONE FEIMA (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201864248R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of coating involves (1) orderly adding butyl acrylate, isobornyl methacrylate, acrylic acid, hydroxyethyl methacrylate, styrene and tert-amyl peroxyacetate to a flask, stirring and mixing to obtain mixed solution, and transferring to a constant pressure liquid dripping funnel, (2) adding epoxy resin, lauryl mercaptan, and dimethyl carbonate to a four-necked flask, placing flask in oil bath, heating, stirring and mixing to obtain epoxy resin solution, stirring, continuously increasing the temperature, dripping mixed solution in funnel into flask, stirring and reacting, dripping tert-amyl peroxyacetate, continuously heat-preserving, finishing the reaction, and cooling to obtain epoxy modified acrylate resin, and (3) dispersing distilled water, sodium dodecyl sulfate, OTD defoaming agent, titanium dioxide, sodium methylsilanolate, wollastonite powder, zinc sulfate, talc powder and epoxy-graft modified acrylic resin, to obtain slurry, and grinding in a sand mill.
   USE - Preparation method of coating for inhibiting carbon dioxide corrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of coating with excellent waterproof performance, and which reduces the corrosion between carbon dioxide and water to produce carbonate to metal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of coating involves (1) orderly adding 48-60 g butyl acrylate, 8-11 g isobornyl methacrylate, 5-8 g acrylic acid, 28-35 g hydroxyethyl methacrylate, 15-18 g styrene and 3-5 g tert-amyl peroxyacetate to a flask, stirring and mixing for 10-15 minutes to obtain mixed solution, and transferring the mixed solution to a constant pressure liquid dripping funnel, (2) respectively adding 8-10 g epoxy resin, 1-3 g lauryl mercaptan, and 40-50 g dimethyl carbonate to a 500 mL four-necked flask, placing the flask into an oil bath, increasing the temperature to 70-80 degrees C, stirring and mixing for 25-35 minutes under the protection of nitrogen atmosphere to obtain epoxy resin solution, stirring, continuously increasing the temperature to 120-130 degrees C, dripping the mixed solution in the funnel into the flask within 2-3 hours, stirring and reacting for 1-2 hours, dripping 2-4 g tert-amyl peroxyacetate within 10-20 minutes, continuously heat-preserving for 2.5-3.5 hours, finishing the reaction, and cooling to room temperature to obtain epoxy modified acrylate resin, (3) respectively selecting 20-25 pts. wt. distilled water, 1-3 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate, 0.5-0.8 pt. wt. OTD defoaming agent, 8-10 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. sodium methylsilanolate, 2-5 pts. wt. wollastonite powder, 12-15 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 5-7 pts. wt. talc powder and 45-55 pts. wt. epoxy-graft modified acrylic resin, adding to a dispersing kettle, dispersing at 800-1000 rpm for 15-25 minutes to obtain slurry, adding to a sand mill, and grinding to fineness of 15-20 mu m.

granularity of                more than 80 mesh sieve, and weighing the                above-mentioned raw materials, weighing anhydrous                ethanol and povidone k30, and uniformly stirring to                obtain povidone k30 ethanol solution, (i) adding                zinc gluconate, ferrous gluconate and vitamin c and                mixing evenly, (ii) adding vitamin A acetate,                vitamin D 3, vitamin E, vitamin B1, vitamin B2 and                vitamin B6 and mixing evenly, (iii) uniformly                mixing substance obtained in steps (i) and (ii),                (iv) adding acid source to the mixed material                obtained in step (iii), and mixing uniformly to                obtain acid material, (b) slowly adding povidone                k30 ethanol solution obtained in step (a) to acid                material obtained in step (iv) under stirring                condition, so that the acid material can be                dispersed to the extent that the acid material can                be held into contact with one another, continuously                stirring for 5-15 minutes, stopping stirring, and                carrying out wet mixing to obtain acidic soft                material, (c) adding acidic soft material obtained                in step (b) to the swinging granulator, and sieving                acidic granule using 15-30 mesh sieve, (d) drying                acidic granule at 45-55 degrees C until the                moisture content is less than or equal to 1.5%, (e)                (i) uniformly mixing dried acidic granule with                filler, (ii) adding sweetener, essence and beta                -renieratene and mixing uniformly, (iii) mixing the                components obtained in steps (e)(i)-(ii) to obtain                acid mixture, (iv) mixing carbon dioxide source                with calcium carbonate to obtain an alkali                material, (v) slowly adding povidone k30 ethanol                solution to alkali material under stirring                condition, so that the alkali material can be                dispersed to the extent that the alkali material                can be held into contact with one another,                continuously stirring for 5-15 minutes, stopping                stirring, and carrying out wet mixing to obtain                alkaline soft material, (vi) adding alkaline soft                material to the swing granulator, and sieving                alkaline granule through 15-30 mesh sieve, (vii)                drying alkaline granule at 45-55 degrees C until                the moisture content less than or equal to 1.5%,                (f) uniformly mixing dried alkaline granule with                lubricant, and mixing with acid mixture obtained in                step (e) (iii) to obtain total mixture and (g)                tableting total mixture.

PN CN108658898-A
TI Producing epichlorohydrin involves introducing raw material such as chlorine gas, water, calcium carbonate and chloropropene to apparatus containing e.g. reaction units of hypochlorous acid and epichlorohydrin and carrying out reaction.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 201883634J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing epichlorohydrin by improved chlorohydrin method involves: (1) taking an apparatus containing a hypochlorous acid reaction unit, a dehydrochlorination reaction unit, a hypochlorous acid concentration unit, a chlorohydrin reaction unit, a saponification reaction unit, an epichlorohydrin separation unit, a saponification residue dechlorination unit, and a carbon dioxide recovery unit; (2) introducing raw material containing chlorine gas and water into the hypochlorous acid reaction unit to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride; (3) introducing aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride and calcium carbonate into the dehydrochlorination reaction unit, in which calcium carbonate is reacted with hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide; and (4) introducing the hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid aqueous solution of the dehydrochlorination reaction unit into the hypochlorous acid concentration unit to concentrate the hypochlorous acid aqueous.
   USE - The method is useful for producing epichlorohydrin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of simply and economically producing epichlorohydrin in an environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing epichlorohydrin by improved chlorohydrin method involves: (1) taking an apparatus containing a hypochlorous acid reaction unit, a dehydrochlorination reaction unit, a hypochlorous acid concentration unit, a chlorohydrin reaction unit, a saponification reaction unit, an epichlorohydrin separation unit, a saponification residue dechlorination unit and a carbon dioxide recovery unit; (2) introducing raw material containing chlorine gas and water into the hypochlorous acid reaction unit to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride; (3) introducing aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride and calcium carbonate into the dehydrochlorination reaction unit, in which calcium carbonate is reacted with hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide; (4) introducing the hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid aqueous solution of the dehydrochlorination reaction unit into the hypochlorous acid concentration unit to concentrate the hypochlorous acid aqueous solution to obtain a hydrogen chloride-free high-concentration hypochlorous acid aqueous solution and then introducing into chlorination reaction unit, where the hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid aqueous solution or the hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid aqueous solution in the dehydrochlorination reaction unit are not introduced into the hypochlorous acid concentration unit and the chlorohydrin reaction unit; (5) reacting chloropropene with hypochlorous acid in the chlorohydrin reaction unit to generate dichloropropanol and discharging the unreacted chloropropene from the chlorohydrin reaction unit; (6) introducing the liquid phase material containing dichloropropanol in the chlorohydrin reaction unit and sodium hydroxide into the saponification reaction unit, in which dichloropropanol is reacted with sodium hydroxide to form a target product epichlorohydrin; (7) feeding the liquid phase material of the saponification reaction unit to the epichlorohydrin separation unit, in which the epichlorohydrin is separated from the saponification residue; (8) introducing carbon dioxide and sodium hydroxide in the dehydrogenation reaction unit into the carbon dioxide recovery unit, in which the carbon dioxide is reacted with sodium hydroxide to form sodium carbonate and water, and introducing the obtained sodium carbonate and water into the saponification residue dechlorination unit; (9) introducing the saponification residue of the epichlorohydrin separation unit and sodium carbonate into the saponification residue decalcification unit (7), in which the calcium chloride in the saponification residue is reacted with sodium carbonate to form sodium chloride and calcium carbonate; and (10) recycling the obtained calcium carbonate to the dehydrochlorination unit, or further processing to obtain commercial calcium carbonate, where the material of the saponification residue decalcification unit is an aqueous solution of sodium chloride.

PN CN115273465-A
TI Method for realizing signal cooperative control of            urban arterial road based on enhanced artificial bee            colony algorithm, involves establishing carbon dioxide            emission estimation model of traffic flow at entrance            of signalized intersection.
AU YE Z
   ZHAO H
   HE R
AE UNIV LANZHOU JIAOTONG (UYLJ-C)
GA 2022D8481K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing an optimization                model process and a solution algorithm. The delay                and parking rates are calculated. The delays and                stop rates are calculated in through-lanes within a                coordinated control system. The cycle average                vehicle delay and parking rate of the traffic flow                is obtained. Statistical analysis of simulation                results of the large number of traffic situations                is performed based on delay and parking rates. The                carbon dioxide emission estimation model of traffic                flow at an entrance of signalized intersection is                established. A timing optimization model for the                coordinated control of trunk timing parameters is                established under the constraints of signal period,                inlet channel saturation and phase                difference.
   USE - Method for realizing signal cooperative                control of urban arterial road based on enhanced                artificial bee colony algorithm for traffic trunk                technology based on traffic emission of a trunk                road system for global collaborative optimization                of research.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing better search                performance and higher solution quality. The method                enables effectively optimizing the speed of the                algorithm by guiding the food source to search the                optimal individual direction of the neighborhood,                providing the neighborhood of each food source with                different individuals, and enhancing the global                optimizing capability of algorithm while optimizing                the speed of algorithm. The method enables                realizing small neighborhood radius of the                algorithm iteration initial set, continuously                increasing the neighborhood radius of the food                sources, and improving depth search capability of                the algorithms, so that the mechanism can make the                algorithm to obtain effective equalization in                global search capability and depth search                precision.
