AB    NOVELTY - The device has a processing tank (1) whose                circumferential inner wall is fixedly connected                with a carbon dioxide sensor and an adsorption net                box. A three-way pipe (3) is fixedly connected with                an electromagnetic valve. The three-way pipe is                provided with a main control valve (10). An end of                the three-way pipe is provided with a filtering                mechanism (4) for filtering gas entering the                processing tank. The processing tank is provided                with a turbulent flow mechanism for disturbing the                gas.
   USE - High concentration-type carbon emission                reduction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device matches the processing tanks, so                that the processing tank absorbs carbon dioxide and                the processing tank is used for heating and                desorbing, thus avoiding an operation of a stopping                machine, improving processing efficiency, and                improving an adsorption effect. The device sets a                filter mechanism at the end of the three-way pipe                to filter the gas entering into the processing tank                by a filter cotton roll and rotating the filter                cotton roll to be convenient to replace the filter                cotton after use, thus improving efficiency for                changing the filter cotton roll. An air inlet pipe                is connected with a gas pipeline to be processed at                an outer side when using the device, and the two                processing tank are used for exchanging, thus                preventing gas centralized blowing to a side of the                adsorbent net box, and hence improving production                efficiency of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for utilizing a high concentration-type carbon                emission reduction device.

PN WO2022148837-A2; WO2022148837-A3
TI Layered structured catalyst for conversion of            carbon monoxide into ethylene, comprises substrate,            inner transition metal layer, intermediate organic            layer, and outer ionomer layer comprising            short-side-chain ionomer.
AU OZDEN A
   WANG Y
   LI F
   SINTON D
   SARGENT E
AE TOTALENERGIES ONETECH (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 202290684R
AB    NOVELTY - A layered structured catalyst comprises a                substrate, inner transition metal layer,                intermediate organic layer and outer ionomer layer                containing ionomer which is a short-side-chain                (SSC) ionomer having a hydrophobic backbone and a                hydrophilic side chain, in order. The transition                metal is copper or a copper alloy.
   USE - Layered structured catalyst used as gas                diffusion electrode for membrane electrode assembly                used in integrated cascade system for converting                carbon monoxide into ethylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high Faradaic efficiency                values, and promotes the selectivity of an                electroreduction reaction of CO into ethylene up to                65 not greater than 1% at a 2.5 V full-cell                potential, or of at least 60% at an applied current                density of 130 mA.cm2, or at most 85% at most 160                mA cm - 2. The catalyst stability is maintained for                a long period of time in the membrane electrode                assembly (MEA).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) membrane electrode assembly (MEA), which                comprises cathode containing layered structured                catalyst, anode, anion-exchange membrane, anolyte,                reactant inlet in fluid communication with the                cathode to provide a carbon monoxide-enriched gas                component and a product outlet in fluid                communication with the cathode to release a product                mixture containing ethylene;
   (2) integrated cascade system, which comprises                solid oxide electrolysis cell (SOEC) for converting                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and the                membrane electrode assembly for receiving carbon                monoxide as at least a portion of the carbon                monoxide-enriched gas component;
   (3) use of layered structure catalyst;
   (4) manufacture of layered structured                catalyst; and
   (5) process for electrochemically converting                carbon dioxide into ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The electrodepositing the                  transition metal is performed in a flow                  electrolyzer involves preparing a cathode through                  electroreduction of copper bromide in presence of                  potassium hydroxide and carbon dioxide and                  further involves preparing another electrolyte                  solution containing 0.1-1 M potassium carbonate                  and 10-50 mM N-tolyl-pyridinium triflate                  precursor for electrodeposition of the                  intermediate organic layer, and depositing outer                  ionomer layer by spraying ionomer solution                  containing cation exchange perfluorosulfonic acid                  ionomer and methanol. The process for                  electrochemically converting carbon dioxide into                  ethylene involves separating ethylene by pressure                  swing adsorption gas separation method from the                  product mixture containing at least 25% ethylene                  together with at least one of carbon monoxide,                  methane, hydrogen, acetate, ethanol and propanol.                  Preferred Components: The copper alloy is                  selected from copper-silica alloy,                  copper-aluminum alloy, and copper-silver alloy.                  The anolyte is a solution containing potassium                  bicarbonate, potassium sulfate, potassium                  hydroxide (preferred), sodium hydroxide or their                  combinations. The anode is platinum-carbon anode                  containing platinum on graphitized carbon powder.                  The counter electrode in the flow electrolyzer is                  selected from nickel foam, nickel mesh, nickel                  felt, iridium oxide supported on titanium foam                  iridium oxide supported on titanium mesh, or                  iridium oxide supported on titanium felt. The                  electrodeposition of the intermediate organic                  layer is performed in three-electrode system                  containing silver/silver chloride electrode as                  reference electrode, electrodeposited copper as                  working electrode and platinum foil as counter                  electrode. Preferred Composition: The anolyte is                  a potassium hydroxide-containing solution                  containing 0.1-10 M, preferably 2-4 M potassium                  hydroxide. The anode is an oxygen evolution                  reaction (OER) anode such as titanium-iridium                  oxide having 2-4 mg cm-2 iridium. The catholyte                  solution in flow electrolyzer contains 0.1-0.5 M                  copper bromide, 0.2-1.0 M sodium tartrate dibasic                  dehydrate and 1.0-3.0 M potassium hydroxide.                  Preferred Properties: The inner transition metal                  layer has porosity of 60-70% and thickness of                  250-700 nm, preferably 300-600 nm as determined                  using transmission electron microscopy. The                  intermediate layer has thickness of 10-20 nm and                  outer ionomer layer has thickness of 10-15                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The intermediate                  organic layer contains N-aryl pyridinium-derived                  layer such as N-tolyl-tetradihydro-bipyridine                  layer. The outer ionomer layer contains a                  perfluorosulfonic acid ionomer of formula:                  (C4HF7O4SC2F4)x, where x is 1. The anolyte                  further contains glucose. Preferred Composition:                  The carbon dioxide absorption solution released                  from the cascade system is an amine-based                  solution containing 10-60 wt.% aqueous                  ethanolamine solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The substrate contains                  PTFE or PTFE-modified hydrophobic carbon paper.                  Preferred Properties: The substrate has average                  pore size of 100-800 mu m, preferably 400-500 mu                  m as determined by transmission electron                  microscopy.

The liquid temperature in the water box is continuously detected. The circulating pump is started until the temperature is less than the liquid pump starting temperature. The refrigeration starting temperature of the refrigeration system is determined. Determination is made to check whether temperature of liquid is greater than a degree centigrade.

The compressor of running under the condition of over-high current operation condition is reduced and the service life of compressor is protected.

AU DATTA R
   PURANAM S V
   DATTA K
AE VOLTA ENERGY INC (VOLT-Non-standard)
   VOLTA ENERGY INC (VOLT-Non-standard)
GA 2022746470
AB    NOVELTY - The cell (100) has a negative electrode (125) configured to be in fluid communication with alkaline negative electrolyte. A positive electrode (130) is configured to be in communication with acidic positive electrolyte. A first ion-exchange membrane e.g. cation exchange membrane or anion exchange membrane, is in contact with the negative electrode. A second ion exchange membrane is in contact the positive electrode. An inert intermediate neutralyte layer (110) is provided between the first and second ions exchange membranes. The ion exchange membranes are configured to maintain a pH differential between the negative and positive electrodes.
   USE - Carbon-capturing Regenerative Redox Fuel electrochemical Cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell has high areal power densities, and provides efficient and scalable carbon-capturing redox-flow battery that synergistically combines electrical energy storage and grid arbitrage with capture of carbon-dioxide either directly from air or from a point source such as a flue gas from a power generating or a manufacturing plant, or as an industrial feedstock. The cell electrochemically maintains a pH differential between two electrodes via a uniquely structured membrane-electrode assembly (MEA) that boosts the cell voltage dramatically to provide efficient energy storage with high area power density. The pH differential further enhances the cell potential and power density for cost-effective energy storage. The CC-RFB maintains electrolyte at the negative electrode at a high pH needed to efficiently absorb carbon dioxide in situ or in a separate absorber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic of a CC-RFB electrochemical carbon capture and energy storage cell in charge/carbon capture mode.

PN CN114480754-A
TI Blast furnace iron-making method of carbon            hydrogen coupling involves spraying high temperature            coke oven gas and pre-heating oxygen in air circulating            area to realize reforming, and realizing air port            hydrogen-rich full oxygen smelting.
AU ZHANG W
   LIU L
   TIAN B
AE XINJIANG BAYI IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 202270523B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves spraying high-temperature                coke oven gas and pre-heating oxygen in a tuyere of                a blast furnace. The coke air in the air                circulation area is circulated to realize the                rrecoating of the coke gas. The effective hydrogen                component reaches more than 70% of the hydrogen                content, and realizes the hydrogen-rich full oxygen                smelting of the air port. The furnace is sprayed                with high-carbon monoxide (CO) from the top of the                furnace body. The CO ratio between the hydrogen                component and the CO is adjusted by adjusting the                air outlet. The carbon dioxide is removed from the                coal gas output by the furnace top, and the furnace                is heated at 950-1020degreesC.
   USE - The method is used for blast furnace                iron-making carbon hydrogen coupling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method of carbon hydrogen coupling using                fast reduction characteristics and strong                penetration ability at high temperature of                hydrogen, replacing carbon by hydrogen, reducing                carbon consumption in iron-making process, at the                same time, using reduction heat absorption of high                temperature hydrogen and reduction heat release                characteristic of CO, realizing the maximum                amplitude reduction efficiency and reduction target                of CO consumption in the furnace according to the                optimal proportion, and removing carbon dioxide                from the output gas with a certain carbon-hydrogen                ratio, recycling the blast furnace, finally solving                the problem of high carbon consumption and high                carbon emission in the iron making process proposed                in the background technology. The method maintains                reasonable theoretical combustion temperature by                adjusting the air blowing coal powder amount and a                large amount of top coal gas for removing CO.

PN RU2766182-C1
TI METHOD FOR PRODUCTION OF LOAD-BEARING STRUCTURAL INSULATING AND HEAT-INSULATING BUILDING PRODUCTS BASED ON RICE HUSK.
AU NIKOLAENKO V V
   LYUBOMIRSKII N V
   NIKOLAENKO E YU
   BAKHTIN A S
AE UNIV CRIMEAN FEDERAL (UYCR-Non-standard)
GA 202247324W
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the construction materials industry, namely to the production of structural and thermal insulation and thermal insulation building products, for example, wall blocks and thermal insulation boards, using rice processing waste and a binder. The method for production of structural and heat-insulating building products based on rice husks includes the preparation of a raw mixture of a binder and a pre-prepared filler from rice husks, mixing, feeding of the raw mixture, molding by pressing in molding equipment, treatment of the mixture with carbon dioxide, stripping of finished products, and the rice husk is pre-processed by grinding to a specific surface area of 1200-1400 cm2/g, soaking for 120 minutes until the humidity reaches 60% in mass and in an amount of 70-85 wt. %, is mixed with quicklime in an amount of 15-30 wt. %, in terms of Ca(OH)2, or lime-containing waste in the form of lime dust composition, wt. %: quicklime 15, slaked lime 35, fine limestone 50, as a binder in an amount of 35-50 wt. %, by dry matter is mixed with the specified filler in an amount of 50-65 wt. %, kept in the mixer for 60 minutes after mixing, compaction of the mixture is carried out on a hydraulic press in a mold under a pressure of 4.0 MPa, hardening is carried out by forced carbonation with carbon dioxide concentration of 10-95% for 6-8 minutes.
   USE - Construction materials industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Development of a new resource-saving technology that makes it possible to dispose of large-tonnage waste without harm to human health without the use of energy-intensive disposal methods while maintaining the strength and thermal characteristics of products, increasing their biostability.

PT P
PN EP3945138-A1; WO2022023505-A1
TI Processing leather, e.g. dried leather, by subjecting leather to supercritical carbon dioxide under increased pressure and temperature, including e.g. fat-liquoring leather, dyeing, coating, waterproofing, and/or impregnating leather.
AU TRAMBITAS D O
   BOHUS B
   DE VRIES T J
   WOERLEE G F
   BRIGITTA B
AE FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
   FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
GA 202218275Q
AB    NOVELTY - A leather is processed by subjecting the leather to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) under increased pressure of greater than or equal to 0.01 MPa, a temperature of greater than or equal to 30 degrees C, during a period of greater than or equal to 1 minute. The method comprises drying leather, fat-liquoring leather, dyeing tanned and dried leather, fat-liquored leather, or finished leather, coating leather, waterproofing leather, and/or impregnating leather.
   USE - The method is useful for processing leather selected from low grade leathers, thin leathers, crust leathers, dried leather, finished leathers, wet blue leathers, and tanned leather (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The resulting dyed leather exposes a homogeneous distribution of the dye on the leather surface as well as in depth without any stains. The process is much more efficient compared to a classical water dyeing with a result in the finished dyed leather material in less than 4 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) leather which has a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 1 MPa, and predetermined elongation at break, softness, average tear load, and average grain crack strength;
   (2) stand-alone apparatus for performing the leather processing method, comprising quick-closure autoclave, greater than or equal to 1 CO2 pump, optionally CO2 storage, such as a bottle, or an autoclave, integrated cooling/heating system, greater than or equal to 1 chemical supply system, processor and software for operating the apparatus, and control panel for selecting at least one process step; and
   (3) computer program comprising instructions for operating the stand-alone apparatus, where the instructions cause the computer to carry out the the leather processing method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Fat-liquoring material is selected from fats and oils, such as fish oil, and sulfonated fish oil, land animals oils and fats, such as claw oil, beef tallow, pig fat, and bone fat, vegetable oils and fats, such as palm oil, sunflower oil, rapeseed oil, soybean oil, coconut fat, palm kern fat, and turkey red oil, waxes, such as carnauba wax, montane wax, and wool grease, and synthetic fats/oils, such as paraffin oil, mineral oil, fatty alcohol, fatty acid esters, and/or silicones. Dye is selected from a polyester dye, a natural or synthetic pigment or colorant, and paints. The dye is provided on a support, such as on glass beads, on porous supports, or on textile materials, preferably textiles made from natural fibers like cotton, silk, or linen, preferably pre-dyed polyester materials.

TI Mixed air detection system for shield tunnel construction process in environmental geology technology field, has compensation model that is obtained by training measured value and standard value after mixing different air with known concentration, and alarm module performs over-limit alarm.
AU FANG Z
   BA X
   MEN Y
   SUN S
   XIONG Y
   LI L
   HAN Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2022202120
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a mixed air detection module, a processing module and an alarm module connected in sequence, and air separation membrane arranged between an environmental air detecting module and a combustible air detection device. The environment air detecting device is used to receive the measured mixed air and detect a concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide in a mixed air. A combustible air detection device is configured to detect the concentrations of a combustible air separated by air separating membrane.The compensation model is obtained by training the measured value and the standard value after mixing different air with known concentrations. The alarm module is used for performing an over-limit alarm. A communication module adopts a LoRa-modulated digital radio, the working frequency band is 850.125-930.125MHz, and an RS232/RS485 interface is provided, and support wide voltage input.
   USE - Mixed air detection system for a shield tunnel construction process in an environmental geology technology field. Uses include but are not limited to nitrogen oxide, carbon monoxide, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables an alarm module is used for performing an over-limit alarm. realizing eight gas monitoring, realizing over-limit alarm and information linkage through wireless data transmission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a detection method of the mixed air detection system using the shield tunnel.

PN KR2353652-B1
TI System for controlling exhaust gas of fire fighting vehicle i.e. diesel vehicle, in garage of fire station, has control unit provided with calculation unit for calculating degree of pollution based on value input by input unit.
AU MUN H
AE MUN H (MUNH-Individual)
GA 202216355J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a grabber (100) provided with a dismounting/mounting unit for installation with an exhaust port (20) of a fire fighting vehicle. A dust collection filter is provided with a magnet (148) that is fixed to a flange and is installed on an exhaust hose close to the grabber. A warm-up catalyst is installed at a rear end of a dust collecting filter. A control unit is provided with a calculation unit for calculating degree of pollution based on the value input by an input unit. An integrated sensor is provided with a carbon dioxide measurement module for measuring carbon dioxide content included in the exhaust gas using non-dispersive infrared rays. A temperature sensing module senses the temperature of the exhaust gas. A flow rate sensing module senses the flow rate of the exhaust gas.
   USE - System for controlling exhaust gas of a fire fighting vehicle i.e. diesel vehicle, in a garage of a fire station.
   ADVANTAGE - The control unit controls the operation of the blower when the set value is greater than the preset value so as to smoothly discharge the exhaust gas, reduce pollution of the garage and protect health of firefighters. The system is easy to attach and detach the exhaust port and the exhaust hose of the fire fighting vehicle, so that the firefighting vehicle can be dispatched in a quick manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of a system for controlling exhaust gas of a fire fighting vehicle in a garage of a fire station.

   Exhaust port flange (30)
   Grabber (100)

TI Heat pump device for generating high temperature            air, has refrigerant cooler discharged by compressor,            heat pump condenser and heat pump compressor, where            flow path of air to be heated is orderly provided with            cooler of condenser.
AU SONG X
   LIU X
   MA J
AE GUANGZHOU HEIMA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202219055K
AB    NOVELTY - The heat pump device comprises a refrigerating                compressor (1) of a low-temperature heat pump, an                evaporator (3), a subcooler (5) of the condenser, a                heat exchanger (6), a refrigerant cooler (7)                discharged by the compressor, heat pump condenser                (8), a heat pump compressor (9), where the flow                path of the air to be heated is orderly provided                with a cooler, a condenser of the heat pump. The                refrigerant coolers that are cooled by the                refrigeration compressor and the low temperature                heat pump are discharged by a refrigeration                condenser. The heat exchangers and heat pump                compressors orderly form a third loop at the same                time.
   USE - Heat pump device for generating high                temperature air in adsorption rotating wheel                regeneration such as dehumidifying rotating wheel,                carbon dioxide absorbing rotating wheel used in                industrial production field such as pharmacy                workshop and lithium battery production                workshop.
   ADVANTAGE - Device uses the heat output by the auxiliary                radiator as heat source to generate high                temperature air, so that the highest temperature                can reach 170 degrees centigrade, and the heating                efficiency of the high temperature heat pump can                reach more than 2.5, thus saving more than 50                percent of energy. The device allows the                high-temperature air generated by the device to be                applied to various adsorption rotating wheel                regeneration such as dehumidifying rotating wheel,                volatile organic compounds (VOCs) rotating wheel                and carbon dioxide absorbing rotating wheel and                coating machine air heating, thus greatly expanding                the application range.

PN US11224944-B1
TI Method for manufacturing three-dimensional            additively manufactured component, involves peening            portion of wall of part with peening system, where            peening comprises shot peening of wall with shot from            system, and shot comprises carbon dioxide            particles.
AU MARTIN J H
   ROPER C S
AE HRL LAB LLC (HRLH-C)
GA 2022105922
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves additively manufacturing a                wall of a part from powder in a powder bed with an                additive manufacturing machine, where the wall has                a thickness. A portion of the wall of the part is                peened with a peening system, and the peening                induces plastic deformation in the portion. Another                thickness of the portion is less than a minimum                thickness limit achievable by the machine. The                peening comprises shot peening the portion with a                shot from a shotpeening system. The shot comprises                carbon dioxide particles and ferromagnetic metallic                particles. The part is annealed.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a three-dimensional                additively manufactured component. Uses include but                are not limited to selective laser sintering, laser                melting, scanned laser fusion and sinterings,                electron beam melting, selecting inkjet binding,                and fused deposition modeling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing an additive                manufacturing machine to perform additively                manufacturing and the peening process alternately                and repeatedly so as to reduce or eliminate line of                sight access to the powder bed, thus reducing or                eliminating the need for line-of-sight access to a                post-processing system. The method allows the                additively manufactured part to be manufactured in                a cost-effective manner, and reduces the amount of                time required to manufacture the three-dimensional                part, thus improving the quality of the part and                reducing the time required for post-processing the                part, and hence reducing the manufacturing cost of                the parts and improving the manufacturing                efficiency of the additive manufacturing system in                an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for manufacturing a three-dimensional                additively manufactured component.
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); P52 (Metal punching, working, forging (B21DL).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).); P61 (Grinding, polishing (B24).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography); X24 (Electric Welding); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN113867459-A
TI Greenhouse integrated fertilizing system for controlling growth environment of crops in greenhouse, has power supply module for supplying power, and gas fertilizer control module, illumination control module and liquid control module,.
AU JIA Y
   LIN S
   LI Z
   MA H
   XIAO Y
   LIU F
   ZHOU L
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 202210771H
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a fertilizer system upper computer (1), a gas fertilizer control module (2), a light-emitting module (3) and a liquid fertilizer control device (4) connected with each other. The input port of the fertilizer system is used to receive greenhouse interior plant growth environment data. The output port of fertilizer plant upper computer is used for sending instructions for improving greenhouse plant growth environmental. The gas fertilizer module is connected with the fertilizer station upper computer for monitoring carbon dioxide content in the greenhouse and adjusting carbon dioxide amount. The light emitting diode (3), the illumination control module and the liquid fertilizer module are connected to the fertilizing plant upper computer for monitoring the illumination intensity in the greenhouse and adjusting the light intensity. The clapboard is obliquely arranged, so that the gas in the vertical direction slowly enters the aerogel glass preform, and the gas flow rate is slow and uniform. The gas flow uniformly flows in a supercritical state, when releasing gas, thus reducing the internal stress caused by gas entering and releasing, and inhibiting the phenomenon of bending and cracking. The hole diameter of the ventilation hole is 0.3-1 mm, thus preventing the box body from sliding, and providing a skid-proof pad to the bottom of the supporting column.

The method: forms the industrial waste residue by calcining at high temperature; hardly generates the carbon dioxide by calcining at high temperature again; reduces the limestone dosage; utilizes the mineralizing effect of the microelement in the industrial waste residue; adds an activating agent which is good for improving the burning property of the raw material; reduces the sintering stability of the clinker; improves = the performance of the clinker; reduces the sintering temperature of the low-carbon cement clinker by 100-150 degrees C than the sintering temperature of the traditional silicate cement clinker at 1450 degrees C; reduces the coal consumption; and reduces the discharge amount of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the resource saving type low-carbon cement clinker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The cement clinker specifically comprises 55 wt.% high-calcium low-magnesium limestone, 22.8 wt.% waste stone, 8 wt.% shale or sandstone, 7 wt.% slag, 2 wt.% sulfate or iron slag, 2 wt.% fly ash, 1 wt.% coal slag or coal gangue, 1 wt.% phosphorus slag and phosphogypsum, and 0.2 wt.% activating agent. Preferred Components: The waste stone includes high-magnesium waste stone, high-silica waste stone, and high-sulfur waste stone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CaO content of the waste stone is 35-46 wt.%. The MgO content of the high-magnesium waste stone is 3-6 wt.%. The silicon dioxide content of the high-silica waste stone is 10-20 wt.%. The sulfur content of the high-sulfur waste stone is 0.4-1 wt.%. The silicon dioxide content of the shale is 60-80 wt.%, and the aluminum oxide content is greater than 8 wt.%. The silicon dioxide content of the sandstone is greater than 75 wt.%, and the aluminum oxide content is less than 8 wt.%. The slag comprises electric furnace slag or converter slag. The CaO content of the electric furnace slag is greater than 35 wt.%, and the iron oxide content is greater than 15 wt.%. The CaO content of the converter slag is greater than 40 wt.%, and the iron oxide content is less than 10 wt.%. The iron oxide content of the iron slag and sulfuric acid slag is greater than 50 wt.%.

GA 2021A39256
AB    NOVELTY - Intelligent robot (100) for detecting the presence of insects and mosquitoes, to trap and cause natural death comprises: (a) an attraction module (101) in which the mosquitoes get attracted towards the robot by producing a carbon dioxide (105) in it by the reaction of an acetic acid and a sodium bicarbonate; (b) to capture the mosquitoes from longer distance, a microdrone can be launched; (c) if mosquitoes are detected then the robot signals the micro-drones to launch which has small suction units to get actuated as soon as the drone is launched; (d) the drone pushes the captured mosquitoes inside a trapping box; (e) the robot detects (102) the sound frequencies (107) using a sound sensor and fed to a microcontroller in which the detected frequency is compared with the sound frequency range of female mosquitoes and if the frequency matches then a suction unit (108) is triggered for pulling the mosquitoes inside the thermally insulated trapping box; and (f) an annihilation module.
   USE - The intelligent robot is useful for detecting the presence of insects and mosquitoes, to trap and cause natural death.
   ADVANTAGE - The mosquito eradication robot can autonomously locate mosquitoes, attract them and assemble them with in the robot for their eventual controlled natural death utilizing no environmentally harmful ways, performing these tasks on a schedule and mapped route as decided by the user with a potential to upgrade itself with new software and features as needed.

   Suction unit (108)

The light-transmitting components (140) are fastened with the electrode mounting components to form a sealed space in the electrode mounting components, and have a light-transmitting window, so that light can be irradiated to the gas diffusion electrode. The gas diffusion electrode is conductive and waterproof, and includes a current collector forming a contour in a planar structure and carbon paper filling the inside. A pump pressurizes the gas discharged from the exhaust port and injects the gas back into one of the inlet ports. The photocatalytic coating is made of titanium dioxide and tungsten trioxide. The optical window is made of a quartz material.
   USE - Gas diffusion reactor for utilizing photoreaction and electric reaction in carbon dioxide decomposition or air pollutant removal reactor.

The degree of reaction can be measured in real time while minimizing the bias of the photoreaction. The gas diffusion reactor is connected to the gas analyzer, so that the degree of the gas diffusion reaction can be checked in real time.

AE SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGIN INST (CNCC-C)

GA 202195043Y
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering and purifying hydrogen involves                subjecting semi-coke residue gas to purification,                compression and desulfurization processes, and                entering the semi-coke residue gas into two-section                pressure swing adsorption hydrogen purification                device under lower pressure of 0.3-1.0 MPa. The                pressure swing adsorption device is regenerated by                using a flushing method, where flushing gas is                partial desorption gas of another pressure swing                adsorption device. The former stage of pressure                swing adsorption device will most of the blue                carbon residue gas removal of carbon dioxide,                carbon, and some impurity gasses such as nitrogen,                carbon monoxide, methane to obtain purified gas                mainly hydrogen and nitrogen.
   USE - Method for recovering and purifying hydrogen                from semi-coke residue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes most of impurity gas by the                former section of pressure swing adsorption device                under the conditions of no vacuum pump and low                pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering and purifying hydrogen involves                subjecting semi-coke residue gas to purification,                compression and desulfurization processes, and                entering the semi-coke residue gas into two-section                pressure swing adsorption hydrogen purification                device under lower pressure of 0.3-1.0 MPa. The                pressure swing adsorption device is regenerated by                using a flushing method, where flushing gas is                partial desorption gas of another pressure swing                adsorption device. The former stage of pressure                swing adsorption device will most of the blue                carbon residue gas removal of carbon dioxide,                carbon, and some impurity gasses such as nitrogen,                carbon monoxide, methane to obtain purified gas                mainly hydrogen and nitrogen. The purified gas is                compressed and boosted for the second time, and                entered the second stage of the pressure swing                adsorption device at a higher pressure of 1.0-2.5                MPa to remove the remaining impurity gas in the                purified gas to obtain product hydrogen with                hydrogen purity greater than or equal to 99%.

PN CN112811999-A
TI Preparing acetylenic acid or its derivatives comprises e.g. performing lithium hydrogen exchange reaction of terminal alkyne solution and butyl lithium solution, and performing nucleophilic addition reaction of terminal alkynyl lithium.
AU WANG L
   XU Z
   CHEN D
   ZHANG Y
AE BIRDO SHANGHAI PHARMATECH CO LTD (BIRD-Non-standard)
GA 2021585869
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly preparing acetylenic acid or its derivatives (I) based on microchannel continuous flow technology, comprises (i) pumping terminal alkyne solution (II) and butyl lithium solution into first-stage microchannel reactor in certain equivalent ratio, carrying out lithium hydrogen exchange reaction at certain temperature for a certain time to obtain terminal alkynyl lithium, and (ii) passing terminal alkynyl lithium obtained in step (i) into second-stage microchannel reactor, mixing with certain equivalent ratio of carbon dioxide gas, reacting at certain temperature for certain time to obtain terminal alkynyl carboxylate lithium salt, and then acidifying product to obtain product terminal alkynyl carboxylic acid products.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly preparing acetylenic acid or its derivatives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high product efficiency, low-cost, mild reaction conditions and strong substrate universality through coupling of the microchannel continuous flow technology; and is environmentally friendly; reduces the risk factor of lithium-containing reagents, and enables the reaction to obtain higher product purity under controllable continuous conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rapidly preparing acetylenic acid or its derivatives of formula (I) based on microchannel continuous flow technology, comprises (i) pumping terminal alkyne solution of formula (II) and butyl lithium solution into first-stage microchannel reactor in certain equivalent ratio, carrying out lithium hydrogen exchange reaction at certain temperature for a certain time to obtain terminal alkynyl lithium, and (ii) passing terminal alkynyl lithium obtained in step (i) into second-stage microchannel reactor, mixing with certain equivalent ratio of carbon dioxide gas, reacting at certain temperature for certain time to obtain terminal alkynyl carboxylate lithium salt, and then acidifying product to obtain product terminal alkynyl carboxylic acid products.
   R = hydrogen or substituted/unsubstituted alkyl, alkoxy or phenyl, where substituent is alkyl, halo or phenyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): solvent used in terminal alkyne solution is at least one of tetrahydrofuran, toluene and anhydrous ether; and solvents used in the butyl lithium solution are n-hexane and n-heptane. In step (i): equivalent ratio of the terminal alkyne to butyl lithium is 1:1-2; in steps (i) or (ii), hydraulic diameter of the single channel and/or multiple channels in first stage microchannel reactor and the second stage microchannel reactor is 100-4000 mu m. In step (i): equivalent ratio of the terminal alkyne to butyl lithium is 1:1-1.5; in steps (i) or (ii), hydraulic diameters of the single channel and/or multiple channels in the first stage microchannel reactor and the second stage microchannel reactor are 100-2000 mu m. Preferred Method: In step (i): reaction temperature of lithium hydrogen exchange is -50 to 20 degrees C, and the reaction residence time is 5 seconds to 5 minutes. In step (i): reaction temperature of lithium hydrogen exchange is -20 to 10 degrees C, and the reaction residence time is 10 seconds to 2 minutes. In step (ii) during nucleophilic addition reaction, the equivalent ratio of terminal alkyne to carbon dioxide is 1:1-5; in step (ii): reaction temperature is -30 to 20 degrees C, and the reaction residence time is 0.1-30 seconds. In nucleophilic addition reaction in step (ii), the equivalent ratio of terminal alkyne to carbon dioxide is 1:1-3; the reaction temperature is -20 to 20 degrees C, and the reaction residence time is 0.1-10 seconds.

PN CN112588292-A
TI Methanation catalyst useful in carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide methanation reaction, comprises active component nickel, first auxiliary agent, second auxiliary agent, and carrier titanium dioxide aerogel.
AU YU B
   YU M
   ZHAO R
   MEI C
   LI C
AE DATANG INT CHEM TECHNOLOGY RES INST LTD (DATA-Non-standard)
GA 202135958F
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation catalyst comprises active component nickel (which accounts for 3-60 wt.% of weight of catalyst based on elemental metal), first auxiliary agent (which accounts for 2-30 wt.% of total weight of the catalyst based on oxide), second auxiliary agent (which accounts for 0.5-30 wt.% of total weight of catalyst based on the oxide), and carrier titanium dioxide aerogel (remaining amount); where, the first auxiliary agent is lanthanum oxide, cerium dioxide, praseodymium oxide and/or samarium(III) oxide, the second auxiliary agent is ferric oxide, chromium(III) oxide, cobalt(II,III) oxide, copper(II) oxide, zinc oxide, molybdenum trioxide, zirconium dioxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, strontium oxide and/or barium oxide.
   USE - The methanation catalyst is useful in carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide methanation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methanation catalyst: has improved low-temperature activity, and good thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the methanation catalyst comprising (1) mixing tetrabutyl titanate, absolute ethanol and water uniformly, adjusting the pH value to 1-5 with hydrochloric acid, allowing to stand, adjusting the pH value to 6-10 with aqueous ammonia, allowing to stand and aging, washing it with absolute ethanol, and supercritically drying to obtain a titanium dioxide aerogel carrier; (2) mixing and dissolving precursor of active component, precursor of first auxiliary agent, and precursor of second auxiliary agent in absolute ethanol to prepare an alcohol solution; (3) soaking the titanium dioxide aerogel carrier obtained in step (1) in the alcohol solution obtained in step (2), heating and stirring for 4-48 hours, washing with absolute ethanol, drying and roasting, obtaining oxidation state catalyst; and (4) reducing the oxidized catalyst in H2 or H2/N2 mixed gas to obtain a pre-reduced state catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the molar ratio of tetrabutyl titanate, absolute ethanol and deionized water is 1:5-20:1-5, preferably 1:8-15:2-4. In the process of preparing the alcohol solution in step (2), relative to 1000 g of anhydrous ethanol, the amount of the precursor of the active component is 20-60 g based on the elemental Ni; the amount of the precursor of the first auxiliary agent is 10-25 g based on oxide; the amount of the precursor of the second auxiliary agent is 10-35 g based on oxide. In the step (4), the H2 content in H2/N2 mixed gas is greater than 30 vol.%. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the temperature for preparing the alcohol solution is 30-80 degrees C. In the step (3), the roasting temperature is 600-850 degrees C. in the step (4), the reduction temperature is 500-650 degrees C. In the step (4), reduction condition includes volumetric space velocity of the reducing gas is 800-4000 per hour, heating to 500-600 degrees C at rate of 0.5-1 degrees C/minutes, and keeping this temperature for 4-8 hours.

PN CN112574494-A
TI Ethylene-vinyl acetate foaming material comprises            main material, filling agent, foaming agent, bridging            agent, foaming accelerator, lubricant, deodorant doping            material, and wear-resistant doping material.
AU ZHANG W
AE FUJIAN MEIKAIYUAN SHOES CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202138930N
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene-vinyl acetate foaming material                comprises 94-98 pts. wt. main material, 0.5-1 pts.                wt. filling agent, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. foaming agent,                1-1.5 pts. wt. bridging agent, 0.7-1.3 pts. wt.                foaming accelerator, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. lubricant,                0.01-0.03 pts. wt. deodorant doping material, and                0.05-0.1 pts. wt. wear-resistant doping material.                The main material is ethylene-vinyl acetate. The                filling agent is calcium carbonate. The foaming                agent is AC-3000H. The bridging agent is a mixture                of DTAIC and bis(t-butylperoxy isopropyl)benzene.                The foaming accelerator is a mixture of zinc oxide                powder and zinc stearate powder. The lubricant is                stearic acid lubricant. The deodorant doping                material adopts nano-silver particles. The                wear-resistant doping material adopts carbon fiber                component.
   USE - Used as ethylene-vinyl acetate foaming                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good ventilation,                deodorization effect and wear resistance; and                suitable for manufacturing sports material and                advanced slippers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing ethylene-vinyl acetate foaming material,                comprising (i) adding mixed material into                high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon                dioxide for adjusting pressure to 15 MPa, stirring                the high-pressure reaction kettle, and fully mixing                the material uniformly; (ii) adjusting the                temperature, controlling the temperature at 220                degrees C, and continuously stirring the material                to fully dissolve; (iii) controlling the pressure                in the reaction kettle at 10 MPa in discharging                part of carbon dioxide, keeping the temperature at                constant for 20 minutes, completely melting,                cooling, and shaping; and (iv) placing shaping                material in sealed rubber mixing machine for mixing                and plasticating, placing in an internal mixer for                mixing treatment, opening and refining,                discharging, vulcanizing and foaming, cooling and                forming, cutting, punching, printing, and obtaining                desired ethylene-vinyl acetate foaming                material.

TI All-solid-state lithium-ion secondary battery            system for reducing carbon-dioxide emissions by the            introduction of EV in the auto industry, comprises a            positive electrode that contains a positive electrode            active material layer.

AE NISSAN MOTOR CO LTD (NSMO-C)
   RENAULT SAS (RENA-C)
GA 2021298266
AB    NOVELTY - The all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery system (1) comprises a positive electrode                that contains a positive electrode active material                layer of a positive electrode active material. A                negative electrode contains a negative electrode                active material layer of negative electrode active                material that provides metallic lithium or                lithium-containing alloy. An all-solid-state                lithium-ion secondary battery (10) is provided with                an electric power generation element, which has a                solid electrolyte layer interposed between the                positive electrode active material layer and the                negative electrode active material layer. A                negative electrode thickness measurement unit                measures the thickness of the negative electrode                active material layer. A control unit (8)                calculates the first presumed State of Charge (SOC)                of the all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery.
   USE - All-solid-state lithium-ion secondary battery                system for reducing carbon-dioxide emissions by the                introduction of an Electric Vehicle (EV) and a                Hybrid Electric Vehicle (HEV) in the auto                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery system computes electrolyte resistance and                the reaction resistance of a secondary battery with                high precision from the measurement result of an                alternating current impedance, and easily estimates                SOC of the secondary battery based on the estimated                value of SOC.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a SOC                estimation apparatus for all-solid-state                lithium-ion secondary batteries.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary battery                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   1All-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery system
   2Voltage sensor
   3Temperature sensor
   8Control unit
   10All-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery

PN CN112521968-A; CN112521968-B
TI Recycling light hydrocarbon comprises sending hot and/or cold low oil from hydrocracking unit to debutanizer for separation, condensing gas phase, pressurizing and cooling liquid phase and removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
AU HUANG M
   WU D
   YAO A
   HAO S
   LIU K
AE SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 202131823A
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling light hydrocarbon comprises (i) sending the hot low oil and/or cold low oil from the hydrocracking unit to the debutanizer for separation, and sending liquid phase at the bottom of the debutanizer to the fractionation tower for separation of naphtha, diesel and tail oil, condensing gas phase by the condenser and sending to the reflux tank, compressing gas phase at the top of the reflux tank and cooling and sending to the gas-liquid separation tank I, and pressurizing and cooling liquid phase and sending to the gas-liquid separation tank I; (ii) mixing gas and liquid phases from the debutanizer, pre-absorbing and gas-liquid equilibrium in the gas-liquid separation tank I and removing the separated gas and liquid phases impurity; (iii) cooling top gas phase separated by the gas-liquid separation tank I, removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide with the lean amine liquid as the absorbent in the rich gas desulfurization tower and sending to the gas-liquid separation tank II.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling light hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process flow, mild operating conditions, and low cooling capacity consumption, realizes the separation and recovery of light hydrocarbons in the hydrocracking process with less equipment, especially ethane, achieves efficient separation and recovery of propane and methane components with recovery rate of ethane more than 97 wt.%, the recovery rate of propane is more than 98 wt.%, the methane content in the recovered ethane is not greater than 1 vol.%, and the ethane content in the recovered propane is not greater than 300 ppmv.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: a device for recycling light hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iv) sending liquid phase at the bottom of the tank bottom separated by the gas-liquid separation tank I to the gas-liquid separation tank II after cooling and removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower; (v) mixing the removed gas phase light hydrocarbons and liquid phase light hydrocarbons again in the gas-liquid separation tank II, pre-absorption and gas-liquid equilibrium and sending the separated gas and liquid phases to the absorption tower and the separation unit respectively; (vi) in the absorption tower, taking the mixed methane as the absorbent to absorb carbon and the components above ethane in the gas phase from the top of the gas-liquid separation tank II, while co-absorbing part of the methane, and sending the gas phase at the top of the absorption tower to the downstream device, and cooling the bottom liquid phase, returning to the gas-liquid separation tank II; (vii) separating liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank II into ethane, propane, and methane through the demethanizer, deethane tower, depropanizer I, and optional depropanizer II and isobutane tower in the separation unit, sending at least part of the separated methane component to the absorption tower as a mixed methane absorbent, and extracting the rest as a methane product; and (viii) in the absorbent recovery tower, taking at least part of the naphtha separated used as an absorbent to recover methane and above methane components in the gas phase from the top of the absorption tower, and the gas phase above the top of the absorbent recovery tower is used as dry gas Produced, returning the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the debutanizer. In the step (vii) the separation process includes: in the first mode, the separation steps includes: making the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank II to remove methane in the demethanizer, and simultaneously removing a small part of carbon dioxide and components above carbon dioxide, cooling and returning gas phase at the top of the methane tower to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the deethanizer, separating liquid phase from the bottom of the demethanizer from the carbon dioxide component in the de-ethanizer, extracting separated top gas phase as an ethane product, and sending the liquid components above propane and propane to the de-ethanizer propane tower I, separating the liquid phase components from the bottom of the deethanizer in the depropanizer I, and extracting the separated propane from the upper part of the depropanizer I, and taking at least a part of the bottom components as a mixed methane absorbent, sending to the absorption tower, and extracting the remaining part is extracted as methane product; in the second mode, the separation steps include: making the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank II to remove methane in the demethanizer, and simultaneously, removing a small part of carbon dioxide and components above carbon dioxide, cooling and returning gas phase at the top of the methane tower to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the isobutane removal tower; separating the liquid phase from the bottom of the demethanizer from the components below propane and propane in the deisobutane tower, and sending the separated top gas phase to the deethanizer, and taking at least part of the bottom liquid phase as a mixture, sending methane absorbent to the absorption tower, and extracting the rest as n-butane products; separating the liquid phase from the bottom of the isobutane removal tower the carbon dioxide components in the deethanization tower, extracting the separated top gas phase as an ethane product, and sending the liquid phase components at the bottom of the tower to the depropanization tower for separating the liquid phase components from the bottom of the de-ethanizer in the depropanizer I, and extracting the separated propane product from the top of the depropanizer I, and extracting the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower as an isobutane product; in the third mode, the separation steps include: making the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank II to remove methane in the demethanizer, and removing a small amount of carbon dioxide and components above carbon dioxide, cooling gas phase at the top of the methane tower and returning to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the depropanizer I; separating the liquid phase components from the bottom of the demethanizer in the depropanizer I, and extracting the separated components with propane and below propane from the top of the depropanizer I and sending to the deethanizer and sending at least a part of the bottom components to the absorption tower as a mixed methane absorbent, and extracting the rest as a methane product; separating the gas phase from the upper part of the depropanizer I in the de-ethanizer, extracting the separated top gas phase as an ethane product, and extracting the bottom liquid phase as a propane product; in the fourth mode, the separation steps: making the liquid phase from the bottom of the gas-liquid separation tank II to remove methane in the demethanizer, and, removing a small part of carbon dioxide and components above carbon dioxide, cooling gas phase at the top of the methane tower and returning to the gas-liquid separation tank II, and sending the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the depropanizer I; separating liquid phase components from the bottom of the demethanizer in the depropanizer I, and sending the separated top gas phase to the deethanizer, and taking at least a part of the liquid phase in the bottom of the tower as a mixed methane absorbent, sending to the absorption tower, and extracting the remaining part as methane product; separating the gas phase from the top of the depropanizer I in the de-ethanizer, extracting the separated top gas phase as an ethane product, and sending the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the depropanizer II for further separation; further separating the liquid phase from the bottom of the de-ethanizer tower in the depropanizer tower II, and extracting the separated top gas phase as a propane product, sending at least a part of the liquid phase at the bottom of the tower to the absorption tower as a mixed methane absorbent, and extracting the rest as a methane product. The operating temperature of the top of the debutanization tower is 50-80 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1-1.6 MPaG. The temperature of the reflux tank at the top of the debutanization tower is 35-45 degrees C. The initial boiling point of the naphtha is 25-45 degrees C, and the dry point is 165-200 degrees C. The operating temperature of the rich gas desulfurization tower is 35-45 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.15-2.75 MPaG. The operating temperature of the liquid hydrocarbon desulfurization tower is 35-45 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.55-3.15 MPaG. The operating temperature of the gas-liquid separation tank I is 35-45 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.15-2.75 MPaG. The operating pressure of the absorption tower is 2.1-2.7 MPaG, and the operating temperature is 5-25 degrees C and the absorbent used in the absorption tower does not need to be introduced from outside the system. The operating temperature of the top of the demethanizer is 15-40 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.25-2.85 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the deethanizer is 10-40 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 2.6-3.2 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the depropanizer I is 45-70 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1.4-2 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the depropanizer II is 45-65 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 1.5-1.9 MPaG. The operating temperature of the top of the isobutane removal tower is 45-65 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 0.4-1 MPaG.

The cooling gas loop has a cooling gas after-cooler (15) for adjusting the flow rate of the cooling gas introduced into the vertical reducing furnace and adjusting the temperature of the cooling gas at the inlet of the reducing furnace. The direct-reduced iron manufacturing equipment is capable of directly manufacturing the reduced iron by reducing iron oxide raw materials using a reducing gas and cooling in the vertical reducing furnace.
   USE - Equipment is used for manufacturing direct-reduced iron (claimed) for manufacturing steel.

PN US2020292240-A1; WO2020186044-A1; US11396828-B2
TI Cogeneration system for small-scale applications            used in refrigeration system, has backflow vapor line            that is placed along conduit loop and heat exchanger            which is placed along conduit loop, so as to extract            heat from working fluid.
AU CHASE D M
AE PRACTICAL SOLUTIONS LLC (PRAC-Non-standard)
   PRACTICAL SOLUTIONS LLC (PRAC-Non-standard)
   CHASE D M (CHAS-Individual)
GA 202090729A
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a conduit loop which is                configured to carry a working fluid using a Rankine                cycle. A valve system is placed along the conduit                loop, and is comprised with multiple valves that                are configured to manage flow of the working fluid                through a chamber (113). A backflow vapor line is                placed along the conduit loop, and is configured to                direct working fluid in a gaseous state to the                chamber. The working fluid in the gaseous state is                configured to displace working fluid in a liquid                state in the chamber and the working fluid in the                liquid state is advanced through the conduit loop                without requirement of a mechanical pump. A first                heat exchanger is placed along the conduit loop,                and is configured to extract heat from the working                fluid and direct the heat to a practical use. The                working fluid is carbon dioxide (CO2 ).
   USE - Cogeneration system for small-scale                applications such as domestic heating, cooling and                power generation, charging vehicles used in                refrigeration system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency and reliability of the                cogeneration system are improved. The improved                working fluid piping and conduit options to the                condenser and evaporator are allowed without                requiring lines and pipes to cross each other. The                larger diameter prevents excessive carbon dioxide                from travelling the shorter path to the adjacent                motor and pump. The downtime and latency in                performing the charge process is eliminated and the                overall cost of charging electric car vehicle (EV)                batteries is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                refrigeration system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                cogeneration system.
   Cogeneration system (100)
   Heat source (110)
   Chamber (113)
   Loop (114)
   Curved arrow (116)

   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
GA 202087873J
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises (E)-1,2,3,3,3-pentafluoro-1-propene, 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, difluoromethane (R-32), and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Composition used for producing cooling and heating in to-be-cooled and to-be heated components, and replacing R-410A and R-32 in air conditioning system, heat pump system, and refrigeration system (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to commercial, industrial and residential refrigerators and freezers, ice machines, self-contained coolers and freezers, vending machines, flooded evaporator chillers, direct expansion chillers, water chiller, centrifugal chillers, walk-in and reach-in coolers and freezers, transport refrigeration in trucks, airplanes and rail, and to-be-cooled and to-be heated components such as space and location such as an apartment, building, university dormitory, townhouse, single-family home, hospitals, office buildings, supermarkets, college and university classrooms, administration buildings and automobile and truck passenger compartments, to-be-cooled electronic devices such as computer equipment, CPU and personal computers.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition improves the refrigeration capacity, energy efficiency, and cooling performance of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) process of producing cooling in to-be-cooled component, which involves condensing the composition, and evaporating the composition in the vicinity of to-be-cooled component;
   (2) process of producing heating in to-be-heated component, which involves evaporating the composition and condensing the composition in the vicinity of to-be-heated component;
   (3) method of replacing mixture of 50 wt.% difluoromethane (HFC-32) and 50 wt.% 1,1,1,2,2-pentafluoroethane (HFC-125) (R-410A), or R-32 in refrigeration, air conditioning, and heat pump system, which involves providing the composition as replacement of R-410A or R-32; and
   (4) air conditioning system, heat pump system, or refrigeration system, which comprises the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system comprises an evaporator, compressor, condenser, and expansion device, and heat exchangers that operate in counter-current mode or cross-current mode with counter-current tendency.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises 28-37 wt.%, preferably 28-29 wt.% (E)-1,2,3,3,3-pentafluoro-1-propene, 27-36 wt.%, preferably 27-29 wt.% 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, 18-30 wt.%, preferably 28-30 wt.% R-32, and 9-15 wt.%, preferably 11-14 wt.% carbon dioxide and 1-11 wt.%, preferably 1-4 wt.% 1,1,1,2,2-pentafluoroethane. Preferred Property: The composition has global warming potential of 150 or less.

PT P
PN CN111569821-A
TI Composite adsorbent is prepared by loading metal oxide promoter in multi-level pore structure of alkali oxide solid waste carrier degassed under negative pressure.
AU LIU R
   WANG X
   XIAO T
   LIU L
   GAO S
AE CHINA HUANENG GROUP CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 202084482Y
AB    NOVELTY - Composite adsorbent is prepared by loading a metal oxide promoter in the multi-level pore structure of the alkali oxide solid waste carrier degassed under negative pressure, where the mass ratio of the metal oxide promoter to the solid waste carrier is (1-5):20.
   USE - The composite adsorbent is useful for desulfurization and decarbonization of biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite adsorbent effectively increases the removal rate of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, increases the concentration of the produced biomass natural gas product, effectively improves the utilization rate of alkaline oxide solid waste, has increased activity and capacity and extended life, speeds up the reaction rate, simultaneously removes carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in the biogas without secondary pollution, and is an efficient and cost-effective biogas purification technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the composite adsorbent, involving (i) sieving alkaline oxide solid waste by particle size before use, and formulating the metal oxide promoter into a salt solution, (ii) degassing the alkaline oxide solid waste under negative pressure at a pressure of 1-4 kPa, continuously maintaining the negative pressure after degassing, and soaking the alkaline oxide solid waste in the metal oxide salt solution, and (iii) after the dripping is completed, leaving still under normal temperature and pressure, drying, fully grinding, and calcining to obtain the product; and
   (2) an application of the composite adsorbent for desulfurization and decarbonization of biogas, where the composite adsorbent and the alkali liquid are made into a suspension with a pH of 8-10 according to the material-to-liquid ratio of 50-500 g composite adsorbent/l alkali liquid, and the biogas is introduced at 60-150 degrees C for desulfurization and decarbonization reaction under airtight conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the sieved alkaline oxide solid waste is irregular particles with a particle size of 90-300 microns. The metal oxide promoter comprises potassium oxide, sodium oxide, iron oxide, calcium oxide, copper oxide, zinc oxide or cerium oxide. The alkaline oxide solid waste comprises fly ash, calcium carbide slag, steel slag or waste cement. The alkali liquid is ammonia, sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide solution, sodium carbonate solution, sodium bicarbonate solution, potassium carbonate solution or potassium bicarbonate solution. Preferred Method: The salt solution is prepared by dissolving 1-15 %mass metal oxide promoter in acid and adding deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The degassing step (ii) is carried out under a negative pressure of 1-5 hours. The leaving still step (iii) is carried out for 1-24 hours, and the drying step (iii) is carried out at 100 degrees C. In step (iii), the heating rate of calcination and the cooling rate after calcination are both 5 degrees C/minute, and calcining for 2 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN111471507-A; CN111471507-B
TI New amide derivative as a recyclable carbon            quantum dot as an additive of lubricating oil and            prepared by mixing the carbon source with carbon            dioxide responsive amine and reaction medium uniformly            to obtain mixture and refluxing mixture.
AU TANG W
   LI Y
   ZHU X
   JIANG Z
AE UNIV PANZHIHUA (UPZH-C)
GA 202074516C
AB    NOVELTY - Amide derivative (I) as a recyclable carbon                quantum dot is new, where the average particle size                of the additive is 1-20 nanometer.
   USE - Amide derivative used as an additive of                lubricating oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The amide derivative is good for mass                production, and easy to prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amide derivative of formula (I) as a                recyclable carbon quantum dot is new, where the                average particle size of the additive is 1-20                nanometer..
   R=oxygen-containing group; and
   M=carbon chain containing an amine                group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an amide                derivative. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing a recyclable carbon                quantum dot nano lubricating oil additive, which                involves mixing carbon source with carbon dioxide                responsive amine and reaction medium uniformly to                obtain a mixture, refluxing mixture at 180-240                degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain a black                dispersion, distillating black dispersion under                reduced pressure to remove the reaction medium to                obtain a black jelly, washing and drying obtain the                target product, where the carbon source is                preferably one or more of citric acid monohydrate,                ascorbic acid, and gluconic acid; and
   (2) a method for recovering and recycling the                carbon quantum dot nano lubricating oil additive,                which involves collecting the used lubricating oil                containing carbon quantum dot nano lubricating oil                additive, mixing with water in a volume ratio of                1:0.53 after centrifugal treatment to obtain a                mixed solution, where the water is preferably                deionized water or ultrapure water, passing                carbondioxide into the mixed solution, transferring                carbon quantum dot nano lubricating oil additive to                the water phase, and collecting water phase,                passing nitrogen into the water phase collected,                and collecting precipitate and drying to obtain                final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation Claimed: The amide derivative is                  prepared by mixing the carbon source with carbon                  dioxide responsive amine and reaction medium                  uniformly to obtain a mixture. The mixture is                  refluxed at 180-240 degrees C for 2-6 hours to                  obtain a black dispersion. The black dispersion                  is distilled under reduced pressure to remove the                  reaction medium to obtain a black jelly, washed                  and dried to obtain the target product. The                  carbon source is citric acid monohydrate,                  ascorbic acid, and gluconic acid. The amine with                  CO2 responsiveness is preferably                  2-amino-5-diethylaminopentane,                  3-(dibutylamino)propylamine,                  1,4-bis(3-aminopropyl)piperazine, (s)-                  2-(aminomethyl)-1-ethylpyrrolidine and                  N,N-diisopropylethylenediamine and the reaction                  medium is n-hexanol, n-heptanol, and n-octanol.                  Preferred Conditionas: The molar ratio of the                  carbon source to the carbon dioxide responsive                  amine is 1:1-4.5. The ratio of the volume of the                  reaction medium to the molar mass of the carbon                  source is 500-1000:1 ml/mole. The reflux is                  performed under the protection of nitrogen, and                  stirring is also performed during the reflux,                  where the nitrogen gas flow rate is 10-4-10-2                  m3/minutes, and the magnetic stirring rate is                  50-500 rpm/minutes. The washing is carried out                  using acetone, and the number of washing times is                  preferably 2-8, the acetone in the solution after                  washing is removed by vacuum distillation. The                  drying temperature is 50-100 degrees C, and the                  drying time is 12-48 houtrs. Preferred                  Components: The lubricating oil is a                  low-viscosity polyalphaolefin base lubricating                  oil, and PAO4-PAO10 base lubricating oil.
