PN CN111333957-A; CN111333957-B
TI Insulating masterbatch comprises polypropylene,            silicon aerogel, graphite, and processing auxiliary            agent, where the polypropylene is propylene-ethylene            block or random copolymer, propylene-butene block or            random copolymer.
AU ZENG J
   HE R
   LIU H
   JIANG F
   YE M
AE WUXI HUITONG LIGHTWEIGHT MATERIALS CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202061147N
AB    NOVELTY - Insulating masterbatch comprises 100 pts. wt.                polypropylene, 10-40 pts. wt. silicon aerogel, 5-15                pts. wt. graphite, and 0.5-1 pats. wt. processing                auxiliary agent.
   USE - Insulating masterbatch.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulating masterbatch has good heat                insulation effect, excellent flame retardant                effect, improves the flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing a insulating                masterbatch, which involves mixing polypropylene                and processing aids uniformly, and adding to the                main feed port, adding silica aerogel and graphite                to the two side feeding ports under negative                pressure, setting the feed ratio of the main and                side feed ports, extruding and pelletizing to                obtain final product, a low thermal conductivity                and high flame retardant foamed polypropylene                beads, which comprises 100 pts. wt. polypropylene                resin, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. carbon black, 0.2-1 pts.                wt. talcum powder, 8-12 pts. wt. halogen-free flame                retardant, 3-5 pts. wt. antimony trioxide, 20-30                pts. wt. thermal insulation masterbatch, 0.5-5 pts.                wt. foaming auxiliary supercritical carbon dioxide,                and 0.3-0.7 pts. wt. auxiliary agent; and
   (2) a method for preparing low thermal                conductivity and high flame retardant foamed                polypropylene beads, which involves weighing each                component in proportion, mixing components, adding                to the extruder, performing, mixing, extrusion, and                wire drawing, using pelletizing equipment to                pelletize to form modified polypropylene particles,                placing modified polypropylene particles into the                reactor, introducing the foaming assistant                supercritical carbon dioxide, heating to 150-160                degrees C, pressurizing to 2.0-4.0 megaPascal,                producing an outwardly expanding interior under                high temperature and high pressure, releasing                pressure to atmospheric pressure in an instant to                obtain expanded polypropylene beads with an                expansion ratio of 45-60 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The particle size of                  the silicon aerogel is 5-50 nanometer, and the                  refractive index is 0.8-1.2. The graphite is                  flake graphite with a particle size of 3000-3500                  mesh and a carbon content of 99.9%. The accuracy                  of the weightlessness scale of the main feeding                  port is accurate to 1g, and the accuracy of the                  weightlessness scale of the two side feeding                  ports is accurate to 0.01g. The preparation                  process is carried out in a dust-free                  environment. The propylene content in the                  polypropylene resin is 90% by mole, and the melt                  index is 5-10 g/10 minutes. The particle size of                  the carbon black is 18-30 micrometer. The                  particle size of the talc powder is 5-15                  micrometer. The halogen-free flame retardant is                  an organic small molecule flame retardant, and                  the organic small molecule flame retardant has a                  phosphorus content of 15-25 wt.%, a moisture                  content of 0.5 wt.%, and a thermal decomposition                  temperature of 300 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The polypropylene is one or more of a                  propylene-ethylene block or random copolymer, a                  propylene-butene block or random copolymer, and a                  homopolypropylene, and the propylene content in                  the polypropylene is 90% mnole, melt index of                  5-10 g/10minutes. The processing auxilairy agent                  is one or more of compatibilizers, lubricants,                  and antioxidants. The polypropylene resin is a                  propylene-ethylene block or random copolymer, a                  propylene-butene block or regular copolymer and                  homopolypropylene. The auxiliary agent includes                  stabilizers, antioxidants, lubricants, and                  antistatic agents.

PT P
PN WO2020126974-A1; CN113195409-A; AR117466-A1; EP3898517-A1; US2022048783-A1
TI Producing lithium carbonate useful in lithium ion batteries, comprises e.g. forming brine containing lithium ions, calcium and magnesium ions, precipitating calcium and magnesium ions by adding precipitation agent and generating supernatant.
AU STEINHILBER D
   NEUFEIND S
   TZUSUKI S
   KRALIK J
   TSUZUKI S
   SHINTARO T
AE LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
   LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
   LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
GA 202057267R
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lithium carbonate, comprises (a) forming a brine containing at least lithium ions, calcium and magnesium ions, precipitating calcium and magnesium ions by adding precipitation agent, and generating supernatant, (b) reacting the supernatant with at least one chelate resin containing functional groups (I), (c) contacting the obtained product with at least one chelate resin containing functional groups (I), (d) adsorbing lithium on chelate resin containing functional groups (I), eluting by adding inorganic acids and, and adjusting the eluate to pH to greater than or equal to 7, and (e) mixing lithium-containing eluate with at least one carbonate or with carbon dioxide or its acid.
   USE - The lithium carbonate is useful in lithium ion batteries to store energy in electric vehicles, mobile telephones and network storage devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high purity lithium carbonate in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing lithium carbonate, comprises (a) forming a brine containing at least lithium ions, calcium and magnesium ions, precipitating calcium and magnesium ions by adding precipitation agent, and generating supernatant, (b) reacting the supernatant with at least one chelate resin containing functional groups of formula (G1-CH2BR1R2) (I), (c) contacting the obtained product with at least one chelate resin containing functional groups (I), (d) adsorbing lithium on chelate resin containing functional groups (I), eluting by adding inorganic acids and, and adjusting the eluate to pH to greater than or equal to 7, and (e) mixing lithium-containing eluate with at least one carbonate or with carbon dioxide or its acid.
   G1 = polystyrene copolymer backbone and FL;
   R1 and R2 = -CH2COOX, -CH2PO (OX1)2, -CH2PO(OH)OX2, - (CS)NH2, -CH2-pyridyl or H, where R1 and R2 both are not hydrogens at the same time; and
   X, X1 and X2 = H, Na, or K.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adjusting pH by adding basic precipitant or base into the precipitant and supernatant to 10-12. The steps (c) and (d) are carried out at pH of 10-12. The step (d) is carried out using hydrochloric acid as inorganic acid for elution. Preferred Components: The lithium carbonate is produced with purity of greater than or equal to 99 wt.%. The lithium-containing brine has lithium concentration of 0.1-5000 ppm, preferably 0.1-1000 ppm, sodium concentration of 0.1-100 g/l, calcium concentration of 0.1-100 g/l, and magnesium concentration of 0.1-100 g/l. The precipitation agent is sodium carbonate and/or sodium hydroxide. The molar ratio of precipitation agent and calcium and magnesium ions in the step (a) is 3:1-1:1. The weight concentration of calcium and magnesium in the step (b) is 5-50 ppb. The eluate in the step (d) has lithium concentration of 1-10 g/l, calcium and magnesium ions concentration of 2-20 ppb and sodium concentration of 1-50 g/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The bead polymer of the chelate resin containing functional groups (I) is monodisperse and macroporous; and has diameter of 250-450 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202044048J
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid fertilizer supply control system comprises a first temperature and humidity sensor to measure the temperature and humidity of the indoor air, a carbon dioxide sensor to measure the carbon dioxide concentration, and a first light quantity sensor to measure the amount of light in a room. A first environmental monitoring unit (1) has a medium monitoring module for measuring the temperature and moisture of the medium. A liquid fertilizer management unit (3) includes a supply tank and a dispensing par which selectively transfers the nutrient solution contained in the storage tank to an amount set in the supply tank. A liquid fertilizer supply unit (4) includes a pump to supply the mixed liquid of the storage tank to an indoor medium, a flow meter for measuring the amount of the mixed liquid supplied, and a solenoid valve to block supply. An interlocking control unit to control the liquid fertilizer management unit in response to the measurement result of the medium monitoring module.
   USE - The liquid fertilizer supply control system is useful for smart farm manure.
   ADVANTAGE - The system remotely checks and manages growth environment and operation status of crops such as temperature and humidity, soil moisture, nutrient solution; prevents excessive supply of nutrient solution by measuring concentration, and achieves efficient crop management by deriving by maintaining optimal crop growth conditions by controlling various indoor and outdoor environments.

TI Electromechanical device for operating artificial            manual breathing unit (AMBU) bag automatically, has            non-contact type proximity sensor that is attached to            limit switches, and microsensors comprising of carbon            dioxide sensors.
AU CHAUHAN R
AE CHAUHAN R (CHAU-Individual)
GA 202023916X
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a linear actuator portion                (8), two brushless stepper motors (3, 10), a lead                screw (2) mechanism, two plungers (1,4), and AMBU                bag (15). The brushless stepper motors are direct                current (DC) motors operating at 12 to 24 volts and                controlled by an open loop mechanism. The actuator                portion is attached to two plungers and is provided                with a speed regulator. The lead screw mechanism                comprises of a bearing block (5), two lead rotating                screws (6, 7), motor coupling, and linear bearings                (13) and linear guide (14). The non-contact type                proximity sensor is attached to the limit switches                (11, 12). The microsensors comprising of carbon                dioxide sensors and sensors to control volume and                pressure.
   USE - Electromechanical device for operating                artificial manual breathing unit (AMBU) bag                automatically.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is to provide a low cost, light                weight mechanical device to automatically operate                an artificial manual breathing unit in emergency                and trauma centres. The automatic delivery of                positive pressure ventilation and a controlled                tidal volume of air/oxygen is provided at a regular                rate without any human effort. The device is to                increase the survival chances and minimize                mortality due to respiratory failure due to paucity                of ventilators in developing countries, and take                care of complications and avoid lung injury due to                volutrauma and barotrauma as a result of                nonprofessional use of AMBU by over inflation and                excessive pressure.

PT P
PN CN110564332-A; CN110564332-B; WO2021057254-A1
TI Adhesive layer for adhesive tape, is formed by curing emulsion glue containing water-in-oil emulsion containing deionized water and solvent, thermosetting polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin, epoxy curing agent, solvent and color paste.
AU GU Z
   ZHOU S
   JI J
AE SUZHOU SHIHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU SHIHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6648E
AB    NOVELTY - An adhesive layer is formed by curing emulsion glue comprising 1-10 pts. wt. water-in-oil emulsion comprising 1-3 pts. wt. amphiphilic nonionic surfactant, 1-2 pts. wt. deionized water and 10-20 pts. wt. solvent (B), 100-200 pts. wt. thermosetting polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin, 30-150 pts. wt. tackifying resin, 1-5 pts. wt. epoxy curing agent, 10-30 pts. wt. isocyanate, 150-250 pts. wt. solvent (A) and 0-70 pts. wt. color paste. Several closed air bubbles are formed inside elastic pressure-sensitive adhesive layer.
   USE - Adhesive layer for adhesive tape (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive layer and the adhesive tape can be customized in thickness, viscosity, and compressibility based on requirements. The adhesive tape has excellent cushioning property, compressibility and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) adhesive tape, which comprises release film and adhesive layer; and
   (2) preparation of adhesive tape, which involves coating emulsion glue on release film, heating in drying tunnel at 50-80 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, removing solvents, passing epoxy curing agent and carboxyl groups in polyacrylate pressure sensitive resin, reacting isocyanate with hydroxyl groups in polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin to make adhesive layer initially cured, sending to 95-100 degrees C drying tunnel, heating for 2-8 minutes, breaking outer envelope of water droplets, releasing carbon dioxide through reaction of water with isocyanate, forming polyurea structure to surround carbon dioxide bubbles, where several closed bubbles are formed inside polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin, sending to 105-120 degrees C drying tunnel, heating for 1-3 minutes, removing solvent, curing and forming pressure-sensitive adhesive layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The isocyanate is a difunctional or trifunctional isocyanate, preferably a trimer of toluene diisocyanate or hexamethylene diisocyanate. The solvent (A) and solvent (B) are one or more of toluene, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, methyl ethyl ketone, and cyclohexanone. The amphiphilic non-ionic surfactant is one or more of DuPont FSN100 (RTM: Fluoro surfactant), FS10 and FS3100.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The thermosetting polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin has molecular weight distribution of 250000-650000 daltons calibrated with standard styrene, and bulk glass transition temperature of (-40)-(-15) degrees C. The softening point of the tackifying resin is 70-110 degrees C. The epoxy curing agent has molecular weight of 800 daltons or less. The diameter distribution range of the closed air bubbles is 30-150 mu m. The thickness of the elastic pressure-sensitive adhesive layer is 50-250 mu m. At an ambient temperature of 20 plus minus 0.5 degrees C and a relative humidity of 50 plus minus 5%, the viscosity of the emulsion glue is 2000-5500 mPa.second. The polyethylene terephthalate silicone release film has 20-hour aging release force of 10-50 gf/25 mm. The substrate has surface energy of 36-68 dyne. Preferred Composition: The thermosetting polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin comprises 5-7 wt.% functional monomer, 0.2-1 wt.% hydroxyl-containing functional monomer and balance carboxyl-containing functional monomers. Preferred Component: The tackifying resin is any one of a rosin resin, a terpene resin, and a petroleum resin. The epoxy curing agent is a tri-functional to tetra-functional epoxy resin. The adhesive tape further comprises substrate. The material of the release film is a polyethylene terephthalate silicone release film. The substrate is any one of polyethylene terephthalate, polyimide, polycarbonate, and polyurethane. Preferred Process: The preparation of emulsion glue involves stirring amphiphilic non-ionic surfactant and deionized water, dispersing to form uniform mixed solution, leaving still for 1-3 hours, observing that no bubbles are visible to naked eye, passing through an emulsification pump with solvent (B) to form a water-in-oil emulsion containing emulsion particles having diameter of 1-3 mu m, adding tackifying resin and thermosetting polyacrylate pressure-sensitive resin to solvent (A), stirring, dispersing, stirring, adding water-in-oil emulsion, stirring, adding color paste, epoxy curing agent and isocyanate to glue, stirring and filtering. The preparation of elastic pressure-sensitive adhesive tape further involves bonding the non-adhesive release film surfaces of the elastic pressure-sensitive adhesive layer to the two sides of the substrate or pasting non-adhesive release film of the elastic pressure-sensitive adhesive layer to make a complete product.
DC A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.);

TI Oxygen fire-proof function featured heat            dissipation power distribution cabinet, has control            plate electrically connected with fire inducing device,            and fire extinguisher whose outlet is connected with            electromagnetic valve.
AU LI X
AE HUNAN GUOFENG POWER EQUIP CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6483L
AB    NOVELTY - The cabinet has a power distribution cabinet                main body (1) whose upper end is rotationally                connected with a power distribution cabinet door                (2) that is provided with a transparent window (3).                A top upper end of the power distribution cabinet                door is formed with a ventilation hole (4). A                negative pressure fan (5) is fixed with the                ventilation hole. An inner side of the power                distribution cabinet main body is connected with a                fire sensing device (7) that is provided with a                temperature sensor (701) that is provided with a                smoke sensor (702). A lower part of the smoke                sensor is connected with a control circuit board                (703). A control plate (803) is electrically                connected with the fire inducing device. An outlet                of a fire extinguisher (802) is connected with an                electromagnetic valve (804). The fire extinguisher                is a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher.

PN US2019303859-A1
TI Method for designing vessel schedules for liner            shipping vessels, involves determining optimal vessel            schedule using multi-objective mixed integer nonlinear            optimization model, and delivering schedule to            graphical user interface.
AU DULEBENETS M A
AE UNIV FLORIDA A & M (UYFL-C)
GA 201983584E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a computer with                a graphical user interface and a processor. A first                objective e.g. total vessel weekly operation cost,                total container inventory cost and total vessel                late arrival cost, is retrieved to improve                scheduling for a vessel, where the first objective                conflicts with a second objective e.g. total fuel                consumption cost, total port handling cost and                total cost of carbon dioxide emissions. The                conflicting objectives are delivered to the                computer. An optimal vessel schedule is determined                using a global multi-objective mixed integer                nonlinear optimization model executed by the                processor in the computer using the objectives, and                the optimal vessel schedule is delivered to the                graphical user interface, where an optimal vessel                schedule minimizes monetary losses in operating the                vessel.
   USE - Method for designing vessel schedules for                liner shipping or oceangoing vessels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables facilitating vessel service                and handling rates at ports of call to improve G6                alliance flexibility in terms of selection of                vessel sailing speeds at voyage legs and vessel                handling times at ports, thus facilitating                efficient management of liner shipping operations                and reducing fuel consumption, port handling costs                and total amount of produced carbon dioxide                emissions. The method enables loading and unloading                containers at the ports to reduce a number of                containers that stay on vessels so as to decrease                container inventory cost at the ports.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a tangible non-transitory                computer-readable medium comprising a set of                instructions for running a software program on a                computing device
   (2) a system for optimizing vessel scheduling                in liner shipping.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                structure illustrating estimation of vessel waiting                time at ports.

PT P
PN US2019242338-A1; US10851744-B2
TI Method for combusting fuel stream, involves flowing portion of exhaust stream comprises carbon dioxide into multiple permeate zones of ion transport membrane assembly to form oxygen-enriched stream, delivering to combustion chamber.
AU HABIB M A M
   NEMITALLAH M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201968531B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves delivering an oxygen-containing stream (128) to a feed zone of an ion transport membrane assembly (102), where molecular oxygen present in oxygen-containing stream is transported across ion transport membranes to permeate zones. The molecular oxygen present in permeate zones is delivered to a combustion chamber (108). A fuel stream (122) is delivered to the combustion chamber to combust the fuel stream with the molecular oxygen to form an exhaust stream (124). The molecular oxygen and the fuel stream are mixed with a mixer placed upstream and a swirler placed inside the combustion chamber. The oxygen-containing stream is heat-exchanged with the exhaust stream through a heat exchanger (112). A portion of exhaust stream comprising carbon dioxide and optionally water vapors is flowed into permeate zones of the ion transport membrane assembly to form an oxygen-enriched stream (120). The oxygen-enriched stream is delivered to the combustion chamber.
   USE - Method for combusting fuel stream through combustion system.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel stream with a reduced sulfur content prevents formation of sulfur oxides in the exhaust stream, while a fuel stream with a reduced nitrogen content prevents formation of nitrogen oxides in the exhaust stream. The presence of a combustion chamber provides a continuous combustion, so that the exhaust flow is steady and no need for the use of accumulators to make the flow steady and prevent pulsation of the flow.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow of a combustion system.
   Ion transport membrane assembly (102)
   Combustion chamber (108)
   Heat exchanger (112)
   Oxygen enriched stream (120)
   Fuel stream (122)
   Exhaust stream (124)
   Oxygen containing stream (128)

PT P
PN WO2019151714-A1; KR2019094650-A
TI Multi-layer electrode of electrochemical cell for reduction reaction of substance, has reactant diffusion layer in which fine diffusion pores for moving reactants to catalyst and discharging products are randomly distributed.
AU JI K
   KYE J
   KIM K H
   KIM D B
   LIM E J
   KYEJEONGLL
   KIM G
   JA L
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 2019685959
AB    NOVELTY - The multi-layer electrode has a reduction reaction layer (AA) which takes place to reduce the substance. The reduction reaction layer includes pores through which ions penetrate, and includes a catalyst. A reactant diffusion layer (BB) in which the fine diffusion pores for moving the reactants to the catalyst and discharging products are randomly distributed. The pores in the reduction reaction layer are larger than the pores in the reactant diffusion layer. The specific surface area of the reactant diffusion layer is greater than the specific surface area of the reduction reaction layer.
   USE - Multi-layer electrode of electrochemical cell for reduction reaction of substance (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical reaction at the cathode surface is maximized such that the decomposition of carbon dioxide and the production efficiency of products such as carbon dioxide and higher hydrocarbons can be increased. The reactant diffusion layer is enabled to be uniformly supplied with the reaction gas to the reduction reaction layer by having disordered and dense fine pores. The backflow of the electrolyte during the reaction is prevented. The gaseous products converted in the reduction reaction layer can be extracted easily after the reduction reaction thus the change over time is minimized. The series resistance and contact resistance during the movement of electrons required during the electrochemical reaction can be minimized. The electrical loss can be minimized when implementing a commercialized or large area electrochemical cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the structure of the multilayer electrode. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Reduction reaction layer (AA)
   Reactant diffusion layer (BB)
   Electron movement (CC)
   Input (DD)
   Membrane (FF)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The catalyst is selected from copper, copper oxide, copper nitride, tantalum nitride, osmium nitride, niobium nitride, molybdenum nitride, iridium nitride, silver nitride, vanadium nitride, ruthenium nitride, manganese nitride, rhodium nitride, palladium nitride, nickel nitride, cobalt nitride, iron nitride, chromium nitride, hafnium nitride, titanium nitride, yttrium nitride, zirconium nitride, and scandium nitride.

PN CN110026199-A; CN110026199-B
TI Lanthanum carbonate-modified alumina-supported nickel-base catalyst useful for reforming methane reaction of synthesis gas.
AU GONG J
   LI K
   ZENG L
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019651799
AB    NOVELTY - Lanthanum carbonate-modified alumina-supported nickel-base catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is uniformly dispersed on the surface of the lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate (La2O2CO3) modified aluminum oxide support, where the total mass of the catalyst is used as a reference, the amount of the lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate supported in the catalyst is 20-25 wt.%, and the loading amount of the nickel nano particles is 1-10 wt.%.
   USE - The lanthanum carbonate-modified alumina-supported nickel-base catalyst is useful for reforming methane reaction of synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst proves that lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate can be adjusted by adjusting the interaction between nickel and aluminum oxide, improves reduction resistance of nickel, increases degree of reduction of nickel and active surface area, and improves catalytic activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing lanthanum carbonate-modified alumina-supported nickel-base catalyst, comprising loading 20-25 wt.% lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate, dissolving lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water to obtain precursor solution of lanthanum nitrate, and impregnating onto aluminum oxide, (ii) drying the sample obtained in the step (i) to dryness, calcining in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at 600-700 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate modified aluminum oxide carrier, (iii) dissolving nickel nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water to obtain precursor solution of nickel according to a loading amount of nickel nano particles of 1-10 wt.%, impregnating lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate modified aluminum oxide carrier obtained in the step (ii) into the precursor solution of the nickel in an equal volume, drying the sample to dryness, grinding, calcining carbon dioxide atmosphere at 600-700 degrees C for 4-6 hours, reducing at the same reaction temperature as the calcination temperature in an atmosphere of 10-20 vol.% hydrogen/nitrogen for 1-2 hours to obtain target catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The particle diameter of nickel nano particles is 7.5-10.4 nm. The lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate in the lanthanum dioxymonocarbonate modified aluminum oxide support is formed by calcination under carbon dioxide atmosphere. The catalyst is prepared by a stepwise equal volume impregnation method.

PT P
PN WO2019095031-A1; US2020386090-A1
TI Method for recovering and processing associated gas from oil-producing well, involves compressing cold natural gas stream to produce cold compressed natural gas (CCNG) stream.
AU LOURENCO J
   MILLAR M
AE 1304338 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
   1304342 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
   1304338 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
   1304342 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
GA 201943949V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves capturing associated gas produced from a wellhead. The associated gas is provided with methane and natural gas liquids (NGL) in vapor form. The associated gas is compressed to produce a pressurized natural gas stream. The pressurized natural gas stream is cooled to produce a cooled rich natural gas stream. The compressed natural gas (CNG) stream is produced by compressing a portion of the lean natural gas stream to a pressure greater than the fuel gas stream. The portion of the lean natural gas stream is passed through a carbon dioxide stripping unit (51) to produce a stripped gas stream. The stripped gas stream is expanded to achieve cryogenic temperatures sufficient to condense a portion of the stripped gas stream. The cooled, condensed stripped gas stream is passed to obtain the LNG stream and a cold natural gas stream. The cold natural gas stream is compressed to produce a CCNG stream.
   USE - Method for recovering and processing associated gas from oil-producing well to produce NGL, CCNG, CNG and liquid natural gas (LNG).
   ADVANTAGE - The higher recovery of associated gas co-produced from the oil production facility economically is achieved both in capital and operating costs. The likelihood of the products being able to be transported economically is greatly increased. The density is increased by increasing the pressure and decreasing the temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the process used to recover and process hydrocarbyl gases co-produced at oil production facilities equipped with compressors, heat exchangers, an in-line mixer, separators, pumps, a fractionator, a stripper and a regenerator.
   Heat exchanger (14,16,44,54,73)
   Hydrocarbyl separator (18,56,63,99)
   Warmer stream (22)
   Carbon dioxide stripping unit (51)
   Methanol pump (105)

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21)); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PT P
PN CN109480134-A
TI Pneumatic fruit juice gel beverage useful for polyethylene terephthalate bottle production, comprises e.g. white sugar, fruit syrup, concentrated juice, thickening agent, coagulant, pH regulator, preservatives and food flavor.
AU HUANG B
   WANG L
   CUI L
   FU Q
   JIN Y
   OU K
   LI Y
AE HANGZHOU WAHAHA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU WAHAHA GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019284772
AB    NOVELTY - Pneumatic fruit juice gel beverage for polyethylene terephthalate bottle production, comprises 0-110 g white sugar, 0-150 g fruit syrup, 0-30 g concentrated juice, 0.5-3 g thickening agent, 1-5 g coagulant, 0.5-2 g pH regulator, 0.05-0.5 g preservatives, 0.1-1 g food flavor, 1.5-3 carbon dioxide times the volume of the liquid and water (remaining amount).
   USE - The beverage is useful for polyethylene terephthalate bottle production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also preparing the beverage comprising (i) adding 8-15 times of normal temperature pure water, adding pH regulator, stirring for 5-10 minutes, sterilizing and cooling to 10-15 degrees C, (ii) dispersing thickener in pure water at room temperature of 20-30 degrees C, dissolving thoroughly and cooling, (iii) adding white sugar to 70-80 degrees c water, dissolving thoroughly, filtering and cooling, (iv) dissolving preservative with water at 20-30 degrees C, (v) dissolving coagulant thoroughly with pure water at 20-30 degrees C, (vi) dissolving flavor thoroughly with pure water of 20-30 degrees C, (vii) adding sugar, preservative G, concentrated juice, acidity regulator, thickener, coagulant, flavor, and constant volume under stirring, where the coagulant is added after the thickener, preservative G before the juice concentrate and pH regulator, (viii) cooling solid solution to 5-15 degrees C and filling with carbon dioxide gas, (ix) filling the liquid filled with the gas in a PET bottle and heating by a returning bed at 55-85 degrees C for 5-70 minutes, and (x) sterilizing the product, labeling and coding, naturally cooling to normal temperature to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The gel comprises 0-100 g white sugar, 0-130 g fruit syrup, 16-30 g concentrated juice, 0.8-2.5 g thickening agent, 1.5-4 g coagulant, 0.8-1.8 g acidity regulator, 0.1-0.4 g preservatives, 0.3-0.8 g food flavor, 1.5-3 carbon dioxide times the volume of the liquid and water (remaining amount). Preferred Components: The concentrated juice comprises concentrated grape juice, concentrated apple juice, concentrated pear juice, concentrated white peach juice and/or concentrating honey peach juice. The thickening agent comprises 50-100% of the A component and 0-50% of the B component by weight ratio. The A component is carrageenan, and the B component is locust bean gum. The coagulant comprises 30 to 70% of a C component and 30 to 70% of a D component by weight. The C component is potassium chloride, and the D component is calcium lactate. The pH regulator comprises 70-100% E component and 0-30% F component by weight ratio. The E component is citric acid, and the F component is potassium citrate. The preservative comprises 0 to 50% of the G component and 50-100% of the H component by weight ratio; the G component is potassium sorbate or sodium benzoate, and the H component is Velcorin (RTM: Dimethyldicarbonate). Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises i) adding 8-15 times of normal temperature pure water, adding pH regulator, stirring for 5-10 minutes, sterilizing and cooling to 10-15 degrees C, (ii) dispersing thickener in pure water at room temperature of 20-30 degrees C, dissolving thoroughly and cooling, (iii) adding white sugar to 70-80 degrees c water, dissolving thoroughly, filtering and cooling, (iv) dissolving preservative with water at 20-30 degrees C, (v) dissolving coagulant thoroughly with pure water at 20-30 degrees C, (vi) dissolving flavor thoroughly with pure water of 20-30 degrees C, (vii) adding sugar, preservative G, concentrated juice, acidity regulator, thickener, coagulant, flavor, and constant volume under stirring, where the coagulant is added after the thickener, preservative G before the juice concentrate and pH regulator, (viii) cooling solid solution to 5-15 degrees C and filling with carbon dioxide gas, (ix) filling the liquid filled with the gas in a PET bottle and heating by a returning bed at 55-65 degrees C for 5-70 minutes, and (x) sterilizing the product, labeling and coding, naturally cooling to normal temperature to obtain the finished product. In the step (i), the sterilization conditions are maintained at 85-95 degrees C for 3-10 minutes.

GA 201914337X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting or predicting inside temperature of the vehicle, an outside temperature, a temperature of an engine serving as a heating source, an indoor humidity of the vehicle, carbon dioxide concentration and rear seat passenger information by a controller. The outdoor state of the vehicle, an indoor state of the vehicle and a user's intention based on the detected or predicted information and a blower setting mode are comprehensively determined. An inlet door of the air conditioner is controlled according to one of multiple designated steps.
   USE - Method for controlling air conditioner for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The heating efficiency of the front seat is improved, using the characteristic of the indoor air circulating in the vehicle when the inlet door control is performed in which partial indoor air is additionally introduced while the air mixture of the outdoor air and the indoor air is discharged through the top of the blower fan. The power consumption for heating is reduced, while improving the fuel efficiency. The control method can control the blower inlet door of the air conditioner in a stepwise manner depending on a detailed heating state, while minimizing heat ventilated to the outside of the vehicle and increasing the average indoor temperature and the thermal sensation of the upper portion of the passenger. The control method can properly control moisture on the window and carbon dioxide concentration which occurs when excessive indoor air is continuously circulated and therefore the indoor air quality of the vehicle is maintained while securing a view for safe driving.

PT P
PN CN109201005-A; CN109201005-B
TI Preparing dynamic covalent for adsorbing phenolic compound in water, involves taking raw material tetraamine compound, adding water and catalyst to dissolve, adding glyoxal at room temperature, stirring and placing gel in oven.
AU YANG Z
   GAN C
   ZHANG J
   JI H
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2019076127
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dynamic covalent imine gel adsorbent for adsorbing phenolic compound in water, involves taking a raw material tetraamine compound, adding water and a catalyst to dissolve, slowly adding glyoxal at room temperature, stirring and placing the gel in an oven at 80 degrees C for 2 to 3 days, cooling to room temperature, soaking for 3 to 4 days with tetrahydrofuran , and change the solvent every other day, and protecting under argon gas, taking the gel to the double-mouth round bottom, adding chloromethyl ether and tetrahydrofuran at 30-40 degrees C under reflux conditions, stirring the reaction for 50-80 hours. The mixture is wash with acetone, exchange solvent with absolute ethanol, centrifuged to remove the supernatant, and subcritical carbon dioxide to dry the gas.
   USE - Method for preparing dynamic covalent imine gel adsorbent for adsorbing phenolic compound in water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare dynamic covalent imine gel adsorbent in simple manner which has stable adsorption performance and does not generate secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for adsorbing and recovering phenolic compounds in industrial wastewater, which involves:
   (A) using the dynamic covalent imine gel adsorbent, putting the adsorbent into phenol-containing material;
   (B) oscillating industrial wastewater of the compound at room temperature, filtering and separating, and absorbing phenolic compound in the solution and retaining in the adsorbent;
   (C) adsorbing adsorbed material by the adsorption is put into the desorbent, oscillating at room temperature, and separating by filtration;
   (D) recovering desorbent solution containing the phenolic compound and the adsorbent; and
   (E) adding adsorbent to subcritical carbon dioxide drying and storing for the next repeated use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material tetraamine compound is tetraphenylaminomethane or tetraphenylaminoethylene. The catalyst is glacial acetic acid. The phenolic compound contains at least one of bisphenol A, 1-naphthol, phenol and p-methylphenol. Preferred Condition: 5 to 30 mg adsorbent material is added per 100 mL wastewater, the shaking time is 10 to 24 hours, and the temperature is 15 to 35 degrees C. The desorbent is anhydrous ethanol. The desorption time is 10 to 20 hours, and the temperature is 15 to 35 degrees C.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN108384945-A
TI Leaching dephosphorization of phosphorus-containing iron ore by refining phosphorus-containing iron ore raw material, alkali leaching, performing liquid-solid separation, leaching, concentrating, crystallizing, and dephosphorizing.
AU FAN Y
   FENG L
   ZHANG D
   YUAN C
AE BGRIMM TECHNOLOGY GROUP (BEJM-C)
GA 201864236T
AB    NOVELTY - Leaching dephosphorization of phosphorus-containing iron ore comprises refining phosphorus-containing iron ore raw material; subjecting to alkali leaching treatment, performing liquid-solid separation to obtain combined iron ore and leaching solution containing aluminum and phosphorus, supplementing leachate with caustic alkali, circulating, leaching, and enriching; concentrating leaching solution containing aluminum and phosphorus, crystallizing crude sodium phosphate and dephosphorized high aluminum solution, processing, partially dephosphorizing high alumina solution, and returning to second step; and evaporating partially dephosphorized high aluminum solution, passing carbon dioxide, crystallizing sodium carbonate, separating sodium carbonate by seeding aluminum hydroxide, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain aluminum hydroxide and seed mother liquor, calcining aluminum hydroxide to produce alumina, and returning seed mother liquor to second step.
   USE - Method for leaching dephosphorization of phosphorus-containing iron ore.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Leaching dephosphorization of phosphorus-containing iron ore comprises refining phosphorus-containing iron ore raw material by mechanical method to control particle size of phosphorus-containing iron ore raw material; subjecting to alkali leaching treatment, performing liquid-solid separation to obtain combined iron ore and leaching solution containing aluminum and phosphorus, supplementing aluminum and phosphorus containing leachate with caustic alkali, circulating, leaching, and enriching with aluminum and phosphorus content; concentrating leaching solution containing aluminum and phosphorus by evaporating, crystallizing crude sodium phosphate and dephosphorized high aluminum solution, processing to prepare sodium phosphate product, partially dephosphorizing high alumina solution, and returning to second step to carry out alkali leaching dephosphorization; and evaporating partially dephosphorized high aluminum solution, placing carbon dioxide, adjusting concentration of alumina and sodium oxide to crystallize sodium carbonate, separating sodium carbonate by seeding aluminum hydroxide, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain aluminum hydroxide and seed mother liquor, calcining aluminum hydroxide to produce alumina, returning seed mother liquor to second step, alkaline leaching and dephosphorizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The leaching is carried out at 50-200 degrees C for 1-10 hours.

PT P
PN WO2018141067-A1; WO2018141067-A9; CA3052317-A1; US2020010348-A1; US11235998-B2; US2022212972-A1
TI Usage of ionic liquid for e.g. treating liquid waste streams from industrial process, involves separating solid, finely dispersed particulate phase from liquid waste streams which are tailings composed of water, bitumen, and particles.
AU BRYANT S
   STEPHENSON T
   ROGERS R D
   BERTON P
   BRYANT S L
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
   UNIV MCGILL (UMCG-C)
   ADJACENCY LABS CORP (ADJA-Non-standard)
   ADJACENCY LABS CORP (ADJA-Non-standard)
   ADJACENCY LABS CORP (ADJA-Non-standard)
GA 201861438Q
AB    NOVELTY - Usage of ionic liquid for treating liquid waste streams in the form of slurry from industrial process e.g. processing of oil sand involves separating a solid, finely dispersed particulate phase from liquid waste streams which are tailings composed of an intimate mixture of water, bitumen, and particles, which are dispersed to form slurry.
   USE - Usage of ionic liquid e.g. surface active ionic liquid for treating liquid waste streams in the form of slurry from industrial process e.g. processing of oil sand, tar sand, oil shale, oil contaminated sand or oil contaminated earth, tailings pond material and/or sand containing crude oil, for breaking oil-water emulsion in treatment (breakage) of steam assisted gravity drainage and heavy oil emulsions, for eliciting miscible displacement of oil from porous mineral reservoir, for transport carbon dioxide, and as bitumen diluent for providing stable ionic liquid-dilbit having very low vapor pressure and density less than that of water, and as corrosion inhibitor for steel infrastructure (all claimed). Can also be used in solids removal from oil processing stream, in-situ bitumen recovery, remediation of spilled bitumen and dilbit, in-situ extraction from mineral reservoir, production well chemical, and carbon dioxide sequestration and fracking fluids.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid has improved selective solubility and miscibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for processing of mature fine tailings (MFT), which involves mixing ionic liquid with a MFT mixture comprising clay particles and bitumen contaminants suspended in saline MFT water to form an ionic liquid/MFT phase, and separating the ionic liquid/MFT phase into two phases including a phase (p1) comprising the clay particles, bitumen contaminants and the ionic liquid, and an aqueous phase (p2) comprising more than 30% MFT water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The particles comprise fine clay particles. Preferred Property: The particles have a particle size of 44 mu m. Preferred Process: The waste stream is gravity settled in a tailings pond, such that its solids content is more than 40 wt.% to form MFT. The ionic liquid is mixed with the slurry to cause aggregation and/or gelation of the mixture. The slurry is diluted with a saline solution prior to the addition of the ionic liquid. The mixture comprising slurry, saline solution, and ionic liquid is gravity settled to promote the separation of the solid phase that is a mixture of clay particles, ionic liquid, and bitumen. The mixture is centrifuged at 0-14000 rpm to promote the separation of the solid phase. The ionic liquid is mixed with (nano)particles which are magnetic and/or superparamagnetic particles to form a nanoparticle-ionic liquid dispersion prior to its addition to the slurry. The saline solution is an aqueous solution of sodium chloride or another salt with a concentration of 0-2.57 mol/L. The superparamagnetic nanoparticles are added to the slurry and mixed so as to promote aggregation of the clay particles with the superparamagnetic nanoparticles, where the solid phase is rendered magnetic. A magnetic field from a permanent or electromagnet is used to separate the solid phase which is rendered magnetic, and is comprised of clay particles, ionic liquid, bitumen, and superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles. The ionic liquid is recovered from the solid phase by solvent extraction to form a solvent loaded with ionic liquid. The ionic liquid is recovered by a thin film treatment or pervaporation. The usage of the ionic liquid is performed at 0-200 degrees C and 0-4 atm. The usage of the ionic liquid further involves treating the MFT with carbon dioxide so as to lower pH, where carbon dioxide is transported for MFT treatment as a dissolved gas in the ionic liquid. The bitumen is extracted from oil sand into a phase comprising a mixture of the ionic liquid and bitumen, at ambient temperature and pressure. The mixture is separated by freeze-thawing at -10 degrees C to 80 degrees C, and/or by heating, filtration, centrifugation and/or gravity settling. Carbon dioxide reacts with one of the ions of the ionic liquid, or the carbon dioxide dissolves in the ionic liquid. The processing of mature fine tailings further involves adding an aqueous saline MFT diluent to the MFT mixture prior to mixing with the ionic liquid, adding magnetic particles, preferably superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles with a particle size of 20-30 nm to the ionic liquid, removing ionic liquids from the phase (p1) by solvent extraction to form a solvent loaded with ionic liquid, and recovering ionic liquids from the solvent loaded with ionic liquid by evaporation, thin film treatment or pervaporation. The saline MFT diluent is added to provide MFT mixture diluted to 30 wt.% solids, and the ionic liquid is added for mixing to provide 9 vol.% ionic liquid. The aqueous saline MFT diluent is approximately 1.7 mol/L sodium chloride. The separation the ionic liquid/MFT phase is performed by centrifugation. The separation of the ionic liquid/MFT gel into two phases involves magnetically separation into the phases (p1) and (p2). The processing of mature fine tailings is performed at 0-100 degrees C and at atmospheric pressure. Preferred Property: The ionic liquid has a melting point of less than 200 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The superparamagnetic nanoparticles are phase of iron oxide, magnetite (Fe3O4), or maghemite ( gamma -Fe2O3), with a particle size of 1-100 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The waste stream comprises 20-50 wt.% solids, 1-2 wt.% bitumen, and remainder of water. The solvent is a volatile organic solvent or a fatty acid, preferably acetone, isopropyl alcohol, ethyl acetate, octylamine, or oleic acid. The content of volatile organic solvents is 0-30 wt.%. The ionic liquid comprises cation(s) comprising amines with alkyl chain(s), preferably primary ammonium cation(s), secondary, tertiary, quaternary amines, or cyclic amines, and anion(s) comprising optionally saturated primary carboxylic acid(s). The ionic liquid is preferably octylammonium oleate, butylammonium acetate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate, hexylammonium butyrate, or choline acetate. The ionic liquid comprises an unsaturated fatty acid anion, preferably oleate or linoleate anion.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H03 (Transportation and storage - only large scale systems are included. Road tankers and retail petrol station-type applications are excluded. Treatment of pollution from marine oil tankers is included.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)
MC H03-G02; H04-E; D04-A01F1; D04-A01N; D04-B03
IP C02F-001/00; C02F-001/26; C02F-001/38; C02F-001/48; C10G-001/04; C10G-033/04; C10G-075/02; C02F-009/00; C02F-101/32; C02F-103/10; C02F-103/36

PT P
PN CN207553426-U; CN109296124-A
TI Fireproof building composite board has main portion whose side end is opened with concave containing groove provided with cover sheet which is arranged with array arrangement of air holes.
AU LV H
AE LV H (LVHH-Individual)
GA 201852822S
AB    (CN207553426-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fireproof building composite board, comprising a surface main body provided with a concave holding groove; the holding groove opening is provided with a cover sheet is provided with array arrangement of air hole, the containing groove is filled with sodium bicarbonate powder; the inner side of the cover is covered with a latex layer.    USE - Furfuryl alcohol-rich plant blend oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The furfuryl alcohol-rich plant blend oil increases the nutrient value and the commodity value of the product oil, and improves the nutritive value of the plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing furfuryl alcohol-rich plant blend oil, which involves:
   (A) weighing raw materials, shelling raw material after microwave treatment, crushing raw material crushing, mixing uniformly, then adding into extracting kettle of multistage supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, regulating the extraction at 40-60 degrees C with 28-40 megapascal for 1.5-3.5 hours, adding carbon dioxide and supercritical state mixture into separation kettle for decompressing and separating, adjusting process conditions separation kettle, separating kettle I at 35-55 degrees C, separating kettle I pressure 10-20 megapascal to obtain vegetable blend oil from the separating kettle I; and
   (B) refining vegetable blend oil obtained in the extraction of the plant blend crude oil into molecular distilling sample tank, adjusting the molecular distillation technology parameter with 2-9 pascal vacuum degree, where distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, scraping speed is 100-300 revolution per minute, feeding speed is 10-50 mL per minute and the condensing temperature is 30-50 degrees C, refining process, refining process is finished, collecting and refining the obtained blend to obtain furfuryl alcohol-rich plant blend oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The furfuryl alcohol-rich plant blend oil comprises 32-40 wt.% of rapeseed, 31-40 wt.% of maize germ, 2-4 wt.% of sesame, 15-18 wt.% of rice bran, 10-11 wt.% of seabuckthorn seed. Preferred Process: The shelling in the tempering machine, stirred and water is added. The stirring speed is 120-150 revolution per minute until the content of the raw material is adjusted to 11-15%. The tiled tunnel microwave oven conveyor belt adjusts moisture content of raw material. The raw material thickness is 2-4 cm. The microwave power is 2.5-4.8 kilowatts. The microwave processing time is 2-6 minutes. The final temperature of the material in the container is 100-150 degrees C. The method of separating kettle further involves separation kettle II and separation vessel III. The separating kettle II temperature is 30-50 degrees C. The separating kettle II pressure is 4-8 megapascal. The separating temperature is 30-50 degrees C. The separation vessel III pressure is 4-8 megapascal.

PN CN107897762-A; CN107897762-B
TI Preparing seaweed fish sausage comprises preparing            seaweed ultrafine powder, pre-treating fresh fish,            rinsing, dehydrating, treating, mixing fish pellets,            adding, subjecting to radio frequency treatment,            heating, cooling and packaging.
AU GUO Z
   WANG J
   ZHANG Y
   SUN S
   LIN X
   LIN H
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2018302087
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing seaweed fish sausage comprises (i)                preparing seaweed ultrafine powder, pre-treating                fresh fish and harvesting meat to obtain fish meat;                (ii) rinsing fish meat with neutral electrolyzed                water and dehydrating; (iii) treating the                dehydrated fish with high-density carbon dioxide                technology and mincing to obtain fish pellets; (iv)                mixing fish pellets, adding e.g. salt, monosodium                glutamate and seaweed ultrafine powder and mixing                to obtain fish gills and filling gills into protein                casings; and (v) subjecting to radio frequency                treatment, heating, cooling and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing seaweed                fish sausage.
   ADVANTAGE - The seaweed fish sausage has low salt content                and good gel strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing seaweed fish sausage comprises (i)                passing seaweed coarse powder through 60-mesh                screen, crushing by a gas flow ultrafine pulverizer                and sieving through 300-600 mesh to obtain seaweed                ultrafine powder, pre-treating fresh fish and                harvesting meat to obtain fish meat; (ii) rinsing                fish meat with neutral electrolyzed water having a                temperature of 20 degrees C or less and                dehydrating; (iii) treating the dehydrated fish                with high-density carbon dioxide technology and                mincing to obtain fish pellets; (iv) mixing the                fish pellets for 3-5 minutes, adding salt,                monosodium glutamate, pepper, vinegar, cooking                wine, taste nucleotide disodium, and seaweed                ultrafine powder, mixing continuously for 5-8                minutes to obtain fish gills and fill the gills                into protein casings, where the mixing temperature                is controlled at 0-5 degrees C, the amount of the                salt accounted for 1-1.5% of the mass of fish used;                and (v) subjecting the product to radio frequency                treatment and heating by resistance heating,                cooling and packaging. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for the seaweed fish sausage by the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the gas                  flow pressure during the crushing process of the                  air flow ultrafine pulverizer is 0.5-0.8 MPa, and                  the feed rate is 80-100 kg/hour. In the step (i),                  the pretreatment comprises subjecting fish to                  initial water washing, removing scale, fish head,                  fish tail, fish bone and skin, visceral washing,                  and secondary water washing. In the step (ii),                  the neutral electrolyzed water is obtained by                  treating with an electrolytic water processor at                  a current of 3 A and a voltage of 4.7-5.3 V, and                  the chloride ion concentration of the neutral                  electrolyzed water is 0.012 mol/l, and                  dehydrating of fish is performed using a screw                  filter. In the step (iii), the high-density                  carbon dioxide technology processing conditions                  for the pressure is 20-30 MPa, temperature is                  32-37 degrees C and time is 12-15 minute. In the                  step (iv), the raw materials comprises 0.5-2.0%                  monosodiumglutamate, 0.1-0.5% pepper powder,                  0.5-1.5% vinegar, 1.0 -2.5% cooking wine,                  20.04-0.08% taste nucleotide disodium and 20-25%                  seaweed ultrafine powder. In the step (v), the                  frequency of radiofrequency processing is carried                  out at 60-70 degrees C under 25-28 MHz. In the                  step (v), the resistance heating of                  radiofrequency-treated product is carried out at                  85 degrees C for 1-2 minutes, and the applied                  voltage gradient is 4.0 V/cm and the total                  heating time is 5-8 minutes.

PN CN107879326-A
TI Method for preparing dry ice-based graphene, involves mixing high-purity expanded graphite, anionic surfactant, dispersant, defoamer and solvent, adding deionized water and ethanol, injecting dry ice solid, melting.
AU LIU X
   WEI X
   WEI Z
AE CHENGDU GELAIFEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201828028L
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing dry ice-based graphene, involves mixing high-purity expanded graphite, anionic surfactant, dispersant, defoamer and solvent, mechanically stirring to form mixed slurry, crushing, adding crushed particles to liquid carbon dioxide, solidifying to form dry ice solid, adding deionized water and ethanol, injecting dry ice solid into the bottom of the container through the injection mechanism, melting, suspending the crushed particles on the surface of mixture of deionized water and ethanol.
   USE - Method for preparing dry ice-based graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of dry ice-based graphene with excellent powder suspension effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing dry ice-based graphene, involves (S1) mixing high-purity expanded graphite, anionic surfactant, dispersant, defoamer and solvent in the reactor, mechanically stirring for 30-120 minutes to form mixed slurry, (S2) crushing, placing the mixed slurry in an oven, drying at 100-150 degrees C to form solid, crushing the solid to form crushed particles with particle size of less than 100 mu m, (S3) mixing in a low temperature environment at -56--78 degrees C, adding crushed particles to liquid carbon dioxide, stirring, heating at -80--120 degrees C, solidifying to form dry ice solid, cutting dry ice solid at low temperature, (S4) adding deionized water and ethanol to a container at room temperature, providing injection mechanism at the bottom of container, injecting dry ice solid into the bottom of the container through the injection mechanism, melting, contacting the crushed particles to the surface of deionized water and ethanol, suspending the crushed particles on the surface of mixture of deionized water and ethanol, filtering and rapid drying to obtain high purity graphene powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anionic surfactant is selected from sodium fatty acid and titanate. The dispersant is polyvinyl alcohol. Preferred Process: The rapid drying is microwave drying or vacuum drying.

PT P
PN WO2018019701-A1; TW201811673-A; US2019135649-A1; CN109689574-A; EP3487812-A1; JP2019528222-W; US10745288-B2; EP3487812-B1
TI Preparing amorphous calcium carbonate for e.g. paper involves mixing calcium-oxide-containing material with water, separating dissolved calcium hydroxide, and simultaneously drying solution and carbonating dissolved calcium hydroxide.
AU SCHLOTTERBACH T
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201809039X
AB    NOVELTY - Amorphous calcium carbonate is prepared by preparing milk of lime by mixing calcium-oxide-containing material with water to convert calcium oxide partially into dissolved calcium hydroxide, separating dissolved calcium hydroxide in the milk of lime from undissolved materials to obtain solution of dissolved calcium hydroxide, and simultaneously drying solution of dissolved calcium hydroxide and carbonating dissolved calcium hydroxide with gaseous carbon dioxide source to convert calcium hydroxide partially into amorphous calcium carbonate.
   USE - Preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate for product selected from paper, paper product, wood product, cellulose composite, protein composite, bone composite, ink, paint, coating, plastic, polymer composition, adhesive, building product, foodstuff, agricultural product, biomimetic material, cosmetic product, or pharmaceutical product and for use in paper making, paper coating, food, plastic, agricultural, paint, coatings, adhesives, sealants, pharmaceuticals, agricultural, biomimetic, construction, and/or cosmetic applications (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Amorphous calcium carbonate, especially a stable calcium carbonate, can be obtained at high purity, in a simple manner without the use of complex purification steps.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amorphous calcium carbonate is prepared by (a) providing calcium-oxide-containing material; (b) providing gaseous carbon dioxide (CO2) source; (c) preparing milk of lime by mixing calcium-oxide-containing material with water to convert calcium oxide partially into dissolved calcium hydroxide; (d) separating dissolved calcium hydroxide in the milk of lime from undissolved materials to obtain a solution of dissolved calcium hydroxide; (e) drying the solution of dissolved calcium hydroxide; and (f) carbonating dissolved calcium hydroxide with gaseous CO2 source to convert the calcium hydroxide partially into amorphous calcium carbonate, where process steps (e) and (f) are carried out simultaneously. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of a spray dryer for the preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Gaseous CO2 source is gaseous CO2-containing gas, preferably containing greater than or equal to 20 vol.% CO2 based on the total volume of the gas. Preferred Conditions: Step (c) is carried out in that calcium-oxide-containing material is mixed with water in a weight ratio of 1:2-1:25, preferably 1:3-1:15, at 0-100 degrees C, preferably 35-45 degrees C. Drying step (e) and carbonating step (f) are carried out at an inlet temperature of 50-400 degrees C, preferably 200-300 degrees C. Carbonating step (f) is carried out at a gas pressure of 1-20 bar, preferably 2-10 bar. Preferred Processes: The method further includes removing particles having average particle size of less than or equal to 100 mu m from the milk of lime obtained in step (c) before separating step (d) is carried out. Separating step (d) is carried out via mechanical separation techniques, e.g. in a vertical plate pressure filter, a tube press, or a vacuum filter, preferably in a vacuum filter, such as a vacuum suction filter. Solution of dissolved calcium hydroxide obtained in step (d) is adjusted to 10-60 degrees C, preferably 15-28 degrees C. Drying step (e) and carbonating step (f) are carried out in a spray drier. Preferred Parameters: Milk of lime obtained in step (c) has Brookfield viscosity at 25 degrees C of 1-1000 mPa.s, preferably 10-600 mPa.s; and/or solids content of 5-40 wt.%, preferably 8-20 wt.%, based on the total weight of the milk of lime. Amorphous calcium carbonate has average particle size of 30-150 nm, preferably 50-100 nm; has moisture content of 10 wt.%, preferably 0.2-8 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the amorphous calcium carbonate; has specific Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of 1-60 m2/g, preferably 10-40 m2/g; forms aggregates with average particle size of 0.5-10 mu m, preferably 1-5 mu m; and/or is present in the form of deagglomerated particles.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); ; G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. MC A08-R; F05-A06D; G02-A03; G02-A03D; G02-A04A; G03-B01; G03-B02; D03-H; D08-B; D08-B10; E12-A02; E34-D03A

PN CN107551795-A
TI Using method of indoor air pollutant purifying agent by atomizing and spraying formaldehyde scavenger on furniture to decompose formaldehyde and spraying antifungal, antibacterial and odor removing cured liquid on surface of furniture.
AU WANG C
   ZHANG X
AE HONGMENG GUANGDONG ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HONG-Non-standard)
GA 201805055R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for using indoor air pollutant purifying agent involves mixing silicone quaternary ammonium salt solution and deionized water to obtain formaldehyde scavenger, uniformly atomizing and spraying formaldehyde scavenger on a surface of furniture object to decompose free formaldehyde in the surface of the pollution source and air into water and carbon dioxide, and mixing silicone quaternary ammonium salt solution and deionized water to obtain antifungal, antibacterial and odor removing cured liquid having excellent antibacterial property and inhibition and killing effects on molds and germs.
   USE - Method is useful for using indoor air pollutant purifying agent (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for using indoor air pollutant purifying agent involves mixing silicone quaternary ammonium salt solution and deionized water in a mass ratio of 5:95 to obtain formaldehyde scavenger, uniformly atomizing and spraying formaldehyde scavenger on a surface of furniture object to decompose free formaldehyde in the surface of the pollution source and air into water and carbon dioxide, mixing silicone quaternary ammonium salt solution and deionized water in a mass ratio of 3:97 to obtain antifungal, antibacterial and odor removing cured liquid having excellent antibacterial property and inhibition and killing effects on molds and germs, uniformly spraying the antifungal, antibacterial and odor removing cured liquid on the surface of the furniture object to continuously decompose formaldehyde, benzene, total volatile organic compound, ammonia, radon and other harmful substances and inhibit and kill bacteria and germs in a long-acting manner, opening the window to keep ventilation, forming a protective film after 24 hours, continuously decomposing and killing harmful substances inside and outside to maintain excellent indoor air quality.

PT P
PN CN114279254-A
TI Performing flue gas waste heat utilization and            carbon dioxide capture and recovery process used in            electric power, involves liquefying carbon dioxide in            flue gas, storing in liquid carbon dioxide storage            tank, and conveying carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG J
   WU K
   ZHAO H
AE UNIV YANTAI (UNYT-C)
GA 2022523893
AB    NOVELTY - Performing flue gas waste heat utilization and                carbon dioxide capture and recovery process,                involves drying under 180-200degrees Celsius flue                gas, increasing pressure to 1.5 MPa, and increasing                temperature to 300degrees Celsius, entering the                waste heat boiler of the steam power generation                system. The high temperature flue gas is exchanged                heat with the water in the waste heat boiler,                increased the temperature of the water in the waste                heat boiler, recovered the heat in the flue gas.                The temperature of the flue gas is reduced to                180-220degrees Celsius, and under the action of the                circulating pump, the water in the waste heat                boiler is heated and then passed through the steam                turbine (2) and the condenser (1) in turn to                generate electricity. The flue gas whose                temperature is reduced to 180-220degrees Celsius                flowed through the generator (6) of the absorption                unit to recover the heat in the flue gas                again.
   USE - Method for performing flue gas waste heat                utilization and carbon dioxide capture and recovery                process. Uses include but are not limited to                electric power, machinery, chemical engineering,                medical treatment and food processing paper making                industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The flue gas waste heat and carbon dioxide                capturing and recycling technique has improved                water replenishing temperature, energy saving and                eco-friendly double-profit, and improved                circulation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing flue gas waste heat utilization and                carbon dioxide capture and recovery process,                involves drying under 180-200degrees Celsius flue                gas, increasing pressure to 1.5 MPa, and increasing                temperature to 300degrees Celsius, entering the                waste heat boiler of the steam power generation                system. The high temperature flue gas is exchanged                heat with the water in the waste heat boiler,                increased the temperature of the water in the waste                heat boiler, recovered the heat in the flue gas.                The temperature of the flue gas is reduced to                180-220degrees Celsius, and under the action of the                circulating pump, the water in the waste heat                boiler is heated and then passes through the steam                turbine and the condenser in turn to generate                electricity. The flue gas whose temperature is                reduced to 180-220degrees Celsius flowed through                the generator of the absorption unit to recover the                heat in the flue gas again, and the temperature of                the flue gas is further reduced to 100-120degrees                Celsius. The flue gas is used as a heat medium to                provide heat for the absorption unit, which                promotes the separation of refrigerant and solution                in the generator. The flue gas from the generator                of the absorption unit flowed through the primary                heat exchanger, the secondary heat exchanger and                the water for heat exchange, and the waste heat is                used to heat the water from the water inlet A1 and                the water inlet A2, the make-up water entering the                water inlet A3. The heat in the flue gas is                recovered again, and the flue gas temperature is                reduced by 50-70degrees Celsius. The flue gas                flowing out of the secondary heat exchanger flowed                through the first evaporator (10) of the absorption                unit again, and the absorption unit is used the                heat in the flue gas to heat the make-up water                entering from the water inlet A1. The carbon                dioxide in the flue gas is liquefied and stored in                the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, and the                obtained liquid carbon dioxide is transported to                the subsequent process for processing. The make-up                water entering from the water inlet A1 is flown                through the absorber of the absorption unit, the                make-up water is heated, and then the temperature                is further increased after heat exchange with the                first condenser (12) of the absorption unit. The                make-up water is entered from the water inlet is                first heated by the second condenser of the cascade                refrigeration system, and then mixed with the water                heated by the first condenser of the absorption                unit entering from the water inlet A1. The make-up                water entering from the water inlet A2 is heated by                the secondary heat exchanger. The temperature is                rised and then mixed with the heated water entering                from the water inlet A1 and the water inlet A3. The                mixed water flowed through the primary heat                exchanger to be reheated, and part of the heated                make-up water flowed into the boiler, and the other                portion flowed into the waste heat boiler.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow chart of a flue gas                waste heat utilization and carbon dioxide capture                recovery process.
   Condenser (1)
   Steam turbine (2)
   Waste heat boiler (3)
   First circulating pump (4)
   Generator (6)
   Solution throttle valve (8)
   First evaporator (10)
   First throttle valve (11)
   First condenser (12)
   First-stage heat exchanger (13)
   Second-stage heat exchanger (14)
   Second condenser (15)
   Second throttle valve (16)
   First evaporation condenser (17)
   First compressor (18)
   Third throttle valve (19)
   Second evaporation condenser (20)
   Second compressor (21)
   Fourth throttle valve (22)
   Second evaporator (23)
   Third compressor (24)
   Second circulating pump (25)
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank                (27)
   Water inlet (A1)
   Water inlet (A2)
   Waste heat water inlet (A3)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q78 (Heat exchange in general (F28))
