PN KR2022073109-A
TI Environmental cost standard coefficient calculation system for e.g. time period comprises power market analysis unit for generating power market information and standard coefficient calculation block for calculating carbon dioxide emissions.
AU KO J M
   SONG J J
   HWAN C S
   JIN K M
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 202276997G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power market analysis unit for generating power market information, and a standard coefficient calculation block for calculating carbon dioxide emissions using the power market information. An analysis classification sub-block calculates a production rate by a generation resource or standard production rate based on power generation resource groups. An output system is connected to the calculation block. A carbon credit management unit manages a standard environmental cost coefficient for a time period and carbon dioxide emission. The power market information comprises operation power generation plan result data and power production information according to preset operating power generation scenario.
   USE - Environmental cost standard coefficient calculation system for time period, DR (Demand Response) operators, VPP (Virtual Power Plant) operators, PPA (Power Purchase Agreements), BTM (Behind to Meter) part of government policy, business operators, local governments, carbon credit trading operators, liquid crystal display (LCD), light emitting diode (LED) display, plasma display panel (PDP), organic light emitting diode (OLED) display, touch screen, cathode ray tube (CRT), flexible display, public switched telephone network (PSTN), public switched data network (PSDN), ISDN, broadband ISDN, Local Area Network (LAN), Metropolitan Area Network (MAN), Wireless Local Area Network (WLAN), etc., wireless communication networks e.g. Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA), Wideband Code Division Multiple Access (WCDMA), Wireless Broadband (WiBro), Wireless fidelity (WiFi), High-Speed Downlink Packet Access (HSDPA), network, Bluetooth, Near Field Communication (NFC) network, satellite broadcasting network, analog broadcasting network, Digital Multimedia Broadcasting (DMB) network and communication terminals e.g. mobile phones, smart phones, laptop computers, digital broadcasting terminals, PDA (Personal Digital Assistants), PMP (Portable Multimedia Player), navigation, note pad, etc.,.
   ADVANTAGE - The system calculates carbon dioxide emission and standard environmental cost for each time period based on the amount of power generated by the power operation of the exchange and the function to verify, so that carbon dioxide emissions can be calculated and applied in an accurate manner. The system provides accurate information on what power generation resources are used and emits carbon in a current time zone by calculating exact carbon emissions of power generation fuels that produce electricity by season and time zone. The customers who do not use the time period are calculated through standard emissions to accurately calculate carbon emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for calculating standard coefficient of environmental cost using time period.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system for calculating standard environmental cost coefficients for each time period (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC T01 (Digital Computers); U12 (Discrete Devices); U14 (Memories, Film and Hybrid Circuits); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs); W01 (Telephone and Data Transmission Systems); W04 (Audio/Video Recording and Systems); W05 (Alarms, Signalling, Telemetry and Telecontrol)
MC T01-J05B2; T01-J21; T01-M06A1; T01-N01A2E; T01-N01A3; T01-N02A1B; T01-N02B1E; U12-A01A1E; U12-A01A7; U14-K01A4C; V05-A01A3; W01-A03B; W01-A06B5A; W01-A06C4A; W01-A06C4E; W01-A06E1; W01-A06G2; W01-C01D3C; W01-C01G8S; W01-C01R; W04-E30A1; W05-E05F

PN CN114405431-A
TI Multiple chemical synthesis reactor for collecting and measuring pure carbon dioxide to extract, has liquid storage box fixed on top of reaction box, and whose left side is fixedly connected to liquid outlet pipe in liquid storage mechanism.
AU YANG S
   CHEN J
AE CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202264463E
AB    NOVELTY - The reactor has a waste residue recovery box (12) fixed on the left side of the bottom end of the casing (11). A waste liquid recovery box (13) is fixed in the middle inside the bottom end of the casing. The upper part of the waste liquid recovery box is fixedly connected with the discharging mechanism (3). A screw shaft (33) and a pushing rod (34) are respectively installed on the left and right sides of the discharging mechanism. And see that the two ends of the screw shaft and the push rod pass through the casing respectively. The right end of the discharging mechanism is movably connected to the transmission mechanism (4). The upper end of the transmission mechanism is connected with the air pipe (26) of the reaction box (2) through the piston. A liquid storage box (21) is fixed on the top of the reaction box. The left side of the liquid storage box is fixedly connected to the liquid outlet pipe (54) in the liquid storage mechanism (5).
   USE - Multiple chemical synthesis reactor for collecting and measuring pure carbon dioxide to extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide generated by the reaction flows back to the reaction box with the liquid container, and flows out through the gas outlet pipe, and then the gas will be collected, which is convenient for the precipitation in the solution to be reacted again, and carbon dioxide is generated and recycled. The carbon dioxide produced by the reaction is concentrated in the reaction box, which converges to the top and starts to be discharged from the air outlet pipe at the top right end of the reaction box, which can effectively avoid the occurrence of danger.

   TAKEUCHI A
   TOKORO H
   TAKANO S

AE HITACHI METALS LTD (HITK-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of positive electrode active                material involves mixing lithium carbonate and                compound containing metallic elements other than                lithium, obtaining precursor, baking and obtaining                lithium complex compound (I). The heat                treatment apparatus (1) has a firing furnace, and                oxygen supply unit (20) for supplying oxygen gas to                the sintering furnace.    USE - Manufacture of positive electrode active                material for lithium ion secondary battery.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of positive electrode active                material involves mixing lithium carbonate and                compound containing metallic elements other than                lithium, obtaining precursor, baking and obtaining                lithium complex compound of formula: Li1+aM1O2+b                (I). The baking process involves heat-treating in                an atmosphere having oxygen concentration of 45% or                more and carbon dioxide concentration of 0.02% or                less in a firing furnace of heat treatment                apparatus. The heat treatment apparatus has a                firing furnace, and oxygen supply unit for                supplying oxygen gas to the sintering furnace. At                least one portion of the gas discharged from baking                furnace to baking furnace, is recycled.
   M1 = metal element other than lithium and                nickel;
   a = -0.1 to 0.2;and
   b = -0.2 to 0.2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for heat                processing apparatus used for manufacture of                positive electrode active material, which has                oxygen concentration detection unit (s2) which                detects oxygen concentration in the gas inlet of                the kiln, or gas outlet, recycling gas flow rate                adjustment unit to adjust the flow volume of gas                recycled and carbon-dioxide removal unit to remove                carbon dioxide contained in the gas discharged from                kiln.

PN WO2022145217-A1
TI Reducing carbon dioxide in living space, such as            house, involves contacting carbon dioxide adsorbent            with gas containing carbon dioxide and water vapor in            living space to adsorb carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide            adsorbent, and releasing separated carbon dioxide out            of living space.
AU HAYASHI M
AE SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
GA 202287903L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing carbon dioxide in living                space, involves (a) contacting a carbon dioxide                adsorbent with a gas containing carbon dioxide and                water vapor in a living space to adsorb carbon                dioxide to the carbon dioxide adsorbent, (b)                separating carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide                adsorbent by heating the carbon dioxide adsorbent                to which carbon dioxide is adsorbed at 50-900                degrees C, and (c) releasing the separated carbon                dioxide out of the living space, where the carbon                dioxide adsorbent is a metal compound containing at                least one alkali metal carbonate containing an                alkali metal and at least one element chosen from                Group 3 elements of Periodic Table and Group 4                elements of Periodic Table.
   USE - The method is used for reducing carbon dioxide                in living space, such as a house, an office, a                school, or a vehicle, a carbon dioxide adsorbent,                and adsorbing and separating carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently reduces carbon dioxide                in a living space, improves carbon dioxide                adsorption rate and improves maintenance                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) (1) a carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising                a porous material, at least one alkali metal                carbonate containing an alkali metal, and a metal                compound containing at least one element chosen                from Group 3 elements of Periodic Table and Group 4                elements of Periodic Table;
   (2) (2) a method for producing a carbon                dioxide adsorbent, involving (i) contacting a                solution containing the metal compound with the                porous material and heating to obtain a carbon                dioxide adsorbent precursor and (ii) adding an                alkali metal to the carbon dioxide adsorbent                precursor, contacting and heating a solution                containing a carbonate to obtain the product;                and
   (3) (3) a method for adsorbing and separating                carbon dioxide, involving (ia) contacting the                carbon dioxide adsorbent with a gas containing                carbon dioxide to adsorb carbon dioxide to the                carbon dioxide adsorbent, (iia) heating the carbon                dioxide adsorbent on which carbon dioxide is                adsorbed to 50-900 degrees C, and (iiia) separating                carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide                adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal compound                  comprises at least one element chosen from                  cerium, titanium and zirconium. The metal                  compound is zirconium oxide. The alkali metal                  carbonate comprises at least one element chosen                  from sodium, potassium and cesium. The carbon                  dioxide adsorbent comprises two or more of the                  alkali metal carbonates. The porous material is                  at least one chosen from silicon oxide, aluminum                  oxide, activated carbon and calcium silicate. The                  carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises the alkali                  metal carbonate and the porous material on which                  the metal compound is supported. Preferred                  Composition: The alkali metal content is 1-50                  mmol/g. Preferred Property: The carbon dioxide                  which has an average pore diameter of 0.3-500                  nm.
   EXAMPLE - Prepare an aqueous solution of cerium nitrate                (III) hexahydrate (using ion-exchanged water,                concentration of 61.7 %mass). 0.8 ml of an aqueous                solution of cerium (III) nitrate hexahydrate was                evenly added dropwise to 1.0 g porous silicon                oxide. After absorption, dried at room temperature                for 1 hour, and then dried in a multi-oven at 40                degrees C until there was no change in weight.                Then, it was calcined at 600 degrees C for 2 hours                using a muffle furnace to obtain a carbon dioxide                adsorbent precursor 1X. To the total amount of the                obtained carbon dioxide adsorbent precursor 1X, 0.8                ml of an aqueous solution of potassium carbonate                (using ion-exchanged water, concentration of 46.3                %mass) was dropped evenly. After absorbing it by                contacting it with the carbon dioxide adsorbent                precursor 1X, it was dried at room temperature for                1 hour, and then dried at 40 degrees C until there                was no weight change using a multi-orange oven. The                carbon dioxide adsorbent 1 was obtained by removing                the solvent.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   GUANGZHOU POLYTECHNIC SPORTS (GUAN-Non-standard)

   USE - Thyme sports drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The thyme sports drink improves juvenile exercise tolerance, reduces oxygen deficit and sport injury, strengthens anti-fatigue capability and is natural and safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: Thyme youth sports drink comprises 0.3-0.5g/L, preferably 0.4g/L thymol, 55-65g/L, preferably 60 g/L maltodextrin, 0.004-0.005g/L, preferably 0.0045g/L vitaminB2, 1.4-1.6g/L, preferably 1.5g/L citric acid, 0.05-0.06g/L, preferably 0.055g/L magnesium chloride, 0.4-0.5g/L, preferaly 0.45g/L casein and 550-650ml/L, preferably 600 ml/L pure water. PN CN107686099-A; CN208617364-U
TI Device useful for producing yellow phosphorus by using medium and low-grade phosphate ore, comprises a grinding unit, ball drying unit, a reducing reaction unit, a slag cooling unit and separating and purifying unit.
AU ZHAO J
   MAO W
   HUANG P
   ZHANG Y
AE BEIJING MAIWEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CAER-C)
   BEIJING MAIWEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CAER-C)
GA 201814988W
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a grinding unit (2), ball drying unit (3), a reducing reaction unit (4), a slag cooling unit (5) and separating and purifying unit (6). The grinding unit comprises milling device and air conveying device. The device comprises raw materials of phosphorus ore, silica, coal powder and/or coke into the grinding device to grinding and the powder particle size requirement is satisfied through air conveying device conveying to each powder ball drying unit. The ball-drying unit comprises a mixer, ball making machine and a dryer. The powder and binder are weighed and taken into mixer.
   USE - The device is useful for producing yellow phosphorus by using medium and low-grade phosphate ore (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a grinding unit, ball drying unit, a reducing reaction unit, a slag cooling unit and separating and purifying unit. The grinding unit comprises a milling device and an air conveying device. The device comprises raw materials of phosphorus ore, silica, coal powder and/or coke into the grinding device to grinding and the powder particle size requirement is satisfied through air conveying device conveying to each powder ball drying unit. The ball-drying unit comprises a mixer, ball making machine and a dryer. The powder and binder are weighed and taken into a mixer, mixing evenly, transmitting into the ball-making machine for making material ball, and taking the material ball into dryer into drying, drying the material ball after conveying to reaction equiside reduction reaction unit, where the drying temperature is carried out at 200-800?Oc. The reducing reaction unit comprises a high temperature flue gas generating furnace and reaction KHz, separating from the carbon monoxide purifying unit and the external delivery of the fuel with the oxygen to the high temperature flue gas generator furnace for oxidation reaction. The reaction equiside sent into the high temperature smoke gas generated by high temperature smoke gas generator, heating the material ball to 1200-1400 degrees C and reacting for 10-90 minutes, discharging the solid material balls from the furnace bottom to the slag cooling unit and the steam containing phosphorus, the furnace gas of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide KHz; by reaction is discharged from the top to separating and purifying unit. The high temperature smoke gas generator is connected with the slag cooling unit and configured to transmit gas to dust and water washing, separating the liquid yellow phosphorus product. The separated carbon monoxide gas conveying to high temperature smoke gas generator, and the carbon dioxide as the cooling medium conveyed to the slag cooling unit to recycle the heat of slag. The slag cooling unit is connected with the ball-drying unit is connected, using separating and purifying unit from carbon dioxide gas as coolant, cooling the high temperature slag, heating the carbon dioxide into ball drying unit as drying heat source. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing yellow phosphorus by using medium and low-grade phosphate ore comprising (a) mixing the raw material ore, silica, coal powder and/or coke for preliminary crushing and mixing each kind of raw material after crushing into the grinding unit, (b) mixing each kind of raw material into the grinding device to grind, taking the particle size requirement of the powder through the air conveying device conveying to the ball-making drying unit, (c) taking the various powder and adhesive according to a certain proportion after weighing, metering and feeding into the mixer, after uniformly mixing placing into the ball making machine for making material ball, placing the ball into dryer for drying, placing the dried material ball into the reaction equiside, (d) taking the fuel, high temperature flue gas oxygen and carbon monoxide from the separating and purifying unit into the high temperature smoke gas generator, to produce in excess of 1200 degrees C, where the high-temperature flue gas is used as a heat source to heat the pellets in the reaction phosphor furnace to 1100-1400 degrees C, starting the reduction reaction and generating comprises phosphorus steam and gas of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, where the furnace gas discharged from equiside top, where the reacted solid material balls discharged from the furnace bottom, (e) removing dust from the furnace gas, washing with water and decarbonising to obtain yellow phosphorus product, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and the carbon monoxide as fuel sent to the high temperature smoke gas generator, where the carbon dioxide as a coolant for cooling the slag, and (f) high-temperature pellets discharged from the bottom of the phosphorus furnace exchange heat with carbon dioxide gas from the separation and purification unit, heat carbon dioxide used as drying heat source and cooling the material ball and discharging.

The reaction phosphorus furnace is a shaft furnace, from top to bottom, are preheat zone and reaction zone, where the pellets are preheated to 1000-1200 degrees C in the preheat zone by high-temperature flue gas; in the reaction zone, heated to 1200-1400 degrees C to react with elemental yellow phosphorus. PN CN110256188-A; CN210855897-U
TI Deep purification of ethylene gas containing high carbon dioxide for downstream ethylene plants, involves treating ethane feed gas using semi-lean amine solution, performing gas-liquid separation, and regenerating semi-lean amine solution.
AU LIU Z
   QIAO G
   QIU P
   CHANG Z
   ZHAO Y
   HAN W
   LIANG X
AE XIAN CHANGQING TECHNOLOGY ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   XIAN CHANGQING TECHNOLOGY ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201992071H
AB    NOVELTY - A deep purification of ethylene gas containing high carbon dioxide involves heating the high carbon dioxide-containing ethane feed gas using heat exchanger, passing the heated ethane gas to the decarburization absorption tower, reverse contacting with semi-lean amine solution, and lean amine solution, performing mass transfer, and to obtain treated ethane gas, and rich amine solution, passing the treated ethane gas into the gas heat exchanger, cooling, passing the ethane gas into wet ethane purification gas separator to obtain wet ethane purification gas and the liquid phase-rich amine solution, passing the rich amine solution into the flash tank, flashing, passing the regenerated tail gas into the reflux tank, passing the gas phase in the reflux tank into the exhaust gas treatment device, pressurizing the liquid phase in the reflux tank using reflux pump, and passing to the amine liquid regeneration tower to obtain semi-lean amine solution.
   USE - Deep purification of ethylene gas containing high carbon dioxide for reducing the consumption of sodium hydroxide lye in downstream ethylene plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The purification method reduces the 18.7 %mass carbon dioxide to 100 ppm or less, 0.02 %mass sulfur dioxide to 1 ppm or less, which improves the purity of ethane gas, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A deep purification of ethylene gas containing high carbon dioxide involves heating the high carbon dioxide-containing ethane feed gas having temperature of 9.5 degrees C using heat exchanger at 30 degrees C, passing the heated ethane gas to the lower part of the decarburization absorption tower, reverse contacting with semi-lean amine solution, and lean amine solution, performing mass transfer to remove carbon dioxide, and sulfur dioxide in the ethane feed gas, and to obtain treated ethane gas which is extracted at the top of the absorption tower, and rich amine solution which absorbs carbon dioxide, and sulfur dioxide at the bottom of the decarburization absorption tower, passing the treated ethane gas into the gas heat exchanger, cooling, passing the ethane gas at 50 degrees C into wet ethane purification gas separator for gas-liquid separation to obtain wet ethane purification gas at 26 degrees C and the liquid phase-rich amine solution, passing the rich amine solution, and liquid phase-rich amine solution into the flash tank at 3MPa, flashing, passing the gas phase in the flash tank into the downstream processing device at 0.6 MPa, passing the rich amine solution into the amine liquid regeneration tower, contacting with rising regeneration gas, performing mass transfer to extract regenerated tail gas at 62.6 degrees C from the top of the amine liquid regeneration tower, and carbon dioxide, cooling the regenerated tail gas at 40 degrees C using overhead air cooler, and overhead water cooler, passing the regenerated tail gas into the reflux tank, passing the gas phase in the reflux tank into the exhaust gas treatment device, pressurizing the liquid phase in the reflux tank using reflux pump, and passing to the amine liquid regeneration tower to obtain semi-lean amine solution, separating the semi-lean amine solution into two parts, pressurizing one part of semi-lean amine solution at 0.06-3 MPa g using circulation pump, cooling at 70 degrees C using air cooler, and passing into the decarburization absorption tower as absorption liquid, pressurizing the other part of the semi-lean amine solution at 0.06-0.08 MPa g using feed pump, and lean-rich liquid heat exchanger, heating at 70-93 degrees C, passing into the upper part of the stripping section of amine liquid regeneration tower, contacting counter currently with the rising regeneration gas for mass transfer, distilling the residual carbon dioxide, and sulfur dioxide in the semi-lean amine solution, extracting the bottommost tray of the amine liquid regeneration tower, passing the amine liquid on the tray at 117 degrees C to the reboiler, heating using heat transfer oil at 118 degrees C, and 0.08 MPa to obtain gas, and liquid, refluxing the gas to the gas phase space of the bottom of the amine liquid regeneration tower, passing the liquid to the buffer section of the bottom of the amine liquid regeneration tower, cooling at 73 degrees C using lean-rich liquid heat exchanger, pressurizing using lean liquid booster pump at 0.1 MPa, cooling at 50 degrees C using lean liquid water cooler, and pressurizing into the upper half of the decarburization absorption tower at 3 MPa using lean liquid circulation pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for deep purification of ethylene gas containing high carbon dioxide which comprises feed line, gas heat exchanger, decarburization absorption tower, wet ethane purification gas separator, flash tank, amine liquid regeneration tower, semi-lean amine circulating pump, semi-lean amine liquid air cooler, lean-rich liquid heat exchanger, reboiler, overhead cooler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rising regeneration gas in the amine liquid regeneration tower is carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethane feed gas comprises 76.43 %mass ethane mass content, 18.71 %mass carbon dioxide, 0.48 %mass methane, 4.36 %mass liquefied gas and heavy hydrocarbon, and 0.02 %mass hydrogen sulfide. The treated ethane gas comprises 93.58 % mass ethane, 0.000855 % mass content carbon dioxide, 0.59 % mass methane, 5.75 %mass liquefied gas and heavy hydrocarbon, 0.0799 %mass saturated water, and balance hydrogen sulfide. The gas phase in the flash tank comprises 0.24 % mass methane, 61.16 %mass ethane, 33.20 %mass carbon dioxide, 3.27 %mass gas content, 2.09 %mass saturated water content, and 0.04 %mass hydrogen sulfide. The regenerated tail gas comprises 0.33 %mass ethane, 97.28 %mass carbon dioxide, 0.02 %mass liquefied gas, 2.26 %mass saturated water, and 0.11 %mass hydrogen sulfide.

PN CN114471107-A; CN216935418-U
TI Dual-alkali decarburization comprises placing            mixed solution of calcium carbonate solid suspension            and sodium hydroxide to cyclone separator under action            of centrifugal separation.
AU LI J
   CAI Y
   SONG D
AE BEIJING SPC ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202270426U
AB    NOVELTY - Dual-alkali decarburization comprises mixing                calcium oxide in calcium oxide storage silo with                sodium carbonate-containing slurry from the wet                carbon dioxide absorption tower. The calcium oxide                digestion reaction occurs in the reaction tank to                generate calcium hydroxide, where the calcium                hydroxide and sodium carbonate are replaced in the                reaction tank. The reaction generates a mixed                solution containing calcium carbonate solid                suspension and sodium hydroxide. The mixed solution                containing calcium carbonate solid suspension and                sodium hydroxide enters the cyclone separator under                the action of centrifugal separation. The calcium                carbonate solid is centrifuged out, and flows out                from the underflow of the cyclone separator in the                state of high concentration calcium carbonate                suspension, and enters the subsequent dehydrator                for dehydration.
   USE - Method for carrying out dual-alkali                decarburization, preferably carbon dioxide,                methane, nitric oxide, ozone and                chlorofluorocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides dual-alkali                decarburization in a simple and cost-effective                manner with high decarbonization efficiency by                using low the actual consumption of the absorbent.                The absorbent is recycled and regenerated in the                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dual-alkali decarburization comprises mixing                calcium oxide in calcium oxide storage silo with                sodium carbonate-containing slurry from the wet                carbon dioxide absorption tower. The calcium oxide                digestion reaction occurs in the reaction tank to                generate calcium hydroxide, where the calcium                hydroxide and sodium carbonate are replaced in the                reaction tank. The reaction generates a mixed                solution containing calcium carbonate solid                suspension and sodium hydroxide. The mixed solution                containing calcium carbonate solid suspension and                sodium hydroxide enters the cyclone separator under                the action of centrifugal separation. The calcium                carbonate solid is centrifuged out, and flows out                from the underflow of the cyclone separator in the                state of high concentration calcium carbonate                suspension, and enters the subsequent dehydrator                for dehydration. The clear liquid of the cyclone                separator enters the subsequent grit chamber for                solid-liquid separation, and uses a mud scraper or                a mud pump to transfer the sediment containing                calcium carbonate at the bottom of the grit chamber                to a dehydrator for dehydration. The supernatant                liquid containing sodium hydroxide in the pool                enters the wet carbon dioxide absorption tower. The                flue gas containing carbon dioxide after                desulfurization and denitrification reacts with                sodium hydroxide for decarbonization and then                discharged through the chimney, where the slurry                containing sodium carbonate generated in the wet                carbon dioxide absorption tower is returned to the                reaction tank to continue to participate in calcium                hydride substitution reaction.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                double-alkali decarburization equipment, which                comprises a calcium oxide storage bin (1), a                reaction tank (4), a cyclone separator (5), a grit                chamber (6), wet carbon dioxide absorption tower                (7), and dehydrator (8).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the method                for carrying out dual-alkali decarburization.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   1Calcium oxide storage bin
   4Reaction tank
   5Cyclone separator
   6Grit chamber
   7Wet carbon dioxide absorption tower
   8Dehydrator

PN CN114149320-A
TI Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid with high yield involves reacting phenol and potassium hydroxide solution, reacting obtained potassium phenolate with supercritical carbon dioxide, adding hot water, dissolving into solution and filtering, adjusting pH of obtained filtrate, filtering and decolorizing.
AU XU L
   YU R
   ZHANG D
AE NINGXIA QINGYAN POLYMER NEW MATERIAL CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202241012B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid with high yield, comprises e.g. (1) adding phenol and potassium hydroxide solution to reaction vessel, reacting under constant temperature stirring for 0.5-1 hour, transferring reacted solution to vacuum oven, evaporating water, then cooling, taking out precipitated solid, grinding and drying to obtain potassium phenolate powder, (2) adding potassium phenolate to reaction kettle, drying under reduced pressure for 0.5-1.5 hours, heating, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide and reacting at 190-210 degreesC for 1-3 hours, then releasing excess supercritical carbon dioxide gas, cooling to below 60 degreesC, adding hot water, dissolving into solution, and filtering, and (3) taking the filtrate obtained in step (2), adjusting pH to acidic, separating out potassium sulfate, filtering and decolorizing, filtering, adjusting pH of the filtrate to acidic, cooling obtained precipitate and washing to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid with high yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as a medium, which solves the problems of long reaction time, complicated process and large pollution; adopts supercritical carbon dioxide fluid reaction conditions, adjustment of the ratio of potassium hydroxide and phenol, limitation of the grinding size of potassium phenolate, supercritical carbon dioxide gas pressure in the carboxylation process, optimization of reaction temperature and reaction time, and optimization of acid concentration and adjusted pH value during acid precipitation, and thus improves the yield of p-hydroxybenzoic acid; has simple process flow, and simple and convenient operation; has no special equipment requirement; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing p-hydroxybenzoic acid with high yield, comprises (1) adding phenol and potassium hydroxide solution to reaction vessel, reacting under constant temperature stirring for 0.5-1 hour, transferring the reacted solution to vacuum oven, gradually evaporating the water in the reaction solution until all solids are precipitated out, then cooling, taking out the precipitated solid, grinding and drying to obtain potassium phenolate powder, (2) adding potassium phenolate prepared in step (1) to reaction kettle, drying under reduced pressure for 0.5-1.5 hours, heating, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide and reacting at 190-210 degreesC for 1-3 hours by stirring at a low speed, then releasing excess supercritical carbon dioxide gas, cooling the reaction system to below 60 degreesC, adding hot water to the reaction kettle, dissolving the product into a solution, and filtering for later use, and (3) taking the filtrate obtained in step (2), adjusting pH to acidic with sulfuric acid, separating out potassium sulfate, filtering and decolorizing with activated carbon, filtering again to obtain filtrate, adjusting the pH of the filtrate to acidic with sulfuric acid to precipitate p-hydroxybenzoic acid, cooling to room temperature, washing with water and filtering to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) comprises adding phenol and potassium hydroxide solution to the reaction vessel, heating to 100 degreesC, and reacting by stirring for 0.5-1 hour. In step (1): the potassium phenolate needs to be ground to particle size of less than or equal to 20mum; the evaporation temperature in the vacuum oven is 130-150 degreesC, and the vacuum degree is 0.08-0.095 MPa; and the ground solid is dried in blast oven at 55-65 degreesC for 5.5-6.5 hours to obtain potassium phenolate powder. In step (2): the temperature of drying under reduced pressure is 100-110 degreesC, and the vacuum degree is 0.08-0.095 MPa; and the pressure of introducing supercritical carbon dioxide is 7-9 MPa. In step (3), the pH of the solution is adjusted to 5.5-6.5 for the first time, and the pH of the solution is adjusted to 2-3 for the second time.Preferred Components: In step (1), the mass fraction of the potassium hydroxide solution is 40-60%, and the molar ratio of potassium hydroxide to phenol is 1:1-1.3. In step (3), the concentration of sulfuric acid used in the two times pH adjustment is 40-60%.

TI Producing hydrogen by subjecting a gaseous mixture of hydrocarbon and steam to adiabatic pre-reforming, subjecting to water-gas shift stages, cooling, passing reformed gas to carbon dioxide separation unit, and passing to purification unit.
AU COTTON W J

   BOROUJENI M S
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen is produced by (i) subjecting a gaseous mixture comprising a hydrocarbon and steam, and having a steam to carbon ratio of greater than or equal to 0.9:1 to adiabatic pre-reforming in a pre-reformer (40) followed by autothermal reforming with an oxygen-rich gas in an autothermal reformer (46) to generate a reformed gas mixture, (ii) increasing the hydrogen content of reformed gas mixture by subjecting to greater than or equal to 1 water-gas shift stages in a water-gas shift unit to provide a hydrogen-enriched reformed gas, (iii) cooling the hydrogen-enriched reformed gas and separating condensed water therefrom to provide a de-watered hydrogen-enriched reformed gas, (iv) passing the de-watered hydrogen-enriched reformed gas to a carbon dioxide separation unit to provide a carbon dioxide gas stream and a crude hydrogen gas stream, and (v) passing the crude hydrogen gas stream from the carbon dioxide removal unit to a purification unit to provide a purified hydrogen gas and a fuel gas.
   USE - Production of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts hydrocarbons to hydrogen while minimizing carbon dioxide production. The heating requirement and oxygen demand for the reforming stages is reduced and the front-end equipment (e.g. fired heater, pre-reformer, and autothermal reformer) is smaller and lower in cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrogen is produced by (i) subjecting a gaseous mixture comprising a hydrocarbon and steam, and having a steam to carbon ratio of greater than or equal to 0.9:1 to adiabatic pre-reforming in a pre-reformer (40) followed by autothermal reforming with an oxygen-rich gas in an autothermal reformer (46) to generate a reformed gas mixture, (ii) increasing the hydrogen content of reformed gas mixture by subjecting to greater than or equal to 1 water-gas shift stages in a water-gas shift unit to provide a hydrogen-enriched reformed gas, (iii) cooling the hydrogen-enriched reformed gas and separating condensed water therefrom to provide a de-watered hydrogen-enriched reformed gas, (iv) passing the de-watered hydrogen-enriched reformed gas to a carbon dioxide separation unit to provide a carbon dioxide gas stream and a crude hydrogen gas stream, and (v) passing the crude hydrogen gas stream from the carbon dioxide removal unit to a purification unit to provide a purified hydrogen gas and a fuel gas, where the fuel gas is fed to greater than or equal to 1 fired heaters used to heat greater than or equal to 1 process streams within the process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a diagrammatic flowsheet in which all of the process steam is added upstream of the reforming unit operations.

   Pre-reformer (40)
   Autothermal reformer (46)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon is a methane-containing gas stream, preferably containing greater than or equal to 50 vol.% methane. The hydrocarbon is desulfurized.

PN CN111337587-A
TI Determining soil microbial biomass based on            high-throughput headspace gas chromatography comprises            taking dry soil sample, drawing standard curve,            measuring carbon dioxide, comparing signal value and            calculating microbial biomass carbon.
AU TU X
   HU Z
   LI G
   XU B
   SUAN S
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 202061063L
AB    NOVELTY - Determining soil microbial biomass based on                high-throughput headspace gas chromatography                comprises (a) removing the visible plant residues                and soil animals first in the soil sample, (b)                taking dry soil sample or dry sludge sample in the                headspace bottle, adding glucose solution,                immediately sealing and mixing, (c) preparing a                series of sodium bicarbonate standard solutions,                sealing, setting the operating conditions of the                headspace sampler and the operating conditions of                the gas chromatograph, drawing a standard curve to                obtain the calibration factor, (d) adding                hydrochloric acid, measuring the carbon dioxide                produced by the sample in different incubation                times and (e) comparing the signal value of the                carbon dioxide chromatographic peak area with the                standard curve, calculating the respiration rate of                the test sample during SIR by calculation as given                in the specification and calculating microbial                biomass carbon (MBC).
   USE - The method is useful for determining soil                microbial biomass based on high-throughput                headspace gas chromatography.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: simplifies the operation process;                improves the accuracy of the measurement results;                does not require the use of chloroform (toxic                organic solvent), which is in line with the purpose                of green chemistry and is especially suitable for                rapid and batch detection of SMB in different soil                samples.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining soil microbial biomass based on                high-throughput headspace gas chromatography                comprises (a) removing the visible plant residues                and soil animals first in the soil sample,                naturally drying, sieving and mixing, naturally                drying the sludge sample first and sieving and                mixing well, (b) taking 0.1-1g dry soil sample or                dry sludge sample in the headspace bottle, adding                1-5ml glucose solution, immediately sealing and                mixing, placing into a constant temperature shaking                incubator at 20-35 degrees C and 150-300                revolutions/minute, (c) preparing a series of                sodium bicarbonate standard solutions in the                headspace bottle, sealing them, injecting 0.1-0.6ml                sulfuric acid solution, placing the headspace                bottle in the headspace sampler, setting the                operating conditions of the headspace sampler and                the operating conditions of the gas chromatograph,                where the HS-GC adopts a batch automatic detection                mode to measure the carbon dioxide produced by the                sample, drawing a standard curve based on the                obtained carbon dioxide chromatographic peak area                signal value and the known concentration of                sulfuric acid for the correspondence between                solutions to obtain the calibration factor, (d)                adding 0.5-3 ml hydrochloric acid to the headspace                bottle to acidify, placing in the headspace                sampler, using the same headspace sampler operating                conditions as above and the operating conditions of                the gas chromatograph, measuring the carbon dioxide                produced by the sample in different incubation                times, recording the carbon dioxide chromatographic                peak area signal value of the sample and (e)                comparing the signal value of the carbon dioxide                chromatographic peak area with the standard curve,                calculating the respiration rate of the test sample                during SIR by calculation as given in the                specification and calculating microbial biomass                carbon (MBC).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The size of the sieve                  is 0.25-2mm. The concentration of the glucose                  solution is 0.5-2g/l. The sulfuric acid solution                  is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. The concentration of                  hydrochloric acid is 0.0005-0.005 mol/l. The soil                  type is loam, including cinnamon soil, laterite.                  The sludge is the product of municipal sludge                  ultra-high temperature aerobic compost                  fermentation. Preferred Method: In the step (3),                  the operating conditions of the HSGC batch                  automatic detection headspace sampler are:                  vigorously shaking the bottle at 40-70 degrees C                  for 1-6 minutes, where the pipeline temperature                  is 50-100 degrees C and the transfer line                  temperature is 50-100 degrees C. The carrier gas                  balance time in the headspace sample bottle is                  10-30 seconds, the pipeline inflation time is                  10-30 seconds, the pipeline balance time is 1-10                  seconds, and the loop equilibrium time is 10-30                  seconds. The operating conditions of the gas                  chromatograph are: using a GS-Q capillary                  chromatography column (30mx 0.53mm), the column                  temperature is 30-150 degrees C, the carrier gas                  is nitrogen, the carrier gas flow rate is 2-6                  ml/min and the temperature of the thermal                  conductivity detector is 150-350 degrees C.

TI Preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate useful for lithium ion battery, comprises e.g. mixing 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane, brominated fluoroalkane, organic base, homogenizing and sending to a drying device for cyclic dehydration.
AU MA H
   LV J
   GU Y
   TU D
   MAO W
   ZHAO B
   WANG B
   HAN S
   BAI Y
   YANG Z
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
   XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 201843411K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate comprises e.g. mixing 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane, brominated fluoroalkane, organic base, homogenizing, sending to a drying device for cyclic dehydration, where organic base is a trialkylamine, an N,N-dimethylamide or a nitrogen-containing heterocycle, bromine-containing, pre-heating the dried 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane material by preheater, mixing with dried carbon dioxide in a mixer, and reacting through a tubular reactor obtaining a product stream including 4-trifluoromethylethylene carbonate, 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane and carbon dioxide, and entering product stream into gas-liquid separation tank, where the top component is carbon dioxide, entering carbon dioxide recovery system, and recycling, where the bottom material enters first rectification column for separation of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide and top component of first rectification column is material containing 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide.
   USE - The 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate is useful for lithium ion battery electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate has continuous and stable process, high space-time yield and has no metal residue and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate comprises (a) mixing 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane, brominated fluoroalkane, organic base, homogenizing, sending to a drying device for cyclic dehydration, where the structure of brominated fluoroalkane is CnH2n+2-a-bc-dFaClcIdBrb (I).
   n = 2-4;
   a = 3-8;
   b = 2-7;
   c = 0-6;
   d = 0-6; and
   a+b+c+d = greater than or equal to (2n+2).
   . The organic base is a trialkylamine, an N,N-dimethylamide or a nitrogen-containing heterocycle, bromine-containing fluoroalkane and the organic base are used in an amount of 0.5-20 mol.% based on the molar mass of 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane; (b) pre-heating the dried 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane material by preheater, mixing with the dried carbon dioxide in a mixer, and reacting through a tubular reactor obtaining a product stream including 4-trifluoromethylethylene carbonate, 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane and carbon dioxide, where the preheating temperature is 50-70 degrees C, and the mixing temperature is 80-200 degrees C, reaction temperature is 70-180 degrees C, reaction pressure is 0.1-5 MPa, reaction residence time is 10-90 minutes; and (c) entering the product stream of step (b) into the gas-liquid separation tank, where the top component is carbon dioxide, entering the carbon dioxide recovery system, and recycling, where the bottom material enters the first rectification column for separation of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide and the top component of first rectification column is a material containing 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide, returning to the organic material recovery unit, and recycling, where the column component enters the second rectification column, where the top component of the second rectification column is 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate, and the components of the column are returned to the organic material recovery unit for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the structural (I), the organic base is trialkylamines, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, imidazole, alkylimidazole, pyridine, 4-dimethylaminopyridine, 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, 1,5-diaza bicycle(4.3.0)-5-pinene, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene and 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo (4.4.0)anthracene-5-ene or triethylenediamine. In the step (a), bromine-containing fluoroalkane is 1,2-dibromotetrafluoroethane, 1,1,2-tribromotrifluoroethane, 2,3-dibromo-1,1,1,2,3,3-hexafluoropropane, 2,3-dibromo-2,3,3-trichloro-1,1,1-trifluoropropane, 2,3-dibromo-2,3-dichloro-1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluorobutane,2,2,3,3-tetrabromo-1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluorobutane, 2,2,3-dibromo-1,1,1,2,3,4,4,4-octafluorobutane or 1,4-dibromooctafluorobutane and the organic base is triethylamine, N,N-dimethylformamide or N-alkylimidazole. The bromine-containing fluoroalkane and organic base has content of 3-10 mol.% based on the molar mass of 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane. In the step (b), 3,3,3-trifluoroepoxypropane material has a moisture content of less than 20 ppm. Preferred Method: In the step (b), the reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.5-2 MPa and the residence time is 30-60 minutes.

PN US2022214079-A1; EP4027075-A1; IN202114046371-A; CN114719460-A
TI Refrigeration system for carbon dioxide based            refrigerant fluid, has refrigerant circuit comprising            bypass line and bypass control valve, where bypass line            provides fluid connection between outlet of heat            rejecting heat exchanger and expansion device.
AU ANTONSSON M
AE CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
   CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
   CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
GA 202288012M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a bypass line (24) providing a                fluid connection between an outlet (203) of a heat                rejecting heat exchanger (14) and an expansion                device (18). A bypass control valve (26) prevents                fluid flow through the bypass line such that all                fluid exiting the heat rejecting exchanger enters                an ejector primary inlet (201). The bypass line is                arranged such that fluid does not undergo heat                exchange with another portion of the system when                flowing from the outlet to an inlet of the                expansion device through the line. A check valve                (28) is provided between a liquid outlet of the                receiver and the device. The valve is a three-port                valve.
   USE - Refrigeration system for carbon dioxide based                refrigerant fluid e.g. carbon dioxide refrigerant                fuel. Can also be used in refrigeration                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-port valve provides the function of                the bypass control valve and the check valve in a                single valve, thus reducing the number of                components of the refrigeration system, and hence                providing improved reliability and reduced                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of controlling a refrigeration system for a carbon                dioxide based refrigerant fluid.

PT P
PN EP4019464-A1; WO2022136200-A1
TI Making synthesis gas having carbon monoxide, by            partially converting first feed stream having steam and            hydrogen to hydrogen rich first process stream,            converting first process stream originates second feed            stream to second process stream.
AU MORTENSEN P M
   BOGILD HANSEN J
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2022842695
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas comprising carbon                monoxide, comprises: (a) partially converting a                first feed stream (1) comprising steam and hydrogen                to a hydrogen rich first process stream (2) by                electrolysis (A); (b) converting first process                stream originates a second feed stream (4) to a                carbon monoxide rich second process stream (5) in a                reforming step (B); (c) cooling the second process                stream comprising carbon monoxide rich syngas and                steam; and (d) providing another stream comprising                steam which enters the first feed stream, where the                molar hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio in the                second process stream is below 4.5 and mixing at                least one of (i) first feed stream or (ii) first                process stream with a carbon dioxide rich stream                (6).
   USE - The method is useful for producing synthesis                gas comprising carbon monoxide for production of                phosgene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improved method for producing                synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) plant for producing synthesis gas                comprising carbon monoxide, where at least one                electrolyzer (A) is arranged upstream to at least                one reformer (B); and
   (2) syngas obtained by the method in the                plant, where the syngas has a module of                (hydrogen-carbon dioxide)/(carbon monoxide+ carbon                dioxide) is 1.8-2.2, suitable for methanol                synthesis in a downstream methanol reactor system                for methanol production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a general overview of the                plant and the method.
   First feed stream (1)
   Hydrogen rich first process stream (2)
   Second feed stream (4)
   Carbon monoxide rich second process stream                (5)
   Carbon dioxide rich stream (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first feed stream                  further comprises carbon monoxide. The first feed                  stream further comprises a carbon dioxide rich                  stream which comprises at least 25 mol%,                  preferably up to 100 mol% carbon dioxide. The                  second feed stream further comprises a carbon                  dioxide rich stream, which comprises at least 25                  mole% preferably up to 100 mol% carbon dioxide.                  The stream preferably comprises more carbon                  dioxide than stream. The second feed stream also                  comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, nitrogen,                  methane, via merging a carbon import stream with                  the carbon dioxide rich stream. The second feed                  stream also comprises oxygen, hydrocarbons,                  alcohols and/or ketones, via merging a carbon                  import stream with the carbon dioxide rich                  stream. The first feed stream also comprises                  carbon monoxide, hydrogen, nitrogen, methane, via                  splitting a carbon import stream between carbon                  dioxide rich streams. The first feed stream also                  comprises oxygen, hydrocarbons, alcohols and/or                  ketones, via splitting a carbon import stream                  between carbon dioxide rich streams. The first                  process stream, when exiting the electrolyzer,                  has a temperature of 600-1000 degrees C,                  preferably 700-850 degrees C, which is lower than                  the temperature of the second process stream,                  when exiting the reformer, of 850-1200 degrees C,                  preferably 950-1050 degrees C. The first feed                  stream and the second feed stream are heated by                  means of electrically heating, condensing steam                  and/or gas heated heat exchangers, where part of                  the residual enthalpy in the synthesis gas and                  air streams is recuperated to be used in a                  downstream synthesis and the second process                  stream comprising syngas and steam is cooled down                  to room temperature, the non-converted water                  being condensed and reused. The part of the                  generated syngas is recycled back into the first                  feed stream to an solid oxide electrolysis cell                  (SOEC). The electrolyzer A is an SOEC, reformer                  (B) is an electrically driven steam methane                  reforming technology (eSMR) and heat exchanger                  (C) is a boiler. The synthesis unit is downstream                  to the production of synthesis gas. The synthesis                  unit is a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor                  system for producing fuels. The synthesis unit is                  a methanol reactor system for producing methanol.                  The syngas has a ratio of hydrogen/carbon                  monoxide is 1.8-2.2, for Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis in a downstream Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis reactor system for crude oil and/or wax                  production. The syngas has a module of (carbon                  monoxide + hydrogen)/(carbon dioxide +water) is                  greater than 7.5 and is suitable as a reducing                  agent. The syngas has a ratio of hydrogen/carbon                  monoxide is less than 1.5 and is suitable as a                  source of carbon monoxide.
   EXAMPLE - Boiler feed water flow of 12298 kg/hour was                sent to the boiler E100 where it was evaporated                attains a temperature of 134 degrees C. After the                boiler 824 kg/hour of carbon dioxide was added as                well 927 Nm3/hour of recycled synthesis gas. The                combined flow was then preheated to 750 degrees C                in the electrical heater, E200, before entering the                cathode chamber of the solid oxide electrolyzer                (SOEC), where 80% of the steam was converted to                hydrogen and 68.2% of the carbon dioxide was                converted to carbon monoxide and methane. The                methane content of the gas leaving the cathode was                0.27 mol% and the carbon monoxide was 3 mol%. The                Boudouard temperature of the gas leaving the                cathode was 612 degrees C. After the SOEC a further                7414 kg/hour of carbon dioxide was added before the                gas enters the eSMR. In the eSMR the reverse water                gas shift and the steam reforming of methane was                performed and the gas ws heated to 1000 degrees C.                The carbon monoxide content of the gas was                increased to 16.3 mol% and the methane content                reduced to 13 ppm. This hot gas was cooled to 150                degrees C by generating steam in the boiler E 100.                The gas was further cooled to 65 degrees C in the                heat exchanger E 300 and used to preheat boiler                feed water before it was finally cooled to 20                degrees C in the water cooler E 400. The                unconverted water was then separated from the gas                in the separator, S100. Part of gas leaving the                separator was recycled back to the SOEC and the                rest sent to the methanol synthesis. On the anode                side of the SOEC 14014 kg/hour of dry air was                compressed and preheated to 730 degrees C in the                feed/effluent exchanger E 500 and finally to 750                degrees C in the electrical heater E 600 before                entering the anode. Due to the steam and carbon                dioxide electrolysis the oxygen content was                increased to 50 mol% in the gas leaving the                cathode. The cathode exit gas was used to preheat                the incoming air in the feed/effluent exchanger, E                500, and was further cooled to 65 degrees C in the                heat exchanger E 700.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuthene/cesium lead tribromide quantum dot composite material of S-type heterojunction comprises (i) dissolving bismuthene in octadecene, and mixing at room temperature to form a bismuthene dispersion, (ii) dissolving lead(II) bromide in octadecene, heating under the protection of an inert gas, adding oleic acid and oleylamine respectively, and keeping the temperature until lead(II) bromide is completely dissolved to obtain a precursor solution, (iii) adding bismuthene dispersion into the precursor solution, heating, adding cesium acetate, keeping the temperature until the reaction is complete, and cooling, and (iv) centrifuging and washing obtained solution of bismuthene/cesium lead tribromide quantum composite material, where the step (i) and (ii) are in no particular order.
   USE - The quantum dot composite material is useful in high-efficiency photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or photocatalytic water splitting to produce hydrogen or photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material can efficiently inhibit the recombination of photo-generated electron-hole pairs and maintains the strong reducing ability of the catalyst, so as to achieve the purpose of improving the ability of photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, can efficiently improve the carbon dioxide conversion ability. The method of is simple and economical, and the composite material has excellent carbon dioxide photocatalytic conversion ability under ultraviolet-visible light and visible light irradiation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for bismuthene/cesium lead tribromide quantum dot composite material of S-type heterojunction, prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the preparation of bismuthene includes (a) dissolving 5-20 wt.% polyvinylidene fluoride in N-N dimethylpyrrolidone solvent, and ultrasonically treating to form uniform dispersion, (ii) mixing bismuth metal powder with the dispersion in mass ratio of 1:5-50, and ball milling to obtain the mixed solution, where the size of the two-dimensional bismuthene material obtained is 100 nm-5 microm, and (iii) treating mixed solution by differential centrifugation at 500-5000 revolutions/minute for 15-30 minutes, and centrifuging at 8000-10000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, and repeatedly washing using N-N dimethylpyrrolidone as a solvent. In the step (ii), the temperature is heated to 110-130degrees Celsius. In the step (iii), the temperature is heated to 160-175degrees Celsius, the reaction time is 4-8 seconds, and the temperature is cooled to 20-40degrees Celsius. In the step (iv), the centrifugal rotation speed is 7500-10000 revolutions/minute, and the washing is performed 2-3 times, and each washing time is 3-8 minutes.Preferred Components: In the step (i), the concentration of the bismuthene dispersion is 2-8 mg/ml. In the step (ii), the inert gas is argon, and the ratio of lead(II) bromide, octadecene, oleic acid and oleylamine is 2-4 mmol:15-30 ml: 1-3 ml:1-3 ml. In the step (iii), the molar ratio of the cesium acetate and lead(II) bromide is 1-6:10. In the step (iv), the centrifugation and washing solvents used in n-hexane and tert-butanol. The bismuthene/cesium lead tribromide quantum dot composite material comprises 10-20 wt.% bismuthene.

PN WO2021256237-A1; JP2022532471-X
TI New polyimide compound used for forming layer for            forming gas separation membrane of module used in            device for e.g. selectively permeating carbon dioxide            from gas comprising carbon dioxide and methane.
AU HIRONAKA K
AE FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
GA 2021E8040H
AB    NOVELTY - A polyimide compound comprising a repeating                unit (I), is new.
   USE - The polyimide compound is used for forming                layer for forming gas separation membrane of module                used in device for selectively permeating carbon                dioxide from gas comprising carbon dioxide and                methane (all claimed). Can also be used for                separating specific gas from gas mixture containing                hydrogen, helium, carbon monoxide, hydrogen                sulfide, oxygen, nitrogen, ammonia, sulfur oxide,                nitrogen oxide, ethane, propylene and                tetrafluoroethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane containing the                polyimide compound has improved mechanical                strength, gas permeability and gas separation                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyimide compound comprising a repeating                unit of formula (I), is new.
   R = group chosen from groups of formulae                (i-1)-(i-28);
   X1-X3 = single bond or divalent linking                group;
   L = -CH=CH- or -CH2-;
   R1,R2 = H or substituent;
   L2 = group chosen from groups of formulae                (ii-1)-(ii-3);
   A' = group chosen from groups of formulae                (iii-1)-(iii-8);
   B' = unsubstituted methyl, unsubstituted                ethyl, unsubstituted n-propyl, unsubstituted                isopropyl, fluoro-substituted 1-5C alkyl,                fluoro-substituted 2-6C acyl, or halo;
   R'a,R'b = H, unsubstituted methyl,                unsubstituted ethyl, unsubstituted n-propyl,                unsubstituted isopropyl, or fluoro-substituted 1-5C                alkyl;
   a = 1-4;
   b = 0-3;
   a+b = 2-4;
   w+x = 1-8;
   y+z = 0-7;and
   w+x+y+z = 2-8.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas                separation membrane, which comprises a gas                separation layer (1) comprising the polyimide                compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the gas                separation membrane.
   Gas separation layer (1)
   Porous layer (2)
   Gas separation composite membrane (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (disclosed): Preparation of the                  polyimide compound involves performing                  condensation polymerization of aromatic diamine                  and tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride, and                  brominating. Preferred Composition: The gas                  separation membrane is a gas separation composite                  membrane (10) comprising the gas separation layer                  on an upper side of a gas permeable support                  layer.
   EXAMPLE - 13.7 g                2-Methyl-5-(trifluoromethyl)benzene-1,3-diamine,                1.2 g 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid and 98 ml                N-methylpyrrolidone were added to a three-necked                flask under a nitrogen stream. Under water cooling,                35.5 g 4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylidene)diphthalic                acid anhydride was added, and the mixture was                washed with 35 mL N-methylpyrrolidone. After                stirring at 40 degrees C for 3 hours, 32 mL toluene                was added, and the mixture was stirred at 170                degrees C for 6 hours. After cooling to room                temperature, the mixture was diluted with 30 mL                N-methylpyrrolidone and 350 mL acetone and                transferred to 5 L three-necked flask. 2 L methanol                was dripped into the solution to precipitate. The                obtained product was vacuum-filtered, washed with                methanol, and air-dried at 50 degrees C for 20                hours to obtain 40.3 g (yield 85%) polyimide having                a weight average molecular weight of 92000. 9 g                Polyimide and 225 g 1,2-dichloroethane were placed                in a three-necked flask. 15.5 g N-bromosuccinimide                and 0.16 g 2,2'-azobis(isobutyronitrile) were                added, and the mixture was stirred at 85 degrees C                for 8 hours and cooled to room temperature.                Methanol was dripped into the reaction solution to                precipitate. The obtained product was                vacuum-filtered, washed with methanol, and                air-dried at 50 degrees C for 18 hours to obtain 10                g polyimide having a weight average molecular                weight of 100000 and a bromination rate of 60%. The                gas separation membrane of the polyimide had a                carbon dioxide permeation rate of 120 GPU or more,                and gas separation selectivity (ratio of carbon                dioxide with respect to methane) of 18 or                more.

The anode (2) for providing a nickel                iron-based catalyst for oxygen evolution reaction                in an anolyte. The cathode (3) for providing a                surface of a multi-level nanoporous metal-based                flexible film for carbon dioxide reduction                reaction. The ion exchange membrane (5) for                separating the anode and the cathode, and for ion                exchange. The positive and negative electrodes of                the solar cell are connected to the anode and the                cathode, respectively.
   USE - Artificial photosynthesis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial photosynthesis system has                improves the maximum photocurrent utilization rate,                and the carbon dioxide, and the product selectivity                of RR, in order, improves the STF efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for artificial photosynthesis by using system,                which involves: (a)providing photocurrent and                photovoltage;(b)using the photocurrent and                photovoltage to driving the nickel iron-based                catalyst to carrying out oxygen evolution reaction                in the anolyte to provide oxygen stream;(c)putting                the multi-level nanoporous metal-based flexible                film into the catholyte;(d)diffusing carbon dioxide                gas onto the porous metal-based flexible                film;(e)using the photocurrent and photovoltage to                driving the surface of the porous metal-based                flexible film to carrying out a carbon dioxide                reduction reaction to provide a product stream,                where the anolyte and catholyte are separated by an                ion exchange membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic view                of the artificial photosynthesis system.
   Solar cell (1)
   Anode (2)
   Cathode (3)
   Ion exchange membrane (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode body (21)                  includes an anode material, and the anode                  material includes a nickel iron-based catalyst.                  The cathode material includes multi-level                  nanoporous metal-based flexible film. The iron                  salt selected from ferric nitrate, ferric                  sulfate, and ferric chloride. The nickel salt is                  selected from nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate, and                  nickel chloride. The solar cell is selected from                  gallium indium phosphide/gallium indium                  arsenide/germanium, gallium indium                  phosphide/gallium arsenide/gallium indium                  arsenide, indium gallium phosphide/gallium                  arsenide/ indium gallium arsenide, copper zinc                  tin sulfide selenium, copper indium gallium                  selenide, and p-silicon. The anolyte is potassium                  hydroxide solution. The catholyte is potassium                  hydroxide solution. Preferred Process: The                  preparation method of the nickel iron-based                  catalyst involves putting the solution of iron                  salt and nickel salt into ethylene glycol, adding                  water and ammonium fluoride, mixing                  ultrasonically to obtain a precursor solution,                  putting the foamed nickel cathode and the carbon                  paper anode into the obtained precursor solution                  after the reaction, and taking out the foamed                  nickel on which the catalyst is deposited, which                  is a nickel iron-based catalyst. The preparation                  method of the multi-level nanoporous metal-based                  flexible film involves depositing a metal film on                  one side of the flexible film by a physical                  vacuum method, anodizing the metal thin film in                  solution and reducing in cathode to obtain a                  multi-level nanoporous metal-based flexible thin                  film. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of                  iron salt and nickel salt is 0.7-1.0:0.7-1.0. The                  volume ratio of nickel salt solution and ethylene                  glycol is 0.7-1.0:100-100.5. The molar ratio of                  nickel salt to ammonium fluoride is                  0.2-0.3:2.9-3.0. The volume ratio of ethylene                  glycol and water is 100-100.5 ml:0.7-1.0 ml. The                  precursor solution is preheated to 40-45degrees                  Celsius. The distance between the foamed nickel                  cathode and the carbon paper anode is 1.8-2.0 cm,                  and the working area is                  0.5x2.0cm-1.0x2.0cm.

TI Operation method for removing human body tattoo by performing common sterilization to tattoo part; coating analgesic agent at patient tattoo, using dot-matrix carbon dioxide laser to treat tattoo part, using nursing antiinflammatory medicine for diminishing inflammation, and wrapping gauze.
AU YUAN S
AE YUAN S (YUAN-Individual)
GA 2021E5649Y
AB    NOVELTY - Operation method for removing human body tattoo, comprises (1) performing common sterilization to the tattoo part of the patient; turning to the step two; (2) coating analgesic agent at the patient tattoo, using dot-matrix carbon dioxide laser to treat the tattoo part, setting energy density to 1530 mJ per unit pixel area, so that the carbon dioxide laser directly acts on the tattoo part, until the tattoo part skin is semi-peeling off, turning to step three; (3) after the lattice laser operation, using nursing antiinflammatory medicine to smear the body for nursing and diminishing inflammation; and wrapping by gauze; changing once every day; and (4) finishing the step 3 of gauze and interval for 90 days, repeating the step 1, until repeating the operation of the step 1 three times, namely finishing a whole course of treatment.
   USE - Operation method for removing human body tattoo.
   ADVANTAGE - The operation method uses carbon dioxide lattice laser with excitation human body repair mechanism, promoting human tissue repair and regeneration characteristics; can peel off the tattoo part tissue, stimulate the bottom skin to generate growth factor, promote local tissue regeneration, reach the purpose of removing the skin color growing new skin, can easily remove the tattoo, with good treatment effect; and can completely remove all kinds of tattoo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The analgesic agent comprises lidocaine cream. After finishing the treatment of the step 3, avoiding spicy and spicy food for 15 days, and keeping the tattoo part hygiene and clean. The nursing antiinflammatory medicine is composed of 30 g Bletilla striata, 10 g borneol, 20 g Vaseline (Petroleum jelly), 5 ml sesame oil and 15 ml Phellodendron liquid extract. Preferred Method: The preparation method of medicine for nursing and diminishing inflammation specifically comprises (1) providing Bletilla striata, borneol, Vaseline (Petroleum jelly), sesame oil and Phellodendron liquid extract; (2) crushing Bletilla striata and borneol in a crusher to obtain Bletilla striata powder and borneol powder; (3) taking Vaseline (Petroleum jelly) into stainless steel pot for heating and melting, dropping 5 ml sesame oil, adding Phellodendron liquid extract, stirring uniformly, at last respectively adding Bletilla striata powder, and borneol powder, continuously fully and uniformly stirring to obtain semifinished product medicine; and (4) placing the semifinished product into the sealing box, allowing to stand and cooling, until the solidification, preparing the product for nursing and diminishing inflammation medicine. When using the nursing antiinflammatory medicine product, using sterile cotton swab to dip nursing antiinflammatory medicine, and coating on the sterile gauze, coating on the afflicted part of the tattoo patient.
