TI Automatic fire extinguishing low voltage capacitance compensation cabinet for extinguishing fire, has embedded sleeve embedded with carbon dioxide cylinder, and right side of inner wall of cabinet body provided with radiating straight slot.
AU ZHANG B
AE YANCHENG HUAXING SWITCHGEAR DEVICE CO (YANC-Non-standard)
GA 2021E45545
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to low voltage capacitance compensation cabinet technology field, specifically claims an automatic fire extinguishing low voltage capacitance compensation cabinet, comprising a cabinet body; the cabinet body is hinged with a cabinet door; the left side of the inner wall of the cabinet body is detachably installed with a smoke sensor; the bottom of the right side of the inner wall of the cabinet body is fixedly installed with a supporting block; the top of the supporting block is detachably mounted with a contact switch; the right side of the inner wall of the cabinet body is detachably mounted with a sliding rail strip; the sliding rail strip is embedded and slidingly mounted with a baffle; the right side of the inner wall of the cabinet body is detachably mounted with a mounting block; the inner wall of the mounting block is detachably mounted with an electromagnet. The utility model monitors whether there is fire in the cabinet body through the smoke sensor; when starting fire, the smoke sensor controls the electromagnet to break; the baffle slides to touch the contact switch; the pump pumps the gas in the carbon dioxide cylinder to the interior of the cabinet body; the interior of the cabinet body is filled with carbon dioxide to quickly realize the fire extinguishing effect; The baffle seals the radiating straight slot which can quickly fill carbon dioxide in the cabinet body.

TI Dual-laser processing machine has carbon dioxide laser cutter head that is set on composite tool holder, where holes are swept in defocused manner using laser, so that cracks between holes are slightly expanded after being heated to break.
AU HU W
AE TAIWAN LI CHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TALI-Non-standard)
GA 2021403879
AB    NOVELTY - The machine (100) has a base (120) which is provided at the bottom of a main housing (110). A working platform (200) is set on the base and used to fix a workpiece (600). A composite tool holder (300) is arranged on the housing, so that the composite tool holder moves relative to the housing to adjust the relative position with the working platform. A UV laser cutter head (400) is set on the composite cutter seat. A low-power UV nano laser is configured to intensively penetrate the surface of the workpiece to make the holes full of cracks. A carbon dioxide laser cutter head (500) is set on the composite tool holder. The holes are swept in a defocused manner using carbon dioxide laser, so that the cracks between the holes are slightly expanded after being heated to break to complete the cutting.
   USE - Dual laser processing machine for cutting and finishing method of brittle material such as glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The processing flow of the dual laser processing machine is simplified, which greatly improves the precision and shortens the processing time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a processing method of dual laser processing machine.

TI New m-phenylenediamine compound used for            manufacturing polymer for forming layer of            gas-separation membrane used in gas-separation module            or device for selectively permeating carbon dioxide            from gas containing carbon dioxide and methane.
AU HIRONAKA K
AE FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
   FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
GA 202110654C
AB    NOVELTY - A m-phenylenediamine compound (I) is                new.
   USE - New m-phenylenediamine compound is used for                manufacturing polymer e.g. polyimide compound,                polyurethane compound, polyurea compound, or                polyamide compound for forming layer of                gas-separation membrane used in gas-separation                module or device for selectively permeating carbon                dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide and                methane (all claimed) derived from thermal power                station, cement plant, iron mill blast furnace, and                gas emitted by fermentation of excretion of living                organisms, organic fertilizer, biodegradable                substance, wastewater, refuse, and energy crop, and                anaerobic digestion.
   ADVANTAGE - The m-phenylenediamine compound produces                polymer which is capable of producing                gas-separation membrane having excellent gas                permeability even when exposed to severe drying                condition, gas-separation selectivity, and                mechanical strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A m-phenylenediamine compound of formula (I)                is new.
   R'a,R'b = H, alkyl, or halo;and
   L'a = -CF2-, -CF(CF3)-, -C(=O)-, -CH2-,                -CH(CH3)-, -CH(CF3)-, or divalent group comprising                combination of two or more of these groups, and                comprises 4 or less carbon atoms.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) polymer, which comprises a                m-phenylenediamine structural unit of formula                (II);
   (2) manufacture of the polymer, which involves                using compound (I) as a raw material; and
   (3) gas-separation membrane, which comprises a                gas-separation layer (1) comprising the                polymer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                gas-separation composite film.
   Gas-separation layer (1)
   Porous layer (2)
   Gas-separation composite film (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No general preparation given.                  Preferred Components: Compound (I) is preferably                  of formula (I-1) or (I-2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Structural unit (II)                  is preferably of formula (II-1) or (II-2). The                  polymer constituting the gas-separation layer is                  a polyimide compound comprising a structural unit                  of formula (III), preferably of formula (III-1)                  or (III-2). The gas-separation membrane is a                  gas-separation composite film (10) comprising the                  gas-separation layer on an upper side of a                  gas-permeable support layer.R = group chosen from 28 groups, e.g. groups                  of formulae (a1)-(a2).
   EXAMPLE - 25 g 4-(Heptafluoroisopropyl)toluene was                placed in a three-necked flask and cooled in an ice                bath. 250 mL of 1.84 g/cm3 Concentrated sulfuric                acid and 36 g fuming nitric acid were carefully                dripped and reacted at an internal temperature of                45 degrees C for 10 hours. The mixture was                ice-cooled and carefully poured into ice. The                target product was filtered, washed with water and                saturated aqueous sodium hydrogen carbonate to                obtain 40 g                5-(1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoroprop-2-yl)-2-methyl-1,3-dinitrobenzene                containing water. 40 g                5-(1,1,1,2,3,3,3-Heptafluoroprop-2-yl)-2-methyl-1,3-dinitrobenzene                was dissolved in 700 mL methanol and placed in 2 L                autoclave. 6.8 g Palladium-activated carbon was                added and the autoclave was sealed, filled with 5                MPa hydrogen, and reacted at 35 degrees C for 6                hours. The palladium-activated carbon was carefully                filtered without drying. The filtrate was                concentrated under reduced pressure and the solid                was purified on a silica gel column using ethyl                acetate and chloroform to obtain crystals. The                crystals were vacuum dried at 60 degrees C for 8                hours to obtain 23.1 g                5-(1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoroprop-2-yl)-2-methylbenzene-1,3-diamine                with a yield of 83%. The gas-separation membrane                obtained using a polymer containing                5-(1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoroprop-2-yl)-2-methylbenzene-1,3-diamine                had a carbon dioxide transmission rate of 120 GPU                or more and a gas-separation selectivity of 18 or                more.

   WANG L
   SUN P

AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing the pipe joint in a vertical position with the ground. The manual tungsten argon is used. The pipe joint is placed in a vertical position with the ground. The flux-cored carbon dioxide gas shielded welding is used with a diameter of 1.2mm, welding current 200-260A, voltage 24-28V, gas flow 15L/min, dry wire extension 12mm, and multilayer multi-pass welding. The pipe joint is placed at a position level with the ground using swing welding, when covering the surface layer. The flux-cored carbon dioxide gas shielded welding is used with a diameter of 1.2mm, welding current 200-260A, voltage 24-28V, gas flow 15L/min. The dry ext nsion is 12mm, and the welding method is adopted. The welding torch and the pipe joint are at an angle of 80-90 degrees. The angle of the welding torch is adjusted at any time to keep the weld within the protection range of carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for welding boiler header components by using carbon di oxide gas shielded welding technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon di oxide gas shielded welding adopts slag-gas combined protection, which makes easier to achieve high-quality welds and the product deformation after welding is small which is good for health of welders.

One end of the booster fan is provided with a conveying pipeline is connected with a first discharge valve of feeding system through a three-way connection. A lithium hydroxide material in feeding system enters the conveying pipeline through first discharge valve. The second material gas separator is arranged at the end of the conveying pipeline is connected to one side of the second material gas separator. The return pipeline is provided with a carbon dioxide detector and a moisture detector, and is also provided with an intake valve and an exhaust valve. The carbon dioxide detector is electrically connected to exhaust valve. The end of the return pipeline is connected with the inlet end of the booster fan. The device further comprises the conveying pipeline and the lining is made of ceramic, glass or plastic materials.
   USE - Used as battery-grade lithium hydroxide sealed air flow conveying device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: adopts a completely airtight method for conveying battery-grade lithium hydroxide materials; utilizes nitrogen without carbon dioxide and moisture as the transport medium; sends products from one process to another; prevents the lithium hydroxide material from reacting with the carbon dioxide and moisture contained in the air; and ensures the quality of the product.

PN CN110905505-A
TI Carbon dioxide explosion based coal mining method            involves starting scraper conveyor to transport coal            and moving falling coal to telescopic belt conveyor,            and moving scraper conveyor to coal wall of new working            face after coal is sent out.
AU ZHU Z
   CHEN Y
   RONG H
   HAN J
   SONG W
   HUO B
   WU Y
AE UNIV LIAONING TECHNICAL (ULNT-C)
GA 2020245062
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting a set of parallel                blasting holes in each side of a coal inside a                working face (3) and return air roadway (2) along                the working face. Three empty holes with guiding                effect are provided in the vertical plane between                the blasting hole and the coal wall of the working                face. A blasting is started so that the liquid                carbon dioxide blasting device in each borehole is                blasted at the same time. A damaged coal is fallen                into a scraper conveyor and a front probe beam of a                hydraulic support is moved, so that a newly exposed                coal is supported by a guard board in time. The                scraper conveyor is started to transport coal and                the falling coal is transported to a telescopic                belt conveyor (7) in a transport flat road (1). The                scraper conveyor and hydraulic support are moved in                time and the scraper conveyor is moved to the coal                wall of the new working face after the coal is                transported out. The process is repeated until the                whole working face is finished.
   USE - Carbon dioxide explosion based coal mining                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The coal mining efficiency is improved by more                than 80 percentage and the coal block rate is                increased by more than 20 percentage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                working surface of the carbon dioxide explosion                based coal mining method.
   Transport flat road (1)
   Return air roadway (2)
   Working face (3)
   Telescopic belt conveyor (7)

PN CN110882184-A
TI Sunscreen bubble with carbon dioxide as propellant comprises oil phase component, thickener, pH adjusting agent, preservative, flavor, propellant and water, where oil phase component comprises sunscreen agent and emulsifier.
AU JIN L
   WANG L
   SUN Z
AE SHANGHAI CAL QIDONG DAILY CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202022499V
AB    NOVELTY - Sunscreen bubble with carbon dioxide as a propellant comprises 10.5-38 %mass oil phase components, 0.2-1.5 %mass thickener, 1-5 %mass pH adjusting agent, 0.3-3 %mass preservative, 0.01-0.2 %mass flavor, 0.5-3 %mass propellant and water, where the oil phase component comprises a sunscreen agent and an emulsifier and the propellant is carbon dioxide.
   USE - As sunscreen bubble.
   ADVANTAGE - The sunscreen bubble provides dense and delicate foam, does not provide greasy feeling, is easy to push away on the surface of the skin, convenient for carrying and transportation, belongs to non-flammable products and suitable for large area use, makes the skin fresh and refreshing, has no aspiration risk and improves product safety performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing the sunscreen bubble involving (i) pre-stirring and dispersing the thickener uniformly, (ii) heating the oil phase component at 75-80 degrees C and completely dissolving the oil phase component, (iii) firstly adding the substance obtained in step (i) into an aqueous phase component, heating at 75-80 degrees C, uniformly stirring, adding the substance obtained in step (ii), homogenizing and emulsifying, uniformly stirring, adding the pH adjusting agent, cooling to 40 degrees C, adding the flavor and the preservative and filtering out materials to obtain a stock solution and (iv) filling the stock solution obtained in step (iii) in an aluminum tank, sealing, filling carbon dioxide, carrying out water bath, purging, adding a nozzle and extruding by pressing a pump head to obtain the sunscreen bubble.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sunscreen agent comprises homosalate, 4-methylbenzylidene camphor, octocrylene and diethylhexyl 2,6-naphthalate. The pH adjusting agent comprises alkaline amino acid. The preservative comprises caprylhydroxamic acid, caprylyl glycol and ethylhexylglycerin. The sunscreen bubble further comprises at least one saturated polyol having not greater than 4C. The oil phase component further comprises at least one component of tocopheryl acetate and cyclopentasiloxane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The emulsifier comprises (A) component chosen from glyceryl oleate citrate, caprylic/capric triglyceride or their mixture and (B) component chosen from polyethylene glycol-100 stearate, glyceryl stearate or their mixture. The thickener comprises xanthan gum and an acrylate/alkyl acrylate crosslinked polymer. Preferred Composition: The sunscreen bubble comprises 2-10 %mass homosalate, 2-5 %mass 4-methylbenzylidene camphor, 2-5 %mass octocrylene, 2-5 %mass diethylhexyl 2,6-naphthalate, 2-5 %mass emulsifier component (A), 0.5-2 %mass emulsifier component (B), 0.1-1 %mass xanthan gum, 0.1-0.5 %mass acrylate/10-30C alkyl acrylate crosslinked polymer, 0.1-1 %mass tocopheryl acetate, 2-5 %mass cyclopentasiloxane, 0.1-1 %mass caprylhydroxamic acid, 0.1-1 %mass caprylyl glycol, 0.1-1 %mass ethylhexylglycerin, 1-5 %mass arginine, 1-5 %mass butanediol, 1-5 %mass glycerin, 0.01-0.2 %mass flavor, 0.5-3 %mass carbon dioxide and remaining is water.

PN CN110327958-A; CN110327958-B
TI Catalyst useful for catalyzing hydrogenation            reaction of carbon dioxide to methanol, is prepared by            calcining metal salt at high temperature to form metal            oxide as precursor, and introducing non-metal nitrogen            by nitrogen doping.
AU LI R
   ZHANG L
   YANG B
   DENG W
AE QILIHUA WUHAN RESOURCES & ENVIRONMENT (QILI-Non-standard)
GA 201987723T
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is prepared by calcining a metal                salt at a high temperature to form a metal oxide as                a precursor, and introducing a non-metal nitrogen                by nitrogen doping to prepare a nitrogen-doped                metal oxide catalyst, which has a stoichiometric                composition (I).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing the                hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide to                methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is high in selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst is prepared by calcining a metal                salt at a high temperature to form a metal oxide as                a precursor, and introducing a non-metal nitrogen                by nitrogen doping to prepare a nitrogen-doped                metal oxide catalyst, which has a stoichiometric                composition of formula: MiOjNk(I).
   M=l-valent metal;and
   ix l=2j+k.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a preparation of the catalyst, which involves                (S1) weighing a certain amount of metal salt in a                crucible, then putting the crucible into a muffle                furnace, heating for a period of time, and then                cooling to room temperature to form a metal oxide,                and (S2) placing the metal oxide obtained in the                step (S1) in a tubular furnace, carrying out                nitrogen doping by introducing ammonia gas standard                gas, simultaneously heating the tubular furnace and                nitriding for a certain period of time, naturally                cooling to produce nitrogen-doped metal oxide,                which is used as a catalyst for the hydrogenation                of carbon dioxide to methanol; and
   use of the catalyst for catalyzing the                hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide to                methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The heating rate of                  the muffle furnace in the step (S1) is                  1-5&#176; C/minute. The heating temperature                  of the muffle furnace is 150-450&#176; C, and                  the heating time is 3-5 hours. The last formed                  oxide is one of copper oxide, zinc oxide or                  cerium oxide. The injection rate of the ammonia                  gas standard gas in the step (S2) is 80-400                  ml/minute. The tubular furnace has a heating rate                  of 1-10&#176; C/minute. The nitriding                  temperature is 400-700&#176; C, and the                  nitriding time is 3-5 hours. Preferred                  Components: The metal salt in the step (S1) is                  cerium sulfate, cerium carbonate, cerium oxalate,                  cerium nitrate, copper sulfate, copper carbonate,                  copper nitrate, zinc nitrate and/or zinc                  sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrogenation reaction                  of carbon dioxide to methanol is carried out on a                  pressurized fixed bed continuous flow reactor,                  where the reaction conditions are a pressure of                  1-8 MPa, a temperature of 200-400&#176; C, a                  space velocity of 6000-40000 hour-1and a volume                  ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide of                  2:1-4:1.

TI Three-chamber electrolytic cell device for            electrically reducing carbon dioxide formate, comprises            cathode chamber, first and second anode chamber, where            first anode chamber and second anode chamber are            connected to cathode chamber.
AU ZHANG R

   SHAO L
   WANG W
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2019715628
AB    NOVELTY - Three-chamber electrolytic cell device                comprises cathode chamber (2), first anode chamber                (1) and second anode chamber (3), where first anode                chamber and second anode chamber are respectively                connected to cathode chamber through cation                exchange membrane (8).
   USE - Three-chamber electrolytic cell device for                electrically reducing carbon dioxide formate.
   ADVANTAGE - Three-chamber electrolytic cell device is good                for product separation, solves shortcomings of                carbon dioxide electroreduction of formate, and                reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for performing electrolysis by three-chamber                electrolytic cell device, which involves:
   (A) preparing electrode and assembling                three-chamber electrolytic cell device, and adding                corresponding electrolyte to cathode chamber, first                anode chamber and second anode chamber of                three-chamber electrolytic cell device;
   (B) placing the electrodes in the                corresponding cathode chamber, the first anode                chamber and the second anode chamber, and                connecting the anode electrode and the cathode                electrode through electrolysis device;
   (C) continuously introducing carbon dioxide                gas into the cathode chamber, and then setting a                cell voltage to the electrolysis device for                electrolysis reaction; and
   (D) taking out electrolysis cathode chamber                solution, and collecting distilled solid powder by                vacuum distillation, where solid powder is sodium                formate and collecting gas discharged from first                anode chamber and second anode chamber to obtain                pure oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                three-chamber electrolytic cell device.
   First anode chamber (1)
   Cathode chamber (2)
   Second anode chamber (3)
   First electrode (4)
   Cation exchange membrane (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first anode                  chamber is acidic or neutral electrolyte, the                  second anode chamber is alkaline electrolyte. The                  acidic or neutral electrolyte is potasssium                  sulfate, sodium sulfate, potassium hydrogen                  sulfate, sodium hydrogen sulfate or sulfuric acid                  solution. The alkaline electrolyte is potassium                  hydroxide or sodium hydroxide solution. The                  electrolyte of the cathode chamber is potassium                  hydrogen carbonate or sodiium hydrogen carbonate                  solution. The cathode electrode is tin, bismuth                  or cobalt metal. The anode electrode is iridium,                  ruthenium and titanium, tantalum, titanium or a                  transition metal compound.

AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019357978
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide displacing oil corrosion                inhibitor cleaning agent comprises 17-21 wt.%                benzoate-based thiourea imidazoline, 1-3 wt.%                benzoic acid phosphate-based thiourea imidazoline,                9-13 wt.% isopropyl alcohol, 7-11 wt.% acetic acid,                11-15 wt.% sodium polyepoxysuccinate, 3-7 wt.%                OP-10, 12-16 wt.% quinoline quaternary ammonium                salt, 3-7 wt.% sodium polystyrene sulfonate, 5-9                wt.% ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium,                11-15 wt.% diethylenetriaminepentaacetate sodium                salt, 2-6 wt.% N-dodecylmethylene bis-secondary                ammonium salt and water (balance amount).
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide displacing oil                corrosion inhibitor cleaning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion inhibitor cleaning agent has                good cleaning effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the carbon dioxide displacing                oil corrosion inhibitor cleaning agent, comprising                (i) heating benzoate-based thiourea imidazoline and                benzoic acid phosphate-based thiourea imidazoline                by dissolving uniformly, adding water, mixing                uniformly, adding acetic acid, allowing to stand                for 10-15 minutes, adding sodium                polyepoxysuccinate, stirring for 30-40 minutes,                turning into orange-red transparent solution,                allowing to stand for 12 hours to obtain dark red                transparent solution, and (ii) adding OP-10 into                the dark red transparent solution, allowing to                stand for 10-15 minutes under stirring condition,                adding quinoline quaternary ammonium salt, stirring                and heating to 60-70 degrees C, adding polystyrene                sulfonate, disodium edetate, sodium diethylene                triamine pentaacetate and N-dodecylmethylene                bis-sec-ammonium salt, naturally cooling for 30-40                minutes; and
   (2) use of corrosion inhibitor, comprising                adding 200-300 ppm corrosion inhibitor into                injected water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The corrosion                  inhibitor cleaning agent comprises 18 wt.%                  benzoate-based thiourea imidazoline, 2 wt.%                  benzoic acid phosphate-based thiourea                  imidazoline, 11 wt.% isopropyl alcohol, 8 wt.%                  acetic acid, 13 wt.% sodium polyepoxysuccinate, 6                  wt.% OP-10, 14 wt.% quinoline quaternary ammonium                  salt, 7 wt.% sodium polystyrene sulfonate, 8 wt.%                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium, 12 wt.%                  diethylenetriaminepentaacetate sodium salt, 2                  wt.% N-dodecylmethylene bis-secondary ammonium                  salt and water (balance amount). Preferred                  Method: The benzoic acid phosphate-based thiourea                  imidazoline is prepared by performing medium                  reaction of equimolar amount of thiourea                  imidazoline benzoate and dimethyl phosphite in                  ethanol. The preparation of thiourea imidazoline                  benzoate comprises performing dehydration of                  benzoic acid and tetraethylene pentamine (in a                  ratio of 1:1.2) as raw material using xylene as a                  water-carrying agent at 170 degrees C for 3                  hours, reacting at 220 degrees C for 2 hours,                  reacting at 230 degrees C for 1 hour and reacting                  at 240 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain imidazoline                  benzoate. Preferred Components: The sodium                  polysuccinate has a relative molecular weight of                  400-1500 and solid content of 20-25%. The sodium                  polystyrene sulfonate has a relative molecular                  weight of 50000-100000 and a solid content of                  greater than or equal to 40%.

PT P
PN CN109239770-A
TI Seismic detection method of side-wall in working            face with carbon dioxide gun as seismic source,            involves transmitting collected multiple vibration            signals to computer by collecting host.
AU WANG B
   QIU W
   HUANG L
   LIU S
   ZHOU F
   HAO J
   ZHENG F
   LIU J
   LIANG S
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 201912648Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using carbon dioxide (CO2)                gun (3) as the source of seismic detection on the                working face (5). The CO2 gun emits impact energy                until the coal rock mass of the working face is                cracked to determine the critical pressure value of                the CO2 gun to be excited. Multiple six-component                detectors (1) are connected to the acquisition base                station and the acquisition host through the                connection main line. A low-pressure initiator is                used to cause each CO2 gun to emit an impact energy                along the excitation sub-wave amplitude determined                along the CO2 gun excitation drilling hole to the                recovery working surface. The collection work is                completed after each CO2 gun has been launched at                different depths in the CO2 gun excitation borehole                (4). The collecting host transmits the collected                multiple vibration signals to the computer. The                geological anomaly structure of the side face of                the mining face is predicted.
   USE - Seismic detection method of side-wall in                working face with carbon dioxide gun as seismic                source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs the seismic detection of                the working face side during the process of the                coal case fracturing operation of the CO2 gun, thus                realizing the seismic detection of the working face                side without affecting the construction of the                working face.

PT P
PN WO2018191487-A1; US2020157580-A1; US2020208179-A1; US11180779-B2
TI Biocatalytic conversion system for utilizing co-fermentation of sugar and gaseous substrates, comprise first reactor and second reactor, where first reactor comprise fermentation medium comprising first microorganism.
AU ATIYEH H K
AE UNIV OKLAHOMA STATE (OKLA-C)
   UNIV OKLAHOMA STATE (OKLA-C)
GA 2018812047
AB    NOVELTY - Biocatalytic conversion system comprise first reactor and second reactor, where the first reactor comprise one fermentation medium containing first microorganism. The fermentation medium comprise sugar substrate, where first microorganism comprise one sugar fermenting species that converts sugars into acetone, butanol, ethanol, isopropanol, acetic acid, and butyric acid, and a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen gasses is produced during the fermentation process. The second reactor comprise medium containing second microorganism, where second microorganism comprise a gas fermenting species that converts carbon dioxide and hydrogen gasses into at alcohol and an organic acid. The gas line connects the first reactor to the second reactor for feeding the gaseous substrate produced in the first reactor into the second reactor.
   USE - Biocatalytic conversion system for utilizing co-fermentation of sugar and gaseous substrates.
   ADVANTAGE - The biocatalytic conversion system shows improved yields of alcohol, ketone, or organic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing biocatalytic conversion that utilizes a co-fermentation process for sugar and gaseous substrates, involves contacting one fermentation medium with one first microorganism in a first reactor, where the fermentation medium comprise one sugar substrate, and the first microorganism converts the sugar substrate into acetone, butanol, ethanol, isopropanol, acetic acid, and butyric acid, and where a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and htdrogen gasses is produced during the fermentation process; and feeding the gaseous substrate produced in the first reactor into a second reactor, where second reactor has one medium containing one second microorganism, and second microorganism converts carbon dioxide and hydrogen gasses into alcohol and an organic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The first and second microorganisms comprises one or more species of microorganisms, where species is selected from Clostridium, Butyribacterium, Eubacterium, Moorella, Acetobacterium, Enterobacter, Bacillus, Anaerobaculum, Alkalibaculum, Clostridium acetobutylicum, Bacillus firmus, Anaerobaculum hydrogeniformans, or Clostridium beijerinckii, particularly Clostridium acetobutylicum, which is preserved in American Type Culture Collection (ATCC), under preservation number (ATCC NO: 824). The second microorganism is selected from Clostridium ragsdalei, Clostridium autoethanogenum, Clostridium carboxidivorans, Clostridium ljungdahlii, or Alkalibaculum bacchi, particularly Clostridium ragsdalei Pll strain. The fermentation medium is selected from glucose, fructose, sucrose, xylose, galactose, arabinose, mannose, sugar, starch, cellulose, hemicellulose, other carbohydrates, glucan, xylan, galactan, mannan, cellobiose, or lignocellulosic biomass. The fermentation medium contains a feedstock selected from switchgrass, forage sorghum, redcedar, woody materials, or combination. The first reactor is maintained at a temperature of 20-45 degrees C, and pH is maintained to 7.5, or 4-7. The biocatalytic conversion system produces alcohol, ketone, or organic acid. Preferred Conditions: The ethanol, butanol, hexanol, butyric acid, acetic acid, hexanoic acid, and isopropanol are produced in the reactor containing second microorganism. The feedstock is pretreated and hydrolyzed prior to placement of the feedstock in the at least one fermentation medium. The external carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen gas is fed into the reactor containing one second microorganism.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2018129424-A1
TI Generating mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves introducing flow of dehumidified gaseous source of methane into reaction vessel, and irradiating catalytic material in reaction vessel with microwave energy.
AU KING P E
   LIVNEH B
AE KING P E (KING-Individual)
   LIVNEH B (LIVN-Individual)
GA 201855525C
AB    NOVELTY - A mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen is generated by (a) introducing flow of dehumidified gaseous source of carbon dioxide (230) into reaction vessel (200); (b) introducing flow of dehumidified gaseous source of methane (240) into reaction vessel; (c) irradiating catalytic material (211) in reaction vessel with microwave energy to heat catalytic material and drive endothermic reaction of carbon dioxide and methane, catalyzed by catalytic material, that produces hydrogen and carbon monoxide; and (d) allowing mixture that includes carbon monoxide and hydrogen to flow out of reaction vessel where (e) greater than or equal to 1 portion of heat required to maintain temperature within reaction vessel is supplied by microwave energy irradiating the catalytic material in reaction vessel; and (f) reaction vessel, catalytic material, or irradiating of catalytic material are arranged to suppress electric arcing, electric discharge, and plasma generation within reaction vessel.
   USE - Generation of mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - Portion of heat required to maintain temperature within reaction vessel is supplied by microwave energy irradiating the catalytic material. Electric arcing, electric discharge, and plasma generation are suppressed. Mixture that includes carbon monoxide and hydrogen can flow out of reaction vessel and be introduced into 2nd reaction vessel to undergo catalyzed reactions producing greater than or equal to 1 multiple-carbon reaction products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the microwave-assisted catalyzed conversion of carbon dioxide and methane to carbon monoxide and hydrogen in reaction vessel.
   Reaction vessel (200)
   Passage (210)
   Catalytic material (211)
   Dehumidified gaseous source of carbon dioxide (230)
   Dehumidified gaseous source of methane (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Portion of carbon monoxide and hydrogen are separated from mixture that leaves reaction vessel. Gaseous source of carbon dioxide or gaseous source of methane is dehumidified before introduction into reaction vessel. Less carbon dioxide leaves reaction vessel in mixture than is introduced into reaction vessel. Mixture that leaves reaction vessel is recovered and is reintroduced the recovered carbon dioxide into reaction vessel. Recovered carbon dioxide is dehumidified before reintroduction into reaction vessel. Temperature of reaction vessel is maintained at greater than or equal to 475 degrees C without relying on heat produced by oxidation of methane or other hydrocarbon or carbonaceous fuel. Method further includes interrupting flow of gaseous source of methane into reaction vessel upon observing decrease in rate of carbon dioxide conversion in reaction vessel and restoring flow of gaseous source of methane into reaction vessel upon observing increase in rate of carbon dioxide conversion in reaction vessel after interrupting flow of gaseous source of methane into reaction vessel. Preferred Components: Water content of gaseous source of carbon dioxide and gaseous source of methane is less than or equal to 2 vol.%. Water content of combined gases entering the reaction vessel is less than or equal to 3 vol.%. Gaseous source of carbon dioxide includes non-zero amount of nitrogen of greater than or equal to 80 vol.% nitrogen. Gaseous source of carbon dioxide contains combustion exhaust. Gaseous source of methane contains natural gas. Reaction vessel includes greater than or equal to 1 of quartz, silica, zirconia, cordierite, boron nitride, or alumina. Catalytic material includes magnetite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Multiple-carbon reaction products includes greater than or equal to 1 of greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic hydrocarbons, greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic primary alcohols, greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic aldehydes or ketones, greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic carboxylic acids, greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic esters, or greater than or equal to 1 linear or branched-chain aliphatic acid anhydrides.

TI Processing low-carbon compounding fumigant comprises mixing dextromethrin, fumigant sulfuryl fluoride and carbon dioxide in a pressure-resistant steel cylinder by suitable compatibility technique.
AU JIANG G
   ZHENG J
   LIU Q
   HUANG Q
AE JIANG G (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201847464U
AB    NOVELTY - Processing low-carbon compounding fumigant comprises mixing 0.4-1 wt.% dextromethrin, 10-30 wt.% fumigant sulfuryl fluoride and 65-85 wt.% carbon dioxide in a pressure-resistant steel cylinder by suitable compatibility technique.
   USE - The method is useful for processing low-carbon compounding fumigant.
   ADVANTAGE - The fumigant: is microtoxic; high-efficiency, has synergistic effect, does not contain ozone consumption substances, poisonous, non-explosive, no carcinogen and low greenhouse gas emission and is suitable for ambient temperature -50 degrees C to -55 degrees C and is economical; environmentally friendly; and has low greenhouse gas emission. The method: can uses running water operation for improving the processing efficiency, reduces the overall cost, reduces the labor intensity of the operator, and can meet the rapidly growing demand, is combined with the innovative use of lifts, and possible to post-compound containers that has been stacked in any height or form, or any potentially missing containers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the low-carbon compounding fumigant comprising injecting the quantitative dextromethrin through the valve port of the pressure resistant steel cylinder under normal temperature and normal pressure, then keeping in the open state of the cylinder valve, filling the quantitative sulfuryl fluoride, closing the valve instantaneously, finally filling the carbon dioxide, the machine head instantly opens the carbon dioxide filling valve at the inlet of the pressure-resistant cylinder valve, in an instant, filling head and the pressure-resistant cylinder valve, and both are closing immediately and completing the production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, when using, the one-time total release based on the volume of the treatment space, the product is packaged in a variety of pressure-resistant steel cylinders. In actual processing, according to the volume of the treatment site, the product packaging of the corresponding specifications can be used in combination with one can or multiple cans, which can accurately fill the dosage and ensure the treatment effect. For the widely used 20 ft. container (TEU) (33.4 m3), the 40 ft. large container (67 m3) is designed as one case and one can, i.e. the quantitative full release principle of small box, small tank, large box and large tank.

PN IN201641036896-A; IN374611-B
TI Producing commercially important products from liquid waste, involves producing biopolymer polyhydroxyalkanoate from human urine, recovering, and distilling gaseous ammonia, removing residual organics, and entrapping carbon-dioxide.
AU VASUDEVAN N
   SUMATHI M
AE N V (NVVV-Individual)
   M S (MSSS-Individual)
   VASUDEVAN N (VASU-Individual)
   SUMATHI M (SUMA-Individual)
GA 201836282H
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing commercially important products from a liquid waste stream, involves producing biopolymer polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA) from human urine amended with tapioca extract, recovering nutrients as struvite from bacterium fermented human urine, recovering mineral such as sodium as sodium-bicarbonate from urine, distilling and collecting gaseous ammonia as liquid ammonia, removing residual organics from the urine after sodium recovery by activated carbon column adsorption to produce a mineral rich urine, and entrapping carbon-dioxide in the form of sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - The process is useful for producing commercially important products from a liquid waste, preferably treated human urine, which is used as fertilizer for land application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process removes 80-93% chemical oxygen demand (COD), 98-100% biological oxygen demand (BOD), 96-97% total organic carbon (TOC), 44-60% total Kjeldahl nitrogen (TKN), and 99-100% phosphate from human urine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for producing commercially important products from a liquid waste stream such as human urine amended with extract from an agricultural produce, preferably tapioca, involves producing biopolymer polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA) from human urine amended with tapioca extract by culturing PHA accumulating bacterium, recovering nutrients as struvite from bacterium fermented human urine by dosing seawater, recovering mineral such as sodium as sodium-bicarbonate from human urine after struvite recovery by stripping carbon-dioxide during PHA accumulation process, distilling and collecting gaseous ammonia as liquid ammonia, removing residual organics from the human urine after sodium recovery by activated carbon column adsorption to produce a mineral rich urine as fertilizer, and entrapping carbon-dioxide in the form of sodium bicarbonate during PHA accumulation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Process: The commercially important products are produced from a liquid waste stream such as human urine amended with an extract from agricultural produce, preferably tapioca. The enhanced recovery of nutrient as struvite is achieved by treating human urine amended with tapioca extract by culturing PHA accumulating bacterium. Preferred Components: The biopolymer PHA produced is 0.5-5 g/l preferably not less than 3 g/l. The nutrient recovery as struvite is produced by dosing magnesium in the molar ratio of 1:1:1. The struvite produced is 1-7 g/l preferably not less than 5 g/l. The sodium is recovered by stripping carbon-dioxide from PHA accumulation process. The liquid ammonia is collected by distillation of gaseous ammonia during the process of sodium bicarbonate recovery. The residual organics is removed from human urine after sodium recovery by activated carbon column adsorption. The source of magnesium is seawater for struvite production. The mineral such as sodium is recovered as sodium bicarbonate. The carbon-dioxide is entrapped in the form of sodium-bicarbonate. The sodium bicarbonate produced is 0.2-1 g/l preferably not less than 0.5 g/l. The recovered liquid ammonia is 50-100 ml preferably not less than 50 ml.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN JP6285062-B1; WO2018159038-A1; JP2018144049-A; TW201841706-A
TI Arc welding of molten zinc-plated steel plates for manufacturing welded component involves ejecting mixed gas of argon and carbon dioxide and generating pulse arc at cycle set according to carbon dioxide concentration in gas.
AU HOSOMI K
   NOBUTOKI T
   NAKAKO T
AE NISSHIN STEEL CO LTD (NISI-C)
   NISSHIN STEEL CO LTD (NISI-C)
GA 201816428M
AB    NOVELTY - Arc welding, which welds molten zinc (Zn)-plated steel plates by pulse arc welding process for generating arc by alternately supplying peak current and base electric current, is performed by ejecting mixed gas of argon and carbon dioxide (Ar+CO2) as shielding gas, and generating pulse arc at cycle set according to CO2 concentration in shielding gas, in which CO2 concentration is 5-30 vol.%.
   USE - Arc welding of molten zinc-plated steel plates for manufacturing welded component (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Generation of sputtering and blowholes in arc welding can be suppressed. Welded component with excellent external welding appearance and welding strength can be formed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Arc welding, which welds molten zinc (Zn)-plated steel plates by pulse arc welding process for generating arc by alternately supplying peak current and base electric current, is performed by ejecting mixed gas of argon and carbon dioxide (Ar+CO2) as shielding gas, and generating pulse arc at cycle set according to CO2 concentration in shielding gas, in which CO2 concentration is 5-30 vol.%, and period of pulse is adjusted within range indicated by formula (f=1-- 0.4CCO2+22) (1), where f is pulse period in ms and CCO2 is CO2 concentration in shielding gas in vol.%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing welded component which involves welding (Zn)-plated steel plates by pulse arc welding process, in which plating adhesion amount per one side of molten Zn-based plated steel plate is 15-250 g/m2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Processes: Distance from tip of welding wire to welding target part at contact portion between molten Zn-based plated steel sheets to be welded is set so that welding wire and molten pool generated at contact portion do not cause short circuit, and molten Zn-based plated steel sheets are welded together at length in which arc is not turned off. Arc welding is performed such that blowhole occupation rate (Br) of formula (Br=( Sigma di/L)x 100) (2) is less than or equal to 30%, where di is length of i-th blowhole observed in weld bead and L is length of weld bead, and number of sputter deposits in region of 100 nm in length and 100 nm in width centered on weld bead is less than or equal to 20. Preferred Parameters: Distance from tip of welding wire to welding target part is 2-20 mm. Peak current is 350-650 A. Preferred Compositions: Plated layer of molten Zn-plated steel plate has Zn as main component, and contains 1-22 mass% aluminum (Al). Plated layer of molten Zn-plated steel plate contains 0.05-10 mass% magnesium (Mg). Composition of plated layer of molten Zn-plated steel plate consists of 0.002-0.1 mass% titanium (Ti), 0.001-0.05 mass% boron (B), 0-2 mass% silicon (Si), and 0-2.5 mass% iron (Fe).

PN KR2021154332-A
TI Manufacture of sodium bicarbonate involves using electrolysis device, reacting sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide with carbon dioxide, precipitating sodium bicarbonate, dehydrating, reacting chlorine and hydrogen, converting to hydrochloric acid, cooling, and condensing hydrochloric acid.
AU CHO S S
AE CHO S S (CHOS-Individual)
GA 2021E8399N
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of sodium bicarbonate involves using electrolysis device comprising cathode cell around the cathode, electrolysis unit module, in which anode cell, center cell and cathode cell are symmetrically formed with center cell and anode cell around the anode to produce hydrochloric acid and hypochlorous acid by reacting chlorine in the anode cell and sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide in the cathode cell by electrolysis process, reacting sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide in the anode cell with carbon dioxide to convert to sodium bicarbonate, precipitating sodium bicarbonate in an aqueous solution of carbonate such as sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate, dehydrating, drying, extracting carbon dioxide from sodium bicarbonate, converting into sodium carbonate, reacting chlorine in the anode cell and hydrogen in the anode cell to convert to hydrochloric acid, cooling, and condensing hydrochloric acid.
   USE - Manufacture of sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures reduced operating cost of the equipment and cost of input raw materials by inducing reaction of alkali ions and carbon dioxide directly extracted from seawater, brine and wastewater, and is economical and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode cell is sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution. The central cell comprises seawater and alkaline ionized water. The anode cell comprises aqueous hydrochloric acid solution or aqueous sodium chloride solution. The electrolysis device comprises anion exchange membrane provided between the anode cell and center cell to allow chlorine anions to pass through anode cell, and cation exchange membrane installed between the cathode cell and the center cell to allow sodium cations to pass through cathode cell. Preferred Process: The method involves maintaining pH of sodium bicarbonate and sodium carbonate aqueous solution at 8.5-11 during electrolysis and carbon dioxide reaction, maintaining supply temperature of the exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide at 0-35 degrees C for sodium bicarbonate conversion reaction. The method involves producing hydrochloric acid and sodium hypochlorite in the anode cell, and producing sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide in the cathode cell.

AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a container (100) with                nucleation sites (101) for exposing to a fluid                sample, where a total area of the nucleation sites                is at least 5% of a total surface area of contact                of the fluid sample with the container.
   USE - The system is useful for degassing a fluid                sample and for beverage quality control and food                safety applications, which efficient removal of                carbon dioxide from beverages or aqueous samples,                and concentration and detection of spoilage                organisms and microorganisms in carbonated                beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables rapid and nearly complete                removal of soluble gases from fluid samples,                enhances release of carbon dioxide from sample,                enhances ability to process through membrane                filters by increasing solubility of carbon dioxide,                ensures sterility, allows outflow of gas while                helping to reducing chance of contamination,                ensures carbon dioxide removal, and reduces total                time period required for decarbonation and sample                processing.

   (2) A device.

   Nucleation sites (101)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluid sample                  comprises carbon dioxide, nitrogen, nitrous                  oxide, oxygen or soluble gases or mixtures of                  soluble gases; or a carbonated beverage, beer,                  cider, sparkling wine, champagne, wine cooler,                  alcoholic beverage, juice, lemonade, coffee, soft                  drink, coke, fizzy drink, fizzy juice, cool                  drink, cold drink, lolly water, pop, seltzer,                  soda, soda pop, fountain drink, ginger ale,                  ginger beer, tonic water, mineral water, or                  carbonated beverage. The step of sandblasting is performed                  using aluminum oxide, silicon carbide, crushed                  glass, glass beads, plastic abrasive, pumice,                  steel shot, steel grit, corn cob, walnut shell or                  garnet with a grit of 8-1000.

PT P
PN CN113070048-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent based on paper pulp molding substrate involves crushing waste paper recycled in market into pulp by pulping machine, obtaining spare pulp.
AU ZHU L
   LIANG X
   WANG K
   CHEN X
   ZHENG H
   LIU J
   PAN T
   WEI T
AE SHAANXI ENERGY CHEM IND RES INST (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202185056J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent based on paper pulp molding substrate involves crushing the waste paper recycled in the market into pulp by pulping machine, obtaining the spare pulp, soaking the ion exchange resin particles in sodium carbonate solution, fully stirring for 3 hours, using de-ionized water to wash, repeating the step for 3 times, drying the resin particles at room temperature. The dried resin particle grinding sieve is divided into resin powder. The resin powder is added into the spare pulp and stirred uniformly to obtain the mixture. The nylon net is paved on the pulp molding machine, used the vacuum suction action to absorb the mixture on the pulp forming mould, vacuum dehydrated and formed, and dried. The obtained product is carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorbent taking pulp molding as substrate.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent based on paper pulp molding substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses low-cost pulp as substrate, using nylon net to increase its mechanical property. The adsorbent has low cost, simple preparation process and easy operation, the raw material is easy to obtain, environment-friendly without pollution, and so on.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ion exchange resin is anion exchange resin. Preferred Conditions: The pulp concentration is adjusted to the mass concentration of 0.5-1 wt.% by adding water. The concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 0.5mol/L. The mass ratio of the ion exchange resin and the sodium carbonate solution is 1:5-1:15. The mesh number of the resin powder is 100-500 meshes. The mass ratio of the added resin powder and the raw material paper pulp before adding water is 1:1-1:2. The size of the nylon mesh is 100-500 m.

PN CN110577755-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate by mixing composite            modifier and ionized water, heating calcium hydroxide            slurry, stirring with product, introducing carbon            dioxide, carbonizing, adding carbon dioxide,            carbonizing, precipitating and filtering.
AU HAO J
   HAO Y
   LUAN L
   ZHAO H
AE SHIJIAZHUANG XINXIULIN CHEM CO LTD (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202001461L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium carbonate,                involves (i) configuring aluminate coupling agent,                titanate coupling agent and calcium stearate as                composite modifier, and mixing composite modifier                and ionized water to obtain first product, (ii)                heating calcium hydroxide slurry and stopping                heating when reaches certain temperature, (iii)                adding heated calcium hydroxide solution while                stirring to obtain first product and stirring for                certain time to obtain second product, (iv) adding                second product to reaction kettle and introducing                carbon dioxide gas for carbonization, (v) stopping                reaction when pH value of reaction in (iv) reaches                certain value, where a solid appears, and obtaining                third product, (vi) leaving the third product for a                period of time, adding carbon dioxide again for                secondary carbonization, and (vii) taking out third                product from reaction kettle, precipitating,                filtering precipitated solid and drying filtered                solid to obtain the final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is used to prepare organic                composite calcium carbonate with high whiteness and                low oil absorption value by optimizing the                preparation process conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the                  ratio of ionized water and composite modifier is                  1:(2-5). In step (iv), the amount of carbon                  dioxide used is 0.5-1.5 m3/minute, and the                  concentration is 50 wt.%. Preferred Conditions:                  In step (i), the temperature of ionized water is                  controlled at 30-60 degrees C. In step (ii), the                  heating process is carried out at 2 degrees                  C/minute and 60-80 degrees C. In step (iii), the                  stirring process is carried out at 450 rpm for                  30-60 minutes. In step (iv), the carbonization                  process is carried our for 6-12 hours. In step                  (v), the pH value is controlled at 6-8.

PT P
PN CN110479287-A
TI Monolithic catalyst used for synthesis of dimethyl carbonate from carbon dioxide and methanol, comprises active component copper-nickel nanoparticles, carrier triphenylphosphine porous polymer and honeycomb ceramic matrix.
AU CHEN Y
   YANG Y
   YAN L
   TANG Q
   YE L
   LI B
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2019A0503E
AB    NOVELTY - A monolithic catalyst comprises active component copper-nickel nanoparticles, carrier triphenylphosphine porous polymer and honeycomb ceramic matrix.
   USE - Monolithic catalyst is useful for synthesis of dimethyl carbonate from carbon dioxide and methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The monolithic catalyst provides high yield of more than 8% to dimethyl carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of monolithic catalyst, which involves supporting copper-nickel nanoparticles on triphenylphosphine porous polymer and coating the copper-nickel nanoparticles-supported triphenylphosphine porous polymer on honeycomb ceramic matrix; and
   (2) synthesis of dimethyl carbonate, which involves placing monolithic catalyst in a fixed-bed reactor steel pipe, introducing carbon dioxide, heating at 120-180 degrees C for 5 minutes, transporting liquid methanol to a preheater through a high pressure constant-current pump, gasifying, reacting in the presence of reaction gas including methanol and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 1-3 at 1.5-2.7 MPa, controlling gas flow rate of 2880 gcat-1 hour-1, reacting for 2-6 hours, passing the reaction mixture through a gas chromatography and performing online detection of reactant and product content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of copper and nickel is 3:1-1:3. The usage amount of copper and nickel is 10-25 wt.%. The reducing agent is sodium borohydride and/or hydrazine hydrate. The amount of the reducing agent is 2-5 times the total molar amount of copper and nickel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves: mixing aqueous solution-containing copper ions and nickel ions with organic solution-containing triphenylphosphine porous polymer, adding reducing agent, stirring at room temperature, carrying out reduction reaction for 2-5 hours, drying at 60-100 degrees C under vacuum, grinding into powder, calcining at 200-400 degrees C for 1-3 hours under mixed atmosphere of hydrogen and nitrogen in a volume ratio of 1:9 to obtain shaped powder copper-nickel/triphenylphosphine porous polymer catalyst, grinding, adding glacial acetic acid, silane coupling agent, and deionized water to obtain slurry, immersing the honeycomb ceramic matrix in the slurry, drying and calcining at 100-250 degrees C; Preferred Conditions: The immersing step involves blowing excess slurry with air compressor, controlling quality of the coated copper-nickel/ triphenylphosphine porous polymer to 0.1-0.5 g, drying in an oven under vacuum and baking in a tube furnace at 100-250 degrees C for 3-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Triphenylphosphine porous polymer is made of p-bromostyrene and phosphorus trichloride.

AB    NOVELTY - A flow-through electrolysis cell comprises cathode comprising a hierarchical nanoporous metal, anode comprising a metallic mesh and ion-exchange membrane, where the hierarchical nanoporous metal is a catalytic metal for reduction of a reactant which contacts the hierarchical nanoporous metal.
   USE - Flow-through electrolysis cell for use in frames, reservoirs, and constructed of any suitable material such as polymers, glasses, ceramics, metals, and composite materials, and constructed of polymer such as polyolefins, polyacrylates and/or polycarbonates, and constructed of sealing material such as natural or synthetic rubbers.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow-through electrolysis cell has increased mass-transport, surface area, and improved faradaic efficiency and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for reducing carbon dioxide, which involves contacting carbon dioxide with a cathode housed in a flow-through electrolysis cell, where the cathode comprises hierarchical nanoporous metal, the flow-through electrolysis cell comprises anode and ion-exchange membrane, the anode comprises metallic mesh, dissolving the carbon dioxide in an electrolyte, and contacting carbon dioxide with the cathode comprises flowing the electrolyte through the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hierarchical nanoporous metal comprises copper, platinum, silver, gold, nickel, iron and zinc. The metallic mesh comprises platinum, palladium, carbon and boron-doped carbon/diamond. The reactant is carbon dioxide. The cathode comprises first face and opposite facing second face. The flow-through electrolysis cell further comprises first electrolytic fluid input proximal to first face and first electrolytic fluid output proximal to second face, the cell is configured to convey an electrolyte through the cathode. The electrolyte comprises carbon dioxide, potassium bicarbonate solution, monopotassium phosphate/dipotassium phosphate buffer. The ion-exchange membrane is anion exchange membrane and proton exchange membrane. Preferred Process: The method further involves collecting a reduction product, where the reduction product comprises hydrocarbon, aldehyde, alcohol, ketone, carboxylic acid, ethylene and methane, and applying a pressure gradient across the cathode.

PT P
PN CN110203164-A
TI Automobile heat asphyxia alarming method, involves obtaining current temperature in current vehicle in real-time, and issuing asphyxia warning alarm if current temperature in current vehicle is greater than temperature threshold value.
AU XIAO J
   LUO Y
   LIU J
   WANG L
   WANG C
AE UNIV JIUJIANG (UNJJ-C)
GA 2019785499
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting an infrared sensor in a current vehicle when judging the current vehicle is in a flameout state and a door is in a lock state. A carbon dioxide concentration sensor is set in the current vehicle when the infrared sensor detects a vital sign in the current vehicle. Determination is made to check whether a detecting value of the carbon dioxide concentration sensor satisfies an alarm condition. Current temperature in the current vehicle is obtained in real-time if the detecting value of the carbon dioxide concentration sensor satisfies the alarm condition. Asphyxia warning alarm is issued if the current temperature in the current vehicle is greater than a temperature threshold value.
   USE - Automobile heat asphyxia alarming method.

TI Manufacture of planar titanosilicate molecular sieve for carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves preparing silicon and titanium source-containing solution, preparing and aging gel solution, and adding alcohol to solution.
AU YOON K B
   CHUTHARAT K
   GOMBOVANJIL J
AE UNIV SOGANG RES & BUSINESS DEV FOUND (UYSO-Non-standard)
GA 201968594F
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of planar titanosilicate molecular sieve involves preparing a silicon source-containing solution comprising a silicon source, a source of a alkali metal (I), a base solution, and water, preparing a titanium source-containing solution comprising a titanium source, an organic acid, an acid solution, and water, preparing a gel solution by mixing a titanium source-containing solution with silicon source-containing solution, aging after addition of a source of a alkali metal (II) to the gel solution, and adding alcohol to the aged gel solution and cooling after hydrothermal reaction.
   USE - Manufacture of planar titanosilicate molecular sieve used for carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed), catalyst, and nanomaterial.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of planar titanosilicate molecular sieve with excellent surface area, and provides carbon dioxide adsorbent with excellent adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) planar titanosilicate molecular sieve, which comprises planar titanosilicate crystal of formula: (A,B)5-20Ti1-10Si5-20O20-100xH2O (II); and
   (2) carbon dioxide adsorbent, which comprises planar titanosilicate molecular sieve.
   A,B' = potassium, sodium, cesium, calcium, magnesium, or barium;and
   x = 1-10.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrothermal reaction is performed at 100-300 degrees C. Preferred Components: The titanium source comprises titanium compound of formula (I). The alkali metal (A) and (B) comprises oxide and/or salt. The planar titanosilicate crystal comprises crystal structure of planar ETS-4. The planar titanosilicate crystals have aggregated form along one axial direction. The planar titanosilicate molecular sieve comprises platelet titanosilicate crystal aggregated along one axial direction, and a crystal plane perpendicular to the one axis, and carbon dioxide is adsorbed on the carbon dioxide adsorbent.R1-R4 = H, linear or branched alkyl, alkoxy, aralkyl, or aryl.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of titanium oxide derived from titanium source, and silicon oxide derived from silicon source is 1-10:1:1, ratio of organic acid to acid is 10:1-5:5, ratio of alkali metal (A) to alkali metal (B) is 20:1-15:5, and ratio of alcohol to water is 20:100-500.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is chosen from oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, and tartaric acid.

PN FR3066199-A1
TI Autonomous device for production of thermal and electrical energy and treatment of carbon dioxide from combustion of fossil components, has permselective membrane electrolyser that obtains dihydrogene, dichlore and hydroxide of sodium.
AU AGNELETTI C
AE AGNELETTI C (AGNE-Individual)
GA 2018898885
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a system of cogeneration including a boiler with condensation, an exchanger with coolant, a particle filter, and steam turbines of water and turbines with carbon dioxide to collect all thermal energy and to partially transform it into electrical energy. A circuit of electrolysis is set to convert portion of the electrical energy produced by the cogeneration into chemical energy. A permselective membrane electrolyser of ions for the electrolysis of a sodium sulfate solution, allows to obtain dihydrogene, dioxygene, an aqueous solution of sulphuric acid and sodium hydroxide, either in aqueous solution, or in solid form. The permselective membrane electrolyser of ions for the electrolysis of a sodium chloride solution allows to obtain dihydrogene, dichlore and hydroxide of sodium, either in aqueous solution, or in solid form.
   USE - Autonomous device for production of thermal and electrical energy and treatment of carbon dioxide from combustion of fossil components.
   ADVANTAGE - The techniques of capturing and storing of carbon dioxide, during the combustion of fossil energy, helps in reducing the greenhouse effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the autonomous device.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of sea buckthorn leaf polyphenol                extract by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                comprises crushing sea buckthorn leaf to 20-mesh                powder, adding ethanol solution, soaking overnight                for 10-24 hours, filling in extraction kettle at                sea buckthorn leaf powder to 60% acidic ethanol                aqueous solution mass volume ratio of 1:1, fixing                extraction kettle in heating box, closing all gas                valves, connecting two end interfaces of extraction                kettle, closing heating box door, opening power of                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction                device, setting extraction kettle temperature to 40                degrees C and metering valve temperature to 90                degrees C, opening air inlet switch, adjusting                extraction kettle pressure, keeping for 5 minutes,                opening entrainer pump at entrainer flow speed of                1.5 mL/minute, extracting for 1.5 hours,                concentrating in rotary evaporator at vacuum of                0.09 MPa in water bath at 50 degrees C and vacuum                freeze-drying.

   LI Y

AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201833025W
AB    NOVELTY - Separating acrylic acid/isobutene azeotrope involves reacting acrylic ester reaction liquid in the rectifying tower azeotropic distillation to obtain acrylic acidisobutene azeotrope, spraying polymerization inhibitor solution at tower top of the rectifying tower, adding acrylic acid and azeotrope three-isobutene, adding ionic liquid/supercritical carbon dioxide, crystallizing, filtering to obtain crude isobutylene and crude acrylic acid, and applying rough isobutene pressure for removing carbon dioxide to obtain isobutene of crude acrylic acid, performing distillation.
   USE - Method for separating acrylic acid/isobutene azeotrope.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate acrylic acid/isobutene azeotrope in simple and easy manner which has practical benefit and good application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating acrylic acid/isobutene azeotrope involves reacting acrylic ester reaction liquid in the rectifying tower azeotropic distillation to obtain acrylic acidisobutene azeotrope, spraying polymerization inhibitor solution at tower top of the rectifying tower, adding acrylic acid and azeotrope three-isobutene, adding ionic liquid/supercritical carbon dioxide, crystallizing, filtering to obtain crude isobutylene and crude acrylic acid, and applying rough isobutene pressure for removing carbon dioxide to obtain isobutene of crude acrylic acid, performing distillation to obtain acrylic acid, separating ionic liquid, recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The tower pressure of the rectifying tower is 6-20 kiloPascal, the temperature is 50-96 degrees C, the tower kettle pressure is 7-21 kiloPascal and temperature is 70-110 degrees C. The crystallization temperature is (-20)-5 degrees C, and the crystallization time is 1-24 hours, pressure is 8-20 megaPascal. The top pressure of the distillation is 2-7 kiloPascal, temperature is 45-80 degrees C, tower kettle pressure is 3-8 kiloPascal and temperature is 65-100 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The polymerization inhibitor is selected from nitrogen oxygen free radical polymerization inhibitor, preferably 2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-hydroxypiperidine-1-nitroxide free radical. The concentration of the polymerization inhibitor solution is 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 1-3 wt.%. The solvent of solution is preferably acrylic acid. The polymerization inhibitor is the tert-butyl acrylate reaction solution having dosage of 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.2-0.8 wt.%. The ionic liquid is hydrogen bond donor-choline chloride-based ion liquid which is represented by formula (I). The mole ratio of the ionic liquid and supercritical carbon dioxide is 10:1-1:1. The ionic liquid is acrylic acid and isobutylene azeotrope having dosage of 10-20 wt.%, preferably 15-18 wt.%. The mass ratio of acrylic acid and isobutene is 4:1-100:1, preferably 10:1-40:1. Preferred Process: The acrylic ester reaction solution is prepared by reacting acrylic acid and isobutylene to alkene addition reaction, then through rectifying to remove the tertiary butyl alcohol and diisobutylene. The acrylic acid tert-butyl tert-butyl acrylate tert-butyl acrylate the reaction liquid obtained after, preferably the reaction solution in tert-butanol, diisobutene, acrylic acid tert-butyl ester is less than 100 parts per million, more preferably reacting for three isobutylene solution having amount of 0.05-5 wt.%, preferably 2-4 wt.%, content of acrylic acid is 90-98 wt.%, preferably 94-96 wt.%, and content of tar is 1.5-5 wt.%, preferably 1.6-2 wt.%..Y'=hydrogen bond donor selected from hydroxyl or carboxyl group donor, preferably ethylene glycol, 1,2-propylene glycol, 1,3-butanediol and glycerine, more preferably ethylene glycol and/or 1,2-propylene glycol.

AU MANSOUR R B
   QASEM N
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2019A7409L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent and capturing carbon dioxide involves degassing carbon dioxide adsorbent by heating under vacuum at temperature of 150-400 degrees C, then contacting carbon dioxide-containing stream with carbon dioxide adsorbent to adsorb portion of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing stream. The carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises adsorbent matrix comprises zeolite and/or metal organic framework, and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed with adsorbent matrix. The zeolite is zeolite 13X. The metal organic framework is selected from Mg-MOF-74 and MIL-100(Fe). The weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in carbon dioxide adsorbent is in range of 0.01-5.0 wt.% relative to total weight of carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent and capturing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in carbon dioxide adsorbent is in range of 0.05-1.5 wt.% relative to the total weight of carbon dioxide adsorbent. The CO2-containing stream contains CO2 and gaseous substance selected from nitrogen, oxygen, argon, helium, water vapor, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, and ethane, volumetric ratio of carbon dioxide to one gaseous substance is in a range of 1:50-5:1. Preferred Components: The adsorbent matrix comprises zeolite and metal organic framework. The carbon nanotubes are single-walled carbon nanotubes. The carbon nanotubes are multi-walled carbon nanotubes. The carbon nanotubes are dispersed in adsorbent matrix without forming chemical bonds with adsorbent matrix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
