PT P
PN KR2022014656-A
TI Carbon structure useful as catalyst for electrochemical carbon dioxide conversion, comprises multiple carbon nanowires forming web shape including bonding region bonded to adjacent carbon nanowires.
AU BAE K W
   SU H H
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 2022230800
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon structure comprises multiple carbon nanowires having a predetermined length. The multiple carbon nanowires form a web shape including a bonding region bonded to the adjacent carbon nanowires. At least a portion of the carbon nanowire is doped with nitrogen and sulfur simultaneously.
   USE - The carbon structure is useful as a catalyst for electrochemical carbon dioxide conversion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon structure: induces excellent carbon dioxide adsorption and transfer of charges, thus improving catalytic activity, indicating selectivity to the target material; has high durability; and is economical and suitable for mass-production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) reactor for electrochemical carbon dioxide conversion, comprising a reducing electrode including the carbon structure, an oxidation electrode disposed at a predetermined distance from the reduction electrode, and a reference electrode disposed in the space between the cathode and the anode; and
   (2) manufacturing the carbon structure, comprising mixing a pyrrole monomer and a cationic surfactant to obtain uniform mixed solution, adding persulfate or iron chloride to the mixed solution to obtain polypyrrole by polymerization, adding a sulfur precursor to the polypyrrole and carrying out high temperature thermal decomposition of the polypyrrole.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nanowire has a diameter of 30-80 nm. The doping amount of nitrogen is 5-10 atm.% and sulfur is 1-3 atm.% based on total atomic percentage of the carbon structure. The carbon nanowire includes multiple micropores. The carbon structure has a surface area of 200-600 m2/g and a pore volume of 0.6-1.2 cm3/g. The carbon structure includes functional group of pyridnic nitrogen, pyrrolic nitrogen, graphitic nitrogen, oxidized nitrogen, thiophene-type sulfur and/or oxidized sulfur. The content ratio of thiophene-type sulfur and oxidized sulfur is greater than or equal to 2. One nanowire forming the web forms a bonding region in at least one place with at least one nanowire adjacent to it. The cationic surfactant includes cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, benzalkonium chloride, benzethonium chloride and/or dioctadecyldimethylammonium bromide. The persulfate includes ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate and/or potassium persulfate. The sulfur precursor includes sulfide, sulfur powder and/or sulfonic acid. Preferred Method: The polymerization is carried out at 10-30 degrees C. The high-temperature pyrolysis is carried out at 700-900 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - 0.25 mmol cetrimonium bromide was added to 20 ml ultrapure water and stirred to dissolve. A solution in which 0.03 ml pyrrole monomer was dissolved in 10 ml ultrapure water was added into above solution and stirred to obtain uniform mixed solution. A solution in which 0.5 mmol ammonium sulfate was dissolved was added to 7 ml ultrapure water to undergo polymerization process at 10-30 degrees C. Polypyrrole was mixed with dibenzyl disulfide at 800 degrees C under argon atmosphere. High-temperature pyrolysis was carried out for 1 hour. The amount of dibenzyl disulfide to be mixed was changed to 40 mg, 80 mg, 120 mg and 160 mg to obtain the carbon structure.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.);

PN CN112707746-A; CN112707746-B
TI Preparation of fly ash carbon dioxide capture agent used for treating flue gas, involves e.g. preparing composite micro-ceramic particles by adding raw material containing fly ash, inorganic waste and additive, ball milling, and granulating.
AU SU B
   SU W
AE BEIJING JINXIU NEW TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021515442
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fly ash carbon dioxide capture agent involves (i) preparing a composite micro-ceramic particles by adding a raw material containing 50-65 %mass fly ash, 15-25 %mass inorganic waste, 5-9 %mass pore forming agent, 2-5 %mass carbon forming agent and 8-13 %mass additive machine for dry powder mixing, ball milling and water mixing, granulating and drying, (ii) washing the obtained composite microceramic particles, surface modifying, adding sodium linear alkanebenzene sulfonate and the composite microceramic particles obtained in step (i) to the ethanol coupling agent solution, reacting and drying under predetermined stability, and (iii) modifying the surface-modified composite micro-ceramic particles obtained in step (ii), adding the surface-modified composite micro-ceramic particles to the organic modifier methanol solution, stirring and drying.
   USE - Preparation of fly ash carbon dioxide capture agent used for treating flue gas, and storing and absorbing saline-alkali soil (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective preparation of fly ash carbon dioxide capture agent having excellent performance and storage, and improves saline-alkali soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material preferably comprises 60 %mass fly ash, 20 %mass inorganic waste, 7 %mass pore forming agent, 3 %mass carbon forming agent, and 10 %mass additive. Preferred Components: The inorganic waste is one or more of municipal sludge, construction waste, clay, coal gangue, red mud and slag. The composite pore forming agent includes an active pore forming agent, a passive pore forming agent and an inert pore forming agent. The inert pore forming agent is a material for producing pores by burning out the pore forming agent during the sintering stage. The additives are one or more of bentonite, attapulgite, kaolin, montmorillonite, sodium silicate, and borax. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the active pore forming agent, passive pore forming agent and inert pore forming agent is 3:(2-4):(4.5)-5. Preferred Process: The washing of composite micro-ceramic particles in step (2) involves placing the composite micro-ceramic particles obtained in step (1) into a washing solution of distilled water for 2-3 times the volume of the composite micro-ceramic particles, stirring, washing and removing dust and impurities, replacing the washing liquid twice until the washing liquid is not turbid, drying at 100-120 degrees C for 10 hours and naturally cooling. The surface modification in step (ii) involves pouring 200 g coupling agent into the reaction kettle, adding 1000 mL distilled water, 200 mL absolute ethanol, adding 500 mL deionized water, 50 g linear alkane benzene sulfonate, stirring 10000 g washed micro-ceramic structure particles and heating at 75-85 degrees C to react for 30 minutes, filtering, washing, and drying for 24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methanol solution of the organic modifier is one or more of diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexaamine, N-methyl-1,3-diaminopropane, N,N-dimethylformamide, 2-picolylamine, 3-picolylamine, 4-picolylamine, N'-(pyridine-4-ylmethyl)ethane-1,2-diamine, ethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, tertiary amine N-methylglycolamine, N,N-diethylethanolamine, and N-ethylethanolamine. The carbon forming agent contains three or more long-chain hydrocarbons with hydroxyl groups, and includes one or more of starch, sucrose, glucose, sorbitol, pentaerythritol, urea, thiourea, amino resin, and ammonium phosphate. Preferred Process: The organic modifier methanol solution is prepared by dissolving 0.3 g organic modifier in 2 g methanol and stirring at 40 degrees C for 30 minutes, adding 130 mL 5 mol organic modifier methanol solution to the surface-modified micro-ceramic particles, stirring for 30 minutes, and finally drying at 70 degrees C for 4 hours.

PT P
PN CN110574933-A
TI Extracting soluble dietary fiber from soy sauce residues by coenzyme-ultrasonic technology with supercritical carbon dioxide system comprises e.g. carrying out drying, crushing and desalting pretreatment of soy sauce residue, and extracting.
AU CHANG J
   LIU S
   ZOU Z
   FU Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020001710
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting soluble dietary fiber from soy sauce residues by coenzyme-ultrasonic technology with supercritical carbon dioxide system comprises (i) carrying out drying, crushing and desalting pretreatment of soy sauce residue, (ii) extracting the pretreated soy sauce residue through supercritical carbon dioxide device to extract fat, (iii) adding water to the defatted soy sauce residue, (iv) performing ultrasonic treatment of degreased soy sauce residue solution with ultrasonic cell pulverizer, (v) adjusting the ultrasound to 5.0, and the pH value of the soy sauce residue solution, adding cellulase to the solution to perform enzymolysis at 50 degrees C, adjusting the temperature to 100 degrees C, keeping for 5-10 minutes, and inactivating the enzyme, (vi) filtering the enzymolysis solution, evaporating the liquid part and concentrating for 4 times the volume of absolute ethanol, adding to the concentrated solution for 10 hours, filtering and drying the obtained precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting soluble dietary fiber from soy sauce residues by coenzyme-ultrasonic technology with supercritical carbon dioxide system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, environmentally friendly and has high utilization value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the reaction conditions of the supercritical carbon dioxide device are the reaction temperature of 60 degrees C, with the pressure of 16 MPa, and reaction time for 180 minutes. In step (iii), the added amount of water is that the material-liquid ratio is 1: 30 to 1: 45 g/ml. In the step (iv), the ultrasonic time is 1-9 minutes, and the ultrasonic power is 600W. In step (v), the enzymolysis condition is that the amount of the enzyme added is 3-10% of the mass of the soy sauce residue, and the enzymolysis time is 30 minutes to 4 hours. In step (5), the pH adjusting buffer is specifically 1.0M sodium acetate 14.1ml + 1.0M acetic acid 5.9ml. The method comprises adding the pretreated soy sauce residue to the reaction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide plant using carbon dioxide to replacing the air in the device for three times, turning on the high-pressure pump and heater to increase the reaction temperature to the set value, performing the supercritical carbon dioxide device reaction, the reaction temperature is 60 degrees C, the pressure is 16MPa, the reaction time is 180 minutes, turning off the heater and waiting for the reactor cooling to room temperature, carrying out carbon dioxide recovery, oil and fat collection, taking out the degreased soy sauce residue in the reaction kettle, adding water to the soy sauce residue after degreasing at material-liquid ratio of 1:30-1:45 g/ml, carrying out ultrasonic treatment of soy sauce residue solution with ultrasonic cell pulverizer with the power of 400W, for 1-9 minutes, adding buffer to the solution to adjust the pH of the solution to 5.0, adding cellulase at 50 degrees C for 30 minutes to 4 hours, adding enzyme is 3% to 10% of the mass of the soy sauce residue, adjusting the temperature to 100 degrees C, allowing to stand for 5 minutes to inactivating the enzyme, and carrying out suction-filter to enzymolysis solution to obtain filtrate, rotary evaporating to give concentrated solution, adding 4 times the volume of absolute ethanol to the concentrate for 10 hours, filtering and drying the obtained precipitate to obtain soluble dietary fiber, where the buffer solution is 1.0M sodium acetate 14.1ml + 1.0M acetic acid 5.9ml.

PN CN110104970-A
TI Preparing a carbonated cementitious material using            calcium carbide slag, comprises adding calcium carbide            slag to make a suspended slurry, and adding oxidizing            agent or precipitating agent to suspended slurry to            detoxify and eliminate odor.
AU YIN X
AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201970689H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a carbonated cementitious material                using calcium carbide slag, comprises (i) adding                calcium carbide slag to make a suspended slurry,                and adding oxidizing agent or precipitating agent                to the suspended slurry to detoxify and eliminate                odor, to obtain a non-toxic suspension slurry; (ii)                placing the non-toxic suspension slurry in a                pressure agitation tank, pressing the carbon                dioxide gas captured by the industrial carbon or                pressing into the liquid carbon dioxide,                maintaining calcium carbide under the condition of                maintaining the carbon dioxide pressure, and                converting calcium hydroxide and calcium carbonate                crystal minerals in the slag into water-soluble                calcium bicarbonate, forming a supersaturated                solution of calcium bicarbonate and carbon dioxide,                precipitating impurity compounds of silicon,                aluminum, iron and other heavy metal ions in the                supersaturated solution as precipitates, to obtain                solution mixture of calcium bicarbonate                supersaturated with solid impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonated                cementitious material using calcium carbide                slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, does not                produce secondary pollution, and is                economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of water to calcium carbide slag                  solids in the suspended slurry, that is, the                  water/solid ratio is 20-3:1. In the step (i), the                  water/solid ratio is 15-5:1. In the step (i), the                  oxidizing agent is a substance capable of                  efficiently oxidizing a low molecular weight                  toxic and odorous compound in the calcium carbide                  slag. The substance capable of efficiently                  oxidizing the low molecular weight toxic and                  odorous compound in the calcium carbide slag is                  chlorine dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, sodium                  percarbonate, and ferrate. In the step (i), the                  precipitating agent is a substance capable of                  directly converting sulfur, phosphorus, and                  arsenic ions into a highly insoluble or insoluble                  compound in water. The substance which can                  directly convert sulfur, phosphorus and arsenic                  ions into a highly insoluble or insoluble                  compound in water is copper sulfate and copper                  chloride. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  temperature in the pressure agitation tank is                  0-45 degrees C; and the pressure of the press-in                  is 0.5-10 MPa.

PN CN108103524-A
TI Solid oxide electrolytic cell has electrolyte            layer, and carbon dioxide electrode and oxygen            electrode arranged on sides of electrolyte layer, where            electrolyte layer is made of yttria-stabilized zirconia            and scandium stabilized zirconia.
AU CHENG M
   HUANG Z
   ZHAO Z
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201844966Q
AB    NOVELTY - A solid oxide electrolytic cell comprises an                electrolyte layer, and a carbon dioxide electrode                and oxygen electrode arranged on two sides of the                electrolyte layer. The electrolyte layer is made of                yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) and scandium                stabilized zirconia (ScSZ).
   USE - Solid oxide electrolytic cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid oxide electrolytic cell has                excellent electrolytic carbon dioxide                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide electrode is a composite                electrode made of perovskite oxide of formula:                Al1-yA2yB1O3-d (I) or Al1-yA2yB1O3-d (II) and                doped-cerium oxide of formula: N1xCe1-xO2-c (III).                The oxygen electrode is a composite electrode made                of perovskite oxide of formula: Cl1-zC2zD1O3-a (IV)                or Cl1-zC2zD1O3-a (V) and doped-cerium oxide of                formula: M1kCe1-kO2-b (VI).
   A1,N1,C1,M1 = La, Pr, Sm, Gd, Er, Yb or                Y;
   A2,C2 = Ca, Sr, or Ba;
   B1,D1 = Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, or Cu;
   y,z = 0-0.8;
   d,a = 0-1;
   x,k = 0-0.5;and
   c,b = 0-0.25.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                method for manufacturing the solid oxide                electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Properties: The electrolyte layer                  has a thickness of 100-600 microns. The carbon                  dioxide electrode has a thickness of 10-100                  microns. The oxygen electrode has a thickness of                  10-100 microns. Preferred Composition: The                  content of YSZ is 8-10%. The amount of ScSZ is                  8-10%. The mass fraction of perovskite oxide (I)                  or (II) in the carbon dioxide electrode is                  20-100%. The mass fraction of perovskite oxide                  (IV) or (V) in the oxygen electrode is                  30-100%.

PT P
PN WO2018037322-A1; DE112017004167-T5; CN109689568-A; US2019169027-A1
TI Preparing rod-shaped mesoporous carbon nitride            (MCN) material involves obtaining a template reactant            mixture comprises uncalcined rod-shaped SBA-15            template, carbon source compound, and nitrogen source            compound, and heating reaction mixture.
AU LAKHI K S
   AL-BAHILY K
   RAVON U
   VINU A
   ALBAHILY K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201817642G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rod-shaped mesoporous carbon nitride                (MCN) material involves obtaining a template                reactant mixture comprises uncalcined rod-shaped                SBA-15 template, carbon source compound, and                nitrogen source compound, heating the reaction                mixture at 80-100 degrees C to obtain rod-shaped                template carbon nitride composite, heating the                rod-shaped template carbon nitride composite at                temperature of 500 degrees C to form a rod-shaped                mesoporous carbon nitride material/SBA-15                (MCN-SBA-15) complex, and removing the SBA-15                template from the MCN-SABA-15 complex to obtain                final product.
   USE - Method for preparing rod-shaped mesoporous                carbon nitride (MCN) material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare rod-shaped                mesoporous carbon nitride (MCN) material which                reduces carbon dioxide emissions from large-scale                stationary point sources such as fossil fuel based                power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                subjecting carbon dioxide sequestration process,                which involves contacting the mesoporous carbon                nitride material with a carbon dioxide containing                fluid or gas, and absorbing carbon dioxide, where                mesoporous carbon nitride material is rod shaped                and has a surface area of 650-790 m3g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source                  compound is carbon tetrachloride (CTC). The                  nitrogen source compound is ethylenediamine                  (EDA). Preferred Conditions: The template                  reaction mixture is heated at 90 degrees C. The                  heating is carried out at 600-1100 degrees C,                  preferably 900 degrees C. The heating is                  performed under an inert gas flow. The nitrogen                  flow is carried out at rate of 40-60 mL per                  minute. The uncalcined rod-shaped SBA-15 template                  is prepared at a temperature of 100-150 degrees                  C, preferably 130 degrees C. The uncalcined                  SBA-15 template is removed by contacting the                  mesoporous carbon nitride material/SBA-15 complex                  with a hydrofluoric acid solution. The method for                  preparing rod-shaped mesoporous carbon nitride                  (MCN) material further involves producing a                  uncalcined rod-shaped SBA-15 template by reacting                  a polymerization solution comprises amphiphilic                  triblock copolymer and tetraethyl orthosilicate                  (TEOS) at a predetermined reaction temperature to                  form a SBA-15 template, where predetermined                  reaction temperature determines the pore size of                  the SBA-15 template, extracting the amphiphilic                  triblock copolymer with ethanol at room                  temperature, and drying the SBA-15 template to                  form an uncalcined SBA-15 template. The                  sequestration of carbon dioxide is performed at a                  temperature of 0-30 degrees C at pressure of                  0.1-3 MPa. The mesoporous carbon nitride also has                  a pore diameter of 4.0-4.5 nm, a pore volume of                  0.7-1.5 cm3g1, and a surface nitrogen content of                  2.5-17.0%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107698435-A; CN107698435-B
TI Use of ionic iron complex for preparing propiolic acid by catalyzing carboxylation reaction of alkyne compound and carbon dioxide under normal pressure, and has better catalytic activity and functional group tolerance.
AU SUN H
   ZHU F
   LU B
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2018154473
AB    NOVELTY - Use of ionic iron complex (I) as one-component catalyst is claimed for catalyzing the carboxylation reaction of the alkyne compound and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Ionic iron complex for preparing propiolic acid by catalyzing carboxylation reaction of alkyne compound and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic iron complex enables to prepare in simple and eco-friendly manner, and is suitable of end alkyne substrate that relates to phenylacetylene, substituted phenylacetylene, aryne, heterocyclic aromatic enediyne or fatty alkyne, and has better catalytic activity and functional group tolerance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of ionic iron complex of formula (I) as one-component catalyst is claimed for catalyzing the carboxylation reaction of the alkyne compound and carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing which involves:
   (A) carrying the carboxylation reaction of the alkyne compound;
   (B) cooling the reaction liquid to room temperature, adding the water and diluted hydrochloric acid;
   (C) extracting with ether and washing with saturated sodium chloride; and
   (D) drying through anhydrous sodium sulfate and removing solvent under vacuum to obtain propiolic acid compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyne compound is phenylacetylene, substituted phenylacetylene, aryne, heterocyclic aromatic enediyne or aliphatic alkyne, 4 -methylphenylacetylene, 4-tertbutyl benzene acetylene, 4-ethynylbiphenyl, 4-methoxy-phenylacetylene, 4-bromophenylacetylene, 4-chlorophenylacetylene, 4-fluorophenylacetylene, 2-fluoro Phenylacetylene, 3-fluorophenylacetylene, 4-trifluoromethylphenylacetylene, 2-ethynyl-thiophene, 1,4-diacetylene-benzene, 4-nitrobenzene acetylene, 1-hexyne, 4-ethynylbenzene nitrile or 4-ethynylbenzaldehyde. The terminal acetylene is phenylacetylene, substituted phenylacetylene, aryne, heterocyclic aromatic enediyne or fatty alkyne. Preferred Conditions: The dosage of alkyne compound is 3-6 mol.%, preferably 5 mol.%. The carboxylation reaction of the alkyne compound is carried out with carbon dioxide in the presence of 1.8-2.5 times cesium carbonate at 40-70 degrees C for 12-20 hours. The propiolic acid is obtained by reacting ionic iron complex (I) with alkyne compound and carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN111410616-A
TI Separating capsaicin and capsanthin by            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and molecular            distillation by extracting chili peel powder by            supercritical extraction to obtain capsicum oleoresin            and separating by molecular distillation.
AU HUANG W
   YANG X
   SUN T
AE GUIZHOU TIANYUANTANG BIOMEDICINE HOLDING (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202067914V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating capsaicin and capsanthin                by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and                molecular distillation involves extracting chili                peel powder by supercritical extraction of carbon                dioxide to obtain capsicum oleoresin and separating                capsicum oleoresin by a molecular distillation to                obtain capsaicin and capsanthin.
   USE - The method is useful for separating capsaicin                and capsanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts simple operation and short                extraction time, provides high product yield and                high product purity, saves production cost, does                not use any organic solvents, reduces environmental                pollution during the production process, brings no                new impurities and toxic substances, ensures pure                natural quality of the product and is suitable for                large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  loading the chili peel powder into an extraction                  device, adding carbon dioxide fluid to the device                  for supercritical extraction, extracting the                  capsicum oleoresin and separating and enriching                  the capsicum oleoresin through a molecular                  distillation separation device to obtain                  capsaicin and capsanthin. The chili peel powder                  is prepared by crushing dried pepper fruits with                  water content of less than 15% after removing                  seeds to 60-80 mesh. The method involves adopting                  the extraction device which is two extraction                  kettles and two separation kettles or three                  extraction kettles and two separation kettles,                  where the design pressure of the extraction                  kettle is 50 Mpa, the design pressure of                  separation kettle (I) is 20 Mpa and the design                  pressure of separation kettle (II) is 16 Mpa. The                  method involves extracting at 36-45 Mpa, where                  the extraction temperature is 50-60 degrees C,                  the pressure of the separation vessel (I) is 8-16                  MPa, the temperature is 50-55 degrees C, the                  pressure of the separation vessel (II) is 8-16                  Mpa and the temperature is 40-45 degrees C and                  extraction time 1 hour. The method involves                  performing the molecular distillation separation                  to separate and enrich capsaicin and capsanthin                  by two-stage molecular distillation of capsicum                  oleoresin, where the separation condition                  comprises the molecular distillation first-stage                  sample temperature is 80-90 degrees C, the valve                  temperature is 80-90 degrees C, the distillation                  temperature is 110-130 degrees C, the rotational                  speed is 250-300 rpm, the second-stage                  distillation temperature is 170-190 degrees C,                  the condensing temperature is 40-50 degrees C,                  the vacuum degree is less than or equal to 5 Pa                  and the film scraping speed is 300-350 rpm,                  enriching the light phase to get capsaicin and                  enriching the heavy phase to get capsanthin,                  where the capsaicin content is 30-50% and chili                  red plain color value is 30-80. The method for                  extracting and separating capsaicin and                  capsanthin involves (i) removing seeds from dried                  chili fruits with a water content of less than                  15% and crushing the dried chili fruits into                  60-80 mesh to obtain chili peel powder, (ii)                  loading the chili peel powder into the extraction                  kettle, adding carbon dioxide fluid into the                  extraction kettle, extracting at 36-45 MPa and                  50-60 degrees C, where the pressure of the                  separation kettle (I) is 8-16 MPa, the                  temperature is 50-55 degrees C, the pressure of                  the separation kettle (II) is 8-16 Mpa, the                  temperature of 40-45 degrees C and an extraction                  time is 1 hour, collecting the product in the                  separation kettle to obtain capsicum oleoresin,                  (iii) separating and enriching capsaicin and                  capsanthin with a secondary molecular                  distillation of capsicum oleoresin, where the                  separation conditions comprises the molecular                  distillation first-stage sample temperature of                  80-90 degrees C, the valve temperature of 80-90                  degrees C, the distillation temperature of                  110-130 degrees C, the rotation speed of 250-300                  rpm, the second-stage distillation temperature of                  170-190 degrees C, the condensation temperature                  of 40-50 degrees C, the vacuum degree of less                  than 5Pa and the film scraping speed of 300-350                  rpm, enriching the light phase to get capsaicin                  and enriching the heavy phase to get capsanthin.                  Preferred Components: The molecular distillation                  separation device is one-stage membrane and                  two-stage molecular distillation, where the                  device temperature is lower than 300 degrees C,                  the device vacuum is lower than 6 Pa and the film                  scraping speed is lower than 400 rpm.

PN CN110436540-A
TI Method of removing sulfate from water e.g. drinking water, involves adding barium carbonate to sulfate-containing water, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas, lowering pH of mixture, precipitating and removing sulfate ion.
AU XIAO C
   LUO Y
   YUAN H
AE HUNAN YINA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECH (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019968647
AB    NOVELTY - Method of removing sulfate from sulfate-containing water involves adding barium carbonate to sulfate-containing water, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas into water to lower the pH value of the mixture to less than 6.5, terminating introduction of carbon dioxide gas, blowing air to increase the pH to 7 or more, maintaining the mixed solution, precipitating, and removing sulfate ion from the supernatant.
   USE - Method of removing sulfate from water e.g. drinking water and recycled water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently removes sulfate from drinking water or recycled water at a sulfate removal rate of more than 99%, without introducing other pollutants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of sulfate in water with respect to barium carbonate is 1:1.5. The barium carbonate is in the form of dry powder or suspension formed by mixing barium carbonate in water under pressure of 0.01-0.6 MPa. Optionally, lime is added to increase the pH to 7 or more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: An anionic polyacrylamide is further added to accelerate the precipitation process.

PN CN110004459-A; CN110004459-B
TI Preparing heterojunction photoanode useful for driving carbon dioxide reduction comprises e.g. synthesizing tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheet array, and growing bismuth(III) sulfide nanomaterials on surface of tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheets.
AU LI G
   LI W
   CHEN Z
   XIANG S
   HU X
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2019631947
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heterojunction photoanode comprises firstly synthesizing tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheet array by hydrothermal method, then growing bismuth(III) sulfide nanomaterials on the surface of tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheets by secondary hydrothermal method to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The heterojunction photoanode is useful for driving carbon dioxide reduction. The tungsten(VI) oxide/bismuth(III) sulfide heterojunction photoanode is useful for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction to obtain formic acid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photoanode: exhibits superior photoelectrochemical performance and photoelectrocatalytic ability compared to the original tungsten(VI) oxide photoanode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tungsten(VI) oxide/bismuth(III) sulfide heterojunction photoanode prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) synthesizing vertically grown tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheet array on a conductive glass by hydrothermal method, (ii) using ethylene glycol as organic solvent, adding sulfur source, and helium source, mixing uniformly, and obtaining reaction precursor liquid, placing tungsten(VI) oxide nanosheet array in reaction precursor solution, soaking for 6 hours, then taking out, and annealing at 180 degrees C for 1 hour in nitrogen atmosphere, and (iii) again placing the annealed electrode sheet in reaction precursor liquid, sealing in the reaction kettle, hydrothermally reacting at 100-130 degrees C for 10-14 hours, annealing at 250 degrees C for 1 hour in a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain heterojunction photoanode. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the sulfur source is thiourea, and bismuth source is pentahydrate bismuth nitrate. The mass ratio of sulfur source, bismuth source, and ethylene glycol is 0.25 g:0.45 g:20 ml.

PT P
PN DE102017214043-A1
TI Producing 1,4-butanediol, comprises e.g. reacting acetylene with carbon dioxide in presence of silver or copper salt, inorganic base and solvent, and hydrogenating triple bond in presence of solvent, hydrogenation catalyst and hydrogen.
AU GOOSSEN L J
   RISTO E
   WENDLING T
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 201813396W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing 1,4-butanediol, comprises (a) reacting acetylene with carbon dioxide in the presence of silver or copper salts, inorganic base and solvent, (b) hydrogenating the triple bond in the presence of a solvent, hydrogenation catalyst and hydrogen, (c) performing double esterification in the presence of carbon dioxide and alcohol, and (d) hydrogenating the diester to 1,4-butanediol in the presence of catalyst and base.
   USE - The method is useful for producing 1,4-butanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes recyclable base, and ensures reaction conditions in which the acetylenedicaboxylic acid formed in the process does not decarboxylate again.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver salt includes silver, colloidal silver particles optionally provided with stabilizing additive including phosphine ligand, dimethyl sulfoxide and/or polyvinylpyrrolidone, silver fluoride, silver chloride, silver bromide, silver iodide, silver nitrate, silver tetrafluoroborate , silver trifluoromethanesulfonate, silver carboxylate, silver hexafluorophosphate, silver oxide, silver sulfate, silver hexafluoroantimonate, silver p-toluenesulfonate and silver carbonate. The copper salt includes cuprous fluoride, copper(I) chloride, copper(I) bromide, copper iodide and copper(I) cyanide. The inorganic base includes alkali metal, alkaline earth metal hydroxide, carbonate, bicarbonate, oxide, phosphate, hydrogen phosphate, fluoride and carboxylate. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises reacting copper(I) chloride in the presence of potassium phosphate with acetylene and carbon dioxide under pressure of 1-50 bar and at 50-120 degrees C. The step (b) comprises reacting in the presence of catalyst including platinum, palladium and rhodium on a support material and solvent including tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, water and alcohol under hydrogen pressure of 5-80 bar at 25-35 degrees C. the hydrogenation is carried out in the presence of sodium methoxide and ruthenium catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol is methanol or ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Photocatalyst for converting carbon dioxide to            methane comprises reduced titania nanoparticles and            copper oxide photo-deposited on nanoparticles.
AU IN S I
   SHAHZAD A
   KIM H
   HO L J
   HWANG Y J
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 202285041D
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst comprises reduced titania                nanoparticles and copper oxide photo-deposited on                the nanoparticles.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for converting                carbon dioxide to methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has high stability,                excellent absorption of visible light as well as                ultraviolet light, increases energy conversion                rate, absorbs more sunlight by absorbing not only                ultraviolet but also visible light, enables mass                production and is economical, prevents photo                corrosion, controls electron movement to inhibit                electron-hole recombination and promotes catalytic                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) preparing photocatalyst, comprising                preparing reduced titania nanoparticles and copper                oxide on the nanoparticles and photo-evaporating;                and
   (2) converting carbon dioxide to methane,                comprising using a photocatalyst, reducing titania                nanoparticles and copper oxide (CuO)                photo-deposited on the nanoparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The photocatalyst                  comprises 0.25-0.90 wt.% copper oxide. Preferred                  Components: The photocatalyst comprises Z-scheme                  heterojunction interface between the reduced                  titania nanoparticles and additive copper oxide.                  Preferred Method: The method comprises reducing                  titania nanoparticles after mixing P25 and sodium                  borohydride under an argon atmosphere at 250-450                  degrees C. In the photo-deposition step, mixing                  reduced titania nanoparticles in copper (II)                  sulfate pentahydrate, irradiating xenon lamp and                  drying at 80-100 degrees C under vacuum after the                  irradiating step.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of                reduced titania nanoparticles that do not support                the claims.

PN CN114671404-A
TI Catalyzing hydrogen production from methane            reforming by molybdenum disulfide catalyst under            in-situ induction of hydrogen sulfide, involves            catalyzing molybdenum disulfide under induction of            hydrogen sulfide produced by reforming methane and            carbon dioxide.
AU LIU J
   MEI Y
   LUO Y
   JIN G
   ZENG R
   HE D
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202288471T
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyzing hydrogen production from methane                reforming by molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) catalyst                under the in-situ induction of hydrogen sulfide                (H2S), involves catalyzing molybdenum disulfide at                700-800&#8451; under the induction of hydrogen                sulfide produced by reforming methane and carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for catalyzing hydrogen production from                methane reforming by molybdenum disulfide catalyst                under in-situ induction of hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves conversion rate of methane                and carbon dioxide, and has a good application                prospect. The method is simple and safe to operate,                and there are no dangerous reagents and operation                methods in the preparation process, which is                suitable for industrial production and market                promotion and application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The MoS2 catalyst is a                  mixture of alumina and deionized water, added                  (NH4)6Mo7O24. 4H2O (ammonium heptamolybdate                  tetrahydrate) under ultrasonic stirring, stand                  for aging, drying, and calcining to obtain                  MoO3-Al2O3 catalyst. The MoO3/Al2O3 catalyst is                  placed in a fixed bed reactor, and introduced gas                  containing 10-12 wt.% H2S and vulcanized at                  400-500&#8451; for 4 hours to prepare                  MoS2-Al2O3 catalyst. The loading amount of Mo is                  10-15 wt.%. The alumina is a two-dimensional                  sheet-like alumina prepared by a hydrothermal                  synthesis method, a co-ordinatively unsaturated                  Al3+-rich alumina prepared by an                  evaporation-induced self-assembly method, or                  commercially available alumina. The                  two-dimensional sheet structure of alumina is                  obtained by dissolving aluminium nitrate                  nonahydrate (Al2(NO3)3.9H2O) and urea (CO(NH2)2)                  in deionized water, stirring and mixing, and                  adjusting the pH of the solution to 3-5. The                  solution is placed in an autoclave to dry and                  crystallize, cooled, suction filtered to obtain                  crystals, washed and dried, and roasted to                  obtain. Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of                  aluminium nitrate nonahydrate (Al2(NO3)3.9H2O)                  and urea (CO(NH2)2) is 1-2: 1-2.

PN EP3895783-A1; US2021322920-A1; EP3895783-A4; IN202021016755-A; US11471821-B2
TI Upgrading biogas to bio-compressed natural gas by            synthesizing biomimetic-hybrid solvent system,            evaluating biomimetic hybrid solvent system, and            biologically removing hydrogen sulfide to generate            purified carbon dioxide.
AU SAHOO P C
   KUMAR M
   PURI S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2021C0896S
AB    NOVELTY - A biogas is upgraded to bio-compressed natural                gas (CNG) by synthesizing biomimetic-hybrid solvent                system; evaluating biomimetic hybrid solvent                system; and biologically removing hydrogen sulfide                (H2S) to generate purified carbon dioxide                (CO2).
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading biogas to                bio-compressed natural gas (CNG) by simultaneous                separation of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide                from gaseous composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The biomimetic-hybrid has resistance to an                increase in viscosity during absorption of the                gaseous species and has higher CO2 and H2S loading                capacity as compared to amines and other physical                solvents, in which desorption is carried out at low                temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Gaseous composition                  comprises raw biogas. Amount of colloidal                  nanoparticle for its synthesis is 500-1000 ppm.                  Liquid colloidal nanoparticle is functionalized                  with greater than or equal to 1 aromatic amine                  group and greater than or equal to 1 hydrophobic                  alcohol group. Amount of colloidal nanoparticle                  for functionalization is 50-100 ppm. Hydrophobic                  alcohol group comprises alcohols having chain                  length of 5-14C. Amount of enzyme mimic                  nano-colloid is 100-500 ppm. Concentration of                  tertiary amine is 5-10 wt.% of the total solvent.                  Biological scrubber comprises a biofilm of                  microbes immobilized on a support material.                  Microbes can oxidize H2S, HS-, S, and S2O3.                  Preferred Processes: Biomimetic-hybrid solvent                  system comprises synthesis of a liquid colloidal                  nanoparticle; development of a selective                  biomimetic complex; and addition of greater than                  or equal to 1 tertiary amine solvent.                  Biomimetic-hybrid solvent system comprises                  synthesis of an enzyme mimic nano-colloid with                  greater than or equal to 1 biomimetic complex.                  Biological removal of H2S to generate purified                  CO2 comprises feeding desorbed H2S and CO2 from                  gaseous composition to the bottom of a biological                  scrubber; spraying water from top of the                  biological scrubber and re-circulating to keep                  the media moist; collecting CO2 and traces of                  oxygen (O2) from top of the biological scrubber                  and passing through an electrochemical cell;                  removing H2S from the scrubber; and removing O2                  is removed electrochemically to obtain high pure                  CO2. Preferred Condition: Biological scrubber                  operates at a room temperature and at a pressure                  of 1-1.2 atm.

PN WO2021159202-A1
TI Data center used in carbon capture applications, removes carbon dioxide from atmosphere.
AU DAVIDSON N
AE DAVIDSON N (DAVI-Individual)
GA 2021956996
AB    NOVELTY - A data center (112) removes carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
   USE - Data center used in carbon capture applications.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide can be removed effectively from the atmosphere using data centers waste-heat, and profit can be generated from carbon removal. The captured waste heat can be boosted economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon negative cloud service comprising a cloud operator offering a carbon negative option to a cloud customer, and the cloud operator operating carbon capture apparatus;
   (2) cloud operator offering carbon negative service;
   (3) cloud service offering carbon negative option to its customers; and
   (4) data center operator offering carbon negative service.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of data center with carbon capture.
   Data center (112)
   Air capture plant (114)
   Vehicle (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: Carbon dioxide is removed from the atmosphere by capturing the data centers waste-heat in a liquid form at temperature of less than 100 degrees C, preferably 20-70 degrees C, boosting the captured waste-heat in temperature by directing waste heat to a heat pump, and directing the boosted waste-heat to be used as input energy for carbon capture plant. The data centers waste-heat is captured by air-cooling servers within the data center. The data center comprises a cooling system that captures waste-heat, and carbon capture apparatus. The waste-heat captured by the cooling system is directed into the heat-pump before being directed into the carbon capture apparatus. The cloud operator is a data center operator or cloud service provider, and the cloud customer is a data center customer. The cloud customers services are provided on servers whose waste-heat is used to capture carbon dioxide. The cloud customers services are provided on servers within a first data center, and carbon capture apparatus is operated using waste-heat from a second data center. The carbon capture operation is contracted to a third party. The carbon capture apparatus is operated using virgin energy. The carbon negative option is offered to cloud customer to remove a fixed amount of carbon dioxide per unit of time. The cloud customer is not required by the cloud operator to use any other service offered.

PN CN112795091-A
TI Foamed material used in foamed product comprises thermoplastic polymer and X-type molecular sieve.
AU YU J
   JIANG X
   LI Q
AE SUZHOU SHENSAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202156816R
AB    NOVELTY - A foamed material comprises 70-99 pts. wt. thermoplastic polymer and 1-30 pts. wt. 13 X-type molecular sieve.
   USE - Foamed material used for physical foaming using supercritical carbon dioxide as foaming agent and also used in foamed product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foaming material is doped with a small amount of 13X molecular sieves, which can significantly increase the diffusion rate of supercritical carbon dioxide and the dissolution in the polymer, and at the same time, it is easier to prepare a polymer microporous foaming material with high magnification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation of foamed product, which involves preparing the foaming material to the mixed material with needed shape, then using supercritical carbon dioxide for pressure releasing and foaming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polymer includes at least one of polylactic acid, polypropylene, polyethylene, polyolefin elastomer, polyamide, thermoplastic polyurethane, polyamide elastomer, polyester elastomer, and polyethylene terephthalate. Preferred Composition: The melting point of the thermoplastic polymer is 100-350 degrees C, the molecular weight is 50,000-200,000, and the hardness is 25-85 D. The foamed material also includes 0-0.5 pts. wt. compatibilizer, and the compatibilizer is a maleic anhydride graft of the thermoplastic polymer. Preferred Property: The effective pore size of the 13X molecular sieve is 10A. The mixed material of the desired shape includes beads, plates, sheets or materials with irregular shapes. Preferred Process: The pressure relief foaming specifically refers to putting the mixed material into an autoclave, heating the autoclave to 80-300 degrees C, and then introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, keeping the pressure at 10-30 MPa, and quickly releasing the pressure after 20-150 minutes, and then nucleating and foaming.

PN WO2020157205-A1; NO201900144-A; CA3123742-A1; EP3918113-A1
TI Producing aluminum by producing aluminum chloride from aluminum oxide containing feedstock, recycling carbon monoxide to aluminum chloride production and forming aluminum metal and chlorine by electrolysis of aluminum chloride.
AU ROSENKILDE C
AE NORSK HYDRO AS (NHYD-C)
   NORSK HYDRO AS (NHYD-C)
GA 202074009H
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing aluminum by electrolysis of aluminum chloride, involves producing the aluminum chloride from an aluminum oxide containing feedstock by reaction with carbon monoxide and chlorine, and reducing carbon dioxide formed in the reaction to carbon monoxide, either electrochemically or by use of a non-carbonaceous reductant, recycling the carbon monoxide to the aluminum chloride production and forming aluminum metal and chlorine by the electrolysis of the aluminum chloride, and recycling the chlorine to the aluminum chloride production.
   USE - The process is used for producing aluminum by electrolysis of aluminum chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The process strongly limits carbon dioxide emissions by the internal recycling of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The aluminum oxide feedstock is produced by Bayer process. The carbon dioxide reduction is done by electrolysis of carbon dioxide or by reaction with hydrogen. Preferred Composition: The aluminum oxide feedstock contains more than 95% aluminum oxide. The aluminum oxide feedstock is a naturally occurring mineral such as clay, other aluminosilicates or bauxite. The cathodic product is more than 50% carbon monoxide and the anodic product is more than 50% oxygen.

   DONG J

GA 202033694H
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-constructing oxygen vacancies on surface                of cathode materials involve passing carbon dioxide                gas and ammonia gas together through surface of                cathode material. The cathode material comprises a                lithium-manganese-containing oxide.
   USE - Pre-constructing oxygen vacancies on surface                of cathode materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and economical, and                improves the properties of the cathode                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pre-constructing oxygen vacancies on surface                of cathode materials involve passing carbon dioxide                gas and ammonia gas together through surface of                cathode material. The cathode material comprises a                lithium-manganese-containing oxide of formula:                xLi2MnO3(1-x)LiMO2.
   M = Ni, Co or Mn;and
   x = 0-1 (excluding 0 and 1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably                  involves synthesizing cathode material by sol-gel                  method, putting cathode material in square                  porcelain boat, adding urea in another porcelain                  boat, putting both porcelain boat in tube furnace                  under carbon dioxide atmosphere, calcining at                  200-300 degrees C for 4 hours by heating at a                  rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute, cooling with                  furnace. The urea in the furnace is vaporized at                  200-300 degrees C to provide ammonia gas. The                  urea is placed on the side near the air inlet of                  the tube furnace.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  cathode material to urea is 1:(2.5-3). Preferred                  Component: The ammonia source is urea, ammonium                  bifluoride, and ammonium fluoride.

TI Extraction of cannabidiol by chopping industrial            Cannabis fresh leaves, placing to oxygen-rich            environment, adjusting water content, subjecting to            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, analyzing            cannabidiol of extract, and filtering.
AU ZHANG W
   XIE J
   ZHAN Z
   LI J
AE YUNNAN FENGSHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 201976423H
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of cannabidiol comprises taking                industrial Cannabis fresh leaves, chopping, placing                to oxygen-rich environment having oxygen content of                50-80% for 12-24 hours, adjusting water content to                8-10%, placing to extraction vessel of                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit,                extracting at 50-55 degrees C and at 30-35 MPa for                100-150 minutes at carbon dioxide flow rate of                30-50 kg/hour, subjecting extract to throttling                expansion to analyze cannabidiol, and filtering                through ultrafiltration membrane.
   USE - The method is for extraction of                cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has extraction rate of more than                95% and product purity of more than 28%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxygen-rich                  environment has oxygen content of 60-70%,                  preferably 60%.

TI Preparation of propiolic acid compound comprises adding end alkyne compound with hydrosilane, and performing carbon dioxide domino reaction under catalytic action of Lewis alkali.
AU YU B
   LIU Z
   ZHAO Y
   YANG Z
   ZHANG H
   YANG G
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 201866725U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of propiolic acid compound (II) comprises adding end alkyne compound (I) with hydrosilane, and performing carbon dioxide domino reaction under catalytic action of Lewis alkali.
   USE - Method for preparing propiolic acid compound (II).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has moderate reaction condition, high efficiency, green synthesis and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of propiolic acid compound of formula (II) comprises adding end alkyne compound of formula (I) with hydrosilane, and performing carbon dioxide domino reaction under catalytic action of Lewis alkali.
   R=optionally substituted phenyl, thienyl, pyridyl or alkyl, where substituent is alkyl, alkoxy, halo, nitro, nitrile, ester, hydroxyalkyl, or optionally substituted phenyl; and
   n=1,2 or 3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Lewis alkali is potassium tert-butoxide, potassium fluoride, cesium fluoride and/or potassium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrosilane is alkyl hydrosilane, phenyl hydrosilane and/or methyl hydrosilane. The alkyl hydrosilane is triethylsilane, diethylsilane, triethoxysilane and/or diethoxymethylsilane. The phenyl hydrosilane is phenylsilane, methyl(phenyl)silane and/or dimethyl(phenyl)silane. The methyl hydrosilane has average molecular weight of 200-10000. The mol ratio of compound (I) and Lewis alkali is 1:1.01-1:3. The mol ratio of compound (I) and hydrosilane is 1:1-1:10. Preferred Method: The domino reaction is done at 30-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours using carbon dioxide gas with pressure of 0.1-10 MPa.

GA 2021A65306
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of cyclic carbonate involves reacting polyols with carbon dioxide in the presence of a zinc compound and calcium carbide.
   USE - Manufacture of cyclic carbonate e.g. ethylene carbonate, propylene carbonate, butylene carbonate, glycerol carbonate and butanetriol carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces cyclic carbonate with high yield by removing water as a by-product using easily available calcium carbide, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc compound is at least one of zinc iodide, zinc trifluoromethanesulfonate, and zinc p-toluenesulfonate.    EXAMPLE - 0.5 mmol of glycerol, 2.5 mmol calcium carbide, 0.025 mmol zinc p-toluenesulfonate, 0.075 mmol 1,10-phenanthroline and 3 mL N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone were added to autoclave. The autoclave was filled with carbon dioxide, and the temperature was raised to 180 degrees C. The reaction was carried out for 24 hours to obtain glycerol carbonate with a yield of 92%.

   USE - Method for drying acrylonitrile butadiene styrene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively prevent the valve and pipeline from cracking. The acrylonitrile butadiene styrene has high impact strength and good product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide closed circuit circulation system comprises a gas flow pipe, a fluidized bed dryer, a vibrating bed, circulating fan, carbon dioxide closed circuit drying bed and a carbon dioxide gas source. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of acrylonitrile, 1,3-butadiene, and styrene is 2:6:2. The drying of the closed-circuit drying system is carried out by passing material through the cyclone separator. The material cooled by the cooling tower is returned to the drying closed circuit system for drying. The carbon dioxide concentration in the carbon dioxide carbon closed-circuit drying bed is 99.99%, and the flow rate is 2.6 m3/hour. The moisture content of the material after drying is 1.5. The moisture content of the material after drying is 1.5.

PN CN110066228-A; CN110066228-B
TI Preparing 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate involves carrying out coupling reaction by using 1,5-diaminonaphthalene and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw materials and polyoxometallate as catalyst.
AU YU H
   DAN D
   WU Z
   WEI Y
   HAN S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 201968128K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate involves carrying out coupling reaction by using 1,5-diaminonaphthalene and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw materials and a polyoxometallate as a catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate is required mild reaction conditions, high specific selectivity, eco-friendly recycling, improves cleanliness of industrial reactions, improves process economics, cost-effective and and reduces environmental pressures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is an Anderson-type polyoxometallate centered on iron, aluminum, manganese or copper, or Anderson-type polyoxometallates in which iron, aluminum, manganese or copper modified by a trialkoxy derivative is a central metal. The acid binding agent is pyridine or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is dimethyl sulfoxide or phosphorus oxychloride. The solvent is selected from the group consisting of anhydrous acetonitrile or N,N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is set to 30 degrees C and the reaction time is 12 hours. The molar ratio of the acid binding agent and dehydrating agent is 0.02:2:4:1:2, and the ratio of the solvent and the 1,5-diaminonaphthalene is 3 milliliter/millimole. The polyoxometallate catalyst after the recovery of the organic solvent is recycled. Preferred Process: The 1,5-diaminonaphthalene, a polyoxometallate and a solvent are mixed and added to the reaction vessel, and then benzene silane, an acid binding agent and a dehydrating agent are added, and the mixture is uniformly mixed. The gaseous carbon dioxide is introduced, the reaction temperature is set to 0-30 degrees C, and the reaction is stirred for 6-36 hours, and the product is obtained by separation and purification.

PN CN109351333-A
TI Preparing blue carbon-based adsorbent useful for separating methane/nitrogen comprises oxidizing modified carbon dioxide powder to obtain oxidized blue carbon powder and sequentially subjecting to thermal stripping and activation.
AU YANG Z
   MENG Z
   LIU J
   SONG X
   NING H
   YIN Z
   ZHAO J
   JU X
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 201919274L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing blue carbon-based adsorbent comprises oxidizing modified carbon dioxide powder to obtain oxidized blue carbon powder and sequentially subjecting the oxidized blue carbon powder to thermal stripping and activation.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for separating methane/nitrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has well-developed pore structure, has higher adsorption performance and can absorb and separate methane/nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii) the mass ratio of blue carbon powder to the potassium permanganate is 1: 5-7 and the mixed acid solution composed of sulfuric acid solution having a mass concentration of 95% and phosphoric acid solution having a mass concentration of 85% with a volume ratio of 7-9:1 obtained by formulating. The heating temperature is 50 degrees C, the stirring time is 10-12 hours, the hydrogen peroxide ice water liquid is composed of hydrogen peroxide solution and ice water and the volume concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the hydrogen peroxide solution is 30-40%. In step (iii) the detergent is water, and the volume concentration is 30%, the hydrochloric acid solution and absolute ethanol are required to pass through 40-50 mesh sieve after washing with each of the detergents. Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) sequentially crushing, grinding and passing the blue carbon 45-60 mesh sieve, and then dried to obtain blue carbon powder; (ii) mixing the blue carbon powder with potassium permanganate, then slowly adding the mixed acid solution, heating, stirring, then slowly pouring into the hydrogen peroxide ice water liquid at 0-5 degrees C to obtain mixed solution; (iii) subjecting the mixed solution to solid-liquid separation, subjecting the obtained filtrate to secondary solid-liquid separation again after one centrifugation and washing, centrifuging the obtained filtrate twice to discard the supernatant, then discarding the supernatant, curing with diethyl ether, subjecting to suction filtration and vacuum drying to obtain oxidized blue carbon powder; (iv) placing the oxidized blue carbon powder in porcelain boat and heating in the air for thermal stripping; (v) mixing the oxidized blue carbon powder after the thermal stripping in step (iv) with activator, adding deionized water, stirring, then viscous and not flowing and then being activated in an activation furnace. The blue carbon is prepared by low temperature carbonization of the Jurassic low metamorphic coal in the northwest. In step (i) the drying temperature is 105 degrees C and the time is 2 hours. In step (iii) the first solid-liquid separation and the second solid-liquid separation are through 40-50 mesh sieve, the vacuum drying temperature is 30-40 degrees C, time is 10-12 hours, the time of the first centrifugation is 12 hours, the time of the second centrifugation is 4 hours and the speed of first and second centrifugation was 7000 rotations/minute. In step (iv) the heating temperature is 280-320 degrees C and the holding time is 10-12 minutes. In step (v) the activator is potassium hydroxide, and the potassium hydroxide is separated from the hot stripped in a mass ratio of the oxidized blue carbon powder is 3-6:1 and the activation temperature is 500-800 degrees C, and the time is 30-90 minutes.

PN CN109175347-A; CN109175347-B
TI New gold-iridium nano-alloy useful for preparing catalyst for electro-catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
AU WANG H
   CAO M
   CAO R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 2019094652
AB    NOVELTY - Gold-iridium nano-alloy comprises, is new. The particle size of the gold-iridium nano alloy is 5-10 nm. The molar ratio of gold to iridium in the gold-iridium nano-alloy is 1:3-9:1.
   USE - The alloy is useful for preparing catalyst for electro-catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The alloy has small size, great specific surface area and more active sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a catalyst, comprising the gold-iridium nano-alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal plane of the nano-alloy includes crystal plane and crystal plane. The morphology of the gold-iridium alloy includes gold-iridium bimetallic nanoparticles, gold-iridium bimetallic nano-octahedron, gold-iridium bimetallic nano-tip octahedron, gold-iridium bimetallic nano-cube. The crystal face of the gold-iridium bimetallic nano-particle comprises crystal plane of the gold-iridium alloy. The crystal plane of the gold-iridium bimetallic nano-octahedron includes the crystal plane of the gold-iridium alloy. The crystal plane of the gold-iridium bimetallic nano-intercept octahedron includes a crystal plane and a crystal plane of the gold-iridium alloy. The crystal face of the gold-iridium double metal nano-cube includes the crystal face of the gold-iridium alloy. The metal precursor salt mixture containing iridium, metal precursor salt of gold, and reducing agent. The mixture contains solvent. The solvent includes oleylamine,and/or mixture of oleylamine and/or oleic acid, or mixture of oleylamine and octadecene. The oleylamine used as reducing agent for the reaction. The amount of the reducing agent added is the molar ratio of reducing agent oleylamine to metal precursor salt is greater than 35:1. The metal precursor salt includes a metal precursor salt of iridium and a metal precursor salt of gold. The metal precursor of gold includes tetrachloroauric acid trihydrate and gold acetate. The metal precursor of iridium includes barium chloride trihydrate, hydrated hexachloroantimonic acid, and/or barium 2,4-pentanedione. The inert atmosphere includes nitrogen gas and/or inert gas. The concentration of the metal precursor salt of gold in the mixture is 0.5-10.5 mg/ml. The metal precursor of iridium has concentration of 0.3-8 mg/ml. The mixture further includes surfactant and a surface selection agent. The surfactant includes oleylamine and/or oleic acid. The surface selection solvent includes polyvinylpyrrolidone and/or halogen ion-containing compound. The halogen ion is Br-, I-, of which Br-. The Br- having the formula I as specified, where R1, R2, R3, and R4 are independently from the group consisting of alkyl groups, where R1, R2, R3, R4 are independently from C1-C4 alkyl group. The polyvinylpyrrolidone has molecular weights of 40,000-55,000, and the concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone in the mixture is 1-10 mg/ml. The concentration of halogen ions in the mixture is 2-25 mg/ml. Preferred Method: The gold-iridium nano-alloy is obtained by a co-reduction reaction under an inert atmosphere. The temperature of the co-reduction reaction is 150-250 degrees C, and the reaction time is 30-300 minutes, the temperature of the co-reduction reaction is 200-220 degrees C and the time of the co-reduction reaction is 60-120 minutes. The method comprises (i) mixing and dispersing a raw material of metal precursor salt containing iridium, a metal precursor salt of gold, and oleylamine to obtain mixture, where the concentration of the metal precursor salt of iridium in the mixture is 0.3-8 mg/ml, and the concentration of the metal precursor salt of gold is 0.5-10.5 mg/ml; (ii) heating the mixture to 150-250 degrees C, and reacting at constant temperature for 30-300 minutes; and (iii) cooling the mixture was naturally to room temperature, separating, washing, and drying. In the step (ii, the metal precursor salt containing iridium, a metal precursor salt of gold, a polyvinylpyrrolidone, and a surface selective agent halogen ion-containing compound are added to the oleylamine in proportion, and ultrasonically dispersed to obtain a mixture, the metal precursor salt of gold, and iridium metal precursor salt. The polyvinylpyrrolidone and the surface selection agent have halogen ion-containing compound having mass concentration of 0.5-10.5 mg/ml, 0.3-8 mg/ml, 1-10 mg/ml, and 2-25 mg/ml respectively, the mixture obtained is placed in an oil bath preheated to 150-250 degrees C for 30-300 minutes, after the reaction is completed, the temperature is cooled to room temperature, and the obtained product is centrifuged, washed, and dried in an oven to obtain the gold-iridium nano-alloy.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation, excellent catalyst repeatability, simple product post-processing and low production cost.

PN CN108208144-A
TI Preservation of strawberries, by pre-cooling strawberries in cold storage atmosphere, sterilizing by introducing ozone, packaging in box, filling box with oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, sealing and storing in freezer.
AU LU J
AE SUZHOU SENRUI FRESH KEEPING EQUIP CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018536042
AB    NOVELTY - Preservation of strawberries involves pre-cooling strawberries in cold storage atmosphere for certain period of time, sterilizing strawberries by introducing ozone, packaging strawberries in box, filling box with oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, sealing box and storing strawberries in freezer at 0-4 degrees C.
   USE - Preservation of strawberries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preservation of strawberries with prolonged shelf life and excellent taste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Condition: The pre-cooling step involves placing strawberries in vacuum cooler for 20 minutes to 1 hour. The sterilization step is carried out for 15 seconds. Preferred Method: The method further involves harvesting 8-9-months-matured strawberries and selecting pest-free and disease-free strawberries of uniform size.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of ozone in sterilization step is 2 ppm. The box is filled with 6-12% oxygen, 8-15% carbon dioxide and balance amount of nitrogen.

   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently converts greenhouse gases, has yield of 90.7%, and is economical and environmentally friendly.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 2:1. The mixed gas further comprises hydrogen having relative volume ratio to methane volume of 6:1-2:1. The volume ratio of methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 6:3:2. The volume ratio of methane and hydrogen is 4:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107694555-A
TI Indium gallium mixed oxide catalyst useful in reaction of carbon dioxide with methanol to produce dimethyl carbonate, comprises indium oxide and gallium oxide.
AU CHENG Q
   CAO Y
   CHEN F
   ZHANG J
   WU H
   LI Z
   XIONG Z
   WANG X
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2018155353
AB    NOVELTY - Indium gallium mixed oxide catalyst comprises indium oxide and gallium oxide in a molar ratio of 1-4:6-9.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in reaction of carbon dioxide with methanol to produce dimethyl carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has increased catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing indium gallium mixed oxide catalyst comprising mixing indium oxide precursor and ruthenium oxide precursor to obtain fine powder and then calcining to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between indium and gallium is 2-4:6-8. The molar ratio between indium and cerium is 3:7. The indium oxide precursor is indium hydroxide. The cerium oxide precursor is cerium acetate or ceric ammonium nitrate. Preferred Method: The calcination time is 4-6 hours. The calcination temperature is 500-700 degrees C; the programmed temperature to calcination temperature; and the rate of programmed temperature is 5-10 degrees C/minute. The calcination is carryied out in a muffle furnace.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
