PN CN215103049-U; CN114540081-A
TI Device for preparing nitrogen-free fuel gas comprises oxygen preparation unit, circulating flue gas carbon dioxide recycling unit, blower, oxygen-enriched mixer, non-pure oxygen gasification device, and industrial furnace.
AU WU W
   ZHANG X
   LIU Q
   ZHU H
   ZHANG Y
   YUAN J
   JIANG H
   WANG F
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   SHANGHAI YUANHAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202254996Y
AB    (CN215103049-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for preparing nitrogen-free fuel gas. using oxygen and CO2 in the industrial furnace circulating flue gas to prepare oxygen-enriched as gasification agent for producing nitrogen-free gas, the nitrogen-free gas preparing device comprises: an oxygen preparation unit for preparing oxygen as oxygen source; circulating flue gas CO2 recycling unit, recycling the CO2 in the kiln tail gas as the oxygen-enriched gas distribution; an oxygen-enriched mixer, the oxygen prepared by the oxygen preparation unit and the CO2 recycled by the circulating flue gas CO2 recycling unit are conveyed to the oxygen-enriched mixer in the needed proportion, the oxygen-enriched mixer is mixed to form the oxygen-enriched gas for non-pure oxygen gasification; and a non-pure oxygen gasification device, the oxygen-enriched gas sent from the oxygen-enriched mixer is partially redox reaction with the fossil energy in the non-pure oxygen gasification device; generating the gas for industrial kiln generating heat source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device for preparing nitrogen-free fuel gas involves using oxygen and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the circulating flue gas of the industrial kiln to prepare the oxygen-enriched gas for the gasification of non-pure oxygen as a gasification agent to produce gas. The preparation device of the nitrogen-free fuel gas has oxygen production unit for producing oxygen as an oxygen source; the circulating flue gas carbon dioxide (CO2) recovery unit recovers the carbon dioxide (CO2) in the exhaust gas of the industrial kiln as an oxygen-enriched gas distribution; the oxygen-enriched mixer, the oxygen produced by the oxygen preparation unit and the CO2 recovered by the circulating flue gas CO2 recovery unit are sent to the oxygen-enriched mixer in the required proportions, where mixed to form a rich oxygen-enriched gas for non-pure oxygen gasification and in the non-pure oxygen gasification device, the oxygen-enriched gas sent from the oxygen-enriched mixer undergoes a partial oxidation-reduction reaction with fossil energy in the non-pure oxygen gasification device to generate gas for industrial furnaces to generate heat.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing nitrogen-free fuel gas using oxygen and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the circulating flue gas of an industrial kiln to prepare an oxygen-enriched gas for non-pure oxygen gasification as a gasifying agent to produce fuel gas, the method involves preparing oxygen as an oxygen source; circulating flue gas, recovers the CO2 in the exhaust gas of the industrial kiln as an oxygen-enriched gas distribution; an oxygen-enriched mixing step in which the oxygen produced in the oxygen preparation step and the CO recovered in the recycle flue gas CO recovery step are mixed in a desired ratio to form an oxygen-enriched gas for non-pure oxygen gasification; and in the non-pure oxygen oxidation, the oxygen-enriched gas formed by the oxygen-enriched mixing step undergoes a partial oxidation-reduction reaction with fossil energy to generate gas for industrial furnaces to generate heat sources.
EA    NOVELTY - Device for preparing nitrogen-free fuel gas involves using oxygen and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the circulating flue gas of the industrial kiln to prepare the oxygen-enriched gas for the gasification of non-pure oxygen as a gasification agent to produce gas. The preparation device of the nitrogen-free fuel gas has oxygen production unit for producing oxygen as an oxygen source; the circulating flue gas carbon dioxide (CO2) recovery unit recovers the carbon dioxide (CO2) in the exhaust gas of the industrial kiln as an oxygen-enriched gas distribution; the oxygen-enriched mixer, the oxygen produced by the oxygen preparation unit and the CO2 recovered by the circulating flue gas CO2 recovery unit are sent to the oxygen-enriched mixer in the required proportions, where mixed to form a rich oxygen-enriched gas for non-pure oxygen gasification.
   USE - Device for preparing nitrogen-free gas used in industrial kiln.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-free fuel gas overcomes the technical problem of existing fuel, replaces the nitrogen in the air gasification agent, reduces the generation of the industrial kiln fuel type, which is good for environment protection.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN US2018022617-A1; US10875785-B2
TI Performing Debang process employing any brine solution involves removing oil from brine water, evaporating or adding solid sodium chloride, precipitating salts, and making sodium chloride in brine react with ammonia and carbon dioxide.
AU CORT S L
AE CORT S L (CORT-Individual)
GA 201807278B
AB    NOVELTY - A Debang process employing water produced (Produced Water) (1) from oil or gas wells or water flowing (Flowback Water) back after fracking oil or gas wells is performed by removing oil (3) from Produced or Flowback Water; evaporating or adding solid sodium chloride to Produced or Flowback Water to increase its salinity; adding sodium sulfate (7); adding sodium carbonate (12); adding ammonia and carbon dioxide; and performing Debang process, where sodium chloride in the high-salinity brine reacts with the ammonia and carbon dioxide to yield sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride.
   USE - Performing Debang process employing any brine solution, preferably water produced from oil or gas wells or water flowing back after fracking oil or gas wells, as a source of brine for use in the Debang process.
   ADVANTAGE - The combination of the technologies makes it possible to clean up four significant waste problems encountered in the Mining Industries, which are Produced Water, Acid Mine Drainage discharged from sulfidic waste materials (AMD), coal combustion products (CCP), and coal waste from coal mining (GOB), economically to produce valuable byproducts, such as activated carbon, magnetite, zeolites, divalent carbonates, aluminum metal, ammonium chloride, liquid carbon dioxide, rare earth elements, clean fill, electricity, waste heat, drilling fluid weighting agents, soda ash, and clean water for reuse. Recovering heavy metals greatly reduces the amount of sludge for handling and storage. After the heavy metals are removed, the sulfates can be recovered and used for soil enhancements or industrial products like gypsum. The combustion of GOB uses a waste product that otherwise will require safe disposal, produces electricity, provides a source of carbon dioxide for the treatment of Produced Water, produces waste heat that converts sodium bicarbonate to sodium carbonate used to treat flue gas, and to form divalent carbonates in the pretreatment of Produced Water, which can be removed by precipitation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Debang process employing any brine solution, preferably water produced (Produced Water) (1) from oil or gas wells or water flowing (Flowback Water) back after fracking oil or gas wells, as a source of brine is performed by removing oil (3) from Produced or Flowback Water in an oil/water separator (2); evaporating or adding solid sodium chloride to Produced or Flowback Water to increase its salinity, forming a high-salinity brine; adding sodium sulfate (7) to precipitate sulfate salts from brine in a 1st clarifier (10); adding sodium carbonate (12) to precipitate carbonate salts and metals from brine in a 2nd clarifier (13); adding ammonia and carbon dioxide; and performing Debang process, where sodium chloride in the high-salinity brine reacts with the ammonia and carbon dioxide to yield sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) Solvay process employing water produced from wells for oil or gas, or water flowing back after a fracking operation, as a source of brine which involves removing oil from Produced or Flowback Water in an oil/water separator; evaporating or adding solid sodium chloride to Produced or Flowback Water to increase its salinity, forming a high-salinity brine; adding sodium sulfate to precipitate sulfate salts from brine in a 1st clarifier; adding sodium carbonate to precipitate carbonate salts and metals from brine in a 2nd clarifier; adding ammonia and carbon dioxide; and performing the Solvay process, where sodium chloride in the high-salinity brine reacts with the ammonia, limestone, and carbon dioxide to yield sodium carbonate and calcium chloride; and
   (2) method which combines proven technologies in a unique way to create a total system that makes it possible, where chemical compounds selected from unburned carbon, zeolites, and magnetite removed from fly ash are recovered and modified to produce adsorbents and specifically magnetic adsorbents that can be used to pretreat Produced/Flowback Water before the Debang or Solvay process, as well as for removing other toxic organics from other wastewaters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the SSP process used to pretreat Produced Water (PW) before the conversion process (CP).
   Produced water (1)
   Oil/water separator (2)
   Oil (3)
   Sodium sulfate (7)
   1st clarifier (10)
   Sodium carbonate (12)
   2nd clarifier (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Sodium chloride content of high-salinity brine when reacted is approximately 150000 ppm. Preferred Processes: Products produced from Debang process, such as sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, are used to effectively treat or remove mercury, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, and carbon dioxide from fossil fuel combustion flue gas. Sodium bicarbonate from the Debang process is used to remove divalent elements from brine used in the Debang process. Sodium bicarbonate and sodium sulfate produced from the treatment of fossil fuel combustion flue gas are used to remove divalent cations from Produced Water and to provide carbon dioxide for the Debang process. Chemicals from the group consisting of unburned carbon, zeolites, and magnetite are recovered from fly ash and used specifically to pretreat Produced Water before the Debang process and for removing other toxic organics from other wastewaters. Oil is removed from Produced Water by using a combination of microbubbles and coarse bubbles with a flocculating polymer to float oil out of the Production Water. Microbubbles and coarse bubbles are used in combination with solar energy and waste heat to increase the evaporation rate of Produced and Flowback Water. Either magnetite recovered from coal fly ash or synthetic magnetite produced from iron contained in coal fly ash is used to clean fine coal and fine waste coal in heavy media washers and to produce magnetic adsorbents. Recovered water treatment chemicals from coal fly ash are used to pretreat Produced and Flowback Water with sodium bicarbonate recovered from the removal of carbon dioxide from fossil fuel flue gas.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01; D04-A01F3; D04-B05A; D04-B06; E11-Q01B; E31-N03D; E31-P02A; E32-A03; E33-D; E34-D02; E35-U01; H01-C03; H01-D06A; H01-D08; A11-C07; A12-M01; A12-M02; A12-W11E; A12-W11J
IP B01D-053/60; B01D-053/62; B01D-053/64; C01C-001/16; C01D-007/00; C01F-011/18; C01F-011/46; C01F-013/00; C01G-049/00; C02F-009/00; B01D-001/00; B01D-017/02; C01F-005/24; C02F-001/04; C02F-001/14; C02F-001/24; C02F-001/28; C02F-001/52; C02F-101/10; C02F-101/20; C02F-101/32; C02F-103/10; C02F-103/36

PN WO2021166983-A1; JP2021130846-A
TI Composite production system has compressor that is comprised to have inlet port connected to hydrogen supply lines, so that one of byproduct hydrogen gas and hydrogen gas is compressed, compressed gas is generated, synthetic plant is comprised so that composite is produced by synthesizing hydrogen.
AU IRIE K
   MARUSHIMA S
   TANIOKA T
   FUJITA K
   ANDO Y
   KAMO T
   SATO R
   TSUTSUMI A
AE MITSUBISHI POWER LTD (MIBI-C)
   MITSUBISHI POWER LTD (MIBI-C)
GA 2021990345
AB    NOVELTY - The production system (10) has a first hydrogen supply line (11) for supplying byproduct hydrogen gas, and a second hydrogen supply line (12) for supplying hydrogen gas generated using renewable energy. A compressor (13,14) is comprised so as to have an inlet port connected to the first hydrogen supply line and the second hydrogen supply line. One of the byproduct hydrogen gas and the hydrogen gas is compressed and the compressed gas is generated. The synthetic plant is comprised so that a composite is produced by synthesizing the hydrogen contained in the compressed gas with a carbon dioxide. A first valve adjusts the quantity of the byproduct hydrogen gas that is located in the first hydrogen supply line, and is supplied to the compressor. A second valve adjusts the quantity of the hydrogen gas that is located in the second hydrogen supply line, and is supplied to the compressor. A control apparatus (30,30A) is comprised so that the first valve and second valve are controlled.
   USE - Composite production system for producing composite of carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The stable supply of hydrogen and simplification of the installation in the composite production system is attained. The byproduct hydrogen gas discharged from the carbon dioxide recovery apparatus is utilized unwastefully. The operation rate of the carbon dioxide recovery apparatus is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control apparatus is a first supply mode which supplies the byproduct hydrogen gas to the compressor from the first hydrogen supply line. The opening and closing of the first valve and the second valve is controlled to select from the second hydrogen supply line. The second supply modes supply the hydrogen gas. In the second hydrogen supply line, a storage apparatus is located in an upstream side from the second valve. The storage apparatus for storing the hydrogen gas is provided. The control apparatus is comprised so that the first valve and the second valve are controlled based on the quantity to be stored of the hydrogen gas of the storage apparatus. The purifier is comprised so that the hydrogen component of the compressed gas is purified and refined gas is supplied to the synthetic plant. A first discharge line induces the compressed gas to the synthetic plant through the purifier from the compressor. A second discharge line bypasses the purifier from the compressor and induces the compressed gas to the synthetic plant. A merging unit merges the first hydrogen supply line and second hydrogen supply line to connect to the compressor. A branch unit is connected to the compressor to branch to the first discharge line and second discharge line. A first valve is located in the first hydrogen supply line and opened and closed. A second valve is located in the second hydrogen supply line, and is opened and closed. A third valve is located in the first discharge line, and opened and closed. A fourth valve is located in the second discharge line, and opened and closed. A first supply mode supplies the byproduct hydrogen gas to the compressor from the first hydrogen supply line. The control apparatus is comprised so that opening and closing of the first valve and second valve is controlled to select from the second hydrogen supply line as the compressor. In the first supply mode, the third valve controls opening and closing of the third valve and the fourth valve so that the fourth valve is in a closing state by an open state, and the control apparatus is second supply mode. The third valve controls opening and closing of the third valve and fourth valve so that the fourth valve is in an open state. A carbon dioxide recovery apparatus recovers the carbon dioxides from carbon-dioxide containing gas. Power generation equipment is comprised so as to produce electric power using the byproduct hydrogen gas. A byproduct hydrogen supply line supplies the byproduct hydrogen gas after the carbon dioxide recovery apparatus recovers the carbon dioxides from the carbon-dioxide containing gas to the power generation equipment. The first hydrogen supply line is connected to the byproduct hydrogen supply line.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit block diagram of the composite production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Composite production system (10)
   First and second hydrogen supply lines (11,12)
   Compressors (13,14)
   Carbon-dioxide supply line (15)
   Control apparatuses (30,30A)

PN BE1027514-B1
TI Water mineralization composition for domestic mineralizing and carbonating water, comprises minerals and enzymes catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form carbonic acid and then bicarbonate.
AU TOB F
AE FLAMINGO HOLDING SA (FLAM-Non-standard)
GA 202141654U
AB    NOVELTY - Water mineralization composition comprises minerals and enzymes catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form carbonic acid and then bicarbonate.
   USE - Water mineralization composition for domestic mineralizing and carbonating water (all claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Water mineralization composition comprises 0.01-30 wt.% magnesium and 0.01-40 wt.% calcium, at least one of salts consisting of magnesium sulfate, magnesium bicarbonate, magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate, calcium bicarbonate, sodium chloride, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate calcium and magnesium carbonate, and 0.000,000 01 wt.% enzymes catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form carbonic acid and then bicarbonate. The composition comprises substances which release carbon dioxide on contact with water. Preferred Conditions: The composition is in compacted solid powder form, and in concentrated liquid form.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); Q31 (Packaging processes and equipment (B65B, C)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))

PN CN109336113-A
TI Producing electronic grade ultrapure carbon dioxide comprises e.g. inputting pressurized gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon dioxide desulfurization tank, preheating desulfurized carbon dioxide gas, heating and entering catalytic converter.
AU ZHOU D
   YU J
   CHEN Z
AE SHANGHAI QIYUAN AIR SEPARATION TECHN DEV (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019177644
AB    NOVELTY - Producing electronic grade ultrapure carbon dioxide comprises e.g. inputting pressurized gaseous carbon dioxide into a carbon dioxide desulfurization tank, outputting desulfurized carbon dioxide gas by a carbon dioxide desulfurization tank, preheating the desulfurized carbon dioxide gas into the regenerator, heating to 300-500 degrees C in the heater, entering the catalytic converter, and the catalytic converter outputting carbon dioxide gas after dehydrogenatio, where the dehydrocarbonated carbon dioxide gas first enters the regenerator to recover heat, then enters the water cooler, and enters the regenerative dryer, and the regenerative dryer outputs the dehydrated carbon dioxide gas, the dehydrated carbon dioxide gas enters the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler and the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler outputs the supercooled raw material carbon dioxide liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing electronic grade ultrapure carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing electronic grade ultrapure carbon dioxide comprises inputting pressurized gaseous carbon dioxide into a carbon dioxide desulfurization tank, outputting desulfurized carbon dioxide gas by a carbon dioxide desulfurization tank, preheating the desulfurized carbon dioxide gas into the regenerator, heating to 300-500 degrees C in the heater, entering the catalytic converter, and the catalytic converter outputting carbon dioxide gas after dehydrogenatio, where the dehydrocarbonated carbon dioxide gas first enters the regenerator to recover heat, then enters the water cooler, and enters the regenerative dryer, and the regenerative dryer outputs the dehydrated carbon dioxide gas, the dehydrated carbon dioxide gas enters the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler and the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler outputs the supercooled raw material carbon dioxide liquid, the supercooled raw material carbon dioxide liquid is throttled as a raw material carbon dioxide liquid, the raw material carbon dioxide liquid enters the top of the first stage stripper as a first stage stripper reflux liquid, the top of the first stage stripper outputs crude carbon dioxide containing non-condensable gas, and the crude carbon dioxide containing non-condensable gas first enters the first stage stripper condenser and enters the top separator, the top of the overhead separator outputs a crude carbon dioxide gas containing non-condensable gas, the bottom of the overhead separator outputs a crude carbon dioxide liquid and the crude carbon dioxide liquid is returned to the first stage and the top of the stripper, the tower of the first-stage stripper outputs liquid crude CO2 and is split into two parts: the first part of the liquid crude CO2 enters the C1 bottom reboiler and is evaporated into a gas, returns to the first-stage stripper within (C1), the second part of the liquid crude CO2 enters the middle of the second stage stripper, the top of the second stage stripper outputs gas CO2, which first enters the second stage stripper condenser (RU3) and enters the top separator (G2), the top of the overhead separator outputs gaseous carbon dioxide containing non-condensable gas, and the bottom of the overhead separator outputs liquid ultra-pure carbon dioxide, where a portion of the liquid phase ultrapure carbon dioxide is refluxed to the top of the second stage stripper, another part of the liquid phase ultra-pure carbon dioxide is subcooled by the product carbon dioxide subcooler and input into an electronic grade ultra-pure carbon dioxide liquid storage tank, which is an electronic grade ultra-pure carbon dioxide product, the second-stage stripper (C2) is provided with a C2 bottom reboiler (EH3), and the tower of the second-stage stripper (C2) outputs liquid CO2 containing high-boiling impurities. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electronic grade ultrapure carbon dioxide product produced by the above method, where the purity of carbon dioxide in the electronic grade ultra-pure carbon dioxide product is greater than or equal to 99.9995%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrated carbon dioxide gas is split into two streams, one of which is a carbon dioxide gas for regeneration flowing to the regenerative dryer. The other of these is the carbon dioxide gas to be cooled to the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler. The carbon dioxide gas for regeneration is heated by the heater and flows into the regenerative dryer. The carbon dioxide gas to be supercooled is precooled into the C1 bottom reboiler and then flows into the raw material carbon dioxide subcooler (RU1). The non-condensable crude carbon dioxide gas enters a carbon dioxide recovery storage tank and the liquid CO2 containing high boiling point impurities also enter the carbon dioxide recovery storage tank. A liquefier (RU5) disposed in the carbon dioxide recovery storage tank for cooling the liquefied recovered carbon dioxide. The top of the carbon dioxide recovery storage tank outputs a non-condensable gas, and the bottom of the carbon dioxide recovery storage tank outputs the recovered carbon dioxide liquid. The regenerative dryer is two parallel molecular sieve adsorbers (MS1, MS2). The C1 bottom reboiler (E2) and the C2 bottom reboiler are each independently an electric heater or a gas heater. The hydrogen sulfide and sulfur dioxide in the desulfurized carbon dioxide gas are respectively less than 0.5 mg/NM3. The hydrocarbon in the decarbonated carbon dioxide gas is less than 0.5 ppm. Preferred Method: The method further comprises extracting raw material carbon dioxide from the raw material carbon dioxide storage tank, pressurizing the liquid pressure pump, and entering the raw material carbon dioxide vaporizer to generate the pressurized gaseous carbon dioxide.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN WO2020008169-A1; US2021138391-A1; EP3817839-A1
TI Method for regenerating carbon dioxide lean solvent from carbon dioxide rich solvent, involves providing carbon dioxide rich solvent, and applying microwaves to carbon dioxide rich solvent.
AU BUMB P
   HALL J
   NEELIESETTY G
   HALL J J
AE CARBON CLEAN SOLUTIONS LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON CLEAN SOLUTIONS LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON CLEAN SOLUTIONS LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202004533T
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide lean solvent regenerating method involves providing a carbon dioxide rich solvent, and applying microwaves to the carbon dioxide rich solvent to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for regenerating carbon dioxide lean solvent from carbon dioxide rich solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables regenerating carbon dioxide lean solvent in efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide lean solvent regenerate system, comprising a microwave regeneration unit
   (2) a carbon dioxide capturing system, comprising carbon dioxide lean solvent regenerate system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide rich solvent is intensified solvent. The carbon dioxide capturing system further comprises absorber for contacting flue gas with carbon dioxide lean solvent to form carbon dioxide rich solvent, a stripper in fluid connection with the absorber for regenerating carbon dioxide rich solvent from carbon dioxide lean solvent. The absorber is absorber packed column or rotary packed bed absorber. The stripper is stripper packed column or rotary packed bed stripper.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The intensified solvent comprises tertiary amine such as N-methyl-diethanolamine and/or 2-(diethylamino)ethanol, and/or sterically hindered amine such as 2-amino-2-ethyl-1,3-propanediol, 2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol and/or 2-amino-2- methyl-1-propanol, and/or a polyamine such as 2-piperazine-1-ethylamine and/or 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine, and/or, a carbonate buffer such as potassium carbonate, and/or water such as deionised water, and alkanolamine, reactive amine and carbonate buffer.

PN US11476630-B1
TI Thin film Brewster coupling device for coincident            path coupling and decoupling of polarized pump beam in            laser beam gain medium cavity, comprises optical media            substrate comprising front end and rear end.
AU CAMPBELL R N
AE CAMPBELL R N (CAMP-Individual)
GA 2022D1582X
AB    NOVELTY - Thin film Brewster coupling device (100)                comprises an optical media substrate comprising a                front surface (111), and dielectric stack(s)                optically coupled to the substrate front surface.                The stack comprises an over layer comprising an                optical thickness of a quarter wavelength of a                polarized pump beam. The over layer exhibits an                index of refraction of at least 2 within 2-11.5                &#181;m wavelength range, exhibits an                extinction coefficient near 0 within the wavelength                range and exhibits a significant band gap energy,                and is chemically inert to a gain medium gas. The                dielectric layer of the dielectric stack comprises                one of magnesium oxide, lead(II) fluoride, barium                fluoride and strontium fluoride.
   USE - The device is useful for coincident coupling                and decoupling of distinct electromagnetic                wavelengths e.g. materials processing, biomedical                applications, extreme power light and related                physics.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: improves scalability,                practicality, performance, reliability, form                factor, simple mass and costs, initial and                operating of carbon dioxide lasers; facilitates                maximization of axial uniformity of the pumped                carbon dioxide gain medium specifically along the                laser beam path while enabling or facilitating                engineering of optimized pump coupling to the                optically pumped molecular laser (OPML) gas medium;                performs robustly in high pressure, high energy                carbon dioxide gain medium cavity designs, absent                the necessity of employment of relatively thick                pressure containment windows with their concomitant                bulk loss conveyance, and simple structural                difficulty of incorporation and mass; and provides                enhanced gain cavity gas lifetimes and thus reduced                consumables use, improved gain medium volumetric                extractions, enhanced system wall-plug efficiency,                generally resulting in significantly more compact                carbon dioxide laser system footprints and                attributably than that of the associated                periphery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                demonstrating utility.
   100Thin film Brewster coupling device
   111Front surface
   200Gas laser tube
   201Gas gain medium
   210Laser beam

PN CN114992926-A
TI Method for controlling transcritical carbon            dioxide air conditioning system, involves obtaining air            flow at outlet of second heat exchange channel of            indoor heat exchanger, inputting parameters into            pre-designed model predictive controller, and            outputting control quantity by predictive            controller.
AU YIN X
   REN J
   SONG Y
   ZHANG T
   CAO F
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022B9284R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining air flow at an                outlet of a second heat exchange channel of an                indoor heat exchanger. Air temperature of an inlet                of the second heat exchanging channel of the indoor                heat exchange is obtained as indoor temperature.                The air temperature of the outlet of the first heat                exchange of the middle heat exchange as the air                supply temperature is obtained. A fresh air ratio,                solar radiation amount, outdoor air flow rate,                compressor power consumption, compressor speed and                compressor exhaust pressure are obtained. The model                prediction controller is used for predicting                performance change trend of a transcritical carbon                dioxide air conditioning system and indoor                temperature change trend by adjusting a control                parameter. The control parameter is provided with a                compressor speed set value, first fan air quantity                set value and a compressor air pressure set value.                A control quantity is output by predictive                controller.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling a                transcritical carbon dioxide air conditioning                system i.e. railway, high-speed rail and rail                traffic condition of the air-conditioning                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables considering the influence                of the compressor exhaust pressure in the CO2                transcritical air conditioning system to the system                performance, using the neural network for                predicting the system environment temperature,                blowing temperature, exhaust pressure to predict                the performance of the transcritical CO2 system, so                that the system can accurately determine the                optimal exhaust pressure of the system, thus                improving the energy efficiency and comfort level                of the passenger. The method ensures the minimum                total power consumption of energy saving target,                and provides attention to the passenger of the                comfort level.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                control system for transcritical CO air                conditioning system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for controlling transcritical carbon                dioxide air conditioning system (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN WO2022178258-A1; US2022267208-A1
TI Making supplementary cementitious material used in            cement kilns, comprises forming slurry of water and            carbonatable material powder and flowing gas having            carbon dioxide into slurry while maintaining slurry at            specified temperature and time.
AU ATAKAN V
   SAHU S
   DAVIDSON M J
   PELHAM-WEBB A W
   TAS A C
   RAVIKUMAR D
   JAIN J A
AE SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
   SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
GA 2022A8596T
AB    NOVELTY - Making (m1) a supplementary cementitious                material comprises: forming a slurry comprising                water and a carbonatable material powder, where a                weight ratio of water to the carbonatable material                powder in the slurry is at least 1; and flowing a                gas comprising carbon dioxide into the slurry for                0.5-24 hours while maintaining the slurry at a                temperature of 1-99&#176; C to form a                carbonated slurry comprising calcium carbonate and                amorphous silica. The mixture comprises 1-99 wt.%                of the material, based on the total weight of                solids in the mixture. The hydraulic cement                comprises ordinary Portland cement, calcium                sulfoaluminate cement, belitic cement, or other                calcium based hydraulic material, and further                comprises aggregate. The reaction of amorphous                silica in the supplementary material with the                hydraulic composition comprises reacting calcium                hydroxide with the amorphosilica.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonated                supplementary cementitious material (claimed) which                is useful in cement kilns to offset the consumption                of carbon-intensive fossil fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides improved cement                production that reduces carbon dioxide emissions,                thus reducing the global effect of climate change;                reduces the clinker factor of conventional                hydraulic cements and concretes, and incorporates                carbon capture into the production of the                conventional hydraulic cement or concrete material,                thus providing a doubly positive environmental                benefit; provides a novel approach to pre-carbonate                a carbonatable clinker, preferably a low carbon                dioxide emission clinker before adding it to a                hydraulic cement as a supplementary cementitious                material; and improves energy performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                forming (m2) cement or concrete comprising forming                a supplementary cementitious material, combining                the material with a hydraulic cement composition to                form a mixture, and reacting the mixture with water                to form the cement or concrete.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of of a                system for producing a carbonated supplementary                cementitious material.

   54Mixer

   62Baffles

The gas comprises carbon dioxide i.e.                  obtained from a flue gas.

AB    NOVELTY - A hydrosol comprises silica particles having                an average particle diameter of 1-100 nm measured                by dynamic light scattering, and in which surfaces                are at least partially covered by a silane compound                containing hydrolyzable groups, which are dispersed                as a dispersoid in an aqueous solvent as a                dispersion medium having a pH of 1.0-6.0 inclusive.                The hydrosol has improved stability of a foam or                emulsion in a mixture comprising a carbon dioxide,                water and oil in a carbon dioxide foam flooding for                enhanced oil recovery.
   USE - Hydrosol used for recovering crude oil from                underground hydrocarbon-containing layer                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrosol has increased the stability of                foam or emulsion in mixture, high temperature of                30-120 degrees C, high pressure of 70-400 atm, salt                concentration of 10000-230000 ppm, and can form and                maintain a fine carbon dioxide foam long                term-stable which is excellent in long term                stability and salt tolerance, and improved the                sweeping efficiency of the crude oil in the rock                and can recover the crude oil with a high recovery                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) recovery of crude oil from underground                hydrocarbon-containing layer, which involves (A)                press-fitting the aqueous sol, water, and carbon                dioxide into the underground layer, respectively or                at the same time, and (B) recovering crude oil from                a production well excavated in an underground layer                to the ground; and
   (2) production of hydrosol, which involves                coating the silica particles containing surface                with at least portion of the silane compound                containing the hydrolyzing group as a dispersoid,                mixing the aqueous sol of unmodified colloidal                silica and the silane compound containing a                hydrolyzing group at a mass ratio of the silane                compound and the silica particles in the aqueous                sol is 0.01-2:1.00, and treating at pH of 1-6 for                0.1-20 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The aqueous sol has a                  pH of 6 or less and does not contain an                  isoelectric point. The difference between the                  average particle size value measured by the                  dynamic-light-scattering method of this hydrosol                  and the value of the average particle diameter                  before the test is 200 nm or less, after the test                  the hydrosol is stored at 80 degrees C for 30                  days at concentration of 1.0 %mass silica in an                  environment containing sodium chloride, calcium                  chloride and magnesium chloride as main                  components and containing 10000-230000 ppm total                  salt. The foam or emulsion is stable at a                  temperature of 30-120 degrees C and a pressure of                  70-400 atm. Preferred Process: The step (a) is                  performed by alternately press-fitting the                  aqueous sol, water, and carbon dioxide into the                  underground layer. The step (a) is performed at                  the temperature of 30-120 degrees C and a                  pressure of 70-400 atm. The mixing and treating                  is performed at 50-100 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The underground layer is a layer                  containing sandstone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The silane compound                  containing the hydrolyzing group is a silane                  compound containing an epoxy group or an organic                  group to which it is hydrolyzed. The epoxy group                  is a glycidyl group, and/or a cyclohexyl epoxy                  group. The silane compound containing the                  hydrolyzing group further comprises a silane                  compound containing an amino group, a silane                  compound (c2) containing the hydrolyzing group, a                  silane compound containing 1-40C alkyl, 6-40C                  aromatic ring, or organic group. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of the silane                  compound with respect to the silica particles is                  0.01-2.00:1.00.

PN US2021048208-A1; US11243003-B2
TI Method for operating environmental control system            that delivers dehumidified outdoor air into conditioned            space, involves adjusting air valve to deliver            dehumidified air into space at ventilation outdoor air            flow rate in response to determination that metric is            below predetermined threshold.
AU MOFFITT R R
AE TRANE INT INC (INGE-C)
GA 202121965P
AB    NOVELTY - The method (400) involves establishing a                ventilation carbon dioxide set point corresponding                to a ventilation outdoor air flow rate (405). A                dehumidification carbon dioxide set point is                established (410) corresponding to a                dehumidification outdoor air flow rate. A humidity                metric of a conditioned space is determined. An air                valve is adjusted (425) to deliver dehumidified air                into the conditioned space at the dehumidification                outdoor air flow rate in response to a                determination that the humidity metric is above a                predetermined threshold. The air valve is adjusted                (435) to deliver dehumidified air into the                conditioned space at the ventilation outdoor air                flow rate in response to a determination that the                humidity metric is below a predetermined                threshold.
   USE - Method for operating an environmental control                system (claimed) that delivers dehumidified outdoor                air into a conditioned space if a common space                through an air valve. Uses include but are not                limited to classroom, lecture hall, conference room                and lobby.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adapting simple and                cost-effective process to effectively control                humidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) Environmental control system.
   (2) Environmental system controller.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flowchart illustrating a                method for operating environmental control                system.
   Method for operating environmental control                system (400)
   Establishing ventilation carbon dioxide set                point corresponding to ventilation outdoor air flow                rate (405)
   Establishing dehumidification carbon dioxide                set point corresponding to dehumidification outdoor                air flow rate (410)
   Adjusting air valve to deliver dehumidified                air into conditioned space at dehumidification                outdoor air flow rate in response to determination                that humidity metric is above predetermined                threshold (425)
   Adjsuting air valve to deliver dehumidified                air into conditioned space at ventilation outdoor                air flow rate in response to determination that                humidity metric is below predetermined threshold                (435)

PN CN111530467-A; CN111530467-B
TI Carbon-modified copper-based catalyst useful in            preparing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation,            comprises active component composed of e.g. copper            oxide, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide, and carbon coated            on active component surface.
AU MA Q
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO T
   YANG W
   ZHANG J
   GAO X
   FAN H
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2020807881
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-modified copper-based catalyst                comprises active component and carbon coated on                active component surface, where the active                component is composed of copper oxide, zinc oxide                and aluminum oxide, or composed of copper, zinc                oxide and aluminum oxide, and the carbon has a                graphite structure.
   USE - The carbon-modified copper-based catalyst is                useful in preparing methanol by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based catalyst: utilizes carbon                having a graphite structure to avoid agglomeration                of catalyst and ensure that the carbon-modified                copper-based catalyst has a higher active component                content, does not block the pore structure of                carbon-modified copper-based catalyst and does not                damage active sites, and helps to maintain high                catalytic activity of carbon-modified copper-based                catalyst; improves conversion rate of carbon                dioxide, yield of methanol and its selectivity; and                has excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing carbon-modified copper-based                catalyst, comprising either (i) when the active                component comprises copper oxide, zinc oxide and                aluminum oxide, then mixing metal salt,                precipitating agent and water, carrying out                co-precipitation reaction to obtain co-precipitate,                aging, calcining for first time to obtain                copper-based catalyst, mixing copper-based catalyst                and aromatic hydrocarbon and calcining for second                time to obtain final product, or (ii) when the                active component comprises copper, zinc oxide and                aluminum oxide, then mixing metal salt,                precipitating agent and water, carrying out                co-precipitation reaction to obtain co-precipitate,                aging, calcining for first time, carrying out                reduction reaction to obtain copper-based catalyst,                mixing copper-based catalyst and aromatic                hydrocarbon and calcining for second time to obtain                final product, where the metal salt is metal salt                corresponding to the metal element in active                component; and
   (2) use of carbon-modified copper-based                catalyst in preparing methanol by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of                  the carbon-modified copper-based catalyst is                  20-40 meshes. The mass ratio between active                  component and carbon is 1:0.04-0.2. The mass                  ratio between copper oxide, zinc oxide and                  aluminum oxide is 4-6:2.5-3.5:2.5-3.5 when the                  active component comprises copper oxide, zinc                  oxide and aluminum oxide, and the mass ratio                  between copper, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide is                  4-6:2.5-3.5:2.5-3.5 when the active component                  comprises copper, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide.                  In steps (i) and (ii), the metal salt is nitrate                  and/or acetate, the precipitating agent is sodium                  carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium                  hydroxide, and the aromatic hydrocarbon is                  benzene, toluene and/or xylene. Preferred Method:                  In steps (i) and (ii), the co-precipitation                  reaction is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for                  1-5 hours, the pH value of the reaction system in                  co-precipitation reaction is 6-8, the aging is                  carried out at 20-50 degrees C for 10-15 hours,                  the primary calcination is carried out at 200-400                  degrees C for 2-6 hours, and the secondary                  calcination is carried out at 200-500 degrees C                  for 2-5 hours. In step (ii), the reduction                  reaction is carried out at 200-350 degrees C for                  3-10 hours under hydrogen and nitrogen mixed                  atmosphere, where the amount of hydrogen in                  hydrogen and nitrogen mixed atmosphere is 4-7                  vol.%. The conditions of carbon-modified                  copper-based catalyst in in preparing methanol by                  carbon dioxide hydrogenation are: the reaction                  gas is hydrogen, carbon monoxide and argon mixed                  gas in a volume ratio of 65-75:18-28:7-17, the                  contact time W/F is 7-10 g/hour/mol, the reaction                  pressure is 2.95-3.05 MPa, the reaction                  temperature is 180-250 degrees C and the reaction                  time is 30-60 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111151293-A
TI Nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide gas to prepare alcohol, comprises nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide nanoparticles as active component loaded on carrier containing B sites.
AU MA R
   LI Y
   YANG G
   ZHANG Y
   LIU R
   LU X
   WANG N
   WANG X
   PENG A
   XU C
   TU G
   ZHU W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 202044290E
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide catalyst comprises carrier and nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide active component loaded on the carrier. The carrier contains B sites, and the nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide active component is nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide nanoparticles having particle diameter of less than or equal to 10 nm.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped tungsten carbide catalyst is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide gas to prepare alcohol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 can be converted into ethanol and propanol product with high added value, and the conversion and selectivity of ethanol propanol are greatly improved. The method is simple and convenient, does not need to strictly control the concentration of metal salt solution, and provides catalyst, which has active center with rationally matched carbon and nitrogen content, high activity and stability, and regulated tungsten carbide (W2C) electronic structure so as to effectively control CO2 C-C bond formation rate and C-O bond fission rate in the hydrogenation process, greatly reduces the selectivity of C1 product, and improves the ethanol and propanol product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) raw material composition for preparing the catalyst comprising soluble tungsten salt, methylene amine organic compound and B acid site carrier, where the mol ratio of the tungsten salt and methylene amine is 1:0.5-1:5;
   (2) preparation of the catalyst comprising taking the soluble tungsten salt, methylene amine organic compound and water to prepare into mixed solution, impregnating to the carrier containing B acid sites, drying at 80-120 degrees C to obtain catalyst precursor, putting into a mixed atmosphere containing hydrogen, raising the temperature to 600-900 degrees C at 0.5-2 degrees C/minute and carbonizing for 2-12 hours; and
   (3) preparation of alcohol by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide gas comprising reacting carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen (H2) at 1:3-1:4 volume ratio under the action of the catalyst to generate alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material composition has tungsten content of 5-50 wt.% and nitrogen content of 0.5-5 wt.%. The soluble tungsten salt comprises ammonium metatungstate, ammonium p-tungstate, sodium tungstate or phosphotungstic acid. The B acid site carrier comprises molecular sieve carrier containing B acid sites, comprising ZSM-5 type carrier, Y type carrier, beta type carrier or MOR type carrier. The mixed gas further comprises nitrogen, argon gas and/or helium gas, and the volume fraction of hydrogen gas is 10-50 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The raw material composition further comprises 0.5-5 wt.% auxiliary agent comprising copper (Cu), cobalt (Co), indium (In), and/or iron (Fe).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methylene amine organic compound is hexamethylenediamine, hexamethylenetetramine, tetramethylenediamine or trimethylhexamethylenediamine. Preferred Method: The catalytic reaction is conducted at 200-350 degrees C, 0.5-10 MPa and volume space velocity of 1000-10000/hour.

PN CN110628709-A
TI Culture medium useful for improving maturation            quality of porcine oocytes, contains M199 culture            medium, epidermal growth factor (EGF), L-cysteine,            sodium pyruvate, follicular fluid, follicle-stimulating            hormone, and penicillin.
AU LIANG S
   SUN B
   ZHANG J
   QI J
   LI X
   WANG D
   HU W
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202002554M
AB    NOVELTY - Culture medium comprises M199 culture medium,                10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF), sulfite,                0.6 mM L-cysteine, 0.91 mM sodium pyruvate, 10%                follicular fluid, 10 IU/ml luteinising hormone, 10                IU/ml follicle-stimulating hormone, and 100 IU/ml                penicillin.
   USE - The culture medium is useful for improving                maturation quality of porcine oocytes in vitro                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium improves oocyte, promotes                growth rate and embryo quality of parthenogenetic                embryo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for culture                method for improving the maturation quality of                porcine oocytes in vitro, which involves collecting                pig ovary after slaughter from slaughterhouse,                placing pig ovary in normal saline at 37 degrees C                with double antibody, adding the mixture to the                laboratory by staff within 2 hours, adding culture                solution into 38.5 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide,                95% air and saturated humidity in a carbon dioxide                incubator for 3 hours, washing the ovaries for 2-3                times with 0.9% physiological saline at 37 degrees                C, temporarily storing the ovaries in water bath of                37 degrees C, selecting 10 ml syringe with 12-gauge                needle, pressing the ovaries by hand to produce a                certain negative pressure and puncture of 3-8 mm                follicles from side of the ovary, storing extracted                follicular fluid in 10 ml centrifuge tube,                precipitating for 10 minutes, sucking the                supernatant with pasteur tube, adding                4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid                (HEPES) buffer solution to the centrifuge tube to                repeatedly wash the follicular solution for 3                times, adding washed follicular fluid pellet and                HEPES buffer into dish together, selecting two to                three layers of cumulus with pipette under stereo                microscope in four-well plate, adding 600 mu l                culture medium in advance to each well, covering                the culture medium with 450 mu l mineral oil,                placing the four-well plate in 38.5 degrees C, 5%                carbon dioxide and 95% air, and incubating in                saturated carbon dioxide incubator for 42-44                hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The follicular fluid                  is prepared by allowing liquid extracted from the                  3-8 mm follicles to settle for 1 hour, retaining                  supernatant, centrifuging the supernatant at 8000                  revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes,                  removing the precipitate, repeating the steps for                  three times, filtering the liquid through 0.22 mu                  m filter, aliquoting into 1 ml, reserving, and                  storing at -20 degrees C. The culture medium is                  prepared by dissolving 97.74 mg arsenic acid in 2                  ml dimethyl sulfoxide, filtering with 0.22 mu m                  filter to prepare 100 mM arsenic acid                  concentrated stock solution, preparing culture                  solution into basic culture solution, filtering                  with 0.22 mu m filter, storing the mixture in 4                  degrees C, using the basic culture solution to                  dilute 100 mM concentrated arsenic acid stock                  solution to 10 mu m arsenic acid working                  solution, and adding 10 mu M working solution of                  arsenic acid to the basal culture solution to                  obtain culture solution. The HEPES buffer                  solution comprises 1 l high-pressure double                  distilled water, 6.6633 g/l sodium chloride,                  0.2386 g/l potassium chloride, 0.1680 g/l sodium                  bicarbonate, 0.0408 g/l sodium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 1.868 ml/l sodium lactate, 0.1017 g/l                  magnesium chloride, 0.022 g/l sodium pyruvate,                  2.38 g/l HEPES, 0.065 g/l penicillin, 0.294 g/l                  calcium chloride and 1 g/l polyvinyl alcohol. The                  HEPES buffer solution is preheated in a water                  bath at 38.5 degrees C, and the HEPES buffer                  solution has a pH of 7.2-7.4 and osmotic pressure                  is 270-290 mOsm. The formulated HEPES buffer                  solution is filtered through 0.22 mu m filter                  membrane and stored in 4 degrees C. The number of                  oocytes in each well of the four-well plate is                  100-110.

PN KR2019070130-A
TI Supercritical fluid supply device used during e.g. drying process of semiconductor device, has supply pipe that is connected to supercritical fluid tank to supply supercritical fluid into supercritical state to process chamber side.
AU SHIN I C
AE KCTECH CO LTD (KCTE-C)
GA 201952522F
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a supercritical fluid tank that is provided for heating a liquid fluid to convert liquid fluid into a supercritical state. An inflow pipe (140) extends through the supercritical fluid tank from outside to inside to supply the liquid fluid into the supercritical fluid tank. A supply pipe (170) is connected to the supercritical fluid tank to supply the supercritical fluid into the supercritical state to a process chamber side. The fluid is the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Supercritical fluid supply device used during substrate process such as lithography, deposition and etching, coating of photoresist, developing, cleaning, and drying process of semiconductor device.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow path of the fluid inside the supercritical fluid tank is extended, so that the liquid carbon dioxide is completely converted to the supercritical state and then supplied to the process chamber. The need to replace the lower cap is avoided even when a failure occurs in the supply pipe or the inflow pipe by connecting both the inflow pipe and the supply pipe to the upper cap of the supercritical fluid tank. The heater for heating the lower portion of the supercritical fluid tank is additionally provided to provide the supercritical fluid supply device for increasing the amount of heat transferred to the liquid carbon dioxide flowing in the supercritical fluid tank. The supply device prevents the temperature of the supply pipe flowing into the process chamber from decreasing and allows the supercritical fluid to be supplied to the process chamber while maintaining the supercritical state.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical fluid supply device in which the inlet pipe and the supply pipe are both connected to the upper cap of the supercritical fluid tank.
   Supercritical fluid supply device (100)
   Carbon dioxide tank (110)
   Processing chamber (130)
   Inflow pipe (140)
   Supply pipe (170)

PN CN108715453-A
TI Clean production process of sodium metabisulfite and sodium chloride involves introducing sulfur into sulfur-burning furnace, and reacting sulfur oxide produced after incineration and separated sodium carbonate.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI M
   ZHANG K
   YIN S
AE HENAN YUANHENG FINE CHEM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018881773
AB    NOVELTY - The clean production process of sodium metabisulfite and sodium chloride involves heating saline-alkali mixture at above 90 degrees C, converting sodium bicarbonate in solution to sodium carbonate, removing carbon dioxide, concentrating salt-alkali mixture by evaporation, separating the wet sodium carbonate solid by cooling and crystallizing, re-evaporating the remaining mother liquor, stopping evaporation when sodium carbonate in evaporating liquid approaches saturation, crystallizing sodium chloride solid, recycling sodium chloride in mixture, introducing sulfur into sulfur-burning furnace, reacting sulfur oxide produced after incineration and separated sodium carbonate to produce sodium metabisulfite, realizing the resource utilization of alkali in the mixed liquid, desulfurizing tail gas produced during production of sodium metabisulfite by salt-alkali mixture to form sodium sulfate solution and carbon dioxide, and recovering sodium sulfate solution.
   USE - Clean production process of sodium metabisulfite and sodium chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables clean production of sodium metabisulfite and sodium chloride with high quality and low equipment investment by simple and economical process, and does not produce waste water discharge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The clean production process of sodium metabisulfite and sodium chloride involves heating saline-alkali mixture at above 90 degrees C, converting sodium bicarbonate in solution to sodium carbonate, removing carbon dioxide, concentrating salt-alkali mixture by evaporation using mechanical steam recompression, separating the wet sodium carbonate solid by cooling and crystallizing when sodium chloride is nearly saturated to concentrated mixed solution, re-evaporating the remaining mother liquor using mechanical steam recompression, stopping evaporation when sodium carbonate in evaporating liquid approaches saturation, crystallizing sodium chloride solid, recycling sodium chloride in mixture, introducing sulfur into sulfur-burning furnace, reacting sulfur oxide produced after incineration and separated sodium carbonate to produce sodium metabisulfite, realizing the resource utilization of alkali in the mixed liquid, desulfurizing tail gas produced during production of sodium metabisulfite by salt-alkali mixture to form sodium sulfate solution and carbon dioxide, and recovering sodium sulfate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of carbon dioxide in total gas during evaporation is less than 5%. The salt-alkali mixture is saline-alkali mixture. The saline-alkali mixture contains sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium chloride.

PN CN107841761-A; CN107841761-B
TI Nanoporous zinc electrode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises base layer, zinc electrodeposition layer on outer surface of base layer and zinc catalyst nanolayer formed on surface of zinc electrodeposition layer.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG T
   ZHONG H
   QIU Y
   LI X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201825227K
AB    NOVELTY - Nanoporous zinc electrode comprises base layer, a zinc electrodeposition layer on the outer surface of the base layer and a zinc catalyst nanolayer formed on the surface of the zinc electrodeposition layer. The zinc catalyst nanolayer consists of zinc nanoparticles with a particle size of 1-300 nm.
   USE - Nanoporous zinc electrode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoporous zinc electrode improves specific surface area and active area of electrode, and improves electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid by electrode and Faraday efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing nanoporous zinc electrode, which involves:
   (A) washing zinc foil having a thickness of 0.01-1 mm with one or two or more of water, ethanol, or acetone, and is leveling at constant current density or constant potential in 50-95 wt.% phosphoric acid concentration for 5-1200s;
   (B) treating under ultrasonic cleaning with water, drying under inert atmosphere protection as a substrate, and zinc oxide solids are dissolving to 1-15M sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide aqueous solution and use as a plating solution and 10-100g/l zinc oxide solids;
   (C) performing electrodeposition of zinc at a constant current density or a constant potential in a plating solution under an inert atmosphere on a substrate for 5-3000 seconds by washing with ultrapure water and drying under inert atmosphere protection;
   (D) subjecting substrate with the zinc electrodeposition layer in a tube furnace and treating in air at 220-390 degrees C for 4-24 hours; and
   (E) subjecting into heat treatment electrode in a carbon dioxide saturate electrolyte for reduction at constant potential or constant current density for 10 minutes to 2 hours, or under dihydrogen conditions at 50-380 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours for reduction to obtain the desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The zinc catalyst nano-layer of the electrode contains a pore with a diameter of 1-300 nm, and the porosity of the zinc catalyst nano-layer is 2-15%. The thickness of zinc electrodeposited layer is 500-0.05mm, and the thickness of zinc catalyst nanolayer is 100-500nm. The constant current density is 1-50mA/cm2, preferably 2-20mA/cm2, and the constant potential is 0.1-3V, preferably 0.4-1.6V. The leveling time of substrate for 30-600 seconds. The constant current density is -1mA/cm2 to -1500mA/cm2, preferably -5mA/cm2 to -500mA/cm2, and constant potential is -0.1V to -4V, preferably -0.4V to -2V. The deposition time of substrate for 60-1200 seconds. The constant current density is -1mA/cm2 to -100mA/cm2, preferably -3mA/cm2 to -20mA/cm2, and constant potential is -0.8V to -3V, preferably - 1.0V to 1.6V. Preferred Components: The post-treatment electrolyte is selected from one or more of potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, potassium(I) bromide, sodium bromide, hydrochloric acid, or phosphoric acid with concentration of 0.01-1M.

AE JIANGSU PROVINCE METALLURGICAL DESIGN (BJSW-C)
GA 2018171400
AB    NOVELTY - A dual shaft furnace system has shaft furnace (I) having feed port (I), discharge port (I), air inlet (I) and top air outlet (I), and shaft furnace (II) having feed port (II), air inlet (II) and top air outlet (II). The air inlet (I) is connected with a heating furnace, a compressor (I), and mixing tanks. The top gas outlet port (I) is connected in sequence with water wash tower (I), pressure reduction tank, and reformer heating furnace. The air inlet (II) is connected to the reformer furnace in sequence through a pipeline, carbon dioxide remover, and hydrogen extraction device.
   USE - Dual shaft furnace system is used for producing iron-containing product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dual shaft furnace system efficiently provides iron-containing product with reduced consumption of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dual shaft furnace system has shaft furnace (I) having feed port (I), discharge port (I), air inlet (I) and top air outlet (I), and shaft furnace (II) having feed port (II), air inlet (II) and top air outlet (II). The air inlet (I) is connected with a heating furnace, a compressor (I), and mixing tanks. The top gas outlet port (I) is connected in sequence with water wash tower (I), pressure reduction tank, and reformer heating furnace. The air inlet (II) is connected to the reformer furnace in sequence through a pipeline, carbon dioxide remover, and hydrogen extraction device. The top gas outlet port (II) is connected in sequence with water washing tower (II) and hydrogen purging device. The hydrogen extraction device supplies hydrogen-rich gas to the mixing tank through a pipeline. The carbon dioxide remover respectively provides carbon monoxide-rich gas and carbon dioxide-rich gas to pressure reducing tank and reforming furnace, respectively. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of iron-containing product using dual shaft furnace system.

AB    NOVELTY - Propanal is produced by performing oxidative                coupling of methane (OCM reaction) and oxygen as                reactant stream in gas phase reaction to form                ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide (CO2), water and                syngas (CO and H2) as ethylene stream in 1st                reactor containing OCM zone and downstream thermal                cracking zone to form ethylene from ethane, and                forming propanal in 2nd reactor by feeding to the                2nd reactor the ethylene stream from the 1st                reactor in the gas phase and hydroformylating the                ethylene stream to form propanal containing product                stream in the presence of catalyst for a water gas                shift reaction.
   USE - Production of propanal for producing methyl                methacrylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The production method of propanal is simple,                avoids the necessity of any cryogenic or other                separation of ethylene from OCM reaction, and                avoids the need to make additional syngas for                hydroformylation in a separate reformer                reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Propanal is produced by performing oxidative                coupling of methane (OCM reaction) and oxygen as                reactant stream in gas phase reaction to form                ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide (CO2), water and                syngas (CO and H2) as ethylene stream in 1st                reactor containing OCM zone and downstream thermal                cracking zone to form ethylene from ethane, and                forming propanal in 2nd reactor by feeding to the                2nd reactor the ethylene stream from the 1st                reactor in the gas phase and hydroformylating the                ethylene stream to form propanal containing product                stream in the presence of catalyst for a water gas                shift reaction, in which water and carbon monoxide                (CO) form carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas (H2).                Molar ratio of syngas to total amount of ethylene                and ethane in ethylene stream before downstream                thermal cracking zone is 2:1-10:1. Molar ratio of                total CO2 and CO to total ethylene and ethane in                ethylene stream before downstream thermal cracking                zone is 0.97:1-5:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Catalyst for water gas                  shift and for hydroformylating is sulfide                  catalyst. The 1st reactor, the 2nd reactor, and                  all streams leading directly or indirectly from                  the 1st reactor to the 2nd reactor are in an                  enclosed or sealed system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Production method                  includes adjusting molar ratio of H2 to CO in                  syngas in ethylene stream by co-feeding steam                  into 2nd reactor and/or into 1st reactor to                  generate additional H2 in syngas in any case, in                  which molar ratio of H2 to CO in syngas in                  ethylene stream leaving the downstream thermal                  cracking zone of 1st reactor is ~1:1, or                  alternatively, by reacting CO2 and H2 from                  ethylene stream of 1st reactor in 2nd reactor to                  generate additional CO in syngas in the presence                  of catalyst for water gas shift reaction. Steam                  is generated by compressing ethylene stream                  output from 1st reactor to form compressed OCM                  fluid stream containing water, removing water                  from compressed OCM fluid stream, and reheating                  water to form steam. Step of reheating water to                  form steam involves using water as heat exchanger                  to compress output from 1st reactor. Ethylene                  formation involves dehydrogenating any ethane                  formed in upstream zone of 1st reactor to form                  ethylene in downstream thermal cracking zone of                  1st reactor using heat from 1st reactor to drive                  the dehydrogenating process. After                  hydroformylating, propanal-containing product                  stream, including propanal, methane, ethane,                  propanol, carbon dioxide, water vapor and syngas                  are fed to separator. Methane is recycled to 1st                  reactor and ethane is recycled to downstream                  thermal cracking zone in 1st reactor. Method                  includes continuously adding hydrogen sulfide                  (H2S) in ethylene feed stream into 2nd reactor,                  in amount of 0.1-1 vol.% based on total volume of                  ethylene feed stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
