PN KR2022095544-A
TI Production of porous gypsum used as interior            material, involves adjusting concentration and            temperature of spent sulfuric acid to predetermined            crystal concentration and crystal temperature and            introducing into reactor, adding limestone powder, and            agitating obtained mixture.
AU GOON S Y
AE GOON S Y (GOON-Individual)
GA 202289140L
AB    NOVELTY - Production (P1) of porous gypsum involves (i)                preparing spent sulfuric acid by adjusting                concentration and temperature of spent sulfuric                acid to a predetermined crystal concentration and                crystal temperature, (ii) introducing the spent                sulfuric acid into a reactor, (iii) adding                limestone powder in a predetermined amount to the                reactor one or more times and adjusting the input                amount of the limestone powder in response to a                predetermined critical temperature according to                each input frequency to obtain a mixture, and (iv)                agitating the mixture to obtain porous gypsum                having pores and carbon dioxide or vapor generated                while the temperature of the mixture                increases.
   USE - Production of porous gypsum used as interior                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                production of porous gypsum having moisture content                of 25% or less, high purity with no other harmful                substances, and desired thermal insulation                property, sound absorption property and water                absorption property. Carbon dioxide produced during                the production of porous gypsum has a purity of 99%                or more due to instantaneous rapid expansion and                solidification by dehydration reaction at the high                temperature. Since the gypsum uses spent sulfuric                acid generated as a by-product in the semiconductor                process, the spent sulfuric acid has almost no                foreign substances other than silicon components,                and can have very clean components. The porous                gypsum is safe, even when in contact with the human                body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the porous gypsum, which comprises                micropores of 1 mu m or less and macropores of                10-200 mu m which are distributed in gypsum                crystals by carbon dioxide and steam generated                during the reaction; and
   (2) a porous gypsum manufacturing apparatus,                which comprises the reactor comprising an input                unit to introduce the spent sulfuric acid and                limestone powder to form mixture in a mixing space                to form a mixture, and a discharge unit for                discharging the gypsum, a stirrer arranged in the                mixing space and rotatably provided in both                directions to stir the mixture, a cover sealing the                upper portion of the reactor, and comprising an                input unit of the spent sulfuric acid and limestone                powder, and at least one discharge passage through                which carbon dioxide inside the reactor is                discharged, and a carbon dioxide recovery unit                connected to the discharge passage to remove the                sulfur component gas included in the carbon dioxide                and recover the carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide                  recovery unit comprises a water supply unit for                  supplying cooling water to a connection passage                  connected to the discharge passage, a drain unit                  for dissolving sulfur component gas by spraying                  water to drain sulfuric acid water, and a storage                  unit to which the carbon dioxide is supplied. The                  apparatus further comprises an exhaust fan                  provided to discharge carbon dioxide and steam in                  the reactor, when the pressure in the reactor                  increases above a predetermined critical                  pressure. The exhaust fan has a rotational speed                  controlled in proportion to the pressure in the                  reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixing weight                  ratio of the spent sulfuric acid and the                  limestone powder is determined to be 98:100 to                  98:103. The crystal concentration of sulfuric                  acid of the spent sulfuric acid is 30-75 wt.%. In                  the process (iii), the initial critical                  temperature of the mixture at the time of the                  initial input of the limestone powder is 60                  degrees C, and the final critical temperature of                  the mixture at the time of the final input of the                  limestone powder is 80-100 degrees C. The porous                  gypsum comprises 85-95 wt.% anhydrite and                  dihydrate gypsum in total, and has a moisture                  content of 5-25 wt.%, a specific gravity of                  0.5-0.9, and a sulfur trioxide content of 35-45                  wt.%. Preferred Process: The crystal temperature                  before the reaction of the spent sulfuric acid is                  15-50 degrees C. When the pressure inside the                  reactor increases above a predetermined critical                  pressure, carbon dioxide or vapor is discharged                  from the reactor to maintain the pressure of the                  reactor at 1-10 atm. In the process (P1), voids                  are generated in the gypsum as steam, and carbon                  dioxide generated by the reaction of the spent                  sulfuric acid and limestone powder are                  discharged. The process (P1) further involves                  pouring the mixture into a separate molding mold                  and molding into a block or plate shape porous                  gypsum, and collecting carbon dioxide by removing                  sulfur component gas from carbon dioxide, by                  supplying cooling water to carbon dioxide to                  remove sulfur gas and supplying carbon dioxide to                  liquid slaked lime (calcium hydroxide) to remove                  sulfur gas. The process (P1) involves directly                  adding limestone powder to 30-75 wt.%                  high-concentration spent sulfuric acid in the                  reactor, mixing, stirring, evaporating moisture                  by the reaction heat generated from the reaction                  of the spent sulfuric acid and limestone powder,                  reacting, foaming and solidifying using carbon                  dioxide and steam generated during the reaction.                  The weight ratio of anhydrite and dihydrate                  gypsum in the porous gypsum can be adjusted, and                  anhydrite and dihydrate gypsum in the porous                  gypsum can be converted to both gypsum dihydrate                  by increasing the weight ratio of the gypsum                  dihydrate to 5:5. Preferred Property: The                  particle size of the limestone powder is 200 mesh                  or less.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PT P
PN KR2022078081-A
TI System used for forming hydrogen, consists of gas inlet for producing natural gas from gas well and filtering natural gas to remove impurities, gas processing unit, buffer tank, reformer, hydrogen liquefier, hydrogen storage tank and carbon capture and storage unit.
AU CHUNG K
   LEE K N
   PARK H
AE UNIV PUSAN NAT IND COOP FOUND (UYPU-C)
GA 202280931H
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen production system consists of a gas inlet for producing natural gas from a gas well and filtering the natural gas to remove impurities in the natural gas, a gas processing unit for removing moisture in the natural gas passing through the gas inlet and neutralizing acid, a buffer tank in which the natural gas is temporarily stored, a reformer connected to the buffer tank and configured to reform natural gas supplied from the buffer tank to produce hydrogen, a hydrogen liquefier for liquefying hydrogen produced through the reformer, one or more hydrogen storage tanks in which liquefied hydrogen liquefied through the hydrogen liquefier is stored and a carbon capture and storage unit (CCS) for collecting carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, after recovering the exhaust gas generated from the gas inlet and the gas processing unit, discharging the remaining exhaust gas except for carbon dioxide to the outside, and re-supplying the captured carbon dioxide to the gas well.
   USE - System used for forming hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system can be efficiently generated hydrogen without emitting harmful gases and with reduced energy consumption rate of the reformer operating in a high-temperature environment by recovering waste heat generated in the process of natural gas production and hydrogen liquefaction and using in the hydrogen production process. By collecting and recovering carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas generated in the process of reforming high-temperature natural gas into hydrogen and re-supplying to the gas well, the efficiency of natural gas production is increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the hydrogen, which involves producing natural gas from the gas well through the gas inlet, and filtering the produced natural gas to remove impurities in the natural gas, removing moisture in natural gas and neutralizing acidity through the gas processing unit, temporarily storing the natural gas passing through the gas processing unit in the buffer tank, producing hydrogen by reforming the natural gas supplied from the buffer tank through a reformer connected to the buffer tank, liquefying hydrogen produced through the reformer through the hydrogen liquefier, storing liquefied hydrogen in one or more hydrogen storage tanks and recovering the exhaust gas generated from the gas inlet and the gas processing unit through the carbon capture and storage unit, collecting the carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas to discharge the remaining exhaust gas except for the carbon dioxide to the outside, and resupplying the collected carbon dioxide to the gas well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The gas inlet comprises a slug catcher for filtering the natural gas to remove impurities. The gas processing unit comprises a glycol contactor for filtering the natural gas to remove moisture. The system further consists of a heat recovery unit (U1) for recovering heat from the natural gas between the gas inlet and the gas processing unit, and a heat recover unit (U2) for recovering heat generated in the process of liquefying natural gas into liquefied hydrogen, when the hydrogen is liquefied and an oil recovery enhancement (EOR) pump for pressurizing and supplying carbon dioxide captured through the carbon capture and storage unit in the direction of the gas well, between the gas well and the carbon capture and storage unit. The heat recovery units (U1) and (U2) supply the recovered heat to the reformer and a generator connected to the buffer tank and configured to generate electricity using natural gas partially supplied from the buffer tank. The exhaust gas generated from each of the gas inlet unit, the gas processing unit, and the generator is recovered through a recovery pipe and supplied to the carbon capture and storage unit of the tank.

PN CN114539333-A
TI Cyclic production process of sialic acid comprises            adding ammonium sulfate or ammonium chloride in            polysialic acid aqueous solution, carrying out            hydrolysis, collecting ammonia gas, decolorizing            hydrolyzate, carrying out solid-liquid separation,            drying, and separating out ammonium sulfate.
AU LIU C
   HU X
   LU J
AE DEYUANTANG SHANGHAI HEALTH TECHNOLOGY DE (DEYU-Non-standard)
GA 2022755025
AB    NOVELTY - Cyclic production process of sialic acid                comprises (i) adding ammonium sulfate or ammonium                chloride in the polysialic acid aqueous solution,                carrying out the hydrolysis of the polysialic acid                aqueous solution under heating conditions, and                collecting the ammonia gas produced in the                hydrolysis process and pass into the water,                introducing carbon dioxide into the water, and                carrying out ultrasonic oscillation to the                polysialic acid aqueous solution in the hydrolysis                process, (ii) decolorizing obtained hydrolyzate,                concentrating, adding sulfuric acid or hydrochloric                acid into the cooled concentrated solution and                allowing to stand to precipitate sialic acid                crystals, carrying out solid-liquid separation, and                drying, and (iii) collecting the liquid phase and                introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide into the                liquid phase, and collecting the carbon dioxide                produced by the reaction, separating out ammonium                sulfate or ammonium chloride in the mixed                solution.
   USE - The method is useful for cyclic production                process of sialic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the unique process route,                which can not only realize the production of sialic                acid, and recycles the waste liquid and waste gas                generated in the production process to realize the                resource production of sialic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cyclic production process of sialic acid                comprises (i) adding ammonium sulfate or ammonium                chloride in the polysialic acid aqueous solution,                carrying out the hydrolysis of the polysialic acid                aqueous solution under heating conditions, and                collecting the ammonia gas produced in the                hydrolysis process and pass into the water,                introducing carbon dioxide into the water, and                carrying out ultrasonic oscillation to the                polysialic acid aqueous solution in the hydrolysis                process, (ii) after the described hydrolysis is                completed, decolorizing obtained hydrolyzate,                concentrating, adding sulfuric acid or hydrochloric                acid into the cooled concentrated solution and                allowing to stand to precipitate sialic acid                crystals, and after completion, carrying out                solid-liquid separation, and drying the obtained                wet sialic acid crystals to obtain sialic acid                crystals, (iii) collecting the liquid phase                obtained by the solid-liquid separation and                introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide into the                liquid phase to obtain the mixture of the aqueous                solution, and collecting the carbon dioxide                produced by the reaction, separating out ammonium                sulfate or ammonium chloride in the mixed solution,                where the ammonium sulfate or ammonium chloride as                a reaction raw material added into the polysialic                acid aqueous solution, and using collected carbon                dioxide as the reaction raw material passed into                the water in step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  molar ratio of the ammonium sulfate or ammonium                  chloride and the polysialic acid is                  4-5.5:1.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  heating temperature is 60-70&#8451;,                  preferably, the hydrolysis time is 3-4.5 hours;                  the introduction volume of the carbon dioxide is                  more than 0.5 times the volume of ammonia; and                  the power of the ultrasonic treatment is 330-400                  W. In the step (ii), the activated carbon or                  diatomaceous earth is added into decolorize the                  hydrolyzate, where the added mass of the                  decolorizing agent is 2-5 vol.% hydrolyzate; the                  hydrolyzed solution is concentrated to less than                  45% of its volume at a heating temperature not                  more than 70&#8451;. The step (ii) comprises                  adding sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid in the                  concentrated solution to adjust the pH value of                  the concentrated solution to 1-2, and when the                  step (i) adopts ammonium sulfate, the step (ii)                  adopts sulfuric acid to carry out the                  crystallization of sialic acid in the                  concentrated solution, and when the step (i)                  adopts ammonium chloride, step (ii) adopts                  hydrochloric acid to carry out the                  crystallization of sialic acid in the                  concentrated solution, preferably, the                  temperature of the cooled concentrate is                  2-5&#8451;. In the step (ii), the drying                  method comprises vacuum drying, or freeze-drying.                  In the step (iii), the method for isolating                  ammonium sulfate or ammonium chloride in the                  mixed solution comprises: the mixed solution is                  dried or ethanol is added into the mixed solution                  to precipitate ammonium sulfate or ammonium                  chloride crystals, and the precipitate is                  separated and dried to obtain ammonium sulfate or                  ammonium chloride solid.

AE SOBIERAJ A (SOBI-Individual)
   SOBIERAJ M (SOBI-Individual)
GA 2021E3060P
AB    NOVELTY - A set of devices for separation of carbon dioxide from waste gasses e.g., biogas, comprises compressor, a preliminary heat exchanger, a pipeline with a three-way valve supplying gas alternately to cryogenic exchangers, and an installation discharging the obtained liquid carbon dioxide in the form of a pipeline and a parallel pipeline for discharging liquid biomethane. The alternately operating greater than or equal to 2 parallel cryogenic exchangers are each provided with greater than or equal to 1 exchanger of the thermosiphon installation and with greater than or equal to 1 evaporator of the auto-cascade refrigerant cycle system. The exchangers the thermosiphon installation in a closed system are connected in series with the exchanger of the thermosiphon installation located in the preliminary heat exchanger, while the auto-cascade system of refrigerant circulation is supplied by a compressor connected on the discharge side with a condenser supplied by a refrigerant absorption system and a phase separator.
   USE - A set of devices for separation of carbon dioxide from waste gasses e.g., biogas (claimed).

The alternately operating greater than or equal to 2 parallel cryogenic exchangers are each provided with greater than or equal to 1 exchanger of the thermosiphon installation and with greater than or equal to 1 evaporator of the auto-cascade refrigerant cycle system. The exchangers the thermosiphon installation in a closed system are connected in series with the exchanger of the thermosiphon installation located in the preliminary heat exchanger, while the auto-cascade system of refrigerant circulation is supplied by a compressor connected on the discharge side with a condenser supplied by a refrigerant absorption system and a phase separator in which steam connector is connected through a regenerative exchanger with an expansion valve and a three-way valve with evaporators of the auto-cascade refrigerant circulation system. The liquid connector is connected through the expansion valve with the regenerative exchanger and the three-way valve with the evaporators of the auto-cascade refrigerant circulation system. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for separating carbon dioxide from waste gasses e.g., biogas.

PN JP2021109854-A
TI Hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus for manufacturing hydrocarbon compound e.g. ethane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, has hydrogen separation unit which separates hydrogen from gas and purge gas supply unit supplies purge gas which purges inside of reactor.
AU IMAGAWA H
   SAEKI A
   SAKAI M
   KITA T
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
GA 202188497B
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a reactor (10) which accommodates the catalyst (11) containing the metal with the hydrocarbon synthesis catalyst performance, and the metal with carbon-dioxide occlusion capacity. A source gas supply unit (20) supplies the source gas (12) supplied from the supply source of the source gas containing a carbon dioxide to the reactor. A hydrogen supply unit (30) supplies hydrogen to the reactor. A control unit (50) controls the hydrogen supply unit. The control unit supplies the hydrogen to the reactor at first and second flow rates after supplying the source gas to the reactor. The second flow rate is in the range of 60% or less of the first flow rate.
   USE - Hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus for manufacturing hydrocarbon compound such as ethane and propane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The consumption of hydrogen is reduced in a hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus. The conversion ratio to hydrocarbon compound of a carbon dioxide is improved. Since mixing with oxygen and hydrogen can be suppressed when the gas which contains oxygen in source gas is used, the combustion by mixing with oxygen and hydrogen can be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen separation unit is arranged in the downstream side of the reactor. The hydrogen separation unit separates hydrogen from the gas containing the product taken out from the reactor. The hydrogen supply unit supplies the hydrogen separated in the hydrogen separation unit to the reactor. A pressurizing unit is connected with the reactor and the hydrogen separation unit. The pressurizing unit heightens the pressure of the gas containing the product taken out from the reactor. The hydrogen separation unit separates hydrogen from the gas pressurized by the pressurizing unit and contains the product. A purge gas supply unit supplies the purge gas which purges the inside of the reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing method of hydrocarbon compound, involves making the catalyst occluding a carbon dioxide. The hydrogen is supplied to the reactor after the carbon-dioxide occlusion process, and the catalyst is activated. The hydrogen is supplied to the reactor after first hydrogen supply process. The hydrocarbon compound is generated such that the supply speed when supplying hydrogen to the reactor in first hydrogen supply process is slower than the supply speed when supplying hydrogen to the reactor in the second hydrogen supply process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of the structure of the methane production apparatus.
   Reactor (10)
   Catalyst (11)
   Source gas (12)
   Source gas supply unit (20)
   Hydrogen supply unit (30)
   Control unit (50)

PN CN111215150-A; CN111215150-B
TI Preparing organic phosphonic acid chelated copper            ion intercalated magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite            comprises e.g. dissolving magnesium nitrate hexahydrate            and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in carbon dioxide-free            water and vacuum drying.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG J
   WU H
   PAN Z
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 202050522R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic phosphonic acid chelated                copper ion intercalated magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite comprises e.g. (i) dissolving                magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate                nonahydrate in carbon dioxide-free water, adding                alkaline solution, and performing co-precipitation                reaction, crystallizing, centrifugating, washing,                and vacuum drying, (ii) dissolving the magnesium                aluminum hydrotalcite in carbon dioxide-free water,                and stirring to obtain slurry A, (iii) dissolving                ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonic acid in                alkaline solution to obtain solution B, (iv)                dissolving copper nitrate trihydrate in carbon                dioxide-free water to obtain solution C, (v) adding                solution C into solution B, and then stirring to                obtain ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonate                chelated copper ion solution, and (vi) adding                slurry A into the                ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonate chelated                copper ion solution, stirring to perform anion                exchange reaction, centrifuging, washing, and                vacuum drying.
   USE - The organic phosphonic acid chelated copper                ion intercalated magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite is                useful in degradation of dye wastewater                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum hydrotalcite has improved                catalytic stability, and interlayer spacing; and                has higher efficiency of organic matter                degradation, and degradation efficiency of the                catalytic degradation of rhodamine B under visible                light reaches 98.2%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing organic phosphonic acid chelated                copper ion intercalated magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite comprises (i) dissolving magnesium                nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate                nonahydrate in carbon dioxide-free water, then                adding alkaline solution to adjust the pH to 9-11,                and performing co-precipitation reaction under the                protection of nitrogen, crystallizing,                centrifugating, washing, and vacuum drying to                obtain magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite, (ii)                dissolving the magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite in                carbon dioxide-free water at room temperature, and                stirring under nitrogen protection to obtain slurry                A, (iii) dissolving                ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonic acid in                alkaline solution to obtain solution B, (iv)                dissolving copper nitrate trihydrate in carbon                dioxide-free water to obtain solution C, (v) adding                solution C dropwise into the solution B, and then                stirring at room temperature to obtain                ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonate chelated                copper ion solution, and (vi) adding slurry A into                the ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonate                chelated copper ion solution at room temperature,                stirring under the protection of nitrogen to                perform anion exchange reaction, centrifuging,                washing, and vacuum drying to obtain organic                phosphonic acid chelated copper ion intercalated                magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS                are also included for:
   (1) organic phosphonic acid chelated copper                ion intercalated magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite                obtained by above method; and
   (2) use of organic phosphonic acid chelated                copper ion intercalated magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite in degradation of dye wastewater                comprising adding organic phosphonic acid chelated                copper ion intercalated magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite into the dye wastewater in an amount                of 0.1-0.5 g/l under visible light.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): reaction                  temperature of the co-precipitation reaction is                  45-75 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-2.5                  hours; crystallization temperature of is 90-110                  degrees C, and the crystallization time is 18-36                  hours; drying temperature of the vacuum drying is                  45-65 degrees C, and the drying time is 24-48                  hours. In step (v), stirring time of the stirring                  is 12-24 hours. In step (vi), reaction time of                  the anion exchange reaction is 12-18 hours;                  drying temperature of the vacuum drying is 45-65                  degrees C, and the drying time is 24-48 hours.                  Preferred Components: In the step (i): the molar                  ratio between magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and                  aluminum nitrate nonahydrate is 3:1; alkaline                  solution is sodium hydroxide solution, potassium                  hydroxide solution, or ammonia-ammonium chloride                  mixed buffered alkaline solution having pH of                  9-10; and concentration of the sodium hydroxide                  solution or the potassium hydroxide solution is                  0.3-0.5 mol/l. In step (ii), amount of the                  magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite added in 1 l                  carbon dioxide-depleted water is 20-50 g, and the                  stirring time is 18-36 hours. In step (iii),                  amount of the                  ethylenediaminetetramethylenephosphonic acid                  added in 1 l alkali is 87.2-348.8 g. In step                  (iv), amount of copper nitrate trihydrate added                  in 1 l carbon dioxide-free water is 144.6-578.4                  g.

GA 201947087T
AB    NOVELTY - Hydroponic culture method of radix isatidis in plant factory involves selecting seeds with good grain maturity, soaking in clear water, drying, sowing dried seeds on wet seedling medium by hand or machine, watering and moisturizing, germinating the seeds at 15-25 degrees C and 60-100% relative humidity, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1000 ppm, colonizing seedlings with 2-3 true leaves, planting the seedlings on a hydroponic device at cultivation density of 36-60 trees/m2, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1000 ppm, when number of leaves reaches 8-10 or more, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1500 ppm, harvesting, and adjusting environmental conditions.
   USE - Hydroponic culture method of radix isatidis in plant factory.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydroponic culture method of radix isatidis in plant factory involves selecting seeds with good grain maturity, soaking in clear water, drying, sowing dried seeds on wet seedling medium by hand or machine, watering and moisturizing, germinating the seeds at 15-25 degrees C and 60-100% relative humidity, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1000 ppm, colonizing seedlings with 2-3 true leaves, planting the seedlings on a hydroponic device at cultivation density of 36-60 trees/m2, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1000 ppm, when number of leaves reaches 8-10 or more, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1500 ppm, when the number of leaves reaches 15-20, harvesting, controlling day/night temperature to 20-25 degrees C/16-20 degrees C, relative humidity to 10-80% and carbon dioxide concentration to 300-1500 ppm, when number of leaves reaches 8-10 or more, harvesting, and adjusting environmental conditions according to growth, the hydroponic device comprises a cultivation device, cultivation light source, and nutrient fluid circulation supply system, and the device can adjust cultivation light source environment according to different growth periods.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The seeds are soaked in clear water for 0.5-6 hours, where the seeds are sown at a density of 600-1500 grains/m2. PN CN114814184-A
TI Measuring degradation rate of degradable plastic            based on carbon 13 isotope method by obtaining carbon            13 isotope value of experimental soil and each            degradable plastic, mixing target degradable plastic            into experimental soil, obtaining relative contribution            and obtaining degradation rate.
AU YAO H
   WANG J
   LIU X
   YU Y
AE NINGBO BEILUN ZHONGKE HAIXI IND TECHNOLO (NING-Non-standard)
   INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 2022A1104G
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring degradation rate of degradable                plastic based on carbon 13 isotope method comprises                (a) obtaining the carbon 13 isotope value of the                experimental soil and each degradable plastic by                measuring, (b) mixing the target degradable plastic                into the experimental soil, setting experimental                group and the control group, (c) extracting the                soils of the preset weights of the experimental                group and the control group and placing in                transparent containers as the experimental                container and the control container and setting a                hollow transparent container as the reference                container, (d) obtaining the carbon dioxide content                and carbon 13 isotope value of each container, (e)                obtaining the relative contribution of the target                biodegradable plastic to the total carbon dioxide                release based on the difference in the content of                carbon dioxide and carbon-13 isotopes and (f)                obtaining the degradation rate of the target                degradable plastic based on the relative                contribution.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring degradation                rate of degradable plastic based on carbon 13                isotope method .
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can distinguish the degradable                plastic and carbon dioxide generated by the                decomposition of organic carbon and can accurately                calculate the degradation rate of the degradable                plastic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring degradable plastic degradation rate                based on carbon 13 isotope method comprises (a)                obtaining the carbon 13 isotope value of the                experimental soil and each degradable plastic by                measuring, (b) mixing the target degradable plastic                into the experimental soil, setting experimental                group and the control group, (c) extracting the                soils of the preset weights of the experimental                group and the control group, placing in transparent                containers as the experimental container and the                control container, setting a hollow transparent                container as the reference container, (d) obtaining                the carbon dioxide content and carbon 13 isotope                value of each container after one cycle of                cultivation under the preset temperature, preset                water content, preset shading degree, and sealed                condition, (e) obtaining the relative contribution                of the target biodegradable plastic to the total                carbon dioxide release based on the difference in                the content of carbon dioxide and carbon-13                isotopes and (f) obtaining the degradation rate of                the target degradable plastic based on the relative                contribution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for measuring degradation rate of degradable                plastic based on carbon 13 isotope method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a step diagram of a method                for measuring the degradation rate of degradable                plastics based on the carbon 13 isotope method                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  experimental soil is soil with different isotope                  values of the carbon 13 of the target degradable                  plastic. In the step (e), the total amount of                  carbon dioxide released further comprises carbon                  dioxide generated by the decomposing of organic                  carbon.The relative contribution is obtained by                  using the formula (I), as given the                  specification. In the step (f), the degradation                  rate is obtained by formula (II) (Dbio-plastic=                  f&#215;Rco2), where Dbio-plastic is the                  degradation rate and the total release amount of                  carbon dioxide in the experimental serum bottle.                  The experimental soil from the soil with                  different isotope values of the carbon 13 of the                  target degradable plastic in the isotope                  determination unit. The carbon dioxide total                  release amount in the data obtaining unit further                  comprises carbon dioxide generated by organic                  carbon decomposition.

PT P
PN CN112616991-A
TI Extracting protein by performing enzymatic hydrolysis on mixture of enzyme, distilled water and Chlorella powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and adding inorganic salt to aqueous solution to precipitate protein.
AU TIAN S
   LV C
   ZHANG Z
   WANG D
   LONG Y
   ZHOU H
   XIE S
AE UNIV HENAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHY-C)
GA 202140356C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting protein from Chlorella involves (a) adding enzyme, and distilled water to Chlorella powder, adjusting a pH of the solution, soaking for 12-24 hours, performing enzymatic hydrolysis in a water bath, crushing cell wall of Chlorella, and inactivating enzymatic hydrolysis solution to obtain a mixed solution, (b) putting mixed liquid into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, maintaining fixed kettle at 35-45 degrees C, and valve temperature of 90-97 degrees C, adjusting carbon dioxide pressure to 25-35 MPa, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under action of ethanol entrainer, extracting Chlorella algae oil, and recovering aqueous phase solution, (c) adding inorganic salt to the aqueous solution to precipitate the protein in Chlorella, and obtaining crude Chlorella protein after filtration, and (d) removing inorganic salt by evaporation, and recovering Chlorella protein powder.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting protein from Chlorella to obtain Chlorella protein powder, which is used in sweet potato-Chlorella meal replacement powder (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures extraction rate of algae oil of 80-90%, prepares meal replacement powder which is rich in dietary fiber, and has high protein content, and balanced nutrition, and can provide various dietary fibers and nutrients required by consumers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting protein from Chlorella involves (a) adding 0.1-1 %mass enzyme, and distilled water with a material-to-liquid ratio g/ml of 1:30-1:60 to Chlorella powder, adjusting a pH of the solution, soaking for 12-24 hours, performing enzymatic hydrolysis in a water bath at 40-50 degrees C, crushing cell wall of Chlorella, and inactivating enzymatic hydrolysis solution at 80-90 degrees C for 5-15 minutes to obtain a mixed solution after enzymolysis is completed, (b) putting mixed liquid into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, maintaining fixed kettle at 35-45 degrees C, and valve temperature of 90-97 degrees C, adjusting carbon dioxide pressure to 25-35 MPa, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under action of ethanol entrainer, extracting Chlorella algae oil, and recovering aqueous phase solution, (c) adding inorganic salt to the aqueous solution to precipitate the protein in Chlorella, and obtaining crude Chlorella protein after filtration, and (d) removing inorganic salt by evaporation under reduced pressure, and recovering Chlorella protein powder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of Chlorella protein powder prepared by the extraction method in sweet potato Chlorella meal replacement powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves performing enzymatic hydrolysis on mixed solution using enzyme such as alkaline protease, papain or neutral protease for 0.5-2 hours, where the pH of the solution is 6-8. The step (b) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under action of ethanol entrainer in an addition amount of 20-30% for 3-6 hours. The method involves performing reduced pressure evaporation in a triple-effect evaporator. The sweet potato-Chlorella meal replacement powder is prepared by washing sweet potato, oats, black tartary buckwheat, kudzu root, hawthorn, balsam pear powder, and notoginseng respectively, using far-infrared hot air oven for drying, stir-frying it, crushing it, setting it aside, weighing crushed sweet potato powder, oatmeal powder, black tartary buckwheat powder, kudzu powder, hawthorn powder, balsam pear powder, notoginseng powder, and prepared Chlorella protein powder, and pulverizing it in an ultrafine pulverizer by micro pulverization treatment, using a sieve of 80-160 mesh to sieving sweet potato powder, oat powder, black tartary buckwheat, kudzu root powder, hawthorn powder, balsam pear powder, notoginseng powder, and Chlorella protein powder, mixing above-mentioned raw materials in a three-dimensional mixer, performing sterilization and packaging sweet potato-Chlorella meal replacement powder. Preferred Component: The inorganic salt is ammonium sulfate, sodium chloride, sodium sulfate or ammonium chloride. The sweet potato Chlorella meal replacement powder comprises 20-40 pts. wt. sweet potato powder, 5-15 pts. wt. Chlorella protein powder, 10-15 pts. wt. oatmeal powder, 10-15 pts. wt. black tartary buckwheat powder, 5-10 pts. wt. kudzu root powder, 5-10 pts. wt. hawthorn powder, 5-10 pts. wt. bitter melon powder, and 5-10 pts. wt. notoginseng powder.

PN CN110386853-A
TI Processing coupling process for preparing synthesis gas by methane oxidation-coupled ethylene and methane dry-reforming comprises e.g. introducing feed gas containing methane and oxygen, and separating unreacted carbon dioxide.
AU LI N
   WANG Y
   JIANG J
   DENG B
AE LUOYANG INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (LUOY-C)
GA 2019924802
AB    NOVELTY - Processing coupling process for preparing synthesis gas by methane oxidation-coupled ethylene and methane dry-reforming comprises e.g. introducing feed gas containing methane and oxygen into the methane oxidative coupling reactor, where the methane oxidative coupling reaction under the action of catalyst, carrying out catalytic conversion of methane and oxygen to obtain carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, ethane and ethylene, separating the reacted gas by moisture by the methane oxidative coupling cooling unit, conveying into the ethylene separation unit, separating ethylene, ethane and high boiling hydrocarbon products, preheating the remaining mixed gas through the preheating unit, then introducing into the methane dry reforming reactor, removing the gas leaving the methane dry reforming reactor by the methane dry reforming cooling unit, separating the synthesis gas by synthesis gas separation unit, and simultaneously separating unreacted carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for processing coupling process for preparing synthesis gas by methane oxidation-coupled ethylene and methane dry-reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is convenient to separate the target product; and saves lot of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing coupling process for preparing synthesis gas by methane oxidation-coupled ethylene and methane dry-reforming comprises introducing feed gas containing methane and oxygen into the methane oxidative coupling reactor, where the methane oxidative coupling reaction under the action of catalyst, carrying out catalytic conversion of methane and oxygen to obtain carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, ethane and ethylene, separating the reacted gas by moisture by the methane oxidative coupling cooling unit, conveying into the ethylene separation unit, separating ethylene, ethane and high boiling hydrocarbon products, preheating the remaining mixed gas through the preheating unit, then introducing into the methane dry reforming reactor, removing the gas leaving the methane dry reforming reactor by the methane dry reforming cooling unit, separating the synthesis gas by synthesis gas separation unit, simultaneously separating unreacted carbon dioxide, mixing with the mixed gas obtained at the outlet of the ethylene separation unit, sending to the preheating unit and continuously producing syngas, adding carbon dioxide to adjust the molar ratio of methane to carbon dioxide for syngas production, where the methane oxidative coupling process occurring in the methane oxidative coupling reactor and the methane dry reforming process occurring in the methane dry reforming reactor are in two separate spaces, the methane oxidative coupling reactor encloses the methane dry reforming reactor, and conducting heat coupling and maintaining heat transfer relationship.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material gas containing methane and oxygen is obtained by premixing raw material gas containing methane with raw material gas containing oxygen, obtained by directly feeding a raw material gas containing methane and a raw material gas containing oxygen into a methane oxidation coupling reactor. The methane oxidative coupling reactor is tubular fixed bed reactor or fluidized bed reactor. The methane dry reforming reactor is tubular fixed bed reactor or fluidized bed reactor. The feed gas of methane and oxygen includes pure methane or methane-rich gas, pure oxygen or oxygen-containing gas. The methane feed gas is derived from gas mixtures of natural gas, oil field gas, refinery gas, coke oven gas and/or biogas. The catalyst particle size is 10-500 mu m. Preferred Method: In the reaction process in the methane oxidation coupling reactor, the molar ratio of methane to oxygen is 2:1-10:1, reaction temperature is 600-900 degrees C, pressure is 0-1 MPa and the speed is 1000-100000 ml.g1.h1. In the operation of methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reactor, the molar ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 1:1-1.5:1, reaction temperature is 600-900 degrees C, the pressure is 0-3 MPa and the space velocity of raw material gas is 1000-100000 ml.g1.h1.

PT P
PN WO2022050587-A1; KR2022032214-A
TI Producing calcium carbonate useful in agent for supplementing calcium by mixing calcined shells, seawater and sugar to elute calcium, and injecting carbon dioxide into generated calcium eluate.
AU KIM M J
   KIM G Y
   KIM S H
   SHIN S M
   JIN K M
   KIM K
AE UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND-ACADEMIC COOP (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND ACADEMIC COOP (UYKR-C)
GA 2022353332
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing calcium carbonate involves (a) eluting calcium by mixing calcined shells, seawater and sugar and (b) generating calcium carbonate by injecting carbon dioxide into the generated calcium eluate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing vaterite-type calcium carbonate which is useful in calcium agent (claimed) used for supplementing calcium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical as it does not require an additional calcium carbonate grinding process and produces porous, nano-sized vaterite type calcium carbonate, which exhibits an absorption rate in the body superior to that of existing micro-sized calcium preparation and existing calcite crystalline calcium preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vaterite content of calcium carbonate is 100% when the solid-liquid ratio of sugar and seawater added in step (a) is 1:80 (g:ml) or less. The amount of calcium elution is increased in step (a). The grain size of vaterite crystals is 600-800 nm when the solid-liquid ratio of the sugar and seawater added in step (a) is 1:5000-1:500 (g:ml). The pH after the completion of step (a) is 12.5 or more. The step (b) involves applying ultrasonic waves to the solution into which carbon dioxide is injected. The method further involves stirring the produced calcium carbonate at room temperature after the carbonation reaction of step (b), where the stirring is performed for 60 minutes or less. Preferred Product: The calcium carbonate has a particle size of 600-800 nm.
   ACTIVITY - Anabolic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Hundred ml seawater and sugar were mixed and dissolved so that the solid-liquid ratio of sugar and seawater was 1:5000, and then seawater in which sugar was dissolved and calcined calcium oxide were mixed so that the solid-liquid ratio was 1:50. Then, the mixture was stirred at 25 degrees C and 200 rpm for 1 hour. The filtration was performed with a 0.45 mu m membrane filter and the filtered calcium eluate was placed in a beaker, stirred at 400 rpm using a stirrer, and 99.9% carbon dioxide was injected using a gas disperser at a flow rate of 0.15 l/minute. Using a gas flow meter and flow regulator, the flow rate was adjusted to constant and if ultrasound was required, it was applied at 30% intensity using a Branson SFX 550 model equipped with a 1/4-diameter tip, and ultrasound was first activated before carbon dioxide was injected. The carbonation reaction was stopped by stopping the injection of carbon dioxide gas, and stabilization was carried out by stirring at 200 rpm for 10 minutes. The resulting solid was filtered through a 0.1 mu m membrane filter and dried at 60 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain vaterite-type calcium carbonate.

PN JP2021137791-A
TI Producing carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic particles used for detoxifying toxic substance, by adding gallium oxide particles and silver source to reducing solution and irradiating reaction solution with ultrasonic waves to prepare gallium oxide particles carrying metallic silver nanoparticles.
AU NISHIMOTO H
   ABE Y
   TANAKA T
   TERAMURA K
AE SUMITOMO METAL MINING CO (SUMM-C)
   UNIV KYOTO (KYOU-C)
GA 2021A6472W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic particles involves preparing gallium oxide particles and silver source, preparing a reaction solution by adding the gallium oxide particles and silver source to a reducing solution and irradiating the reaction solution with ultrasonic waves to prepare gallium oxide particles carrying metallic silver nanoparticles.
   USE - The photocatalytic particles is used for detoxifying hazardous and toxic substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high dispersion and the high support rate, is environmentally-friendly and stimulates a charge separation by supporting the nanoparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver source comprises silver oxide. Preferred Conditions: The frequency of the ultrasonic wave is 28-45 kHz. The ultrasonic irradiation is performed for 1-10 hours. The gallium oxide particles supporting the metallic silver nanoparticles are heat-treated at a temperature of 100 degrees C or higher in an oxygen-containing atmosphere. Preferred Composition: The average particle size of metal silver nanoparticles is 10-50 nm. The addition amount of the metallic silver nanoparticles supported is 0.3-10 %mass with respect to the gallium oxide particles. In the carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst performance evaluation test, the carbon monoxide selectivity of the carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst particles is 30-70 %or more. The photocatalytic particles has a peak in the wavelength range of 350-550 nm in the diffuse reflection spectrum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing solution comprises alcohols.
   EXAMPLE - Gallium oxide particles (1 g) and 5 mg silver oxide were added to 50 ml ethanol to produce a reaction liquid. The reaction liquid was put into an ultrasonic device and subjected to ultrasonic waves at a power of 100 W for 0-3 hours. The temperature of the reaction liquid was maintained at 40 degrees C. The silver oxide in the reaction liquid was reduced to silver. The product was filtered, washed using 10 ml ethanol, dried at 60 degrees C in air for 0.5 hour to obtain metal silver nanoparticle supported gallium oxide particles.

   USE - The catalyst is used for preparing synthesis gas by reforming methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product improves catalytic activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing catalyst comprising hydrothermally crystallizing nitrate precursor of nickel, magnesium and aluminum in mixed solution of alkaline ammonia water, sodium carbonate and ethylene glycol to obtain catalyst precursor, calcining, and reducing.

Preferred Method: The method involves stirring and mixing nickel nitrate hexahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate to dissolve in water, adding concentrated ammonia water, sodium carbonate and ethylene glycol, and hydrothermally crystallizing at 160-200 degrees C for 12-36 hours to obtain catalyst precursor, washing, drying at 60-120 degrees C for 6-24 hours, calcining at 500-800 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and reducing at 700-800 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours at 10 vol.% hydrogen/argon mixed gas.

PN WO2020118438-A1; CA3121398-A1; US2022023805-A1
TI Ultrafiltration/nanofiltration membrane for            filtering feed, comprises active layer arranged on            porous support layer formed using randomly arranged            cellulose nanocrystals, and carbon-dioxide-responsive            polymer grafted on nanocrystals.
AU FARNIA F
   ZHAO Y
AE SOC COMMERCIALISATION PROD RECH APPLIQUE (COMM-Non-standard)
   SOC COMMERCIALISATION PROD RECH APPLIQUE (COMM-Non-standard)
GA 2020543018
AB    NOVELTY - An ultrafiltration/nanofiltration membrane                comprises an active layer arranged on a porous                support layer (L1) formed using randomly arranged                cellulose nanocrystals, and a                carbon-dioxide-responsive polymer whose chains are                grafted on the surface of the cellulose                nanocrystals. The pores are defined in the active                layer by the free spaces existing between the                randomly arranged cellulose nanocrystals.
   USE - Ultrafiltration/nanofiltration membrane used                for filtering feed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ultrafiltration/nanofiltration membrane is                inexpensive, and has excellent                biodegradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) filtration of feed;
   (2) tuning of apparent pore size; and
   (3) manufacture of the membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane further                  comprises a porous support layer (L2), where the                  active layer is arranged between the porous                  support layers (L1) and (L2). The cellulose                  nanocrystals are covalently bonded to the porous                  support layer (L1). The porous support layers                  (L1) and (L2) are silver membranes, alumina oxide                  membranes, cellulose acetate membranes, mixed                  cellulose ester (MCE) membranes, nylon membranes,                  polyacrylonitrile (PAN) membranes,                  polyethersulfone (PES) membranes, polyester                  (PETE) hydrophilic membrane,                  polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) membranes,                  polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) hydrophilic                  membranes, glass fiber (GF) membranes and                  polycarbonate (PCTE) membranes, preferably                  cellulose acetate membranes. The active layer is                  formed of a porous polyether sulfone (PES),                  cellulose acetate, nylon, cellulose nitrate, or                  polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) membrane. The                  carbon dioxide-responsive polymer includes                  organobase functional groups, amidine, amine                  and/or guanidine functional groups, and comprises                  poly((2-dimethylamino)ethyl methacrylate),                  poly(2-(diethylamino)ethyl methacrylate), or                  poly(3-N',N'-dimethylaminopropyl acrylamide),                  preferably poly((2-diethylamino)ethyl                  methacrylate). When the carbon dioxide-responsive                  polymer includes amidine functional groups, the                  carbon dioxide-responsive polymer comprises                  poly(N-methyltetrahydropyrimidine),                  poly(p-azidomethylstyrene-co-styrene), poly                  (2-methyl-1-(4-vinylbenzyl)-1,4,5,6-tetrahydropyrimidine),                  poly(dimethylacrylamide-co-(N-amidino)ethyl                  acrylamide) or polyethylene                  oxide-b-poly((N-amidino)dodecyl acrylamide). When                  the carbon dioxide-responsive polymer bears                  guanidine, the carbon dioxide-responsive polymer                  comprises poly(L-arginine methyl                  ester-acrylamide-co-N-cyclopropyl acrylamide).                  When the carbon dioxide-responsive polymer                  includes weak-acid carboxyl functional groups,                  the carbon dioxide-responsive polymer comprises                  polymethacrylic acid (PMAA). Preferred                  Properties: The cellulose nanocrystals have a                  width of 2-20 nm, preferably 5-10 and a length of                  100-600 nm, preferably 100-200 nm. The active                  layer has a thickness of 0.5-15 mu m, preferably                  1-6 mu m. The chains of the                  carbon-dioxide-responsive polymer have a                  molecular weight of 5000-25000 Da, preferably                  14500 Da. The membrane has an apparent pore size                  of 0.5-100 nm, preferably 3-10 nm, and a                  molecular weight cut-off of 1000-500000 Da,                  preferably 20000-100000 Da, a pore size                  tunability of 10-70%, preferably about 50%,                  molecular weight cut-off tunability of 20-65%,                  preferably 50%, and a flow-rate tunability of                  2-10, preferably 5.

PN CN111100098-A
TI Preparing 5-(hydroxymethyl)furfural from            dehydration of fructose comprises adding fructose,            deionized water and methyl isobutyl ketone in            high-pressure stirring kettle, stirring, introducing            carbon dioxide, reacting, and converting fructose.
AU ZHANG L
   ZHAO C
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 202040374E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 5-(hydroxymethyl)furfural from                dehydration of fructose comprises adding fructose,                deionized water and methyl isobutyl ketone into a                high-pressure stirring kettle, stirring uniformly                after fully dissolving, introducing carbon dioxide,                and reacting for a period of time at a given                temperature, and converting fructose into product                5-(hydroxymethyl)furfural.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                5-(hydroxymethyl)furfural from dehydration of                fructose.
   ADVANTAGE - 5-(hydroxymethyl)furfural has high yield of                90%. The method has simple operation, mild reaction                condition, and low operation cost, and does not                cause environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of water and                  methyl isobutyl ketone is 1-1:12, the amount of                  fructose is 0.2-10 g, and the ratio of the amount                  of mixed solvent to fructose (ml/g) is 1-13.                  Preferred Method: The stirring time is 4-5 hours;                  the reaction temperature is 160-220 degrees C;                  the reaction time is 20-180 minutes; reaction                  system pressure is 0.5-4.5 MPa. After finishing                  the reaction, cooling the reaction system, and                  restoring the pressure in the system to normal                  pressure.

PT P
PN CN110420291-A
TI Preparing Chinese medicine used for preparing Chinese medicine to regulate five Zang-organs and six Fu-organs by crushing medicinal material, extracting active ingredients by supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction and fermenting extract.
AU WANG Y
AE TIANJIN WENKE YONGKANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201996279G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a Chinese medicine involves selecting Chinese medicinal materials, crushing the materials at low temperature, extracting active ingredients of the medicinal materials using supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction method, and fermenting medicinal extracts.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Chinese medicine for regulating five Zang-organs and six Fu-organs (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for Chinese medicine, which comprises ginseng, Epimedium, rhizoma typhonii, radix achyranthis bidentatae, Ganoderma, Chinese dodder seed, grape seed, dried orange peel, pseudo-ginseng, fructus psoraleae, Dendrobium, licorice and balloon flower or radix rehmanniae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The Chinese medicine comprises 12-20 pts. wt. ginseng, 12-20 pts. wt. Epimedium, 12-20 pts. wt. rhizoma typhonii, 12-20 pts. wt. radix achyranthis bidentatae, 12-20 pts. wt. Ganoderma, 3-7 pts. wt. Chinese dodder seed, 3-7 pts. wt. grape seed, 3-7 pts. wt. dried orange peel, 3-7 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng, 2-4 pts. wt. fructus psoraleae, 2-4 pts. wt. Dendrobium, 2-4 pts. wt. licorice and 2-4 pts. wt. balloon flower or 2-4 pts. wt. radix rehmanniae polysaccharide. Preferred Method: The method involves crushing the materials at low temperature, controlling pulverizing process of the medicinal material to not greater than 10 degrees C, performing supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction process with simultaneously usage of ultrasonic vibration. The method involves fermenting the extract with an inoculation of bacterial strain chosen from Bifidobacterium, lactic acid bacteria, active lactic acid bacteria, Lactobacillus acidophilus, L.plantarum, L.bulgaricus, L.delbrueckii, Clostridium butyricum and fungi in a strain or their combinations, preferably Bifidobacterium, active lactic acid bacteria, L.acidophilus and L.plantarum. The method involves crushing at 10 degrees C, performing supercritical CO2 extraction by ultrasonic vibration at a frequency of 25-50 Hz, humidifying the extract with ultra-small molecular water to form a paste-like fermentation substrate in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-3, adding 8-12 wt.% beneficial bacteria to the fermentation substrate, fermenting at 25-40 degrees C for 8-12 hours, drying fermented extract, dissolving the mixture, decolorizing the mixture, and filtering the mixture. The method further involves adding fermentation aid to the fermentation substrate comprising 1-3 pts. wt. salicin, 0.2-1 pt. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 1-3 pts. wt. agar powder and 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. vitamin B1.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiant; Hepatotropic; Respiratory-Gen.; Nephrotropic; Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Uropathic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108485813-A
TI Rapid extraction of Litsea cubeba volatile oil by            crushing Litsea cubeba fruit, sieving, extracting with            ethanol, controlling mass flow rate of carbon dioxide,            decompressing extracting solution and            concentrating.
AU 
AE QIDONG CHUANGLV LANDSCAPE ENG CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018712755
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid extraction of Litsea cubeba volatile oil                comprises crushing Litsea cubeba fruit, passing by                80 sieve, putting into an extracting kettle with                85-95 vol.% ethanol, extracting at 65-75 degrees C                and 25-27 MPa for 2-3 hours, controlling the mass                flow rate of carbon dioxide at 57-59 kg/hour,                decompressing the extracting solution and                concentrating to obtain the product.
   USE - Method for rapid extraction of Litsea cubeba                volatile oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, can                quickly prepare volatilizable oil from Litsea                cubeba fruits, and can be prepared on a large                scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes                  putting into an extracting kettle with 90 vol.%                  ethanol extraction. The extracting step is                  carried out at 26 MPa and 70 degrees C for 2.5                  hours. The carbon dioxide mass flow rate is                  controlled at 58 kg/hour. 6-8 l Solvent are used                  to extract 1 kg Litsea cubeba fruit.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PT P
PN CN108440485-A; CN108440485-B
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate, comprises e.g. using            epoxide and carbon dioxide as main raw material, and            preparing final product.
AU DING Y
   WANG Y
   WANG W
   YAN L
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201868201B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate, comprises using                epoxide and carbon dioxide as main raw material,                preparing final product, where the catalyst used is                metal porphyrin and multifunctional bionic                multiphase based organic porous copolymer of                quaternary phosphonium salt catalyst, the catalyst                carrier is prepared by mixing an                alkene-functionalized metalloporphyrin with an                olefin-containing quaternary phosphonium salt to                form an organic porous polymer, the active                component of the catalyst is metal porphyrin in                organic porous polymers (as Lewis acid activated                epoxy compounds) with a quaternary phosphonium salt                (as a ring-opening nucleophile) functional group,                and the resulting multifunctional biomimetic                heterogeneous catalyst performs well in the                reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The organic copolymer                  obtained by copolymerizing a metal porphyrin and                  an olefin group-containing quaternary phosphonium                  salt monomer in a heterogeneous catalyst in a                  molar ratio of 1:1-1:50 as a carrier, the metal                  porphyrin and quaternary phosphonium salt                  functional groups are used as active components,                  i.e. the organic copolymer serves as a carrier                  and as an active component. The preparation of                  heterogeneous catalyst, comprises (a) at 273-473                  K (preferably 298-373 K), and under an inert                  atmosphere (argon gas and/or nitrogen gas),                  adding alkene-functionalized porphyrin monomer                  into organic solvent, adding an organic base and                  Lewis metal precursor, stirring at room                  temperature for 6-24 hours, filtering the                  resulting mixture, washing with water, removing                  the solvent at 25-100 degrees C under vacuum                  condition, (b) under an inert atmosphere (argon                  gas and/or nitrogen gas ) at 273-473 K                  (preferably 298-373 K) adding an                  alkene-functionalized metalloporphyrin and an                  alkene-containing quaternary phosphonium salt                  with or without a crosslinking agent into organic                  solvent, adding free radical initiator, mixing,                  stirring resulting mixture for 0.1-100 hours                  (preferably 0.1-1 hour), (c) transferring the                  mixed solution obtained in step (b) to a                  synthetic autoclave, under an inert atmosphere                  (argon gas and/or nitrogen gas), then carrying                  out solvothermal polymerization at 323-473 K,                  allowing to stand for 1-100 hours (preferably                  20-80 hours), and carrying out polymerization                  reaction to obtain organic copolymer containing                  metal porphyrin and quaternary phosphonium salt,                  and (d) vacuum extracting the solvent in the                  copolymer obtained in step (b) at room                  temperature to obtain organic copolymer                  containing metal porphyrin and quaternary                  phosphonium salt having a multistage pore                  structure, i.e. multifunctional pseudo-phase                  heterogeneous catalyst for catalyzing a coupling                  reaction of an epoxy compound with carbon                  dioxide. The reaction process comprises adding                  catalyst into a reactor, feeding raw material                  gas, where the raw material gas is gas containing                  carbon dioxide, the liquid raw material is epoxy                  compound, the reaction temperature is 323-573 K                  (preferably 373-413 K), the reaction pressure is                  0.1-10 Mpa (preferably 1-4 Mpa), and the gas                  space velocity is 100-20000 h(-1) (preferably                  1000-3000 h(-1)), the reaction of the epoxide                  with carbon dioxide is carried out under                  conditions of a liquid having space velocity of                  0.01-10 h(-1) (preferably 0.5-5 h(-1)), and the                  reactor is a fixed bed, a slurry bed, a trickle                  bed or tank reactor. The reactor is a fixed bed,                  slurry bed, trickle bed or tank reactor; the main                  component of the raw material gas is carbon                  dioxide, and the volume content of carbon dioxide                  is 20-100 wt.% (95-100 wt.%) (the remaining is                  nitrogen gas and/or an inert gas; the purity of                  the liquid raw material epoxy compound is 20-100                  wt.% (remaining are dissolved hydrocarbons, ethyl                  chloride, chloropropane, acetaldehyde,                  propionaldehyde, ethylene oxide, 1-chloropropane,                  2-chloropropane and/or 1,2-butylene oxide), and                  the epoxy compound raw material having a purity                  of 99-100%. Preferred Components: The                  functionalized functional group of olefinic                  functionalized metalloporphyrin is                  tetraphenylvinyl functional group, the metal is                  magnesium, cobalt, zinc, aluminum and/or                  chromium; the metal porphyrin is magnesium                  porphyrin, the quaternary phosphonium salt                  containing an olefin group is                  methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) ruthenium chloride,                  methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) cesium bromide,                  methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) cesium iodide,                  ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) ruthenium chloride,                  ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) cesium bromide and/or                  ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) iodide; and the                  quaternary phosphonium salt is preferably                  ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene) cesium bromide. The                  multifunctional biomimetic heterogeneous catalyst                  having a multi-stage pore structure, specific                  surface area of 10-3000 m2/g with large, medium                  and micropores, pore volume of 0.1-5 cm3/g and                  pore size distribution of 0.2-50 nm. In step (a),                  the organic solvent is dichloromethane,                  chloroform, benzene, toluene, methanol, ethanol                  and/or tetrahydrofuran; the organic base is                  triethylamine, triethylenediamine,                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5,4,0)undec-7-ene and/or                  4-dimethylaminopyridine; the metal precursor is                  magnesium bromide diethyl ether, zinc acetate                  hydrate, cobalt chloride hydrate, aluminum                  chloride and/or chromium chloride; in step (b),                  the organic solvent is benzene, toluene,                  methanol, ethanol, tetrahydrofuran,                  dichloromethane and/or chloroform; the                  crosslinking agent is styrene, ethylene,                  propylene, divinylbenzene and/or                  1,3,5-triacetylenylbenzene; and the radical                  initiator is cyclohexanone peroxide, dibenzoyl                  peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide,                  azobisisobutyronitrile and/or                  azobisisoheptanenitrile. In step (a), the molar                  ratio between metal precursor and                  olefin-functionalized porphyrin monomer is 1:1,                  and the molar ratio between organic base and                  metal precursor is 10:1-1:1 (preferably 3:1-1:1).                  In step (b), the molar ratio between                  olefin-functionalized metalloporphyrin and                  olefin-containing quaternary phosphonium salt is                  0.01:1-100:1 (preferably 0.1:1-1:1), in the case                  of addition of a crosslinking agent, the molar                  ratio between metalloporphyrin and crosslinking                  agent is 0.01:1-10:1 (preferably 0.1:1-1:1), the                  molar ratio between metalloporphyrin and radical                  initiator is 300:1-10:1 (preferably 50:1-10:1),                  before the polymerization into an organic                  copolymer, the concentration of the                  metalloporphyrin in the organic solvent is                  0.01-1000 g/l (preferably 0.1-10 g/l); after the                  polymerization into an organic copolymer, the                  amount of the metal supported is 0.01-10 wt.%                  (preferably 0.01-0.2 wt.%).
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Production of sensor for e.g. detecting carbon dioxide comprises forming noble metal electrodes on silicon substrate, connecting electrodes to power source, and forming nano-network of functionalized nanowires.
AU ALAM M M
   SAMPATHKUMARAN U
AE INNOSENSE LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 201855923F
AB    NOVELTY - Production of sensor comprises forming first and second noble metal electrode on silicon substrate, connecting electrodes to power source and current/voltage measuring device, and forming nano-network of functionalized nanowires or nanotubes in situ. Electrodes are separated by 0.5-4 mu m gap. Nanowire/nanotube network spans gap and provides electrically conductive pathway that connects first and second noble metal electrodes.
   USE - Method for producing sensor for detecting carbon dioxide and mercury, and sensing presence of disease biomarker indicating existence of cancer, hepatitis or Alzheimer's, Parkinson's or cardiac disease.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Electrolyte for formic acid, acetic acid, perchloric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid and nitric acid aqueous solution, preferably 0.4-0.6 mol/L nitric acid.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The nano-network is formed from amine functionalized aniline monomers. The nanowire network for detecting Alzheimer's disease or cancer is formed from N-hydroxyphathalo-succinimide or functionalized N-hydroxyphathalo-succinimide polypyrrole nanowires.
