PT P
PN CN114249634-A
TI Phenol refining of low phenol content wastewater discharge comprises e.g. transporting crude phenate from crude phenol salt groove to deoiling distillation process, reacting refined phenolate with carbon dioxide in blast furnace gas, and adopting oil-water separation in decomposition step.
AU GUO H
   LU W
   MENG L
   LEI C
   WANG L
   CHEN X
AE BAOWU CARBON MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOW-Non-standard)
GA 2022507731
AB    NOVELTY - Phenol refining of low phenol content wastewater discharge comprises transporting the crude phenate of the raw material from the crude phenol salt groove to the deoiling distillation process, removing part of the oil and water by distillation to produce low-concentration phenol-containing wastewater (i), entering the generated refined phenate to the carbon dioxide decomposition step, reacting the refined phenolate with the carbon dioxide in the blast furnace gas in the carbon dioxide decomposition step, entering main product crude phenol to continuous distillation step, distilling crude phenol in the continuous distillation process to produce high-concentration phenol-containing waste water with concentration of 6-15%, and adopting the oil-water separation in the sulfuric acid decomposition step to static separation method, where the separation temperature of the sodium sulfate solution (iii) produced in the sulfuric acid decomposition step and the crude phenol is 35-55degrees Celsius.
   USE - The method is useful for phenol refining of low phenol content wastewater discharge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: studies the temperature of the phenol-water conjugated two-phase solution; improves to determine the optimum temperature for separating the phenol-refined sodium carbonate solution and the sodium sulfate solution from the crude phenol; reduces the phenol carried away by the aqueous phase in the process of allowing to stand and stratification; directly recycles the high-concentration phenol-containing wastewater from phenol distillation and other processes by using the optimized phenol refining process to realize the discharge of low-phenol-containing wastewater from the phenol refining process; and improves the phenol yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Phenol refining of low phenol content wastewater discharge comprises transporting the crude phenate of the raw material from the crude phenol salt groove to the deoiling distillation process, removing part of the oil and water by distillation to produce low-concentration phenol-containing wastewater (i), entering the generated refined phenate to the carbon dioxide decomposition step, reacting the refined phenolate with the carbon dioxide in the blast furnace gas in the carbon dioxide decomposition step, keeping the amount of carbon dioxide in the reaction higher than the theoretical value by 20-50%, producing sodium carbonate solution (ii) containing 0.5-2.5% phenol, entering the main product roughly decomposes phenol to the sulfuric acid decomposition step, reacting crude decomposed phenol with sulfuric acid in sulfuric acid decomposition step, keeping the amount of sulfuric acid higher than theoretical value 10-30% in the reaction, producing sodium sulfate solution (iii), entering main product crude phenol to continuous distillation step, distilling crude phenol in the continuous distillation process to produce high-concentration phenol-containing waste water with concentration of 6-15%, simultaneously generating o-cresol product and m-p-cresol product, entering the phenol fraction, xylenol fraction and heavy phenol produced in the continuous distillation step to the batch distillation step for distillation, producing part of high-concentration phenol-containing wastewater (v) with concentration of 20-95%, generating coking phenol products and xylenol products at the same time, sending the high-concentration phenol-containing waste water (iv) produced in the continuous distillation process and the high-concentration phenol-containing waste water (v) produced in the batch distillation process to the crude phenate tank, mixing with the raw material crude phenol, reacting free base in the salt to regenerate the phenate, recycling, adopting the oil-water separation of carbon dioxide decomposition step to static separation method, where the separation temperature of the sodium carbonate solution (ii) and crude phenol that carbon dioxide decomposition step (3) produces is 55-75degrees Celsius, and adopting the oil-water separation in the sulfuric acid decomposition step to static separation method, where the separation temperature of the sodium sulfate solution (iii) produced in the sulfuric acid decomposition step and the crude phenol is 35-55degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sodium carbonate solution (ii) produced in the carbon dioxide decomposition step can be reacted with calcium oxide through causticization process to produce calcium carbonate and sodium hydroxide solutions and then recycled. The raw material crude phenate is subjected to the deoiling distillation process to produce low-concentration phenol-containing wastewater (i) with concentration of 0.03-0.15%. The sodium sulfate solution (iii) produced in the sulfuric acid decomposition step is discharged as waste water. The raw material crude phenate is the most common crude phenate in the coking industry at present, and its content is at the level of 8-25%. In the deoiling distillation step, the temperature at the top of the deoiling distillation tower is 95-100degrees Celsius, and the temperature at the bottom of the tower is 105-115degrees Celsius. The continuous distillation operation is technique in which five vacuum distillation towers are connected in series, and the first distillation tower removes the moisture in the crude phenol to produce high-concentration phenol-containing waste water (iv) with partial concentration of 6-15%. The second distillation column removes the heavy components in the crude phenol to produce heavy phenol. The third distillation column separates the phenolic component in the crude phenol to produce phenolic fraction. The fourth distillation column separates the o-cresol component in the crude phenol to produce the o-cresol product. The fifth distillation column separates the m-cresol component of the crude phenol to produce m-p-cresol product and xylenol fraction. In the batch distillation operation, each still is underpressure distillation, the vacuum degree is different because the raw material is different, the temperature parameters are slightly different, and some high-concentration phenol-containing wastewater (v) with concentration of 20-95% is produced in the early stage of heating, coking phenol product with purity of 99.5-99.9% after the column top temperature is stabilized, and xylenol product with purity of 88-95% are generated.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC A01-E13; E11-Q01B; E10-E02B1; E34-D01; E31-N05C; E31-F05
IP C07C-037/68; C07C-039/04; C07C-039/07

The principle is simple, the                universality is strong, by optimizing aggregate,                gel material proportion and improving the                mechanical property of the auxiliary material,                preparing each age strength satisfy the filling                requirement at the same time with efficient                absorption of CO2 performance of the filling body,                so as to comprehensively process the industrial                solid waste mining area, at the time, recycling the                resource. The method provides a new idea for                reducing the carbon mine and green mine.

PN KR2022089192-A; KR2466619-B1
TI Covalent organic framework comprising            porphyrin-based repeating unit, useful in preparing            visible light absorbing photocatalyst as composition to            convert oxidoreductase cofactor for producing formic            acid from carbon dioxide.
AU BAEG J
   KIM J Y
   PARK N J
   YADAV D
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202285066R
AB    NOVELTY - Covalent organic framework comprising                porphyrin-based repeating unit (I), where the                porphyrin-based repeating unit (I) is connected by                cross-linking alkene-based repeating unit (IV) and                cross-linking styrene-based repeating unit                (V).
   USE - The framework is useful in preparing visible                light absorbing photocatalyst as composition to                convert oxidoreductase cofactor for producing                formic acid from carbon dioxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The framework improves performance as                photocatalyst by controlling reaction time to                control the thickness of the thin film; contributes                to the thinning of various organic materials; and                is prepared in the form of thin film in which                crystallinity and porosity is maintained even in                easy synthesis environment by overcoming the                disadvantages i.e. difficult to form thin film in                the prior art. The possibility of commercialization                of the carbon dioxide conversion industry using                solar power is further increased in the future. The                photocatalyst absorbs visible light and uses solar                energy to produce cofactors of oxidoreductase with                high efficiency without wasting additional energy                costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Covalent organic framework comprising                porphyrin-based repeating unit of formula (I),                where the porphyrin-based repeating unit (I) is                connected by cross-linking alkene-based repeating                unit of formula (IV) and cross-linking                styrene-based repeating unit of formula (V).
   zig-zag line = bonding position.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) visible light absorbing photocatalyst                comprising the covalent organic framework;
   (2) preparing visible light absorbing                photocatalyst comprising contacting an organic                solution layer containing 5,10, 15,                20-tetra(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin and                2-vinylbenzene-1,4-dicarbaldehyde and an aqueous                solution layer containing glyoxal;
   (3) composition for oxidoreductase cofactor                conversion comprising oxidoreductase cofactor,                redox mediator and visible light absorption                photocatalyst;
   (4) converting oxidoreductase cofactor                comprising adding the oxidoreductase cofactor, the                redox mediator, and the visible light absorbing                photocatalyst in a reactor, and stirring while                applying light;
   (5) producing formic acid from carbon dioxide                comprising adding the composition and formate                dehydrogenase into a reactor and stirring while                applying light under carbon dioxide atmosphere to                produce formic acid; and
   (6) photocatalyst film comprising the visible                light absorbing photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The redox mediator is                  ruthenium(II) complex or rhodium(III)                  complex.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The visible light                  absorbing photocatalyst is in the form of thin                  film. The thin film has thickness of 300-400 nm.                  The oxidoreductase cofactor includes nicotinamide                  adenine dinucleotide, nicotinamide adenine                  dinucleotide phosphate, flavin adenine                  dinucleotide and/or flavin mononucleotide.                  Preferred Method: The contact process is                  performed for 30 seconds to 50 minutes.
   EXAMPLE - 0.5 eq. 5,10, 15,                20-Tetra(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin and 0.5 eq.                2-vinylbenzene-1,4-dicarbaldehyde were dissolved in                25 ml nitrobenzene and sonicated for 20 minutes.                Then, the solution was filtered through 0.5 mu m                polytetrafluoroethylene micro filter to obtain                transparent nitrobenzene solution. Then, the                filtered (5,10, 15,                20-Tetra(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin and                2-vinylbenzene-1,4-dicarbaldehyde dissolved)                nitrobenzene solution was added in a sealed                container, and 1.5 mmol aqueous glyoxal solution                (glyoxal 30%) was added into contact with the                nitrobenzene solution and the glyoxal aqueous                solution. Then, the container was placed in an oven                preheated to 51 degrees C and maintained for 1                minute to form covalent organic framework at the                interface between the two solutions.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)

A support platform is placed above the                floor region of the housing and below the soil,                where the support platform comprises an open                aerated region, and the open aerated region is                formed between the soil and the floor region, and                the open aerated region is adapted to receive                oxygen to provide the oxygen to the soil. An oxygen                or carbon dioxide injection module is connected to                a tubing, where the tubing extends from an upper                region of the housing. The soil is partially filled                below leaves of the plants, where the tubing is                adapted to dispense oxygen or carbon dioxide from                below the leaves of the plants. A circulation fan                module diverts carbon dioxide gases released from a                carbon dioxide module. The support platform                comprises a grid or lattice configuration.
   USE - True living organic (TLO) soil bed growing                system for growing a plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The True living organic (TLO) system solves                problem of preventing opportunities for anaerobic                micro-organism activity from being building up                within the soil, thus creating toxic chemicals, and                hence killing microbes. The True living organic                soil bed growing system improves growth efficiency                of the plant, while oxygen is simultaneously                allowed to penetrate the bottom portion of the                soil. The system allows the soil to determine soil                microbiology improves over time. The casters are                placed under the bed, when the bed is moved in and                out of different growing environments, thus                providing versatility and increased ability to hone                the environment to what the plants are increased to                determine a point, and measures growth cycle. The                True living organic system improves ability to fill                soil over, so that crop can be grown.

PT P
PN CN112441641-A
TI Processing multi-tower serial waste water ammonia evaporation comprises e.g. feeding the wastewater into the first-stage tower and distills ammonia, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, other light components and volatile components.
AU WANG Q
   WANG L
AE NANJING DUNXIAN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   ANHUI JUYUAN MACHINERY EQUIP CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   NANJING DUNXIAN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202125997D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing multi-tower serial waste water ammonia evaporation comprises (i ) feeding the wastewater into the first-stage tower and distills ammonia, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, other light components and volatile components from the top of the tower according to the principle of physical separation, (ii) using secondary tower to separate the vaporized components from the top of the primary tower according to the different solubility of the gas in water, extracting the hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and other light components from the top of the secondary tower and dissolving ammonia in the bottom liquid of the secondary tower, (iii) using three-stage tower to separate ammonia and water in the bottom liquid of the two-stage tower according to the principle of physical separation to obtain ammonia water with an ammonia mass fraction of 1-20%, and (iv) using four-stage tower to absorbs, evaporates and concentrates the ammonia separated from the three-stage tower.
   USE - The method is useful for processing multi-tower serial waste water ammonia evaporation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environment-friendly; and saves water resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing multi-tower serial waste water ammonia evaporation comprises (i ) feeding the wastewater into the first-stage tower and distills ammonia, hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, other light components and volatile components from the top of the tower according to the principle of physical separation, (ii) using secondary tower to separate the vaporized components from the top of the primary tower according to the different solubility of the gas in water, extracting the hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and other light components from the top of the secondary tower and dissolving ammonia in the bottom liquid of the secondary tower, (iii) using three-stage tower to separate ammonia and water in the bottom liquid of the two-stage tower according to the principle of physical separation to obtain ammonia water with an ammonia mass fraction of 1-20%, and (iv) using four-stage tower to absorbs, evaporates and concentrates the ammonia separated from the three-stage tower according to the principle of physical separation to prepare ammonia greater than or equal to 99.98% liquid ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The wastewater in the first-stage tower is heated, the pressure at the top of the tower is controlled at 0.05-0.30 MPa and the pressure at the bottom of the tower is controlled at 0.05-0.40MPa, the temperature at the top of the tower is controlled at 110-160 degrees C and the temperature at the bottom of the tower is controlled at 120-300 degrees C, when the molar fractions of ammonia, H2S and carbon dioxide in the bottom liquid of the first-stage tower are respectively less than or equal to 1PPm, The tower still liquid of the first-stage tower meets the requirements for production cycle use or direct discharge to complete wastewater purification. The second-stage tower is used to separate the components evaporated from the top of the first-stage tower and the pressure at the top of the tower is controlled at 0.05-0.20MPa, the pressure at the bottom of the tower is controlled to 0.05-0.30MPa, the temperature at the top of the tower is controlled at 20-60 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the tower is controlled at 60-90 degrees C, the H2S, CO2 and other light components are discharged from the secondary tower. The top is pumped out and send to the sulfur recovery unit to prepare sulfuric acid or sulfur or send to the torch for combustion. The bottom liquid of the second-stage tower is send to the three-stage tower and the bottom liquid of the three-stage tower is heated, the pressure at the top of the tower is controlled at 0.05-0.20MPa, the pressure at the bottom of the tower is controlled at 0.05-0.50MPa, the temperature at the top of the tower is controlled at 20-60 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the tower is controlled at 120-300 degrees C, when the molar fractions of NH3, H2S and CO2 in the bottom liquid of the three-stage tower are respectively less than 1 ppm, the bottom liquid of the three-stage tower meets production requirements for recycling or direct discharge. The gas phase ammonia extracted from the middle of the three-stage tower is taken at -0.5-3.0MPa and temperature at -60to300 degrees C, the four-stage tower makes gas-phase ammonia into liquid ammonia with a quality of 99.98%. Preferred Components: The first-stage tower, second-stage tower, third-stage tower and fourth-stage tower are all set to one. The internals of the first-stage tower, second-stage tower, third-stage tower and the fourth-stage tower are all are all filled or tower. The first-level tower, second-level tower, third-level tower and four-level tower matched heat exchanger are all set of U-tube, straight tube, coil tube and/or winding tube. The heat supply from bottom of first-stage tower, second-stage tower, third-stage tower and fourth-stage tower set to steam, hot water, heat transfer oil and waste heat. The first-stage tower, second-stage tower, third-stage tower, fourth-stage tower and auxiliary equipment are corrosion-resistant, carbon steel, stainless steel and/or non-metallic substances.

PN RU2736021-C1
TI METHOD OF CONTROLLING COVERAGE OF A FORMATION BY GAS-CYCLIC PUMPING CARBON DIOXIDE AT SUPERCRITICAL CONDITIONS TO A PRODUCTION WELL USING FOAM SYSTEMS.
AU VOLKOV V A
   BELIKOVA V G
   PROKHOROV P E
   TURAPIN A N
   KEROSIROV V M
AE DELTA-PROM LLC (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4404E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to production of hard-to-recover oil and gas condensate reserves. Method involves pumping two fringes - before and after pumping of carbon dioxide into production well. When implementing gas-cyclic injection of carbon dioxide at supercritical conditions - temperature more than 31.1C and pressure of more than 7.38 MPa, pumping of the first fringe is provided to the tubing string of the production well in the form of a reagent containing a surfactant. Then, a solid foaming agent is dosed and dissolved in the liquid contained in the well bore for at least 3 hours. Further, carbon dioxide is pumped under supercritical conditions. Prior to impregnation period, second rim is pumped. Its injection involves pumping to the production tubing string a composition containing a mixture of a corrosion inhibitor and a reagent containing a surfactant. Then, a solid foaming agent is dosed and dissolved in the liquid contained in the well bore for at least 3 hours. After that, the well is closed for the period of impregnation with subsequent extraction of oil from the same production well. First rim is used to inject the ML-super reagent containing the surfactant into the well tubing string to the wellhead. Then, hard foaming agent rods are introduced at rate of 0.2-0.5 kg per 1 m3 of liquid, taking into account the volume of the tubing string for dissolution in the wellbore, and as second fringe is pumped mixture of corrosion inhibitor grade "Inkorgaz-21T-A" and reagent containing surfactant, grade "ML-super" in established ratios and in volume sufficient for filling of tubing string of well, as well as at rate of 1-5 m3 per meter of the perforated interval of the treated header depending on the permeability of the collectors. Then solid foam maker rods are introduced at 0.2-0.5 kg per 1 m3 of liquid taking into account volume of tubing string for dissolution in the well shaft.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing extraction of hard-to-recover oil reserves with reduced content of water and gas condensate due to use of liquid carbon dioxide and foam systems.
1 cl, 3 ex

PN RU2724764-C1
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING NANOSIZED NITROCELLULOSE OR COMPOSITES BASED THEREON, Invention relates to the technology of high-energy materials, specifically to a method of producing nanosized nitrocellulose.
AU ZHARKOV M N
   KUCHUROV I V
   ZLOTIN S G
AE AS RUSSIA ORGANIC CHEM INST (ASOR-Non-standard)
GA 202063465G
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the technology of high-energy materials, specifically to a method of producing nanosized nitrocellulose or composites based on it, consisting in that 13 wt% solution of nitrocellulose in acetone or suspension of carbon nanotubes in 13 wt% solution of nitrocellulose in acetone, or suspension of iron (III) oxide nanoparticles in 13 wt% solution of nitrocellulose in acetone, or suspension of mixture of carbon nanotubes and iron (III) oxide nanoparticles in 13 wt% solution of nitrocellulose in acetone is treated with supercritical carbon dioxide at temperature of 3550C and pressure of 915 MPa, and the process is carried out in a precipitation chamber pre-filled with supercritical carbon dioxide by continuous and simultaneous supply to it of a solution of initial nitrocellulose or a suspension therein through a capillary with an internal diameter of 0.76 mm at rate of 0.14 ml/min and supercritical carbon dioxide at rate of 550 g/min followed by further processing of the end product obtained in the process of precipitation in the form of a powder with a fivefold relative to the precipitation chamber the volume of supercritical carbon dioxide. Method is universal and enables to obtain nanosized nitrocellulose individually and nanocomposites based on it without significant changes in the process scheme and process parameters. Obtained materials are widely used in production of nitrocellulose press powders, powders and other high-energy compositions.
   USE - High-energy materials technology.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is improvement of fire safety of process due to its performance in medium of incombustible, thermally and chemically stable carbon dioxide, simplification of process due to elimination of stage of washing and drying of target product, complete absence of waste water.

PN CN111022006-A
TI Method for improving recovery factor of shale oil wells involves adding oil detergent, hot water from casing, shut downing, injecting ammonium chloride or potassium chloride or liquid carbon dioxide from casing and immersing well.
AU ZHAO J
   KANG Z
   ZHAO M
   HU L
AE DAQING JINJU PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 202033874L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving recovery factor of shale oil wells involves adding 200 kg oil detergent from casing when pumping unit is in normal operation, adding hot water above 20-30 m at 370 degrees C from casing, shut downing the well after 48 hours of normal operation, injecting ammonium chloride or potassium chloride having mass concentration of 10-20% anti-swelling agent liquid from the casing, injecting liquid carbon dioxide from the casing through the electric heating tube, maintaining injection temperature of liquid carbon dioxide to above 10 degrees C, injecting low pressure slowly and immersing the well for 30 days.
   USE - Method for improving recovery factor of shale oil wells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving recovery factor of shale oil wells involves adding 200 kg oil detergent from casing when pumping unit is in normal operation, adding hot water above 20-30 m at 370 degrees C from casing, shut downing the well after 48 hours of normal operation, injecting ammonium chloride or potassium chloride having mass concentration of 10-20% anti-swelling agent liquid from the casing, injecting liquid carbon dioxide from the casing through the electric heating tube, maintaining injection temperature of liquid carbon dioxide to above 10 degrees C, injecting low pressure slowly and immersing the well for 30 days. The injection method is mainly natural pressure injection. The injection pressure does not exceed to 15 MPa. The amount of injection is 50-75 t and the injection amount should be 1.5 times the effective thickness of the formation. The amount of injection is 3-5 ton/m of oil layer and amount reaches the effective thickness of the formation having treatment radius of 0.5 m. The casing pressure is greater than 4 MPa. The well was continued until the casing pressure was less than 4 MPa. If the effective thickness of the stratum is not reached when the treatment radius is 0.5 m, and the plunger pump is used for injection to achieve the minimum injection volume.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The oil detergent comprises 5 %mass OP-10 (Alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether), 25 %mass petroleum sulfonate and 70 %mass water.

PT P
PN DE102019110897-A1; WO2019215275-A1
TI Gas pipeline for discharge of carbon dioxide gas from fermentation tank for cultivation of fruit or vegetable, has gas conduit which connects discharge opening of fermentation tank to supply opening of greenhouse.
AU KAMIL G
AE GRANMALT AG (GRAN-Non-standard)
GA 201995238L
AB    NOVELTY - The gas pipeline (10) has gas conduit which connects a discharge opening of fermentation tank (20) to a supply opening of greenhouse (30). The carbon dioxide (CO2) gas measuring device (11) measures the CO2 gas volumetric flow and mass flow between the fermentation tank and the greenhouse. An illumination system (34) in the greenhouse is detected and controlled by a sensor.
   USE - Gas pipeline for discharge of carbon dioxide gas from fermentation tank for cultivation of fruit or vegetable, or root or tuber e.g. radish (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to tomatoes, cucumbers, strawberries or flowers such as roses, Gerberas and orchids, and fruits such as strawberries, apples, dates, grapes or bananas.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 fertilization for greenhouses is simplified. The release of the excess CO2 gas produced in a fermentation tank into the atmosphere of the earth is prevented, and thus the excess CO2 gas is used, for CO2 fertilization in a sustainable and value-adding manner. The length of the intervals between two measurements is constant. The length of the intervals between two measurements is extended if the change in the measured values per unit of time falls below a lower threshold value. Thus the power consumption and the data volume for transmitting the measured values are reduced. The average value of the temporal changes between a series of successive measurements is determined, and the time interval between two measurements is increased if the mean value falls below a first reference value in absolute value and reduced in size if the mean value exceeds a second reference value in amount, which reduces the effects of statistical measurement errors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a greenhouse;
   (2) a method for fertilizing plants in greenhouse; and
   (3) a process product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas pipeline for discharge of carbon dioxide gas from fermentation tank.
   Gas pipeline (10)
   Carbon dioxide gas measuring device (11)
   Gas pipeline (20)
   Greenhouse (30)
   Illumination system (34)

PN DE102017107529-A1
TI Process for producing and stabilizing oxidic passivation layer on metals or metallic materials used for e.g. inhibiting corrosion of metal, involves setting pH of corrosion/passivation solution and contacting metal with passivation solution.
AU SCHMIDT-WINKEL P
   KORMANN R
   BEHMEL U
AE DRAEXLMAIER GMBH LISA (DRAE-C)
GA 2018862276
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing and stabilizing an oxidic, mixed-oxidic or carbonate-oxidic passivation layer on metals or on metallic materials, involves providing an aqueous passivation solution or corrosion inhibitor solution or a tempering medium comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkaline earth carbonate or bicarbonate, setting a suitable pH of the corrosion/passivation solution satisfying the relation (I) during reaction of calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water to obtain aqueous calcium(II) ions and aqueous bicarbonate, and contacting the metal to be passivated with the corrosion inhibitor or passivation solution or with the tempering medium at a temperature above 15 degrees C.
   USE - Process for producing and stabilizing an oxidic, mixed-oxidic or carbonate-oxidic passivation layer on metals or on metallic materials e.g. aluminum or aluminum-containing alloy (all claimed), for passivating or inhibiting corrosion of metal or metallic material for device e.g. laminating tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical production and stabilization of oxidic, mixed-oxidic or carbonate-oxidic passivation layer on metals or on metallic material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for producing and stabilizing an oxidic, mixed-oxidic or carbonate-oxidic passivation layer on metals or on metallic materials, involves providing an aqueous passivation solution or corrosion inhibitor solution or a tempering medium comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkaline earth carbonate or bicarbonate, setting a suitable pH of the corrosion/passivation solution satisfying the relation: 7.91-1g(2.8CO2/KH) (I), where KH is the carbonate hardness (in degrees dH) and CO2 is the carbon dioxide equilibrium concentration in the concentration (mg/l) in the solution, during reaction of calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water to obtain aqueous calcium(II) ions and aqueous bicarbonate, and contacting the metal to be passivated with the corrosion inhibitor or passivation solution or with the tempering medium at a temperature above 15 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for tempering system for metallic tool.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a laminating tool.
   Laminating tool (1)
   Water (2)
   Tank (3)
   Control device (7)
   Water supply (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH of the corrosion inhibitor/passivation solution or the tempering medium is adjusted by adding at least one hydrogen carbonate, or by adding at least one alkaline earth carbonate. The hydrogen carbonate is embodied by at least one alkaline earth hydrogen carbonate. The alkaline earth hydrogen carbonate is embodied by calcium hydrogen carbonate. The alkaline earth metal carbonate is chosen from calcium carbonate, calcium bicarbonate, dolomite and calcium-magnesium carbonate. The passivation solution or tempering medium further comprises at least one salt chosen from hydrogen carbonate, hydrogen phosphates, dihydrogen phosphates, hydrogen sulfate, sulfates and silica. The tempering medium further comprises at least one magnesium salt chosen from magnesium carbonate, magnesium hydrogen carbonate, magnesium sulfate and magnesium hydrogen sulfate. The corrosion inhibition/passivation solution or tempering medium further comprises a corrosion inhibitor and a biocide and/or an indicator. Preferred Properties: The pH of the corrosion inhibitor/passivation solution or the tempering medium is 4.5-8.5, preferably 7.5-8.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The corrosion inhibitor comprises alkane polyol, preferably ethylene glycol and/or propylene glycol.

TI Electrolytic cell useful for electrocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises cathode            assembly, anode assembly, and separator that divides            both.
AU MAO Q
   YING W
   SHI Y
   DU Z
   HUANG Y
   JING W
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201851406W
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolytic cell comprises cathode assembly,                anode assembly, and separator that divides both;                where the anode assembly comprises anode end plate,                anode current collecting plate, anode fluid                distribution plate, anode flow field plate and                anode electrode plate which are sequentially                connected together; the cathode assembly comprises                electrolyte support layer, solution electrode                plate, cathode gas diffusion electrode, cathode                flow field plate, cathode fluid distribution plate,                cathode current collecting plate and cathode end                plate which are sequentially connected together;                the separator is disposed between the anode                electrode plate of the anode assembly and the                electrolyte support layer of the cathode assembly;                the anode end plate and the cathode end plate are                assembled and fixed by connecting holes and bolts,                thus realizing fixing of the anode and cathode                components.
   USE - The electrolytic cell is useful for                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolytic cell: can reduce the amount                of electrolyte used for carbon dioxide                electro-reduction by changing the thickness of the                solution electrode plate; improves stability of                long-term operation of carbon dioxide                electro-reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Electrolytic cell comprises cathode assembly,                anode assembly, and separator that divides both;                where the anode assembly comprises anode end plate,                anode current collecting plate, anode fluid                distribution plate, anode flow field plate and                anode electrode plate which are sequentially                connected together; the cathode assembly comprises                electrolyte support layer, solution electrode                plate, cathode gas diffusion electrode, cathode                flow field plate, cathode fluid distribution plate,                cathode current collecting plate and cathode end                plate which are sequentially connected together;                the separator is disposed between the anode                electrode plate of the anode assembly and the                electrolyte support layer of the cathode assembly;                the anode end plate and the cathode end plate are                assembled and fixed by connecting holes and bolts,                thus realizing fixing of the anode and cathode                components; the electrolyte support layer is                embedded in the groove of the solution electrode                plate to form a place for electrochemical reaction                of carbon dioxide with the cathode gas diffusion                electrode embedded in the cathode flow field plate;                the solution electrode plate has thickness of 2-5                mm; the electrolyte support layer is asbestos film,                quartz wool or wool felt.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cathode inlet and                  outlet holes are respectively disposed at                  diagonal positions of the cathode end plate, the                  cathode fluid distribution plate, the solution                  electrode plate and the cathode flow field plate;                  The electrolyte inlet and outlet holes and the                  cathode inlet and outlet holes are dislocated,                  and are respectively disposed on the diagonal                  positions of the cathode end plate, the cathode                  fluid distribution plate, the solution electrode                  plate and the cathode flow field plate; The anode                  inlet and outlet holes are respectively disposed                  at diagonal positions of the anode end plate, the                  anode fluid distribution plate and the anode flow                  field plate; When the components are assembled                  together, the inlet and outlet holes on the                  adjacent components correspond to each other to                  form the inlet and outlet passages of the                  cathode, the anode inlet and outlet channels, and                  the electrolyte. The positioning holes are                  respectively disposed on the cathode flow field                  plate, the solution electrode plate and the anode                  flow field plate, when the three are assembled                  together, the positioning holes correspond to                  each other to realize fixing between adjacent                  members through the positioning shaft; the                  temperature measuring hole is disposed on the                  peripheral wall of the cathode and anode flow                  field plates and extends to the inside thereof,                  and a thermocouple is placed therein to monitor                  the temperature in the reaction tank disposed in                  the middle of the anode and cathode flow field                  plates. The separator is an ion exchange                  membrane.

PN US2019169517-A1; US10450522-B2
TI Biogas upgrading system based upon para-toluene sulfonic acid (PTSA) and gas separation membranes, comprises e.g. source of raw biogas, main compressor, PTSA unit, and first gas separation membrane stage.
AU MITARITEN M J
AE AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
GA 201950340B
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas upgrading system based upon para-toluene sulfonic acid (PTSA) and gas separation membranes, comprises e.g.: a source of raw biogas (1) comprising methane, carbon dioxide, water, volatile organic compounds (VOCs), and hydrogen sulfide; a main compressor (3) including a suction inlet in fluid communication with the source and receiving a stream of raw biogas from the source; a PTSA unit comprising at least two adsorbent beds each of which is selective for water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide over carbon dioxide and for hydrogen sulfide over methane; and a first gas separation membrane stage (11) comprising at least one gas separation membranes selective for carbon dioxide and oxygen over methane, a feed gas inlet of the first gas separation membrane stage being in downstream fluid communication with the PTSA unit.
   USE - Used as biogas upgrading system.
   ADVANTAGE - The biogas does not have an unnecessarily high cost or level of complexity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biogas upgrading system based upon para-toluene sulfonic acid (PTSA) and gas separation membranes, comprises: a source of raw biogas (1) comprising methane, carbon dioxide, water, volatile organic compounds (VOCs), and hydrogen sulfide; a main compressor (3) including a suction inlet in fluid communication with the source and receiving a stream of raw biogas from the source; a PTSA unit comprising at least two adsorbent beds each of which is selective for water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide over carbon dioxide and for hydrogen sulfide over methane, where the PTSA unit is in downstream fluid communication with the main compressor and receiving a stream of a PTSA feed gas from it, the PTSA unit being adapted and configured to absorb water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide from the PTSA feed gas and produce a PTSA product gas deficient in water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide in comparison to the PTSA feed gas; a first gas separation membrane stage (11) comprising at least one gas separation membranes selective for carbon dioxide and oxygen over methane, a feed gas inlet of the first gas separation membrane stage being in downstream fluid communication with the PTSA unit, where the first gas separation membrane stage is configured and adapted to separate the PTSA product gas into a first stage permeate gas stream (13) and a first stage retentate gas stream; a second gas separation membrane stage (17) comprising at least one gas separation membrane selective for carbon dioxide and oxygen over methane, a feed gas inlet of the second gas separation membrane stage being in downstream fluid communication with the retentate outlet of the first gas separation membrane stage and receiving the first stage permeate gas stream from it, where the second gas separation membrane stage is adapted and configured to separate the first retentate gas stream into a second permeate gas stream and a second retentate gas stream, where the PTSA unit is in fluid communication with the permeate gas outlet of the first gas separation membrane stages so as to receive a flow of the first stage permeate gas for use as a thermal regeneration gas to thermally regenerate and desorb water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide that was adsorbed upon the adsorbent beds from the PTSA feed gas stream, and the PTSA unit is in fluid communication with the permeate gas outlet of the second gas separation membrane stage so as to receive a flow of the second permeate gas stream for use as a cool down stream for cooling at least one adsorbent beds after its thermal regeneration; and a treatment unit in downstream fluid communication with the PTSA unit so as to receive a waste gas from the PTSA comprised of the thermal regeneration gas and water, VOCs, and hydrogen sulfide, where the treatment unit is adapted and configured to either oxidize or burn the VOCs contained in the waste gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the first phase of the system.
   Source of raw biogas (1)
   Main compressor (3)
   First gas separation membrane stage (11)
   First stage permeate gas stream (13)
   Second gas separation membrane stage (17)

PN CN109796955-A; CN109796955-B
TI Preparation of trapezoidal branched siloxane            modified polymer-based supercritical carbon dioxide            thickening agent involves dissolving            polydimethylsiloxane polymer and methyl siloxane            polymer, adding chloroplatinic acid, and stirring.
AU WANG Y
   LI Q
   ZHANG C
   RONG X
   LI Y
   TANG L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 201947422F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of trapezoidal branched siloxane                modified polymer-based supercritical carbon dioxide                thickening agent involves (i) preparing functional                group-terminated linear polydimethylsiloxane                polymer by adding cyclic siloxane, small molecule                blocking agent, toluene and basic catalyst to a                reaction vessel, performing reaction at a                temperature of 100-115 degrees C for 2-11 hours,                performing vacuum removal of toluene solvent and                low- molecular weight siloxane, (ii) preparing                ladder-like hydrogen-containing methyl siloxane                polymer by reacting chlorosilane with                phenylenediamine, adding deionized water and                reacting at 140-210 degrees C for 1.5-4.2 hours,                stirring and drying the obtained mixture by                steaming, and (iii) dissolving functional                group-terminated linear polydimethylsiloxane                polymer prepared in the step (i) and ladder-like                hydrogen-containing methyl siloxane polymer                prepared in step (ii) in toluene, dripping                chloroplatinic acid catalyst, reacting, cooling,                and stirring to remove small molecule.
   USE - Preparation of trapezoidal branched siloxane                modified polymer-based supercritical carbon dioxide                thickening agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of trapezoidal                branched siloxane modified polymer-based                supercritical carbon dioxide thickening agent with                improved solubility in supercritical carbon                dioxide, thickening performance and ability to                carry proppants, and reduced fluid loss                coefficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of trapezoidal branched siloxane                modified polymer-based supercritical carbon dioxide                thickening agent involves (i) preparing functional                group-terminated linear polydimethylsiloxane                polymer by adding cyclic siloxane, small molecule                blocking agent, toluene and basic catalyst to a                reaction vessel, sealing the reaction vessel by                introducing nitrogen gas, performing reaction at a                temperature of 100-115 degrees C for 2-11 hours,                raising the temperature to 140-155 degrees C for                30-40 minutes, lowering the temperature to room                temperature, performing vacuum removal of toluene                solvent and low- molecular weight siloxane, (ii)                preparing ladder-like hydrogen-containing methyl                siloxane polymer by reacting chlorosilane with                phenylenediamine at a temperature of 160-270                degrees C, adding 0.1-4.3 %mass deionized water and                reacting at 140-210 degrees C for 1.5-4.2 hours,                cooling to room temperature, separating the solid                material by filtration, evaporating small molecule                low-boiling substance and tetrahydrofuran to obtain                a concentrated filtrate, stirring the concentrated                filtrate for 3-5 hours, washing with a saturated                saline solution to pH 7 and drying the obtained                mixture by steaming, and (iii) dissolving                functional group-terminated linear                polydimethylsiloxane polymer prepared in the step                (i) and ladder-like hydrogen-containing methyl                siloxane polymer prepared in step (ii) together in                toluene, dripping chloroplatinic acid catalyst at a                temperature of 65-125 degrees C, reacting for 2-7.5                hours, cooling to room temperature, removing                chloroplatinic acid catalyst, and stirring to                remove the small molecule. The cyclic siloxane is                at least one chosen from                octamethylcyclotetrasiloxane,                tetramethyltetravinylcyclotetrasiloxane,                octavinylcyclotetrasiloxane,                tetramethyltetrahydrocyclotetrasiloxane, methyl                dihydrocyclotetrasiloxane,                hexamethylcyclotrisiloxane,                octaphenylcyclotetrasiloxane, and                tetramethyltetraphenylcyclotetrasiloxane. The small                molecule blocking agent is tetramethyldisiloxane,                hexamethyldisiloxane, heptamethyltrisiloxane,                octamethyltrisiloxane, pentamethyldisiloxane,                hexaethylenedisiloxane, hexaphenyldisiloxane or                heptaphenyltrisiloxane. The molar ratio of the                cyclic siloxane with respect to small molecule                blocking agent is (1.7-15):1. The concentration of                cyclic siloxane and small molecule blocking agent                in toluene is 30-85%. The molar ratio of cyclic                siloxane to basic catalyst is (92-224):1. The molar                ratio of chlorosilane with respect to                phenylenediamine is (0.55-3.2):1. The                phenylenediamine is dissolved in a tetrahydrofuran                solvent. The concentration of chlorosilane and                phenylenediamine in tetrahydrofuran is 30-74%. The                molar ratio of functional group-terminated linear                polydimethylsiloxane polymer with respect to                ladder-like hydrogen methylsiloxane polymer is                (0.4-5.3):1. The molar ratio of ladder-like                hydrogen methylsiloxane polymer with respect to                chloroplatinic acid is (41-132):1. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for trapezoidal branched siloxane                modified polymer supercritical carbon dioxide                thickening agent, which comprises trapezoidal                branched siloxane modified polymer of formula                (I).
   R1 = methyl, ethyl or vinyl;
   m = 3-16;
   R2 = methyl, ethyl, phenyl, vinyl or active                hydrogen;
   a = 22-54;
   b = 17-48;and
   R3 = methyl, phenyl or vinyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The basic catalyst is                  tetramethylammonium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, aluminum hydroxide or                  lithium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the cyclic siloxane with respect to small                  molecule blocking agent is preferably (4.5-14):1.                  The molar ratio of cyclic siloxane to basic                  catalyst is preferably (113-185):1. The                  concentration of cyclic siloxane and small                  molecule blocking agent in toluene is preferably                  35-70%. The chlorosilane is at least one chosen                  from trichloromethylsilane, trichlorohydrosilane,                  trihydrochlorosilane, trimethylchlorosilane,                  trichloroethylsilane and                  trihydrotrimethoxysilane. The molar ratio of two                  chlorosilanes is (0.5-4.5):1. The molar ratio of                  chlorosilane with respect to phenylenediamine is                  preferably (0.85-2.7):1. The concentration of                  chlorosilane and phenylenediamine in                  tetrahydrofuran is preferably 35-68%. The mass                  concentration of deionized water in the reaction                  system is 0.3-3.2%. The molar ratio of functional                  group-terminated linear polydimethylsiloxane                  polymer with respect to ladder-like hydrogen                  methylsiloxane polymer is preferably (0.9-4.1):1.                  The molar ratio of ladder-like hydrogen                  methylsiloxane polymer with respect to                  chloroplatinic acid is preferably (68-112):1.                  Preferred Process: The flow rate of nitrogen in                  the reactor is 0.5 L.minute-1. The reactor is                  sealed after being introduced with nitrogen for                  30 minutes, and placed in a high-temperature                  rotating roller furnace. The cyclic siloxane,                  small molecule blocking agent, toluene and basic                  catalyst in the reaction vessel are preferably                  reacted at 110 degrees C. The reactant is                  vacuum-removed to a toluene solvent and a small                  molecule siloxane low-boiling substance under a                  vacuum of 0.06 MPa and a temperature of 90                  degrees C. The step (ii) involves reacting                  chlorosilane with phenylenediamine preferably at                  180-240 degrees C for 0.3-2.3 hours and drying                  the obtained mixture at a vacuum of 0.09 MPa and                  a temperature of 75 degrees C.

   USE - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to utilizes carbon dioxide reduces discharge amount of the carbon dioxide and reduces the effect on the greenhouse effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for utilizing carbon dioxide, which comprises a carbon dioxide purging module, where the carbon dioxide purging module comprises an injection system and a borehole, where the borehole is set in the coal seam, and the injection unit is connected to the borehole to inject carbon dioxide that meets preset conditions into the borehole, and expand and release the carbon dioxide to the coal seam, the coal seam gas in the coal seam is expelled, and the coal seam gas is discharged from the coal seam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The preset condition is that the temperature and pressure of the carbon dioxide make it in a supercritical state in the coal seam. The method involves injecting carbon dioxide into the coal seam, drilling the coal seam, and the drilling in a vertical direction first sealing zone, where the carbon dioxide injection zone and the second sealing zone are arranged in sequence. The method involves obtaining carbon dioxide meeting preset conditions and injecting carbon dioxide into the coal seam further comprises: using carbon dioxide meeting preset conditions for power generation. The carbon dioxide remaining after being used for power generation is injected into the coal seam. Ther method involves using carbon dioxide meeting preset conditions for power generation, boosting and heating the working fluid, so that the working fluid after the boosting and heating carries the carbon dioxide into the turbine to expand and perform work to drive the generator to work. The gaseous medium discharged from the turbine is recovered, cooled and compressed to recycle the gaseous medium.

PN CN108267725-A; CN108267725-B
TI Method for inversion multi-wavelength laser radar stratified carbon dioxide concentration, involves stratifying integral path of carbon dioxide concentration inversion and constructing carbon dioxide concentration model.
AU HAN G
   SHI T
   XU H
   LIANG A
   MA X
   GONG W
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2018574680
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-wavelength laser radar stratified carbon dioxide concentration inversion method involves stratifying the integral path of carbon dioxide concentration inversion in the vertical direction according to the degree of influence of climate and carbon cycle on carbon dioxide concentration and closing the carbon dioxide concentration in the same layer. The corresponding carbon dioxide concentration multi-wavelength inversion model and weight model are constructed for each layer and obtaining the corresponding carbon dioxide concentration multi-wavelength inversion model and weight model for each layer to obtain the carbon dioxide column concentration calculation model of each layer. The linear equations are calculated to obtain the observation equations with the carbon dioxide column concentration of each layer as the parameters to be solved, solving the carbon dioxide column concentration of each layer and calculated the carbon dioxide concentration of each layer.
   USE - Method for inversion multi-wavelength laser radar stratified carbon dioxide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables inversion of multi-wavelength laser radar stratified carbon dioxide concentration with high precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A multi-wavelength laser radar stratified carbon dioxide concentration inversion method involves stratifying the integral path of carbon dioxide concentration inversion in the vertical direction according to the degree of influence of climate and carbon cycle on carbon dioxide concentration and closing the carbon dioxide concentration in the same layer. The corresponding carbon dioxide concentration multi-wavelength inversion model and weight model are constructed for each layer and obtaining the corresponding carbon dioxide concentration multi-wavelength inversion model and weight model for each layer to obtain the carbon dioxide column concentration calculation model of each layer. The linear equations are calculated according to the carbon dioxide column concentration calculation model of each layer to obtain the observation equations with the carbon dioxide column concentration of each layer as the parameters to be solved, solving the carbon dioxide column concentration of each layer by linear least squares method according to the carbon dioxide column concentration and calculated the carbon dioxide concentration of each layer.

TI Preparing mineralized red mud aggregate water            permeable brick, involves mixing red mud, tailing sand            and steel slag powder, adding water, mineralizing in            carbon dioxide, refilling mineralized particles in            porous mold, and soaking in slurry formed by mixing            steel slag powder, retarder and water.
AU ZHANG G
   ZHANG X
   WU W
   LI L
   ZHANG T
   YAO F
   CUI D
   MA Y
AE SHANDONG HANBO YUZHOU NEW MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202273717R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of the mineralized red                mud aggregate water permeable brick involves:                uniformly mixing 35-50 wt.% red mud, 20-30 wt.%                tailing sand and steel slag powder, adding 15-25                wt.% water for granulation, mineralizing the                obtained particles in a carbon dioxide atmosphere,                and refilling the mineralized particles in a porous                mold; soaking the porous mold in slurry formed by                mixing steel slag powder, a retarder and water,                taking out the porous mold after the slurry is                wrapped on the surfaces of mineralized particles,                discharging the slurry capable of flowing in the                porous mold, pre-mineralizing the slurry in a                carbon dioxide atmosphere, and demolding to obtain                a shaped brick; and mineralizing the shaped brick                in the carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the                water permeable brick.
   USE - Method for preparing mineralized red mud                aggregate water permeable brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The red mud, the tailing sand and the steel                slag are uniformly mixed and granulated. The formed                aggregate is simple in proportioning, the forming                operation is convenient, the strength forming                period is short, and the obtained water permeable                brick can reach high strength in tens hours to                twenty hours through specific conditions such as                twice mineralization, and once pre-mineralization.                The method realizes the purposes of bonding the                interior of the brick body and having strength                under specific conditions by the functions of the                added steel slag during granulation and the steel                slag powder in the soaking slurry. The method uses                the solid waste red mud and steel slag of                metallurgical industry as raw materials, does not                need to add cementing materials such as cement, has                high utilization rate of solid waste, and is green                and environment-friendly. The water permeable brick                is produced at normal temperature without                high-temperature sintering in the forming process,                and the energy consumption is low. Carbon dioxide                is absorbed in the mineralization reaction process                of the particle aggregate and the brick body, so                that the national double-carbon strategy is met.                The emission of the carbon dioxide is reduced, and                the resource is saved and utilized. The method                provides a solution for aluminum production                enterprises, responds to the national carbon                neutralization policy, and promotes the green                development of the public society. The mineralized                red mud aggregate water permeable brick has good                water permeable effect (the permeability                coefficient is more than 2.0x10-2cm/s). The                strength reaches the standard and the cost is                low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                mineralized red mud aggregate water permeable                brick.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The retarder is sodium                  gluconate, salicylic acid or citric acid. The                  steel slag and the steel slag powder are                  converter steel slag or electric furnace steel                  slag. The tailings are gold tailings, iron                  tailings or high-iron bauxite tailings.

PN KR2021080151-A; KR2357191-B1
TI Producing a carbon and sulfur complex having            hierarchical structure for lithium-sulfur battery            electrode comprises reducing carbon dioxide-containing            gas in presence of reducing agent e.g. magnesium,            mixing sulfur and heat-treating.
AU LEE J
   PARKJAEHYUN
   KIM H S
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202175700H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a carbon and sulfur complex having a                hierarchical structure comprises reducing the                carbon dioxide-containing gas in the presence of a                reducing agent and a carbonate-based pore template                to prepare a carbon material, mixing sulfur with                the prepared carbon material and                heat-treating.
   USE - The carbon and sulfur complex is useful for                next-generation lithium-sulfur battery electrode                for small electronic devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium-sulfur battery anode material has                voltage of 1.7-2.8 V, current density of 0.1-7.0 C                and charge and discharge capacity of 700-1300                mAh/g. The cathode material has charge/discharge                capacity of 600-1300 mAh/g based on voltage of                1.7-2.8 V and a current density of 0.1 to 5.0 C.                The method: is economical and environmentally                friendly; improves stability. The complex improves                electrical conductivity of battery, electric                capacity and battery life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) carbon and sulfur complex having a                hierarchical structure comprising hierarchical                carbon structure in which mesopores and micropores                coexist is formed and has mesopores of 1.5-3.0                cm3/g, and 3-6 times the amount of sulfur compared                to the carbon mass is contained in the carbon                structure;
   (2) producing porous carbon fiber for an                intermediate layer of a lithium-sulfur battery                comprising electrospinning with a carbon dioxide                reducing agent and conductive polymer in the                presence of an organic solvent, and heat-treating                under carbon dioxide conditions;
   (3) porous carbon fiber having total surface                area of 100-700 m3/g, total pore volume of 0.1-1.2                cm3/g, and a surface nitrogen of 5-25%;
   (4) lithium-sulfur battery anode material                comprising the carbon and sulfur complex;
   (5) lithium-sulfur battery intermediate layer                comprising the porous carbon fiber;
   (6) producing carbon fiber and sulfur complex                comprising (a) electrospinning in the presence of a                carbon dioxide reducing agent and a conductive                polymer in the presence of an organic solvent, heat                treating under carbon dioxide conditions to prepare                a porous carbon fiber, and (b) mixing sulfur with                the carbon fiber, heat-treating, and filtering                graphene;
   (7) carbon fiber and sulfur complex comprising                hierarchical carbon structure in which mesopores                and micropores coexist, where mesopores are 0.1-1.0                cm3/g, and contains 1-2 times the amount of sulfur                compared to the carbon mass in the carbon fiber;                and
   (8) lithium-sulfur battery cathode material                comprising carbon fiber and sulfur complex.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the method for producing a carbon and sulfur                complex having hierarchical structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  sodium borohydride, magnesium, copper or zinc.                  The carbonate-based pore template is calcium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate                  and magnesium carbonate. The amount of                  carbonate-based pore template is 0.1-300 wt.%.                  The amount of sulfur is 100-500 pts. wt. The                  conductive polymer is polyacrylonitrile,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinylacetate or                  polyvinyl alcohol. The organic solvent is                  dimethylformamide, alcohol, acetone or                  dichloromethane. Based on 100 pts. wt. organic                  solvent, the conductive polymer is 1-50 pts. wt.,                  and the carbon dioxide reducing agent is 0.1-50                  pts. wt. Preferred Method: The sulfur is mixed                  through ball milling for 12-48 hours at a                  rotation speed of 100-400 revolutions/minute. The                  heat treatment step includes heating at 140-155                  degrees C for 12-48 hours by raising the                  temperature at a rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute                  under argon conditions of 1-100 atm and a flow                  rate of 10-500 ml/minute. After the heat                  treatment, washing with strong acid i.e.                  hydrochloric acid, perchloric acid, nitric acid                  or sulfuric acid to remove salts. After the step                  of removing the salt, washing with distilled                  water or alcohol to neutralize the carbon fiber.                  After the neutralization, the carbon fiber is                  dried at a temperature of 50-130 degrees C at                  pressure of 0-1 bar for 0.5-2 days. The carbon                  dioxide reducing agent and the conductive polymer                  are stirred in the presence of an organic solvent                  at a rotation speed of 100-1000                  revolutions/minute at 25-40 degrees C for 0.5-1                  hour at a flow rate of 0.5-2.0 ml/hour at a                  voltage of 10-20 kV. After the electrospinning                  treatment, heat treating under argon and carbon                  dioxide conditions at pressure of 1-100                  atmospheres and a flow rate of 10-500 ml/minute.                  The heat treatment is performed by increasing the                  temperature at a rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute to                  150-250 degrees C, maintaining for 2-5 hours                  under argon conditions, at 400-500 degrees C for                  1-3 hours under carbon dioxide conditions, and at                  600-700 degrees C 0.5-2 hours. The method further                  comprises neutralizing the carbon fiber by                  washing with distilled water or alcohol after                  removing the salt. The method further comprises                  drying the carbon fiber at a temperature of                  50-130 degrees C at pressure of 0-1 bar for 0.5-2                  days. The 5-40 pts. wt. sulfur is mixed with                  respect to 100 pts. wt. carbon fiber and sulfur                  complex. In the process, immersing the carbon                  fiber in a sulfur solution and perform ball                  milling for 1-24 hours at a rotation speed of                  50-150 revolutions/minute. In step (b), raising                  temperature at a rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute                  under argon conditions of 1-100 atm and flow rate                  of 10-500 ml/minute, maintaining at 140-155                  degrees C for 12-48 hours, and heat treating at                  200-255 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. In the                  process, solution containing more than 0-10 pts.                  wt. graphene with respect to 100 pts. wt. water                  is filtered using vacuum filtration device to                  filter graphene in an amount of 0-20 pts. wt.                  After the graphene filtration, the carbon fiber                  and sulfur complex are heated at a temperature of                  50-130 degrees C, and pressure of 0-1 bar for                  0.5-2 days.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202170249S
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus consists of recovery device (11) for accommodating adsorbent (11a) which is capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbon generation unit for producing hydrocarbon compound using hydrogen and carbon dioxide recovered by the recovery device. The recovery device consists of exhaust gas supply port, in which exhaust gas is supplied from an exhaust gas supply source and exhaust gas supply port to recovery device, adsorption off gas discharge port for discharging adsorption off gas which is not adsorbed by the adsorbent and exhaust gas which is not supplied into the recovery device from exhaust gas supply port, and residual gas discharge port for discharging residual gas remaining in the recovery device. The exhaust gas supply port is arranged on the side opposite to the adsorption off gas discharge port. The residual gas discharge port is arranged on the same side as the adsorption off gas discharge port.
   USE - Apparatus used for manufacturing hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus efficiently produces hydrocarbon compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of hydrocarbon, which involves adsorbing carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas supplied from the exhaust gas supply source through the exhaust gas supply port by the adsorbent accommodated in the recovery device, discharging the adsorption off gas which is not adsorbed by the adsorbent from the adsorption off gas discharge port arranged on the opposite side of the exhaust gas supply port in the recovery device, discharging the residual gas remaining in the recovery device from the residual gas discharge port arranged on the side opposite to the exhaust gas supply port in the recovery device, and producing a hydrocarbon compound using hydrogen and carbon dioxide recovered by the recovery device; and
   (2) computer program, which comprises adsorption function for adsorbing carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas supplied from the exhaust gas supply source through the exhaust gas supply port by the adsorbent accommodated in the recovery device and discharging the adsorption off gas which is not adsorbed by the adsorbent from the adsorption off gas discharge port arranged on the opposite side of the exhaust gas supply port in the recovery device, discharge function for discharging the residual gas remaining in the recovery device from the residual gas discharge port arranged on the opposite side of the exhaust gas supply port in the recovery device, and production function for producing a hydrocarbon compound using hydrogen and carbon dioxide recovered by the recovery device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the recovery device in the methane production apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

The recovery device comprises cleaning gas supply port, in which the cleaning gas is supplied from the cleaning gas supply unit when the exhaust gas is not supplied from the exhaust gas supply port. The cleaning gas supply port is arranged on the same side as the exhaust gas supply port. The control unit supplies the cleaning gas to the recovery device, stops the supply of the cleaning gas and depressurizes the inside of the recovery device, when the exhaust gas is not supplied from the exhaust gas supply port. The recovery device comprises heating unit for heating the adsorbent. The control unit further controls the heating unit, and cleaning gas is supplied to the recovery device, after the heating unit starts heating the adsorbent.
