PN US2020331751-A1
TI Performing hydrocarbon reforming, involves e.g. reacting fuel mixture, oxygen-containing stream under combustion in combustion zone within reactor, separating flue gas and exposing hydrocarbon-containing stream to catalyst composition.
AU ONEAL E J
   SKOULIDAS A I
   WANG Z
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2020A2883Y
AB    NOVELTY - Performing hydrocarbon reforming, involves reacting fuel mixture comprising fuel stream, oxygen-containing stream comprising nitrogen and recycle stream under combustion in combustion zone within reactor to form flue gas and to heat at least one surface in reaction zone, the reaction zone comprising catalyst composition, the fuel mixture comprising oxygen and carbon, separating the flue gas to form at least carbon dioxide-containing stream comprising a second pressure and the recycle stream and exposing hydrocarbon-containing stream to the catalyst composition in the reaction zone at the regenerated surface temperature under reforming conditions to form reforming product stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, direction of flow for the hydrocarbon-containing stream within the reaction zone being reversed relative to a direction of flow for the fuel mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for performing hydrocarbon reforming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing hydrocarbon reforming, comprises reacting a fuel mixture comprising a fuel stream, oxygen-containing stream comprising less than or equal to 15 vol.% nitrogen and a recycle stream under combustion conditions comprising a combustion pressure of greater than or equal to 0.7 MPa-g in a combustion zone within a reactor to form a flue gas and to heat at least one surface in a reaction zone to a regenerated surface temperature of greater than or equal to 600 degrees C, the reaction zone comprising a catalyst composition, the fuel mixture comprising greater than or equal to 0.1 vol.% oxygen and greater than or equal to 20 vol.% carbon dioxide relative to a volume of the fuel mixture, separating the flue gas to form at least a carbon dioxide-containing stream comprising a second pressure of greater than or equal to 0.7 MPa-g and the recycle stream and exposing a hydrocarbon-containing stream to the catalyst composition in the reaction zone at the regenerated surface temperature under reforming conditions to form a reforming product stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, a direction of flow for the hydrocarbon-containing stream within the reaction zone being reversed relative to a direction of flow for the fuel mixture. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a reverse flow reactor system comprising a reactor comprising a reactor inlet end, a regenerator inlet end and a reaction zone comprising reforming catalyst, a recycle loop providing intermittent fluid communication between the reactor inlet end and the regenerator inlet, the recycle loop comprising a recycle compressor, a fuel source inlet, an oxygen-containing gas inlet and a carbon monoxide-containing gas outlet an air separation unit in fluid communication with the oxygen-containing gas inlet and a water separation stage in fluid communication with the carbon dioxide-containing gas outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The combustion conditions comprise a combustion pressure of greater than or equal to 1.4 Mpa-g, preferably 3.4 Mpa-g and/or where the second pressure is greater than or equal to 1.4 MPa-g, preferably 3.4 Mpa-g. The method further comprises: compressing the flue gas prior to separating the flue gas to form at least the carbon dioxide-containing stream and the recycle stream; separating the carbon dioxide-containing stream to form a stream containing water and a carbon dioxide-enriched stream comprising greater than or equal to 80 vol.% carbon dioxide and compressing the carbon dioxider-enriched stream to a pressure of greater than or equal to 7 MPa-g; exposing the reforming product stream to water gas shift reaction conditions to form a shifted synthesis gas product stream and separating the shifted synthesis gas product stream to form a Hz-containing stream and a stream comprising carbon dioxide; and separating air in an air separation unit to form the oxygen-containing stream. The shifted synthesis gas product stream is separated by pressure swing adsorption, and where the stream comprising carbon dioxide comprises a tail gas. The regenerated surface temperature is greater than or equal to 800 degrees C. The combustion conditions comprise a laminar flame speed of less than or equal to 100 cm/second. Preferred Components: The recycle stream and the fuel mixture comprises less than or equal to 15 vol % nitrogen. The fuel mixture comprises at least a portion of the stream comprising carbon dioxide, the stream comprising further comprises greater than or equal to 5 vol.% carbon monoxide. The shifted synthesis gas stream comprises a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of less than 10. The fuel stream, the oxygen-containing stream, and the recycle stream are combined to form the fuel mixture prior to entering the reactor or where the fuel stream, the oxygen-containing stream and the recycle stream are combined to form the fuel mixture prior to entering the combustion zone. The recycle stream comprises greater than or equal to 25 vol.% carbon dioxide relative to a volume of the recycle stream. The fuel mixture comprises greater than or equal to 2 vol.% carbon monoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reactor comprises: a reverse flow reactor; and a reactor system comprising a recuperator portion and a portion including the reaction zone. The reverse flow reactor system further comprises a pressure swing adsorption separator comprising an adsorber inlet, a product outlet, and a tail gas outlet, the regenerator inlet end being in intermittent fluid communication with the adsorber inlet, the tail gas outlet being in intermittent fluid communication with the recycle loop.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 2018398661
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises                e.g. (i) adding the calcium hydroxide slurry into                the stirring reaction kettle, where the stirring                kettle is provided with a long paddle blade surface                self-suction-type stirring paddle through the                constant temperature circulating medium jacket,                heating or cooling the reaction slurry, starting                agitating motor, uniformly dispersing the                dispersant in the reaction slurry, and closing the                stirring motor, (ii) opening the inert gas vent                valve, introducing inert gas until the pressure in                the stirring reaction kettle is P1, closing the                inert gas vent valve, opening the carbon dioxide                air inlet valve, adjusting the constant pressure                regulator valve, maintaining the gas pressure P in                kettle at constant value, (iii) automatically                sucking the inert gas and carbon dioxide mixture                above the liquid level in the tank is under the                liquid surface, (iv) discharging the slurry through                the discharge valve, centrifuging the reaction                solution, washing, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product having small                particle size, and shortens carbonization                time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                adding the calcium hydroxide slurry into the                stirring reaction kettle, where the stirring kettle                is provided with a long paddle blade surface                self-suction-type stirring paddle through the                constant temperature circulating medium jacket,                heating or cooling the reaction slurry to the set                temperature, adding dispersing agent into the                stirring reaction kettle, starting agitating motor,                uniformly dispersing the dispersant in the reaction                slurry, and closing the stirring motor, (ii)                opening the inert gas vent valve, introducing inert                gas until the pressure in the stirring reaction                kettle is P1, closing the inert gas vent valve,                opening the carbon dioxide air inlet valve,                adjusting the constant pressure regulator valve,                maintaining the gas pressure P in kettle at                constant value, (iii) turning on the stirring                motor, adjusting the rotational speed of the                self-priming impeller on the surface of the short                blade into the rotational speed N of the impeller,                automatically sucking the inert gas and carbon                dioxide mixture above the liquid level in the tank                is under the liquid surface, forming large number                of small bubbles in the reaction slurry, measuring                the pH of the reaction solution by using pH                measuring probe installed in a stirred reactor,                when the pH is less than 8, turning off the                stirring motor and the carbon dioxide inlet valve,                and stopping the reaction, (iv) discharging the                slurry through the discharge valve, centrifuging                the reaction solution, washing, and drying to                obtain nano-calcium carbonate powder product, where                method uses the long paddle and short blade surface                self-suction stirring paddle to obtain nano calcium                carbonate, under the presence of dispersants, and                reacting calcium hydroxide slurry with carbon                dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The long and short                  paddle blade surfaces used by self-suction                  stirring paddle comprises a fixed disc, a turbo                  type agitating valve, connecting plate, short                  blade, fixed tray, a turbo type agitating valve,                  connecting disc and short blade. The upper part                  of the lower connecting turbo type agitating                  valve is connected to the fixing plate. The lower                  part of the turbo type agitating valve is                  connected with the connecting disc. The                  connecting disc is connected under the short                  blade. In the step (i), the set temperature is                  controlled at 0-60 degrees C, preferably 15-45                  degrees C. In the step (i), the calcium hydroxide                  slurry has concentration of 0.01-20 wt.%,                  preferably 2-10 wt.%. In the step (i), the                  dispersant is sucrose, sulfuric acid, nitric                  acid, and phosphoric acid, and the amount added                  is 0.01-5 wt.% of the total amount of the                  reactants. In the step (ii), the inert gas is gas                  that does not react with both carbon dioxide and                  calcium hydroxide slurry. The inert gas is                  introduced to pressure of P1 and the total                  pressure of the gas in the kettle P. The amount                  of the gas-phase volume fraction (1-P1/P) of                  carbon dioxide is not less than 0.1 wt.%. In the                  step (iii), the rotational speed N of the                  stirring paddle should meet the following                  conditions: N x D is greater than 10, where D is                  the diameter of the circle corresponding to one                  revolution of the long paddle.

AU CHEN C
   DING K
   CONG X
   LI Y
   HUA W
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201851531F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monoethanolamine by hydrogenation of hydroxyacetonitrile comprises hydrogenating hydroxyacetonitrile solution with hydrogen in the presence of catalyst and carbon dioxide to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monoethanolamine by hydrogenation of hydroxyacetonitrile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the service life of the catalyst; and improves the selectivity of monoethanolamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of carbon dioxide added is related to the pH of the reaction system i.e. adjusting the flow rate of carbon dioxide added during the reaction, and the pH of the reaction system is maintained at 6-7. The catalyst is hydrogenation catalyst, the amount of the hydrogenation catalyst is 1-2% of the mass of the liquid in the reaction system. The hydrogenation catalyst is a supported hydrogenation catalyst and/or a framework metal catalyst; the supported hydrogenation catalyst comprises cobalt/aluminum oxide catalyst; the framework metal catalyst comprises framework nickel catalyst and/or framework cobalt catalyst. The solvent is mixture of water and alcohol or ether. The addition amount of hydroxyacetonitrile solution is at a rate of 0.01-0.1 mol of hydroxyacetonitrile/(g catalyst\hour), preferably the concentration of the hydroxyacetonitrile in the reaction system is controlled to be within 10 parts per million (during the reaction). The concentration of hydroxyacetonitrile in the hydroxyacetonitrile solution is 10-50 wt.%, preferably 10-25 wt.%, the ratio between water and alcohol or ether is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1-1:2. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol and/or butylene glycol, preferably methanol and/or ethanol; and the ether is methyl ether, dimethyl ether, diethyl ether, diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran and/or dioxane, preference tetrahydrofuran or dioxane. The hydrogenation reaction is suitable for a continuous production process, preferably using a reactor in full mixed flow, more preferably using a continuous tank reactor or continuous Circulation reactor. Preferred Method: The hydrogenation temperature is 50-150 degrees C, preferably 70-90 degrees C, and hydrogenation pressure is 1-10 MPaG, preferably 2-4 MPaG.

PN US2021369462-A1
TI Using comparative calcium salt solubilities to prepare bone void filler substrate having calcium particles by applying carbonatable calcium salt precursor component to bone void filler substrate, and treating with transformation component.
AU SHIMP L A
AE CAP BIOMATERIALS LLC (CAPB-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9663E
AB    NOVELTY - Using comparative calcium salt solubilities to prepare a bone void filler substrate comprising calcium particles on it, involves providing a carbonatable calcium salt precursor component having a water solubility; applying the precursor component to a bone void filler substrate; and treating the applied precursor component with a transformation component selected from alkali metal hydroxide and alkali metal carbonate components to provide on the substrate a calcium component having a water solubility less than the precursor component water solubility, the calcium component selected from calcium hydroxide and calcium carbonate particles.
   USE - The method is useful for using comparative calcium salt solubilities to prepare bone void filler substrate comprising calcium particles on it (claimed); and for preparing substrates with carbonate surface coatings to promote bone growth.
   ADVANTAGE - Fabrication is simplified and scaffold effectiveness is increased because the material is all one phase rather than a mixture of different materials that may segregate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preferred Method: Alternatively, using comparative calcium salt solubilities to prepare a bone void filler substrate comprising calcium particles on it, involves providing a carbonatable calcium salt precursor component having a water solubility; applying the precursor component to a bone void filler substrate comprising a material selected from calcium salts, collagen, natural mammalian bone, polymers, metals and combinations; and treating the applied precursor component with a transformation component selected from alkali metal hydroxide and alkali metal carbonate components to provide on the substrate a calcium component having a water solubility less than the precursor component water solubility, the calcium component selected from calcium hydroxide and calcium carbonate particles.
   Using comparative calcium salt solubilities to prepare a bone void filler substrate comprising calcium particles on it, involves providing a carbonatable calcium salt precursor component having a water solubility; applying the precursor component to a bone void filler substrate; treating the applied precursor component with a transformation component selected from alkali metal hydroxide components to provide on the substrate a calcium hydroxide component having a water solubility less than the precursor component water solubility; and contacting the substrate with a carbon dioxide source, optionally in the presence of water, to convert portion of the applied carbonatable calcium precursor component to calcium carbonate and provide a coating of discrete, dispersed calcium carbonate particles on the substrate.
   The method further involves contacting the substrate with a carbon dioxide source to provide calcium carbonate particles on it. The method also involves contacting the substrate with a carbon dioxide source, optionally in the presence of water, the carbon dioxide source selected from gaseous carbon dioxide, liquid supercritical carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate, to provide calcium carbonate particles on it.
   Preferred Components: The calcium salt precursor component is selected from calcium chloride, calcium oxide and calcium acetate. The transformation component is selected from sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate. The calcium component is calcium hydroxide, and the transformation component is sodium hydroxide, the calcium component having a water solubility less than the transformation component water solubility. The carbon dioxide source, optionally in the presence of water, is selected from gaseous carbon dioxide, liquid supercritical carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate. The presence of water is selected from atmospheric water vapor about the substrate and water applied to the substrate. The bone void filler substrate comprises a material selected from calcium salts, collagen, natural mammalian bone, polymers, metals and combinations. Carbonatable calcium precursor component is provided as an aqueous solution, and application of the carbonatable calcium precursor component is selected from dipping, soaking and spraying the substrate with it. The substrate is applied to a mammalian bone void.

AU YANG Y
   LI Y
   LI F
AE YANG Y (YANG-Individual)
   HUAIBEI ZHIHUAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5754K
AB    NOVELTY - Segmental hollow square column mold comprises a base (1). A mold main body is clamped on the base. The mold main body includes a pair of top plates (201). A pair of L-shaped templates (202) that are clamped to each other are clamped between a pair of the top plates. A hollow tube (3) is inserted into the circular through hole. A positioning rod (4) is fixedly connected to the base. An injection tube (5) matching the hollow tube which is inserted into the jack of the top plate. The injection pipe includes a concrete injection pipe. An air injection cap is fixedly connected to the bottom end of the concrete injection pipe. The top end of the concrete injection pipe is fixedly connected with a connecting port. A carbon dioxide reaction tank (6) is clamped at the bottom end of the gas injection cap. The carbon dioxide reactor includes a shell. A sealing tube is fixedly connected to the housing.
   USE - Segmental hollow square column mould used for regenerated concrete section.
   ADVANTAGE - The segmental hollow square column mold realizes the introduction of carbon dioxide when using recycled concrete to pour concrete columns, and improves the quality of the formed concrete square column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using a recycled concrete segment hollow square column mold, which involves:
   (A) placing the spliced main body on the base;
   (B) inserting the hollow tube into the mold body;
   (C) matching the mold body with the slot on the base;
   (D) clamping the carbon dioxide reactor to the injection pipe;
   (E) inserting the injection tube into the through hole on the L-shaped template, entering the carbon dioxide reactor in the hollow tube;
   (F) inserting the positioning rod on the base into the sealing tube;
   (G) pushin the push rod up by the positioning rod, jacking up the sealing plug;
   (H) flowing the aluminum sulfate solution stored in the storage tube through the annular groove;
   (I) flowing into the housing from the gap between the sealing tube and the storage tube, the aluminum sulfate solution flowing out of the sealed tube reacts with the calcium hydroxide in the shell;
   (J) generating a large amount of carbon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide enters the mold body from the vent hole on the gas injection cap, at the same time, technicians inject recycled concrete into the main body of the mold through the concrete injection pipe;
   (K) mixing the recycled concrete with carbon dioxide during the injection process;
   (L) producing calcium carbonate in recycled concrete to improve the quality of recycled concrete after solidification;
   (M) injecting concrete for 10-15 minutes, make the height of the concrete accumulated in the main body of the mold close to the upper end of the hollow pipe, and then taking out the injection pipe;
   (N) filling in a hollow ring with the same cross-sectional size as the hollow tube; and
   (O) bonding the hollow tube and the hollow ring to completely close the mold main body and complete the grouting process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of an segmental hollow square column mould.
   Base (1)
   Hollow tube (3)
   Positioning rod (4)
   Injection tube (5)
   Carbon dioxide reaction tank (6)
   Top plates (201)
   L-shaped templates (202)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The recycled concrete includes cement, water, recycled coarse aggregate, recycled fine aggregate, and water reducer in a mass ratio of 1:0.6-0.8:2-3:1-2:0.05-0.08, and calcium hydroxide solution is mixed in the water reducing agent. Preferred Condition: A sodium bicarbonate solution is stored in the shell, and an aluminum sulfate solution is stored in the storage tube.

PN DE102020110357-A1
TI Method for controlling hybrid cooling system in food processing industry, involves using types of cooling such that cooling capacities of indirect cooling device and carbon dioxide compression refrigeration system are optimally merged.
AU UNGERMANN K
AE WOLFRAM UNGERMANN SYSTEMKAELTE GMBH & CO (WOLF-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8481B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining whether need for cooling in the area of a cooling point (5) is made on the basis of a target temperature valid at a point in time in the area of the cooling point. The determination whether temperature difference to the current actual temperature is required in which period by a room control device (13,14), so that the room control device specifies which type of cooling is to be used at the point in time if need for cooling in the area of the cooling point is made. Both types of cooling are used by a control system (15) when a defined temperature level is present such that cooling capacities of an indirect cooling device (3) and a carbon dioxide compression refrigeration system (2) are optimally merged. The indirect cooling device is deactivated and only cooled with the refrigeration system at a given lowered temperature level.
   USE - Method for controlling hybrid cooling system in hot-cold system such as food processing industry, dough or dough products processing industry, meat and sausage processing industry, and industry for processing dairy products e.g. cheese products (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The more controllable and less wear-resistant operation is brought about in energetically unfavorable operating points. The unfavorable operating points for the carbon dioxide injection are avoided and replaced with alternative cooling media in the critical areas. The negative effects of excessive cooling due to directly evaporating carbon dioxide are thus mitigated and damage to the goods is systematically avoided. The liquid medium of the indirect cooling device dampens aggressive cooling effect of the carbon dioxide compression refrigeration system and ensures uniform cooling of the heat exchanger, where the cooling effect is uniform and balanced over the entire surface of the heat exchanger.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hybrid cooling system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the hybrid cooling system.
   Carbon dioxide compression refrigeration system (2)
   Indirect cooling device (3)
   Cooling point (5)
   Room control device (13,14)
   Control system (15)

PN IN326298-B
TI Preparing carbon aerogel, comprises contacting dihydroxy benzene and aldehyde in medium, adding catalyst to reaction mixture, allowing for gelation, and immersing resulting gel in solvent, modified supercritical drying and carbonizing gel.
AU PRAMANIK N C
   POOVAKULATHU A A
   KOLLANNOO R J S
   NARAYANAPANICKER R P
AE DEPT ELECTRONICS & INFORMATION TECHNOLOG (ELIN-Non-standard)
   CENT MATERIALS ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY (MATE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5929C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon aerogel (CAG), comprises: (a) contacting dihydroxy benzene and aldehyde in an aqueous or non-aqueous medium; (b) adding catalyst to the reaction mixture of step (a), followed by allowing it for gelation to obtain a gel; and (c) immersing the gel of step (b) in a solvent, followed by modified supercritical drying and carbonizing the gel to obtain the CAG, where the modified supercritical drying comprises maintaining condition above critical condition of a solvent and the process is devoid of liquid carbon dioxide or super critical carbon dioxide injection.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing CAG which is useful: in an energy storage device (claimed); and as electrodes for supercapacitors e.g. aerocapacitors and electric double layer capacitors.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the CAG that exhibits an average pore diameter of 2-3.6 nm, a total pore volume of 0.25-0.5 cm3/g, an internal surface area of 225-555 m2/g, a density of 0.2-1.15 g/cm3, and an electrical conductivity of 1-7.3 S/cm (claimed) and provides tunable physical properties, large internal surface area with desired pore size and good electrical conductivities by providing accessibility to electrolyte through desired pores of desired sizes for electric double layer capacitors electrodes; utilizes the modified supercritical drying which is inventive over the conventional processes, thus making the process cost effective and energy efficient; provides control over the formation of gel network and retention of gel network during post-processing of gel which are critical for applicability of the CAG obtained in the energy storage devices; eliminates exchange of pore acetone with liquid carbon dioxide and extraction of liquid carbon dioxide with supercritical carbon dioxide; eliminates need of additional high pressure liquid carbon dioxide circulation pump, cooling system and other expensive infrastructures; and imparts improved properties and less time required to complete process, when compared to the CAG prepared by the conventional processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a CAG having a surface area of 470-710 m2/g, prepared by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is either alkali hydroxide or carbonate or acid, where the alkali hydroxide or carbonate comprises sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate or potassium hydroxide, the acid comprises hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or acetic acid and/or hexamethylene tetramine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The dihydroxy benzene and the aldehyde are in a molar ratio of 1:2. The dihydroxy benzene and the catalyst are in a molar ratio of 1:0.001-1:0.010 and the molar concentration of the catalyst is with respect to molar ratio of the dihydroxy benzene. The gelation is carried out by incubating a mixture comprising the dihydroxy benzene, the aldehyde and the catalyst in aqueous and/or non-aqueous medium at 20-70 degrees C for 2-90 hours. The modified supercritical drying, carried out at 240-260 degrees C for 30-90 minutes. The condition is temperature and pressure of the solvent, where the solvent comprises a pore liquid comprising acetone, ethanol and/or propanol. The carbonization of gel is carried out by heating the gel to a temperature of 800-1100 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours in an inert atmosphere comprising gas comprising argon, nitrogen and/or a nitrogen-hydrogen gas mixture. Preferred Components: The dihydroxy benzene comprises resorcinol, catechol and/or hydroxy quinone. The aldehyde comprises formaldehyde and/or furfural. The aqueous or the non-aqueous medium comprises water, ethanol and/or 2-propanol. The solvent comprises a pore liquid comprising acetone, ethanol and/or propanol.
   EXAMPLE - 78.4 g 1-3 Dihydroxy benzene (resorcinol, R) and 117.3 g formaldehyde (F1) in the molar ratio of 1:2 were dissolved in 124 ml aqueous medium (water), followed by adding 0.75 g sodium carbonate (C1). Molar ratio of R:F1:C1 in the aqueous medium was 1:2:0.01 under stirring condition at 25 degrees C for 60 minutes. Obtained polymer mixture was allowed to form gel through gelation at 25 degrees C. Formed gel was immersed in acetone with acetone height of 5 cm above the gel for 20 days in order to exchange the pore water of the gel by acetone. Solvent exchanged gel was placed inside the specially designed high temperature autoclave, followed by heating to 250 degrees C for 60 minutes followed by release of solvent vapors to 1 bar at the vapor release rate of 0.5 bar/minute and natural cooling to ambient temperature of 30 degrees C in order to collect organic aerogel. Organic aerogel obtained was then carbonized at 1000 degrees C for 1 hour under nitrogen atmosphere in an atmosphere controlled furnace to obtain carbon aerogel.

GA 2022D74718
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon sequestration and emission reduction                comprises mixing kiln ash and water discharged from                the cement plant according to a mass ratio of                1:(1-4), aging for 2-24 hours, and then stirring to                obtain a kiln ash slurry. The carbon                dioxide-containing flue gas emitted by the cement                plant is collected in the storage tank. 100 pts.                wt. kiln dust slurry and 5-20 pts. wt. flue gas are                fed into the horizontal wet grinding equipment and                added grinding media for wet grinding. The                wet-milled slurry is dried to obtain the carbon                dioxide-cured material.
   USE - Method for carbon sequestration and emission                reduction by wet grinding kiln dust in a cement                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the carbon dioxide emission                in the factory, neutralizes the high alkalinity of                cement plant kiln ash, and reduces the harm to the                environment. The method has simple technique, and                low cost.

The water is                  tap water or wastewater discharged from a cement                  plant. The acidity and alkalinity of the gas                  overflowing from the horizontal wet grinding                  equipment are detected, where the overflowing gas                  is neutral or alkaline. The wet grinding is                  stopped. The grinding medium is a mixture of                  stainless-steel balls, alumina balls, zirconia                  balls, zirconium silicate balls, and agate                  balls.

   ADVANTAGE - The process provides for improved selectively                for olefins over undesirable side products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for preparing olefins from a mixed                gaseous feed stream, where the mixed gaseous feed                stream comprises three or more components                comprising carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide,                hydrogen, methanol or dimethyl ether, comprises                contacting the mixed gaseous feed stream with a                catalyst comprising an aluminum phosphate compound                of formula: M(II)xAl(1-x)PO4 (I).

   x = 0.002-0.5.

Preferred Components: The mixed                  gaseous feed stream comprises methanol and/or                  dimethyl ether. The mixed gaseous feed stream                  comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and                  optionally carbon monoxide. The mixed gaseous                  feed stream comprises hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide.

PN CN114019036-A
TI Detecting rapid distinguishing commercial sterility of canned bird nest, involves detecting carbon dioxide content in top airand before and after heat preservation of normal bird nest can with spoilage nest tin.
AU HUANG X
   JIANG Y
   YIN P
   TANG J
   JIA J
   LI Z
AE QINGDAO CUSTOMS TECHNOLOGY CENT (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202228286F
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting rapid distinguishing commercial sterility of canned bird's nest involves using headspace gas chromatography-mass spectrometry technology for detecting the carbon dioxide content in top air before and after heat preservation of normal bird nest can with spoilage nest tin. The commercial sterile of the bird's nest can is distinguished based on whether the carbon dioxide content has obvious change.
   USE - Method for detecting rapid distinguishing commercial sterility of canned bird's nest by using headspace gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (HS-GC-MS) technology for controlling spoilage and toxin production caused by microorganisms during the production of canned food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide rapid distinguishing bird nest can commercial sterile detection method to overcome the disadvantages of the existing technology, and provide scientific basis and guidance function for food safety. The detection method is used as a primary tool filter commercial sterile of cubilose can, which reduce the detection cost, and is suitable for fast development of international trade demand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The determination of whether there is a significant change in carbon dioxide content is based on a peak area percentage difference of more than 10%.Preferred Process: The method for detecting commercial sterility of a quickly distinguishable bird's nest can specifically involves taking out 5 mL of contents into a sterilized headspace sample injection bottle by using a sterilization pipette in an aseptic operation after the package is opened, detecting sealed headspace automatic sample injection bottle before heat preservation, setting the conditions of headspace automatic sample injector, and extracting 1 mL of upper layer gas sample injection by using headspace needle. The package is opened, 5 mL contents is taken simultaneously into sterilized headspace sample injection bottle by using sterilization suction pipe in an aseptic operation, headspace bottle is sealed, placed at 30-40degrees Celsius for 1-5 days, detection is performed after heat preservation, the conditions of a headspace automatic sample injector is set, and 1 mL of upper layer gas sample injection is extracted by using headspace needle. The carbon dioxide content of headspace gas before and after the normal bird nest can is insulated is not obviously changed, and carbon dioxide content in the headspace gas before and after the spoilage bird nest can is insulated is obviously increased (the peak area percentage difference exceeds 10%).
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

TI Methane fermentation method of organic waste            discharged from e.g. home, involves adjusting carbon            dioxide partial pressure of gaseous-phase portion of            relay tank so as to be smaller than that of fermenter            and membrane separation tank.

AE JFE ENG CORP (JFES-C)
   JFE ENG CORP (JFES-C)
GA 2021C07867
AB    NOVELTY - Fermentation of methane in organic waste                involves providing a relay tank equipped with a                suction mechanism for gas phase between the                fermenter and membrane separation tank, and                adjusting the carbon dioxide partial pressure of                the gas phase portion of the relay tank which is                less than the carbon dioxide partial pressure of                fermentation tank or membrane separation tank, and                precipitating calcium carbonate in the relay                tank.
   USE - Fermentation of methane in organic waste. Uses                include but are not limited to organic refuse and                daily dishes discarded from home, restaurant,                municipal solid waste, processing factory of                agricultural products or marine product, and sewage                sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents precipitation of scale of                calcium carbonate within fermentation apparatus,                and methane fermentation can be stably operated                over a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for methane                fermentation apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                methane fermentation apparatus. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PN CN113414193-A
TI Polycarbonate film peeling method, involves using carbon dioxide sand blasting machine to spray sand and peel off film layer after surface of substrate becomes crisp, and processing film layer for separating product substrate.
AU FU Q
AE PENTA TECHNOLOGY SUZHOU CO LTD (PENT-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2410A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a substrate to-be-peeled off a film layer under oxygen environment. The film layer is fully irradiated under fixed wavelength UV light. Resonant fluorenyl compound and six-element ring product are generated. A surface of the film layer is found to become yellow and crisp. Irradiation is stopped after the surface of the film layer is found to become yellow and crisp. A carbon dioxide sand blasting machine is used to spray sand and peel off the film layer after a surface of the substrate becomes crisp. The film layer is processed for separating a product substrate. Gaseous dry ice is sprayed by the carbon dioxide sand blasting machine.
   USE - Polycarbonate film peeling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables peeling a polycarbonate film without damaging the product substrate and completely peeling the film layer without residue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a polycarbonate film peeling method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113336638-A
TI High-efficiency synthesis of salicylic acid comprises efficient dehydrating sodium phenate solution by placing sodium phenate solution in medium-pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into above-mentioned dry leavening compound.
AU SONG G
   SHEN H
   YANG Y
   LI X
AE NINGXIA WANXIANGYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021A45754
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency synthesis of salicylic acid comprises (a) efficient dehydrating sodium phenate solution by placing sodium phenate solution in medium-pressure kettle, removing part of the water under reduced pressure, adding phenol, separating part of the phenol and water form a binary azeotrope and binary azeotrope under vacuum to obtain a dry leavened compound, which is composed of phenol and sodium phenate, (b) carboxylation reaction by introducing carbon dioxide into the above-mentioned dry leavening compound, where in the leavening compound, the sodium phenolate reacts with carbon dioxide to generate sodium salicylate, separating phenol that has not participated in the reaction under vacuum to obtain sodium salicylate after the completion of reaction and (c) acidification reacting by adding sodium salicylate with inorganic acid for reacting to obtain salicylic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for high-efficiency synthesis of salicylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has greatly shortens reaction time and improves production efficiency; recycles binary azeotrope and phenol separated in this process; saves resources and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (d) preparing sodium phenolate solution by reacting phenol and sodium hydroxide solution to obtain sodium phenolate solution. The binary azeotrope separated in the step (a) is used in the step (d) to react with the sodium hydroxide solution to prepare a sodium phenate solution, thus realizes recovery and utilization of phenol. The phenol separated in the step (b) is used in the step (a) to realize the recycling of phenol. The reaction temperature of phenol and sodium hydroxide in the step (d) is 75-85 degrees C. In the step (b), the pressure of the carboxylation reaction is 0.6-0.8 MPa. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of phenol to sodium phenolate added in the step (a) is 0.8-1.5:1. The molar ratio of phenol to sodium phenolate is 1:1. The purity of the phenol added in the step (a) is greater than or equal to 99%. In the step (c), the inorganic acid is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid or phosphoric acid.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC B10-C03
IP C07C-051/02; C07C-051/15; C07C-065/10

PN WO2021137176-A1; EP3845290-A1; EP4084894-A1; BR112022013216-A2
TI Producing clathrate hydrates carbon dioxide, by            lowering temperature of aqueous solution stream to            pre-defined temperature, introducing solution stream            into hydrator, and collecting aqueous slurry of            hydrates from outlet of hydrator.
AU BRITO LOPES J C
   GOMES DE QUEIROZ DIAS M
   DOS SANTOS COSTA M F
   DE MOURA TEIXEIRA C A
   NOGUEIRA DOS SANTOS R J
   LOPES J C B
   DIAS M M G D
   COSTA M F D S
   TEIXEIRA C A D M
   SANTOS R J N
AE PETROGAL SA (PETR-Non-standard)
   PETROLEOS PORTUGAL PETROGAL SA (PETR-Non-standard)
   PETROGAL SA (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 202177101K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing clathrate hydrates in a                continuous process involves lowering the                temperature of an aqueous solution stream to a                pre-defined temperature range, introducing the                aqueous solution stream into a hydrator through a                first inlet, introducing a stream of at least one                hydrate-forming compound into the hydrator through                a second inlet, contacting the hydrate-forming                stream and the aqueous stream inside a mixing                element of the hydrator for producing an aqueous                slurry of clathrate hydrates, where the hydrator                has an operational temperature of 0-5 degrees C,                pressure of 10-40 bar and, hydrate-forming stream                rate of 1-500 ton/h, and collecting the aqueous                slurry of hydrates from an outlet of the hydrator,                where the hydrator is an array of static mixing                elements comprising chambers.
   USE - The method is useful for producing clathrate                hydrates carbon dioxide in a continuous process,                capturing, storing, or transporting gas, or gas                separation, water desalination, or water                purification (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                crystalline hydrate of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrate conversion                  rate is at least 80% or 90%. The method further                  involves concentrating the hydrate crystals, or                  gasification of the crystalline hydrates. The                  operational temperature of the hydrator is 2-5                  degrees C. The pressure is 20-40 bar, preferably                  30-40 bar. The aqueous solution stream and the                  hydrate-forming stream are introduced into the                  static mixing elements of the hydrator through                  high energy jets that enhances the mixing                  intensity of the hydrate-forming stream and the                  aqueous stream inside the chamber. The                  hydrate-forming stream is introduced into the                  hydrator at a rate hydrate-forming compound and                  water mass flow rate of at least 5%, preferably                  10-30%. The hydrate-forming stream and the                  aqueous stream are mixed in a chamber of the                  hydrator at a Reynolds number of the inlet                  streams of at least 100 and local mixing times                  not more than 1 second. The heat transfer                  coefficients of the hydrator is 1000-8000                  W.m-2.k-1, preferably W.m-2.k-1. Preferred                  Component: The hydrate-forming compound is chosen                  from a list of molecules with molecular radii                  less than 9 Angstrom comprising carbon dioxide                  stream, carbon monoxide stream, hydrogen stream,                  nitrogen stream, 1-5C hydrocarbons stream,                  hydrogen sulfide stream, or their mixtures. The                  1-5C hydrocarbons stream is methane, ethane,                  propane, or butane, and/or the aqueous solution                  stream contains salts, gases, organic promoters,                  or their mixtures. The hydrator is an array of                  static mixing elements comprising chambers,                  preferably a series of interconnected static                  mixing elements. The hydrator comprises a stack                  of a network mixer plate for performing the                  hydration and a heat exchange plate, where the                  network mixer plate comprises an array of static                  mixing elements comprising chambers, where each                  chamber being interconnected by at least two                  channels to at least two other chambers for                  mixing and dividing one or more fluids                  sequentially through the chambers, where the heat                  exchange plate comprises a channel for the flow                  of a cooling liquid, where the channel of the                  heat exchanger plate and the chambers of the                  network mixer plate are lined up to transfer heat                  between the chambers and the channel, where each                  chamber of the network mixer plate is a spherical                  or cylindrical chamber, comprising at least two                  or three channels and at least two or three                  apertures for connection to the channels, where                  the temperature of the cooling liquid is -5                  degrees C to 0 degrees C.

PN CN112898800-A; CN112898800-B
TI Method for extracting indigo blue, involves            washing blue grass leaves containing indigo and soaking            in water, and separating leaves after soaking, adding            alkali to liquid to adjust pH to alkaline.
AU GAO M
AE SHANDONG YUNQING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202165283K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting indigo blue involves                washing the blue grass leaves containing indigo and                soak them in water, and separating the leaves after                soaking, (2) adding alkali to the liquid obtained                in step (1) to adjust the pH to alkaline, and                passing nitrogen or carbon dioxide into the liquid                under airtight conditions, the dissolved oxygen in                the liquid is below 20% of the saturated dissolved                oxygen value at the corresponding temperature, (3)                continuing to stir the liquid with nitrogen or                carbon dioxide for more than 1 hour, adding acid to                it to adjust the pH to acid sedimentation indigo,                (4) obtaining a precipitate by solid-liquid                separation and drying.
   USE - Method for extracting indigo blue.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide and other gases are                introduced to reduce the dissolved oxygen value in                the liquid under alkaline conditions, thereby                reducing the generation of by-products and the                sedimentation of plant tissues, and effectively                increasing the concentration of indigo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Bioenzymes are added to                  water, adding 0.1-2 g bioenzymes per 1 L of                  water, the bioenzymes are one or more                  combinations of glucose oxidase, chymotrypsin or                  trypsin. Preferred Composition: The soaking time                  after adding the bioenzyme is 0.5-12 hours in                  step (1). Nitrogen or carbon dioxide is                  introduced into the liquid for 1-3 hours in step                  (2). The alkali is added to adjust the pH to 9-13                  in step (2). The liquid with nitrogen or carbon                  dioxide is continuously stirred for 1-5 hours in                  step (3) after aeration. The acid is added to                  adjust the pH to 1-5 in step (3).

PN CN112830873-A
TI Continuously synthesizing formic acid, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of a catalyst in an autoclave to form formic acid, and using a selective permeable membrane to separate formic acid solution from the catalyst.
AU ZHANG Z
   LIU S
   HAN B
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 202160703T
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously synthesizing formic acid, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst in an autoclave to form formic acid, and using a selective permeable membrane to separate the formic acid solution from the catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously synthesizing formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - Without the need for alkali additives, carbon dioxide and hydrogen generate formic acid under the action of a homogeneous catalyst, and the generated formic acid solution permeates through the semi-permeable membrane, and the catalyst is retained in the solution due to its large volume, thus realizing continuous production of formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for the continuous synthesis of formic acid, which is an autoclave, the inside of the autoclave is a polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, and the reactor is divided into two parts by a selective permeation membrane, namely the left and right parts or the upper and lower parts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a compound containing ruthenium, iridium or rhodium and a corresponding ligand in the molar ratio of 0.25:0.6-1.2; the ligand is 1,3,5-triaza-7-phosphadamantane, meta-trisulfonated triphenylphosphine sodium salt, meta-monosulfonated phenylphosphine sodium salt, 2,2'-bipyridine-6,6'-diol, and/or 2,2'-biimidizoline. The selective permeation membrane is a permeable membrane arranged horizontally or a permeable membrane arranged vertically. Preferred Method: The total pressure of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1-30 MPa; the temperature of the reaction is 20-90 degrees C; and the reaction time is 10-240 hours. The selective permeation membrane is reverse osmosis membrane, anion exchange membrane, and a cation exchange membrane. The reaction is carried out in water. The reaction is carried out under alkali-free and acid-free conditions, that is, no alkaline substance or acidic substance is used as a promoter, that is, there is no alkali and carbonate, bicarbonate, formate and related salts added in the system.

PN CN112679625-A
TI Extraction of red jujube polysaccharide involves extracting red jujube, adding natural clarifying agent, centrifuging, concentrating, countercurrent chromatography separating, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, and combining extracts.
AU BAI B
   SONG L
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG C
   HOU X
   CHENG M
AE UNIV TARIM (UTAR-C)
GA 2021457896
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of red jujube polysaccharide comprises cleaning and drying red jujube, crushing, adding 75-95 wt.% ethanol solution, microwave heating, cooling to room temperature, decompressing, and concentrating to obtain extract; dissolving natural clarifying agent in distilled water, dissolving natural clarifying agent in 1 wt.% glacial acetic acid solution, dissolving the extract in water at 55-80 degrees C, adding second solution, stirring for 30-40 minutes, adding first solution after 1-2 hours, stirring for 40-60 minutes, taking out, centrifuging, taking supernatant, decompressing and concentrating; taking n-butanol, ethyl acetate and water at ratio of 3-5:1-2:6-8:6-8, mixing, layering, and high-efficiency countercurrent chromatography separating; and adding into separation kettle with carbon dioxide, extracting, decompressing, separating mixed solution of carbon dioxide and red jujube polysaccharide, repeating process for 3-5 times, and combining red jujube polysaccharide fine product.
   USE - The method is used for extraction of red jujube polysaccharide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts high purity red jujube polysaccharide extract and has high extracting efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The microwave heating is done for 3-5 minutes at 100-300 W. The centrifuging is done for 10-15 minutes at 5000-8000 revolutions/minute (rpm). The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done using carbon dioxide pressure of 10-15 MPa, carbon dioxide storage tank temperature of 25-35 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow of 12-18 L/hour, separating kettle pressure of 6-10 MPa, extraction temperature kettle of 40-50 degrees C, and extracting time of 90-120 minutes. Preferred Components: The clarifying agent is II-type ZTC1+1 natural clarifying agent or clarifying agent KBT-ZTC..

   XIONG L
   ZHANG Y
   QIAN X
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)

The                polyimide-based carbon fiber is composed of                ultra-micropores with diameter of less than or                equal to 0.8 nm and micropores with diameter of                less than or equal to 2 nm, where the volume of the                micropores is 0.05-0.75 cm3/g, the specific surface                area of polyimide-based carbon fiber is 200-2500                m2/g and the nitrogen content is 1-7.5%.
   USE - The polyimide-based carbon fiber is useful in                adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the polyimide-based carbon fiber                comprising, uniformly mixing polyimide fiber and                activating agent and then placing in inert gas                atmosphere for high-temperature treatment to obtain                final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume of the                  ultra-micropores is preferably 0.2-0.4 cm3/g. The activating agent is                  potassium carbonate, zinc chloride, phosphoric                  acid, potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide.                  The mass between polyimide fiber and activating                  agent is 1:0.3-8. Preferred Method: The                  temperature of the high-temperature treatment is                  500-1200 degrees C. The method comprises mixing,                  where the mixing is solid-state mixing or                  solid-liquid mixing, the solid-state mixing is to                  uniformly disperse the activating agent on the                  surface of the polyimide fiber and the                  solid-liquid mixing is to soak the polyimide                  fiber in the aqueous solution of activating                  agent, mixing and stirring and then taking out                  and drying. When the activating agent is                  potassium carbonate or zinc chloride, the mixing                  is solid-state mixing and when the activating                  agent is phosphoric acid, potassium carbonate or                  zinc chloride, the mixing is solid-liquid                  mixing.

TI Controlling morphology of tin dioxide in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves preparing tin dioxide by hydrothermal method, and controlling volume ratio of the solvent in hydrothermal method to volume of reaction kettle.
AU ZHANG J
   CAO X
   MA J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2020549953
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling morphology of tin dioxide involves preparing tin dioxide by hydrothermal method, and where morphology of tin dioxide is controlled by controlling the volume ratio of the solvent in hydrothermal method to the volume of the reaction kettle.
   USE - Method for controlling morphology of tin dioxide used in the preparation of tin-tin dioxide composite materials and in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to control morphology of tin dioxide that shows a faster kinetic reaction speed, higher current density and stronger stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a tin-tin dioxide composite material, which involves calcining tin dioxide in a mixed atmosphere for not more than 4 hours, where the calcination temperature is not higher than 450 degrees C, the mixture atmosphere refers to an inert atmosphere and a reducing atmosphere; and
   (2) a working electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, whcih comprises the tin dioxide or the tin-tin dioxide composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ratio of the volume of the solvent to the volume of the reactor is 18-22:50. The terephthalic acid is added to the hydrothermal reaction system,w here the ratio of tin source to terephthalic acid is 1:0.9-1.1. The reaction conditions of the hydrothermal method are: 175-185 degrees C for 20-28 hours. The tin source is added to the mixture of tetramethylammonium hydroxide and water, and then the hydrothermal reaction is performed. The calcination time is 1.8-2.2 hours. The calcination temperature is 395-405 degrees C. Preferred Components: The solvent in the hydrothermal method is tetramethylammonium hydroxide and water mixture.

TI Preparation of dialkyl carbonate used as gasoline additive, and diesel fuel additive, involves performing carbonic acid ester formation reaction between carbon dioxide and alcohol in presence of carbodiimide compound.
AU HONGYU L
   TAKEHIKO I
   YOUSUKE S
   HIDEFUMI H
   LIU H
   ISOBE T
   SHINKAI Y
   HARADA H
AE MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
GA 2020507149
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dialkyl carbonate involves performing a carbonic acid ester formation reaction between carbon dioxide and an alcohol (I), in the presence of a carbodiimide compound (II).
   USE - Preparation of dialkyl carbonate. Uses include but are not limited to gasoline additive, diesel fuel additive to reduce particles in exhaust gas, pharmaceutical, agricultural chemical, alkylating agent, carbonylating agent, solvent, electrolyte for lithium-ion battery, raw material for lubricating oil, and oxygen scavenger for rust prevention of boiler pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, easy, and effective preparation of dialkyl carbonate even at short period of time with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of dialkyl carbonate involves performing a carbonic acid ester formation reaction between carbon dioxide and an alcohol of formula (I), in the presence of a carbodiimide compound of formula (II).
   R1 = optionally substituted 1-10C alkyl;and
   R2,R3 = 5C or less alkyl (optionally substituted by amino), 1-20C alkyl, optionally branched 1-20C cycloalkyl, optionally substituted 12C or more alkyl, or 6-30C aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves generating a dialkyl carbonate and water by carbonic acid ester formation reaction, reacting the carbodiimide compound with water, using 0.1-10 mol% carbodiimide compound with respect to 1.0 mol% alcohol, performing carbonic acid ester formation reaction in the presence of a solid catalyst comprising cerium oxide and/or inorganic catalyst, and performing carbonic acid ester formation reaction without using solvent. The carbonic acid ester formation reaction is performed at temperature of 100-200 degrees C, and pressure of 1-20 MPa. Preferred Components: The carbodiimide compound is preferably of formula (III).R'2, R''2, R'3, R''3 = H, 3C or less alkyl (optionally substituted by amino), optionally branched 6C or less alkyl;andR'2+R''2,R'3+R''3 = optionally branched 5-10C cycloalkyl.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R2,R3 = isopropyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Chemical looping process catalyst used for            producing ethylene form ethane and carbon monoxide from            carbon dioxide, comprises complex metal oxide            comprising iron, cerium, and titanium.
AU BAE J W
   JEONG M H
   HYE J M
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
   UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 2020318177
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical looping process catalyst comprises                a complex metal oxide comprising iron, cerium, and                titanium.
   USE - Chemical looping process catalyst used for                producing ethylene from ethane and carbon monoxide                from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent ethylene                selectivity, carbon monoxide conversion, and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) production of catalyst, which involves                producing a mixed solution (S1) by mixing iron                precursor and cerium precursor with an aqueous                solution comprising titanium precursor, producing a                mixed solution (S2) by adding urea to the mixed                solution (S1), producing precipitate (P1) by                cooling and filtering the mixed solution (S2), and                producing a complex metal oxide by performing hot                calcination of the precipitate (P1); and
   (2) production of ethylene and carbon                monoxide, which involves arranging the catalyst                inside a chemical looping reactor, heating (H1) the                reactor to a reduction temperature or more,                injecting ethane-containing gas into the reactor to                perform a reduction reaction, heating (H2) the                reactor, and injecting carbon dioxide-containing                gas into the reactor to perform an oxidation                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  iron to titanium is 0.075-0.3. The molar ratio of                  cerium to titanium is 0.075-0.3. The catalyst has                  a ethane conversion of 0.7% or more, and an                  ethylene selectivity of 62.0% or more. Preferred                  Components: The complex metal oxide further                  comprises at least one of a rutile phase titania                  or an anatase phase titania, ceria, or                  iron-titanium oxide containing iron and titanium.                  The complex metal oxide comprises a carrier, and                  an active component substituted onto the carrier.                  The carrier comprises titania. The active                  component comprises a perovskite-structured                  material comprising iron and cerium substituted                  onto titanium. The titanium precursor comprises                  titanium oxysulfate. The iron precursor comprises                  iron chloride. The cerium precursor comprises                  cerium chloride. Preferred Conditions: The hot                  calcination is carried out at 500-1100 degrees C.                  The heating (H1) is carried out at 450-650                  degrees C. The heating (H2) is carried out at                  600-800 degrees C. Preferred Process: The                  production of ethylene and carbon dioxide further                  involves converting an inside of the reactor into                  an inert gas atmosphere after injecting the                  ethane-containing gas and before the heating                  (H2). The production of ethylene and carbon                  dioxide further involves repeating the                  arrangement of the catalyst, the heating (H1),                  the injection of the ethane-containing gas, the                  heating (H2), and the injection of the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas in sequential                  manner.

TI Black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode direct methanol fuel battery, has black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode whose bottom end is formed with carbon dioxide discharge hole.
AU JU J
   JU Y
   ZHANG Q
   YUAN H
   YU Y
   HUANG P
   FENG Y
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 202033693R
AB    NOVELTY - The battery has a film electrode connected with a battery shell. An air chamber is connected with the battery shell and the film electrode. The film electrode is provided with a methanol gas reaction chamber, a cathode diffusion layer, a cathode catalyst layer, a Nafion film and a black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode. The cathode diffusion layer is fixedly connected with the battery shell through a welding point. A top end of a methanol gas reaction chamber is formed with feeding holes. A feeding sealing cover seals the feeding hole. The air chamber is formed with a water discharging hole. A bottom end of the black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode is formed with a carbon dioxide discharge hole.
   USE - Black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode direct methanol fuel battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The battery has low production cost, high catalytic activity and strong anti-carbon dioxide poisoning ability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a black phosphorus-titanium dioxide nanotube/titanium anode direct methanol fuel battery.

PN WO2020050976-A1; CN112533933-A; EP3847178-A1; US2021292346-A1; US11168098-B2; EP3847178-B1
TI Forming reaction product comprising            alkylalkoxysilane by heating ingredients comprising            alkyl ether and carbon dioxide and source of silicon            and catalyst and forming reaction product.
AU ROBERTS J
   PUSHKAREV V
   KATSOULIS D
AE DOW SILICONES CORP (DOWO-C)
   DOW SILICONES CORP (DOWO-C)
   DOW SILICONES CORP (DOWO-C)
GA 2020207629
AB    NOVELTY - Method for forming a reaction product                comprising alkylalkoxysilane involves (i) heating                ingredients comprising (a) an alkyl ether and                carbon dioxide and (b) a source of silicon and                catalyst at 150-400 degrees C and (ii) forming the                reaction product.
   USE - The method is useful for forming a reaction                product comprising alkylalkoxysilane, preferably                dimethyldimethoxysilane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the need to use halogenated                compounds such as halosilanes, hydrogen halides                and/or halogenated hydrocarbons (such as methyl                chloride) which are considered less                environmentally-friendly agents (due to the                subsequent hydrochloric acid generation upon                preparation of siloxane polymers) during the method                and avoids the need of preparation of                hydrocarbylhalosilane first and converting,                minimizes production of undesirable by-products and                is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for forming a reaction product                comprising alkylalkoxysilane involves (i) heating                ingredients comprising (a) alkyl ether and carbon                dioxide and (b) a source of silicon and catalyst at                150-400 degrees C and forming the reaction product,                where alkylalkoxysilane has of formula:                R(4-a)Si(OR)a.
   R=alkyl group;and
   a=1-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves using                  hydrocarbyl ether chosen from dimethyl ether,                  diethyl ether or methyl ethyl ether. The method                  further involves adding a promoter. The method                  further involves adding hydrogen during the step                  (i). The method further involves purging and/or                  treating the ingredient (b) before the step (i).                  Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  copper. The ingredient (b) is copper silicide,                  where copper silicide is binary copper silicide                  and copper silicide has of formula: Cu5Si and                  comprises copper, silicon and an additional metal                  chosen from aluminum, tin, titanium and their                  combinations. The ingredient (b) comprises a                  contact mass comprising silicon and copper. The                  promoter comprises aluminum, tin, titanium or                  their combinations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction product                  comprises alkylalkoxysilane comprising                  methyltrimethoxysilane, dimethyldimethoxysilane,                  trimethylmethoxysilane and tetramethoxysilane.                  The reaction product comprises alkylalkoxysilane                  of formula: R2Si(OR)2. Preferred                  Definitions:R=methyl, ethyl or phenyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN DE102018212580-A1
TI Electrolyzer used for carbon dioxide reduction, comprises electrolytic cell with cathode which is designed as gas-diffusion electrode arranged between cathode gap and gas space comprising liquid electrolyte containing conductive salt.
AU FERNANDEZ S E M
   HANEBUTH M
   KRAUSE R
   RELLER C
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2020093173
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolyzer (10) comprises an electrolytic cell (1) with an anode (A) in an anode space (AR) and a cathode (K) in a cathode space (KR). The cathode is designed as a gas-diffusion electrode (GDE) arranged between a cathode gap (KS) and a gas space (GR). The gas space has a feed gas inlet (2) and a product gas outlet (3), and comprising a liquid electrolyte comprising a conductive salt and an additive to avoid solid formation.
   USE - Electrolyzer is used for reduction of carbon dioxide generated in fossil fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolyzer can be operated at a higher current density and is reliable over long term.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of electrolyzer in electrolysis operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a circuit of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer.
   Electrolytic cell (1)
   Feed gas inlet (2)
   Product gas outlet (3)
   Electrolyzer (10)
   Anode (A)
   Anode space (AR)
   Gas-diffusion electrode (GDE)
   Gas space (GR)
   Cathode (K)
   Cathode space (KR)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The conductive salt is potassium conductive salt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive comprises a complexing agent which is a chelating agent which is being predominantly chemisorbed or polymerized within the gas-diffusion electrode, a crown ether or a crown ether derivative, preferably 18-crown-6-chelating agent or 18-crown-6-derivative, and a kinetically-inhibiting substance including 0.01-1% surface-active substance based on cation concentration. The kinetically-inhibiting substance is chosen from metaphosphates, organic polyacids such as polyacrylic acid or polymaleic acid, phosphonates, and polyphosphates. The electrolyzer is used in combination with additives as required in a long-term operation of the electrolyzer.

PN CN110575680-A
TI Method for performing double-super-extraction of sea nutrients, involves cleaning to-be-extracted seafood followed by crushing to obtain seafood powder, mixing with tert-butylhydroquinone and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU YU J
AE FUJIAN QIYUANTANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202000147H
AB    NOVELTY - A double-super-extraction of sea nutrients performing method involves cleaning to-be-extracted seafood, placing the cleaned to-be-extracted seafood in ultra micro crusher for crushing, obtaining seafood powder, mixing 100 mass parts seafood powder with 0.05-0.2 mass parts tert-butylhydroquinone, transferring the mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, passing anhydrous ethanol at a flow rate of 5-8 l/h as carrier and carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 400-500 l/hour and extracting at pressure of 30-40 MPa and 40-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
   USE - Method for performing double-super-extraction of sea nutrients.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing double-super-extraction of sea nutrients by realizing better extraction effect, avoiding thermal degradation of extract at high temperature and introduction of other impurities, ensuring integrity of nutrients and utilizing tert-butylhydroquinone to encapsulate and protect sea nutrients before extraction and prevent deterioration and damage of sea nutrients during extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The to-be-extracted seafood includes shrimp, fish, crab or seaweed.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves utilizing seafood powder and hydrochloric acid solution in a ratio of 1 kg:(2-4) l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves utilizing tert-butylhydroquinone and absolute ethanol in a ratio of (0.05-0.2) kg:(0.5-1) l.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   USE - Hop extract feed additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively remove the pungent odor of hops, and retain hop's active ingredients to the utmost extent, such as beta -acid and alpha -acid main active substances. The feed additive is convenient so store and transport and great significance to the development of animal husbandry, widens application range of hops, can improve health condition and immunity of the body, and reduces ratio of meat to meat.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the feed additive comprising crushing hops, sieving, and adding to extracting kettle; cooling carbon dioxide gas through liquefier to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, high pressure pumping to extracting kettle at 15-30 MPa, heating to 365-50 degrees C for 2-4 hours at liquid carbon dioxide flow rate of 25-30 L/hour, adding to separation device, separating hop's active ingredient, adding licorice powder and corn cob powder, and stirring to obtain solid additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particles sizes of licorice powder and corn cob powder are 400-500 mu m. Preferred Method: The separating is done at first-stage separation temperature and pressure of 30-45 degrees C and 5-20 MPa, and at second-stage separation temperature and pressure of 20-35 degrees C and at 3-10 MPa.

TI Coffee degassing device comprises coffee bin, hollow tree within bin, comprising opening in communication with interior of coffee bin, and diffusion gas supply conduit in communication with hollow tree.
AU EPHRAIM D
   LEPPER S
   WILLEBRAND B
AE MODERN PROCESS EQUIP INC (MODE-Non-standard)
GA 201969734R
AB    NOVELTY - Coffee degassing device (11) comprises a coffee bin (14); a hollow tree within the bin, comprising an opening in communication with the interior of the coffee bin; and, a diffusion gas supply conduit (28) in communication with the hollow tree.
   USE - A coffee degassing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The conduit provides an exit path for carbon dioxide off-gassing from the coffee to exit through on a passive basis without a diffusion gas necessarily being used or circulated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coffee degassing method which involves supplying a diffusion gas into a degassing tree in an enclosed bin containing roasted coffee, the degassing tree is at least partially embedded in the roasted coffee; diffusing a carbon dioxide from the coffee with the diffusion gas; and, exhausting the carbon dioxide and the diffusion gas from the bin.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of the coffee degassing device.
   Coffee degassing device (11)
   Coffee bin (14)
   Trunk (18)
   Branches (20, 22, 24, 26)
   Supply conduit (28)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The tree comprises a trunk (18) and at least one branch (20, 22, 24, 26) in communication with the trunk. It comprises branches, each of the branches comprising an at least partially open sidewall to expose the branch to coffee within the bin. Preferred Process: Exhausting involves releasing the carbon dioxide and the diffusion gas through a one way valve. The diffusion gas is not forced through the roasted coffee outside a space occupied by the degassing tree. Diffusing is further defined in that the diffusing a carbon dioxide from the coffee with the diffusion gas occurs at the degassing tree.
