TI Photo-catalyst used in photo-catalytic reduction            of carbon dioxide comprises bismuth molybdate as            lamellar connected by intermolecular force and            hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide adsorbed on the            surface of the lamellar bismuth molybdate.

   YU J

   YANG L

AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 201933613X
AB    NOVELTY - Photo-catalyst comprises bismuth molybdate as                lamellar connected by an intermolecular force to                form a spheroidal structure, and hexadecyl                trimethyl ammonium bromide adsorbed on the surface                of the lamellar bismuth molybdate by bromide                ions.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in photo-catalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide in the reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has pores volume of greater than                0.14 cm3.g-1, specific surface area greater than 41                m2.g-1 and has average diameter of 3-4 mu m; and                has excellent catalytic performance on                photo-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, which                can reaches the methanol content to 30.4-106.5 mu                mol gcatal.-1 and ethanol content to 8.7-45.4 nu                mmol gcatal.-1 after reacting for 4 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing photo-catalyst comprising providing                bismuth source, molybdenum source, hexadecyl                trimethyl ammonium bromide and glycol to reaction                material liquid and heating by self-assembly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  mixing bismuth chloride dissolved alcohol                  solution with hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium                  bromide solution, and molybdenum source solution                  to obtain reaction material liquid. The heating                  process is carried out at 120-180 degrees C for                  1.5-6 hours. Preferred Components: The molar                  ratio of bismuth to molybdenum source bismuth                  molybdate ion source is 2: 1. The molar ratio of                  hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide and bismuth                  source whose mol ratio is 1.8-2.5:5. The                  concentration of bismuth ions in bismuth source                  is 0.04- 0.055mmol/ml. The concentration of                  molybdenum ion in the molybdenum source is                  0.020-0.030 mmol/ml. The bismuth source is                  bismuth nitrate and the molybdenum source is                  sodium molybdate.

PT P
PN KR2019030361-A; KR2002640-B1
TI Producing mesoporous hollow silica material used as carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g. forming polystyrene as core material by adding styrene and AIBA to mixed solvent containing distilled water and acetone, and adding CTACl and TEOS.
AU BAE J Y
   YUN T K
   LEE J T
   LEE J Y
   JANG S G
   LEE J
AE UNIV KEIMYUNG IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYKM-C)
GA 201929724Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mesoporous hollow silica material comprises (a) forming polystyrene as a core material by adding styrene as a precursor and 2,2'-azobis(2-amidinopropane) dihydrochloride (AIBA) as a thermal initiator to a solvent in which distilled water and acetone are mixed at predetermined ratio, (b) adding the polystyrene to mixed solution containing distilled water and ethanol at predetermined ratio, adding cetyltrimethylammonium chloride (CTACl) as cationic surfactant, and precursor of tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS), stirring for predetermined time, adding ammonia water, and calcining at predetermined temperature for predetermined time to produce mesoporous hollow silica material, and (c) introducing an amine functional group into the mesoporous hollow silica material by adding predetermined amine-based precursor, stirring mixture for predetermined time, and drying at predetermined temperature for predetermined time.
   USE - The mesoporous hollow silica material is useful as carbon dioxide adsorbents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves adsorption rate of carbon dioxide of mesoporous silica material by introducing amine functional group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), distilled water and acetone are mixed in volume ratio of 3:0.5-2. In the step (b), 1-20 ml styrene and AIBA are added to 1 L mixed solvent, where the AIBA is added in accordance with the molar ratio of the styrene, and molar ratio of styrene and AIBA is 1:0.1-0.3. In the step (b), distilled water and ethanol are mixed in volume ratio of 1:1-3. The step (b) comprises adding 10-50 ml CTACl to 1 l mixed solvent, mixing 10-50 ml TEOS, adjusting pH to 8-10 with aqueous ammonia and calcining at 600 degrees C for 5 hours. The method further comprises drying the synthesized material obtained in the step (a) and (b) at 70-90 degrees C for 15-24 hours. In the step (c), the amine-based precursor is added in accordance with the weight ratio of the mesoporous hollow silica material formed in the step (b), where mesoporous hollow silica material and amine-based precursor are in weight ratio of 1:0.1-1.5, and the weight ratio is increased to increase the carbon dioxide adsorption amount, stirred at room temperature for 1 hour, solvent is removed through vacuum concentrator, and dried at 80-100 degrees C for 2 hours. In the step (c), mesoporous hollow silica material is added according to weight ratio of ethanol, where mesoporous hollow silica material and ethanol are in weight ratio of 1:100-150. Preferred Components: The amine-based precursor includes polyethylenimine, tetraethylenepentamine, (2-methylbutyl)amine, tris(2-ethylhexyl)amine, ethyl amine, hydrazine, (3-methylpentyl)amine, melamine, ethylenediamine, 3,5-diamino-1,2,4-triazole, adenine, 2-amino-1,4-benzenedicarboxylic acid, isopropanolamine, 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, ethylenediamine, tetraethylenepentamine, aziridine, 5-amino-tetrazole, triethylene tetramine, 3-picolylamine, monoethanolamine, 5-(4-carboxybenzoylamino)isophthalic acid, N,N'-aminopropyl triethoxysilane, N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, poly-(L-lysine), 1.8-diazabucyclo-(5,4,0)-undec-7-ene, dodecylamine, bis((triethoxysilyl)propyl)ethylenediamine, tri-(2-aminoethyl)-amine, (3-trimethoxysilylpropyl)diethylenetriamine, N-carboxyanhydrides of L-amine, 1,2-bis(4-pyridylmethylene)hydrazine, and/or L-arginine. The pore structure of mesoporous hollow silica material forms hexagonal structure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

The air inlet pipeline is connected with a sterilizing plate (22) that is connected between the air exhaust fan and the dust filter plate. An inner part of the air inlet pipeline close to the sterilizing plate is provided with the sterilizing plate. A spraying mechanism (5) sprays a sterilizing liquid.
   USE - Catalytic biological carbon dioxide reduction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The sterilizing plate and the dust filtering plate are respectively used for blocking the bacteria and dust in the gas after the gas containing a large amount of carbon dioxide enters the air inlet pipeline, thus preventing it from entering into the carbon reducing cavity, inactivating the bacteria carried in gas, and preventing the pollution to the carbon-reducing cavity. The device drives the spraying mechanism to continuously spray the sterilizing liquid on the sterilization plate after starting the exhaust fan, so that the device can continuously absorb and convert the carbon dioxide in gas to solve the problem in the background technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for implementing a catalytic biological carbon dioxide reduction device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front sectional view of a Catalytic biological carbon dioxide reduction device.

TI Preventing melting of hypochlorite crystal, used in chlorine-containing gas generator for sterilization applications, involves removing carbon dioxide from storage container for storing hypochlorite crystal by supplying decarbonated gas having carbon dioxide to storage container.
AU IWATA T
   EJIMA Y
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 202257887R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preventing melting of a hypochlorite crystal, involves (a) removing carbon dioxide from a storage container for storing the hypochlorite crystal, where the removal step involves supplying a decarbonated gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of 10 ppm or lower to the storage container until the effective chlorine concentration of the gas discharged from the storage container of 0.2 ppm or lower.
   USE - The method is useful for preventing melting of a hypochlorite crystal in a chlorine-containing gas generator for sterilization applications for sterilizing industrial areas, such as food, medicine, housing and mass-assembled equipment, bacteria and fungi, and liquids, such as treated water or cleaning water or purification of contaminated spaces (spatial sterilization).
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates stable chlorine-containing gas for long period of time by using crystals of hypochlorite, improves storage stability of hypochlorite crystals, ensures high chlorine-containing gas efficiency, increases generating efficiency of chlorine-containing gas, maintains crystal properties of hypochlorite for long period of time, and ensures long-term storage of hypochlorite crystals (powder). The generator has excellent decolorizing effect and deodorizing effect other than sterilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) (1) a maintenance method for a chlorine-containing gas generator, involving contacting a starting material gas with the hypochlorite crystal to generate a chlorine-containing gas, and preventing the melting of the hypochlorite crystal after the operation of the chlorine-containing gas generator is stopped; and
   (2) (2) a chlorine-containing gas generator which generates a chlorine-containing gas by bringing a raw material gas into contact with the hypochlorite crystal, where the generator is provided with a storage container for storing the hypochlorite crystal, and a melting prevention unit for removing carbon dioxide from the storage container. The melting prevention unit supplies a decarbonated gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of not greater than 10 ppm to the storage container until the effective chlorine concentration of the gas discharged from the storage container is not greater than 0.2 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decarboxylation gas is supplied to the crystals of the hypochlorite that have been brought into contact with the raw material gas to generate a chlorine-containing gas. Preferred Device: The melting prevention unit supplies the decarbonate gas to the storage container while at least the supply of the raw material gas to the hypochlorite crystal is stopped.
   EXAMPLE - Examples describes about method for preventing melting of a hypochlorite and method of measuring amount of effective chlorine, so do not support the claims.

TI Preparing product such as concrete product and            steel product, by combining synthesized oxide compound            with component to form intermediate material and adding            carbon dioxide to intermediate material to form            carbon-modified product.
AU BROWN G W
   FORREST B A
   BEAUCHAMP D
   BROWN J G W
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202101013S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a product, involves                combining a synthesized oxide compound with a                secondary component to form an intermediate                material and adding carbon dioxide to the                intermediate material such that at least a majority                of the carbon dioxide that is added to the                intermediate material is combined with the                intermediate material to form a carbon-modified                product that has an economic benefit, or adding                carbon dioxide to a synthesized oxide compound that                is at least partially in solid form such that at                least a majority of the carbon dioxide reacts with                synthesized oxide compound to form a synthesized                carbonate compound that is at least partially in                solid form and combining the synthesized carbonate                compound with a secondary component to form a                carbon-modified product that has an economic                benefit.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing product                such as concrete product, steel product, asphalt                product and/or plastic product (claimed) for                buildings, roads and bridges.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables monetization of economic                benefits or incentives that are available from                third parties (private or public), such economic                benefits or incentives being associated with                products that have been formed to be lower carbon,                carbon neutral, or carbon negative arising directly                from the incorporation of carbon dioxide, and/or                from being prepared using at least one                carbon-depleted component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon-modified product prepared by the                above method, where the carbon-modified product has                economic benefit; and
   (2) a method for optimizing production of                products, which involves determining a cost                associated with preparing carbon-modified products                by carrying out one or more production steps                effective to impart an economic benefit related to                one or both of a carbon incentive and a climate                incentive, determining the value of the economic                benefit associated with each carbon-modified                product, determining, for each carbon-modified                product, a net benefit as a difference between a                value of the economic benefit associated with                carbon-modified products and a cost associated with                preparing each of the carbon-modified products, and                forming one or more of the carbon-modified products                such that the net benefit has a positive                value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The portion of the                  carbon dioxide that is removed from the starting                  carbonate compound is carbon-captured. The                  synthesized oxide compound comprises an alkali                  oxide compound, an alkaline oxide compound, a                  transition metal oxide compound and/or a                  crystallogen oxide compound. The secondary                  component comprises a silicate, an alumina, an                  oxide, water, a cellulose-based component, a                  lignin-based component and/or a                  hemicellulose-based component.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves forming the synthesized oxide compound.                  The step of forming the synthesized oxide                  compound involves removing carbon dioxide from a                  starting carbonate compound. The method further                  involves determining a net benefit of the                  carbon-modified product as a difference between a                  value of the economic benefit associated with the                  carbon-modified product and a cost associated                  with preparing the carbon-modified product. The                  step of determining the cost associated with                  preparing each of the carbon-modified products                  involves determining, for each of the                  carbon-modified products, a cost associated with                  removing carbon dioxide from a starting carbonate                  compound to synthesize an oxide compound, a cost                  associated with combining a synthesized oxide                  compound with a secondary component to form the                  intermediate material, a cost associated with                  adding carbon dioxide to the intermediate                  material such that at least a majority of the                  carbon dioxide that is added to the intermediate                  material is combined with the intermediate                  material, a cost associated with adding carbon                  dioxide to a synthesized oxide compound that is                  at least partially in solid form such that at                  least a majority of the carbon dioxide reacts                  with the synthesized oxide compound to form a                  synthesized carbonate compound that is at least                  partially in solid form and a cost associated                  with combining the synthesized carbonate compound                  with a secondary component to form the                  carbon-modified product. The step of determining                  the value of the economic benefit associated with                  each carbon-modified product involves determining                  at least a portion of a carbon-dioxide                  sequestration credit for and associated with each                  product, the carbon dioxide sequestration credit                  being redeemable based on one or both of                  construction and financing of an entity                  incorporating the product. The step of                  determining the value of the economic benefit                  associated with each carbon-modified product                  involves determining a value of one or more of a                  tax credit, a tax inapplicability, a tradable                  value, a transferable value, a carbon standard, a                  climate standard, a carbon benefit designation, a                  climate benefit designation, a carbon benefit                  certification and a climate benefit                  certification. The method involves forming a                  product by carrying out one or more production                  steps effective to impart an economic benefit                  related to one or both of a carbon incentive and                  a climate incentive so that the product                  so-produced has a value that is greater than the                  cost of production, such value being at least                  partially attributable to one or both of the                  carbon incentive and the climate incentive. The                  production steps effective to impart an economic                  benefit related to one or both of a carbon                  incentive and a climate incentive involve                  combining a synthesized oxide compound with a                  secondary component to form an intermediate                  material. The production steps effective to                  impart an economic benefit related to one or both                  of a carbon incentive and a climate incentive                  further involve adding carbon dioxide to the                  intermediate material such that at least a                  majority of the carbon dioxide that is added to                  the intermediate material is combined with the                  intermediate material to form a carbon-modified                  product. The production steps effective to impart                  an economic benefit related to one or both of a                  carbon incentive and a climate incentive include                  adding carbon dioxide to a synthesized oxide                  compound that is at least partially in solid form                  such that at least a majority of the carbon                  dioxide reacts with the synthesized oxide                  compound to form a synthesized carbonate compound                  that is at least partially in solid form. The                  production steps effective to impart an economic                  benefit related to one or both of a carbon                  incentive and a climate incentive further involve                  combining the synthesized carbonate compound with                  a secondary component to form a carbon-modified                  product. The production steps effective to impart                  an economic benefit related to one or both of a                  carbon incentive and a climate incentive involve                  utilizing one or both of heat and power that is                  transferred from a further process that captures                  carbon. Preferred Components: The carbon-modified                  product comprises a concrete product, a steel                  product, an asphalt product and/or a plastic                  product. The economic benefit related to one or                  both of a carbon incentive and a climate                  incentive comprises a tax credit, a tax                  inapplicability, a tradable value, a transferable                  value, a carbon standard, a climate standard, a                  carbon benefit designation, a climate benefit                  designation, a carbon benefit certification                  and/or a climate benefit certification.

PT P
PN CN111943791-A; CN111943791-B
TI Preparing aluminum/bismuth oxide nano-energetic material comprises adding bismuth oxide powder into supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, and adding bismuth oxide/fluorosilane modified graphene oxide nanocomplex and aluminum powder.
AU ZHU W
   QIAO Z
   ZENG G
   HE R
   YANG F
   BAI W
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020B72653
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum/bismuth oxide nano-energetic material comprises (i) adding bismuth oxide powder into supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, adding fluorosilane modified graphene oxide dispersion and polyethylene glycol, stirring uniformly, injecting carbon dioxide, stirring, releasing pressure, cooling, centrifuging, washing, and vacuum drying, (ii) adding bismuth oxide/fluorosilane modified graphene oxide nanocomposite and aluminum powder into N,N-dimethylformamide, ultrasonically dispersing, centrifuging, vacuum drying, adding vacuum-dried product into ball mill tank, and ball mill balls to ball mill tank, adding liquid nitrogen into ball mill tank, so that the vacuum-dried product is immersed in the liquid nitrogen, keeping the volatilization amount of liquid nitrogen in balance with the input amount to stabilize the liquid level, ball milling, transferring ball mill jar into vacuum glove box for 5-8 hours, and collecting ball milling materials.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing aluminum/bismuth oxide nano-energetic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material generate high exothermic redox reaction with active fuel aluminum, hence improves combustion performance of nano aluminum heat agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aluminum/bismuth oxide nano-energetic material comprises (i) adding bismuth oxide powder into supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, adding fluorosilane modified graphene oxide dispersion and polyethylene glycol, stirring uniformly, injecting carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, stirring reaction at 100-120 degrees C under pressure of 12-17 MPa for 1-3 hours, releasing pressure, cooling, centrifuging, washing, and vacuum dry to obtain bismuth oxide/fluorosilane modified graphene oxide nanocomposite, (ii) adding Bi2O3/fluorosilane modified graphene oxide nanocomposite and aluminum powder into N,N-dimethylformamide, ultrasonically dispersing for 5-8 hours, centrifuging, vacuum drying, adding vacuum-dried product to the ball mill tank, and ball mill balls to ball mill tank, adding liquid nitrogen into ball mill tank, so that the vacuum-dried product is immersed in the liquid nitrogen, where the temperature is -150 to -175 degrees C, keeping the volatilization amount of liquid nitrogen in balance with the input amount to stabilize the liquid level, starting ball milling after 8 minutes of constant temperature, ball milling for 60 minutes, transferring ball mill jar into vacuum glove box for 5-8 hours, collecting ball milling materials
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of bismuth oxide powder and fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide in the fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide dispersion is 30-60:1, the mass ratio of the fluorosilane modified graphene oxide dispersion to polyethylene glycol is 3-5:1, the fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide dispersion is obtained by adding fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide into ethylene glycol ether, and ultrasonically dispersing for 3-5 hours to obtain 0.5-2.5 mg/ml fluorine silane-modified graphene oxide dispersion. The fluorosilane solution is perfluorodecyltriethoxysilane solution, perfluorodecyltrimethoxysilane solution, perfluorododecylsilane solution, tridecafluorooctyltrimethoxysilane solution, or trifluoropropylmethyldimethoxysilane solutions. The solvent of the fluorosilane solution is at least one of ethanol, propanol, propylene glycol, and butylene glycol. Preferred Method: The fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide dispersion is obtained by adding fluorosilane-modified graphene oxide to ethylene glycol ether by weight, and ultrasonically dispersing for 3-5 hours to obtain 0.5-2.5 mg/ml fluorine Silane-modified graphene oxide dispersion. The wavelength of the ultraviolet light irradiation is 254-400 nm, and the light intensity is 5-12 mW/cm2. The mass ratio of the graphene oxide to deionized water is 0.0004-0.0006:1, the volume ratio of the hydrogen peroxide to the deionized water is 1:3-5, the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide is 35-45 wt.%, the concentration of the fluorosilane solution is 2-4 mg/ml, and the mass ratio of fluorosilane to graphene oxide in the fluorosilane solution is 2-5:1. The particle size of aluminum powder is 40-100 nm, and the particle size of the Bi2O3 powder is 50-120 nm.

PT P
PN CN111634924-A
TI Carbon-containing energy-air-water co-production of ammonia and hydrogen by subjecting metal or composite metal-based materials to nitridation reaction, hydrolyzing metal nitride and water vapor, separating mixed gas, and storing ammonia.
AU CAO Y
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 202089573J
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-containing energy-air-water co-production of ammonia and hydrogen includes subjecting metal or composite metal-based materials to nitridation reaction under air or nitrogen to obtain metal nitride and nitrogen, passing metal nitride and water vapor through hydrolysis reactor to obtain ammonia gas and hydrogen gas mixture and metal oxide, subjecting to gas-solid separation to obtain ammonia and hydrogen gas mixture as gas phase, and storing ammonia; returning back portion of separated hydrogen to first reaction while storing other portion as product; and adding metal oxide to reduction reactor, reacting with carbon source to obtain solid phase reduced metal, metal mixture and gas phase carbon dioxide, subjecting to gas-solid separation to obtain pure carbon dioxide, returning back reduced metal and metal mixture to nitridation reactor to participate in the reaction, and collecting carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is for carbon-containing energy-air-water co-production of ammonia and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves efficient hierarchical conversion and energy cascade utilization of carbon-containing energy, and has effective product separation and targeted utilization with low energy consumption, and mild reaction thermodynamic equilibrium movement conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon-containing energy-air-water co-production of ammonia and hydrogen comprises subjecting metal or composite metal-based materials as circulating solid-phase media to nitridation reaction under air or nitrogen as nitrogen source to obtain metal nitride and nitrogen, passing metal nitride and water vapor through hydrolysis reactor to obtain ammonia gas and hydrogen gas mixture and metal oxide, subjecting to gas-solid separation to obtain ammonia and hydrogen gas mixture as gas phase, and storing ammonia after being condensed or absorbed by water as hydrogen source; returning back portion of separated hydrogen to first reaction for deoxygenation while storing other portion as product; and adding metal oxide to reduction reactor, reacting with carbon source to obtain solid phase reduced metal, metal mixture and gas phase carbon dioxide, subjecting to gas-solid separation to obtain pure carbon dioxide, returning back reduced metal and metal mixture to nitridation reactor to participate in the reaction, and collecting carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal is iron, aluminum and/or manganese. The molar ratio of metal and/or metal nitride and water vapor is 1:1.2-1:2.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is carbon-containing primary fossil energy, renewable biomass, carbon-containing solid waste and all possible post-processing products.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E35; J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110451547-A; CN110451547-B
TI Preparation of carbonate powder used in e.g. papermaking, involves preparing solidified carbon dioxide initial solution using stainless steel smelting slag, adding structure directing agent and metallurgical waste gas, and precipitating.
AU ZHAO Q
   MEI X
   LIU C
   SHI P
   JIANG M
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 201998029G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbonate powder involves adding a mineral phase modifier to stainless steel smelting slag, discharging the slag, cooling, mechanically crushing the ore smelting stainless steel slag, ion-leaching using metallurgical waste acid solution, filtering, obtaining solidified carbon dioxide initial solution as filtrate, adding a pH adjusting agent to obtained solution, adjusting pH, adding a structure directing agent to the solution, adding metallurgical waste gas containing carbon dioxide to obtained solution, reacting, obtaining solution containing precipitate, terminating aeration process, subjecting to precipitation and centrifugation with a centrifuge, obtaining a solid product and a magnesium-rich solution, washing the solid product, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of carbonate powder e.g. calcium carbonate powder and magnesium carbonate powder used in construction application, papermaking application and rubber/pharmaceutical industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and sequentially produces calcium carbonate powder and magnesium carbonate powder having regular shape and size without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbonate powder involves adding a mineral phase modifier to stainless steel smelting slag, discharging the slag, cooling to less than 100 degrees C, mechanically crushing the ore smelting stainless steel slag, ion-leaching using metallurgical waste acid solution in ultrasonic oscillator, filtering, obtaining solidified carbon dioxide initial solution as filtrate, adding a pH adjusting agent to obtained solution, adjusting pH of the solution as 3 to less than 6 or 12-14, adding a structure directing agent to the solution, adding metallurgical waste gas containing carbon dioxide to obtained solution, reacting, obtaining solution containing precipitate, terminating aeration process, subjecting to precipitation and centrifugation with a centrifuge, obtaining a solid product and a magnesium-rich solution, washing the solid product, drying, obtaining calcium carbonate powder, adding a pH adjusting agent to obtained magnesium-rich solution, adjusting the pH to 6 to less than 12, continuously adding the metallurgical waste gas containing carbon dioxide into the solution, reacting, obtaining solution containing precipitate, terminating aeration process, subjecting obtained solution to precipitation, centrifuging, obtaining a solid product, washing obtained solid product, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The stainless steel smelting slag is argon oxygen decarburization (AOD) refining slag, converter slag or electric furnace slag. The mineral phase modifier is manganese oxide, magnesium oxide or alumina. When the stainless steel smelting slag is converter slag, electric furnace slag or AOD slag, then 1-12 %mass of manganese oxide, magnesium oxide or alumina is added with respect to content of stainless steel smelting slag. When the mineral phase modifier is chosen from 2 or more of manganese oxide, magnesium oxide and alumina, then the usage amount is 2-14 %mass with respect to content of stainless steel slag. The solid-liquid ratio of the mineral phase modifier and metallurgical waste acid solution is 1:10-50 g/mL. The metallurgical exhaust gas comprises more than 10 vol.% carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The metallurgical industry waste acid is anionically adsorbed by Rohm and Haas strong acid resin, so that the salt in the metallurgical industry waste acid is separated from the acid, and then the acid blocked in the resin is washed using distilled water. The pH of the acid solution after washing is adjusted to 2-6, and the obtained metallurgical waste acid solution is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or hydrofluoric acid. The metallurgical waste gas is subjected to dust removal and temperature-reduction treatment. Preferred Conditions: The leaching process is carried out at 20-100 degrees C for 30-180 minutes under ultrasonic vibration power of 100-700 W and ultrasonic frequency of 20-40 kHz. When the waste gas does not contain sulfur dioxide gas, the cooling temperature is less than 100 degrees C. When the metallurgical waste gas includes sulfur dioxide gas, the cooling temperature is -10 degrees C to -70 degrees C. During preparation of calcium carbonate powder, the metallurgical waste gas is introduced in the gas at a flow rate of 0.05-2 L/minute for 20-180 minutes at the stirring speed of 100-500 rpm, and reacted at 20-100 degrees C. During preparation of magnesium carbonate powder, the metallurgical waste gas is introduced in the gas flow rate of 0.05-2 L/minute for 20-180 minutes at the stirring speed of 100-500 rpm and reacted at 20-100 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The structure directing agent is a copolymer formed by containing at least one unit of an olefin, an alkane, an alcohol group and an organic acid. The mass ratio of structure directing agent with respect to the volume of the solution is 1-20:1000 g/mL.

PT P
PN WO2019204974-A1; CA3098188-A1; AU2018420564-A1; US2021138403-A1; CN112996587-A
TI System for regeneration of acidic gas e.g.            alkanolamine, has regeneration cell that is provided            with gas permeable membrane which separates chambers,            and is arranged to vent acidic gas stripped from            solvent by steam.
AU MANAN N A
   KANG G
   CHAN Z P
   SALEH S M
   CAO Y
   CHAN Z
AE PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 201990479P
AB    NOVELTY - The regeneration system (5) has a regeneration                cell that is provided with a solvent chamber which                is arranged to receive a solvent flow, and an                internal chamber which is arranged to receive a                steam flow. The regeneration cell is provided with                a gas permeable membrane that separates the solvent                chamber and internal chamber. The regeneration cell                is arranged to vent acidic gas stripped from the                solvent by the steam. A re-boiler (80) is placed in                connection with an outlet (70) from the solvent                chamber and is arranged to heat the stripped                solvent so as to remove water, and directs the                consequential steam flow to the internal                chamber.
   USE - System for regeneration of acidic gas solvent                such as alkanolamine laden with carbon dioxide and                hydrogen sulfide for removal of carbon dioxide from                liquefied natural gas (LNG) process stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat from the steam is more efficiently                applied to the stripping process instead of being                lost as waste energy by applying steam into a                chamber adjacent to the carbon dioxide laden                solvent. The residual heat of the steam is reused,                thus the net waste energy lost is reduced during                the regeneration process. The carbon dioxide                bubbles diffuses the membrane by introducing the                gas permeable membrane as foaming commences, thus                foaming is prevented and the flow restrictions are                avoided. The membrane provides a path by which the                carbon dioxide is quickly removed from the solvent                before foaming commences. The reduction in                vessel-volume/flow consequently reduces the                required infrastructure, and therefore a                significant reduction in capital expenditure. The                steam is not used in the first stage, and hence a                low temperature low pressure environment in the                membrane contactor cell efficiently removes a                portion of the carbon dioxide before delivering to                the second stage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for regenerating acidic gas solvent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                regeneration system.
   Regeneration system (5)
   Membrane contactor cell (62)
   Outlet (70)
   Valve (75)
   Re-boiler (80)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110127743-A
TI Preparing transparent or opal calcium carbonate            handicrafts using calcium carbide slag comprises            performing slurry detoxification, calcium liquefaction            and impurity solidification, solid-liquid separating,            deposing and solidifying.
AU YIN X
AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201973601A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing transparent or opal calcium                carbonate handicrafts using calcium carbide slag                comprises (i) adding calcium carbide slag with                water, adding oxidizing agent or precipitating                agent to suspended slurry to detoxify and                eliminating odor, (ii) placing non-toxic suspension                slurry in pressure agitation tank, capturing carbon                dioxide gas, converting calcium hydroxide and                calcium carbonate crystal minerals in calcium                carbide slag into water-soluble calcium bicarbonate                and liquefying, forming solution in which both                calcium bicarbonate and carbon dioxide are                supersaturated and precipitating impurity compounds                of silicon, aluminum, iron and other heavy metal                ions in supersaturated solution and calcium                bicarbonate supersaturated solution mixture                containing solid impurities as precipitates, (iii)                filtering mixture of solid impurity-containing                calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution, and                (iv) injecting filtrate directly into mold.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing transparent                or opal calcium carbonate handicrafts using calcium                carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly                and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing transparent or opal calcium                carbonate handicrafts using calcium carbide slag                comprises (i) adding calcium carbide slag with                water to prepare a suspended slurry at normal                temperature, adding oxidizing agent or                precipitating agent to the suspended slurry to                detoxify and eliminating the odor to obtain                non-toxic suspension slurry, (ii) placing non-toxic                suspension slurry in a pressure agitation tank,                capturing carbon dioxide gas by industrial carbon                is injected or pressing into the liquid carbon                dioxide, converting calcium hydroxide and calcium                carbonate crystal minerals in the calcium carbide                slag into water-soluble calcium bicarbonate and                liquefied under an excess of carbon dioxide under                pressure, forming a solution in which both calcium                bicarbonate and carbon dioxide are supersaturated,                and precipitating impurity compounds of silicon,                aluminum, iron and other heavy metal ions in the                supersaturated solution and calcium bicarbonate                supersaturated solution mixture containing solid                impurities as precipitates, (iii) filtering mixture                of the solid impurity-containing calcium                bicarbonate supersaturated solution under the                pressure state to obtain a filtrate, where the                filtrate is a supersaturated solution containing                calcium bicarbonate, and (iv) injecting filtrate                directly into the mold to solidify the deposited                gel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of water to calcium carbide slag                  solids in the suspended slurry, that is, the                  water/solid ratio is 20-4:1, preferably 15-5:1                  and the oxidizing agent is a substance capable of                  efficiently oxidizing a low molecular weight                  toxic and odorous compound in the calcium carbide                  slag, the substance capable of efficiently                  oxidizing the low molecular weight toxic and                  odorous compound in the calcium carbide slag is                  preferably at least one of chlorine dioxide,                  hydrogen peroxide, sodium percarbonate, and                  ferrate, the amount of the oxidizing agent is                  0.50-0.005% of the mass of the carbide slag. The                  precipitating agent is a substance capable of                  directly converting sulfur, phosphorus and                  arsenic ions into a highly insoluble or insoluble                  compound in water. The copper sulfate and copper                  chloride is preferred. The amount of precipitant                  is determined by the content of the odorous                  compound in the calcium carbide slag, and the                  addition of the odor to the odor is completely                  eliminated. The amount of the precipitant is                  generally 0.20-0.002% of the mass of the calcium                  carbide slag. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  the temperature in the pressure stirring tank is                  0-40 degrees C, preferably 2-35 degrees C, the                  pressure to be pressed is 0.5-10.0 MPa,                  preferably 1.0-8.0 MP. In the step (iii), the                  pressure in the pressure state is 0.5-10.0 MPa,                  preferably 1.0-8.0 MP. In the step (iv), the                  temperature at which the deposition or gelation                  is solidified is room temperature to 98 degrees                  C, preferably 20-95 degrees C and the process                  comprises adding coloring agent, foaming agent,                  solid material, and animal and plant sample                  during the deposition and solidification process,                  cementing amorphous calcium carbonate,                  solidifying into a specific color, or coating                  with a hollow foam, or coating with a specific                  solid or sample and is decomposed by calcium                  bicarbonate and injecting supersaturated solution                  of calcium bicarbonate into the mold once or                  multiple times, utilizing multiple injection                  method and discharging clean water in the mold                  before each injection.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN CN109999807-A
TI Preparation of catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol by dissolving copper nitrate, cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate in ethanol or deionized water, and dissolving complexing agent oxalic acid in ethanol.
AU QU Z
   WANG W
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201962869E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst comprising high-dispersion copper oxide and copper-cerium-zirconium solid solution comprises dissolving copper nitrate, cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate in ethanol or deionized water to prepare solution A, where the final fraction of copper oxide is 2-40 mass%, and the mol ratio of cerium to zirconium is 0.2-0.8, dissolving complexing agent oxalic acid in ethanol or deionized water to prepare solution B having a concentration of 0.02-0.2 mol/L, slowly dropwise adding excessive solution B to solution A, keeping stirring for 1 hour to fully and uniformly mix the solution, standing at room temperature for 2 hours, washing the obtained precipitates with ethanol and deionized water, centrifuging, drying the precipitates at 60-100 degrees C for 8-24 hours, roasting at 350-550 degrees C for 4 hours, tableting, crushing and screening by 40-60-mesh sieve.
   USE - The method is for preparation of catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high efficiency, easy to obtain raw materials, mild conditions, is simple,efficient, reliable, and easy for mass production, and provides catalyst with high-dispersion copper oxide and copper-cerium-zirconium solid solution through one-step. The catalyst has improved carbon dioxide conversion rate, improved methanol selectivity, and excellent anti-sintering performance, and is capable of long-term operation without deactivation and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of catalyst comprising high-dispersion copper oxide and copper-cerium-zirconium solid solution for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol, where reaction is carried out in a continuous type fixed bed reaction device, catalyst reducing conditions comprise pure hydrogen reduction at 200-400 degrees C for 1-4 hours, flow rate of 50 ml/minute, and after adjusting the water bath to the reaction temperature, switching to introduce reaction gas, and carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol reaction conditions comprise reaction pressure of 1.0-5.0 MPa, reaction temperature of 200-300 degrees C, volume space velocity of 2000-10000/hour, and n hydrogen (H2):n carbon dioxide (CO2) of 3:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying step is conducted at 80 degrees C for 12 hours. The roasting step is conducted at 450 degrees C for 4 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN108864826-A
TI Preparing high-conductive graphite alkenyl conductive ink comprises e.g. placing the graphene dispersion into a centrifugal device, and removing the non-exfoliated graphite powder and large pieces of graphene by high speed centrifugation.
AU GAO Y
   YIN G
   WANG L
   ZHANG S
AE LUOYANG INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (LUOY-C)
GA 201895472F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-conductive graphite alkenyl conductive ink comprises (i) dissolving stabilizer and auxiliary agent into an organic solvent to obtain dispersing medium for graphene; (ii) adding graphite powder into a high-pressure stainless steel reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, and starting ultrasonic wave generation system; (iii) controlling the ultrasonic power, and reacting, completing the reaction and quickly releasing the pressure, where graphene and some un-stripped graphite powder are released with carbon dioxide, entering the graphene collection container containing the dispersion medium obtained in step (i), where the carbon dioxide gas overflows, and the graphene is uniformly dispersed in the dispersion medium to obtain a graphene dispersion; and (iv) placing the graphene dispersion obtained in step (iii) into a centrifugal device, and removing the non-exfoliated graphite powder and large pieces of graphene by high speed centrifugation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-conductive graphite alkenyl conductive ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains the perfect properties of graphene, and provides excellent electrical conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-conductive graphite alkenyl conductive ink comprises (i) dissolving stabilizer and auxiliary agent into an organic solvent to obtain dispersing medium for graphene; (ii) adding graphite powder into a high-pressure stainless steel reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide through a high pressure pump into the kettle after the temperature of the reaction kettle reaches the preset temperature value, and starting ultrasonic wave generation system after the pressure in the reaction kettle reaches the pre-set pressure; (iii) controlling the ultrasonic power, and reacting at a preset reactor pressure, reactor temperature, ultrasonic power, and ultrasonic time, completing the reaction and quickly releasing the pressure, where graphene and some un-stripped graphite powder are released with carbon dioxide, entering the graphene collection container containing the dispersion medium obtained in step (i), where the carbon dioxide gas overflows, and the graphene is uniformly dispersed in the dispersion medium to obtain a graphene dispersion; and (iv) placing the graphene dispersion obtained in step (iii) into a centrifugal device, and removing the non-exfoliated graphite powder and large pieces of graphene by high speed centrifugation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The graphite alkenyl conductive comprises 0.5-5 wt.% graphene, 1-5 wt.% stabilizer, 1-20 wt.% auxiliary agent and 70-97 wt.% organic solvent. The obtained graphite alkenyl conductive ink has a square resistance of less than or equal to 1.6 k Omega and conductive agent content of less than or equal to 1%. Preferred Components: The stabilizer is ethyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose or cellulose acetate. The auxiliary agent is terpineol, ethylene glycol or diethylene glycol. The organic solvent is ethanol, cyclohexanone or isopropanol. The mass ratio of stabilizer, auxiliary agent, and organic solvent is 161:50-100. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the preset temperature in the reaction kettle is 40-60 degrees C and preset pressure is 8-18 MPa. The graphite powder is natural graphite powder or expanded graphite powder. In the step (iii), the ultrasonic power is 60-240 W and the ultrasonic time is 0.5-4 hours. In the step (iii), the graphene collecting container is previously placed in an ultrasonic water bath and uniformly dispersing the dispersion medium and graphene by ultrasonic waves in the graphene collection container to obtain graphene dispersing solution. In the step (iv), the centrifuging is carried out at 1000-10000 revolutions/minute for 0.5-1.5 hours. The method comprises repeating step (ii) and (iii) for controlling the graphene mass fraction to obtain graphite alkenyl conductive ink. The step (ii) comprises adding graphite powder and the dispersion medium into a high-pressure stainless steel reaction kettle and sealing for subsequent heating and boosting operation. The step (iii) comprises reacting to obtain graphene dispersion in the reaction kettle, slowly releasing the pressure, completely pouring out the graphene dispersion liquid into the reaction kettle, and performing high-speed centrifugation operation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The preparation of ink comprises providing a carbon dioxide gas cylinder, a refrigerating system, a high pressure pump, a high-pressure stainless steel reaction kettle, an ultrasonic generating system and a graphite collecting container, where the carbon dioxide gas cylinder is connected to the refrigeration system. The carbon dioxide is passed through the refrigeration system from the high pressure pump to the high pressure stainless steel reaction kettle. The ultrasonic generating system is connected to the top of the high pressure stainless steel reaction kettle. The inlet of the top of the high-pressure stainless steel reaction kettle is also used to pass raw graphite powder or graphite powder and dispersion medium. The graphite collecting container and the high pressure stainless steel reaction kettle are connected by a pipe. The graphene is vented from the reactor into the graphene collection vessel as the carbon dioxide is released from the reactor.

PN CN216654037-U
TI Coal upgrading and carbon reducing power generation system comprises dry distillation furnace, carbon monoxide reactor, hydrogen reactor, and coal burning boiler.
AU JIN H
   CUI H
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
   HEPU ENERGY & ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HEPU-Non-standard)
GA 202278897W
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims a coal upgrading and carbon reducing power generation system, comprising a dry distillation furnace, which is used for coal under the condition of isolating heating, decomposing, generating coke, coal gas; carbon monoxide reactor, which is connected to the carbonization furnace through the coke conveying pipeline, for generating coal gas for coke and carbon dioxide; hydrogen reactor, which receives coal gas generated by reaction of carbon monoxide reactor, and reacting with high temperature steam to generate carbon dioxide and hydrogen; A coal burning boiler, which is used for generating high temperature steam needed by the hydrogen reactor. Compared with the traditional carbon capture the invention does not need the separation of carbon dioxide and flue gas, reduces the total energy consumption of the energy, improves the overall efficiency.

PT P
PN EP3935945-A1; WO2022008333-A1
TI Composition useful for producing carbon dioxide for trapping insect, and attracting blood-sucking insects and arthropods, comprises first yeast strain having low alcohol tolerance, second yeast strain having high alcohol tolerance, and nutrient source.
AU GEIER M
   ROSE A
   GORDON S W
   EPPLE M
   GORDON S
AE BIOGENTS AG (BIOG-Non-standard)
   BIOGENTS AG (BIOG-Non-standard)
GA 202207336K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises (a) at least one first yeast strain having low tolerance of 100 g/l or less alcohol, (b) at least one second yeast strain having a high tolerance of 100 g/l alcohol or more, and (c) at least one nutrient source.
   USE - The composition is useful for producing carbon dioxide for trapping insect, and attracting blood-sucking insects and arthropods (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition can metabolize quickly and effectively, and is inexpensive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing carbon dioxide, which involves using aqueous solution comprising a carbohydrate source and the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises components (a)-(c) in freeze-dried form, and in a weight ratio of 1-10:1-10:1-10, preferably 2:1:1, 1:2:1 or 1:1:2. Preferred Method: The method involves preparing aqueous solution by mixing 500 g sugar and 20 g composition in 2 l water. Preferred Components: The component (a) is formed by using baker's yeast. The component (b) comprises a calorific yeast, a brewer's yeast and/or a wine yeast. The component (b) or (c) is formed by a turbo yeast. The component (c) is formed by a yeast extract. The carbohydrate source is formed by a monosaccharide, preferably glucose or fructose, by a disaccharide, preferably sucrose, by a flour or other suitable starch source. The water is at a temperature of 30-40 degrees C, preferably 35 degrees C to produce the aqueous solution. Preferred Organism: The first yeast strain and/or the second yeast strain are osmotolerant and tolerate relatively high initial concentrations of sugar.
   ACTIVITY - Insect Attractant. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113862703-A
TI Preparation of oxaflutole intermediate involves subjecting 3-(3-bromo-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole and carbon dioxide to electrocatalytic carboxylation in polar solvent in presence of phase transfer catalyst.
AU GU Y
   ZHANG H
   YAN Z
   JI L
   QU R
AE JIANGSU SEVENCONTINENT GREEN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU QIZHOU GREEN TECHNOLOGY RES INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202210800C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of oxaflutole intermediate (I) involves subjecting 3-(3-bromo-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole (II) and carbon dioxide to electrocatalytic carboxylation in polar solvent in presence of phase transfer catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of compound (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product with high yield and high efficiency, and is economical, environmentally-friendly and suitable for large-scale industrial production. The method is carried out under mild condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of oxaflutole intermediate of formula (I) involves subjecting 3-(3-bromo-6-methanesulfonyl-2-methylphenyl)-4,5-dihydro-1,2-oxazole of formula (II) and carbon dioxide to electrocatalytic carboxylation in polar solvent in presence of phase transfer catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide, butanol, water, and/or acetonitrile. The phase transfer catalyst is tetrabutylammonium fluoride, tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium iodide, 18 crowns 6, chain polyethylene glycol, chain polyethylene glycol dialkyl ether and/or cyclodextrin. Preferred Property: The concentration of the compound (II) in the polar solvent is 0.05-1 mol/L. The concentration of the phase transfer catalyst in the polar solvent is 0.05-1 mol/L. Preferred Process: The method further involves post processing by removing polar solvent, adding ethyl acetate, filtering and drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The materials used for the positive electrode and the negative electrode of the electrocatalysis are one or more of copper, silver, magnesium, nickel, and titanium, respectively. Preferred Property: The current density used in the electrocatalysis is 1-6 mA/cm2.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing quinazoline-2,4(1H,3H)-dione derivatives (I) comprises taking the 2-aminobenzyl cyanide derivative (II) and carbon dioxide as raw materials and reacting in 2-hydroxypyridine type ionic liquid to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing quinazoline-2,4(1H,3H)-dione derivatives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: involves mild reaction conditions, relatively simple process of product separation and purification, high product yield and wide range of substrate applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing quinazoline-2,4(1H,3H)-dione derivatives of formula (I) comprises taking the 2-aminobenzyl cyanide derivative of formula (II) and carbon dioxide as raw materials and reacting in 2-hydroxypyridine type ionic liquid to obtain final product.
   x = 1-4; and
   R = H, 1-4C alkyl, halogen, 1-4C alkoxy or trifluoromethyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 2-hydroxypyridine type ionic liquid is 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepin-1-ium pyridin-2-olate ((HDBU) (2-OP)) of formula (III), tetramethylammonium pyridin-2-olate ((N1111) (2-OP)) of formula (IV), 2,4-dimethylpentan-3-iminium pyridin-2-olate ((HTMG) (2-OP)) of formula (V), 2-hydroxy-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium pyridin-2-olate ((Ch) (2-OP)) of formula (VI) or tetrabutylammonium pyridin-2-olate ((N4444) (2-OP)) of formula (VII). PN CN110591875-A
TI 0.0 vol Lager beer is prepared by preheating, degassing, dealcoholizing, cooling, filling classic Lager beer with carbon dioxide, modifying, filtering, stabilizing, and filling.
AU FAN W
   DONG J
   YIN H
   XING L
   CHANG Z
   QIAN Z
   HUANG S
   HU X
   LIU X
   YANG M
   YAN P
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 202001279Q
AB    NOVELTY - 0.0 vol.% Lager beer has alcohol content less than 0.05 vol.%, and the bitterness is 10-15EBC. The original wort concentration is 4.5-7.5 degrees P, the carbon dioxide content is 0.50-0.60 wt.%, total alcohol more than or equal to 15mg / L, total ester more than or equal to 5mg / L.
   USE - 0.0 vol.% Lager beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The 0.0 vol.% Lager beer solves the problem that the current non-alcohol beer lacks the alcohol ester flavor of traditional beer, and has a light mouthfeel and prominent acidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a 0.0 vol.% alcohol-free Lager beer, which involves preheating, degassing, dealcoholizing, cooling, filling classic Lager beer with carbon dioxide, modifying, filtering, stabilizing, and filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Condition: The classic Lager beer preheated to 40-43 degrees C is degassed by vacuum to a vacuum of 80-90mbar until the carbon dioxide in the classic Lager beer is completely discharged. The degassed classic Lager beer is vacuum distilled at 40-43 degrees C with a vacuum of 80-90mbar until the ethanol content drops below 0.05 vol.%. The post-modification step includes adding natural beer flavors and natural sweet ingredients to the classic Lager beer after dealcoholization. The added amount of the natural beer flavor is 0.05-0.2%, preferably 0.1%. The added amount of the natural sweet component is 0.1 g / 100 mL-3.1 g / 100 mL, preferably 0.6 g / 100 mL, based on the amount of sugar. The natural sweet component is fructose syrup, maltose syrup, sucrose syrup, glucose syrup, and caster sugar. The natural beer flavor and the natural sweetness ingredient are added in an online manner. The classic Lager beer is prepared by taking Australian barley malt, wheat malt, supplemented with rice, corn or syrup. After crushing, gelatinizing, saccharifying, filtering, boiling, swirling precipitation, cooling and oxygenation, adding the following yeast, fermentation, and filtering steps, a classic Lager beer is prepared. Australian barley malt, wheat malt with rice, corn or syrup as auxiliary materials is mixed, crushed, mixed with water-to-water ratio 1: 3-1: 4, and then saccharified with malt after protein rest. The rice or corn is used as an auxiliary material, the auxiliary material is pulverized and then mashed at 80-90 degrees C for gelatinization. After saccharification, the mashed mash is filtered at 73 1 degrees C to obtain a clear wort and boiled. The hops or hop products are added during the boiling process, and carry out swirling precipitation after boiling, with or without adding hops or hop products during the swirling precipitation, cooled to 8-10 degrees C, and at the same time oxygenated and connected to the yeast below for fermentation to obtain beer fermentation broth. The beer fermentation broth is filtered by diatomite filtration to obtain a classic lager beer.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A04D; D05-B02
IP C12C-012/00; C12C-005/02; C12H-003/00

PN CN110551084-A
TI Preparing andrographolide comprises passing Andrographis paniculata into liquid carbon dioxide, adding activated carbon, refluxing, performing high-speed countercurrent chromatography separation, collecting, concentrating and drying.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 2019A51847
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing andrographolide comprises placing Andrographis paniculata in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, maintaining a temperature at 40-65 degrees C, pressure of 20-40 MPa, passing into liquid carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 20-50 L/hour, extracting for 30-200 minutes and analyzing to obtain the extract, and (ii) dissolving the extract with ethanol solution, adding activated carbon, refluxing, decoloring, concentrating the decolored solution, extracting with ethyl acetate, concentrating, crystallizing, then dissolving the crystals in ethanol, performing high-speed countercurrent chromatography separation, collecting the fractions based on chromatography, concentrating and drying under vacuum.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing andrographolide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly and easy to achieve industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-speed countercurrent chromatography in step (ii) is carried out by mixing into acetonitrile, methanol, disodium hydrogen phosphate solution (pH 6.2) as a solvent system, mixing in a ratio of 2-4:1-5:4-7, taking the upper phase as stationary phase and lower phase as mobile phase.

PT P
PN CN109970682-A; CN109970682-B
TI Recovering epoxy cyclohexane from cyclohexane oxidized light oil comprises e.g. subjecting cyclohexane oxidized light oil to addition reaction with carbon dioxide to obtain first reaction liquid and subjecting to rectification.
AU ZHANG Z
   JIANG W
   XIAO Z
   LUO X
AE YUEYANG CHANGDE ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY (YUEY-Non-standard)
GA 201961444V
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering epoxy cyclohexane from cyclohexane oxidized light oil comprises subjecting cyclohexane oxidized light oil to an addition reaction with carbon dioxide under an addition catalyst to obtain first reaction liquid, subjecting first reaction liquid to rectification to obtain cyclohexene carbonate-containing fraction, subjecting cyclohexene carbonate-containing fraction to decarboxylation reaction under a decarboxylation catalyst to obtain second reaction liquid and subjecting second reaction liquid to rectification.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering epoxy cyclohexane from cyclohexane oxidized light oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the use of the halide; does not require acid and alkali treatment, and carbon dioxide can be recycled and reused; is environmentally friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises carrying out addition reaction under the conditions of a reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at a gas pressure of 0.5-10 MPa with a reaction temperature of 50-230 degrees C and reaction time of 0.1-25 hours. The decarboxylation reaction comprises maintaining gas pressure of -0.09 to 0.5 MPa, reaction temperature of 60-400 degrees C and mass space velocity of 0.1-5 h-1 . The method for performing rectification of first reaction liquid comprises subjecting first reaction liquid to rectification, and number of theoretical plates is more than 15 and collecting fraction having a boiling point of between 150-260 degrees C at -0.09 MPa to 0 Mpa is collected. The method for performing rectification of second reaction liquid comprises subjecting second reaction liquid to rectification, where number of theoretical plates is more than 30 and collecting fraction having a boiling point of between 128-130 degrees C under normal pressure. The pretreatment comprises oxidizing light oil of the cyclohexane before performing the addition reaction, subjecting cyclohexane oxidized light oil to rectification and number of theoretical plates is more than 30 and collecting fraction having a boiling point of 120-137 degrees C at normal pressure. Preferred Components: The addition catalyst is at least one of quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, an alkali metal salt catalyst and an ionic liquid catalyst. The decarboxylation catalyst is at least one of alkali metal sulfate catalyst, alkali metal carbonate catalyst, alkali metal nitrate catalyst, alkyl imidazolium salt catalyst, ionic liquid catalyst, alkali metal catalyst and alkali metal oxide catalyst. The mass ratio of addition catalyst to cyclohexane oxidized light oil is (0.05-5):100. The mass ratio of decarboxylation catalyst to cyclohexene carbonate-containing fraction is (1-60):100.

MC E06-A02D; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-E03; E11-W; N01-A; N05-D; N07-C03; N07-F07; N07-F08

PN CN109053448-A
TI Separating alcohol and dimethyl carbonate mixture comprises adding organic alkali to mixture of dimethyl carbonate and alcohol, introducing carbon dioxide, stirring reaction, filtering to obtain dimethyl carbonate, and heating liquid.
AU LIU P
   ZHANG K
   SONG Q
   JI K
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201900912X
AB    NOVELTY - Separating alcohol and dimethyl carbonate mixture comprises adding organic alkali to mixture of dimethyl carbonate and alcohol, where total liquid amount accounts for 5-30% of total volume of reactor, introducing carbon dioxide with pressure of 0.1-8 MPa, stirring reaction for 0.5-24 hours at separation reaction temperature of -10-40 degrees C, filtering system to obtain dimethyl carbonate, heating liquid to 50-100 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours to separate the alcohol, carbon dioxide and organic alkali.
   USE - The method is useful for separating alcohol and dimethyl carbonate mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: consumes less energy, is environmentally-friendly, economical and uses simple device; and has high separation efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of dimethyl carbonate in the dimethyl carbonate and alcohol mixture is 1-99 wt.%. The organic alkali is strong alkali capable of forming an adduct with alcohol and carbon dioxide. The organic alkali is strong alkali capable of forming an adduct with alcohol and carbon dioxide is 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, bicyclo(4.3.0)-1,5-diaza-5-undecene, 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine, 1,1,2,3,3-pentamethylguanidine, 1,1,3,3-tetramethyl-2-cyclohexyl fluorene, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene, and/or 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene. The amount of the organic alkali added is organic base to alcohol molar ratio of (1-2):1. The alcohol is alcohol or many of alcohols between C1-C8. The monohydric alcohol is methanol or ethanol, the glycol is ethylene glycol or propylene glycol, and the triol is glycerol.

Preferred Method: The natural non-irritating plant surfactant comprising hydrolyzing natural palm oil to obtain palm kernel fatty acid, hydrolyzing coconut oil, taking distilled grain supercritical carbon dioxide, and extracting fatty acid; and conveying plant surfactant to spray tower, spraying, blowing caustic soda from tower, spraying particle packing caustic soda fine particle, evaporating, drying, dewatering, forming into vegetation surface promoting agent, and mixing plant surfactant and caustic soda at weight ratio of 1:1-5 at 140-170 degrees C with tower top temperature of 45-60 degrees C.

TI Producing ammonia involves dissolving hydrogen,            carbon dioxide, and nitrogen in solution, placing            glutamine synthetase inhibitor and autotrophic            diazotroph bacteria in solution.
AU COLON B C
   LIU C
   NOCERA D G
   SILVER P A
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
   HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
   HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
   HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 2018029412
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing ammonia involves                dissolving hydrogen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen in                a solution, and placing a glutamine synthetase                inhibitor and autotrophic diazotroph bacteria in                the solution.
   USE - The method is useful for producing ammonia                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the yield of ammonia. The                water splitting-biosynthetic process operates at                ambient conditions and can be distributed for an                on-demand supply of nitrogen fertilizer. The method                yields a much higher efficiency and provides a                sustainable route for fertilizer production without                the use of fossil fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing ammonia, comprising a reactor chamber                with a solution, where the solution comprises                dissolved hydrogen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen,                glutamine synthetase inhibitor and autotrophic                diazotroph bacteria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of dissolving                  hydrogen in the solution involves splitting water                  in the solution to form hydrogen and oxygen,                  where splitting water in the solution involves                  splitting water using a cathode including                  cobalt-phosphorus alloy and an anode comprising                  cobalt phosphate. The step of dissolving carbon                  dioxide and nitrogen in the solution involves                  bubbling carbon dioxide and nitrogen through the                  solution. Preferred Components: The solution                  comprises a phosphate. Preferred System: The                  system further comprises (a) a power source                  connected to a first electrode comprising a                  cobalt phosphorus alloy and a second electrode                  comprising cobalt phosphate, where the first and                  the second electrodes are at least partially                  immersed in the solution within the reactor                  chamber, (b) a phosphate in the solution, and (c)                  a gas inlet which bubbles gas through the                  solution within the reactor chamber. The gas                  source comprises at least one of nitrogen,                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide fluidly connected to                  the gas inlet.

TI System for separating and recovering carbon            dioxide in gas to be treated using carbon dioxide            adsorbent, has transfer device which is configured for            transferring carbon dioxide adsorbent to adsorption            container.
AU NISHIBE S

   YOSHIZAWA K

AE KAWASAKI JUKOGYO KK (KAWJ-C)

   KAWASAKI JUKOGYO KK (KAWJ-C)
   KAWASAKI HEAVY IND LTD (KAWJ-C)
GA 2020375068
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1A) has a regeneration container                (32) into which the carbon dioxide adsorbent (10)                that has adsorbed carbon dioxide in the adsorption                container (31) has flowed in. A desorption steam                supply line (7) is configured for supplying                desorption steam to the regeneration container. A                carbon dioxide recovery line (8) discharges a                recovery gas containing the desorption steam that                has come into contact with the carbon dioxide                adsorbent and carbon dioxide that has desorbed from                the carbon dioxide adsorbent from the regeneration                container. A transfer device (12) is configured for                transferring the carbon dioxide adsorbent from                which carbon dioxide has been desorbed in the                regeneration container to the adsorption container.                The carbon dioxide recovery line has a heat                exchanger using the recovered gas as a heat                source.
   USE - System for separating and recovering carbon                dioxide in gas to be treated using carbon dioxide                adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - An effective utilization of energy is promoted                in carbon dioxide separation and capture                technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for separating and recovering carbon dioxide in gas                to be treated using carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram                illustrating the entire structure of the carbon                dioxide separation collecting system. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide separation collecting system                (1A)
   Desorption steam supply line (7)
   Carbon dioxide recovery line (8)
   Carbon dioxide adsorbent (10)
   Transfer device (12)
   Adsorption container (31)
   Regeneration container (32)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Indium hydroxide-coated copper bimetallic material            useful in carbon dioxide electro-catalysis, comprises            indium hydroxide and cubic cuprous oxide.
AU ZHOU B

   LI L
