   QIAO J
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2022B49962
AB    NOVELTY - Indium hydroxide-coated copper bimetallic                material comprises indium hydroxide and cubic                cuprous oxide. The indium hydroxide is coated on                the outer surface of the cubic cuprous oxide. The                molar ratio of copper and indium is 2:1-1:9. The                copper salt is cupric chloride dehydrate. The                indium salt is indium trichloride. The surfactant                is polyvinylpyrrolidone. The reducing agent is                ascorbic acid Vc.
   USE - The material is useful in carbon dioxide                electro-catalysis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate,                economical and environmentally friendly. The                material has large specific surface area, which can                greatly inhibit the hydrogen evolution reaction in                the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                reaction and achieve high current density with                extremely high carbon monoxide Faradaic                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing indium hydroxide-coated copper bimetallic                material.

   USE - Preparation of bimetallic catalyst used in                catalysis of olefin oxide homopolymerization and                catalysis of carbon dioxide and olefin oxide                copolymerization (all claimed).

The                  water-soluble metal zinc salt comprises one or                  more of zinc chloride, zinc bromide, zinc                  acetate, zinc acetylacetonate, zinc benzoate,                  zinc nitrate and zinc sulfate.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  metal cyanide complex to water-soluble metal zinc                  salt is 1:1.5-10.Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried                  out under a pressure of 0.1-15 MPa at                  20-150&#8451; for 0.5-12 hours.Preferred Process: The process further                  involves centrifuging, washing and drying the                  obtained precipitate in vacuum to constant                  weight.Preferred Properties: The bimetallic                  catalyst has particle size of 18 nm to 50                  &#181;m and polydispersity index (PDI) of                  less than 0.2.

AB    NOVELTY - Constructing a stable three-phase interface involves providing an electrode with a copper-based surface structure, using octadecyl mercaptan to perform hydrophobic treatment on the electrode, performing in-situ evolution on the surface of the electrode during the hydrophilic treatment to form a stable chemical bond with the electrode to obtain an electrode having a stable three-dimensional interface.

PT P
PN CN114289010-A
TI Titanium oxide-tin oxide composite photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises titanium oxide nanotubes and tin oxide nanoparticles, and tin oxide nanoparticles are loaded at mouth of titanium oxide nanotubes.
AU DAI J
   YANG F
   SHI F
   PAN X
   LI D
   CAI G
AE CHIZHOU NANHUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (CHIZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV JIANGSU NANJING HUAXING ENVIRONMENT (UYJI-Non-standard)
GA 2022551410
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium oxide-tin oxide (TiO2-SnO2) composite photocatalyst, comprises titanium oxide nanotubes and tin oxide nanoparticles. The tin oxide nanoparticles are loaded at the mouth of titanium oxide nanotubes, where tin oxide is obtained by physical and/or chemical method growth on the surface of the nozzle of titanium oxide nanotubes.
   USE - Titanium oxide-tin oxide composite photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst effectively promotes the separation of photogenerated carriers, and effectively improves the photocatalytic reduction efficiency of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a composite photocatalyst, i.e. TiO2-SnO2 composite photocatalyst, involves using TiO2 nanotubes as cathode, and conductive anode into tin chloride (SnCl4) solution for electrodeposition and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The composite photocatalyst is obtained by electro-depositing titanium oxide nanotubes as cathode materials in tin cation (Sn4+) solution. The potential of titanium dioxide nanotubes are gradually decreased along its length and reached the lowest potential at one of the nozzles. The current of electrodeposition is 5-100 mA, and the concentration of Sn4+ in the solution is 0.01-0.1 mol/L. The composite photocatalyst is prepared by cleaning the pure titanium foil and putting it into ethylene glycol electrolyte for anodic oxidation, cleaning and drying the material, calcining in a muffle furnace to obtain titanium dioxide nanotubes. The TiO2 nanotube is used as the cathode and platinum electrode as the anode in 0.03-0.1 mol/L tin tetrachloride (SnCl4) solution for electrodeposition with a current of 8-80 mA. The material is washed and then dried in an oven to obtain a TiO2-SnO2 composite photocatalyst. Preferred Compositions: The electrolyte is 0.1-1.0 wt.% ammonium fluoride (NH4F) solution. The solvent includes ethylene glycol and water in ratio of 10:1-100:1. The SnCl4 solution also contains a refiner, which includes 0.1-1.0 mol/L cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, and the solution temperature is 30-80degrees Celsius. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the electrolyte is 35-60degrees Celsius, the DC voltage is 30-80 volt, and the electrolysis time is 10-60 minutes. The calcination temperature is 350-450degrees Celsius, the time is 90-140 minutes, and the heating rate is 1-10degrees Celsius/min. The drying temperature is 90-110degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 8-12 hours.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E35-K02; E31-U02; E11-Q02C; E31-U01; E35-H; J04-E04C; J04-E04A

AB    NOVELTY - A capture agent includes magnetic polyaniline ultrafine nanofibers combined to form a cluster-like structure, where the cluster-like structure has a cage-like structure, and active amino groups distributed on surface of the magnetic polyaniline ultrafine nanofibers.
   USE - Capture agent used for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The capture agent can replace ammonia water, and has carbon dioxide adsorption performance of greater than or equal to12.81 mmol/g. The preparation method has low economic cost and high industrial production feasibility, and is non-toxic and harmless.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the capture agent, which involves immersing the cluster-like magnetic polyaniline ultrafine nanofibers in an alkali solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cluster-like magnetic polyaniline ultrafine nanofibers are prepared by soaking nano-magnetic powder in aniline, mixing with solution of acidic substance, adding initiator, and reacting and copolymerizing.Preferred Components: The solute of the alkali is an inorganic alkaline substance, preferably sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, and/or ammonia monohydrate. The acidic substance includes p-toluenesulfonic acid, camphorsulfonic acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or nitric acid. The initiator includes ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, and/or potassium persulfate.Preferred Composition: The solvent of the alkali is water in the concentration of 8-12 wt.%. The addition rate of the initiator is 1.2-1.6 g/minute. The weight ratio of the nano-magnetic powder to aniline is 1-5:10. The weight ratio of aniline, acidic substance and initiator is 1-10:1-10:1-100.

PT P
PN CN112812091-A; CN112812091-B
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves reacting            pyridine saccharinate as catalyst, epoxy compound and            carbon dioxide under catalytic condition.
AU GUO K
   TONG H
   LI Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202158586P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves                reacting pyridine saccharinate (I) or (II) as                catalyst, epoxy compound (III) and carbon dioxide                under catalytic condition.
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, economical                and carried out at mild reaction condition using                metal-free and halogen-free catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves                reacting pyridine saccharinate of formula (I) or                (II) as catalyst, epoxy compound of formula (III)                and carbon dioxide under catalytic condition.
   R1 = H or dimethylamino;
   R2 = pyrrolyl;
   R3,R4 = H, linear or branched 1-4C alkyl,                n-butyl, phenyl, halo, alkylphenyl, chloroalkyl or                bromoalkyl, or R5-O-CH2-;
   R5 = phenyl (optionally substituted by 1-3C                alkyl), allyl or linear or branched 1-4C                alkyl;and
   R3+R4 = 5 or 6-membered ring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The pyridine                  saccharinate is preferably chosen from compounds                  of formulae (1)-(3). The compound (III) is                  preferably chosen from 13 compounds e.g.                  compounds of formulae (IIIa)-(IIIe). Preferred                  Condition: The reaction is carried out at 40-120                  degrees C, preferably 120 degrees C under initial                  pressure of 0.1-2 MPa, preferably 0.1 MPa.                  Preferred Process: The preparation involves                  adding compound (III) and catalyst to the                  reaction vessel, replacing the air in the                  reaction vessel with carbon dioxide, filling                  carbon dioxide to 0.1-2 MPa, raising temperature                  to 40-120 degrees C, reacting under solvent-free                  condition for 6-24 hours, cooling and subjecting                  to column chromatography. Preferred Composition:                  The ratio of catalyst to epoxy compound is 1-10                  mol%, preferably 10 mol%.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R5 = tolyl, tert-butyl or methyl.

   JINHUA SHOUXIANGU PHARM CO LTD (JINH-Non-standard)
GA 201967032S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises (1) mixing the mature-broken G.lucidumspore powder with water and granulating to obtain G.lucidum spore powder particles, (2) sequentially subjecting G.lucidum spore powder particles to vacuum microwave drying and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and collecting the extract and (3) removing carbon dioxide from the extract to obtain G.lucidumspore oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple. The oil product: has low oxidation degree and high quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for preparing a Ganoderma lucidum spore oil (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the method for preparing mature-broken G. lucidum spore powder comprises: heating G.lucidum spores, maturing in boiling water, curing, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain matured G.lucidum spores, and drying matured G.lucidum spores and breaking to obtain mature broken G.lucidum spore powder. In the step (1), the ratio of G.lucidum spore to the boiling water is 1:20-30, and the heating and curing time is 20-120 minutes. The method of solid-liquid separation is plate and frame pressure filtration, and the filter cloth filter frame has a pore diameter of 4-10 mu m. In the step (2), the drying method is microwave vacuum drying, and the vacuum degree of the microwave vacuum drying is 0.06-0.10 MPa, the vacuum microwave drying temperature is 70-75 degrees C, and the vacuum microwave drying time is 60-120 minutes. The method of breaking the wall is physical breaking. In the step (1), the method for preparing the mature-broken G.lucidum spore powder further comprises: (I) drying G.lucidum spores and breaking to obtain broken G.lucidum spore powder, and (II) heating broken G.lucidum spore powder, maturing in boiling water, curing and subjecting solid-liquid separation to obtain mature broken G.lucidum spore powder. In the step (2), the vacuum degree of the vacuum microwave drying is 0.06-0.10 MPa, the vacuum microwave drying temperature is 60-65 degrees C, and the vacuum microwave drying time is 60-120 minutes. The pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 20-30 MPa, the temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 35-45 degrees C, and the time of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 4-6 hours. In the step (3), the method for removing carbon dioxide in the extract comprises first separation and second separation, during first separation, the separation pressure of 4-7 Mpa and the separation temperature of 45-55 degrees C, and during second separation, the separation pressure of 5-6 MPa and separation temperature of 40-50 degrees C. In the process of removing the carbon dioxide in the extract, further comprises: performing membrane filtration on the extracted oil, where the membrane for membrane filtration has a pore diameter of 2-4 mu m. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the mass ratio of the mature-broken G.lucidum spore powder and water matured is 10:3-10:7. The particle size of the G.lucidumspore powder particle is less than or equal to 14 mesh. The G.lucidum spore oil is storing at room temperature for 6 months after preparation, and zperoxide value does not exceed 0.1 g/100 g and the acid value of the G.lucidum spore oil does not exceed 10.0 potassium hydroxide mg/g.

PN CN110026160-A; CN110026160-B
TI Preparation of weak base ion exchange fiber for adsorbing heavy metalate anion dichromate or carbon dioxide, involves modifying pretreated PTFE fiber, copolymerizing fiber with vinyl trichlorosilane monomer and treating copolymer.
AU ZHANG Q
   LIU B
   YU L
   WANG Y
   ZHANG L
   LI S
AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 2019651808
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of weak base ion exchange fiber involves high-energy physical modifying pretreated PTFE fiber, graft copolymerizing modified PTFE fiber with a vinyl trichlorosilane monomer to obtain a copolymer and treating copolymer with an alkali solution.
   USE - Preparation of weak base ion exchange fiber for adsorbing heavy metalate anion dichromate or carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency of the grafting reaction after pre-irradiation is relatively high. The grafting efficiency can be effectively controlled within a certain range, thus avoiding the waste of the monomer and avoiding the environmental pollution problem occurring in the conventional preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for weak base ion exchange fiber for adsorbing heavy metalate anion dichromate or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graft copolymerization initiator is benzoyl peroxide, lauroyl peroxide, cumene hydroperoxide, t-butyl hydroperoxide, peroxide tert-butyl, dicumyl peroxide, ammonium ferrous sulfate, tert-butyl peroxybenzoate, and/or ammonium persulfate. The alkali solution is ammonium hydroxide solution, sodium hydroxide solution or sodium carbonate solution. Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of alkaline solution is 3-25%. Preferred Process: The high energy physical method is particle accelerator method or cobalt 60 gamma ray method.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent for graft copolymerization is ethylene glycol, methanol, dimethylformamide, aniline, acetonitrile, acetic acid, nitromethane, acetone, isopropanol, ethanol, tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane and/or ethyl acetate. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of modified PTFE fiber, vinyltrichlorosilane and initiator is 5-15:6.32-12.65:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Process: The PTFE fiber is washed 2-3 times with alcohol solution, dried, immersed in an organic solvent for 48-72 hours, filtered, vacuum dried, and packaged for use.

TI Preprocessing cement kiln synergistic treatment of fly ash comprises e.g. mixing sodium hydroxide strengthens calcium retention, fly ash and water, stirring with stirrer, washing with water, suction filtering and collecting fly ash solids.
AU CHEN Z

   BEIJING JINGKE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201945807G
AB    NOVELTY - Preprocessing cement kiln synergistic treatment of fly ash comprises (i) mixing sodium hydroxide strengthens calcium retention, fly ash and water, stirring with stirrer and washing with water, and adding sodium hydroxide during the stirring process, (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into the system to control carbon dioxide ventilation, where carbon dioxide strengthens calcium retention, (iii) subjecting the water-ash mixture to suction filtration separation, and (iv) collecting the fly ash solids, and the cement kiln co-disposal, and collecting the separated water washing liquid for water treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preprocessing cement kiln synergistic treatment of fly ash.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), controlling the water-cement ratio to be 3-10 ml/g, preferably 5 ml/g. The water washing time is 5-30 minutes, preferably 10 minutes. The water washing temperature is 20-50 degrees C, preferably 20 degrees C. In the step (i), controlling the amount of carbon dioxide to be introduced in a ratio of carbon dioxide to fly ash is 1-5 l/10 g, preferably 3 l/10 g. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of sodium hydroxide to fly ash is 1: (5-20), preferably 1:10.

   USE - Absorbent for the selective removal of                hydrogen sulfide from a fluid stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Absorbent comprises carbon dioxide and                hydrogen sulfide, where the absorbent contains an                aqueous solution, comprising an amine or a mixture                of amine of formula (I) and an ether or a mixture                of ethers of formula (II).
   R1=1-5C alkyl;
   R3=selected from hydrogen and 1-5C                alkyl;
   X=an integer from 2 to 10;
   R4=1-5C alkyl; and
   y=an integer from 2 to 10.

The molar amount of the primary amine of formula                  (III) exceeds the molar amount of the ether of                  formula (II) during the reaction. PT P
PN CN108728241-A
TI Preparation of antioxidant sesame oil involves preparing sesame powder, preparing antioxidant powder, mixing sesame powder with antioxidant powder, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU OUYANG D
AE ANHUI LAOOUMOFANG FOOD TRADE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201888305F
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of antioxidant sesame oil involves frying sesame, pulverizing to 130-150 mesh to obtain sesame powder, drying and pulverizing broccoli to 4-8 mesh to obtain dried broccoli, frying the dried broccoli with tea leaves, bamboo leaves and lemon grass, pulverizing to 130-150 mesh to obtain antioxidant powder, mixing the sesame powder with antioxidant powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 30-40 degrees C and pressure of 25-35 MPa for 150-200 minutes under carbon dioxide flow rate of 18-25 l/hour to obtain sesame oil and taking out sesame oil.
   USE - Preparation of antioxidant sesame oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of antioxidant sesame oil with excellent oxidation resistance, excellent storing effect and high anti-deterioration effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of sesame powder to antioxidant powder is (240-270):1. The weight ratio of dried broccoli, tea leaves, bamboo leaves and lemon grass is (10-12):(4-6):(3-4):1. The addition amount of adsorbent is 0.2-0.5% of weight of sesame oil. Preferred Process: The frying process of sesame is carried out by stirring the sesame at 50-60 degrees C for 8-10 minutes, stirring at 130-150 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, and frying at 70-80 degrees C for 10-15 minutes. The impurity removing process is carried out by adding adsorbent to sesame oil, stirring for 30-40 minutes, and filtering to obtain sesame oil. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of entrainer ethanol is 20-30% of carbon dioxide flow rate. The pressure of separation vessel (I) is 7-9 MPa and temperature is 38-42 degrees C. The pressure of separation vessel (II) is 3-5 MPa and temperature is 32-36 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material of adsorbent comprises activated clay, montmorillonite and activated carbon with weight ratio of (8-10):(3-5):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Ethanol is used as entrainer in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process. Two separation kettles are provided in supercritical carbon dioxide extractor.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-D03B; D10-A
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/10; C11B-005/00; A23D-009/02

PT P
PN CN108640141-A; CN108640141-B
TI Preparation of different crystalline calcium carbonate involves passing aqueous solution of carbon dioxide to water-soluble organic matter and water to form (bi)carbonate, reacting (bi)carbonate with calcium salt solution.
AU LI J
   DAVID M
   BENOIT L
   SU Y
   HONG Y
   WANG H
   BENOIT R
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2018813244
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of different crystalline calcium carbonate involves passing aqueous solution of carbon dioxide to compound (A) which is an insoluble or slightly water-soluble organic matter reactive with carbon dioxide and water to form compound (B) which is (bi)carbonate of the compound (A), adding calcium salt solution to compound (B), reacting, and separating the solid calcium carbonate product. After separation of the product, the aqueous solution is converted into two phases of oil and water, and oil phase is separated and reused.
   USE - Preparation of different crystalline calcium Carbonate e.g. vaterite calcium carbonate and aragonite calcium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When compound (A) is 1,8-diazacyclo(5,4,0)undecene-7, vaterite calcium carbonate is produced at 30.0 degrees C, and aragonite calcium carbonate is produced at 70.0 degrees C. The separation and conversion of aqueous solution is carried out by adding sodium hydroxide alkaline substance or directly heating to N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine as compound (A) to form aqueous phase of sodium chloride solution and immiscible oil phase, and separating two phases. Preferred Components: The calcium salt comprises calcium chloride and/or calcium acetate. The crystalline calcium carbonate comprises calcite, aragonite and vaterite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic matter comprises 2-N-dibutylaminoethanol, N-butyl dimethylamine, 1,8-diazacyclo(5,4,0)undecene-7 and/or N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN BR102016028290-A2
TI Portable device for measuring alveolar carbon dioxide from e.g. athlete, has infrared source for directing infrared rays to gas sampling chamber, where numbers of infrared detector and waves are proportion to number of infrared waves.
AU LOPES TORRES D
AE LOPES T D (LOPE-Individual)
GA 2018865827
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an infrared (IR) detection device provided with an IR source for directing infrared rays to a gas sampling chamber, where number of an IR detector is proportion to number of IR waves to be filtered. Different gaseous molecules are absorbed at different IR wavelengths, where the device is configured to measure volume of respiratory carbon dioxide, to calculate the caloric expenditure, to measure oxygen to be consumed, to indicate respiratory quotient.
   USE - Portable device for measuring alveolar carbon dioxide to measure caloric expenditure of an athlete or amateur while performing physical activities.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is in a form of a bracelet, thus increasing comfortability of a user.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide cooling-heating machine set, comprising a compressor, a gas cooler, a filter, an expansion valve, an evaporator, the first refrigerant flows through the user end main tube, the second refrigerant flows through the surface cooler, a first three-way valve, a second three-way valve. the third three-way valve, the fourth valve, the solution, the user end pipe through the pipeline connected with the gas cooler and the evaporator form the first coolant circulation loop, cooler through pipeline connected with the gas cooler and the evaporator form the loop of the second refrigerant cycle, it can realize carbon dioxide cooling-heating machine set of switching.

PT P
PN KR2018023581-A; KR1934404-B1
TI Preparing magnesium oxide-based absorbent useful for carbon dioxide capture module, comprises e.g. preparing precursor mixture solution for dissolving magnesium oxide precursor, carbonate precursor, and nitrate precursor in solvent.
AU NA K S
   CHO S J
AE UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
GA 2018212942
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium oxide-based absorbent, comprises (a) preparing precursor mixture solution for dissolving magnesium oxide precursor, carbonate precursor, and nitrate precursor in solvent, (b) removing the solvent from the precursor mixture solution to obtain dried precursor mixture, and (c) plasticizing the precursor mixture.
   USE - The absorbent is useful for carbon dioxide capture module (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and environmentally friendly. The absorbent has high absorption capacity, high absorption rate, and excellent regeneration in repeated adsorption/desorption process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) magnesium oxide-based absorbent produced by the above method; and
   (2) carbon dioxide capture module comprising magnesium oxide-based absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), magnesium oxide precursor, carbonate precursor and nitrate precursor are prepared such manner that the molar ratio of magnesium oxide, carbonate and nitrate is 1:0.1-5:0.01-0.5. The step (a) comprises (a1) adding the carbonate precursor to solvent and dissolving the precursor to prepare first precursor solution, (a2) adding nitrate precursor to the first precursor solution and dissolving to prepare second precursor solution, and (a3) adding magnesium oxide precursor to the second precursor solution and dissolving to prepare precursor mixture solution. The step (c) is carried out by supplying air at 370-430 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carbonate precursor includes lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, and/or calcium carbonate. The nitrate precursor includes lithium nitrate, sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, and/or calcium nitrate. The magnesium oxide-based absorbent has carbon dioxide absorption capacity of greater than or equal to 8 wt.%/g, carbon dioxide absorption rate of greater than or equal to 2.5 wt.%/min which is controlled by controlling composition and content of the magnesium oxide precursor, the carbonate precursor, and the nitrate precursor, has residual absorption capacity maintained at greater than or equal to 50% of the initial absorption capacity after the absorption/desorption process is performed for greater than or equal to 20 times, desorption temperature of 400-500 degrees C,
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is polar solvent. The carbon dioxide capture module has carbon dioxide absorption capacity of greater than or equal to 30 wt.%/g and carbon dioxide absorption rate of greater than or equal to 3 wt.%/min at 300-500 degrees C under normal pressure and mixed flue gas (2-15% carbon dioxide/ 85-98% nitrogen).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional intelligent emergency cart                comprises pressing frequency reminder module, in                the standby state, once the first aid box is                opened, the pressing frequency reminder is turned                on; the pressing frequency reminder device provides                an indication of the pressing frequency during the                cardiopulmonary resuscitation process. The pressing                frequency reminder module comprises a display; an                electrocardiogram (ECG) sampling circuit for                sampling the patient's ECG signal in real time and                obtaining a sampling sequence; a signal corrector                for calibrating the ECG signal; a controller whose                output end of the circuit is connected, and the                output end is connected to the display is used to                obtain the compression frequency according to the                sampling sequence corresponding to the chest                compression when detected that the patient is                performing chest compressions according to the                sampling sequence and output it to the display for                display.
   USE - Multifunctional intelligent emergency cart                used in common sickroom, emergency department and                intensive care unit, in the operating room and the                public places task.
   ADVANTAGE - The multifunctional intelligent emergency cart                has better rescue assistance, reaches the purpose                of saving life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multifunctional intelligent emergency cart                comprises pressing frequency reminder module, in                the standby state, once the first aid box is                opened, the pressing frequency reminder is turned                on; the pressing frequency reminder device will                provide an indication of the pressing frequency                during the cardiopulmonary resuscitation process.                The pressing frequency reminder module comprises a                display; an ECG sampling circuit for sampling the                patient's ECG signal in real time and obtaining a                sampling sequence; a signal corrector; for                calibrating the ECG signal; a controller whose                output end of the circuit is connected, and the                output end is connected to the display is used to                obtain the compression frequency according to the                sampling sequence corresponding to the chest                compression when detected that the patient is                performing chest compressions according to the                sampling sequence and output it to the display for                display. The controller comprises a sampling                filtering module, configured to first low-pass                filter each sampling signal in the sampling                sequence corresponding to the chest compression                when it is detected that the patient is performing                chest compressions according to the sampling                sequence, and then perform a low-pass filtering                process, median filtering; the frequency obtaining                module is used to determine the time interval                between adjacent minimum or maximum points from the                filtered sampling sequence; the ECG sampling                frequency is divided by the time interval to                obtain, press the frequency sequence and output it                to the display for display. The high-frequency jet                ventilation ventilator control method acquiring the                input frequency of the high-frequency jet                ventilation ventilator; when the input frequency is                lower than a preset frequency, the high-frequency                jet ventilation ventilator performs                normal-frequency ventilation control, the                constant-frequency ventilation control includes the                high-frequency oscillation module is turned off,                and the turbine fan control is performed according                to the patient's breathing information, and the                turbine fan control includes when the patient                inhales, controlling the rotational speed of the                turbine fan to increase; hen exhaling, control the                rotational speed of the turbo blower to decrease;                when the input frequency is greater than or equal                to the preset frequency, the high-frequency jet                ventilation ventilator performs high-frequency                ventilation control.irst aid medicine                classification storage module, classify and store                first aid medicines through first aid medicine                classification equipment. The first-aid medicine                classification storage module includes a scanner, a                storage bin, and an alarm; the barcode of the                first-aid medicine is scanned by the scanner to                obtain the type and time information of the                first-aid medicine, and whether it is expired is                judged according to the time information; classify                and store emergency medicines according to the type                of medicine information through the storage bin;                monitoring module to monitor oxygen saturation,                ECG, end-tidal carbon dioxide and body temperature;                intelligent calculation usage module, which is used                to calculate usage and rescue time through                intelligent computing equipment; the communication                module notifies the pre-set important rescue                members to seek better rescue assistance.

   (2) Use of the multifunctional intelligent                emergency cart in common sickroom.

PT P
PN CN110433137-A
TI Use of mesenchymal stem cell paracrine factor, in preparation of retinal macular degeneration eye drops.
AU LIU X
   ZHANG C
AE BEIJING SINO TIANYI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201996941B
AB    NOVELTY - Use of mesenchymal stem cell paracrine factor, is claimed, in preparation of retinal macular degeneration eye drops.
   USE - The mesenchymal stem cell paracrine factor is useful in preparation of retinal macular degeneration eye drops (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The eye drops maintain protein activity to the maximum extent, where the protein is stored frozen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the eye drop, which involves (a) taking a full-term healthy fetal umbilical cord, detecting umbilical cord serum surface antigen of the hepatitis B virus (HBV) (HbsAg), anti-HCV, anti-HDV, anti-HEV, anti-HIV-1/2, HCV-RNA, HIV-1-RNA and CMV-DNA, and determining that the test results as negative, (b) after wiping the normal saline bottle with gauze sprayed with alcohol, removing the sealing plastic at the bottle opening and placing into a clean table in a running state, (c) washing in normal saline to remove umbilical cord blood stains, then immersing in ethanol to disinfect, then washing with normal saline again, cutting the umbilical cord into 2-3 cm segments in a Petri dish filled with physiological saline, washing with physiological saline to remove congestion and clots in the small umbilical cord blood vessels, peeling off Wharton's jelly and washing with normal saline, (d) preparing cut umbilical cord Wharton's jelly by cutting the umbilical cord Wharton's jelly into tissue portions of 1-3 mm and centrifuging, (e) inoculating by after removing the supernatant from the tissue homogenate after centrifugation, inoculating into an inoculation culture bottle, and flattening the entire bottom surface of the inoculation culture bottle, (f) culturing the umbilical cord Wharton's jelly by inoculating the culture flask, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, after 6-12 hours, slowly raising the culture flask upright, adding basal medium high sugar (Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM)) containing 5-10 wt.% serum substitute to the bottom of the culture bottle, then slowly letting the medium infiltrate the tissue block to continue the culture, and culturing at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C with carbon dioxide concentration of 5 plus minus 0.2%, (g) changing fluid for the first time by culturing the umbilical cord Wharton's jelly until the 6th-7th day for full liquid exchange, discarding the old medium and unattached tissue pieces, adding an equal volume of high-glucose DMEM medium containing 10 wt.% serum substitute, placing flask flat, placing in the carbon dioxide incubator to continue cultivation, after culturing the umbilical cord Wharton's jelly for 9-10 days, repeating the operation of the first liquid exchange, observing the growth of the cells under the mirror image, if the degree of cell fusion does not reach 70-80%, after the umbilical cord Wharton's jelly is cultured to 12-13 days, and adding a full volume change, (h) observing primary cells by observing all culture flasks under a microscope, if it is observed that the degree of cell fusion reaches 70-80%, performing primary cell harvest by mixing trypsin and physiological saline in a volume ratio of 1:4 to obtain a mixed solution, gently shaking the culture bottle to remove the non-adherent tissue portions, then pouring the tissue portions with the old culture solution into the waste solution bottle, pouring physiological saline into each bottle from which the old culture solution is removed, gently shaking the culture bottle to make the saline solution infiltrate the bottom surface of the culture bottle and the tissue block, then pouring out the saline solution, repeating the washing process, adding mixed solution to each culture bottle after washing, letting to stand for 1 minute, shaking the culture bottle up and down to make the digestive solution fully contact the cells, observing that 90% of the cells fall off and round under the microscope to stop digestion, adding high-sugar DMEM medium containing serum substitutes to each bottle that stops digestion, shaking the culture bottle back and forth, and transferring to a centrifuge tube for centrifugation, (i) performing passage of primary cells, by centrifuging the cells after the previous step, removing the supernatant, adding the high-glucose DMEM medium containing serum substitutes, resuspending the cell pellets, combining into a centrifuge tube, counting on the cell counting plate, inoculating the cell suspension obtained after resuspension into a culture flask, adding a stem cell culture medium containing a serum substitute, shaking well, placing into a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C until the cell fusion reaches 80-90%, inoculating T150 culture bottles at 50000 cells/cm2, and adding 15-20 ml high-sugar DMEM medium containing serum substitutes to each bottle, and (j) when the cells are passaged to the P4 passage, using the cells, raising the cell fusion to 80-90%, removing culture medium from the culture flask, washing with physiological saline buffer for 2-3 times, then sucking the saline solution in the culture flask, adding a high-glucose DMEM medium containing 5 wt.% serum substitute, placing the culture flask containing the cells in a hypoxic incubator with 2% oxygen concentration at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C in carbon dioxide concentration of 5 plus minus 0.2%, continuously culturing for 48 hours, collecting mesenchymal stem cell paracrine factor suspension, placing into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, collecting supernatant, then sterilizing and filtering through a 0.22 mu m sterile filter to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cells: The mesenchymal stem cells for obtaining paracrine factors are derived from mesenchymal stem cells of various tissues of mammals, preferably humans. The source of mesenchymal stem cells for obtaining paracrine factors is chosen from umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, umbilical cord blood mesenchymal stem cells, adipose mesenchymal stem cells, placental mesenchymal stem cells, and amniotic mesenchymal stem cells. Preferred Composition: The eye drops comprise combination of all mesenchymal stem cell paracrine factors.
   ACTIVITY - Ophthalmological.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-H01; B12-M07; B12-M12H; B14-N03; D05-A02C; D05-H08B; A11-B04A; A11-C07; A12-V; A12-V01
IP A61K-035/28; A61K-038/17; A61K-009/08; A61P-027/02

TI Pitch/yaw bearing ring race of laser phase change hardening machining device comprises laser generation system and auxiliary processing system, laser generation system comprising computer, concave lens and first refractor.
AU YANG C
   WU L
   ZHOU Z
   WANG M
   XU X
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2018779561
AB    NOVELTY - Pitch/yaw bearing ring race of laser phase change hardening machining device comprises laser generation system and an auxiliary processing system. The laser generation system comprises a computer, a high power carbon dioxide laser generating device, a concave lens, a first convex lens, a first refractor, a collimation beam expanding mirror, a focusing device, and a computer with high power carbon dioxide laser generating device. The control laser emitted by the needed parameter of laser is emitted by the laser through the collimating beam expanding mirror, a concave lens, a first convex lens, a first refractor to the focusing device, the focusing device by the second convex lens and the second refractive mirror. The auxiliary processing system comprises a digital controller, a cantilever beam, a support frame, revolving platform, the first ball screw, a second ball screw, a platform, a first servo motor, a second servo motor, a coupler and a third refractor.
   USE - Used as pitch/yaw bearing ring race of laser phase change hardening machining device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: increases maximum hardened layer depth, improves bearing contact strength and fatigue resistance of the ferrule raceway improves wear resistance and corrosion resistance of the bearing workpiece.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pitch/yaw bearing ring race of laser phase change hardening machining device comprises laser generation system and an auxiliary processing system. The laser generation system comprises a computer, a high power carbon dioxide laser generating device, a concave lens, a first convex lens, a first refractor, a collimation beam expanding mirror, a focusing device, and a computer with high power carbon dioxide laser generating device. The control laser emitted by the needed parameter of laser is emitted by the laser through the collimating beam expanding mirror, a concave lens, a first convex lens, a first refractor to the focusing device, the focusing device by the second convex lens and the second refractive mirror, reflected by the focusing lens focuses the emitted third refractor. The auxiliary processing system comprises a digital controller, a cantilever beam, a support frame, revolving platform, the first ball screw, a second ball screw, a platform, a first servo motor, a second servo motor, a coupler and a third refractor. The digital controller is connected with the computer. The angular velocity is set by controlling the rotating platform to rotate to adjust the circumferential bearing ring race processing position. The rotary platform is fixed with the bearing inner race or bearing outer race, at the same time, on the rotary platform of the outer working surface is fixed with a cantilever beam. The cantilever beam is provided with a first ball screw. Left side of the cantilever beam is connected with the first servo motor through bolt. The first servo motor to realize the coaxial degree is set with the first coupling and the first ball screw. A first coupler the first servo motor outputs torque to the first ball screw. The supporting frame is fixed on the first sliding block of the ball screw. The supporting frame is provided with a second ball screw. The second servo motor fastened on the supporting frame baffle by a bolt. The second servo motor and the second ball screw coaxial degree are set by the second coupler. The second coupler the second servo motor output torque to the second ball screw and the second ball screw upper slide block is connected by the bolt and the sloping bench is fixed with a third servo motor is connected with the third refractor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of machining device comprising (i) pre-processing double-row four-point contact ball bearing ring raceway, washing the ferrule raceway surface is by absolute ethyl alcohol, and spraying black paint on the raceway surface, (ii) fixing bearing ring on the rotating platform, fixing cantilever beam, controlling the first servo motor to adjust the horizontal position of the support frame, adjusting second servo motor position on the vertical direction of the refractor, adjusting the digital controller controls the focusing device in the refraction angle of the second refractor so that the laser can enter the third refractor on the mirror surface after refraction, the ferrule raceway surface of the laser beam and the surface tangent direction is vertical, (iii) opening the high power carbon dioxide laser generating device through computer set parameter of laser, high power laser generating device emits a laser focused on the third refractor and the laser enters the third refractor, third refractor rotating by the third servo motor control, processing laser from the raceway to the bottom, digital controller controls the rotary platform to rotate to the next processing point, laser processing and then to the top from the ferrule raceway bottom, repeating the rotating platform rotates for a circle, processing the ferrule rollaway nest, the third refractor by the second ball screw processing position moving from the upper raceway to the ring race processing position, the moving length of shortest distance is two race center until finishing the whole ring race surface processing, (iv) closing all the devices, the ferrule after hardening by phase change, removing surface residual paint and cleaning the surface with ethanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the pitch/yaw bearing ring race of laser phase change hardening machining device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the paint thickness at 0.21-0.76 mm. In the step (ii), the laser wavelength is 10600 nm, the laser power is 900-1600 W, the spot width is 9 mm. In the step (ii), third refractor rotating by the third servo motor control, such that the lasers beam scanning speed of the ferrule raceway surface is 120 mm/minutes. In the step (ii), the digital controller controls the rotary platform to rotate to the next processing point, namely the raceway surface level rotating at 5 mm.

TI Preparing SSZ-13 molecular sieve membrane by            microwave heating useful for separating carbon            dioxide/methane system, comprises preparing SSZ-13            molecular sieve seed crystals, pretreating long and            short supports, and preparing SSZ-13 molecular sieve            membrane.
AU LIU B
   SUN C
   HE Z
   ZHU Y
   ZHOU R
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2022A16131
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing SSZ-13 molecular sieve membrane by                microwave heating comprises (i) mixing silicon                source, aluminum source, alkali source and                structure directing agent (SDA) to obtain sol,                aging at room temperature for 12 hours, aging for                period of time, pouring sol into reaction kettle,                crystallizing under microwave heating reaction                conditions, collecting out, centrifuging product,                washing to neutrality, drying and calcining to                obtain SSZ-13 molecular sieve seed, (ii) dispersing                seed in an ethanol solution, obtaining seed crystal                suspension by ultrasonication, sealing both ends of                tubular carrier with tetrafluoro plugs, coating                layer of seed crystals on porous support, and                drying in oven to obtain continuous and dense seed                crystal layer, and (3) preparing sol, pouring sol                into reaction kettle, microwave heating at                110-200&#8451; for 1-30 minutes, completing                reaction, collecting out membrane tube, washing to                neutrality, drying and calcining to obtain SSZ-13                molecular sieve membrane.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing SSZ-13                molecular sieve membrane by microwave heating for                separating carbon dioxide/methane system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short reaction time; provides                molecular sieve membrane layer, which is uniform                and dense and has no macropore defect; adopts                microwave heating, which greatly shortens synthesis                time. The membrane has good separation performance                for carbon dioxide/methane system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  obtained sol components in the step (i) is                  silicon dioxide/aluminium oxide=10-500, sodium                  oxide/silicon dioxide=0.02-0.5, SDA/Silicon                  dioxide=0.01-0.6, water/silicon dioxide=10-500. PN CN114031500-A
TI Preparation of dimethyl carbonate using catalyst,            by obtaining pre-treated sulfuric acid residue; adding            precipitator in liquid phase to make precipitate,            mixing precipitate and solid slag, granulating,            calcining particles to obtain catalyst precursor;            drying precursor to obtain catalyst.
AU YAO P
   YANG Q
   ZHANG S
   LI F
   XIE H
   LU W
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202234041K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dimethyl carbonate comprises:                (1) after drying the sulfuric acid residue,                grinding, then performing acid leaching treatment,                reducing the copper, lead, zinc, sulfur, arsenic                element in the sulfuric acid residue, solid-liquid                separating to obtain pre-treated sulfuric acid                residue; (2) acid leaching the pre-treated sulfate                slag again, leaching the iron element, solid-liquid                separating, cleaning the obtained solid slag, and                drying; (3) adding excessive precipitator in the                liquid phase obtained by solid-liquid separation to                make the iron element form precipitate, separating                the precipitate, and washing; (4) mixing the                precipitate and the solid slag, calcium carbonate,                and water uniformly, granulating, calcining the                obtained particles in the air atmosphere to obtain                the catalyst precursor; and (5) soaking the                catalyst precursor by clean water, drying to obtain                the catalyst, and using the catalyst to catalyze                carbon dioxide and methanol.
   USE - Preparation of dimethyl carbonate using a                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses catalyst prepared by                industrial waste residue to catalyze carbon dioxide                and methanol to synthesize dimethyl carbonate,                which improves the conversion rate of methanol and                has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                catalyst for catalyzing carbon dioxide and methanol                to synthesize dimethyl carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  precipitator comprises sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, aqueous ammonia, sodium                  carbonate or potassium carbonate. In the step                  (5), the ratio of the methanol and the catalyst                  is 100ml: 6-10 g. Preferred Method: In the step                  (1), the acid leaching ratio of sulfuric acid                  slag and acid is 1 g: 1.4-1. 7 ml, the acid                  comprises 5-15% sulfuric acid, or nitric acid; in                  the step (1), the acid leaching temperature is                  40-65degrees Celsius, and the time is 1.5-2                  hours. In the step (2), the ratio of sulfuric                  acid to acid in the acid leaching process is 1 g:                  2.5-4 ml, the acid comprises 30-45% sulfuric                  acid, or nitric acid; in the step (2), the acid                  leaching temperature is 40-65degrees Celsius, and                  the time is 2.5-4 hours, and the acid leaching                  process is carried out in the process of                  stirring. In the step (2), washing the solid slag                  with clean water, removing the residual acid                  liquid, then drying the solid slag at 70-90                  degrees Celsius for 1-1.5 hours, and grinding the                  solid slag powder into micro powder. In the step                  (4), the total weight of the precipitate, solid                  slag, calcium carbonate is standard, the mass                  fraction of the precipitate and calcium                  respectively is 20-30%, 5-10%, and the remaining                  is the solid slag; in the step (4), the adding                  amount of the water is precipitate, solid slag,                  20-28% of the total weight of the calcium                  carbonate; in the step (4), the particle diameter                  of the particle is between 2-3 mm; in the step                  (4), the calcining temperature is 850-950degrees                  Celsius, and the time is 1.5-2 hours. In the step                  (5), the clean water is replaced every half an                  hour to soak, until the pH value of the soaking                  liquid is constant, and the ultrasonic                  oscillation in the soaking process, separating                  the solid product and drying to the constant                  weight to obtain the product. In the step (5),                  the process of synthesizing dimethyl carbonate is                  110-135 degrees Celsius, 1.5-4 MPa under the                  condition of reacting, after finishing reaction,                  fine distilling the dimethyl carbonate in the                  reaction liquid to obtain the product.

   OUYANG Z
   HAN S
AE TIANJIN AIKE KAISHENG GRAPHENE TECHNOLOG (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202218685S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dissolving the expanded graphite in deionized water, stir evenly in the reactor to obtain a dispersion. The dispersion obtained is sent to the microfluidic device for processing, and the above steps are repeated 3-5 times. The freeze-drying or microwave drying the solution obtained in microfluidic device prepares powder. The powder obtained is passed into carbon dioxide for activation treatment. The powder obtained is continuously fed with water vapor for activation to obtain porous graphene powder. The microwave frequency is 1000MHz-2000MHz, microwave output power density is 70-100mW/cm3, and the microwave radiation time is 6-30s. The powder obtained is activated by feeding carbon dioxide into the activation furnace. The activation temperature is 1000-1100degreesC under the condition of constant temperature for 4-6h, and the feeding amount of carbon dioxide is 2-3L/min.
   USE - Preparation method for porous graphene powder.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is odorless kerosene, and the mineral oil is base oil or paraffin oil.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating is carried out at 80-100degrees Celsius, and the stirring is carried out at 2000-3000 revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes. In the step (ii), the temperature is controlled at 100-120degrees Celsius when stirring and the stirring is carried out at 1500-2000 revolutions/minute for 10-12 minutes. In the step (iii), the temperature is controlled at 150-180degrees Celsius, and the stirring is carried out at 2500-3000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes. In the step (vi), pneumatic filling device is used during filling, and aluminum cans are fully sealed and filled.

PN CN113680360-A
TI Multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst used for preparing low-carbon olefins by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, comprises magnetite powder, nano-magnesium powder, plant ash and quartz powder.
AU SHEN J
   LIU C
   WANG Y
   WANG C
   MA N
   CHEN H
AE LUOYANG NORMAL COLLEGE (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1053E
AB    NOVELTY - Multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst comprises 60-75 pts. wt. magnetite powder, 15-20 pts. wt. nano-magnesium powder, 5-10 pts. wt. plant ash and 5- 10 pts. wt. quartz powder. The multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst is prepared by adding 60-75 pts. wt. magnetite powder, 15-20 pts. wt. nano-magnesium powder, 5-10 pts. wt. plant ash and 5-10 pts. wt. quartz powder into ball mill in sequence. The mass ratio of grinding balls and materials in the ball mill tank is 55:1~30:1. The air is replaced in the ball mill jar with hydrogen. The ball mill is put on the ball mill for 3-5 hours after completely replacing air with hydrogen to obtain powder. The obtained powder is placed in a tablet machine for cold pressing at a pressure of 250-300 MPa. The multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst is prepared after crushing and granulating.
   USE - Multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst used for preparing low-carbon olefins by carbon dioxide hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst has low raw material cost, simple preparation method and mild use conditions, obviously improved selectivity of low-carbon olefin and conversion rate of carbon dioxide by pre-activating with hydrogen with a certain concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The granularity of the magnetite powder is below 200 meshes. The particle size of the multivalent heterojunction iron-based catalyst is 0.5-1 mm.

   WU Z
   CHENG Z
AE UNIV FUJIAN (UYFJ-C)
GA 2021B74359
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cation metal-organic frameworks                (MOFs) derivative catalyst FJU-14-perchlorate                involves dissolving                4-(4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)benzoic acid and                copper(II) perchlorate hexahydrate in a mixed                solution of dimethylacetamideand and methanol, and                adding hydrochloric acid, heating and reacting at                80-100degrees Celsius for 24-36 hours, filtering,                washing with methanol, and drying in air for 12-24                hours to obtain a green FJU-14-perchlorate.
   USE - The cationic MOFs derivative material is                useful for preparing ruthenium-copper/nanoporus                carbon, which is used as cathode catalyst for                lithium-carbon dioxide battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained composite material as a catalyst                for lithium-carbon dioxide secondary battery anode                has a high-efficiency synergistic catalytic effect,                enhances the catalytic decomposition capability of                lithium carbonate, the battery has excellent cycle                performance and significantly reduced                over-potential, and due to its unique structure,                ruthenium-copper/nanoporus carbon has very high                electrochemical specific surface area and high                activity center density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method for preparing cationic MOFs                derivative material (ruthenium                tetroxide)/FJU-14-perchlorate;
   (2) a method for preparing high-efficiency                catalyst material ruthenium-copper/nanoporus carbon                based on cationic MOFs derivative material;
   (3) a method for preparing electrode.

PT P
PN EP3868842-A1
TI Producing particle coating on items by mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with solution comprising dissolved material for forming particles, spraying mixture into precipitation chamber, and capturing precipitated particles on items.
AU PADRELA L
   VERMA V
   RYAN K
AE UNIV LIMERICK (UYLI-Non-standard)
GA 2021989018
AB    NOVELTY - Particle coating is produced on one or more items by mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with solution comprising dissolved material for forming particles; spraying mixture into a precipitation chamber to precipitate particles, where chamber is at a pressure below a supercritical pressure of the supercritical fluid; and capturing the precipitated particles on one or more items located within the chamber.
   USE - Production of particle coating on one or more items.
   ADVANTAGE - Mixing a supercritical fluid, such as supercritical carbon dioxide, with a solution comprising dissolved material for producing particles prior to introducing the formed mixture to a precipitation chamber may allow the chamber to be held at a pressure below a threshold pressure required to maintain the supercritical fluid in a supercritical state (e.g., at atmospheric pressure). The supercritical fluid in the mixture may act as either or both of a supercritical antisolvent and an atomization enhancer. A rapid pressure drop as the mixture is sprayed into the chamber may cause droplets of the solution to be formed as the supercritical fluid depressurizes. Due to the below supercritical pressure in the chamber, the droplets may then rapidly dry to form particles. The pressure of the chamber may provide a dual effect in aiding the precipitation of particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Solution comprises an active pharmaceutical ingredient dissolved in an organic solvent, and optionally where (i) the active pharmaceutical ingredient comprises carbamazepine, risperidone, or ketoprofen; and/or (ii) the organic solvent comprises an alcohol, preferably methanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Processes: The method includes introducing a flow of drying gas into the chamber; orienting a flow direction of drying gas and a spray direction of the mixture in substantially the same direction; directing the flow of drying gas from a top portion of the chamber towards the one or more items; spraying the mixture through a nozzle into the chamber to precipitate particles, and optionally where nozzle orifice size of the nozzle is less than or equal to 150 mu m; capturing the precipitated particles on carrier particles in a fluidized bed located within the chamber; and fluidizing the bed of carrier particles using the flow of drying gas. The method comprises controlling a concentration of the solution to control particle loading on the one or more items; supporting the one or more items on a surface configured o allow fluid to pass through, and optionally where the surface comprises a mesh; selecting or controlling an area of the surface configured to allow fluid to pass through, and optionally by controlling movement of a cover disposed over the surface; and mixing the supercritical carbon dioxide with the solution in a nozzle, and/or where the nozzle comprises a coaxial nozzle. Preferred Components: Carrier particles comprise microcrystalline cellulose. Solution comprises a saturated solution. Preferred Parameters: Distance between an entry location of the spray and the one or more items is 10-20 cm, preferably 15 cm.
DC A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).)

   YAN T

GA 202176073L
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-based catalyst comprises a metal oxide used as a carrier, metal nickel supported on carrier, and boron nitride coated on the surface of the oxide-supported nickel-based catalyst. A boron nitride protective layer is formed to form nickel-based catalyst composite material for dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Nickel-based catalyst for dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-based catalyst has certain general applicability, low cost and universality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing nickel-based catalyst, which involves:
   (A) ultrasonically dispersing nickel precursor and metal oxide in aqueous solution under stirring;
   (B) rotary evaporating to remove solvent of suspension;
   (C) drying in an oven at 80 degrees C for 3-4 hours to obtain a solid powder;
   (D) dispersing the solid powder into the aqueous solution with boric acid and urea under stirring thoroughly;
   (E) removing the solvent by rotary evaporation;
   (F) placing in an oven at 80 degrees C to obtain a high-temperature calcined product; and
   (G) using hydrogen temperature program to reduce under hydrogen and nitrogen mixed gas at 700-800 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours to get the activated nickel-based methane dry reforming catalyst composite material.

PN CN111455127-A; WO2021239161-A1; CN111455127-B
TI Performing blowing control process for maintaining            mushroom head of bottom spraying converter, involves            measuring gas flow and gas pressure in annular slot            channel of bottom-blowing nozzle, before starts            smelting.
AU HU S
   ZHU R
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202072701P
AB    NOVELTY - Performing blowing control process involves                measuring the gas flow and gas pressure in the                annular slot channel of the bottom-blowing nozzle,                before the bottom-spraying converter starts                smelting, and calculating the mushroom head state                coefficient, after the bottom powder injection                converter starts smelting, using oxygen as the                carrier gas to inject lime powder, after the lime                powder injection is finished. The oxygen is                replaced with the same amount of carbon dioxide to                form a carbon dioxide-oxygen mixed gas and continue                blowing until the end of the smelting process,                completing the maintenance of the mushroom head of                the bottom-spraying converter according to the                mushroom head state coefficient and the molten                steel superheat in the smelting process. The carbon                dioxide injection intensity is adjusted.
   USE - Method for performing blowing control process                for maintaining mushroom head of bottom spraying                converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform blowing control                process which is used to maintain the basic                stability of the size of the mushroom head at the                end of the bottom blowing nozzle, avoids the nozzle                blockage caused by the oversized mushroom head,                realizes the effective protection of the mushroom                head to the bottom blowing nozzle and prolongs the                service life of the bottom blowing nozzle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is                  injected at the same time as the lime powder                  injection is completed.

PT P
PN CN111360073-A; CN111360073-B
TI Method for enhancing rolling copper foil surface roughness, involves using liquid carbon dioxide to treat surface of rolled copper foil that passes through smoothing roller to increase surface roughness of rolled copper foil.
AU YU H
   LEI G
   CUI X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202063791T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves taking a rolled copper foil as raw material. The material is placed on a crimping machine. A leveling roller is turned on. Surface roughness of the leveling roller is adjusted as required. A liquid nitrogen spray gun is turned on. The liquid nitrogen is used to reduce surface temperature of the smoothing roller to below minus 100 degree Celsius. Thickness of the smoothing roller is adjusted to the thickness of the rolled copper foil raw material. The rolled copper foil is passed through the smoothing roller. Liquid carbon dioxide spray gun is turned on behind the smoothing roller. Liquid carbon dioxide is used to treat a surface of the rolled copper foil that passes through the smoothing roller to increase the surface roughness of the rolled copper foil.
   USE - Method for enhancing rolling copper foil surface roughness for copper foil and lithium batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables enhancing the roughness of the rolled copper foil with too low surface roughness without changing the thickness of the rolled copper foil so as to realize increase in the interface bonding strength of the copper/lithium composite foil during rolling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic illustration of a method for enhancing rolling copper foil surface roughness.

PT P
PN WO2020100161-A1; IN201811042594-A
TI Recovering desalted organics from sugarcane molasses based alcohol distillery effluent used in cattle feed formulation as binder, involves adding lime into spent, separating carbon rich sludge as solid residue after filtration of slurry.
AU MAITI P
   PATEL B
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   CHEM PROCESS SYSTEMS PVT LTD (CHPR-Non-standard)
   CHEM PROCESS SYSTEMS (CHPR-Non-standard)
GA 2020440284
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering desalted organics (DSO) from sugarcane molasses based alcohol distillery effluent (spent wash, SW) involves adding lime into spent wash having pH 4.2 under stirring till pH is 12.5, separating carbon rich sludge as solid residue after filtration of the slurry, adding the carbon rich sludge in water under stirring to prepare a slurry, and adding carbon dioxide gas into the slurry to reduce the pH to 8.5. The slurry is filtered to separate organic rich liquid-1 and solid calcium carbonate, acid is added to the organic rich liquid- 1 as obtained till pH is 4 and separated organic rich liquid-2 while precipitating gypsum. The organic rich liquid-2 is evaporated till the solid concentration reaches 50-60% to obtain desalted organics suitable for cattle feed formulation, calcined precipitated calcium carbonate to produce calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, recycled calcium oxide and recycled carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for recovering desalted organics from sugarcane molasses based alcohol distillery effluent used in cattle feed formulation as binder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover desalted organics from sugarcane molasses based alcohol distillery effluent, which uses strong acid-cation exchange resin to reduce potassium level in molasses vinasses and regulates potassium level in cattle feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The lime used is selected from calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide. The acid used is selected from sulphuric acid or phosphoric acid. The potassium content in de-salted organics is less than 3% on dry matter basis. The raw material consumption is minimized through calcination of precipitated calcium carbonate and recycling the resultant calcium oxide and carbon dioxide. The desalted organics obtained are useful in the preparation of cattle feed formulation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Silicon-carbon composite electrode material useful for honeycomb-shaped lithium ion battery, comprises nano-silicon dioxide particle coated with flaky carbon, and is prepared by e.g. using magnesium thermal reduction silica method.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   LI L
   WANG Y
   YIN H
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2020336952
AB    NOVELTY - Silicon-carbon composite electrode material comprises nano-silicon dioxide particle coated with flaky carbon, and the electrode material is prepared by magnesium thermal reduction silica method, and then introducing carbon dioxide gas to cover nano-silicon during the magnesium thermal reduction process to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The material is useful for honeycomb-shaped lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: uses greenhouse gas carbon dioxide as raw material for flake-shaped carbon; is economical and environmentally friendly; achieves nano-silicon particles coated with flake carbon; has simple preparation process, which greatly shortens the preparation process flow and reduces the production cost; and is suitable for multiple raw materials of silica, and market promotion and application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the silicon-carbon composite electrode material, comprising (i) mixing silicon dioxide, magnesium oxide and magnesium powder uniformly to obtain first mixture, (ii) placing first mixture in a tube furnace, heating in first stage under an inert atmosphere, subsequently introducing carbon dioxide gas under an inert gas load to perform second-stage heating, and finally obtaining first product, and (iii) cooling first product, acid washing, and drying to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diameter of the silicon nanoparticles is 5-500 nm. In the step (i), the particle diameter of the silicon dioxide is 10 nm-500 mu m. The average particle size of the magnesium oxide is 10 nm-500 mu m. The average particle size of the magnesium powder is 1-500 mu m. The mass ratio of silicon dioxide, magnesium oxide and magnesium powder is 1:0-10:0.5-10. In the step (ii), the gas pressure is 0.5-10 MPa, the gas flow rate of the inert gas is 5-200 sccm, and gas flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 5-150 sccm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the heating temperature in the first stage is 400-1200 degrees C, and the heating temperature in the second stage is 400-1000 degrees C. In the step (iii), in the acid washing using acid solution having concentration of 0.01-2 mol/l, and drying at 60-150 degrees C for more than 1 hour.

PN CN110314408-A; CN110314408-B
TI Extraction of high-purity cannabidiol comprises extracting dried and pulverized industrial hemp flower leaves twice using ethanol, filtering, concentrating, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, separating, and refluxing.
AU ZHANG W
   JIN W
   LV Y
   HE C
AE KUNMING CUITI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 2019873900
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of high-purity cannabidiol comprises extracting dried and pulverized industrial hemp flower leaves twice using 4-8 times 92% ethanol each time, filtering, and concentrating to obtain extract having a density of 1.2-1.4 g/cm3; starting supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, slowly pumping extract into extraction tank, extracting at 35-85 degrees C, 20-40 MPa, and 10-25 L/h carbon dioxide flow rate, driving cannabidiol intermediate into primary separation tank at 6-12 MPa and 55-80 degrees C, and driving ethanol and water in the industrial hemp flower leaf extract in the secondary separation tank at 5 MPa and 30-50 degrees C; transporting cannabidiol intermediate to the tower kettle through a heat-insulating closed pipe, preheating to 220 degrees C, entering steam into super-gravity rotating bed at 0.1-10 Pa, refluxing for 30 minutes, changing reflux ratio from 2:1 to 5:1 when column top temperature reaches 130-150 degrees C, and collecting top fraction as high purity cannabidiol.
   USE - The method is for extracting high-purity cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient, reduces processing cost of the supercritical extraction, has low investment, short heating time, and high mass transfer efficiency, and avoids decomposition of cannabidiol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide used in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction section is food grade carbon dioxide with a purity of more than 99%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 85 degrees C, 40 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 15 L/h. The primary separation kettle is operated at 80 degrees c and 10 MPa.
