   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalyst comprising (i) mixing at                least one metal salts with organic ligand,                dissolving, dispersing in a solvent, and                hydrothermally treating at 80-200 degrees C for                1-48 hours, filtering the obtained mixture or                evaporating to dryness to obtain metal organic                skeleton compound A, and (ii) dispersing the above                metal organic skeleton compound in silicon source                and/or aluminum source solution, adding templating                agent, uniformly mixing, performing hydrothermal                treatment at 100-240 degrees C, filtering and                drying to obtain solid powder 2, calcining the                solid powder at 300-600 degrees C for 1-5 hours to                obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor of the                  metal oxide is metal organic skeleton compound A.                  In step (i), the metal salt is nitrate, chloride,                  sulfate, and acetate of zinc(II) ion,                  zirconium(II) ion, iron(III) ion, cobalt(II) ion,                  nickel(II) ion, copper(II) ion, chromium(III)                  ion, manganese(II) ion. In step (i), the solvent                  is water, methanol, ethanol, formic acid, acetic                  acid, acetone, and/o N,N-dimethylformamide, the                  organic ligand is nitrogen-containing                  heterocyclic compound and/or carboxylic acid                  compound. In step (ii), the silicon source                  solution is ethanol or an aqueous solution of                  silica sol, sodium silicate, and/or ethyl                  orthosilicate; the aluminum source solution is                  ethanol or an aqueous solution of aluminum sol,                  sodium meta aluminate, and/or aluminum nitrate.                  In step (ii), the templating agent is                  tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, diethylamine,                  polyethylene glycol, tetrapropylammonium bromide,                  triethylamine, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide,                  symmetric triblock copolymer comprising poly and                  poly in an alternating linear fashion (P123),                  F127 (RTM: Non-ionic detergent), piperidine,                  and/or hexamethyleneimine. Preferred Method: In                  step (ii), dispersing the obtained solid powder 2                  in aluminum source or silicon source solution,                  the templating agent is added, uniformly mixed,                  and then subjected to hydrothermal treatment at                  100-240 degrees C, and then filtered and dried to                  obtain solid powder 3, which is calcined at                  300-600 degrees C for 1-5 hours.

The copolymer does not show change                  in morphology before, after or during the                  pyrolysis and has reduction in size of up to 50%                  is after pyrolysis. AB    NOVELTY - The system has adsorption container (3) into                which a particulate carbon dioxide adsorbent has                flown. A processing target gas supply line (5)                supplies processing target gas containing carbon                dioxide to adsorption container. A processing gas                discharge line discharges processing target gas                from which carbon dioxide is adsorbed and removed                by contacting carbon dioxide adsorbent (10), from                adsorption container. A regeneration container (4)                is provided into which carbon dioxide adsorbent                that adsorbs carbon dioxide in adsorption container                is flowed. A superheated steam supply line (7)                supplies superheated steam to regeneration                container. A transfer device (9) transfers carbon                dioxide adsorbent from which carbon dioxide has                desorbed in the regeneration container to                adsorption container. The pressure of superheated                steam in regeneration container is predetermined                pressure equal to or lower than saturated steam                pressure at temperature of carbon dioxide adsorbent                that contacts superheated steam.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation and recovery system                for separating and recovering carbon dioxides in                process target gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation expense and operating cost in                carbon dioxide separation collection management                technique are reduced. The temperature rise of the                adsorbent due to the heat of condensation is                suppressed. The load fluctuation of the compression                pump is suppressed and stable operation is                performed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The switching device switches between a state                in which the processing target gas supply line and                the processing gas discharge line are connected to                the processing container and a state in which the                superheated steam supply line and the carbon                dioxide recovery line are connected to the                processing container. The carbon dioxide recovery                line has a condenser for condensing water in the                recovered gas. The carbon dioxide recovery line has                a compression pump that compresses the recovered                gas, and a carbon dioxide holder that stores the                compressed recovered gas. The compression pump                forcibly inhales the adsorption container or the                processing container to the predetermined pressure.                The temperature of the superheated steam is higher                than the temperature of the carbon dioxide                adsorbent in contact with the superheated steam. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon                dioxide separation and collection method involves                contacting a particulate carbon dioxide adsorbent                and a treatment target gas containing carbon                dioxide, and adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in                the treatment target gas to the carbon dioxide                adsorbent. The carbon dioxide adsorbent that has                adsorbed carbon dioxide is brought into contact                with superheated steam to regenerate the carbon                dioxide adsorbent by desorbing carbon dioxide from                the carbon dioxide adsorbent and recover the                desorbed carbon dioxide. The saturation temperature                of the superheated steam to be brought into contact                with the carbon dioxide adsorbent is equal to or                lower than the temperature of the carbon dioxide                adsorbent to be contacted with the superheated                steam. The regenerated carbon dioxide adsorbent is                used again for carbon dioxide adsorption without                going through a drying process.

TI Producing calcium oxide comprises e.g. placing            natural rock containing calcium carbonate into device,            adding block calcium oxide to crusher, sieving calcium            oxide powder through winnowing machine, and using            designated packaging machine.
AU LIU L
AE JIANGXI FUDELI CALCIUM CARBONATE IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202191789G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium oxide comprises (1) placing                natural rock containing calcium carbonate into                device and calcining at high temperature to                generate massive calcium oxide and carbon dioxide,                and discharging generated carbon dioxide to prevent                the carbon dioxide from reacting with calcium                oxide, (2) adding the block calcium oxide to                crusher, controlling crusher to crush the block                calcium oxide, and obtaining coarse calcium oxide                particles, (3) placing coarse particles of calcium                oxide in the crusher into pulse dust collector                using hoist, and falling coarse particles of                calcium oxide after dust removal into raw material                warehouse to be processed, (4) placing coarse                calcium oxide particles into raw material bin to be                processed and placing into Raymond mill by screw                feeding, and (5) sieving calcium oxide powder                through winnowing machine, and packing calcium                oxide powder in finished product raw material                warehouse by using designated packaging                machine.
   USE - The method is useful for producing calcium                oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: separates the ashes and calcium                oxide produced by firewood; discharges the flue gas                produced by the combustion of firewood; avoids                excessive internal pressure; discharges the carbon                dioxide generated by the reaction; avoids the                generated calcium oxide reacting with carbon                dioxide; completely converts into calcium carbonate                after long enough time; leads to the failure of                calcium oxide production; avoids unnecessary                economic losses; and increases its output.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing calcium oxide comprises (1) placing                the natural rock containing calcium carbonate into                the device and calcining at high temperature to                generate massive calcium oxide and carbon dioxide,                discharging the generated carbon dioxide to prevent                the carbon dioxide from reacting with calcium                oxide, and taking out the massive calcium oxide,                (2) adding the block calcium oxide prepared in the                (1) to the crusher, controlling the crusher to                crush the block calcium oxide, and obtaining coarse                calcium oxide particles after the crushing is                completed, (3) placing the coarse particles of                calcium oxide in the crusher in the step (2) into                the pulse dust collector using hoist, using the                pulse dust collector to remove the coarse particles                of calcium oxide, controlling the lower hopper of                the pulse dust collector to discharge the material                after the dust removal is completed, and falling                the coarse particles of calcium oxide after dust                removal into the raw material warehouse to be                processed, (4) placing the coarse calcium oxide                particles in the step (3) into the raw material bin                to be processed and placing into the Raymond mill                by screw feeding, controlling the Raymond mill to                finely grind the coarse particles of calcium oxide,                removing and collecting after processing so as to                obtain calcium oxide powder, and (5) sieving                calcium oxide powder obtained in the step (4)                through winnowing machine, where the calcium oxide                powder that does not meet certain particle size is                separated from it, placing into the Raymond mill                for re-grinding, conveying the calcium oxide powder                that meets a certain particle size to the finished                raw material bin through the conveyor, and packing                the calcium oxide powder in the finished product                raw material warehouse by using designated                packaging machine. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for calcium oxide preparation                device.

PN CN111471817-A
TI Gas quenching for improving the stability of steel            slag and recycling the physical heat of molten steel            slag, comprises e.g. conveying molten steel slag from            converter steel making system slag tank to liquid steel            slag ladle.
AU JIN Y
   LI H
   WANG Q
   WANG X
   LI L
AE HEBEI IRON & STEEL CO LTD (HBIS-C)
GA 202074509Y
AB    NOVELTY - Gas quenching comprises e.g. (i) conveying the                molten steel slag from the converter steel making                system slag tank to the liquid steel slag ladle,                and (ii) the molten steel slag in the liquid steel                slag ladle flows to the gas quenching chamber                through the steel slag chute, introducing carbon                dioxide from the high pressure storage tank to the                gas quenching chamber through the pipeline for gas                quenching, the quenching pressure is 0.25-0.65 MPa,                the gas-liquid mass ratio is 1:6-1:12, rapidly                cooling the molten steel slag, quenching the molten                steel slag into particles not less than 3 mm, the                molten steel slag exchanges heat with                low-temperature carbon dioxide, and the free                calcium oxide, magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide                in the steel slag generate stable water-insoluble                calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for gas quenching for                improving the stability of steel slag and recycling                the physical heat of molten steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method comprehensively solves the problem                of free active material in steel slag for reuse,                physical heat recovery and utilization of steel                slag, using problem of partially collecting the                carbon dioxide captured in coal gas, and realizes                safe, efficient, and environmentally friendly steel                slag quenching, sorting and recycling                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas quenching comprises (i) conveying the                molten steel slag from the converter steel making                system slag tank to the liquid steel slag ladle,                (ii) the molten steel slag in the liquid steel slag                ladle flows to the gas quenching chamber through                the steel slag chute, introducing carbon dioxide                from the high pressure storage tank to the gas                quenching chamber through the pipeline for gas                quenching, the quenching pressure is 0.25-0.65 MPa,                the gas-liquid mass ratio is 1:6-1:12, rapidly                cooling the molten steel slag, quenching the molten                steel slag into particles not less than 3 mm, the                molten steel slag exchanges heat with                low-temperature carbon dioxide, and the free                calcium oxide, magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide                in the steel slag generate stable water-insoluble                calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, (iii)                discharging the gas-solid mixture doped with                metallic iron and fine slag particles in the gas                quenching chamber from the upper part of the gas                quenching chamber through pneumatic action,                introducing the waste heat recovery device through                the settling device, realizing the recovery and                utilization of the physical heat of the molten                steel slag, removing and purifying the dust by the                dust remover, and recycling cooled carbon dioxide,                and (iv) screening the iron-containing steel slag                in the gas quenching chamber and precipitation                device by the sieving device, introducing coarse                particles less than or equal to 3 mm into                mechanical crushing or fine grinding device to be                broken into particles not less than 3 mm, the                particles is not less than 3 mm, the powder                recovered by the waste heat recovery device and                powder collected by the dust collector enters the                fine-particle steel slag collection device                together, the fine particle powder and powder of                the mechanical crushing device passes through the                magnetic separator, and separating into qualified                metal iron or scrap steel and qualifying steel                tailings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  quenching the molten steel slag into not greater                  than 85% fine particles of not greater than 3 mm.                  In the step (i), the temperature of the molten                  steel slag gas quenching in the liquid steel slag                  ladle is not greater than 20 degrees C higher                  than the melting point temperature of the steel                  slag.

PN CN108947983-A; CN108947983-B
TI Preparing covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar comprises reacting intermediate with 2,4,6-trihydroxy-1,3,5-benzenetrialdehyde monomer in a solvothermal reaction.
AU YAO B
   DING L
   LIAO M
   JIN W
   SHI S
   CHEN G
   ZHANG X
   HOU S
AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 2018A1447T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar comprises reacting intermediate with 2,4,6-trihydroxy-1,3,5-benzenetrialdehyde monomer in a solvothermal reaction to obtain final product, where intermediate is 1-allyl-3-(2,5-di(hydrazinecarbonyl)benzyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium compounds (I).
   USE - The crystal material is useful for carbon dioxide selective gas separation or catalytic carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar comprises reacting intermediate with 2,4,6-trihydroxy-1,3,5-benzenetrialdehyde monomer in a solvothermal reaction to obtain final product, where intermediate is 1-allyl-3-(2,5-di(hydrazinecarbonyl)benzyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium compounds of formula (I).X = halo.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar obtained by above method;
   (2) deviceizing a covalent organic frameworks crystal material based on covalent bond driving comprising dissolving chitosan in a solvent, adding acid, stirring until a clear solution is formed, adding covalent-organic framework crystal material containing the ionic liquid structural element into the chitosan transparent solution, illuminating purple light, stirring, ultrasonically oscillating, transferring the composite solution into the mold, allowing to stand for few hours to obtain a hydrogel, transferring the obtained hydrogel into a cooler to obtain ice crystals, freezing the frozen sample in a freeze dryer, further transferring the composite solution into the mold, where ratio between chitosan, solvent, acid and covalent-organic framework crystal material containing the ionic liquid structural element is 0.1-0.3 g:15-25 ml:100-120 mu l:0.1-0.3 g, solvent is water, acid is acetic acid, ultraviolet light is 365 nm, power of 100-120 W, irradiation time is 1-3 hours, refluxing time is 5-20 hours, temperature is room temperature, cooler temperature is -15 degrees C to -10 degrees C, cooling time of the cooler is 10-36 hours, freeze dryer temperature is -50 degrees C to -40 degrees C, and freeze drying time is 24-48 hours;
   (3) use method of covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar in catalytic carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction for carbon dioxide selective gas separation comprising adding epoxy compound as a substrate and covalent-organic framework crystal material containing the ionic liquid structural unit as a catalyst, passing carbon dioxide gas under normal pressure, heating at 25-50 degrees C, reacting, where epoxy compound is epichlorohydrin, amount of covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar is 1-1.5% by mole of the substrate, and pressure of carbon dioxide is normal pressure, filling the composite aerogel material in a fixed bed reaction column, passing carbon dioxide gas and epoxy compounds, where amount of aerogel is 500-600 mg, epoxy compound is butylene oxide, carbon dioxide rate is 40-100 ml/minute, rate of the epoxy compound is 0.5-1.5 ml/minute, and reaction temperature is 60-80 degrees C; and
   (4) basic structural unit of a covalent-organic framework crystal material containing ionic liquid structure basic dollar is (Z)-2,4,6-tris(aminomethylene)cyclohexane-1,3,5-trione compounds of formula (II).
   G = (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between intermediate L and 2,4,6-trihydroxy-1,3,5-benzenetrialdehyde monomer is 0.5-0.8:1; reaction is carried out under a vacuum atmosphere, heating temperature is 110-130 degrees C, heating time is 70-80 hours; solvent is mesitylene, 1,4 dioxane, acetic acid and/or deionized water; and purification process includes refluxing, filtering liquid, washing and activating the material with a solvent, where solvent is acetone, tetrahydrofuran or ethanol. Preferred Method: The preparation method of intermediate comprises adding methanol and concentrated sulfuric acid into 2-Methyl-1,4-benzenedicarboxylic acid, using concentrated sulfuric acid as a catalyst, heating, refluxing, adjusting pH to neutral, filtering and washing to obtain intermediate A, reacting intermediate A and N-halosuccinimide, halogenating methyl group directly attached to an aromatic ring in the intermediate A, adding initiator, heating and purifying to obtain intermediate B, where initiator is an organic peroxide initiator or an azo initiator; molar ratio between intermediate A, N-halosuccinimide and initiator is 1:1-1.5:0.1-0.2; purifying intermediate B, cooling the refluxed liquid, distilling off solvent under reduced pressure to obtain a crude product, subjecting crude product to column chromatography to obtain an intermediate B, where reaction temperature is 70-90 degrees C, time is 4-6 hours, solvent is acetonitrile; purification process for intermediate C includes distilling off liquid after refluxing under reduced pressure, removing solvent, recrystallizing to obtain intermediate C, where molar ratio between intermediate B and allyl imidazole is 1:1.2-2; further reacting intermediate C and hydrazine hydrate in a solvent, where reaction temperature is 50-70 degrees C, reaction time is 14-16 hours, and solvent is methanol; and purification process of the dihydrazide monomer includes distilling off the liquid after refluxing under reduced pressure, removing solvent and filtering to obtain dihydrazide monomer, where precipitating agent is diethyl ether; intermediate A is dimethyl 2-methylterephthalate; intermediate B is dimethyl 2-methylterephthalate compounds of formula (III), and intermediate C is 1-allyl-3-(2,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)benzyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium compounds of formula (IV).X = halo.

PN WO2020237564-A1; CN113891850-A; IN202117060945-A
TI Separating first and second gaseous streams each containing one acid gas, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to remove carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves using first gaseous stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and one acid gas.
AU HERNANDEZ A
   GUAN W
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   HERNANDEZ A (HERN-Individual)
   GUAN W (GUAN-Individual)
   GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
GA 2020C0226K
AB    NOVELTY - Separating first and second gaseous streams each containing one acid gas, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to remove carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves using first gaseous stream (5) containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and one acid gas is purified in a first acid gas removal absorber (AGR1A) to remove one acid gas, purified in a first temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA1) to remove carbon dioxide and then separated at a cryogenic temperature in a separation unit (CB) to produce a stream of fluid enriched in carbon monoxide (33), a stream of fluid enriched in hydrogen (11) and a stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and possibly methane. The second gaseous stream (7) containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and one acid gas is sent to a shift reaction unit (14) in which carbon monoxide and water vapor in the second stream are converted to carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   USE - Method for separating first and second gaseous streams each containing one acid gas, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to remove carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate first and second gaseous streams each containing one acid gas, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to remove carbon monoxide and hydrogen which is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating first and second gaseous streams each containing one acid gas, carbon monoxide and hydrogen to remove carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves using first gaseous stream (5) containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and one acid gas is purified in a first acid gas removal absorber (AGR1A) to remove one acid gas, purified in a first temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA1) to remove carbon dioxide and then separated at a cryogenic temperature in a separation unit (CB) to produce a stream of fluid enriched in carbon monoxide (33), a stream of fluid enriched in hydrogen (11) and a stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and possibly methane. The second gaseous stream (7) containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and one acid gas is sent to a shift reaction unit (14) in which carbon monoxide and water vapor in the second stream are converted to carbon dioxide and hydrogen, the shifted second stream is purified in a second acid gas removal absorber (AGR2A) to remove carbon dioxide and the purified second stream is sent as a feed stream to a pressure swing adsorption unit (PSA) to produce a hydrogen enriched stream (21) richer in hydrogen that the feed stream and an offgas (23) less rich in hydrogen than the feed stream, the offgas containing carbon dioxide and methanol. The offgas and/or the stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and possibly methane is/are purified to remove carbon dioxide and methanol in a unit (TSA2) other than the first acid gas removal absorber and the gas produced with a reduced carbon dioxide and methanol content is then sent to the inlet of the separation unit (CB).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows block diagram form.
   First gaseous stream (5)
   Second gaseous stream (7)
   Stream of fluid enriched in hydrogen (11)
   Shift reaction unit (14)
   Carbon monoxide (33)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and possibly methane is/are purified to remove carbon dioxide and methanol in a second temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA2), preferably only in the second temperature swing adsorption unit. The offgas and/or the stream containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and possibly methane is/are compressed upstream of the second temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA2). The offgas is mixed with the stream of fluid containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide (31, FG) from the separation unit (CB) and sent to the second temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA2). The stream of fluid containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen is compressed upstream of the second temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA2). The stream of fluid containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen and the offgas are compressed in the same compressor. The offgas is compressed in a first stage of the compressor and the stream of fluid containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen and the offgas are compressed in one subsequent stage of the contains methane. The gas purified from carbon dioxide and methanol in the second temperature swing adsorption unit (TSA2) is sent to the separation unit (CB) without any further step to remove carbon dioxide or methanol.

TI Producing liquid carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-rich gas used e.g. in field of petroleum, comprises boosting carbon dioxide-rich mixed feed gas by compression process, purifying to produce liquid carbon dioxide products, and concentrating non-condensable gas of liquefaction process.
AU FENG L
   YU Q
   ZHANG H
   WANG J
   TAO Y
   ZHANG J
   BU L
   LIU X
   CHEN J
AE SOUTHWEST RES & DESIGIN CHEM IND INST (CNCC-C)
GA 202241460E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing liquid carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-rich gas, comprises boosting the carbon dioxide-rich mixed feed gas by the compression process, allowing the boosted raw material gas to enter the purification process for purification, allowing the purified raw gas to enter the liquefaction process to be liquefied and purifying to produce liquid carbon dioxide products, concentrating the non-condensable gas of the liquefaction process to the level of carbon dioxide content in the carbon dioxide-rich raw material gas through the carbon dioxide concentration process, and allowing the concentrated carbon dioxide gas in the concentration process to enter the compression process to increase the pressure and continue to circulate. The method utilizes water and methanol.
   USE - Producing liquid carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-rich gas used in fields of petroleum, chemical engineering, metallurgy, electronics, food, medical treatment and fire fighting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare liquid dioxide by carbon dioxide-rich gas, which has high recovery rate of carbon dioxide, and reduces energy consumption of unit carbon dioxide and investment.

PN US2019091624-A1; US10434457-B2
TI Capturing carbon dioxide comprises contacting carbon dioxide having stream with carbon dioxide adsorbent having adsorbent matrix of zeolite and/or metal organic framework and carbon nanotubes, to adsorb portion of carbon dioxide from stream.
AU MANSOUR R B
   QASEM N
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201927844N
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide, comprises contacting a carbon dioxide-containing stream with a carbon dioxide adsorbent to adsorb at least a portion of carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-containing stream, where the carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises an adsorbent matrix comprising a zeolite and/or a metal organic framework, and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed within the adsorbent matrix, and a weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in the carbon dioxide adsorbent is 0.01-5 wt %, relative to the total weight of the carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the carbon dioxide adsorbent that exhibits carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 0.5-20 mmol carbon dioxide per one gram of the carbon dioxide adsorbent, at a temperature of 10-40 degrees C and thermal conductivity of up to 500% higher than a thermal conductivity of a pristine adsorbent that comprises MIL-100(iron) and does not include carbon nanotubes (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising an adsorbent matrix comprising MIL-100(iron), and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed within the adsorbent matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises degassing the carbon dioxide adsorbent in a sub-atmospheric pressure prior to the contacting. The carbon dioxide adsorbent is degassed in a temperature of no more than 400 degrees C, for no more than 24 hours. The carbon dioxide-containing stream is contacted with the carbon dioxide adsorbent at -20 to 100 degrees C. The carbon dioxide-containing stream is contacted with the carbon dioxide adsorbent at a pressure of 0.5-10 bars. Preferred Components: The weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in the carbon dioxide adsorbent is 0.05-1.5 wt.%, relative to the total weight of the carbon dioxide adsorbent. The adsorbent matrix comprises a zeolite and a metal organic framework. The adsorbent matrix comprises the metal organic framework which comprises magnesium-MOF-74 and MIL-100(iron). The adsorbent matrix comprises the zeolite which is zeolite 13X. The carbon nanotubes are multi-walled carbon nanotubes (preferred) or single-walled carbon nanotubes. The carbon dioxide-containing stream contains carbon dioxide and at least one gaseous substance comprises nitrogen, oxygen, argon, helium, water vapor, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, and ethane, where a volumetric ratio of carbon dioxide to the gaseous substance is 1:50-5:1. The carbon nanotubes are dispersed in the adsorbent matrix without forming chemical bonds with the adsorbent matrix. The adsorbent matrix further comprises zeolite 13X and/or magnesium-MOF-74. The carbon nanotubes are homogenously dispersed in the adsorbent matrix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 2021B35973
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining water by injecting into water a mixture of gasses including carbon dioxide and oxygen. The different mixture is implemented depending on the species of fish transported, a venturi is used making it possible to suck in ambient air in order to adapt the carbon dioxide content in the overall mixture injected. The low pressure oxygen concentrator generator is used, the oxygenated gas thus produced being mixed with carbon dioxide, a mixture which is then sucked in by a venturi mounted on a water pump, whether on the suction or on the discharge side, the water or gas mixture then traversing a sufficiently long circuit to achieve a sufficient contact time for the gas or dissolved in the liquid.

PN CN112694977-A
TI Culturing chlorella comprises e.g. sterilizing            culture container by high temperature sterilizing            method or chemical medicine sterilizing method, using            water to clean culture container, preparing culture            solution, inoculating and culturing.
AU CAO L
AE CAO L (CAOL-Individual)
GA 2021446206
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing chlorella comprises (i) sterilizing                the culture container by high temperature                sterilizing method or chemical medicine sterilizing                method, then using water to clean the culture                container, (ii) preparing culture solution, (iii)                inoculating according to the proportion of the                algae liquid and the culture liquid 1:3-4, (iv)                culturing, phase I where, the illumination                intensity is 3000-4500 Lx, the water temperature is                25-30 degrees C, the morning 9 to the afternoon 2,                supplementing carbon dioxide, each liter of culture                liquid supplying carbon dioxide 2-3 mg, culturing                for 2-4 days, Phase II by the illumination                intensity is 4000-5500Lx, the water temperature is                0-5 degrees C, between 9 am and 2 pm, supplementing                carbon dioxide, each liter of culture liquid                supplying carbon dioxide 4-6 mg, culturing for 5-10                days, and (v) harvesting when the content of                chlorella in the culture solution is not less than                200 per liter.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing                chlorella.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables chlorella to grow at low                temperature, and has a simple cultivation method,                which can reduce the mortality of chlorella and                avoid the production of other weeds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), phase II                  by the illumination intensity is 2000-4500Lx, the                  water temperature is 5-10 degrees C, between 9 am                  and 2 pm, supplementing carbon dioxide, each                  liter of culture liquid supplying carbon dioxide                  3-5 mg, culturing for 1-2 days. In the step (i),                  the high-temperature disinfection method                  comprises boiling disinfection and oven drying                  disinfection. The chemical disinfection method is                  to use an aqueous solution of bleaching powder or                  an aqueous potassium permanganate solution or an                  aqueous quicklime solution to soak the culture                  container for disinfection. In the small-scale                  cultivation, the cultivation container adopts a                  barrel or a cylinder, and in the large-scale                  cultivation, the cultivation container adopts a                  cement pool or a transparent tank. The soaking                  time of the bleaching powder aqueous solution or                  quicklime aqueous solution in the culture vessel                  is not less than 0.5 hours. The immersion time of                  the potassium permanganate aqueous solution in                  the culture vessel is not less than 5 minutes. In                  the step (ii), the preparation method of the                  culture solution comprises using component A                  comprises 45-50 mg of iron trichloride, 1.8-2.0                  mg of manganese chloride, 200-210 mg of 45% boron                  oxide, 1-1.25 g of potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 200-210 mg of tetrasodium edetate ,                  300-310 mg of sodium carbonate or 100-110 mg of                  sodium EDTA. The component B comprises 0.5-0.6 g                  zinc chloride, 0.5-0.6 g cobalt chloride,                  0.2-0.25 g ammonium molybdate, 0.5-0.6 g copper                  sulfate, adding component A and component B into                  1 ton of water, dissolving, adding 2.0-2.5 g each                  of vitamin B1 and B12, stirring evenly, and                  prepare a culture solution. The culture solution                  also includes component C, 2-5 g chlorella                  powder, adding component A, component B, and                  component C into 1 ton of water, mixing                  thoroughly, and adding 2-2.5 g of vitamin B1 and                  B12, stirring evenly, sonicating, and filtering                  to obtain the culture solution. The preparation                  method of the chlorella powder is drying the                  chlorella at a temperature of 50-60 degrees C,                  the moisture content of the dried chlorella is                  less than 5%, and then pulverizing into a powder                  with 200-300 meshes and/or, the ultrasonic                  condition is 50-60 degrees C for 6-8 hours.                  Preferred Components: The bleaching powder                  contains 25-35% of available chlorine, and the                  concentration of the bleaching powder aqueous                  solution is 200-300 mg/l. The concentration of                  the potassium permanganate aqueous solution is                  5-6%, and the concentration of the quicklime                  aqueous solution is 2-3%.

PN RU2727557-C1
TI Manufacturing method of functional element of semiconductor device which is oxide or carbon dioxide or mixture of carbon dioxide or dioxide with inert gas.
AU KUKUSHKIN S A
   OSIPOV A V
   FEOKTISTOV N A
AE NEW TECHNOLOGIES SCI TECH CENT CO LTD (NEWT-Non-standard)
GA 202072396N
AB    NOVELTY - Method of manufacturing the functional element of the semiconductor device is carried out in a vacuum furnace in two stages, the first of which is carried out heating to a temperature of bases 950-1,400 degrees C and synthesis of a silicon carbide film on its surface in a gaseous medium, which is an oxide or carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide or dioxide with an inert gas and / or nitrogen (and / or silane) at pressure of 20-600 Pa. At second step in vacuum furnace in medium of carbon tetrafluoride at temperature of 1,300=1,400 degrees C and pressure of 2,500-6,000 Pa carbon nanofilm with a crystal lattice of diamond type is formed from the obtained film of silicon carbide by coordinated replacement of atoms up to complete replacement of silicon atoms by carbon atoms in silicon carbide film obtained at the first stage. Best result is achieved when orientation (111) silicon is used as the base.
   USE - Manufacturing technology.
   ADVANTAGE - Obtaining, for the first time, a silicon-based carbon nanocrystalline material with a high-quality diamond type crystal structure by storing a diamond-like type of a crystal lattice in SiSiCC conversion.

PN CN110871062-A; CN110871062-B
TI Composite nanoparticle catalyst used for preparing            carbon dioxide absorption liquid, comprises nano metal            particles comprising nano silver, nano copper or nano            aluminum and partial acid salt comprising metatitanic            acid or metaferric acid.
AU WU Y
   LIU W
   ZHANG Q
   LIU Y
   GAO Y
   LIU D
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2020216002
AB    NOVELTY - Composite nanoparticle catalyst comprises                80-90 %mass nano metal particles comprising nano                silver, nano copper or nano aluminum and 10-20                %mass partial acid salt comprising metatitanic acid                or metaferric acid, where the catalyst does not                comprises nano aluminum and metaferric acid.
   USE - The composite nanoparticle catalyst is useful                for preparing carbon dioxide absorption                liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has stable structure, high                carbonation conversion rate, less corrosion on                equipment and high cycle utilization rate, and does                not deactivate easily. The absorption liquid                reduces the adsorption/desorption temperature of                the alkali metal carbon dioxide absorbent in the                power plant, and is suitable for low-quality heat                sources in coal-fired power plants and                environmentally-friendly, and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst,                involving (a) dripping water to an aqueous solution                of the nano metal particles and n-tetrabutyl                titanate in ethanol, and stirring the mixture, and                (b) hydrolyzing at room temperature, and drying to                obtain nano metal particle/metatitanic composite                nano particle catalyst; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide absorbing liquid                comprising the above-mentioned composite                nanoparticle catalyst, where the absorption liquid                is an alkali metal-based absorption liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The partial acid salt                  is coated on the surface of the nano metal                  particles. The absorbing liquid comprises                  potassium carbonate solution or sodium carbonate                  solution. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio                  of water to n-tetrabutyl titanate is 4:1. The                  absorbing liquid comprises 0.05-0.10 wt.%,                  preferably 0.07-0.10 wt.% composite nanoparticle                  catalyst. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  stirring the mixture for 2-5 hours. The step (b)                  involves drying at 100-150 degrees C for 10-15                  hours. The method involves (i) adding the nano                  metal particles to 0.1 mol/l ferric chloride                  solution, dripping sodium hydroxide to adjust pH                  to 7, and stirring the mixture at room                  temperature for 10-15 hours to obtain a solid                  particles, and (ii) washing the solid particles                  with water to remove impurities, and drying at                  50-80 degrees C for 20-25 hours to obtain the                  catalyst.

PT P
PN CN110791596-A; CN110791596-B
TI Waste biomass regeneration and utilization by            pretreating waste biomass with aqueous alkaline            electrolyte and high-pressure carbon dioxide and            treating with ionic liquid, another aqueous alkaline            electrolyte and high-pressure carbon dioxide.
AU JIANG Y
   CHEN K
   XUE Y
   XIAO W
   YANG Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020154026
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration and utilization of waste biomass                using aqueous alkaline electrolyte comprises adding                waste biomass and first aqueous alkaline                electrolyte to reactor, injecting high-pressure                carbon dioxide (CO2) into the reactor, maintaining                at 60-80 degrees C and 12-25 MPa for 30-90 minutes,                releasing pressure, adding ionic liquid and second                aqueous alkaline electrolyte, injecting                high-pressure CO2 into the reactor, maintaining at                85-125 degrees C and 10-20 MPa for 60-90 minutes,                releasing pressure, subjecting reaction material to                solid-liquid separation, recycling liquid, and                drying solid. The first aqueous alkaline                electrolyte is prepared by diluting alkaline stock                solution 10-20 times. The second aqueous alkaline                electrolyte is prepared by diluting alkaline stock                solution 40-50 times. The alkaline stock solution                is prepared by electrolysis using potassium                carbonate or sodium carbonate as electrolyte, and                has oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) of - 800 mV                to - 1300 mV and pH of 12-13.50.
   USE - The method is for regeneration and utilization                of waste biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of aqueous alkaline electrolyte and                supercritical carbon dioxide to preprocess biomass                effectively destroys biomass structure and provides                convenient conditions for subsequent hydrolysis                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline stock                  solution is prepared by electrolysis at 8-12 A                  using potassium carbonate solution having                  concentration of 10-25 wt.% and conductivity of                  12000-50000 mS/m, or sodium carbonate solution                  having concentration of 25-45 wt.% and                  conductivity of 5000-10000 mS/m. The weight ratio                  of waste biomass and first aqueous alkaline                  electrolyte is 1:12-25. The weight ratio of waste                  biomass and second aqueous alkaline electrolyte                  is 1:8-10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of                  waste biomass and ionic liquid is 1:5-8. The                  ionic liquid is 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole                  acetate, 1-allyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride                  salt, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride, or                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride. The waste                  biomass comprises straw, forest product                  processing residue, or garden greening waste. The                  straw is rice straw, corn straw, wheat straw,                  Sorghum stalk, or bamboo stems. Preferred Method:                  The method further includes, prior to                  solid-liquid separation, adding 20-35 wt.%                  hydrogen peroxide solution to reaction material                  at material-solution weight ratio of 1:2-3,                  stirring and mixing, putting the mixture in                  vacuum packaging bag, controlling vacuum degree                  at 0.1 MPa, and subjecting to                  high-static-pressure processing by pressurizing                  to 400-650 MPa at 8-10 MPa/s and maintaining                  pressure for 15-25 minutes under normal                  temperature.

PT P
PN EP3540435-A1; EP3540435-B1
TI Method for determining concentration of acidic center of solid e.g. silica, involves applying test solid with amine reacted with carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and determining organic carbon value of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU NADOLNY F
   PEITZ S
   STOCHNIOL G
   FRANKE R
   QUANDT T
   FABIAN N
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201980149E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for determining concentration of acidic center of a solid involves applying a test solid having decomposition temperature of 400 degrees C or more with an amine compound, heating dried amine-treated solid at more than 1000 degrees C, preferably more than 1200 degrees C, quantitatively determining total organic carbon value by infrared spectroscopy of resulting carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, determining mass of the adsorbed amine compound based on the carbon value, and determining concentration (in mmol/g) of acidic centers from the mass of the amine compound, assuming that amine interacts with acidic center. The amine compound is capable of interacting with the acidic centers of the solid to form an amine-loaded solid in which an amine compound is adsorbed to the acidic sites. The amine charged on the solid is reacted with carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for determining concentration of acidic center of solid e.g. aluminosilicate, alumina, silica, inorganic acid treated-silica and silicon carbide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, rapid and economical, and is capable of easily evaluating test results for calculating the concentration of acidic centers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The application of solid is preferably carried out by producing a dispersion of the solid to be tested, amine compound which is liquid at room temperature and optionally an organic solvent to obtain amine-charged solid in which amine compound is adsorbed to the acidic centers, filtering off and drying the amine-charged solid at 40-400 degrees C for 1-24 hours, preferably 2-18 hours to remove any solvent present and the excess amine compound, and washing the amine-charged solid with organic solvent to remove excess amine. The determination of the carbon value is carried out using a carbon and sulfur analyzer. Preferred Components: The amine compound is liquid or gaseous at room temperature. The amine compound is primary amine, preferably methylamine, ethylamine, n-propylamine or isopropylamine. The organic solvent is a non-polar organic solvent, preferably chosen from 5-12C alkane, 5-12C cycloalkane and 6-12C heteroatom-free aromatic hydrocarbon.

PN CN110006986-A
TI Analysis of carbon and oxygen isotopes of mixed lithologic carbonate rock samples involves adding powder sample and phosphoric acid in reactor and connecting to sample preparation system, reacting, and collecting carbon dioxide.
AU LIU J
   ZHAO X
   CHEN X
AE NANJING INST GEOLOGY & PALAEONTOLOGY CHI (CISS-C)
GA 201963137B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for analyzing carbon and oxygen isotopes of mixed lithologic carbonate rock samples involves adding 60-250 mg powder sample and 2.3-2.7 mL 100% phosphoric acid in the reactor reaction tube, connecting the reactor to the external sample preparation system, vacuuming and heating for 30 minutes, placing the sample reactor with 100% phosphoric acid at 25 degrees C for 10 minutes and then placing in an ice water mixture, terminating the reaction, placing the reactor in a cold liquid through a external sample preparation system, collecting carbon dioxide using vacuum transfer system, again reacting the mixture at 25 degrees C for 120 minutes, placing the mixture in ice water mixture and then in cold liquid, collecting carbon dioxide and transferring, reacting again at 50 degrees C in water bath and completing the reaction, collecting carbon dioxide, reusing the isotope mass spectrometer dual injection system to complete carbon and oxygen isotope determination, and studying the test data of gas collection.
   USE - Method for analyzing carbon and oxygen isotopes of mixed lithologic carbonate rock samples.
   ADVANTAGE - The process verifies difference in analytical values produced by sample analysis using carbonate device Kiel IV.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of the powder sample to the phosphoric acid is 1:3.

PT P
PN CN109907253-A
TI Preparing a low temperature puffed dried sea cucumber involves cleaning fresh sea cucumber, placing it at the bottom of the puffing kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and performing a pressurized cycle.
AU HU Y
   FENG T
AE TAERPU BEIJING PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TAER-Non-standard)
GA 201957389V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a low temperature puffed dried sea cucumber involves cleaning fresh sea cucumber, cutting it along abdomen, removing internal organs, and retaining peritoneal tendon of sea cucumber. The peritoneal tendon is cut with a knife, placed in boiling water, blanched for 1-10 minutes, and drained. The sea cucumber is placed at bottom of puffing kettle, supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is introduced into the puffing kettle, and a pressurized cycle is performed. When pressure in puffing kettle reaches 10-40 mega Pascal (MPa), passage of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is stopped. The puffing kettle is heated to puff sea cucumber at 20-50 degrees C. The puffing is stopped when upper and lower pressure difference of puffing kettle is greater than preset pressure. The pressure of puffing kettle is released to 0 MPa. The puffing kettle is pressurized to 5-20 MPa, and water in sea cucumber is taken out from puffing kettle by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   USE - Method for preparing low temperature puffed dried sea cucumber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves low temperature processing, does not require additive addition, and completely preserves the nutrition of sea cucumber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The preparation method further comprises adjusting the pressure of puffing kettle and separation vessel, pressurizing and circulating the puffing kettle, and adjusting the temperature of the puffing kettle, so that the sea cucumber is dried. The supercritical carbon dioxide is prepared by pressurizing liquid carbon dioxide once by the high pressure pump, and changing the pressure of carbon dioxide from 2-3 MPa to 8-10 MPa. Preferred Conditions: The preset pressure is 1-5 MPa. The pressure of the puffing kettle is adjusted to 15-20 MPa at 35-40 degrees C for 15-25 minutes. The pressure in the separation vessel is adjusted to 5.5-6.5 MPa.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

AU CHI J H
   HONG J P
   JOO Y J
   SEO D K
   CHI J
   PYO H J
   SUH D
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
   KOREA WESTERN POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
GA 201937156H
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1000) has an aqueous gas conversion effector (100) for transferring synthetic gas and vapor including carbon monoxide and producing first gas mixture including carbon dioxide and hydrogen. A pressure swing adsorption top unit (120) transfers second gas mixture and produces third gas mixture. A molten carbonate fuel cell (400) flows methane into a cathode and oxide pole.
   USE - Fuel cell power generating device for an electric vehicle or an electric hybrid car.

   (2) carbon dioxide hydrogenation synthesis comprising charging catalyst into continuous fixed bed reactor at 240-280 degrees C, pre-reducing for 4-6 hours, introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and reacting at 240-280 degrees C and 3.0-5.0 MPa with space velocity of 9000-12000 mL/g hour with inert atmosphere.

A microcontroller is connected to ultrasonic transmitter and receiver (3) and performs operations to convert speed of sound through exhaled air to concentration of carbon dioxide. Acoustic virial equations are used to predict concentration of carbon dioxide from measured speed of sound.
   USE - Portable non-invasive sensor system to display capnograph.

PN WO2022119707-A1
TI Preparing carboxylic acids by passing feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and carbon-containing gas, converting feed stream into stream comprising 2-4C hydrocarbons in presence of catalyst comprising metal oxide catalyst and microporous catalyst and converting stream into 2-4C carboxylic acids.
AU DEWILDE J F
   LIMBACH K W
   CHAKRABARTI R
AE ROHM & HAAS CO (ROHM-C)
GA 2022780448
AB    NOVELTY - A process comprises introducing a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing gas comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide into a reaction zone of a first reactor, converting the feed stream into an intermediate stream comprising 2-4C hydrocarbons in the reaction zone in the presence of a first catalyst, where the intermediate stream further comprises carbon dioxide and the first catalyst is a composite catalyst comprising a metal oxide catalyst component and a microporous catalyst component and converting the intermediate stream into a product stream comprising 2-4 C carboxylic acids in the presence of a second catalyst in a second reactor.
   USE - The process is used for preparing 2-4C carboxylic acids from synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process efficiently convert various carbon-containing streams to carboxylic acids via hydrocarbons with high yield, by eliminating the need for breaking any azeotropes formed with carbon dioxide, achieves significant savings in capital and operating costs in addition more complete carbon utilization that can be achieved when the carbon dioxide is recycled back to the syngas feed stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide catalyst component comprises gallium oxide, zinc oxide, chromium oxide and/or zirconium oxide, preferably gallium oxide and phase pure zirconium oxide. The microporous catalyst component is a molecular sieve having 8-MR pore openings or a molecular sieve having a chabazite (CHA) framework, preferably SAPO-34 or SSZ-13.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The intermediate stream comprising 2-4C hydrocarbons comprises 2-4C olefins, 2-4C paraffins, carbon dioxide and/or unreacted carbon monoxide and hydrogen. Preferred Process: The second reactor converts the 2-4C olefins in the intermediate stream to 2-4C carboxylic acids. The process further comprises separating the carboxylic acids and 2-4C paraffins from the product stream and recycling carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to the feed stream. The process further comprising separating the 2-4C carboxylic acids from the product stream and recycling carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and unreacted 2-4C paraffins to the feed stream. The unreacted hydrogen is removed from the intermediate stream prior to converting the intermediate stream into the product stream.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113609621-A
TI Method for adjusting blade consistency for optimizing carbon dioxide axial flow compressor pipe network matching performance in supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation technology field, involves determining whether efficiency of blade cascade does not change significantly.
AU LI C
   LI X
   ZHANG X
   JIANG S
   ZHANG Y
   YAO M
   LI H
   LI K
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C99593
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating a compressor inlet and outlet isentropic enthalpy increase according to optimized compressor outlet pressure. Optimization coefficient of the optimized isentropic enthalpy increase is calculated. Optimization coefficient of the torque speed is obtained according to the relationship between the number of energy heads and the torque speed. An optimized diffusion factor of each rotor blade is calculated through the change of inlet and outlet airflow angle and consistency. Determination is made to check whether the diffusion factor exceeds a design critical value. Cascade efficiency of each stage of the rotor blade is calculated after optimization through the change of the average airflow angle. Determination is made to check whether efficiency of the blade cascade does not change significantly. The blade consistency adjustment process is ended if the conditions are satisfied.
   USE - Method for adjusting blade consistency for optimizing carbon dioxide axial flow compressor pipe network matching performance in supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quickly and economically improving the compatibility of the compressor and the pipe network and the operating efficiency of the compressor.

AB    NOVELTY - An electrocatalyst material comprises copper(I) oxide (Cu2O) containing antimony, where amount of antimony is 5-30 wt.%.
   USE - An electrocatalyst material for electrode used in selective electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrocatalyst material shows higher values of faradic efficiency and selectivity in the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide than known materials, while using inexpensive and widely available components, i.e. copper and antimony.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) electrode comprising powder of an electrocatalyst material and conductive material deposited on a support, in a weight ratio between electrocatalyst material and conductive material of 9:1-19:1;
   (2) production of electrocatalyst material which involves dissolving copper(II) salt and antimony(III) salt in a solvent selected from ethanol, ethylene glycol, acetylacetone, diethylamine, ethylenediamine, oleylamine, N,N-dimethylformamide, mixtures of these solvents with each other, with water, or with aqueous solutions of D-glucose, hydrazine hydrate, amino acids, or sodium carboxymethylcellulose, to obtain a solution, heating the solution in a microwave oven at 180-230 degrees C for 1-10 minutes, separating precipitate from solution, and drying; and
   (3) selective electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) to carbon monoxide (CO), which involves using the electrode at a potential between - 0.69 V to - 1.09 V.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Electrocatalyst material contains 5.2-26.4 wt.%, preferably 17.2-23.9 wt.% antimony. Conductive material is in the form of powder. Conductive material is carbon based. Conductive material is selected from carbon black, graphite, graphene, and/or carbon nanotubes. Support is selected from conductive carbon paper, conductive carbon cloth, and metal mesh. Powder of electrocatalyst material and possibly of the conductive material are stabilized on the support with an ionomer. Copper(II) salt is selected from acetate, sulfate, and nitrate; and antimony(III) salt is selected from acetate, sulfate, and nitrate.

PN IN201821027581-A
TI Exhaust gas pressure used for braking and achieve a degree of autonomy, comprises exhaust air, mainly composed of carbon dioxide, is more compressible than air, due to the higher compressibility factor of carbon dioxide.
AU VEDULA R
   VISWANATHAN V
AE VEDULA R (VEDU-Individual)
   VISWANATHAN V (VISW-Individual)
GA 2021353645
AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust gas pressure, comprises AEBS is unique in the front of cultivation of exhaust for braking. The exhaust air, mainly composed of carbon dioxide, is more compressible than air, due to the higher compressibility factor of carbon dioxide. Exhaust air has high pressure when it just leaves the engine during the fourth stroke of the engine. This pressure is down-converted into a lower pressure due to the catalytic converter and the muffler silencing. The AEBS functions as a manual-autonomous co-existent system. Due to the utility of air brakes, the stopping distance is much lower than compared to traditional disk brakes, where the oil is driven by pure mechanical action. This ensures better brake performance and at the same time, exhaust's usage.
   USE - Exhaust gas pressure used for braking and achieve a degree of autonomy.
   ADVANTAGE - An exhaust gas pressure has low latency time between the decision made by the processor and the mechanical braking action, and avoids the effect of toppling and imbalance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of an exhaust gas pressure. (Drawing includes the non-English language text).

PN CN112519582-A; WO2022109788-A1
TI High-safety electric automobile, has brittle tube distributed between battery pack shell and shell and filling nozzle for spraying flame-retardant polyurethane for filling gap between battery units to reduce oxygen content in battery pack.
AU ZHU H
   QIAN C
   GAO Y
AE JIANGSU JINPENG GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU JINPENG GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202131555H
AB    NOVELTY - The automobile has a polyurethane cabin filled with flame-retardant polyurethane. A carbon dioxide cabin is filled with liquid carbon dioxide. A shell is sleeved on a battery pack shell. A brittle tube is densely distributed between the battery pack shell and the shell and made of hard plastic. A collision force weakening component weakens impact of automobile collision on the polyurethane cabin and the carbon dioxide cabin. A control unit controls a filling valve to connect a filling hose. A filling nozzle sprays flame-retardant polyurethane for filling a gap between battery units to reduce oxygen content in the battery pack.
   USE - High-safety electric automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The automobile breaks the brittle tube at crack when an electric automobile main body collides the battery pack shell and causes the battery pack shell to crack and sprays flame-retardant polyurethane and carbon dioxide from the brittle tube for sealing the crack and preventing oxygen from entering the automobile main body so as to reduce risk of spontaneous combustion of the battery.

PN CN111974449-A
TI Inorganic-organic composite nanoparticles used in            photocatalysis of reducing carbon dioxide to generate            methanol comprise silver and organic matter composition            including crosslinkable vinyl benzyl chloride and            divinyl benzene.
AU LIU T
   WU Y
   WEN S
   CUI X
   TIAN T
   WEI J
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 2020B94702
AB    NOVELTY - Inorganic-organic composite nanoparticles                comprise silver as core and organic matter                composition coated on outer layer. The organic                matter comprises crosslinkable vinyl benzyl                chloride, divinyl benzene, and benzoyl                peroxide.
   USE - The inorganic-organic composite nanoparticles                are used in photocatalysis of reducing carbon                dioxide to generate methanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite nanoparticles can efficiently                catalytically reduces nitrogen dioxide to methanol                under light condition, can be recycled multiple                times, and effectively improve ability of a unit                material to convert carbon dioxide into methanol                fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the nano particles                comprising ultrasonic dispersing silver                nanoparticle in solvent, adding vinyl benzyl                chloride, divinylbenzene, and benzoyl peroxide,                slowly raising temperature, keeping boiling,                distilling most solvent, centrifuging, dispersing,                and drying. The ultrasonic dispersion solvent is                acetonitrile; and
   (2) production of methanol comprising taking                composite nanoparticles, ultrasonic dispersing in                solvent, adding potassium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide,                stirring at room temperature under normal pressure,                blowing carbon dioxide gas to saturated state,                stirring, reacting, centrifuging to remove                supernatant, continuously dispersing in                1,4-dioxane, adding deionized water and                N,N,N',N'-tetramethylbenzidine, irradiating with a                mercury lamp, centrifuging, taking supernatant, and                distilling out methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  silver, vinyl benzyl chloride, divinyl benzene,                  and benzoyl peroxide is                  (30-50):(40-60):(40-60):1. The solvent is                  tetrahydrofuran. The ratio of 1,4-dioxane,                  deionized water, and                  N,N,N',N'-tetramethylbenzidine is                  1-10:50-100:10-20, preferably 1:50:1. Preferred                  Method: The silver nano-ion is modified by                  3-methycryloylpropyl trimethoxysilane, preferably                  taking silver nitrate solution, stirring, slowly                  adding sodium citrate solution, oil bath heating                  for 1 hour at 100 degrees C, transferring to                  normal temperature water bath, continuously                  stirring, adding silver nano-particle sol mixed                  solution and absolute ethanol, stirring at room                  temperature, adding ammonia water, continuously                  stirring, adding 3-methycryloylpropyl                  trimethoxysilane, stirring for long time at room                  temperature, centrifuging to remove supernatant,                  washing for 3 times by ethanol, and vacuum                  drying, where silver nitrate concentration is 1-5                  mmol/L, preferably 1.5 mmol/L, mass fraction of                  sodium citrate solution is 1-5 wt.%, and volume                  ratio of silver nanoparticle sol mixture,                  absolute ethanol and ammonia is 1-5:1-5:0.1-0.5,                  preferably 1:3:0.1. The method further comprises                  centrifuging and separating the reacted solution,                  ultrasonically dispersing in deionized water,                  adding diluted hydrochloric acid, stirring,                  centrifuging, taking composite nano particles,                  and vacuum drying.

PN TW202003925-A; TW737911-B1
TI Device of Generating Hydrogen through Hydrolysis along with Reduction of Carbon Dioxide.
AU LIU Y
   LU Y
   CHUNG J
AE INST NUCLEAR ENERGY RES ATOMIC ENERGY CO (ATEN-C)
GA 2020C7140A
AB    NOVELTY - A device is provided to generate hydrogen through hydrolysis while carbon dioxide is reduced. The device comprises a first panel of a translucent material; a first electrode deposed at an end under the first panel; a separator membrane deposed under the first panel; a second electrode deposed under the separating membrane; and a third electrode deposed at another end under the first panel. The present invention provides an efficient device to generate hydrogen with methanation. A photocatalytic composite of reforming catalysts is developed. Therein, the composite comprises a titanium-based catalytic material and a metal-, oxide- or metal-oxide-based catalytic material. Its composition include 1~90 weight percent (wt%) of the titanium-based catalytic material and 0~10wt% pf the metal-, oxide- or metal-oxide-based catalytic material. Thus, the composite obtains very high activity and selectivity to be directly applied in both the anode and the cathode. After the catalyst electrodes are deposed under light or applied with heat or bios voltage to hydrolyze water into electrons and holes for generating hydrogen, protons (H+) are directly supplied to the cathode catalyst electrode containing carbon dioxide by passing through the separator membrane; and chemical fuels of hydrocarbons or alcohols, such as methane, methanol or the like, are obtained through reforming reduction of carbon dioxide.

AU LU J

   WEI M
   JIANG H
   HU X

AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 2019407261
AB    NOVELTY - Groundwater treatment involves treating cation exchange of water to obtain hydrogen-containing ions, adding hydrochloric acid to the treated water to obtain acid-containing water, mixing hydrogen ion water with acid-containing water, co-reacting to obtain water containing carbon dioxide gas, aerating and stripping the water containing carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Groundwater treatment for removing both hardness and reducing alkalinity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Groundwater treatment involves treating cation exchange of water to obtain hydrogen-containing ions, adding hydrochloric acid to the treated water to obtain acid-containing water, mixing hydrogen ion water with acid-containing water, co-reacting to obtain water containing carbon dioxide gas, aerating and stripping the water containing carbon dioxide gas. The amount of hydrochloric acid added per liter of water to be treated is of formula (S2): 3.33x Bx C-S1, where B represents the temporary hardness of the water to be treated and the unit is mg/L, C represents the concentration of hydrochloric acid and the unit is mg/L, and S1 represents the amount of acid exchanged per liter of water to be treated after ion exchange and the unit is mg/L. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for groundwater treatment device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The total hardness of the water to be treated is 400-550 mg/L. The water alkalinity to be treated is 200-350 mg/L. The amount of acid exchanged per liter of water to be treated after ion exchange is 0-5 mmol/L, and the amount of hydrochloric acid added per liter of water to be treated is 0-6 mmol/L.

TI Excavating and welding repair of cast iron parts            comprises e.g. cutting the damaged part of the cast            iron piece by plasma cutting, and selecting the steel            plate according to the shape of the cut part to make            replacement insert.
AU HUANG J
   HUANG M
   CHEN X
   HU Z
   XIANG J
   WANG D
   TANG C
AE GUANGZHOU WENCHONG DOCKYARD CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 2018707270
AB    NOVELTY - Excavating and welding repair of cast iron                parts comprises (i) cutting the damaged part of the                cast iron piece by plasma cutting, selecting the                steel plate according to the shape of the cut part                to make replacement insert, (ii) according to the                thickness of the steel plate, cutting the edge of                the cut iron piece and replacing the edge of the                insert with a groove and polishing it to reveal                base metal, and (iii) selecting carbon dioxide                welding machine, the polarity of the power supply                is DC reverse connection, the protection gas of the                molten pool is 100 % carbon dioxide, selecting the                carbon dioxide flux cored wire, performing the                carbon dioxide protection welding at normal                temperature, and inserting the replacement insert                to the cast iron piece.
   USE - The method is useful for excavating and                welding repair of cast iron parts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, easy to operate, easy                to solder; makes the cast iron component reusable;                saves resources; reduces the components that are                scrapped due to corrosion and mildew and the cost                of repairing the ship.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The steel plate is one                  of Q235, A plate, E plate or H plate. The carbon                  dioxide flux-cored wire is a CHT711 or CHT512                  wire having a diameter of 1.2 mm. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), for cast iron parts of                  specific shape, the replacement inserts of the                  original shape are made of steel plates before                  cutting, and then cut by plasma. The welding                  current in the step (iii) is 140-180 amps, the                  voltage is 23-29 volts, the welding speed is                  140-210 mm/minute, and the molten pool shielding                  gas flow rate is 15-20 liters/minute. In the step                  (iii), a small portion of the welding is                  immediately tapped with nail hammer to remove the                  stress crack and if a crack occurs during the                  welding process, the crack is removed and the                  welding is continued. The excavation and welding                  repair of the cast iron piece is gradually cooled                  to room temperature with a heat insulating cotton                  package. After the repair and replacement of the                  cast iron piece is completed, a zinc block is                  added to the new member.

   USE - Method of using dipropylamine as reversible extraction solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is energy saving and environmentally friendly; and has mild operating conditions, less solvent loss, and adjustable hydrophilicity and hydrophobicity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) mixing the carbon-containing organic residue and the dipropylamine solvent according to a mass ratio under normal temperature and pressure, stirring, allowing to stand, and conveying to the separation equipment for separation to obtain solution A and solid residue, (2) sending solution A to the first oil-water separation device/equipment, adding water of 1-3 times the volume of the dipropylamine solvent, passing in carbon dioxide gas until the oil is completely precipitated, stop ventilating and separating liquids to obtain oil products and solutions B, (3) transferring the solution B to a stirring device or equipment, add inorganic alkali while stirring, and separating until the solution B is completely separated into two phases to obtain solution C and a carbonate solution, (4) sending the solution C to the second oil-water separation device/equipment, allowing to stand, and separating the solution to obtain dipropylamine solvent and water, and (5) drying carbonate and using as a product. Preferred Components: The inorganic alkali is sodium hydroxide solution or solid, or barium hydroxide solution or solid. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is mixture of coke oven flue gas from a coking plant, flue gas from a power plant, flue gas produced by calcining limestone in a lime plant, and/or exhaust gas produced by a fossil fuel/natural gas burning boiler.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
