PN US2020096254-A1; EP3628389-A1; FR3086187-A1; CN110938480-A; FR3086187-B1
TI Producing biomethane from biogas stream involves            drying the biogas stream partially removing one            impurity contained in the dried biogas stream by            solidification and removal of the solidified one            impurity to yield partially purified biogas.
AU CARDON G
   TRUEBA A
   VALENTIN S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202023110C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomethane from biogas stream                involves drying the biogas stream partially                removing one impurity contained in the dried biogas                stream by solidification and removal of the                solidified one impurity to yield at least partially                purified biogas, and separating the methane and the                carbon dioxide contained in the purified biogas so                as to produce a biomethane stream and a carbon                dioxide stream.
   USE - Method for producing biomethane from biogas                stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  removing one of the at least one impurity                  comprises dried biogas stream to a pressure of 5                  bar, cooling the compressed biogas stream to a                  temperature of -70 degrees C, solidifying                  impurity resulting in deposition of the                  solidified at least one impurity, and removing                  solidified at least one impurity from the cooled                  compressed biogas stream. The compressed biogas                  stream is cooled with a cooling stream of carbon                  dioxide produced during separating. The cooling                  is carried out to remove the impurity by                  sublimation of impurity and by flushing with a                  gaseous flushing stream. The sublimation is                  performed at a temperature above a sublimation                  temperature and at a pressure below 2 bar. The                  cooling stream resulting from cooling which is                  used as the gaseous flushing stream. The cooling                  and removal are performed simultaneously.                  Preferred Conditions: The gaseous flushing stream                  is a stream of recycled carbon dioxide produced                  during separating. The separating is performed by                  washing with water, washing with amines,                  adsorption, solidification of the carbon dioxide,                  or membrane separation. The drying is performed                  by cooling and condensation followed by                  adsorption. The removing of impurity includes the                  use of a heat exchanger. The compressing is                  performed using a compressor. The gaseous                  flushing stream is obtained from a storage                  capacity. The biogas stream is cooled with a                  cooling stream with the biogas stream passing                  through a first channel of the heat exchanger and                  the cooling stream passing through a second                  channel of the heat exchanger and removing                  impurity. The impurity is removed by sublimation                  of impurity and flushing with a gaseous flushing                  stream, with the gaseous flushing stream passing                  in the first channel of the heat exchanger and                  with the biogas stream passing in the second                  channel. Preferred Components: The gaseous                  flushing stream is a stream of carbon dioxide or                  nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107996808-A
TI Preparation of fig dried fruit by peeling fig, slicing, cleaning, drying, immersing in fixative solution, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, immersing in sugar solution, ultrasonically auxiliary dipping and microwave drying.
AU LUO Z
AE LIUZHOU FEISHENGPENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201836596A
AB    NOVELTY - A fig dried fruit is prepared by peeling fig, slicing, cleaning, drying, immersing in fixative solution for 1-2 minutes, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, immersing in sugar solution, ultrasonically auxiliary dipping for 3-5 hours, and microwave drying to moisture content of 8-12%.
   USE - Method for preparing fig dried fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is not easy to mildew, and has long shelf life and good color, luster and healthcare effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of ethyl acetate and fig fruit is 0.1-1:100. The ratio of petroleum ether and fig fruit is 1-5:100. The ratio of ethanol solution and fig fruit is 5:100. The fixative solution comprises fig fruit peel extract and alcohol solvent at ratio of 30-40:100. The alcohol solvent is ethanol, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol or butanol. The solution comprises 5-10 wt.% naringin, 20-30 wt.% glucose, 12-20 wt.% maltose and 10-15 wt.% soybean oligosaccharide. Preferred Method: Extraction is done by fig peel crushing by 200-mesh sieve, putting into supercritical extraction kettle, primary extracting to obtain primary extract, adjusting extraction conditions, secondary extracting to obtain secondary extract, adjusting extraction condition, tertiary extracting to obtain tertiary extract, and mixing extracts. Primary extraction is done by supercritical carbon dioxide extracting using ethyl acetate as entrainer at 25 degrees C for 10-12 minutes, at 35 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20 L/minute. Secondary extraction is done by supercritical carbon dioxide extracting using petroleum ether as entrainer at 25 degrees C for 10-12 minutes at 25 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 23 L/minute. Tertiary extraction is done by using 20% ethanol solution as entrainer at 35 degrees C for 10-12 minutes at 45 MPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 25 L/minute. Drying is done at 500 W and 250 MHz.

PN CN107999057-A
TI Regenerating load type noble metal catalyst comprises oxidizing deactivated supported noble metal catalyst by mixed gas of carbon dioxide and oxygen and then carrying out reduction reaction by reducing agent in tetrahydrofuran solvent.
AU YAN D
   GONG Y
   YAN S
   GONG D
AE SHENYANG RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNCM-C)
GA 201837593R
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating load type noble metal catalyst comprises oxidizing deactivated supported noble metal catalyst by mixed gas of carbon dioxide and oxygen and then carrying out reduction reaction by reducing agent in tetrahydrofuran solvent to obtain final product.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic hydrogenation reaction of aromatic nitro compound containing sulfonic acid group (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has low cost, high efficiency and has long service life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inactivated supported noble metal catalyst is treated with alkaline solution. The method comprises (i) mixing deactivated load type noble metal catalyst with alkali solution in a mass ratio of 10:1-200:1, total pressure of 0.1-4 MPa, mixing a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen in a volume ratio of 1:10-10:1, stirring at 100-300 degrees C for 0.5-5.5 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing cake with deionized water to neutrality, and (ii) adding filter cake obtained in step (i) into tetrahydrofuran solvent with a catalyst in a mass ratio of 5:1-100:1, adding reducing agent and catalyst in a mass ratio of 0.1:1-10:1, stirring at 0-60 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours, filtering, washing with tetrahydrofuran solvent, washing filter cake to neutral with deionized water to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The alkaline solution is aqueous alkaline solution having a concentration of 0.05-1%; alkali is basic sodium salt or basic potassium salt; and reducing agent is sodium borohydride, diimide hydrate, formaldehyde or hydrogen gas. The base is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, trisodium phosphate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate or dipotassium phosphate.

PN EP4049745-A1; US2022274056-A1; CN114950069-A; JP2022131439-A
TI Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus for separating            carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas by            causing electrochemical reaction, has discharge section            for discharging liquid supplied from liquid supply            section.
AU OGURA H
   UMEDA H
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 2022B00397
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a voltage applicator                for applying a voltage between the working                electrode and the counter electrode. The counter                electrode supplies electrons to the working                electrode when the voltage is applied by the power                supply. The working electrode includes an                electrochemical cell to adsorb carbon dioxide upon                receiving the electrons from the counter electrode.                An adsorber accommodates the electrochemical cell                to receive the carbon dioxide-containing gas. The                liquid supply sections (142,143) supply liquid to                the adsorber while the voltage is applied between                the working electrode and the counter electrode.                The discharge sections (1b,144,145) discharge the                liquid supplied from the liquid supply section and                carbon dioxide-removed gas obtained by separating                carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-containing                gas, from the adsorber while the voltage is applied                between the working electrode and the counter                electrode.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus of carbon                dioxide recovery system (claimed) for separating                carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas                containing carbon dioxide by causing                electrochemical reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The degree of purity of the carbon dioxide can                be increased in the adsorber, and the purity of                carbon dioxide as recovered can accordingly be                enhanced. The carbon dioxide adsorbent is an                electrochemically active species, adsorption and                desorption of the carbon dioxide to and from the                carbon dioxide adsorent can be switched by changing                a potential between the working electrode and a                counter electrode. The liquid supply section and                the discharge section discharge gas excluding                carbon dioxide with liquid from the adsorber due to                provision of the liquid supply and discharge                section, so that the degree of purification of the                recovered carbon dioxide is increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for separating carbon dioxide from                carbon dioxide-containing gas containing carbon                dioxide by causing electrochemical reaction;                and
   a carbon dioxide recovery system.

PN KR2020002259-A; KR2120543-B1
TI Gas treatment system for offshore structure, has control valve provided with downstream of carbon dioxide pump, where opening of carbon dioxide pump is controlled to reduce pressure fluctuation of carbon dioxide in re-injection well.
AU HWANG S B
   SEO Y T
   PARK G H
AE KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD (HHIH-C)
   UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 202004812S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooling unit for cooling gas produced in a gas well. A distillation unit realizes cryogenic distillation to separate carbon dioxide from cooled gas. A carbon dioxide processing unit (30) re-injects separated carbon dioxide into an underground injection well. A liquefaction unit liquefies gas from which carbon dioxide is separated. The carbon dioxide processing unit is provided with a carbon dioxide pump (34) for pressurizing the carbon dioxide in a liquid state to an ultra-critical state. A control valve (35) is provided with downstream of the carbon dioxide pump, where opening of the carbon dioxide pump is controlled based on a pressure measurement value for the carbon dioxide in the liquid state to reduce pressure fluctuation of the carbon dioxide in a re-injection well.
   USE - Gas treatment system for an offshore structure (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces discharged gas to normal range to prevent freezing of the carbon dioxide contained in gas by using the heater, and determines a temperature sensor a measured value that is less than or equal to a preset value, and improves opening degree of a blow-down valve to reduce cooling due to reduced pressure, thus preventing carbon dioxide freezing. The system realizes cryogenic distillation, carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide re-injection in an efficient and safe manner during processing of natural gas, and maximizes gas production efficiency, and can reduce pressure fluctuation of carbon dioxide in the re-injection well by using a control valve, and changes amount of the carbon dioxide generated in the production gas, and can secure accuracy of pressure measurement by measuring pressure of a carbon dioxide upstream of a carbon dioxide pump with respect to carbon dioxide phase change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial conceptual diagram of a gas treatment system.
   Carbon dioxide processing unit (30)
   Carbon dioxide pump (34)
   Control valve (35)
   Choke valve (38)
   Controller (39)

AB    NOVELTY - A plant (100) for producing a hydrogen (H2)-rich stream (8) from a hydrocarbon feed (1), comprises autothermal reformer (ATR) (110) which is arranged to receive a hydrocarbon feed (2) and convert it to a stream of syngas (3); a shift section comprising a high temperature shift unit (115) arranged to receive a stream of syngas from ATR and shifts it in a high temperature shift step, providing a shifted syngas stream (5); carbon dioxide (CO2) removal section which is arranged to receive the shifted syngas stream from shift section and separate CO2-rich stream from shifted syngas stream, providing a CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream (7); and hydrogen purification unit (125) which is arranged to receive CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream from CO2 removal section, and separates it into a high-purity H2 stream (8) and an off-gas stream (9).
   USE - A plant for producing a hydrogen-rich stream from a hydrocarbon feed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant reduces consumption of hydrocarbon feed and fuel in a hydrogen plant and/or process, thus, increasing energy efficiency. The plant and/or process has overall lower investment and operating costs compared to plants based on steam methane reformers (tubular reformers) without compromising energy efficiency. The plant recovers, i.e. captures, as much as possible the carbon present in the hydrocarbon feed, while at the same time not compromising with the energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A plant (100) for producing a hydrogen (H2)-rich stream (8) from a hydrocarbon feed (1), comprises autothermal reformer (ATR) (110) which is arranged to receive a hydrocarbon feed (2) and convert it to a stream of syngas (3); a shift section comprising a high temperature shift unit (115) arranged to receive a stream of syngas from ATR and shifts it in a high temperature shift step, providing a shifted syngas stream (5); carbon dioxide (CO2) removal section which is arranged to receive the shifted syngas stream from shift section and separate CO2-rich stream from shifted syngas stream, providing a CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream (7); and hydrogen purification unit (125) which is arranged to receive CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream from CO2 removal section, and separates it into a high-purity H2 stream (8) and an off-gas stream (9). The plant is absent of a primary reforming unit; is arranged to feed greater than or equal to 1 portion of the off-gas stream from hydrogen purification unit as an off-gas recycle stream to the feed side of the ATR; and/or as an off-gas recycle stream to the feed side of the shift section; and is arranged to provide an inlet temperature of hydrocarbon feed to the ATR of less than or equal to 600 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing a H2-rich stream from a hydrocarbon feed which involves providing a plant; supplying a hydrocarbon feed to ATR and converting it to a stream of syngas; supplying a stream of syngas from ATR to the shift section, and shifting it in a high temperature shift step, providing a shifted syngas stream; supplying the shifted gas stream from the shift section to the CO2 removal section and separating a CO2-rich stream from shifted syngas stream, providing a CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream; supplying the CO2-depleted shifted syngas stream from CO2 removal section to a hydrogen purification unit, and separating it into a high-purity H2 stream and an off-gas stream; where the process is absent of primary reforming; feeding greater than or equal to 1 portion of the off-gas stream from hydrogen purification unit as an off-gas recycle stream to the feed side of the ATR and/or as an off-gas recycle stream to the feed side of the shift section; and the inlet temperature of hydrocarbon feed to the ATR is less than or equal to 600 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the layouts of the ATR-based hydrogen process and plant.

PN CN112080423-A
TI Docking carbon dioxide incubator and an isolator involves opening outer box door to expose the inner flange ring and inner box door, and moving mobile cart to outer flange door of isolator.
AU ZHAO Z
   XIA X
   YE D
AE ZHEJIANG TAILIN MEDICAL ENG CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7969J
AB    NOVELTY - Docking carbon dioxide incubator (1) and an isolator involves using a glove opening which seals the inside and outside of the isolator. The docking of the incubator and the isolator further includes opening outer box door (111) more than 180 degrees to expose the inner flange ring and inner box door, and moving the mobile cart to the outer flange door of the isolator. The incubator is moved toward the side of the outer flange door, so that the inner flange ring enters the outer flange ring, and inflate the third inflatable sealing ring so that the inner flange ring and the outer flange ring are connected in a sealed manner. The sterilization medium generation module is started to fill the sterilization space with an oxidizing biocide to sterilize the sterilization space, and activated the sterilization medium decomposition module to decompose the biocide and maintained through a high-efficiency air filter.
   USE - Method for docking carbon dioxide incubator and an isolator.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the uniform temperature in the incubator and effectively prevents the formation of condensation inside the inner door.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Docking carbon dioxide incubator and an isolator involves using a glove opening which seals the inside and outside of the isolator. The incubator includes an outer box movably arranged on the mobile vehicle and an inner box located in the outer box. The outer box is provided with a rotatable outer box door on one side of the moving direction. An auxiliary electric heating element is provided, the inner box is provided with a docking outlet on the side corresponding to the outer box door, and an outwardly protruding inner flange ring is provided on the edge of the docking outlet he isolator, and an outward direction is provided on the edge of the transmission outlet. The raised outer flange ring is provided with an outer flange door in the outer flange ring, and a second inflatable sealing ring is arranged between the outer flange ring and the outer flange door. The docking of the incubator and the isolator further includes opening outer box door more than 180 degrees to expose the inner flange ring and inner box door, and moving the mobile cart to the outer flange door of the isolator. The incubator is moved toward the side of the outer flange door, so that the inner flange ring enters the outer flange ring, and inflate the third inflatable sealing ring so that the inner flange ring and the outer flange ring are connected in a sealed manner. The sterilization medium generation module is started to fill the sterilization space with an oxidizing biocide to sterilize the sterilization space, and activated the sterilization medium decomposition module to decompose the biocide and maintained through a high-efficiency air filter. The first inflatable sealing ring is deflatten, disconnected the connection between the inner door and the inner flange ring, and deflatten the second inflatable sealing ring, disconnected the connection between the outer flange door and the outer flange ring, put hand into the isolator through the glove opening, and opened the outer flange door and inner box door. The petri dish is placed in the isolator into the inner box, closed the inner box door and the outer flange door, and inflatten the first inflatable sealing ring so that the inner box door and the inner flange ring are connected in a sealed manner. The inflatable sealing ring is inflated to make the outer flange door and the outer flange ring hermetically connected. The third inflatable sealing ring is defeated, disconnected the sealing connection between the inner flange ring and the outer flange ring, moved the incubator back to separate the incubator from the isolator, and then closed the outer door. The auxiliary electric heating element is reopened in the outer box door to continue heating the inner box door to maintain the culture temperature required by the inner box. The electric heating element of the inner box is in the incubator and isolator keep running during the docking process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of incubator.
   Incubator (1)
   Outer box (11)
   Inner box (12)
   Outer box door (111)
   Docking outlet (121)

TI Treating sour gas mixture of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide by means of Claus process involves introducing mixture into Claus furnace, oxidizing part of hydrogen sulfide, feeding air into furnace and introducing part of air pure oxygen.
AU NEUHAUS O
   GUZMANN M
   POLLITT S
   LEWIS J
   LEVIS Y
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   WORLEYPARSONS GROUP INC (WORL-Non-standard)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   WORLEYPARSONS GROUP INC (WORL-Non-standard)
GA 201937546X
AB    NOVELTY - Method (100) of treatment of a sour gas mixture by means of a Claus process (10), where the sour gas mixture contains hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, the sour gas mixture is introduced into a Claus furnace (11) and a part of the hydrogen sulfide in the sour gas mixture is oxidized by free-flame oxidation in the Claus furnace, at least comprises a first operation mode in which an amount of air is fed into the Claus furnace to provide oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, and a second mode in which instead of at least a part of the amount of air pure oxygen or a gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air is introduced into the Claus furnace to provide the oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, where in the first operation mode the amount of air is at least in part provided in the form of air compressed by a main air compressor (21) of an air separation unit (20).
   USE - The method is useful for treatment of the sour gas mixture by means of the Claus process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: treats sour gas mixtures including the Claus process operated with oxygen enrichment; provides carbon dioxide and other components e.g. hydrogen in a simpler and more cost-effective manner, e.g. by cryogenic technology alone and without energy-intensive wet technology; and provides synergy effects between the two processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (100) of treatment of a sour gas mixture by means of a Claus process (10), where the sour gas mixture contains hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, the sour gas mixture is introduced into a Claus furnace (11) and a part of the hydrogen sulfide in the sour gas mixture is oxidized by free-flame oxidation in the Claus furnace, at least comprises a first operation mode in which an amount of air is fed into the Claus furnace to provide oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, and a second mode in which instead of at least a part of the amount of air pure oxygen or a gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air is introduced into the Claus furnace to provide the oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, where in the first operation mode the amount of air is at least in part provided in the form of air compressed by a main air compressor (21) of an air separation unit (20), and in the second operation mode the pure oxygen or the gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air is at least in part provided in the form of an air separation product provided by the air separation unit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for treatment of the sour gas mixture by means of the Claus process, comprising means at least configured to operate the apparatus in a first operation mode in which an amount of air is fed into the Claus furnace to provide oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, and to operate the apparatus in a second mode in which instead of at least a part of the amount of air pure oxygen or a gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air is introduced into the Claus furnace to provide the oxygen for the free-flame oxidation, where the apparatus is configured to provide, in the first operation mode, the amount of air at least in part in the form of air compressed by a main air compressor of an air separation unit and the apparatus is configured to provide, in the second operation mode, the pure oxygen or the gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air at least in part in the form of an air separation product provided by the air separation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of treatment of the sour gas mixture by means of the Claus process in a first and second operation mode.
   Claus process (10)
   Claus furnace (11)
   Air separation unit (20)
   Main air compressor (21)
   Treatment of the sour gas mixture by means of the Claus process (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises an intermediate operation mode where the amount of air fed into the Claus furnace substituted by the pure oxygen or by the gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air in a gradual or stepwise manner, to switch from the first operation mode to the second operation mode. The gas mixture comprising an oxygen content higher than that of ambient air is used in the second operation mode, and the gas mixture comprises at least 30 vol.%, 40 vol.%, 50 vol.%, 60 vol.%, 70 vol.%, 80 vol.%, 90 vol.% or 95 vol.% oxygen. In the method, only gases or gas mixtures which are poor in or free from nitrogen are introduced into the Claus furnace in the second operation mode.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN JP2018128201-A
TI Carbon dioxide liquefaction system of thermal-power-generation plant, has carbon dioxide condenser that is comprised by direct heat exchange with liquefied natural gas (LNG) pressure-risen by LNG pump so that carbon dioxide is liquefied.
AU UECHI H
   HORIZOE K
   SUEMORI S
   MATAKE T
   AOKI Y
   KONNO Y
AE MITSUBISHI JUKOGYO KK (MITO-C)
GA 201863492F
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide liquefaction system (2) has liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply system (12a) that supplies LNG. The LNG pump (14) pressure-rises the LNG from the LNG supply system. The carbon dioxide condenser (16) is comprised by the direct heat exchange with LNG pressure-risen by the LNG pump so that carbon dioxide is liquefied. The discharge pressure of the LNG pump is 4 Mpa or more. A compressor (36) for compressing carbon dioxide before flowing in into the carbon dioxide condenser is provided so that the pressure of carbon dioxide in the inlet port of the carbon dioxide condenser is set to 0.52 Mpa or more. The LNG line (10) guides the LNG from LNG supply system. The first heat exchanger is provided in LNG line, in the downstream of LNG pump, and the upstream of carbon dioxide condenser, for cooling thermal medium by heat exchange with the LNG. The LNG is utilized as low temperature heat source of heat cycle in first heat exchanger or second heat exchanger.
   USE - Carbon dioxide liquefaction system of thermal-power-generation plant (claimed) for installation to generate waste gas containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is liquefied by effective heat exchange with LNG. Since the pressure of the vaporized high pressure natural gas is used effectively and made to take out as motive power, the output and efficiency as an entire plant can be improved. Since the discharge pressure of LNG pump is specific range or more, the temperature difference with carbon dioxide is suppressed and LNG becomes excessive in carbon dioxide condenser. The solidification of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide condenser can be suppressed. The reduction of the heat exchange amount between carbon dioxide and the LNG in carbon dioxide condenser can be suppressed, and decline in carbon dioxide liquefaction efficiency can be suppressed. The LNG and carbon dioxide high pressure can be heat-exchanged effectively at the time of liquefaction of carbon dioxide. The thermal medium can be cooled using LNG higher temperature than the temperature range which can liquefy carbon dioxide effectively, so that the thermal efficiency as the whole system including carbon dioxide liquefaction can be improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has a separation membrane (32) that separates carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide rich gas. The carbon dioxide condenser is comprised so that the impurity gas is discharged with gas, while liquefying carbon dioxide among carbon dioxide rich gas containing carbon dioxide and impurity gas. The expander is provided in the LNG line in the downstream of the carbon dioxide condenser, so that vaporized LNG is expanded and motive power is taken out from the LNG. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide liquefaction method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide liquefaction system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide liquefaction system (2)
   LNG line (10)
   LNG supply system (12a)
   LNG pump (14)
   Carbon dioxide condenser (16)

AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 2019692284
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing canthaxanthin by oxidizing beta -carotene comprises oxidizing sodium nitrate and carbon dioxide as oxidants and beta -carotene in the presence of a catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing canthaxanthin by oxidizing beta-carotene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction conditions, high yield and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a reactive metal iodide, preferably potassium iodide and/or sodium iodide, more preferably potassium iodide. The amount of catalyst is 1-8%, preferably 3-6 % by mass of the beta-carotene. The weight ratio of the sodium nitrate to the catalyst is 1-4:1, preferably 2-3:1. An aqueous solution of sodium nitrate is used, and the weight ratio of sodium nitrate to water in the aqueous sodium nitrate solution is 1:1-10, preferably 1:6-8. The carbon dioxide reaction process is continuously introduced into the reaction liquid, and the gas flow rate is controlled at 200-400 ml/minute per 10 mmol of beta -carotene. The oxidation reaction is carried out in a carbon dioxide atmosphere. The oxidation reaction is carried out in an organic solvent is dichloromethane, dichloroethane, diethyl ether and n-hexane, preferably dichloromethane, and the amount of the organic solvent is beta - carotene is 20-25 times the weight. Preferred Method: The method comprises oxidizing, recrystallizing, where the solvent used for the recrystallization is acetone, dichloromethane, ethanol, diethyl ether, n-hexane, cyclohexane, and n-propyl acetate, preferably acetone and the amount of the solvent is preferably 5-10 times the mass of the crude canthaxanthin, refluxing at 70-75 degrees C for 8-12 hours, cooling to 20-30 degrees C, where the temperature decreasing rate is preferably 8-15 degrees C/h. The oxidation reaction has a reaction temperature of -10 to 50 degrees C, a reaction time of 1-4 hours, and a reaction system of pH 6.5-6.8. The oxidation reaction adopts a temperature-programming process, specifically: the firstly reacting at a reaction temperature of -10 to 0 degrees C, preferably -9 to -5 degrees C for 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2 hours and the temperature is then raised to 40-50 degrees C, preferably 41-48 degrees C for 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2 hours. The heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute, preferably 1-3 degrees C/minute.

Absorption liquid manufacturing unit (120) prepares and supplies high-concentration carbon dioxide absorption liquid. TI Plant cultivation apparatus for controlling growth rate of plant using air adjuster, has cabinet that is defining cultivation space to accommodate plant to be cultivated, and controller reduces carbon dioxide concentration in cultivation space to perform delayed cultivation mode.
AU KIM Y
   KIM Y S
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 202281711P
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) comprises a cabinet that is defining a cultivation space (S1) to accommodate a plant to be cultivated. A cultivator is arranged in the cultivation space and accommodates a portion of the plant. An air adjuster is arranged at the cabinet, and has a blower fan to blow external air toward the space. The adjuster adjusts an inflow amount of the external air into the space to adjust a carbon dioxide concentration in the space, and a controller controls the adjuster. The controller reduces the concentration to perform a delayed cultivation mode in which a growth rate is lower than a default growth rate.
   USE - Plant cultivation apparatus for controlling a growth rate of a plant using an air adjuster that adjusts a carbon dioxide concentration of a cultivation space inside a cabinet.
   ADVANTAGE - The growth rate of a plant is reduced by reducing the carbon dioxide concentration of the cultivation space at a timing desired by a user. The plant cultivation apparatus allows the user to sufficiently consume the plant that has been completely grown by controlling the concentration of carbon dioxide inside the apparatus to control the harvest timing of the plant. The controller controls the liquid supply so that a supply amount of the nutrient liquid supplied to the cultivator in the delayed cultivation mode is lower than the supply amount in the default cultivation mode.

PT P
PN WO2021262922-A1
TI Modified copper-zinc-alumina catalyst used in composition for reducing carbon dioxide for preparing liquid product mixture, contains copper, zinc, aluminum, oxygen, elements chosen from cobalt, iron or nickel and optionally elements chosen from e.g. manganese, silver, niobium and zirconium.
AU SHEEHAN S W
   CHEN C
AE AIR HOLDINGS CO INC (AIRH-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0919E
AB    NOVELTY - A modified copper-zinc-alumina (CZA) catalyst comprises copper, zinc, one or more elements (E1) chosen from cobalt, iron, or nickel, aluminum, oxygen, optionally, one or more elements (E2) chosen from group (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), (X), and (XI) metal (e.g., manganese, silver, niobium, zirconium, molybdenum, ruthenium, or palladium), and optionally, one or more group (IA) metals. The modified CZA catalyst comprises 1-40 wt.%, preferably 35-40 wt.% element (E1) with respect to copper, zinc, element (E1), element (E2) and group (IA) metal (in total).
   USE - Modified copper-zinc-alumina catalyst used in catalytic composition for reducing carbon dioxide in feed mixture for preparing liquid product mixture comprising methanol, ethanol, n-propanol and formaldehyde (all claimed). Can also be used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity, produces ethanol from carbonaceous feedstocks at a higher rate, and inhibits the formation of gaseous byproducts during operation, e.g. methane, to further enable effective recycle of unreacted gases during product gas recycle in a multipass gas to liquids reactor for methanol production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalytic composition, which comprises the catalyst, and an additional support;
   (2) preparation of catalyst, which involves preparing the catalyst by coprecipitation, wet impregnation, or ball milling; and
   (3) reducing carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the catalyst or the catalytic composition with a feed mixture comprising carbon dioxide and a reductant gas at a reduction temperature and a reduction pressure to obtain a liquid product mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method involves (a) providing a solution (S1) comprising a source of element (E1), copper, zinc, aluminum, a base, and water, (b) heating the solution (S1) at a temperature (TM1) for a period of time (T1) to produce a reaction mixture (M1), (c) heating the reaction mixture (M1) at a temperature (TM2) for a period of time (T2) to remove the water to produce a solid precursor, and (d) heating the solid precursor to a temperature (TM3) for a period of time (T3) to isolate the catalyst. The preparation method further involves contacting the solid precursor with a solution comprising group (IA) metal before step (d). The preparation method involves (a) providing a solution (S2) comprising a source of element (E1), copper, zinc, iron, and water, (b) providing a solution (S3) comprising a base, (c) heating the solution (S3) at the temperature (TM3) for the period of time (T3), (d) adding alumina to the solution (S3) to produce a reaction mixture (M2), (e) adding the solution (S2) to the reaction mixture (M2) at a temperature (TM4) for a period of time (T4) to produce a reaction mixture (M3), (f) heating the reaction mixture (M3) at a temperature (TM5) for a period of time (T5) to produce a solid precursor, (g) isolating the solid precursor, (h) contacting the solid precursor with a solution comprising group (IA) metal to produce a catalyst precursor, and (i) heating the catalyst precursor to a temperature (TM6) for a period of time (T6) to isolate the catalyst. The reduction of carbon dioxide involves contacting the catalyst with the feed mixture for at least 24 hours, preferably at least 168 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of copper with respect to zinc in the catalyst is 1-3, preferably 2-2.5 or 2-4, preferably 2.33. The molar ratio of zinc with respect to copper in the catalyst is 0.5-1.5. The molar ratio of iron with respect to copper in the catalyst is 0.5-1.5. The molar ratio of aluminum with respect to copper in the catalyst is 0.4-2.1, preferably 0.5-1. The molar ratio of group (IA) metals with respect to copper in the catalyst is 0.05-0.5, preferably 0.15. The molar ratio of copper with respect to cobalt in the catalyst is 2.5. The molar ratio of zinc oxide with respect to cobalt in the catalyst is 1. The molar ratio of alumina with respect to cobalt in the catalyst is 0.35. The molar ratio of copper with respect to nickel in the catalyst is 2.5. The molar ratio of zinc oxide with respect to nickel in the catalyst is 1. The molar ratio of alumina with respect to nickel in the catalyst is 0.35. The molar ratio of copper with respect to iron in the catalyst is 2.5. The molar ratio of zinc oxide with respect to iron in the catalyst is 1. The molar ratio of alumina with respect to iron in the catalyst is 0.35. The molar ratio of group (IA) metal with respect to cobalt in the catalyst is 0.1. The molar ratio of group (IA) metal with respect to nickel in the catalyst is 0.1. The molar ratio of group (IA) metal with respect to iron in the catalyst is 0.1. The catalytic composition comprises 5-80 wt.% catalyst. The feed mixture comprises less than 25%, preferably less than 1% carbon monoxide. The content ratio of carbon dioxide and reductant gas in the feed mixture is (1:10)-(10:1), preferably (1:3)-(1:1). Preferred Components: The element (E1) preferably comprises iron. The element (E2) preferably comprises molybdenum. The group (IA) metals comprise cesium. The catalyst comprises zinc oxide, copper oxide, cobalt oxide, iron oxide, nickel oxide and alumina. The catalyst comprises cobalt, copper, zinc oxide, alumina and group (IA) metal or nickel, copper, zinc oxide, alumina and group (IA) metal or iron, copper, zinc oxide, alumina and group (IA) metal. The additional support comprises one or more materials chosen from an oxide, nitride, fluoride, or silicate of an element chosen from aluminum, silicon, titanium, zirconium, cerium, magnesium, yttrium, lanthanum, zinc, and tin. The additional support comprises ( gamma )-alumina and one or more carbon-based material chosen from activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphene, and graphene oxide. The additional support is a mesoporous material. The catalytic composition is in a form of particles having an average size of 20 nm-5 mu m, preferably 50-300 nm. The feed mixture does not contain carbon monoxide. The liquid product mixture comprises at least 2 wt.%, at least 3 wt.% ethanol. The method does not produce formaldehyde. Preferred Properties: The additional support has mesopore volume of 0.01-3.0 cc/g, and surface area of 10-1000 m2/g. The reactant gases and recycle gases introduced into the reactor has gas hourly space velocity (GHSV) of 10-20000 or less than 500. Preferred Conditions: The reduction temperature is 100-450 degrees C. The reduction pressure is 50-3000 psi, preferably 1000 psi. The partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the feed mixture is 20-1000 psi, preferably 750-1000 psi.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reductant gas comprises hydrogen and hydrocarbon, such as methane, ethane, propane or butane, preferably methane. The reductant gas is derived from, flare gas, waste gas or natural gas. The liquid product mixture comprises methanol, ethanol, and n-propanol. Preferred Composition: The liquid product mixture comprises less than 50 ppm formaldehyde.

PN CN113731474-A
TI Preparation of multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve for e.g. carbon dioxide catalysis, involves adding prepared hybrid zeolite molecular sieve and ethylene polyamine organic amine into organic solvent, and ultrasonically treating.
AU XIA Q
   LU X
   ZHANG X
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV HUBEI (UYUB-C)
GA 2021E4990A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve involves under normal temperature, dispersing the organosilicon source in the aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide under the ultrasonic condition of power 200-400 W, adding the non-precious metal source, stirring at room temperature until it is completely dissolved to obtain solution (I), adding the solution (I), then adding the inorganic silicon source, stirring for 2-4 hours to obtain the reactant gel, adding the reactant gel into the solution, treating in a constant temperature oven to obtain a dry gel, then grinding it into powder, at 120-200degrees Celsius for static crystallization for 0.5-7 days, quenching (A) with cold water, filtering, washing and drying to obtain the hybrid zeolite molecular sieve, adding the prepared molecular sieve and ethylene polyamine organic amine into an organic solvent, ultrasonically treating the mixture, and then performing rotary evaporation and drying on a rotary evaporator.
   USE - Preparation of multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve used for carbon dioxide catalysis and epoxide cycloaddition to synthesize cyclocarbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve with excellent catalytic activity and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve for catalyzing carbon dioxide and epoxide cycloaddition to synthesize cyclocarbonate. The catalysis process involves adding multifunctional hybrid zeolite molecular sieve, epoxide and/or solvent, sealing the reactor, replacing the air in the reactor three times with carbon dioxide gas, and then filling the Carbon dioxide gas under a pressure of 0.5-3.0 MPa, stirring, heating the reaction kettle to make the reaction temperature to 60-150degrees Celsius, and reacting at constant temperature for 2-8 hours to obtain cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is chosen from absolute ethanol, isopropanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, methanol, acetone, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate, ethylene polyamine organic amine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenediamine, tetraethylenepentamine, ethyleneimine, and triethylenetetramine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum source is aluminum oxide. The inorganic silicon source and the organic silicon source are silicon oxide. The sodium source is sodium oxide. The non-precious metal source is non-precious metal. The organic silicon source is chosen from methyltriethoxysilane, dimethyldiethoxysilane, hexadecyltriethoxysilane, N-(beta-aminoethyl-gamma-aminopropyl) methyldimethoxysilane, vinyltriethoxysilane, 3-mercaptopropyltriethoxysilane, and 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The aluminum source is sodium metaaluminate or aluminum isopropoxide. The inorganic silicon source is chosen from silica sol, water glass, tetraethyl orthosilicate, fumed silica, and silicon tetrachloride. The non-precious metal source is chosen from nickel acetate tetrahydrate, nickel sulfate, nickel chloride hexahydrate, ferric nitrate nonahydrate, ferric trichloride anhydrous, ferrous sulfate, manganese nitrate tetrahydrate, manganese sulfate, manganese acetate tetrahydrate, chromium acetate, chromium nitrate, cobalt sulfate heptahydrate, cobalt acetate tetrahydrate, cobalt trichlorohexaammine, copper nitrate trihydrate, and copper chloride dihydrate.Preferred Composition: The content of the hybrid zeolite molecular sieve is 2-4 %mass of the organic solvent. The content of the ethylene polyamine organic amine is 0.2-2 %mass of the organic solvent. The molar ratio of aluminum source, organic silicon source, inorganic silicon source, sodium source, non-precious metal source, and water in the xerogel is 1:(0.4-6.0):(15.2-20.8):(4-10.5):(0.05-0.15):(42-98).Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonically treating at 30-100degrees Celsius, preferably 60-80degrees Celsius, a power of 100-600 W, preferably 400-500 W, and a stirring rate of 50-400 rpm, preferably 250-400 rpm for 3-8 hours, preferably 4-6 hours.

PT P
PN WO2021225448-A1; GB2595635-A
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide produced by offshore hydrocarbon production facility, involves mixing pressurised stream with injection water stream to form carbonated water stream, and injecting water stream into reservoir.
AU NEERAAS B O
   PETTERSEN J
   MAINZA DE KOEIJER G
   SKOURAS-ILIOPOULOS E
   ILIOPOULOS E S
   KOEIJER G M D
AE EQUINOR ENERGY AS (DENO-C)
GA 2021C58698
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating electrical power and/or heat for use by a hydrocarbon production facility at offshore location, where the generation produces an exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is separated from portion of the exhaust gas to form carbon dioxide stream at the offshore location. The pressure of the stream is increased to an injection pressure suitable for injecting a carbonated water stream into reservoir e.g. hydrocarbon reservoir. The pressurized stream is mixed with an injection water stream to form the water stream. The water stream is injected into the reservoir.
   USE - Method for capturing CO2 produced by offshore hydrocarbon production facility i.e. offshore oil and gas production facility in subsurface formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a technically simple and economically viable method of sequestering carbon dioxide emissions from hydrocarbon production facilities, thus reducing their CO2 emissions. The method utilizes the water injection system already present in the production facility to inject the CO2 into the reservoir concurrently with the injected water, in the form of carbonated water, rather than requiring dedicated CO2 injection system and well, thus making it possible to use existing production facility infrastructure to sequester CO 2 emissions in a reservoir. Injecting the carbonated stream into a hydrocarbon reservoir may advantageously support reservoir pressure and stimulate hydrocarbons production, i.e. to support formation pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a CO2 capture system for capturing CO2 from exhaust gas; and
   (2) a offshore hydrocarbon production facility for capturing CO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of method for capturing and sequestering CO2 generated on an offshore hydrocarbon production facility.
   Production facility (1)
   Pressure system (2)
   Gas turbine (3)
   Co2 capture system (5)
   Mixing unit (15)

PN CN113107440-A; CN113107440-B
TI Device for injecting well carbon dioxide foam, includes liquid inlet pipe that is connecting with upper surface of foaming box, centrifugal cylinder is connecting with fixing shaft, and foaming box is connecting with driving unit.
AU GUO X
   WANG Y
   ZHOU M
   FU J
   WANG S
   SHI Z
   ZHANG N
   YU Z
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202185405F
AB    NOVELTY - The device includes a liquid inlet pipe (201) that is rotatably connected with an upper surface of a foaming box (1). A bottom end of the liquid inlet pipe passes through an inner top wall of the foaming box and is fixedly connected with a centrifugal cylinder (202). A lower surface of the centrifugal cylinder is fixedly connected with a fixing shaft. A surface of the fixing shaft is fixedly connected with a foaming paddle (204). An inner wall of the foaming box is fixedly connected with an air spraying piece (205). An upper surface of the foaming box is fixedly connected with a driving unit (206).
   USE - Device for injecting well carbon dioxide foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The device drives the driving belt wheel to rotate through the output end of the motor so as to make the driven belt wheel rotate under the cooperation of the transmission belt and the driven belt wheel, thus driving the centrifugal cylinder to rotate, generating a centrifugal force, so that the foam liquid in the centrifugal cylinder is thrown out under the action of centrifugal force, and the carbon dioxide entering from the outside. The centrifugal cylinder where the air ejecting piece to premix with the foam liquid that is thrown out, so as to improve the mixing degree, thus allowing the good carbon dioxide foam injection device to solve the problem in the present technique. The device has a low foaming rate, which affects the oil flooding effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the device for injecting well carbon dioxide foam.
   Foaming box (1)
   Liquid inlet pipe (201)
   Centrifugal cylinder (202)
   Foaming paddle (204)
   Air spraying piece (205)
   Driving unit (206)

The                outlet of oxygen collection unit is connected to                oxygen inlet of fuel power generation unit.

PT P
PN EP3831982-A1; WO2021110552-A1; CN114616359-A; EP4069892-A1
TI Use of electrochemical cell for electrochemical            conversion of carbon dioxide in which electrochemical            cell has gas diffusion electrode as cathode, and carbon            dioxide is supplied in gaseous form to cathode through            catholyte ion solution.
AU KOENIG M
   METIN B
   VAES J
   KLEMM E
   DEEPAK P
   KONIG M
   BULUT M
   PANT D
AE VITO NV (VLAA-C)
   VITO NV (VLAA-C)
GA 202162747P
AB    NOVELTY - Use of an electrochemical cell is claimed for                electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide (CO2),                where electrochemical cell comprises gas diffusion                electrode (GDE) as cathode, and CO2 is supplied in                gaseous form to the cathode through a catholyte ion                solution comprising an aprotic solvent.
   USE - An electrochemical cell is useful for                electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - With an increased CO2 supply to the active                site, applying aprotic solvents and GDEs, the CO2                reduction at high current densities can be realized                even at reduced CO2 concentrations in the feed gas.                This has two advantages, i.e., depending on the CO2                waste stream source, a purification of the gas may                not be necessary or may be confined to gas                impurities which act as catalyst poisons or enable                side reactions, and a reduced CO2 concentration in                the vicinity of the electrode is reported to be                beneficial in terms of the selectivity between the                dimerization reaction to oxalate and the unwanted                side disproportion reaction to CO and carbonate.                Consequently, by skipping a cost- and                energy-intensive purification step in a multi-step                carbon capture and utilization process, the                Faradaic efficiency (FE) of the process can be                further improved. By use of finely-dispersed metal                electrocatalyst on cheap carbon support, the amount                of metal catalyst applied can be reduced                significantly, reducing the overall production cost                of the electrode when compared to flat or porous,                skeletal-type full-metal electrodes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                electrochemical cell comprising GDE cathode,                gaseous CO2 inlet to the GDE cathode, and                supporting electrolyte in an aprotic solvent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                single chamber electrochemical cell for use in the                electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide in an                aprotic solvent using a GDE as cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Anode is a sacrificial                  anode, such as sacrificial zinc or aluminum                  anode. GDE comprises metal or metal oxide                  catalyst, preferably metal or metal oxide                  catalyst nanoparticles. Metal or metal oxide                  catalysts are selected from lead (Pb), titanium                  (Ti), iron (Fe), and/or molybdenum (Mo). Metal or                  metal oxide catalyst is provided on a porous                  support, preferably finely dispersed as                  nanoparticles on a porous support. GDE cathode is                  double-layer-GDE cathode comprising catalyst                  layer comprising the metal or metal oxide                  catalyst, and gas diffusion layer consisting of a                  hydrophobic porous material, preferably PTFE                  mixed with a pore former, such as ammonium                  bicarbonate. GDE comprises a current collector,                  preferably a current collector consisting of a                  layer of an electrochemically inert but highly                  conductive material, such as graphite or                  stainless steel mesh. Metal or metal oxide                  catalyst is provided on a support of hydrophobic                  carbon black agglomerates. Electrochemical cell                  further comprises catholyte inlet and outlet. The                  cell is a single-chamber electrochemical                  cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Supporting electrolyte                  in the catholyte ion solution is selected from                  tetraalkylammonium salts of tetrafluoroborates,                  perchlorates, or hexafluorophosphates as cations                  and ions, respectively, dissolved in aprotic                  solvents, preferably tetraalkylammonium salts,                  such as tetraethylammonium or tetrabutylammonium.                  Aprotic solvent is selected from acetonitrile,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, or                  propylene carbonate, preferably                  acetonitrile.

TI Carbon dioxide eluting device for use in liquefied natural gas production, has tower body whose bottom is provided with liquid level monitor and pH meter, where upper part of tower body is located above liquid inlet and provided with gas-liquid separating layer.
AU WANG C
   GUO J
   CAI Q
   WANG J
   ZHANG L
AE CHINA NATURAL GAS GUANGYUAN CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202084207B
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide eluting device in liquefied natural gas production, comprising a carbon dioxide eluting tower and a regeneration circulating device; the carbon dioxide eluting tower comprises an air inlet set at the lower part of the tower body, a liquid outlet and a gas outlet and a liquid inlet set on the upper part of the tower body; the tower body is provided with a filler layer; the liquid inlet is provided with a liquid distributor; the upper part of the filler layer, the liquid inlet and the liquid outlet are connected with the regeneration circulating device through the pipeline; the middle part of the tower body is provided with a liquid replenishing port connected with the regeneration circulating device; the liquid replenishing port is located between the two filler layers; the liquid replenishing port is also provided with a liquid distributor; the liquid inlet, the liquid inlet and the liquid outlet are provided with an electromagnetic valve; the bottom of the tower body is provided with a liquid level monitor and a pH meter; the liquid level monitor and the pH meter are associated with the electromagnetic valve; the upper part of the tower body is located above the liquid inlet and provided with a gas-liquid separating layer and a cyclone separator. The device improves the eluting efficiency of the carbon dioxide, effectively removes the solvent contained in the exhaust gas, the production energy consumption is low, the product quality is high.

AB    NOVELTY - The installation tool (80) has a control panel                (50) which is provided with a control portion that                controls a combustion machine (10) is installed. An                attaching portion is provided to which the control                panel is attached. A first fixed portion (82) is                provided to fix the attaching portion to the                combustion machine. A second fixed portion (83) is                provided to fix the attaching portion to a mounting                portion (41) in which the combustion machine is                mounted. The first fixed portion fixes the upper                portion of the attaching portion to the side                surface of the combustion machine. The second fixed                portion fixes the lower portion of the attaching                portion so that the attaching portion is supported                by the mounting portion from the downward                direction.
   USE - Installation tool used in carbon dioxide                supply apparatus (claimed) and installed in                combustion machine for supplying carbon dioxide in                cultivation rooms e.g. greenhouse and vinyl                greenhouse and carbon dioxide supplied to plant                e.g. agricultural product grown within cultivation                room.

PN US2020190386-A1
TI Composition used for performing drilling operations of boreholes comprises mixture of nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide gas, Y-grade fluids mixture and surfactant foaming agent.
AU DAAR R A
AE DAAR R A (DAAR-Individual)
GA 202053943C
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises: either a mixture of nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide gas, Y-grade fluids mixture and a surfactant foaming agent; or a mixture of nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide gas, Y-grade fluids mixture, surfactant foaming agent and a high density, non-toxic, inert gas compound; or a mixture of nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide gas, Y-grade fluids mixture and a flame retardant chemical; or a mixture of nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide gas, Y-grade fluids mixture and a higher quality foam, as well as containing gas and/or chemical additives that alter its thermal properties.
   USE - The composition is useful for performing drilling operations of boreholes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: inhibits formation damage and prevents additional pressure drop in the wellbore created when drilling fluid filtrate invades the near wellbore reservoir pore network during drilling operations, where the reduced formation damage will improve oil and gas production rates over those achieved using conventional drilling fluids; retains in-situ permeability in the reservoir rock surrounding the wellbore; stimulates the production from oil wells during over-balanced pressure operations; reduces the need to subsequently perform expensive remedial stimulation operations; shortens or eliminates well cleanup and flow-back operations after well handover to field operations; minimizes tank and pit overflow situations; can be produced into the production gathering infrastructure and recovered for reuse or subsequent sale; utilizes high density compound, hence can be employed in deeper drilling operations and allows the drilling fluid to achieve greater working depths in higher pressure reservoirs; utilizes flame retardant chemical, hence reduces fire hazard at the drilling site; alleviates thawing problem and its associated sloughing; and provides more stable boreholes that deliver less sloughing, fewer key seats, competent cement bonds between the frozen soil horizon and the surface casing, and fewer abandoned boreholes.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2019239381-A1; CA3102348-A1; BR112020025549-A2; EP3806982-A1; US2021229027-A1
TI Multi-stage pressure swing adsorption process to remove contaminant gases from raw methane streams, comprises first and second stages to remove nitrogen and oxygen comprising e.g. adsorption, equalization provided and evacuation.
AU DA SILVA BARCIA P
   SANTOS SILVA FERREIRA D
   DA SILVA CARVALHO MONTEIRO S
   DA SILVA B P
   SANTOS S F D
   DA SILVA C M S
   BARCIA P D S
   FERREIRA D A S
   MONTEIRO S D D S
   DA SLLVA C M S
AE SYSADVANCE & SISTEMAS ENG SA (SYSA-Non-standard)
   SYSADVANCE SISTEMAS ENGENHARIA LTDA (SYSA-Non-standard)
   SYSADVANCE & SISTEMAS ENG SA (SYSA-Non-standard)
   SYSADVANCE SISTEMAS ENGENHARIA LTDA (SYSA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7352G
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-stage pressure swing adsorption process to remove contaminant gases from raw methane streams, comprises at least two pressure swing adsorption stages of first stage to remove nitrogen and oxygen, comprising feed, adsorption, equalization provided, blowdown, evacuation, purge and equalization received, where the first stage comprises at least one adsorbent, or their mixtures, with strong affinity for carbon dioxide and methane, and second stage to remove carbon dioxide and residual nitrogen and oxygen comprising adsorption, co-current depressurization, first equalization provided, second equalization provided, evacuation, first equalization received, idle, second equalization received and idle, where the second stage comprises at least one adsorbent, or their mixtures, with strong affinity for carbon dioxide and methane.
   USE - The process is useful for removing contaminant gases from raw methane streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: removes contaminant gases from raw methane streams; enhances adsorbents that has preferably of not having methane/oxygen molecule selectivity, hence increasing the oxygen content in their products stream and compromising the target of the grid biomethane specs; utilizes first stage separates hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide from methane using a kinetic- driven adsorbent, and hence the second stage improved biomethane concentration by reducing the nitrogen content, using ETS-4 molecular sieve; utilizes multiple stage that generate potentially wasteful byproduct streams that further reduce the attractiveness of membrane separation to refine methane; exhibits a strong adsorption capacity and high mass transfer rate at the micropores of the selected molecular sieve; and utilizes second-grade, methane-enriched stream, the recycling stream which is delivered and redirected to the first-stage feed in order to improve and slightly enrich the feed concentration in methane and reduce the levels of carbon dioxide, nitrogen and oxygen, also increasing methane recovery in more than 2%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The second stage further comprises blowdown stage after the Second equalization provided step and before the evacuation step. The Co current depressurization step of the second stage occurs after the second equalization provided and before the evacuation step. The second stage comprises a backfill stage after the second equalization received and before the second idle stage. The second stage comprises a backfill stage after the second idle stage. The process comprises a pre-treatment step before the first stage. The first stage operates at a temperature of -50 to 120 degrees C. The operating pressure during the adsorption step in the first stage is 60-1500 kPa . The purge to feed ratio varies 0.3-0.9 depending on nitrogen feed stream content. The operating desorption pressure in the evacuation step of the first stage ranges of 1-100 kPa. The intermediate biogas stream resultant of the first stage is pressurized of 200-4000 kPa and fed to the adsorption beds of the second stage. The second stage operates at a temperature between of -50 to 150 degrees C . The operating pressure during the adsorption step of the second stage is 200-4000 kPa. The operating desorption pressure in the evacuation step of the second stage is 0.001-100 kPa. The adsorbents used in the first and second stage comprises zeolites, titanosilicates , metal-organic frameworks, activated carbons, carbon molecular sieves, alumina, silica gel, novel adsorbents like ionic liquid zeolites or other mesoporous materials with silicon/aluminum-based. The equalization occurs through the top. The equalization occurs through the bottom. The desorption in the evacuation step of the second stage occurs with purge. The desorption in the evacuation step of the second stage occurs without purge.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110420669-A; CN110420669-B
TI Supported copper cluster used for catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, has specific copper atoms and mass content.
AU ZHU Y
   CAI X
   CHEN M
   DING W
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
   BEIJING COMPUTATIONAL SCI RES CENT (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201995609L
AB    NOVELTY - Supported copper cluster has a number of atoms of 6 and a mass content of 0.1-10 wt.%.
   USE - The copper cluster is used for catalyzing a carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper cluster has low copper content, high efficiency and low cost, and reduces environmental pollution. The copper cluster can be mass produced industrially, and exhibits excellent catalytic performance in the catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide-based chemicals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the supported copper cluster, involving (i) preparing a solution having a mass fraction of 0.1-0.5% copper salt, (ii) preparing a solution of sodium borohydride having a mass fraction of 0.1-0.3%, (iii) adding 2-mercaptobenzothiazole having the mass of 0.6 times of the copper salt to the solution in step (i), (iv) adding the solution obtained in step (iii) to the sodium borohydride solution in step (ii), and stirring at 25 degrees C for 3 hours, (v) subjected the mixture obtained in step (iv) to rotary evaporation to remove an acetonitrile solvent, washing the remaining solids with an organic solvent, and then extracting the product with dichloromethane to obtain the copper cluster (Cu6) with precise structure, and (vi) adding the copper cluster obtained in step (v) to a certain amount of oxide carrier such as magnesium oxide, adding to an ultrasonic instrument, processing for 15 minutes, stirring overnight, drying the solvent, and taking the copper cluster as an active center supported catalyst; and
   (2) a method for catalyzing a carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, involving using the above-mentioned supported copper cluster, and carrying out catalytic reaction at a temperature of 100-150 degrees C and a pressure of 1-4 MPa, where the reaction gases used are carbon dioxide, hydrogen and nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The support is a transition metal oxide. The copper salt is copper acetylacetonate or copper nitrate. The oxide carrier is one of silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide, zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, molybdenum oxide, zinc oxide and cobalt oxide. Preferred Method: In the method, the catalytic reaction temperature is 130 degrees , where the ratio of the hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1-3, and nitrogen is used as an internal standard.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium borohydride solution is one of water, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol and butanol solution. The organic solvent is one of dichloromethane, toluene, acetone, acetonitrile, diethyl ether, dimethylformamide and tetrahydrofuran.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN US2019282952-A1; WO2019178350-A1; US11117092-B2
TI Capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas stream containing carbon dioxide by introducing flue gas stream to high carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity membrane, introducing retentate stream to high flux membrane, and treating permeate stream.
AU LI S
   PYRZYNSKI T
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201980196S
AB    NOVELTY - An energy efficient process for capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from flue gas stream containing greater than or equal to 5 vol.% CO2 involves introducing flue gas stream containing greater than or equal to 5 vol. % CO2 to 1st stage membrane separator containing 1st stage high CO2/nitrogen (N2) selectivity membrane to produce 1st stage CO2-depleted retentate stream and 1st stage CO2-enriched permeate stream; introducing 1st stage CO2-depleted retentate stream to 2nd stage membrane separator containing 2nd stage high flux membrane to produce 2nd stage further CO2-depleted retentate stream and 2nd stage CO2-enriched permeate stream; treating 2nd stage CO2-enriched permeate stream to recover water and form recyclable CO2 stream; introducing recyclable CO2 stream to 1st stage membrane separator; treating 1st stage CO2-enriched permeate stream to recover water and form non-condensable CO2-rich stream; and compressing CO2 of CO2-rich stream to form capture quantity of CO2.
   USE - An energy efficient process for capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) from flue gas stream containing greater than or equal to 5 vol.% CO2, such produced or resulting from power plant operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method integrates a high CO2/N2 selectivity membrane and a high CO2 flux membrane, offering new opportunities to explore significant reductions in the cost of CO2 capture. The system, functionally, has the benefits of a hybrid system but the simplicity of a membrane system which also reduces the up-front installation costs and footprint and does not add circulating liquids to the power plant environment, facilitating the ease of integration into a power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for capture of CO2 from flue gas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Compositions: The 1st stage high CO2/N2 selectivity membrane is a graphene oxide-based membrane. The 2nd stage high flux membrane is a graphene oxide-based membrane. Preferred Properties: The 1st stage high CO2/N2 selectivity membrane has target CO2/N2 selectivity of greater than or equal to 120. The 2nd stage high flux membrane has a target CO2 permeance of greater than or equal to 1000 GPU.

PN JP2019147099-A; JP6955700-B2
TI Adsorbent used for removing carbon dioxide from e.g. waste gas of motor vehicle, comprises organic silica-type porous material including silicon-containing compound, in which basic functional group is bonded with silicon in skeleton.
AU YANO K
   SETOYAMA N
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2019756647
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises an organic silica-type porous material. The organic silica-type porous material includes silicon-containing compound (I), in which basic functional group is bonded with silicon in skeleton of the porous material. The organic silica-type porous material has a specific surface area of 1-30 m2/g calculated based on nitrogen adsorption isothermal line of the porous material. The ratio of mass ratio of an organic component in the porous material with respect to average pore diameter of a sample calculated based on nitrogen adsorption isothermal line of sample obtained by heat-processing the porous material at 550 degrees C for 6 hours is more than 0.06/nm.
   USE - Adsorbent is used for removing carbon dioxide from waste gas of power station, and waste gas of motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent can fully adsorb carbon dioxide even at high-temperature range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises an organic silica-type porous material. The organic silica-type porous material includes silicon-containing compound of formula: X-Si (I), in which basic functional group is bonded with silicon in skeleton of the porous material. The organic silica-type porous material has a specific surface area of 1-30 m2/g calculated based on nitrogen adsorption isothermal line of the porous material. The ratio of mass ratio of an organic component in the porous material with respect to average pore diameter of a sample calculated based on nitrogen adsorption isothermal line of sample obtained by heat-processing the porous material at 550 degrees C for 6 hours is more than 0.06/nm.
   X = basic functional group of formula: R1-NH-(CH2)n-;
   R1 = H, aminoalkyl or aminoalkyl-aminoalkyl;and
   n = 1-6.
TF Preferred Definitions:
   X = aminopropyl, aminoethyl-aminopropyl, or aminoethyl-aminoethyl-aminopropyl.

PT P
PN CN110125158-A; CN110125158-B
TI Method for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by low-level leaching and high-level extraction technique, involves stopping eluent after introducing eluent, and closing vacuum system after stopping eluent.
AU NA P
   WANG C
   QIAO B
   JIAO H
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019728170
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves vertically constructing a sand well on a surface of soil to be treated and deepened into a soil layer at a junction of layers for forming a well spacing between sand wells about 1-3 m such that the sand wells are arranged in a circular or square symmetrical arrangement. A deep silty clay working cushion is laid on a surface such that layer thickness of a working mat is about 5-10 cm. A carbon dioxide eluent is prepared for passing the eluent into an eluent tube, controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide and simultaneously opening a vacuum system to control absolute vacuum degree about 50-100 kPa, such that suction filtration speed is controlled at 2-5 cm/s. The eluent is stopped after introducing the eluent for 30-60 minutes. The vacuum system is closed after stopping the eluent for 10-30 minutes.
   USE - Method for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by low-level leaching and high-level extraction technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing carbon dioxide eluent passed into the eluent tube so as to control carbon dioxide flow while opening the vacuum system, control vacuum and eluent time, control vacuum extraction time, so that leaching process and extraction process are simultaneously carried out, and the carbon dioxide is upwardly diffused for accelerating and expanding range of bicarbonate-forming soil, thus completely performing leaching treatment without affecting heavy metal salt in a mature soil layer by vacuum pressure, and effectively preventing the heavy metal salt from polluting groundwater and the soil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating a method for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by low-level leaching and high-level extraction technique. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
