PN CN109897154-A
TI Polyurethane material based on supercritical carbon dioxide preparation comprises isocyanate derivative, polyether polyol, auxiliary agent, foaming agent, composite catalyst, and metal crystal whisker.
AU BAO K
AE BAO K (BAOK-Individual)
GA 201956297H
AB    NOVELTY - Polyurethane material based on supercritical carbon dioxide preparation comprises first component and second component, where the first component is isocyanate derivative, and the second component includes 60-100 pts. wt. polyether polyol, 2.2-8.6 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 1.2-3.8 pts. wt. foaming agent and 0.15-1.25 pts. wt. composite catalyst, the polyurethane material, and the polyurethane material further comprises metal crystal whisker, and the amount of metallic crystal whisker is 1.8-5.2% relative to the mass percent of the polyurethane material.
   USE - Used as polyurethane material based on supercritical carbon dioxide preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane material has excellent sound-absorbing bending resistance, small size distortion and excellent heat preservation effect, performance and vibration damping ability; and realizes noise furthest absorb.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polyurethane material based on supercritical carbon dioxide preparation, comprising (i) mixing polyether glycol, composite catalyst, auxiliary agent and foaming agent by stirring and to obtain second component, (ii) adding the obtained second component into the mold cavity according to the proportion to the first component, carrying out auxiliary foaming reaction using ultrasonic vibration for one time, set mold cavity temperature to 60-100 degrees C and reacting for 15-180 minutes to obtain pre-foamed polyurethane foam, and (iii) ultrasonic dispersing the nano-aluminum oxide or nickel metal raw material in remaining amount of auxiliary agent, then dissolving auxiliary agent by supercritical carbon dioxide and spraying into the pre-expanded polyurethane foam skeleton to perform swelling and secondary foaming, then quickly relieving pressure, precipitating the metal raw material and adding into a nucleation in the polyurethane foam skeleton and growig into a metal whisker to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal whiskers are alumina whiskers or nickel oxide whiskers. The composite catalyst is piperidine salt ionic liquid with dibutyltin dilaurate, dibutyltin dimethacrylate tin, dibutyltin diacetate, pentaisopropyl stannate and/or chloromonobutyl stannate. The cation of the ionic liquid is substituted by amino and tetrafluoroborate anion. The foaming agent is water, sodium carbonate, magnesium carbonate and/or calcium carbonate. The polyether polyol is phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol, where the amount of phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol is 3-15 wt.%, and the amount of phosphorus is 15-42 wt.% based on phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol. The mass ratio of first component to second component is 1:1-1.5. The size of the prepared polyurethane material by the supercritical carbon dioxide is 3-5% larger than the size of the obtained polyurethane foam. Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises nucleating the obtained polyurethane material, then washing with acetone and deionized water in order to remove monomer and auxiliary agent remaining in the polyurethane material.

   OSTBERG M
   WSTBERG M
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2019509577
AB    NOVELTY - Reformation of hydrocarbon feed                steam-containing hydrocarbon gas and steam involves                providing hydrocarbon gas and steam, synthesizing                gas generation reactor, optionally comprising a                catalyst (I), generating a synthesis gas (A) from                the hydrocarbon feed stream, providing a heated                carbon dioxide rich stream to a post converter                comprising a catalyst (II) active for steam                reforming, methanation and reverse water gas shift                re-actions, obtaining steam reformer, methanation                and reverse water gas shift reactions of synthesis                gas (A) and heated carbon dioxide rich stream to                produce a product synthesis gas. The catalyst (II)                is heated electrically by component of an                electrical power source.
   USE - Reformation of hydrocarbon feed                steam-containing hydrocarbon gas and steam e.g.                natural gas, town gas, mixture of methane, and                higher hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient reformation of                hydrocarbon feed steam-containing hydrocarbon gas                and steam with low hydrogen/carbon monoxide                ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for reforming of hydrocarbon feed stream-containing                hydrocarbon gas and steam, which comprises                synthesis gas generation reactor, optionally                comprising catalyst (I), and arranged to generate                synthesis gas (A) from hydrocarbon feed stream,                post converter housing a catalyst (II) active for                steam reformer, methanation and reverse water gas                shift reactions, conduit for conducting the                synthesis gas (A) to post converter, means for                adding a heated carbon dioxide rich stream to first                synthesis gas (A) upstream of post converter and/or                for adding a heated carbon dioxide rich stream                directly into post converter, in which system                further comprises electrical power source arranged                for heating catalyst (II) electrically.

   Hydrocarbon gas stream (1)
   Steam (2)
   Hydrocarbon feed stream (3)
   Steam methane reformer reactor (10)
   System (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst (II) is                  heated by resistance heating and/or inductive                  heating. The hydrocarbon feed stream comprises                  one or more of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon                  dioxide, nitrogen, argon, and/or higher                  hydrocarbon. The heated carbon dioxide rich                  stream comprises one or more of steam, hydrogen,                  methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide,                  sulfur dioxide, nitrogen, and/or argon. The                  catalyst (I) is reforming catalyst. The catalyst                  (II) is catalyst active for steam methane                  reformer, methanation and reverse water gas shift                  reactions. Preferred Properties: The mole ratio                  of carbon dioxide in the heated carbon dioxide                  rich stream to hydrocarbon in the hydrocarbon                  feed stream is more than 0.5. The steam to carbon                  ratio of the hydrocarbon feed stream is 0.4-2.0.                  The heated carbon dioxide rich stream comprises                  at least 50 dry mol%, preferably 90 dry mol%                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Condition: The heated                  carbon dioxide rich stream is heated at                  temperature of 350-950 degrees C and addition to                  the synthesis gas (A). Preferred Process: The                  synthesis gas generation reactor is steam methane                  reforming reactor comprises heat source arranged                  to heat catalyst (I) within at least one reformer                  tube to temperature sufficient to ensure that the                  synthesis gas (A) exiting the steam methane                  reforming reactor has temperature of 650-950                  degrees C. The method further involves adding                  composition of heated carbon dioxide rich stream                  is adjusted to ensure that the ratio                  (hydrogen/carbon dioxide) of product synthesis                  gas is 2.5 or less. The post converter is                  arranged to be heated by resistance heating                  and/or inductive heating. Preferred Apparatus:                  The steam methane reforming reactor is heated by                  resistance heating and/or inductive heating. The                  synthesis gas generation reactor is auto-thermal                  reforming reactor with operating conditions                  adjusted to ensure that the synthesis gas (A)                  exiting the auto-thermal reforming reactor has                  temperature of 900-1100 degrees C.

PN EP3491926-A1
TI Method for storing food e.g. baked food, involves injecting carbon dioxide and inert gas inside container, and sealing container that is filled with food, after creating vacuum and inserting carbon dioxide and inert gas.
AU BENETOLLO L
   BENETOLLO M
   BENETOLLO E
AE DUE BI AUTOMAZIONI DI BENETOLLO LUIGI (DUEB-Non-standard)
GA 201949411S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves making an available food item which is at temperature equal to or higher than about 90 degrees C. A container which is made of a material suitable for use with food is filled with food. The vacuum is created inside closed environments (9,9'), in which container is positioned with food for a pre-set time and defined so as to bring food from the initial temperature at a second temperature which is lower than initial temperature. The carbon dioxide and inert gas such as nitrogen are injected inside container which is filled with food. The container that is filled with food is sealed, after creating the vacuum and inserting carbon dioxide and inert gas. The vacuum that is created, and the carbon dioxide and inert gas that are injected inside container are carried out, so that amount of oxygen within container filled with food and sealed is equal to or less than about 2% and ideally equal to about 0.2%.
   USE - Method for storing food such as baked food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows to significantly reduce the energy consumption required for storing the food. The method allows to reduce the cooking time of the food, thus allowing energy saving. The method allows for obtaining preserved food which is hygienic and safe at the sanitary level. The long shelf life of the food is allowed. The method reduces cooking times, thus allowing energy saving during the cooking step of about 15-18%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a machine for implementing process for storing food.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of the machine for implementing the process for storing food.
   Welding system (7)
   Lid (8)
   Closed environments (9,9')
   Handling unit (10)
   Vacuum gauge (28)

PN CN107715831-A; CN107715831-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent such as DD3R molecular sieve used for removing gaseous mixture, by preparing sigma-1 seed crystal, and carrying out hydrothermal crystallization on sigma-1 seed crystal in hydrothermal synthesis liquid.
AU LIU Q
AE LIU Q (LIUQ-Individual)
   SHANGHAI WEICHUANG STANDARD GAS ANALYTIC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201817319X
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of carbon dioxide adsorbent such as DD3R molecular sieve involves preparing sigma -1 seed crystal, carrying out hydrothermal crystallization on sigma -1 seed crystal in a hydrothermal synthesis liquid, mixing the pre-prepared sigma -1 particles with water, and then carrying out wet ball milling at a speed of 400-600 rpm for 3-4 hours, leaving still for 4-5 hours, taking the lower layer precipitate, and drying the precipitate. The hydrothermal crystallization process involves adding sigma -1 seed crystal to synthetic liquid and placing in a synthesis reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent, which is useful for removing a gaseous mixture, which includes industrial tail gas, natural gas and biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares carbon dioxide adsorbent with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of carbon dioxide adsorbent such as DD3R molecular sieve involves preparing sigma -1 seed crystal, carrying out hydrothermal crystallization on sigma -1 seed crystal in a hydrothermal synthesis liquid, mixing the pre-prepared sigma -1 particles with water, and then carrying out wet ball milling at a speed of 400-600 rpm for 3-4 hours, leaving still for 4-5 hours, taking the lower layer precipitate, and drying the precipitate. The hydrothermal crystallization process involves adding sigma -1 seed crystal to synthetic liquid and placing in a synthesis reactor, putting the synthesis reactor into a drying oven, gradually heating the synthesis reactor from room temperature to 100-120 degrees C, keeping the temperature for 0.5-2 hours, then transferring the synthesis reactor to the oven, heating at 180-200 degrees C, and continuously carrying out crystallization for 12-24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sigma -1 particles are prepared by microwave synthesis method. Preferred Components: The synthetic liquid comprises adamantane and silicon dioxide, and further comprises a mineralizing agent, which is a non-fluorinated mineralizing agent, and a mineralizer. The ball mill is made of zirconium oxide materials.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineralizer is ethylenediamine.

TI Carbon dioxide reduction apparatus comprises chamber comprising dispersion unit for dispersing reducing agent including liquid metal and electrolyte and reaction unit for generating reduction reaction of carbon dioxide.
AU LEEDONGHYUN
   SUNG W
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 202124726T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction apparatus (10) comprises chamber (100) comprising a dispersion unit (120) for dispersing a reducing agent including a liquid metal and an electrolyte and a reaction unit (130) for generating a reduction reaction of carbon dioxide. A carbon dioxide supply unit (200) supplying the carbon dioxide to the chamber. A reducing agent supply unit supplying the reducing agent to the chamber. A separation unit for separating the reduced carbon dioxide and the reducing agent. The reducing agent separated from the separation unit is resupplied to the chamber to continuously reduce carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide reduction apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus is economical, increases reaction area between liquid metal and carbon dioxide, and improves carbon dioxide reduction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for continuous reduction method of carbon dioxide using the carbon dioxide reducing device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide reduction apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide reduction apparatus (10)
   Chamber (100)
   Dispersion unit (120)
   Reaction unit (130)

   CUELLAR K

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a hydrogen-enriched product and                recovering carbon dioxide (CO2) from an effluent                stream of a hydrogen production unit involves                processing a feed stream comprising hydrocarbons or                a carbonaceous feedstock in the hydrogen production                unit to produce synthesis gas mixture comprising                hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water and methane, carbon                monoxide, nitrogen, and argon, and separating                effluent stream comprising the synthesis gas in a                pressure swing adsorption (PSA) system that                produces two product streams into high-pressure                hydrogen stream enriched in hydrogen. The hydrogen                depleted low-pressure waste gas stream is                compressed in compressor to form a compressed waste                gas stream. The compressed waste gas stream in a                CO2recovery system is separated into CO2-enriched                product stream and overhead stream comprising a                portion of the hydrogen, a portion of the carbon                dioxide, and the methane, the carbon monoxide, the                nitrogen, and the argon, and recovered the                high-pressure hydrogen stream.
   USE - Method for preparing a hydrogen-enriched                product and recovering carbon dioxide from an                effluent stream of a hydrogen production                unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is improved hydrogen separation                method with improved, cost-effective                CO2recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing hydrogen and recovering                CO2from an effluent stream of a hydrogen production                unit, which includes a hydrogen production process                unit having inlet and outlet, a PSA system having                an inlet, a high-pressure hydrogen outlet, and a                low-pressure waste gas outlet, and the PSA system                inlet in fluid communication with the outlet of the                hydrogen production unit, a compressor having an                inlet and an outlet, the low-pressure waste gas                outlet in fluid communication with the compressor                inlet, and a CO2recovery system having an                inlet.

AB    NOVELTY - The insect trap has a housing having a body                (101) for supporting lid (102). A capture basket                (103) is supported by body. The capture basket is                constructed to retain insects. An ultrasonic                vibration atomizer periodically generates atomized                vapor cloud of carbon dioxide gas that acts as                insect attractant. The ultrasonic vibration                atomizer includes storage cup assembly with first                storage cup to hold first chemical, and second                storage cup to hold second chemical. The second                storage cup operates as a combustion chamber inside                which a chemical reaction occurs during use between                first chemical, second chemical, and atomized water                to generate atomized carbon dioxide. The first and                second storage cups are physically separated so                that the first and second chemical are not in                contact while being stored. A water storage tank is                provided with source of mechanical vibration to                create ultrasonic wave. The first chemical is                sodium bicarbonate and second chemical is aluminum                sulfate.
   USE - Insect trap for attracting and trapping                mosquito. Uses include but are not limited to,                chiggers, insects flies, fleas and ticks.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is lightweight, compact, and                efficient in the production and dispersion of                carbon dioxide, and effectively attracts and kills                mosquitos, even while having a small footprint. The                device can be used without the need for a propane                tank, and is capable of operating continuously for                about one month, generating a vapor cloud of                atomized carbon dioxide at a level above 2000 ppm.                The device is preferably constructed and packaged,                so that it does not require assembly by the                user.

AB    NOVELTY - A mixed substrate film comprises a polymer                substrate, and pyrazine-based metal-organic                framework nanoparticles (I) or                4,4'-bipyridine-based metal-organic framework                nanoparticles (II) dispersed in the substrate,                where metal is copper, zinc, cobalt or                nickel.
   USE - The mixed substrate film is useful in                hydrogen/carbon dioxide gas separation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed substrate film has excellent                separation performance for hydrogen/carbon dioxide.                The mechanical flexibility and stability of the                mixed substrate film can be maintained even when                80% metal-organic framework particles is used. The                strong interaction of copper complex                (Cu(SiF6)(bpy)2) on carbon dioxide molecules can                hinder the transport of carbon dioxide molecules.                The metal-organic framework particles can be well                dispersed without interfacial defects with the                polymer by in-situ steam reforming.

   M1 = Cu, Zn, Co or Ni;
   pyz = pyrazine; and
   bpy = 4,4'-bipyridine.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the mixed substrate film, which                involves: dissolving the polymer substrate in water                to obtain a substrate solution, and dissolving                metal-hexafluorosilicate (MSiF6) in water to obtain                a salt solution; mixing the substrate solution and                the salt solution to obtain a coating solution,                coating the coating solution on a carrier, and                evaporating solvent; and placing the carrier in                ligand vapor to react. The ligand is pyrazine or                4,4'-bipyridine.

   ADVANTAGE - The torch increases welding efficiency by inducing vertical spray of carbon dioxide gas, ensures shielding effect, provides environmentally friendly product by reducing amount of carbon dioxide gas, easily checks life of consumable parts damaged by the thermal environment, improves maintenance by facilitating replacement of consumable parts, and is economical.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the torch for welding using carbon dioxide.
   Torch cable connector (100)
   Diffuser (200)
   Contact tip (300)
   Gas distribution adapter (400)
   Nozzle housing (500)

TI Dissolved air water jet device used in mechanical            processing field, has pressure detection device that            used to detect air pressure at top of water tank, and            bottom of water tank is connected to water jet device            through water outlet.
AU LI D
   KANG Y
   SHI H
   WANG X
   WANG Z
   YUAN M
   PAN H
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2020B54054
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a dissolved air device and a                water jet device. The dissolved air device includes                a water tank (8), a carbon dioxide supply device,                an air supply device, a heating device, a pressure                detection device and a controller (11). The carbon                dioxide gas supply device is connected to bottom of                the water tank through a carbon dioxide gas supply                pipe (21) for supplying carbon dioxide gas to the                bottom of the water tank. The air supply device is                connected to top of the water tank through an air                supply pipe (5) for supplying air to the top of the                water tank. The pressure detection device is used                to detect the air pressure at the top of the water                tank, and the bottom of the water tank is connected                to the water jet device through a water outlet. A                top the water tank is provided with a water inlet                (10) with a valve, and the top of the water tank is                provided with a vent (20) with a valve.
   USE - Dissolved air water jet device mechanical                processing, food medical treatment and ocean                engineering fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method significantly improves the impact                erosion ability of the water jet and improves the                impact efficiency of the water jet. The gas                separates out from the high-speed water stream,                after spraying from the nozzle, thus enhancing the                impact force of the water jet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for generating dissolved air                water jet using dissolved air water jet                device.

PT P
PN CN211688570-U; CN112744943-A
TI Underground water treatment device comprises water            collecting device, pre-processing device, aeration            device and water storage device connected in turn by            pipeline, where upper part of aeration device is            provided with waste gas collecting device.
AU XU B
   SHAN G
   WANG Y
   WANG S
   ZHAO Y
   LIU Y
   SHI Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A6567K
AB    (CN211688570-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an underground water                treatment device. The apparatus includes: a water                collecting device, a pre-processing device, an                aeration device and a water storage device                connected in turn by a pipeline; the upper part of                the aeration processing device is provided with a                waste gas collecting device. the waste gas                collecting device is connected with the gas cooling                device and the palygorskite clay absorbing device.                The gas cooling device can recover part of the                underground water, return to the water collecting                tank, adjust the water quality and water quantity                of the water collecting tank, and prevent the bad                influence of the water vapor to the adsorption                device. The palygorskite clay adsorption device                uses the palygorskite to absorb the polluted gas                volatilized into the gas phase to reduce the                desorption difficulty and save the desorption                device.
EA    NOVELTY - Method for treating strongly alkaline                groundwater polluted by volatile organic compound                (VOC) involves pumping out contaminated groundwater                to be treated, aerating the sewage with acid gas                rich in carbon dioxide with residual heat after                pretreatment, and reinjecting the treated                groundwater into carbon dioxide-rich acid gas with                residual heat, where the content of carbon dioxide                is 10-25%, and the temperature is maintained at                120-240 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for treating strongly                alkaline groundwater polluted by VOC.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively eliminates influence of                water vapor on adsorption device by waste heat of                acid gas, and achieves an ideal groundwater                treatment effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves                  pumping out the contaminated groundwater to be                  treated to the sump, flowing sewage adjusted by                  the collecting tank into primary sedimentation                  tank to perform preliminary precipitation to                  remove inorganic particles in the water,                  clarifying the water, entering the effluent from                  primary sedimentation tank into aeration tank,                  introducing acid gas rich in carbon dioxide with                  residual heat to aerate the sewage, entering                  treated groundwater into water storage tank and                  re-injecting into the ground through water                  injection well, where the polluted groundwater to                  be treated has a pH higher than 9. The method                  involves adopting primary sedimentation tank i.e.                  advection sedimentation tank. The hydraulic                  retention time is 10-45 minutes. The content of                  suspended solids in the effluent from the primary                  sedimentation tank is less than 50 mg/l depending                  on content of suspended solids in the sewage. The                  method involves performing aeration in aeration                  device, using an exhaust gas collection device                  installed above the aeration device, and being                  connected to the gas cooling device and                  palygorskite clay adsorption device, discharging                  VOC-rich waste gas from aeration, entering it                  into gas cooling device through gas transmission                  pipe after being collected by the waste gas                  collection device, using fresh polluted                  groundwater as low-temperature cold hydrazine to                  cool and removing water vapor in the gas,                  entering gas into palygorskite clay adsorption                  device, using the adsorption performance of                  palygorskite clay, removing the VOC gas, and                  introducing remaining organic gas to regeneration                  boiler for auxiliary combustion to provide                  energy, where the air-water ratio of the aeration                  treatment is 5:1-15:1, the carbon dioxide-rich                  acid gas with residual heat is acid flue gas, and                  the acidic flue gas is waste gas after burning                  fossil fuel and undergoing desulfurization and                  denitration treatment or process waste gas in a                  refinery, preferably heating boiler tail gas and                  catalytic cracking process waste gas. The method                  involves returning portion of groundwater after                  treatment to the sump to adjust water quality and                  quantity of the sump. The method involves                  injecting treated groundwater into water                  injection well with a pressurized pump for every                  5-15 hours to enter the ground to prevent surface                  collapse caused by excessive groundwater                  exploitation. Preferred Component: The VOC in the                  groundwater comprises one or more of benzene,                  dichloromethane, 1,2-dichloroethane, and                  trichloroethylene, where groundwater bodies has                  VOC content of higher than 0.5 mg/l.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3)); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111729227-A; CN111729227-B
TI Self-moving indoor carbon dioxide fire            extinguishing ball, has heat-resistant rubber layer            equidistantly embedded with lamp beads and closed coil            to form closed loop, where lamp beads emit light when            ball is hit for forming flashing effect.
AU WANG E
   WANG L
AE WANG E (WANG-Individual)
   PAN Q (PANQ-Individual)
GA 202099362A
AB    NOVELTY - The ball has a spherical shell (1) whose side                wall is provided with a one-way air inlet pipe (2).                The spherical shell is covered with a                heat-resistant rubber layer (3). Piezoelectric                sheets (4) are provided between the spherical shell                and the heat-resistant rubber layer, where the                piezoelectric sheets are uniformly distributed on                an outer side wall of the spherical shell. The                spherical shell is provided with exhaust holes (5).                An inner end of exhaust hole is provided with an                electromagnetic opening and closing mechanism. The                electromagnetic opening and closing mechanism is                provided with a fixing ring (6) that is fixedly                connected on an inner side wall of the spherical                shell. A sliding groove is slidingly connected with                a sealing block. A heat-resistant rubber layer (3)                is equidistantly embedded with lamp beads and a                closed coil to form a closed loop.
   USE - Self-moving indoor carbon dioxide fire                extinguishing ball.
   ADVANTAGE - The ball is collided back and forth in                indoors, sprays carbon dioxide to avoid dead                corners during ejection process, effectively                extinguishes combustion obstacles, prevents carbon                dioxide from being sprayed into a fire-free space,                emit light by lamp beads when the ball is hit and                forms flashing effect, so as to convenient for                firefighters to observe a position of the ball to                quickly understand fire extinguishing                process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a                self-moving indoor carbon dioxide fire                extinguishing ball.
   Spherical shell (1)
   One-way air inlet pipe (2)
   Heat-resistant rubber layer (3)
   Piezoelectric sheets (4)
   Exhaust holes (5)
   Fixing ring (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - The sealing block is made of ferrite                  material.
